description:
Released by Gordon Food Service (GFS) 1986, possibly, only available for employees of the company, unknown for certain.

The Objective of the game is to collect all 10 Product Tokens, Which includes:

Beverage Systems
Center of the Plate
Clean Power
Dairy
Disposables
FACS
"Fresh Cut" Produce
Frozen
Grocery
Table Top

Each player rolls the dice to be able to draw Opportunity cards and if it is a winning card, you get a particular Product token

Simple Trivial Pursuit-like game from Canada in which players can choose to answer questions themselves for 10 points (Trade-In) or pass the question to an opponent for 5 points (Trade-Off) with the goal of getting 50 points in each of 6 categories.

Incorrect answers in the "danger zone" (between 15 and 35 points) means players must choose a penalty card, typically causing a player to lose points or lose their next turn. Players who correctly answer a question passed to them get to choose a bonus card which could earn bonus points or an extra turn.

The six categories are: 
1. Movies, TV, Theatre
2. People, Places, Things
3. Food, Drink, Customs
4. Fiction, Fables, Myths
5. Music, Singers, Songs
6. Sports, Games, Hobbies

There are 120 cards in each category with 6 questions on each, for a total of over 4,300 questions.

This is a regular deck of cards but on each card also has 4 symbols on it with values.

One card is put face down in the middle of the table.
The rest of the cards are dealed.
Cards are put face down in front of each player.

The active player chooses one symbol category from his top card. The other players also play their top card. The person with the highest value in the chosen category wins the round.
Cards won are put on the bottom of your pile.

When you win a Asterix, Obelix or Idifix card you can use the special red looking glass card to look at the hidden code on the back of the card in the middle of the table. Finding the card with those exact symbol values among the Asterix Obelix and Idefix cards  is the mission. When you win that card in a round or find it on your turn on your pile you have won a mission.
The found mission card will have the new mission on it's back.
The first player to complete 2 missions is the winner.

Another variant that comes with the game is just play until you won all cards and not bother about the mission.

Bifrost Brige - gateway to Asgard.

Each round is one or more duels, aimed at gaining Bridge. After start, each player must play a card or cards on the Bridge or in a Storm. If he does not, he resigns from further participation in the duel, saying, "Hagal". The duel is finished when all players but one say "Hagal".

You win when you score 500 points.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Skull & Shackles – "Blessing of Zogmugot" Promo Card
description:
Multi-Month Subscribers Exclusive

Blessing

Check to Acquire: Dexterity, Stealth, Divine 5

Traits: Divine, Zogmugot

Powers: Discard this card to add 1 die to any check.

Bury this card to choose a card from another character's discard pile and add it to your hand.

Discard this card to explore your location.

After you play this card, if the top card of the blessings discard pile is Blessing of the Gods, recharge this card instead of discarding it.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
2-player

8x8 board

20 stackable pieces, 10 black and 10 white each set numbered from 1-10

Play
Board starts out empty

One player is black the other white.

Black starts, turns alternate.

On a turn, a friendly piece may move or jump in a straight line orthogonally or diagonally across any number of empty spaces and over any number of friendly or enemy pieces so long as the face value of the moving/jumping piece is greater than the pieces being jumped. The jumping piece at the end of a jump must either land on an empty space just after the jumped piece(s) or end a jump by landing on top of an enemy piece already on the board.


     If a player can capture on a turn he must.



     Once a stack of three or more pieces is created, it is off limits, no further pieces may be stacked on them.



     A stack cannot be split or moved. 



     The top piece on a stack designates who has control of that stack.


The game ends when a player manages to stack all of his pieces or is reduced to a single piece.

Object
A winner is determined by adding the face values of all the pieces within all the stacks controlled by each of the players. The player with the highest score wins.

2 – 5 players | 10 min | 5+

Object
Roll as many numbers in sequential order as you can to get the highest score.

Materials
10  six-sided dice
A board having numbers in sequential order from 1 to 10 and the number six highlighted.
50 tokens, 10 each in 5 different colors.

Setup
Each player takes 10 tokens in their chosen color and 2 dice.
The empty board is placed between the players.

Play
The youngest player calls out start.

At the start players simultaneously roll one die from their hand trying to roll the first number on the board.

The first player to roll a one calls out the number one, and places one of his color tokens on the number one spot on the board.

Then players continue to roll one die trying to be the first to get the second number in the sequence.

This calling out of numbers and placing a color token continue until a token is placed on the spot numbered six.

From this point on players roll both dice from their hand attempting to roll the remaining four numbers (7,8,9 and 10).

When the last token is placed on the spot numbered 10, the game ends.

Scoring
Add up all the spots on the board in each players color tokens, highest score wins.

Perplexus Warp brings a whole new shape and challenge to Perplexus'!

Roll the ball to the staring point, then flip, twist and spin the ball along the path. Slide the WARP DRIVE to move along the rail. If you fall off, start over! If you make it to the end, enjoy your triumph - then try racing your friends!

This Perplexus includes 80 challenges including Warp drive, pivot, double track, basket and wicked stairs.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
Mix-Econo is an educational game designed to acquaint the players with the meaning and use of everyday economic terms such as money, wages, industry, finance, inflation, banking, savings, etc. It is essentially a game to teach the social science of Economics.

Mix-Econo associates the actions of people with the changing value of money. The cards used in the game represent different phases of the economy and are marked with a monetary value. The object of the game is for each player to choose proper cards so as to end up with an amount of money as close to stable point as possible. The winner is the person having the amount of money nearest (above or below) the set stabilized amount of $200,000.

Thumbs Up is a simulation game for camp management and recreation leadership training. By experiencing various simulated situations the players will gain skill in solving camp leadership problems. The game is intended to provide the student of camp management real-life situations to deal with and gives the experienced camp director a chance to be evaluated on how they are performing their responsibilities.

The players start on a space representing the first week of camp in June and proceed to the end of camp in August. In turn, players must respond aloud to various simulated camp challenges and their movement along the board will be determined by the number of "thumbs up" and "thumbs down" they receive from the other players for their answer. The first players who makes it to the end of camp (in August) wins the game.

The Krymenesian Charter was proclaimed. Thirty-five fearless sea monsters were given the task of protecting the wilderness of the Krymenesian Waters. For years and centuries no creature has had the courage to venture into Krymenesia — until now when the ocean's most feared animals, the orcas, decided to take it over.

In Charta Krymenesiae, you must turn the sea monsters into your allies, use their powers, take over territories, and close off one of the opponent's whales from the open sea to become the King of the Sea.

You can either challenge a single opponent to an amazing intellectual battle, or join a friend so that you can outmaneuver your opponent as a team and conquer the Waters of Krymenesia. No one to play with? The game includes 48 puzzles to offer you a solitaire challenge in which you have to explore territories that'll remind you of nautical symbols.

boardgame: The Castles of Burgundy: 5th Expansion – Pleasure Garden
description:
The 5th Expansion "Der Lustgarten" (Pleasure Garden) consists of 10 six-sided tiles:

- 9 buildings "Pleasure Garden" (beige, 2x with black backs)
- 1 "knowledge" (yellow) 4 VP per "Pleasure Garden"

During game setup the tiles are shuffled in their respective piles. The player who adds a "Pleasure Garden" to his estate, immediately carries out an action with the number of the white die (worker tiles may be used as usual).

The expansion was part of the limited print for the "Stadt Land Spielt"-Event on September 14th 2014 taking place all over Germany.

Village: Customers Expansion II is exactly what the name describes – another four additional customers that can be added to the Village base, inn and port game. Here's what those customers need in order to be served:


     Tile 1, The Beggar: Just pay 2 money and take the beggar. At the end of the game, you score 5 prestige points.
     Tile 2, only with Village Inn: Spend a beer. At the end of the game, you score 1 prestige point for every villager card that shows a burned down candle.
     Tile 3, only with Village Port: Sell a treasure chest you brought home but have not turned into money. At the end of the game, you score 7 prestige points.
     Tile 4: Spend 2 green influence cubes to score 3 prestige points at the end of the game.

Dino Bash is a short, but fun game first published in Roleplayer Independent (Volume 1, Issue 6 - May 1993).

The game is simple board game, designed to provide a little light relief between role-playing games scenarios.

The game is based in a world where Dinosaurs have never died out.  There are three fractions, The Carnivores, The Herbivores and The Mammals.

The game takes places in the bar; The Jurassic Arms (A3 map provided with counters) and two six sided dice are required (miniatures could be useful).

Each character has a simple profile which consists of three basic attributes; Wounds Points, Action Points and Combat Points, but also description on how they attack and any special attractions.

There are a number of special items counters which the characters can pick up around the bar to help them.

Each fraction has a background which gives the player what’s they needs to do in the game.

boardgame: Pixel Lincoln: The Deckbuilding Game – Expansion 2-1: Environments
description: In Pixel Lincoln: The Deckbuilding Game – Expansion 2-1: Environments, Pixel Lincoln is up for re-election and along with that comes all new environment cards that change the way that levels work.  See how wind effects Pixel Lincoln, pushing him back further and further.  Spikes are deadly and prevent players from jumping.  Warp zones allow players to swap levels. You will want to play these!

boardgame: Bulwark: Tsula of the Gold Clan
description:
From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Runestriking is an ancient and powerful magical technique, passed down from dwarf to dwarf. It's most powerful working are jealously guarded, and some secrets have been lost to the ages when no-one worthy was found to learn them. Tsula is known as one of the most powerful living runestrikers, and she chooses to put her art to use serving the good of everyone. Many trouble-makers have made the mistake of assuming that her skills don't extend to the battlefield.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Never disown a dwarf unless you want him and a couple of thousand of his mates knocking on your door with fire cannons.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

This recently-repopulated fortress in the deepest bowels of dwarf territory has formidable defences - but facing equally formidable armies are the stock-in-trade of heroes in Bulwark! With guards, secret tunnels and levers that pour hot lava into the ranks of the enemy, there are are lot of tools at your disposal. Its most valuable assets - the sequence of gates guarding the main causeway - would become its downfall should they be breached.

From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

What slithers in the lightless crevices far underground? These things do, these mockeries of life, these horrors. Fight them as they slip into inhabited territory, fading in and out of sight like smoke and shadows. Feel your very mind assaulted by their presence, even if no blade or magic strikes out at you. Beat them back, or even reason itself might be over-run.

From publisher description:

Go It Alone

You don't need friends to cause mayhem on the battlefield. If you like taking a lone Bulwark Hero into battle, then this pack has you covered. It includes three replacement card types for existing decks (Zsuzsa and Sandor from the base set plus the Tower of Secret Lore from Travails of the Arcane) that work better when there is just a single Hero fighting. It also includes 5 solo challenges, custom Foe decks with cards taken from all sets pre-Twisting Passages.

boardgame: Bulwark: Ahuli of the Tin Clan
description:
From publisher description:

Heroic Co-operative Deck-Building!

Ahuli is hard-working guardsdwarf from a minor Clan, with one hell of a reputation for controlled violence and shrugging off attacks that would kill feeble humans. Strap on his heavy armour, take up his warhammer and demonstrate exactly why dwarves shuld never be taken lightly.

boardgame: Moscow to Paris: Essen & Happy Train promo cards
description:
The Essen card adds the city of Essen. The card features Zollverein (yeah, it is THAT massive alien building you can see in Essen!)

The Happy Train adds a wildcard wagon - it can be played in place of any color.
This promo pack adds seven Cathedral location cards and one Spy card.

boardgame: World of Tanks: Rush – Promo Pack
description:
This promo pack includes 4 tank cards:

1. French - Poniatowski G1P
2. German - VK4502 (P)
3. USA - Tumbleweed Tank
4. USSR - Танк РАФ-НИК

City Council: The Disaster Deck consists of ten event cards that disrupt "normal" city life by causing havoc everywhere, and it introduces a new game mode, creating a fully co-operative experience for City Council players. In each round of play, the players change roles, taking crucial steps to prevent these events from tearing apart the growing city.

Play a soccer match. In The Beautiful Match, players have a hand of seven offensive and defensive cards (5/2,4/3,3/4,2/5 chosen at start of generic match) and two bonus cards. The players who controls the ball plays offensive cards which, combined with a die roll, give the result of action (move of the pawn on the pitch and possession). The defender can play defensive cards (challenge and closed down) which give a result with a die roll (die negative modifier for attack action, foul, take possession of the ball, etc.). When the pawn is near the goal you can shoot (roll dice; you read the result on a chart). All off soccer is here: substitutions, red cards, corners, free kicks,clearance,cross,...moreover the game could easily be modified with houserules. The rating of teams of the beautiful game could be used in the beautiful match.so you can use the beautiful game to play the majority of matchs of the season and the beautiful match to play derby or most intersting matchs.the number of cards is 7 in generic game but this number can be given by a conversion of the rating of the teams given in  the beautiful game.

Rock Paper Scissors meets Strategy.

Rules

1) Each player gets one set of Rock, Paper, Scissors (RPS) cards.

2) Deal 5 trick cards to each player.

3) Each player puts one of their RPS cards face-down in front of them as their active RPS card.

4) Players may not look at any active RPS cards (not even their own).

5) Each player picks a trick card to play and holds it up to indicate they are ready.

6) All players reveal their trick card simultaneously.

7)Resolve the trick cards from lowest number first to highest last.

8) When an RPS card is replaced, reveal the old card to all players. A card may never be replaced by itself.

9) Play trick cards this same way until all players have played 3 of their trick cards.

10) Each player reveals their RPS card, getting one point for each other RPS card they beat. Using the classic rock beats scissors, scissors beats paper and paper beats rock.

11) Play to 7 points.

These cards vary from allowing you to see your opponents unused RPS cards, to swapping yours to your opponents cards, to forcing your opponent to switch your card or even blocking moves.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Theme  -  The Scientists of your world has discovered a nice planet to colonize, Planet 313.  Your mission is to get your ships off of your launchpad, navigate throughout the rotating rings in the space around Planet 313, and manage a perfect landing on the planet.  Ok, that sounds easy enough… except your opponents have the same orders from their worlds.  Now it is a race and a battle to be the first one there with all of your ships.

Game Play & How to Win -  Players blast space ships from launchpads and send them racing through the rings of space around Planet 313.  Navigating and battling their way through the rings, they still need to make a perfect landing with all of their ships to claim victory.

How to make all of this happen--- Players will use 5 dice to roll on each turn. The dice will dictate how much movement the player's ship can travel, whether the rings of space get moved and which one, and finally one of the dice will give a special action that will enhance the turn with different features.

Players will also be armed with a hand of cards with special actions, modifiers for movement, modifiers for battle values, and even space ships.

The trip to planet 313 will be full of decisions for best navigation, how to win a battle you didn't want, hunting for the battles you do want, and eventful surprises along the way.
This expansion includes all the components needed to add a 5th player to the game.

Description from the Publisher

The struggle against the Ancient Ones spreads out of the museum and into the haunted city of Arkham in Elder Sign: Gates of Arkham. Adventures take place in over twenty locations including Velma's Diner and the Curiositie Shoppe, Arkham Asylum, and the Uninvited Isle. Meanwhile, gates to Other Worlds open throughout Arkham, destabilizing time and space.

In this expansion for Elder Sign, four new Ancient Ones threaten humanity and eight new investigators join the quest for elder signs. Investigators can also now acquire skills that give them lasting advantages. Memberships in either the brutal Sheldon Gang or the mysterious Silver Twilight Lodge help investigators complete tasks and grant them added rewards. It will take all the skills, resources, strength, and sanity you can muster to prevent the Ancient Ones from awakening – and to stay alive in the rough streets of Arkham.

Microbadges

 Elder Sign: Gates of Arkham fan
 Elder Sign: Gates of Arkham fan - yellow gate
 Elder Sign: Gates of Arkham fan - blue gate
 Elder Sign: Gates of Arkham fan - red gate

description:
Let the business guru in you run wild as you juggle career, relations, investments, and your private life. Life Manager gives you the opportunity to shape the game, devise a strategy, and make personal choices, while battling it out for happiness points. Have you got what it takes to manage like a pro?

The main goal in Life Manager is to have the highest number of happiness points at game's end. While playing, you first reflect on your personal priorities, then later take care of selected domains: career, relations, private life & finances.

This is a business-strategy board game designed for 2-8 players at the age of 10+.

Producer: Granna
Rules available upon the e-mail request
Full Game available in French, English, German & Polish

•••

Entdecken Sie den Business-Guru in sich, indem Sie im Kampf um Glückspunkte geschickt alles unter einen Hut bringen: Ihre Karriere, Ihre Beziehungen, Ihre Investitionen und Ihr Privatleben. Das Spiel "Life Manager" gibt Ihnen die einzigartige Möglichkeit, die Reise ihres Lebens selbst zu gestalten, ihre eigenen Strategien zu entwickeln und persönliche Entscheidungen zu treffen.
Haben Sie alle dafür notwendigen Profi-Qualitäten?

•••

Laissez le gourou du business qui est en vous agir en liberté en jonglant entre carrière, relations, investissements et vie privée. “Life Manger” vous donne l’opportunité de façonner le jeu, d’échafauder une stratégie et de faire des choix personnels tout en vous battant pour des points de bonheur.

Avez vous ce qui qu’il faut pour manager comme un pro ?

Jeu de stratégie dans le monde du business prévu pour 2 à 8 joueurs de 10 ans et plus.

description:
Introduction

Welcome to the Formula 1 championship of 1976.   James Hunt and Nikki Lauda are battling out to see who will be the champion for the year.  Re-create the drama and excitement of this terrific year of motor racing.

Using your skill in acceleration and braking, managing your tyres and fuel you will battle it out to see who wins the race.

This is an all skill game, with no luck and no dice.  You will rely entirely on your skill to out-finesse your opponents and come in first.

1976 Formula One is suitable for players aged 12 and upwards and can be played solo or with 2-8 players.

Components

Full colour race map

8 cars in colours

8 car control mats in matching colours

40 Markers

Setup

Each driver can allocate 12 points between Engines, Brakes and Tyres.  Throughout the race, drivers may use these to boost their performance.

Turn Sequence

The lead car moves first, the inside car moves before an outside car.   You have 5 seconds to move your car.

The car must move the number of squares indicated on the gear stick.

At the end of the turn the driver sets a new speed, normally plus or minus 1 the current speed.

The next player should already be making their move while you are doing this.

The driver may use one ENGINE point (reduce the marker on the car tile) to accelerate an additional 1 speed

The driver may use one BRAKE point (reduce the marker on the car tile) to decelerate an additional 1 speed

Handling Corners

If the car moves onto the tile too fast then the car crashes.

If the car moves onto the tile at too fast, the driver can use tyre points to stay on the road.

Wakanda: Three Little Coyotes adds three coyote tokens to the Wakanda base game, but these can be substituted by any tokens should you know the rules for how to use them.

At the start of play, place one coyote token on each of the three starting village tiles. At the end of each player's turn, if possible he must move a token from one unclaimed tile to another unclaimed tile. When a player takes a tile with one or more tokens on it, he places these tokens on the table before him. At the end of the game, in addition to their other scoring each player scores 0, 1, 4 or 9 points for having 0, 1, 2 or 3 coyote tokens.

Star Realms: Crisis – Bases & Battleships, an expansion pack for Star Realms, includes a powerful new ship for each faction, along with eight additional ships and bases to add even more variation to your trade deck.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Deck: The Long Dark
description:
Nightmare Decks for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game allow players to revisit their favorite scenarios cast in the shadows of the game's challenging Nightmare Mode. Featuring deadlier enemies, darker locations, and more terrifying surprises, each Nightmare Deck introduces new, alternate encounter cards and instructions for using them to reshape a scenario's encounter deck. The result? A nightmarish journey through territory players thought they knew, but with new twists and turns that will have them on the edges of their seats!

Each Nightmare Deck comes with twenty new encounter cards to add to the matching original quest, as well as a Nightmare Mode card that explains which older cards are removed from the original set-up and a card providing additional game rules.

Nightmare Decks first appeared in FFG's "Game Night Kits", which are available to retail outlets, and were later released as print-on-demand items.

More specifically, this expansion adds new Kingdom cards to the base game and new Story and Adventure decks of cards.

boardgame: Yahtzee: The Walking Dead Collector's Edition
description:
From the publisher:
Play America's all-time #1 dice game in post-apocalyptic Atlanta with the Walking Dead Collector's Edition of Yahtzee. Now with two ways to play you can roll for classic dice combinations or play Battle Yahtzee and try to be the last player remaining.

From the dust jacket:

David Parlett has produced the most original and complete collection of patience games for one player (and in some cases two) for nearly a century. In it he sets out to dispel the notion that patience is a therapeutic pastime for the simple-minded and the idle rich. Whereas previous compilations have tended to copy one another in jumbling good, bad, and indifferent games with no sense of order, the author has here adopted a systematic approach enabling the browser to pick out the type of game most appealing to his or her own taste and mood of the moment. Three major groups are distinguished. First come the attractive and simple games requiring care and patience to bring them out, then games requiring a balance of judgement and card sense and finally those which call for the type of positional analysis more usually associated with chess problems than card games. Each group is further subdivided in a way which generally proceeds from the small and simple to the large and complex.

Description from the publisher:

Nearly 75% of the U.S. population is infected with an airborne disease: the Z-B13 virus. About a quarter of these people are at Stage 3 of the infection, and the numbers of the "Afflicted" are growing by the day. Cities throughout the U.S. Midwest are emptying as healthier citizens flee for the coasts and roving gangs terrorize the highways. Rumors abound as to the cause — a madman with a chip on his shoulder, a military experiment gone wrong, and so on — but if there's any hope for turning back the tide on this illness, someone will need to find a cure. And the best bet for this cure is a small team of survivors unaffected by the virus, searching from city to city as they find and stop the Mastermind behind the Z-B13 virus.

The pulp-adventure cooperative game Zero Hour balances dice placement with a press-your-luck threat track and risk/reward decisions that add tension on every turn. Combined with an optimistic take on a well-loved gaming theme, Zero Hour flips the zombie genre into an epic quest to find the cure for the Z-B13 virus...all in about an hour.

Players are alchemists that collect elemons in order to make potions that grant Alchemy points. The first player that achieves 9 Alchemy points wins the game.

On each turn players will roll the die to activate one of the elemons on the board.

They will then get two action tokens that will allow them to draw elemon cards, play elemon cards, summon gigamons, activate gigamons, make potions and ultimately make the Philosopher's Stone.

Potions and Gigamons grant Alchemy points that will make you win.

Fish'n'Stones: Goldfish is a promotional card for Fish'n'Stones that is available at the Logis booth 3-F101 during Spiel 2014.

This fantastic golden fish, who is shuffled into the deck instead of an empty water card, transforms 1 negative stones card into positive points at the end of the game.

Orléans: Taverne & Lagerhaus (Tavern & Depot) is a promotional postcard featuring two additional place tiles for use in the Orléans base game.

Both Tavern and Depot tiles were later included in Orléans: Invasion expansion.

What is the work of bees? Pollinate the flowers, run from predators and take care of their hives. In this game, players have to move their bees across the board, to get to the flowers, pick up pollen and nectar, and return to their hive. With this win honey (victory points). To move, use the cardinal points, it should protect the trees and escape a spider that likes to steal nectar and pollen that the bees are carrying. Who will produce more honey at the end of the match?

Qual o trabalho das abelhas ? Polinizar as flores, fugir dos predadores e cuidar de sua Colmeia. Neste jogo, os jogadores terão que movimentar suas abelhas pelo tabuleiro, chegar até as flores, pegar néctar, pólen e voltar para sua Colmeia. Com isso ganharão Mel (Pontos de vitória). Para se movimentar, utilizarão os pontos cardeais, se protegerão nas árvores e deverá fugir de uma Aranha que gosta muito de roubar néctar e pólen que as abelhas estão transportando. Quem conseguirá produzir mais mel no final da partida ?

The players have to move their 4 cars from the "Start/Ziel" space to the turning cycle of their color and back again, while observing the traffic rules. The first player who gets this done, wins the game. Players who violate the traffic rules have to pay a fine, if they do this frequently, they have to play a penalty round with one car. Movement is determined by a die. A Special die determines some advantages, like bringing a new car into play, move the traffic cops or turn the traffic lights. 
On some spaces the players have to answer questions about the traffic rules.

Glyphs is a 4 player trick taking game based on Whist. It plays similarly to hearts, spades, and bridge, but with expanded play. Players will create a shape of cards, called a Reference Glyph, and then attempt to gain ownership of it by recreating the actual Glyph through trump style game play. One team must attempt to create the glyph of cards through play, while their opposing team tries to deny them points.

Glyphs is played in turns comprised of three phases. The first phase involves creating the Glyph of cards. The second phase is the conflict to build and control the glyph. The third phase is scoring for the Aggressor team.

boardgame: Battle over the Pacific: Japan vs. United States, 1941-1942
description:
Battle over the Pacific is a complete, stand alone design that takes the Battle over Britain game system to warplanes flown by Japan and the United States in 1941-42.   It is a two-player (with solitaire option) strategy game simulating plane-vs-plane dogfights occurring in the first year in the war in the Pacific after America's entry into the conflict.

Airplanes are individually rated for speed, agility, firepower, performance, and durability.  All planes are compatible with all other planes in the series, so you can fly Japanese planes against British adversaries if you have Battle over Britain, for instance.  Aircraft included are:  American: Grumman F4F Wildcat, Curtiss P-40 Kittyhawk, Brewster F2A Buffalo, Douglas SBD-3 Dauntless, and Douglas TBD-1 Devastator.  Japanese:  Mitsubishi A6M2 Zero, Makajima Ki-43 Oscar, Mitsubishi A5M4 Claude, Nakajima Ki-27 Nate, Aichi D3A Val, Nakajima B5N1 Kate, and B5N2 Kate.  Counters (3/4th inch square) are in color, on thick card, and must be cut apart prior to play.  A new Critical Hit Vulnerability rule has been included, to take into account the fragile nature of some of the early Japanese fighters.

First expansion pack for Platoon Leader. Includes three new scenarios and a new, winterized map. Act as the Americans or try your hand as a German commander. New rules allow for snipers, the Waffen SS, wounded leaders, smoke and an optional advanced panic rule! Comes with a play aid which was not included in the original Platoon Leader base game. Also includes new additional counters and a graphic orders/turn track. Platoon Leader is required to play Cold Combat.

boardgame: Warfare in the Age of Madness
description:
From the publisher:

Warfare in the Age of Madness is a game of company-level battles in a dark near future. Designed for 15mm figures, opposing forces fight to survive by scavenging supply caches from the battlefield, stripping enemy casualties of their gear, and raiding the enemy field camp. Features include easy to learn rules, small armies of just a few dozen 15mm figures (about 10-12 elements/stands) and a few vehicles, and a flexible force creation system. Create a completely custom force representing anything from a former regular rifle company (and now your personal war band!) to a hardened civilian militia from your hometown. The game uses only standard 6-sided dice and can be played on the average dining room or kitchen table.

The game is available in electronic and hard copy formats.  Players can use 'extra' 15mm figures they may have lying around or simply reuse miniatures they have for other games to create post apocalyptic warbands with minimal cash outlay.  Players build customized forces and are able to tweak stats, weapons loadouts, morale and other factors to both optimize and personalize their force.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – House of Talons
description:
Shadowcats prowled those passes, rock slides were common, and the mountain clans were lawless brigands, descending from the heights to rob and kill and melting away like snow whenever the knights rode out from the Vale in search of them. Even Jon Arryn, as great a lord as any the Eyrie had ever known, had always traveled in strength when he crossed the mountains.
   –George R.R. Martin, A Game of Thrones

Venture into the Vale of Arryn with House of Talons, the fifth Chapter Pack in the Wardens cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game. Among its sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards), you’ll find plenty of cards to support every Great House, including iconic characters like Bronze Yohn Royce, Jhogo, and Robert Arryn.

As you ascend the heights of the Eyrie, House of Talons also continues the major themes of the Wardens cycle, adding options for decks built around high-cost, recognizable characters. In addition, this Chapter Pack enhances decks build around key traits, ranging from Rainbow Guard to Dothraki to Sand Snake. It also introduces characters that can interact with new Castle plots, and presents new “limited response” abilities, making them a more meaningful part of your deck-building.

description:
“I meant ta do dat.”
   –Old Zogwort

Unleash the power of the Waaagh! in Zogwort’s Curse, the fourth War Pack in the Warlord cycle for Warhammer 40,000: Conquest. The Orks find a powerful new psyker to lead them in this War Pack, allowing you to spread a green tide of destruction across the planets of the Traxis sector. Alongside the vicious Orks, you'll find powerful new vehicles for the Astra Militarum, dark wargear for the servants of Nurgle, and hideous Dark Eldar torture events.

This War Pack contains sixty cards, including one warlord with its signature squad and three copies each of seventeen unique cards, giving you a full play set of every unit, support, event, and attachment. You can join the hunt with the Space Wolves, fly with an Eldar Vyper Squad, or pilot Tau heavy battlesuits. No matter which faction you support, you’ll find plenty of cards in Zogwort’s Curse.

description:
The birds are on the loose in Flatterspatzen, and you need to put the flock together!

Eight different birds fly on every playing card – four on each side — and players start with the same number of cards on the table before them. To start the game, one card is laid in the center of the table, then everyone tries to play out their cards at the same time starting from this single card, matching the birds as they go. Whoever first lays out all of her cards wins!

A dexterity game in a small footprint, where you push a card to the edge of the table and score based off of how many of the point locations are over the edge. Very simple and very addictive even when alone just trying to score higher and higher.


A one card dexterity game where players flick, push, or slide a card across a table to score points by having dots on the card hanging off the edge.

Differences between the first and second edition:

- Font, Colors, and Layout
- Inner circle of dots are worth two points


Second edition was also included in Button Shy's June 2016 Board Game of the Month Club $10+ package.

Colaville is the soft drink capital of the world where the soda fountains never run dry. Drug stores have popped up all over town, serving up endless varieties of tasty concoctions. In this “take that” style game, players take the role of a Soda Jerk at one of these drug stores where they will try to make as much money as they can by bringing in customers who want what they have, while sending soda and customers to other players to try and cost them money. Stealing customers and soda will help but a fire could strike at any time, forcing a player to start from scratch. The game ends when the draw pile is exhausted with the player who made the most money being declared the winner.

During the draw phase of each player's turn, players draw as many cards as there are players and play each one of them according to their rules. There are four types of cards:

Customers - Who are brought in by playing them in front of the player who drew them but they can sent to other player's drug stores instead by placing them in front of the other players.

Sodas - Like customers they can be played in front of the player who drew them or played in front of another player.

Soda Jerks - Are dealt before the game begins and gives each player a special ability that can be used once per game.

Events - A variety of events can hurt or help a player depending on which one is drawn.

Arthur Bine is a single figure with rules card for Krosmaster: Arena.

Arthur is an environmentalist of sorts, and produces his own energy through seawater and spins! However, he also emits a yucky gas through this process, which makes him not-so-popular...

He was first available to people who pre-ordered Krosmaster: Junior. Future availability is unknown.

The second that Malefix the Sorcerer goes away on a trip, his apprentices secretly explore his enormous book of magic spells! All of the pages are blank upon first glance, but soon enough the sorcerers put the pens to good use by starting to paint! The sorcerer's apprentices take turns guessing what is being painted. This is harder than it seems because the whole picture is covered up by bookmarks at the beginning, so the fastest and most imaginative apprentice will be the first one to guess the picture correctly.

In Zauberer Malefix, the players paint a picture on their easel where the other players can't see it, then they take turns putting their pictures in the magic book while the other players have to guess what they have painted. This is harder than it seems because the whole picture is at first covered up by bookmarks. The bookmarks are gradually unfolded according to how the die is rolled. More and more bits of the picture become visible depending on the roll of the die. The first player to guess the picture correctly receives points, and the less of the picture they can see, the more points they earn. The aim of the game is to be the first player to score the required number of points depending on the number of players.

Bug-eyed munching beetles, fluffy fuzzy caterpillars, and smooth grasshoppers scurry topsy-turvy because they still need to take the big leaves to the feast site before the creepy-crawly feast begins. Who wants to help these cute, crawly creatures?

In Kri-Kra-Krabbelei, the players have to find two matching creepy-crawlies under the leaves. If the players are successful, the crawly leaf trail moves forward toward the feast site and they receive a dewdrop as a reward. Whoever has a great sense of touch in their fingertips and a good memory will have collected the most dewdrops by the end of the game and be the winner.

A new Mini Boss miniature with card for Myth.

The Twilight Knight was offered through the Myth Kickstarter.

This is a set of Kickstarter Exclusive rewards for Myth. These were given out to backers at the "Captain Pledge Level", together with several non-Kickstarter exclusive expansions and the Myth: Twilight Knight Mini Boss. The latter was only given out once per backer, backers could get these Kickstarter Exclusive Rewards multiple times.

This set includes:

    Trickster Hero
    Torchbearer Follower
    Donnchadh Agent
    Connor Mini Boss
    Razorfiend Mini Boss
    Keesi & Og Mini Boss
    10 new Story Quests inside a small booklet with a small accompanying token sheet.
A small deck of cards containing new quests for Myth. These cards get shuffled into the existing deck.

description: A small deck of cards containing new quests for Myth. These cards get shuffled into the existing deck.

What a mess! All of the cats in the area went on an exciting mouse hunt together, but now they want to return home. Both the cat parents and kittens are having a difficult time finding each other amidst the chaos! Luckily the police cat is around to keep order and protect the cats that have already found each other.

In Katzenpolizei, the players collect the cats! If the die lands on the relevant symbol, then the players take a card and lay it down in front of them on the table. However, sometimes a cheeky mouse or the naughty neighbor's dog throws a wrench in the works and their cats run away. The players' cats are safe only when the whole family is reunited OR when they enjoy the protection provided by the police cat. The aim of the game is to match up as many cats as possible.
A small deck of cards containing new treasures for Myth. These cards get shuffled into the existing deck.

In the king's court, more tall tales are being told than players can shake a stick at! The cat cook sometimes likes to pretend to be a princess pig, and it's not uncommon to hear the frog-legs minstrel crow like a knight rooster. The only thing players can be sure of is that the animals are not always who they pretend to be! If players want to go far in the king's court, they have to know a story when they hear one and be able to keep a straight face when telling one of their own!

With animal cards in their hand in Flunkerkönig, players lay them face down in turns, mimicking their sounds as often as the number of the animal appearing on the card.

But careful! A players' card must show a greater number of the same animal played, or at least one animal of a higher-ranking animal. If they don't have the right card in their hand, they have to keep a straight face while pretending they do! Whoever has the fewest penalty points at the end of the game wins.

Ferdi Fuchs tests the knowledge of all the children! It is important to answer exciting, somewhat tricky, but also fun questions: When can you see a rainbow? Which worms can fly? And what are French ​​fries made from?

In Quizzomat Junior, players try to answer these questions by choosing one of the possible answers, then placing their token in the proper hole on the board. Someone then spins the Quizzomat wheel and tokens placed in the wrong holes disappear into the box. Only tokens left in hand count in the end, and whoever has the most tokens wins!

In Arlequin, each player in turn, draw 2 to 5 cards and announces a combination of cards to his neighbor who accepts or checks this combination. If he accepts, he must himself annouces a greater combination of cards. If he checks, and the annonced combination is a bluff, the player how bluff will loses points,... but if that was not a bluff the player who check loses points instead.

description:
Who wants to make some music with Sammy the squirrel? Kleine Musik-Macher, part of HABA's "My Very First Games" line, includes eight different games with simple rules that don't take long to play: Enjoy games of listening to bells, music memory, a musical dice race, games that require you to listen carefully, and cooperative games, as well as games with physical activity. The variety of game ideas, the four handbells, and the wonderfully illustrated game materials ensure hours of entertainment.

Music fosters linguistic and intellectual aptitude, as well as motor skills in children. In games and activities designed specifically for them, children are given the opportunity to experiment with sounds, beats and rhythms, and acquire essential skills while playing.

In 2013, Heartland Consumer Products, the makers of the patented Square Shooters Dice, decided to run a contest to find a new game featuring their signature product. There were dozens of entries and the judges whittled them down over three rounds to find the game that best leveraged the dice in a fun new way. That winner was 9-Shooter Quick Draw by Ryan Strong.

As you might expect with a game about a gunfight in the dusty streets of an old west town, the rules for 9-Shooter Quick Draw (9SQD) are dead-simple.

Each player gets 3 gunslingers, 3 action cards, and 1 set of Square Shooters® dice.

Both players reveal a gunslinger and face it so their opponent can see the poker hand printed on the card.

Both players pick up their dice and shake them in their fists until one player shouts "DRAW!" They both roll their dice and the gunfight begins.

You're trying to roll the poker hand on your opponent's gunslinger card and you can lock in any of your matching dice at any time, but after that first big roll, you can only re-roll unlocked dice that have...

The same number (Aces, 4's, Kings, etc...)

The same suit (Hearts, Clubs, etc...)

If you have no matching dice, you can only re-roll a single die of your choice.

If your opponent catches you rolling mismatched dice, you have to stop, give him a hard look, and re-roll a single random die to re-set.

BTW, Jokers are wild so they can be any number and suit you like.

That's it. You both keep rolling as fast as you can until one of you locks in all of the dice to match the other's gunslinger card. That player shouts "BANG!" and your gunslinger is dead! Lose all three of your gunslingers and the town's undertaker is gonna be one busy man.

Hey, quiet down, I hear ya. What about those action cards? Well, if you think you're ready for them I'll clue you in, but these cards are for the experts. It ain't easy dividing your attention between rollin' dice and readin' cards.

Each action card says when it can be used and what the effect is when it's played. You flip out the card and call out the name, then your opponent has to stop everything and do what the card says. That might mean waiting for you to get in some extra rollin', or maybe having to unlock one die.  Ouch!

Some of the cards are there to help you, too, like letting you re-roll mismatched dice or canceling the card your opponent just played on you.

They don't call it the Wild West for nuthin'!

boardgame: Clifford the Big Red Dog "You're Invited!" Game
description:
It's Clifford's beach party, and You're Invited! In this fun game, each player gets to be Clifford, racing around the board inviting their friends to a beach party. Drop the die down the dog house slide and see how far you'll go! The first player to pass out a complete set of invitations and make it back to the party wins!

Includes a colorful board, four plastic Clifford figures, dog house, die, and rules.

Designed for 2, 3, or 4 players age three and older.
family: Books: Clifford the Big Red Dog

description:
That inevitable zombie apocalypse has finally come, and you find yourself holed up the city with a ragtag group of survivors. You've heard rumors of a safe house out in the countryside, but you need to find a vehicle capable of getting you there and chances are not all of you will make it. Work with the other members of your group to survive and collect vehicle components, but when the time comes you may have to make a choice: do you run away and leave them for dead, or do you stay true to your word and risk being betrayed yourself?

Survivors is a semi-cooperative card game in which players search for vehicles they can use to escape while dodging ravenous zombie hordes. On each round, players are secretly dealt 2 zombie cards each and must choose the best action card in their hand to deal with them. This is where you are faced with an interesting choice: do you help the other players and risk betrayal, or do you betray them yourself and leave them to die?

At the end of each round you will get to search the city for items that help you survive and escape, but even if you find a way out, you may not be able to take everyone with you...

UNO: Disney Princess is the standard game with of UNO with a special wild card and depictions of Disney Princesses on the cards.

The new special wild card is called a Curse card.  When the curse card is played all other players are under a spell.  The player who played the card calls out a color.  Then all other players must take turns drawing cards and adding them to their hands until the color that was called has been drawn.  Then the player who played the curse card calls a new color to be played (the same way a standard Wild card would be played).

Another UNO version named UNO: Disney Princess was released in 2004. This version contained a Dragon special card.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

boardgame: Das Labyrinth des Pharao: Sphinx und Triamide
description: Das Labyrinth des Pharao: Sphinx und Triamide consists of five new double-sided player boards for use with the Das Labyrinth des Pharao base game.

An expansion for Crimebox Investigation which contains new cards. But these cards are talking about strange things: zombies, sprits, ghosts... Investigators  can enter in a paranormal universe in order to explain their stories.

"It's fourth and one, your opponent thinks you will run a Fullback Dive... But, you throw the bomb instead... It's a TOUCHDOWN!!!"

"The Game of Football" is a board game for playing American Football. The game can be played by one or two players on each team. It is a stats-type game, and on each turn players call plays and defenses, then roll dice and consult charts to determine the result of each play.

boardgame: 27th Passenger: A Hunt on Rails – Spiel 2014 Promo Pack
description:
The mini-expansion 27th Passenger: A Hunt on Rails – Spiel 2014 Promo Pack consists of a set of three new event cards.

The new event cards are:
Fool Me Twice (green)
Wild Mood Tonight (yellow)
Finish The Job (red)

Rails of Kered is a card game in which the players battle for railway supremacy! Collect engines, build railway lines, and run the rainbow express to gain points, but be careful! Your opponents will send breakdown trains and steal your engines to stop you from succeeding. Defend your railway lines, and steam your way to victory on the Isle. Get the most points and become the Head Station master of the Isle!

NEW Instructions, NEW lightened artwork, NEW Tokens! Just released April 2016!

• Game design by Heather Michel - https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/rails-of-kered
• Art by Rebecca Norman - http://rnormanmt.com
• Special Thanks to Derek and Miriam Michel, for game development and box text and Douglas Michel for Instructions write up.

Rails of Kered © 2014 Michel Home Publishing

Website: https://heather7086.wixsite.com/railsofkered

Metallum: Extractor Attack, a promotional card for Metallum, is a new Action module that allows a player to remove an opponent's Extractor from a planet, but then providing him no $ during the current scoring.

description:
This promo card was created for Dragon*Con 2013 and was distributed to players who demoed Havok & Hijinks.

Ready Side:

Play: Your dragon gains an affinity of your choice
Crash: If an event with an affinity is flipped, your dragon gains that affinity and you may resolve the event for your dragon (the original dragon resolves it after)

Tired Side:

Crash: If an event with an affinity is flipped, your dragon gains that affinity and you may resolve the event for your dragon (the original dragon resolves it after)

boardgame: Havok & Hijinks: Gen Con Promo Card
description:

Play: Your dragon gains an affinity of your choice

Murder Party à Manhattan is a crime investigation boardgame for 1 to 16 players.

The players are plying either alone or in teams. Each team stands for an official police or law enforcement department : NYPD, NY State Police, FBI, NSA.

The game is turn based. It plays on a map of Manhattan. At your turn, chose where you want to move your pawn to, following the subway lines available. Each move costs points. Depending on where you stop, you open the enveloppe related to this point, that is for example the interrogation of a witness or suspect, or other information that you get in order to solve a case.

At your turn, you can also stop the game instead of moving. In this case, all teams must write down their conclusions, and then open the "Solution" enveloppe.

For each good answer, you get points. For each false anwser, you lose points. If you stopped the game, you get a bonus. If you used the most transportation points, you get a malus.

The player or team with the most points wins the game!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
You battle villains to protect the geocaches that Dr. Apephace so desperately wants to destroy. The clock is ticking!

THE PREMISE

Zzzzzzzzz...  “Captain Cache… this is your old friend Dr. Apephace! I am broadcasting this message over a frequency that only you can hear. Since you won’t let me have my fun, I have decided to take away your fun! I have built a device, a Cache-Crusher, which when operational, will destroy every unprotected Geocache in the world. I have released my Thuggles and their teleportation pods to prevent you from rescuing caches. Well, Captain, Cache me if you Can... HAHAHAHAHAH!"  .... ZZZZZZZZ

Captain Cache is the world’s greatest superhero. His origin story is a safely guarded secret… well actually he just found a radioactive cache which gave him super geocaching abilities. With Dr. Apephace on the loose, Captain Cache has asked his SUPERHERO FRIENDS TO UNITE AND COLLECT ALL OF THE CACHES TO FOIL THE MAD DOCTOR'S SCHEME!

HOW IT PLAYS

In addition to the base game set up, 6 teleportation pods are placed on the board. Each player will be given a superhero character to play. Every time a geocache is take, a villain appears on a teleportation pod. Villains move about the board and start to destroy geocaches until they are successfully captured by a superhero. The straightforward combat system adds strategy to capturing villains and some superheroes are more well-suited to capturing some villains. It will take team work to win because once 10 caches are destroyed the evil Dr. wins and the superheroes lose!

A supplement for Pulp Alley miniatures rules. 
From the website:
This thrilling Pulp Alley expansion provides rules for BIG guns, strange gadgets, riding mounts, vehicles, custom modifications, and much, much more. From dog-sleds to flying characters, and from airplanes to colossal walking robots, our Pulp Gadgets, Guns, and Vehicles book makes it fast and easy to add even more excitement to your Pulp Alley scenarios.

boardgame: Myth: Etrus the Last Mini Boss
description:
Etrus the Last is a new Minotaur mini boss for Myth.

He comes with a miniature and a stat card.
"Urulok the Young", The Fire Drake Boss comes with a large miniature and a deck of new darkness cards for use with Myth.

boardgame: Myth: The Rat King Expansion Boss
description:
From the publisher's webpage:

Few have spoken of the monstrous abomination known as The Rat King. It is said his body is a teeming mass of diseased tailless continuously swarming over his monstrous body, protecting him. This protection is an illusion though as the Rat King is a powerful assassin able to destroy those foolish enough to break apart the swarm. Born of the shadows, the Rat King is a master in the art of becoming invisible and seeing those who dare hide in the dark. Those who face the Rat King should expect to also face an endless swarm of Tailless as they rush to their King’s aid.
"Syclopt", The Cyclops Boss comes with a large miniature and a deck of new darkness cards for use with Myth.
IA, The Foundation, The Elemental Boss comes with a large miniature and a deck of new darkness cards for use with Myth.

From Manufacturers Website:

Roll the symbol die and find the matching puzzle card. Find the correct one and attach it to your picture card. The cards are self-correcting! The first player to place all of the correct puzzle cards onto their picture wins the game. Trains fundamental skills in symbol association, number recognition and counting.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring Deck-Building Game – Merry & Pippin Promos

- Second Breakfast: 10th card for starter deck for a player with 'Merry & Pippin' as their hero. Card text: "Maneuver / If you have already bought or gained a card this turn, +3 Power. Otherwise, +1 Power."

description:
An over-sized hero card for The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey deck-building game allowing players to play as the wizard Radagast, available through participating retailers. 
 Radagast's ability is: "Wizard Hero / You may pay 4 Power. If you do, gain the top card of the main deck."

boardgame: Damn You Autocorrect: The Board Game
description: Damn You Auto Correct is the game of text message madness. Players have 30 seconds to guess what the mixed up message on the card REALLY means. You won't stop laughing until someone reaches the finish line.

Baconmania is in full swing. The average American eats 18 pounds of bacon per year. Yep, and Americans consume 1.7 billion pounds a year. Whoa!?talk about bringing home the bacon! Some folks appreciate bacon so much they accessorize with bacon earrings or bacon tattoos. Skin yourself up and you can apply a bacon bandage. Tired of that new car smell? Get one of those bacon strip car deodorizers. Bacon is not just for breakfast anymore! Buy your favorite bacon delicacies then collect Pounds of Pork and trade them in for Smokehouses and up the rent. It's all lip smackin' good fun until someone gets sent to BURNT and is out of the game. Who knows? You may soon be praising the Lard and wrapping everything in bacon. Whether you like yours with eggs, on a BLT, or on a stick and covered in chocolate, this is the game for you!
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
A member of the Checkers Family also known as "American Pool Checkers"

Very popular in the south of the United States, particularly with the African American community, this game differs from Checkers due to its "flying kings" and men capturing backwards (while still moving only forward).

The most similar game in the family is Brazilian Checkers from which Pool Checkers differs only in the capture rule (maximum number of captures is not compulsory).

Dragoneye Tavern is a casual game set in the Fantasy world of Askrias for 2~4 players, and of all ages. Players play a role of an adventurer, enjoying the night in the taverns after their adventures, but of course drinking and eating everyday is not the only thing they do! So, grab a seat and experience one of the favorite game among the adventurers in Dragoneye Tavern and the whole of Askrias.

Players will take control of a Character, and equip themselves with Shard abilities from the ability decks, and play in a fantasy version of Poker Dice with other players! The game comes with 20 custom dice and 72 game cards.

Each player's main goal is to earn the most shards, but this is no ordinary game, everyone knows that, dirty tricks are always used when played in a Tavern! During the game each player will be able to use their tricks to disrupt other players from getting the best hands, but at the same time they will be doing the same to you! So, never play fair, and use your wits to be the best!

In Battle of the Beetles green and red beetles fight among themselves. Your goal is to be the last player making a valid action. In order to play Battle of the Beetles you need a modified 7 x 7 board with 44 cells, 28 white rings (“eggshells”) and 28 rounder counters (14 green and 14 red “beetles”)

In each turn a player must perform one of the following actions:


    Remove the eggshell from one of your eggs. The removed eggshell is out of the game.
    Capture an enemy beetle. A born beetle makes a capture in two steps: First it moves forward to a cell adjacent to it. Then it flies to a cell occupied by an enemy beetle which removed from the game. The beetle flies exactly as a knight moves in chess. 
    Capture a friendly beetle according to the rules mentioned above. This action can only be carried out if the player is not able to perform the aforementioned actions.


If a player cannot make any of these actions in his/her turn, he/she loses.

iXit was published in issue #109 of the gamers' magazine FAIRPLAY.

Players throws pairs of dice and compose pairs of each one black and one white die to determine where to "iX" fields by marking them with a cross.

By this, regions of adjacent fields are built. In the end, players compare their second largest area. Whoever has the largest of these wins the game.

"BIDittle [bi-DIT-tle] players are dealt 7 cards per hand.  Using a simple bidding language based on high card points, partners work together to bid on the number of tricks the team intends to win and identify which suit or notrump the game will be played.  The game board corners include bidding prompts to help the new player bid more easily.  Once the final bid has been defined, the dummy hand is introduced, and the play of the hand is just like Bridge.  The seven-card hands are easy to hold and help make the game move quickly." [description from website]

BIDittle is actually 3 games in one box, each of which may be played entirely on its own:

"little BIDittle" is a very simple game to introduce trick-taking and trump suits to youngsters and newcomers to card playing. It is recommended for ages 7 and up.

"BIDittle" is the main game, as summarized above.

"The bridge to Bridge" is a more advanced game that deals full hands of 13 cards to each player and uses bidding that is much closer to Bridge itself, though still being a simplified version of Bridge.

The game contains a 2-sided board that has bidding guidelines and a track to show the bidding progress with colored markers. One side of the board is used for "little BIDittle" and BIDittle itself, while the other side of the board is used for "The bridge to Bridge".

The Kaleidos: 2014 Expansion, which is available only at the 2014 Spiel and Lucca Comics & Games fairs, includes two new pictures for the "Cocktail Games/Ystari" edition of Kaleidos. The images each depict a scene packed (to greater and lesser extents) with various objects.

In Kaleidos, each of up to four players or teams uses identical scenes and a deck of cards provides a letter for the round. Everyone gets sixty seconds to write down as many things beginning with that letter as they can find in the scene. Once time has expired, any object on multiple lists scores one point for those who wrote it. Any item unique to a single list scores three points.

boardgame: The Thing: Man Is the Warmest Place To Hide
description:
In the winter of 1982, a twelve-man research team at a remote Antarctic outpost discover an alien buried in the snow for over 100,000 years.
Soon unfrozen, the form-changing alien wreaks almighty havoc.

This set of 54 cards is the second expansion for The Thing, and offers two new Tasks for the outpost crew to work upon. The Thing player also gains four 'attacking Thing' cards to harm the team with, and provide additional nasty surprises. Numerous new weapon and skill cards to confront the chilling, inhuman threat are included.
A clutch of new cards also aim to subtly tip off the human players as to "who's human, and who's a thing," as well as force the El Capitan and others to make further unpleasant decisions.

This expansion contains no new rules or decks of cards. All of the cards fit into existing decks.

Please note: This game is strictly for adults - with profanity and horrific imagery on many of the cards.

The Think Again: Promo Cards is a small promotional deck for Think Again!.  It gives you just enough cards to get a taste for the game, but the questions are also completely distinct from the base game, so this can serve as an expansion, as well.

The town of Piggsburgh is holding their annual Bacon Buddy Cook-Off, and they are looking for this year's winner. Chefs, cooks, and other weekend warriors have come from far and wide to compete and enjoy in the festivities. Do you have what it takes to battle through the odds and win this year's top prize? If you love bacon as much as we do, then heed our call. Enjoy the sights, enjoy the sounds, but most importantly, enjoy the sweet savory samples of bacon delicacies throughout the town of Piggsburgh.

Players will move along the game board collecting ingredients, toll passes and recipes needed to win the Bacon Buddy Cook-Off. Fending off Vegan assaults, players must collect 4 recipes (and their ingredients) and get them back to their kitchen. The first player to acquire 4 recipe tokens and return to their kitchen is the winner.

Drunk Ass is the classic trivia drinking game that will keep you laughing and drinking all night long! Drunk Ass tests your wits before, during and after you chug your favorite beverage. All you need to play are glasses, some liquids to imbibe in and a few friends. 
Players (or teams) compete in these six party-enhancing categories: Drunk Ass Quotes, Drunk Ass Drinks, Drunk Ass Sobriety Test, Drunk Ass Trivia, Drunk Ass Jokes and Drunk Ass Luck.
Each card uses a series of clues leading to a common answer. Every player may have known it, but the ones that didn't get it will be saying, "I knew that!"
Players take turns rolling a die, drawing the corresponding card and gaining points for each correct answer and in the end, whoever has the most points wins!

My Little Pony Connect 4 is Fun for Every pony! Ponies go "mane to mane" when players race to drop their custom checkers in the grid to try and make a line upwards, sideways or diagonal. The first player to get 4-in-a-row wins! Check out the fun with 3 ways to play! Players can play original Connect 4 and also play by popping out their opponent's checkers to get 4-in-a-row or start with a grid full of checkers and pop out 10 checkers for the win.

boardgame: Galaxy of Trian: The Beginning of Conquest
description:
The Beginning of Conquest expansion introduces two new ship classes to Galaxy of Trian: the Corvette and Rogue. These new ships allow players to steal materials and even to block the expansion of other player’s planetary systems.

The expansion adds 12 two-sided galaxy tiles, 12 Corvette ship tokens (3 in each color), 8 rogue ship tokens (2 in each color) and four modules.

boardgame: Galaxy of Trian: Wealth of the Ancients
description: In the Wealth of the Ancients expansion, you will get 12 beautifully illustrated, two-sided tiles and 12 large crystals. The rulebook appendix will introduce a number of new rules and mechanisms based on the large crystals.

boardgame: Adowa: End of an African Dream
description:
Adowa was the first big defeat of an European power in Africa. Ethiopian Emperor Menelik II managed to defeat an Italian army through a combination of bravery, deficiencies in the Italian chain of command, and well exploited chances during the battle.The Italian Army fielded Italian infantry battalions, veteran Ascari battalions, and less experienced native units like Oculè-Cusai bands and mobile militia.

The Ethiopian Army was made of provincial armies made up of feudal militia and led by a Ras (Warlord).

This battalion level game comes with:

    a map, 216 counters, player aid cards and rules booklet
    map, counters and setup rules for Dogali, a scenario for the Ascari game previously published by ACIES.


Will you be able to continue the Italian African Dream by managing your Generals or will you suffer an utter defest? Will you win the day thanks to St George or loose the throne and the Empire?
