boardgame: Trick or Treat Halloween PnP Boardgame
description:
Players are kids trick-or-treating their way through the neighborhood, performing actions based on dice results and exchanging their bounty for VP cards giving them additional abilities towards being the most successful candy collectors of the evening.

Playable with 2-8 players and easily understandable rules (playtested with age ranging from 9 to 75) this game was created to fill a void of Halloween themed games in my collection that I don't want to keep to myself since it was well received by friends and family.

Available for download here on BGG in English and German.

Happy Halloween!

Update: Thanks to Citie (user e0004252) the rules are now also available in Chinese.

Players have 100 life points. They loose points fighting with their cards making attacks, defenses and combos. The goal is to survive the fight combat.

Feel the excitement of combat in your hands

FightQard is your new entertainment that brings all the excitement, strategy, strength and surprise of the fighting to a world of cards that you can play anywhere.

    Meet our complete and easy gameplay.
    Ideal for those who want maximum enjoyment without complicated rules or spend the inheritance of his grandmother.
    And for the advanced player that like an elaborate strategy of combat.
    Generates powerful combos that can turn the balance of combat in an instant.
    Aggressiveoffensive or passivedefensive? your strategy prepared by the stances.
    Unblockable hits, counterattacks, fight flash and more.

boardgame: Chaos & Alchemy: Magnum Opus Kickstarter Promo Card
description:
A promo card for those who backed Chaos & Alchemy during the Kickstarter campaign.

It states:
When Magnum Opus comes into your laboratory, you must discard all card s in your hand and destroy one of your Innovations. If Magnum Opus is your only Innovation, you must destroy it.

Dino Match:
Try to build one of 5 types of dinosaurs from a hand of three cards and from draw pile or receiving a discarded card. first player to complete a dinosaur wins.

Dino Rummy:
With a dealt hand of 6 cards, players in turn try to complete 2 dinosaurs by drawing from draw or discard piles. first player to complete 2 dinosaurs wins.

Or try your creativity mixing various head, body and tail cards into new mysterious dinosaurs.

Tic Tac Stack is "a clever version of the classic game" of Tic Tac Toe. It adds a dexterity element, more players (up to 4) and the ability to change what is in a box on the board. From the box: "To play: Drop your colored chips one at a time down your ramp onto the game tray. Take careful aim and send your chip rolling, bouncing and jumping onto the play board. Your ramp swivels back and forth to help you aim, but it takes a little luck to get it to land where you want it to! As chips are played they stack on top of each other so you might get covered up on any turn." First player to get three in a row of their color wins.

Trivia game about life in PRL (Polska Republika Ludowa, People's Republic of Poland).  The title means "People's Board Pastime".

Players in turn move their pawns by a number of spaces rolled on a single 6-sided die, and follow the instructions printed on the space on which they land.  Most of the spaces have players drawing cards and answering questions in one of four categories; correct answers are rewarded with game banknotes (representing Polish money from before the redenomination in 1990s).

Other spaces may provide players with money or trigger an auction of scarce commodities (represented by tokens).  When an auction is triggered by a player drawing a card or landing on a certain space, everyone, starting with the player on whose turn the auction occurred, gets to bid for the commodity, to increase their previous bid, or to pass.  Whoever offers the most gets the auctioned item.

The game ends when all 25 scarce commodity tokens are sold out.  The player who has the most of them is the winner.  In case of a tie, the victory goes to the richest tied player.

Game can be expanded by an official mobile app with extra questions and digital accessories, available for Android and iOS.

Special Ops Issue #5 includes

ASL scenarios:

     O9: Behind In The Count
     O10: City On The Edge


ASLSK scenarios:

     S56: In Pursuit Of The French
     S57: Haase To Hold On


ASL Map Board 68

In addition, the journal contains the complete game The Battle of Bushy Run and articles covering ASL, Angola!, Lincoln's War, Storm over Dien Bien Phu, Last Chance for Victory, and It Never Snows.
family: Series: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit

description:
In Skydice we are two architects who are going to build a big city. Each arquitect must erect skyscrapers and improve the city skyline by making them bigger, but we must also try to improve our opponent's buildings in order to claim them as our own. To determine who owns each piece, some parts of the skyscraper will have the lights turned on, while the others will be turned off.

To achieve this, arquitects must stack dice vertically and horizontally in a fashion that will resemble, at the end of the game, a beautiful skyline with lights turned on and off, like a New York City night postcard.

The goal is to score more points than the adversary. The final score will depend on the number of skyscrapers we own, their height and the value of the dice. The top dice in each skyscraper is the one which determines who owns the building.

This game is designed for 2 players (or 4 players playing with 2 teams). Each player must roll 6 dice from their supply and one action dice. The latter indicates which action to play - start a new skyscraper, improve an existent one or demolish, and from the six-dice-roll the player must use only dice with the same value. There is one restriction, though, there cannot be two skyscrapers with either the same height or the same value next to each other.

Occasionally some special modifiers - King Kong, Godzilla, Hospital... - can be used to protect or increase the value of the skyscrapers.

Rules incorporate a solo variant for 1 player and another one for 3 players.



En Skydice somos dos arquitectos que conjuntamente vamos a levantar una ciudad, erigiendo nuestros edificios y mejorando en altura tanto nuestros edificios como los de nuestro rival (que pasarán a ser nuestros). Los trozos de edificio de un jugador se distinguirán por tener las ventanas apagadas, mientras que los del otro por tener las ventanas encendidas.Apilando los dados iremos formando edificios de distinta altura, de forma que el conjunto de edificios formará un vistoso skyline (conjunto de rascacielos) con sus ventanas apagadas y encendidas como si fuera una postal nocturna de Nueva York.

El objetivo del juego es conseguir el mayor número de puntos. Para ello, se tendrá en cuenta el número de edificios de nuestra propiedad, la altura de los mismos y el valor de los dados de esos edificios. El último dado (dado superior) de cada edificio es el que determina el propietario del mismo (jugador con ventanas encendidas o jugador con ventanas apagadas).

El juego está pensado para 2 jugadores (o 4 jugando en 2 equipos) Cada jugador tira 6 dados de su reserva y el dado de acción, realizando la acción indicada en éste (construir, mejorar o demoler), para ello deberá utilizar todos los dados de un mismo valor, a su elección, obtenidos en la tirada. También se podrán utilizar en ocasiones modificadores especiales como King Kong, Godzilla, Hospital... que protegerán o darán más puntos a los edificios.

Las reglas del juego incorporan una variante en solitario para 1 jugador y otra para 3 jugadores.
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Imperial-Orion is a print and play derivative of Imperial 2030 by Mac Gerdts.

It  takes the basic premise of Imperial, that the players are amoral financiers and twists it so that players are shadowy 'powers' in a grand space opera. The game takes its name from the Orion Nebula which is used as the backdrop to the central (neutral) region of human (known) space.

At some time in the future humans have expanded among the stars. In the more densely populated areas colonies are connected by wormhole networks whilst vast fleets span the trackless expanses of the void. Contact has been made with a number of alien races around the periphery of human space but for the most part this has been peaceful and limited to scientific missions and the exchange of ambassadors. As a generalisation humanity is content, inward looking and exploited - by you, the players of the game.

This game is played on a board composed of 7 hexagonal tiles, comprising 6 regional powers and a central neutral zone that may have different configurations depending on the episode of galactic 'history' the players choose to replay. Additional part-tiles are arranged around the edge of the board to make the playing area into a regular hexagon. These part-tiles are of 2 different types and can have a significant effect on the topology of the game board.

In Imperial-Orion the players do not take turns, the six regional powers do, in an order that is determined randomly at the start of each game. The players are shadowy organisations who exercise power through unspecified means to further their own ends. By gaining control of the population of the regional powers,
the players influence the politics. The player with more control over a region's population gains control of that power's government and decides what the it will do. As control of a government can change with each new surge of controlled population, players may be able to manipulate several governments at the same time. As investors, players should not get too attached to a particular regional power, but rather focus on where their 'investments' have the best rates of return. Essentially the game is about power, in all its form: economic, cultural, military, biological…

Zooloretto: Langer Ludwig is a single tile expansion for Zooloretto. It was published (together with a few small expansions for other games) in the 20 Jahre Darmstadt Spielt to celebrate the 20th anniversary of Spielekreis Darmstadt, the Darmstadt boardgame club.

Use the normal rules for Zooloretto, except for one rule: the first player to fill his 6-space enclosure takes the Langer Ludwig enclosure from the middle of the table.  Once per game the owner of the enclosure can take up to 3 animal tiles from an enclosure and place them back in the draw pile (in any order). For each tile he placed back, the player receives 2 coins from the bank.

boardgame: The Seven Years War: Frederick's Gamble
description:
The Seven Years War: Frederick's Gamble (7YW:FG) is a wargame based on the innovative and still ever popular card driven game engine The Napoleonic Wars (TNW). If you’re familiar with TNW or its successor games, Wellington and/or Kutuzov; you’ll have little difficulty getting into enjoying this game.

The game’s title stems from Frederick the Great, King of Prussia’s 1756 land grab of the independent central European State of Saxony, right on his arch-rival Imperial Austria’s doorstep. Frederick “gambled” he could get away with this annexation without triggering a general European War. He failed, and when Austria was joined by its Ally France (Imperial Camp) and then Prussia's Ally Britain (Coalition Camp) entered the fray with its mainland interests in  Hanover/Hesse, that European War expanded into one of history’s first Global Conflicts.

To reflect the global nature of this conflagration, 7YW:FG has “mini-maps” off to the side of the main European theatre: one for North America and two areas of the Indian sub-continent. These maps use a point-to-point map of “Duchies” similar to TNW. An abstract “Naval Control Track” accommodates the war on the waters so players can concentrate on the action ashore.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
(from the introduction:)

The Med is a simulation of the campaign in the Eastern Mediterranean during the years between 1940 and 1943. It is a complex simulation of the same order as FITE/SE. But with experience, an extensive Europa library and this package, you can simulate the strategic alternatives open to the Italian Commando Supremo from the entry of Italy into the war through the three years to the point at which the Allies were poised to invade. As might be expected, initiative rests largely with the Axis. The British/Allied side remains on the strategic defensive until late in the game. While it can be played two-player, a better game ensues with multiple players on each side with different players for ground/air and naval commands. Operational commands, such as the Balkans, Levant, Iraq and East Africa, provide opportunities for temporary commanders to participate within the larger campaign. [...]

Most components used in The Med are available in the published GDW aka GR/D games

A. Counters Available. 
Marlta Merkur - German Army, Luftwaffe, Yugoslavs, Greeks, Italian 2nd 9th 11th armies
Western Desert - British Army, RAF, Italians in Libya, DAK, Vichy in Levant, Commonwealth forces
The Near East - Egyptians, Iraquis, Iranians, Turks
Fire in the East - Rules and Combat tables, modified
Their Finest Hour - Some British naval counters, naval gunnery chart
Torch - Americans, Vichy in N.Africa, Italian armor
S8T, issue #128 - Italian East Africa, British African units

B. Maps Required. The maps of the region which are essential for play are: 
     14, 15A - Marita Merkur [Yugoslavia & Greece]
     18, 19 -          Western Desert [Lybia & Egypt]
     24, 25  -       Torch [Morocco, Algeria & Tunisia]
     20, 21, 22 -     The Near East [Palestine, Iraq, Iran & Turkey]
     26, 27 -     Unavailable-use sketch in this pack [Italy, which is on Second Front maps, published after this module]

The following maps may be ignored if both USSR and Turkey are excluded from play, which was the historic case: 
     3B, 4A -     Fire in the East [Southern USSR]
     9A -             Scorched Earth [Southern USSR]


Contents:


    Rules (24 pages)
    Allied, Axis and Neutral Order of Battle (27 pages)
    Tables (2 pages)
    Strategic Display (1 British, 1 Italian - 2 pages)
    Ship Status Charts (Britain: 6 pages - others: 1 page - Italy: 3 pages)
    6 Port Charts (6 pages)
    2 Task Composition chart (2 identical pages)
    Italy sketch map (1 page)
    Air Units Counters (2 pages)

(from the introduction:)

Operation Citadel was the name given by the Wehrmacht to their summer offensive against the Soviets in 1943. Zitadelle is a simulation of that strategic turning point of the Second World War on the Russian Front. This came to be the final major campaigning season for the Wehrmacht before the growing might of Soviet arms became overwhelming. Within the scope of the Europa series of simulations from GDW. Zitadelle attempts to allow gamers to explore the military possibilities open to both sides using components largely available. A number of major additions to the standard Fire in the east / Scorched earth (FITE/SE) rule set have been made, most of which should be seen as experimental and considered optional by anyone playing the scenario. 
These include: 

    Operational headquarters for armies/fronts 
    Attack supply effects. 
    Special effects for German non-div. mot. and Soviet cavalry 
    Limited intelligence rules 
    Completely revised air system. using the same counters 
    Complete optional naval system 



Contents:


    Rules (18 pages)
    Axis and Soviet Order of Battle (13 pages)
    Army/Corps display (Axis: 3 pages - Soviet Union: 5 pages)
    Ship Status Charts (Soviet Union: 2 pages)
    3 Port Charts (3 pages)
    1943 Front line maps (2 pages)
This is a simple spin and move game where player begins his adventure from "camp" (10 separated routes, the bottom of game board). The first player, who reach "victory" area - wins the game.

Avast Ye, Matey!  The song has been sung!  The Pirate lord has called for a gathering at his stronghold to elect a successor. It could be you. All that is needed is for you to answer the calling and bring your ships and crew to the stronghold. But beware, there are pirates everywhere. And what pirate can resist an easy treasure, even if it is already on your ship? There are merchant ships to raid but be watchful of the Royal Navy. Be the pirate with the most prestige at the end and you are unanimously elected the pirate king. That is the pirate rule. He who has the most gold makes the rules.

Overview:
Pursue and Plunder is a strategy game for 2 to 4 players. Each player controls 4 pirate ships in an alcove of 9 islands which each hold various artifacts, items and gold. The players will plunder the islands, establish strongholds and attack merchant ships and each other in order to gain the most prestige. Players also have to avoid the Royal Navy that is patrolling the alcove. The game is played in rounds and at the start of each round each player distributes 7 crew tokens (4 regular crew, 2 drunken sailors, 1 captain) on their game mat secretly behind a player screen. Once all players are ready the screens are removed. Each player then takes 2 actions to move, attack opponents or plunder islands. The success of the actions depend on how the player distributed their crew tokens for the round. Artifact items and experience tokens provide additional flexibility to the actions being performed. At the end of the round, players may upgrade
their cannons and sails, establish strongholds, and train crew to improve their chance of success in future rounds. Players are competing to be the first player to earn a set number of prestige points depending on the number of players. Prestige points are earned by finding artifacts, collecting gold, establishing strongholds and defeating merchants and the Royal Navy.

The players have to get the little engine Ivor from the engine shed (space 1) to Grumbly Station (space 13). Movement is determined by a die. The events on the colored spaces are explained in the game rules. Each time a player reaches Grumbly Station all players sing Ivor's song: "puuup - porp - porrp ..."

boardgame: Rivet Wars: Sturmrad vs. M7 Boss
description:
M7 BOSS: "After seeing the effectiveness of the Blight Landkrieger, the allies decided to accept proposals for a bipedal mechanized armored tank of their own."

Sturmrad: "With the monowheel drive system and suspension proving themselves to be both reliable and effective, the Blightun Empire decided to repurpose this design for a fast moving gun platform."

Two new vehicles with their own special abilities to use with the game Rivet Wars.

Trap a Tank, made in the 1930s by Wolverine, features a gold out metal game board with cardboard battlefield graphics and comes with 4 wood tanks. It is a race type game, you crank the handle on bottom and tanks vibrate along the course trying to avoid traps.

boardgame: Rivet Wars: M3 Vertical Tank vs. Landkrieger
description:
M3: "The M3 was commissioned as a light tank with good scouting and fire support capabilities.  With its shield, the M3 can "block" for troops during forward assaults while its considerable firepower allows it to outgun most infantry squads."

Landkrieger: "Its innovative piston-driven suspension allows it to reach high speeds on rough terrain while also permitting it to navigate vertical grades that a tracked vehicle could not.  Its dual hydraulic "fists" allow the Lankrieger to thrive in close combat and use nearby objects and weapons as thrown projectiles."

boardgame: UNCORKED! The party game of wine, wit & wordplay
description:
Gather your buds and taste! UNCORKED! is a wine-tasting game best played with 4-8 players and 4 different wines (any kind will do). It can be played at home or on the go, and requires no previous wine knowledge, so it’s fun for the fully ripened connoisseur to the unevolved amateur.

Each round, the "wine waiter" introduces a new wine and a new theme card to inspire players to write hilarious wine descriptions. Theme examples include, "If this wine had a job, what would it be?" or "Use sexual innuendo to describe this wine." The wine waiter reads all submissions aloud and the object of the game is to earn “Perfect Pairing” points by correctly guessing WHO wrote WHAT. If you guess correctly, you earn 1 “Perfect Pairing” point and you get to guess again. If you are guessed correctly, you are eliminated from the round.

When there’s just one wino left standing, that person becomes wine waiter of the next round. The wine waiter also  awards 1 point for the answer they like best, and 1 “gratuity” point to themselves. You play as many rounds as you have wines, and the player with the most points at the end of the game is deemed winner and MASTER CONNOISSEUR and will seal their victory with a grand toast!

Jump Sturdy is a game originally designed by MindSports. The game is played on an 8×8 board with corners removed. Each player starts with twelve pieces positioned in the center six columns of their two home rows.

Over the course of the game, pieces can form stacks of two pieces. The player who owns the top piece in the stack owns that stack. Single pieces can move one space sideways or forward. They capture one space forward diagonally. Stacks of two pieces move and capture with the four forward moves of a chess knight. Only the top piece in the stack executes the movement or capture; the bottom piece does not move.

A player can complete a movement onto either an empty space or a single friendly piece. A capture can be executed onto any enemy piece or stack. In both cases, capture occurs by replacement; the enemy piece is removed and the friendly piece is put in its place. In the case of a stack, only the top piece is replaced.

The player who reaches the opponent’s home row first wins.

A fast-paced 2 player card game of risk and luck. Punch, kick, parry and dodge your way to victory!

Fight! is a 2 player game that can be played with a standard deck of cards. Each of the 4 suits represents a different fighting move, either punch, kick, parry or dodge. Each move beats another move; for example, punch beats dodge, but parry beats punch.

Every turn, both players simultaneously play the card that they think has the best chance of beating their opponent's card. The winner gets some points, and the first fighter to get 13 points wins the round.

The first player to win 3 rounds wins the fight!

Fight is currently a creative commons licensed free game that can be played with a standard deck of cards. A commercial release with expanded rules and a custom deck with original graphics is currently in the works.

RainboDisko is an action-dexterity game played on a record player. Players take turns placing pieces on a moving record ("preferably the self-titled album Christopher Cross"). Any pieces that fall before the next player places a piece cost the active player a point. The winner is the player with the lowest score when the record ends.

Monster Consonants is a matching game that teaches basic words and beginning consonants by using cards with incomplete words and picture clues. Players are dealt a hand of three cards, they then use the illustrations and incomplete words displayed on their cards to figure out what words they are trying to complete. Each player in turn asks another player for a letter card he thinks he needs. If the other player has the card, the first player makes sure the picture match, reads the correct word, and places the two cards face-down to score ten points. If the other player does not have the card, the first player may draw a card, and lay down any matches made. If the first player does not read the completed word correctly, another player may challenge, read the word, and steal the points. Play continues until a player reaches 150 points.

A three-dimensional game of running, jumping and climbing to be the highest street gymnast once the city has finished building.

Players roll dice each turn, and select one number to choose their action, and the other two numbers to select a location in the city to act in.  Actions can be placing blocks to build the city, knock down previous built sections, or to attempt a dangerous parkour move.

Movement of the players piece can climb up and down the city scape being built, with parkour moves allowing players to leap further upwards, moving diagonally across roof tops or the gravity defying wall run across open space..

Be the highest runner when the blocks run out!
This expansion extends the scope of UPGRADE! to include the tricky area of licensing. You must choose which licensing models work best for your mix of users, hardware and game variant.

boardgame: Flippin' Fruit:  Bonus Fruit Basket Cards
description:
This expansion consists of 6 Fruit Basket Cards that can be added to the deck.

These cards must be played immediately after drawing them and are designed to bring a little more chaos into the game.

This is an exclusive promo card for backers of the Kickstarter campaign.

The Melon is simply added to the Fruit Basket deck and when played, it counts as any fruit when it comes to flipping your dice.

Send out your ships!  Construct cities!  Can you be the one to control the seas and land?

This is a worker placement game.  Players are managing their resources to send out ships to bring back treasure and bounty, and to build up their cities.  On a turn you:
get workers, get income, remove or add workers, and then place your workers on the various places on the board to get money including going to the casino, going to the witch to gain favour, and sending out your ships.

Qwixx Mixx, first released as Qwixx gemixxt, consists of two new blocks of scoresheets for use with Qwixx. The rules of the game remain the same — with players trying to mark off as many numbers as possible from left to right — but one pad has the colors split across the rows of numbers while the other pad has the numbers in non-numerical order. Heresy!

Friendly Fire Pack 9 contains eight scenarios featured in the Friendly Fire 2014 Advanced Squad Leader tournament. The scenarios are:

FrF69 To Ashes by Matthias Ronnblom
France 1940 British vs. Germans, 6 turns, 3 maps
A German force mostly composed of infantry and towed Guns had to push across open fields to rescue a small German force about to face 12 light British AFV.

FrF70 An Estonian interlude by Erik Leander
USSR 1941 Soviets vs. Estonians, 7 turns, 1 map
Small scenario in which both sides have to rush to a building, possibly taking parallel paths along the board 23 canal.

FrF71 Pulling out by Matthias Ronnblom
USSR 1942 Germans vs. Soviets, 7 turns, 2 maps including the excellemt FrFA map
To simulate confusion, two Soviet AFVs will randomly turn abandoned at the consternation of the Soviet side.

FrF72 The Mubo Decision by Andrew Rogers
New Guinea 1943 Australian vs. Japanese, 5 turns, 2 half-maps
An attack - counterattack bloodbath.

FrF73 Sledgehammers by Peter Struijf and Chris Mazzei
France 1944 Germans vs. British, 6 turns, 2 maps
Low quality infantry supported by 150mm SPAs attacking HIP British commandos.

FrF74 Out of their Element by Matthias Ronnblom
Poland 1944 Germans vs. Soviets, 6.5 turns, 2 half-maps

FrF75 Goodbye Brother by Matthias Ronnblom
Finland 1944 Finns vs. Germans , 6 turns, 2 half-maps
Includes as optional German seaborne assault against the Finns.

FrF76 Pain in the Neck by Michael Koch
Germany 1945 Soviets vs. Germans =, 6.5 turns, 2 half-maps
Retreating SS troops with two captured Shermans run into a small Soviet detachment.

Start your engines.  Get ready to race your way to becoming the champion of addition and subtraction!

Hot Wheels Math is a roll-and-move/spin-and-move game designed to teach addition and subtraction while racing your Hot Wheels cars along a track to the finish line.  The board has two sides, with separate tracks.  On the Addition side, which depicts a race track, the player rolls two dice, adds the numbers together (the addition practice element) and moves that many spaces.  Some spaces have special effects that can help or hinder.  First player to complete three laps around the track is the winner.

The subtraction side represents a cross-USA road course.  On this side, players spin the spinner, subtract the number spun from the number on their current space, and move that number of spaces.  This side also features special spaces that can help or hinder players.

Five Tribes: Dhenim is a single card expansion for Five Tribes that adds a new djinn to the game: Dhenim, who's worth 6 vp at the end of the game. During play, Dhenim awards his holder 1 Gold Coin when his holder takes one or more yellow viziers and 2 Gold Coins when an opponent claims them.

Schollenschubser is one of the games that is contained in the board game collection Die Box. It was originally announced as a stand-alone item under the name Pluff.

It is a small tactical game for in between. You are a team of Arctic explorers trying to reach your ship. Unfortunately the ice is already cracked and you need to cross floating ice sheets. Try to be the first reaching the ship with all your 4 explorers by using dice, action cards and pushing your rivals into the water.

Theme: You are a rich man from Mexico at the turn of the 19th century that has a hacienda in the surrounding regions of the newly founded city of Tampica. Tampica is a developing city under the yoke of General Santa Anna. The General in charge of this part of Mexico, rules with an iron fist this new city. Unfortunately, responsibilities prevent him to pursue his ambitions for military expansion. He therefore summons the richest people in the region (the players) to erect the city as well as produce the greatest possible weapons. As a reward, he promises to offer the keys to the city to the best candidate.

Gameplay: Players will have to send their workers to explore the surrounding regions to discover beds of resources and materials. They will fight for control of these resources, exploit them to construct various buildings, which in turn will enable them to build tools, pistols, rifles and machine guns, aiding them in production or combat. When the General is satisfied with a player, he gives him victory points.

Goal: Earn the most victory points during the game, the points represent that the General is satisfied with a player. Points are given to those who control the resources, that construct buildings, that produce weapons and that use certain special buildings.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Magic Rainbow is a game designed to help children learn colors and read color words.  Players move along the magic rainbow track by drawing a card with either a color or the name of a color printed on it.  They then move to the next space of that color on the track (similar to Candyland).  First player to reach the top of the rainbow wins.  There are also obstacles (such as black clouds with rainbow slides) that can set players back.

This game has an interesting game board.  It is large (20"x26") and comes as four giant puzzle pieces to be assembled.

Esbirros Bizarros (Bizarre Minions) is a party game for up to 25 players, which can be played everywhere with anoyone.

The game is played with only one deck of 25 cards, those are divided in "Power Morfys" green cards, "Skulls" grey cards, and special cards such as "Intergalacticos", "Chuchos Infernales", "The Croten One", "El Cornado" and "El Crack".

Each card will add or substract points and have an special action that the player must do. Some times this actions are imposible like "wash your brain in oil", but should do something to satisfy Goo Zman.

Each player takes 2 cards (or 1 if there are more that 13 players), this cards must not be shown to other players. Only the player that gets the card named Goo Zman, reveals it so all players can see it.
The goal of this player is to find as many Power Morfys can, and adquire the highest points posible, avoiding "Skulls" and negative cards. To do so, he must pick one player at a time to show one of his cards (the picked player choose one of his cards). Gooz Man will loose if he gets -25 points.

The players must decide if they want to help Goo Zman, or not. Since 
they can persuade him to being pick with lies or helping him giving clues to who he should pick, but never reveal their cards until they are picked by Goo Zman. If the player choose to show a negative card, this player will win if Gooz Man loose. If the player choose a positive card, the player will win if Goo Zman wins. When a player already had a picked card and must show his other card, the new card will be his win chance.

There is also an special card named "Diabolo", this player will only win if he is not picked.

It's a very fun and crazy game that can last from 1 minute to 20 minutes.

In Mushrooms, players take turns collecting mushrooms on the forest by moving a shared basket. The mushrooms can be good or bad, and thus score positive or negative points. The basket is moved according to some simple rules:

    You cannot move in the direction of the previous move
    You must move in a straight line
    You must land on a space occupied by a mushroom


The player with more points when no more moves are possible wins the game.

From the publisher's game overview:

The object of the game is to come up with the most entertaining and thought-provoking descriptions of hypothetical objects from different near-, medium-, and long-term futures. Each round, players collectively generate a creative prompt by playing a card game. This prompt outlines the kind of future that the thing-to-be-imagined comes from, specifies what part of society or culture it belongs to, describes the type of object that it is, and suggests an emotional reaction that it might spark in an observer from the present. Players must then each write a short description of an object that fits the constraints of the prompt. These descriptions are then read aloud (without attribution), and players vote on which description they find the most interesting, provocative, or funny. The winner of each round keeps the cards put into play for that round, and whoever has the most cards when the game ends is declared the overall winner.

Loot & Recruit is a deck-building game that brings a twist to the genre through its stacking mechanism, dual currency system, and PvP elements. Become a contestant in a crazy Goblin festival as you compete for the title of Goblin King. During the festival, you will add new cards to your deck, recruit Goblins for victory points, attack other players, and hire minions to perform special actions. Do you have what it takes to loot and recruit well enough to claim the throne for yourself?

Features:

    Deck-building mechanism allows you to create your own strategy as you choose and accumulate more cards for your deck.
    Stacking mechanism leaves your Goblins vulnerable at all times, so stack carefully to ensure their protection.
    Play dirty, attack your opponent's Goblins and send them crumbling to the ground.
    Hire minions to perform powerful actions for you.
    Every round a new Event Card changes up the landscape of the game.

Generations is a collection of three games, also referred to as three "generations" (Discovery, Conquest and War). There are rules to play each game individually, but the set can be played in sequence as one long game, taking the players from the discovery of a new land, through colonization and industrialization, and finally to a bitter war for complete control of the land.

The first generation, Discovery takes about 45-60 minutes to play. You compete to grow your population while creating a unique map on which the other games will be played. Pieces are drawn at random, and different types of placements result in different "scores". It is a relatively simple game of geometric strategy, along the lines of "Dominoes," "Carcassonne" or "Blokus". Your final score is based on how effectively and efficiently you "discovered" the land.

The second generation, Conquest takes about 60-90 minutes to play and is played on top of the map created by Generation One. This game is a race to colonize the map and build industries. You roll dice to determine where you can place your people on the board, or how you can impede your opponents. You trade in people from different land types to build industries. You can also build trade routes (allowing you to move people around) and  fortifications (to protect industries). You have to manage your assets wisely to win, but timing and luck play a role as well. The first player to achieve a "stable society" with four industries wins.

The third generation, War, also takes 60-90 minutes to play and is played from the end state of Generation Two. Your people have all become soldiers, trade routes are now blockades, and your industries are repurposed for the war effort. The basic mechanic for battle is similar to other world conquering games (like "Risk") but your industries and blockades all have unique affects on the battlefield. Your armies can move and span across territories, resulting in a war that can ebb and flow to the very end.  The last remaining player is the winner.

The three games/generations are balanced in such a way to make it unlikely for a single player to win all three. And each generation uses different mechanics and different skills, allowing players of different types to engage with it.

From the box:
Bingo Bandit is a bingo game that "combines the luck of a one-armed bandit and bingo with clever strategy!"

User description:
The game features a large plastic bingo machine with a slot in the top for tokens which fall out through chutes at the bottom when the arm of the slot machine is pulled.

The object of the game is to make complete rows on your game card or complete rows on other players' cards in order to collect points.  The player with the most points wins the game.

This promo card is given as a reward for participating on Organized play. This is part of the Allegiance in Blood story arc.

Dispel

Incantation, Metamagic
Cost X Mana

Destroy ther target. X = total mana cost of target enchantment (casting plus reveal cost)

boardgame: Mage Wars: Minor Heal Promo Card
description:
This promo card is given as a reward for participating on Organized play. This is part of the Allegiance in Blood story arc.

Minor Heal

Incantation, Healing
Cost 5 Mana

Heal target creature the amount rolled on 5 attack dice.

The Alpha Strike Companion adds new rules and expanded options for BattleTech: Alpha Strike, the miniatures war game. This expansion adds new tactical abilities, special warrior options, design quirks, and unit types. It includes an expanded abstract aerospace system that will enable simulation of conflicts from planet to jump point.  The book also incorporates a handy conversion system that allows for translating the stats for any BattleTech unit to its fast-playing Alpha Strike equivalent.

Welcome to the Veggie Mall! Hold your shopping lists tight, Keep your eyes wide opened, get ready for the crazy daily sales and the veggie high! Veggie Sales is a simultaneous action game where players try to fulfill, as quickly as possible, their shopping cards with a variety of vegetables. The player, who collects the most vegetables in order to fulfill their shopping cards, becomes the happiest veggie shopper and wins the game!

How to play:
Players start the game with a certain number of Shopping Cards in hands and share a basket of random Vegetable Tokens in the center of the table. The youngest player shouts out “Veggie Sales!” and everyone tries to manipulate the Vegetable Tokens in the Shopping Basket so as to match the compositions on their Shopping Cards. There are three things players can do: 
(1) put ONE Vegetable Token into the basket, 
(2) remove ONE Vegetable Token from the basket, or 
(3) swap exactly TWO Vegetable Tokens in and out of the basket.
If a player thinks that they can fulfill one of their Shopping Cards in hand, they immediately play the card in front of them, grab the Shopping Basket, and perform exactly one of the three possible actions to fulfill the card they have just played. If it successfully matches the card, they place the Shopping Card, face-up, beside them as their scores, and return the basket to the center of the table. After that they give exactly one Shopping Card in hand to any one player next to them. However, if they fail to match the card, they must turn over one of their scored card (if any), face-down, and return the basket to the center of the table, and other players may try to play cards and grab the basket for themselves.
The game ends immediately when one player has got rid of all their cards in hand. All players count the total vegetable icons shown on their scored Shopping Cards. Whoever collects the most vegetable icons wins.

There are 3 color streets,
6 mafia families.
And just one 'Emperor of darkness'...

Can you stand alone on the Throne of Dark Street?
Can you build the greatest family?
Can you make the offer that can't refuse?

Be aware! 'Undercover cop' is watching you...

1. This is an auction game based on mafia ranks.
2. Players must consider which card to take by strategically placing his card.
3. Form a strategy that works best for your hand.
4. Players must work to raise his(her) Group to a higher level by recruiting big bosses.
5. Players must be diligent to not let an undercover cop infiltrate his group.
6. Players must strategize so that 2 factions of the same background do not grow together.

Gameplay is dynamic and intense. and Every game is different!

"Mitra" is a symbol of light and truth in aryan myth, and "Mithra" is a angel divinity in Zoroastrianism. These beings come into conflict in TetMithra, a game in which you score by placing blocks on your own game board or cut down your opponent's score by placing blocks on his.

To set up the game, each player creates a grid of number tiles by drawing the tiles from a bag and placing them on his game board; these tiles contain four squares in a row, with the squares being red, green, yellow and blue and containing the numbers 3-6 in some order. On the first turn, the starting player draws three block tiles, which show the shape of the blocks available for this turn; the blocks are Tetris-style quadrominoes that have squares in four colors. The player chooses one of these pieces and places it on his board within one minute, scoring points for the like-colored squares touching this block. Thus, if a red 5 on his grid is adjacent to a red square on the block he just placed, he scores five points.

The next player then reveals a new block tile, reveals the new block, then places one of the three blocks in his grid. From a player's third turn on, he can choose to place a block in an opponent's grid, costing him points.

The game ends when either only two blocks remain unplaced, no blocks can be placed in either grid, or one player has more than 180 points or less than -50 points. At this time, the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Villagers & Villains: The Borderlands Kickstarter Promo Pack
description:
This is a set of four cards exclusive to Kickstarter backers of Villagers & Villains: The Borderlands.   It is an expansion to Villagers & Villains and can be added to that base game with or without the Borderlands expansion.

This small promo pack includes one card of each of the four card types:


     Gypsy Vendor (Citizen)
     Hunger (Challenge)
     Convention Hall (Building)
     CosPlay Hero (Hero)

Yomi EX Powerup contains a new gold character card and four copies of an extra rank of face cards called Destiny cards for each of the twenty characters in the second edition of Yomi; it also includes a premium knockdown token and an oversized Deathstrike Dragon character card so that he can play against two other players in a 2v1 match. The box has a sleeve with product information that can be removed, leaving a display-worthy box.

This expansion powers up all twenty Yomi second edition character decks, letting you play the EX version of every character. Note that EX characters are overpowered, not tournament-legal, and not balanced.

Two themed ring sets for the game Spinergy.

Mmm...Chocolate! is a 3-ring set with words pertaining to chocolate. (ie- fudge, sprinkled, dark)

Hullaballoo is a 3-ring set with words pertaining to sounds. (ie- car alarm, sonar, mosquito)
Added Rule: In your response, you must create the sounds represented by each of the 3 words.

The rings from the two sets can be mixed and matched with each other or with the rings of the base game or other expansion.

boardgame: Roman Conqueror: A Solitaire Game of Conquering the Roman Empire.
description:
Roman Conqueror is a semi-historical simulation of the conquest of the Roman Empire. The Player assumes the role of the Consul of Rome during the early period of the Roman Empire. Rome is feeling the confidence of their advancements in technology, culture and military might. 
 OBJECTIVE: As the Consul of Rome you are anxious to spread the knowledge and culture of Rome to neighboring Territories, as well as, increasing the wealth and prosperity of the Roman population (and of course, yourself). The Player must raise Legions and assign Generals to conquer as many Territories as possible before the last Campaign Card is drawn, thus, ending your magnificent reign.

GAME FEATURES: Roman Conqueror includes many colorful counters representing the various Troops of the time, including, Legion Infantry, Auxiliary Infantry, Elephants, Heavy and Light Chariots, Peltasts, Horse Archers and Warbands. The game is Card-Driven and event includes rules for Gladiatorial Combat, Chariot Races, and Naval Combat. Enemy Civilizations include Barbarians (Germans, Belgai, Gauls), Sarmatians (Huns, Goths, Dacians), Parthians (Persians), and Carthaginians.

boardgame: Sheriff of Nottingham: Gen Con 2014 Promo Set
description:
This promo pack contains six new cards for Sheriff of Nottingham:


     Brimstone Oil
         Collect gold at the end of the game or discard immediately to destroy an opponent's pair of legal goods.
    
     Olive Oil
     Strawberry Mead
         Increases the value of all other mead you manage to smuggle past the sheriff
    
     Golden Silk
         Increases the value of all other silk you manage to smuggle past the sheriff
    
     Dragon Pepper
         Increases the value of all other pepper you manage to smuggle past the sheriff
    
     Heavy Crossbow
         Increases the value of all other crossbows you manage to smuggle past the sheriff
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The game consists of 24 dice. Each die has a single letter on each side.

Each player rolls all the dice and places the resulting letters in a crossword style grid. The objective is to use as few words as possible while using all the letters. The player who needs the fewest words to use all the letters is the winner. The game can be played as a single round, multiple rounds, in teams or the players may choose pass the dice undisturbed to the other player or team so each is using the same letters to form a grid.

In the game, you begin with one ant hill in a vast forest of grass.  There is plenty of good foraging.  And valuable items can be found.  You can grow your colony by building new hills in the openings.  All is well until one day…..  you encounter…  OTHER ANTS!

Soon it becomes clear that this is a contest for domination.  You must build an army for defense, and maybe an attack or two.  But you must carefully watch your food supply and the size of your army because of spoilage and fungus plagues. If you can exterminate another colony's home hill or build a great temple hill, all the other colonies will surrender to you and you will be an instant winner. Or if the forest is cleared first, you can win by having the most wealth.

Ants 4X is a diceless game.  This gives the players choices instead of relying on luck.  Battles are resolved by secret deployment of soldiers ants, simultaneously revealed.  Of course, the explore actions, by necessity, will involve finding random items.  Everything you find will be useful for something, but you may need to change your strategy.

Ants 4X is not an educational or epic game, just a fun, fast playing game that includes all the basic 4X actions, resource management and a dab of ant know-how thrown in for good measure.

BLUE is a game from the RED, BLUE, GreenGreenerGreenest trilogy.

BLUE is a three-coloured tile-laying game for three players. It is a crossbreed of the games RED and Hong, by the same designer.

The three colours in the game are white, gray, and blue, and each player will be a different colour. The tiles have the same design as in Hong, but combining those 3 colours in a black background. So there are 3 types of tiles in total.
 A row is a straight line of one or more aligned segments of the same colour with a semicircle of that colour on each end. A spot is a circle made of 2 semicircles of the same colour with no segments in between.

Players place tiles on the table trying to create as many rows and spots of their colour as possible.

Sentymentalna Gra Planszowa PRL (Sentimental Boardgame PRL) is a game about life in People's Republic of Poland. Players do their best to buy a car, but this is no small feat, requiring both money and more than a little luck.

Players in turn roll a pair of 6-sided dice, moving their pawns around the board according to the number rolled and following the printed instructions: drawing cards, earning and losing money, and trading various Communist-era commodities, such as a Washing Machine Frania.

The goal of the game is to buy a car (represented by a cardboard model of Fiat 125p), either directly from the sole producer, Polmozbyt, which requires a ration card, or  at much higher expense from second hand.

The rectangular, Monopoly-style game board depicts the Communist-era Warsaw's skyline. Caricatures on game cards and money represent actual PRL-produced commodities and Polish currency from that period.

18 Card RPG is a semi-cooperative storytelling card game in which players work together to tell a story while individually working to complete their characters' personal goals. Players will choose attributes and goals for their characters from a hand of cards and will then pick from one of two scenarios: "Lab of the Mad Wizard" or "Shopping with Humans."

On a player's turn, the other players pick one of the active character's attributes and one of her goals and narrate a scene of how that attribute gets the character into trouble while she pursues the goal. Then, the active player must describe the character's attempt to get out of that trouble and meet the selected goal using her other attribute.

For example, let's say your group is playing the scenario "Lab of the Mad Wizard." Your character has the attributes "Short term memory loss" and "Sarcasm" and the goals of "Needs to burn the place down (even just a little bit)" and "Wants to hurt the feelings of character on right." The other players might narrate a scene in which you completely forget where you are and what you are doing and then notice a book of matches on the table. You start playing with the matches, which draws the attention of a quivering tower of sentient slime who wants to swallow you and your pretty matches. Now you must narrate your character's effort to get out of this situation using her sarcasm while she tries to meet her goal of burning the place down. Can you manage to crack a sarcastic remark that gets you away from the monster while lighting fire to something?

More importantly, can you tell the tale convincingly enough that the other players will vote for your success? They will decide if your attempt was successful or not and whether or not your goal was met, and they will narrate the results accordingly.

Players WIN who manage to meet the goals of their characters before four rounds have passed, and they celebrate by narrating the fates of their characters freely. Players LOSE whose character's goals are not met before the end of four rounds, and they must narrate the deaths of their characters.

In Frog King, players reveal cards one by one, responding to each one as quickly as possible based on what's shown in the pictures. Who will catch a fly — and who will step on the frog king's feet by catching a fly meant for him?!
Cars are an indispensable part of our lives, and the tiles in MaCar can be put together in any number of ways to build cars in new styles and dimensions.

Once There Was... 3+ is a pre-school learning game for home and kindergarten, a game that develops the imagination and creativity, enlarges the vocabulary, and improves the memory and the powers of observation.

In the game, Moritz the cat has finished playing on the lawn and he's going home. The players help him by rolling the dice and drawing cards with cheerful colored pictures on the basis of which they have to make up a story. The players form sentences using the themes from at least two cards. The purpose of the game is to form coherent sentences and to collect as many cards as possible.

The mail must go through!
On foot, by bike or by taxi, travel the neighbourhood streets to be the first mail to deliver all your letters!

Roll the dice then play your cards! Use them the best way possible to deliver as many letters as you can on your turn!
Beware of locked doors and angry dogs! Grab a taxi (that's not cheating)! Go, Mailman!
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Thanks to your puzzle pieces and your best tactics, create the longest colored line to win!
During the game, block your adversaries’ paths, or flip their pieces to integrate them with your own line. But be careful because these new white pieces can benefit all players!

Following the White Rabbit, Alice entered the Queen’s palace. Some problems happened between them, and Alice is now being chased by the Queen. Alice must find the White Rabbit among the Trump Soldiers, to escape from the palace. The White Rabbit might know where to go. Alice who wants to catch the White Rabbit and the Queen who wants to catch Alice… The hide-and-seek between Alice and the Queen of Heart now begins.

Goal
Chasing Alice is a 2-player game, and each player becomes Alice and the Queen of Heart. With your reasoning, find where the opponent is. The player who catches another first wins the game. The victory condition for each player is different, however, so you’ll need a different strategy on each role. Don’t forget! Catch her before you get caught.

Game Play
Each player takes a role as Alice or the Queen of Heart. Two players share the space of game board and their locations on the board are hidden. Each player uses a recording sheet to mark his location and information of the opponent’s location.
In one’s turn, do one action of two as follow.
1. Move: Choose one of the cards and place it face up in front of the Blind Cover. According to the suit on the card, change your location to the adjacent box. ‘The adjacent box’ includes all 8 directions. You cannot move to the box you have ever been. The Queen may move the White Rabbit only once, and Alice may move herself twice. Make sure you mark your path for each move.
2. Trace: Reveal the two cards in your hand to the opponent, and announce a single location with the card numbers combined. Trace the selected box and the adjacent boxes. The opponent must say how many boxes she had been among those 9 boxes.
After an action, a player can say ‘GOTCHA!’. This action is optional, and you can decide whether to do it or not. If you succeed, you win a victory; otherwise you lose the game immediately. Carefully do this action for the game. The condition for catching the opponent varies in different roles.
- The Queen of Heart: Guess where Alice is, and point exactly one box on the Game Board. If it is correct, the Queen wins a victory. Otherwise the Queen loses the game.
- Alice: With no pointing, both players reveal their location. If Alice and the White Rabbit are near enough to reach within a box, Alice wins a victory. Otherwise Alice loses the game.

Game end
If any of the players succeed or fail the action ‘GOTCHA’, the game ends immediately.
Or if the Game Cards run out (which means each player took 5 turns) without determining any result, Alice fails to escape and the Queen wins the game.

All begins at the time you enter the KILL ZONE. KILL ZONE, an area where a bomb is planted within a radius of 25m, is so dangerous because of the possibility of an explosion. You, as an EOD agent, must render the bomb safe. But be careful! There is a spy in your team who wants the bomb to be set off. I wish you could carry out the duty and come back…

Goal
Each player will be a dealer (spy) in turn. Players including the dealer are charged with the bomb disposal mission. There are 8 wires on the bomb, but only one of them is a detonator. If you successfully cut the wire except the detonator, you will be promoted to the next rank as a reward. Otherwise, however, you and your companion will be demoted if the bomb is exploded. With several missions, the player with the highest rank wins the game.

Game Play
At the start of game, each player takes own rank tiles. The player with the lowest rank takes the Dealer Tile and starts the game as a first dealer (spy).
The player with the lowest rank starts the turn. Each player performs one of the following actions on their turn: Cutting a wire or Play a card.

    Cutting a wire: Place a Nipper Tile on one of the wires of the Game Board. You cannot place a Nipper Tile on previously cut wires. If a player cut a non-detonator wire, the dealer should announce ‘Promotion’ (Or should say some words to announce such as ‘Success’ or ‘Safe’). The player is promoted one step higher. The turn goes to the next player (the player with the lowest rank).
    Bomb Disposal: If all wires except the detonator are cut, the bomb is rendered safe. All players successfully complete their mission, and the round ends. However, the dealer is caught being a spy, and he will be demoted one step lower.
    Bomb Explosion (Cutting a detonator): While playing a round, if a player cut the detonator, the dealer announces ‘Boom!’ The bomb is exploded, and the round ends immediately. The player who cut the detonator will be demoted two steps lower. Also, the player(s) on his left/right side will be demoted a step lower.


The round ends when the bomb is disposed or exploded. Start the next round with the current rank.

Game ends
The game ends if any of the following situations occur:

    All players played as a dealer once each. (The round is played as the number of players.)
    A player achieved the rank ‘General of the Army (Lv. 23)’ (The game ends immediately.)
    A player is demoted even lower than the rank ‘Private (Lv. 0)’ (The game ends immediately.)


After the game ends, the player with the highest rank wins the game.

A turn-based board game for 2-6 players based on the video game Ingress.

Theme:
In the middle of the 19th Century, a trade ship named Niantic departed Connecticut and set sail on a voyage to the Orient coast.  Some say the Niantic was smuggling Opium out of China and Indonesia but while that may have been a side deal worked out for its journey home, the merchant vessel Niantic docked in Whampoa Harbor and unloaded a precious cargo.

A relic, uncovered during an archeological dig hundreds of years earlier, was transported to Whampoa Harbor and buried there.

The relic laid buried in the area that later became known as Whampoa Garden until approximately 150 years later, when in 1999, Liu Fan excavated it and working in combination with a group of international researchers, went to work unlocking its secrets.

Ingress: The Relic casts up to six researchers in roles to unlock the undiscovered power of exotic matter.  Researchers take turns placing portal tiles on the relic board and activating them with resonator tokens.  Connecting three fully activated portal tiles can lead to an even greater power by creating a control field.

Gameplay:
The game plays out on a grid-based board with tiles in a manner somewhat reminiscent of Go or Chinese Checkers.  Winners use a combination of action cards and tile placement to earn action points.  The player to first reach 50 action points or the player with the most action points at the end of 10 rounds wins.

Extra battle map, to move your Scotland Rising battles from Bannockburn 1314 back to the Battle of Sterling Bridge 1297.

Lead the forces of Andrew de Moray and William Wallace to victory, or change history by crushing the Scotish rebels by leading the 6th Earl of Surrey John de Warenne and the king treasurer Hugh de Cressingham and their men to victory!

The game was originally a Kickstarter streach goal but was not met, so the publisher is offering a digital edition which can be printed.
Original counters are needed to play this game.
Rules as base game.

Map size 11 x 17 inches.

The year 1298; almost a year after the Battle of Stirling Bridge, the Scots and the English meet again: William Wallace now face King Edward "Longshanks" I.

In this extra map for Scotland Rising, you and a friend take control of either the Scots Brave or the English Army on the famoues Battle of Falkirk!
Lead your men as King Edward I and secure the English victory - or change history and give Scotland it's freedom by leading Wallace and his men.

This map was originally a Kickstarter streach goal, but unfortunately it was not met, so the publisher decided to publish it digitally on Scotland Rising's webpage.
To play this map you will need the counters (or blocks add-ons for a fog-of-war) from the base game.
The map uses base game rules.

Map size 11 x 17 inces.

A fantasy dice game where 2 or more players control the armies of Sorroah using the forbidden artefacts: the BattleBones.

BattleBones sees players take on the role of a CleroMancer one of the MageCaste who use ancient Artefacts of great power to control the armies of the land. Through the use of these BattleBones, warriors and heroes are summoned to the field of battle, where great battlelines are created with which to attack the enemy.

These BattleBones are represented by special engraved dice, each face showing one of the elements of the army:

Each turn players roll their dice and lay out their battlelines, placing the dice in the order they wish to play them. Dice are then compared in a rock-paper-scissors fashion with the winning dice able to continue the fight. The losing dice are killed and removed to the Graveyard.

It's a great little game suited to killing time between wargames, waiting for a table to come available at the club, or down the pub with your friends.

Monsters in town! Can you stop them before they mess up the Mons-Cube Town? Try to re-arrange your Monster Cubes to match the designated Code Number and defeat the monsters! With the help of some mathematics, saving the town is more than fun!

In Monster Code mode, players try to solve the puzzle by finding the legal combination of three Monster Cubes that matches the Code number shown on the Monster Card. The player who is the first to announce the correct combination beats the monster and scores the victory points on the Monster Card. Whoever scores the most points in the end wins the game and becomes the hero of Mons-Cube Town!

Escape The Maze is a tabletop card game for 2-6 players. Players take turns to explore the maze adding to it as they go. The objective of the game is to gain the most points by either discovering bonus point cards within the maze or by reaching the exit before the other players do!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Valiant charges, bone crushing melee, and lump-in-your-throat defense has been recreated in Swords & Banners, a gaming system that uses 15mm or 25mm miniatures to recreate epic Medieval historical battles from the year 870 AD to 1424 AD.

The Swords & Banners system has been designed to provide miniature players a historical set of parameters without sacrificing playability. Extensive research into actual formations used by medieval armies has been incorporated into these rules.

The Swords & Banners system also uses modifiers that are incorporated into “At a Glance” tables, allowing players to use the tables without having to constantly refer to the rule booklet.

Also included are reproducible game rulers, markers, and overhead view stand counters (so you don’t even need miniatures to play the game), along with scenarios allowing you to get right into game-play!" (From the Introduction)

Swords & Banners is a complete print-and-play game which comes on a CD.
A roll-and-move game by Toy Company Argentina that uses the same cover artwork as Web of Gold, but is a different game. The game uses one d6 and some 3D plastic pieces in a dark grayish color.

From the publisher:

In this fast-paced multi-round game, you control a small castle of three walls, behind which you will defend your Princess, fire your Catapult, and rule with your shrewd and protective King. Simultaneously, you will send forth your own Knight to abduct said rival’s Princess.

For each game purchased, you will receive 14 game cards, an instruction document and a quick reference card.
Both players will begin the game with three castle walls, a King, a Princess, a Catapult and a Knight.

Setup, play, and cleanup can all be completed in 2-5 minutes for most games.

Summary of play:

Players take turns placing their Knight to attack their opponent's castle in order to capture their princess.  The first player to capture a princess wins!  Conflict resolution is chart based, cross referencing which units are participating in the battle.

boardgame: Star Wars: The Card Game – Attack Run
description:
“The battle station is heavily shielded and carries a firepower greater than half the star fleet. Its defenses are designed around a direct, large-scale assault. A small one-man fighter should be able to penetrate the outer defense.”
   –General Dodonna, Star Wars: A New Hope

Relive the climax of Star Wars: A New Hope with Attack Run, the fourth Force Pack in the Rogue Squadron cycle for Star Wars: The Card Game. Within this Force Pack, you’ll find new versions of iconic heroes and villains, alongside the starships they fly. This Force Pack continues the main themes of the Rogue Squadron cycle with pilots like Luke Skywalker, Darth Vader, and Boba Fett.

Whether you pilot Slave I for the Scum and Villainy of the galaxy or fly a B-wing with Keyan Farlander, you’ll find cards to support your affiliation of choice with the ten objective sets (two copies each of five unique sets) in this Force Pack. Evade authorities with the Smugglers and Spies, pilot Vader’s unique TIE Advanced, or fly into the Death Star’s vulnerable trench and start your Attack Run!

Contains objective sets 156-160.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Game: Star Wars – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Number Strategy Solitaire is a simple solitaire game and is an alternative to patience.

It uses a standard pack of playing cards with the picture cards removed. After shuffling 16 cards are placed down in a 4 x 4 grid and the remaining are placed in a deck face up.

The player draw one card at a time and place it down on top of one of the cards in the grid, but at the same time the player can see what the next card is so he can plan ahead.

When a card is place its value has to be two cards adjacent which adds up the same value or equals ten above its value.

In Estiméo, players are presented with questions on challenge cards that must be answered with numbers. Sometimes the cards have fixed answers, while sometimes the answers are variable — possibly even something that needs to be verified with the included tape measure.

What's important, though, is not necessarily the exact answers, but whether you can sort the cards in ascending order...

A settlement building game for fans of the isometric strategy boom of the late 90's
In IsoImperial you are the new lord of a settlement being built in the Middle Ages on the feudal country of Ingland.

Players get to play as competitively or cooperatively as they like, as well as a solitaire single player variant.

IsoImperial gives players over 30 different buildings to build their new settlement from a small hamlet, all the way to a bustling city. Building types consist of residential buildings, goods and economic buildings, buildings required for growth as well as the military and defence buildings required for keeping players, and random events out of your settlement.

Having a constant income is only part of the struggle, with droughts, floods, fires, roaming bandits and more coming into play. As well as balancing your stretch to victory without becoming a prime target for other players and their military.

Multiple paths to victory, be the first to achieve a city, or last 24 in game months and come out with the best score across multiple categories.

Small enough to play anywhere, big enough to get stuck in.

Isometric artwork heralds back to the late 90's strategy and empire building boom.

boardgame: GodZ: the Genesis of Badly Dead
description:
The first expansion for GodZ, the game of creation and battles set in the world of Super Fantasy.

Introduces a new way to score points during the game, giving players more control over the scoring and the endgame systems.

Originally included in Super Fantasy: Night of the Badly Dead

Ready to push your luck? Then reach for the Lucky Dice and try your chances. The game lasts five rounds with each player taking a turn in each round.

To start a turn, a player grabs and rolls two or three dice from the bag. From that point on, the player has a choice of stopping and scoring points, or drawing and rolling three more dice. If a player ever has three of the same number or color showing, that player's turn ends and she scores nothing — unless she also has a die face that shows "Free (number)" or "Free (color)", in which case that number or color can't bust her for the turn. If a player has used all 18 dice in the bag, she must stop.

To determine a score for the round, first remove all of the "Free ____" dice, then sum the digits on the dice that remain. If, however, you've rolled one or both of the lucky dice in the bag, your score might be boosted (or halved). A "1:5" on a lucky die, for example, means that 1s give you five points instead of one. A "1/2" means that your sum is halved, but if you roll both "1/2" symbols, then your score is doubled instead.

The player with the highest score after five rounds wins!

Zombie ApocalyOOPS takes place in a not-so-distant future, where scientists who have been researching immortality have accidentally created zombies instead.  Fortunately, the condition can be reversed, though there may be some residual side-effects.  In the meantime though... players must battle each other to survive!

Zombie ApocalyOOPS is a fast paced card game in which players switch between human and zombie in a free-for-all battle.  Each card is split in half and color coded.  Blue halves are playable by humans, green by zombies, and white by any race.  If a player chooses (or is forced) to change race, they must flip their hand to the corresponding color.

The game also includes 22 Event cards, of which a random 10 are used each game.  This provides high replay value, and also a chaotic element similar to what one might expect in a real zombie apocalypse.

Exploits is a card game where players try to hack each others servers.  The game's main mechanic focuses on combining an exploit and payload to effectively attack your opponents.

Each player has a set of servers behind a firewall, as the game progresses, the firewalls grow and develop vulnerabilities.  Players take turns looking at firewalls to protect themselves and find vulnerabilities with their opponents decks. Players then attack an opponents server with their own payload and exploit, and the vulnerability in their opponents firewall. Other cards allow for DDos, botnets distributing spam, and other abilities.

The chicken crazy party game.

The game is played in teams. One person on the team will use the chicken to depict one of the words of on the card while the rest of his team tries to guess. 
If the word isn't guessed in ten seconds the other team gets one chance to guess. 
First team to ten points wins the game.

Armoured Fury allows you to dive into action as either an American Sherman tank commander or become a Panzer commander of lethal Panther tanks. Filled with loads of awesome plastic tanks this boxed set is not only a great way to get started with Bolt Action Tank War but is superb value too!

The scenario booklet eases you into the game with four scenarios each escalating in scale until you're ready to add more to your fledgling armoured formations. So whether you're a die-hard Bolt Action player or new to the game completely this will get you up to speed quickly.

Bolt Action Tank War allows you to play the award-winning Bolt Action game with armoured formations as opposed to the more usual infantry-heavy forces. So, now your tanks can stalk the Normandy bocage, swarm across the open Russian steppes, duel with the enemy in the desert or ambush your foes in the steamy jungles of the Pacific!

Tank War is an extremely popular way to play Bolt Action and Armoured Fury is the ideal way to join the action!

The ship Batavia was build in Amsterdam in 1628 on the orders of the VOC (united East-Indian Company). A Dutch initiative to send ships to all corners of the world. In October of that year the Batavia, with 341 souls aboard, would set sail to the East Indies. Everything seemed to be going great on it's maiden voyage... But on June 4th of 1629 the ship ran aground near the coast of Australia. Only a handful of people survived the disaster and the following mutiny...

Every player plays the skipper of a VOC ship. The starting player will roll the die determining how many spaces everybody gets to move. If a player ends his move in a port they can stock up on goods (for free). If a player ends in the Home port he can unload his goods thereby securing them from the pirate and the other players. And if a player lands next to another player he can attack them (high roller wins, difference in roll is the amount of goods that can be stolen).

Secured goods can be used to buy parts of a players own Batavia which also yields an advancement for the players current VOC ship.
After a player has build his own Batavia, he will have to sail it from the Home port to Batavia city to win the game.

A diminuation of the designer's Patton's Lorraine Campaign, this is at basically division level and much faster to play.  Weekly turns as in PLC, 10mis/16kms per hex, 9 starting Allied units and 13 starting Germans - 17 units each side.

If Patton does well enough that Operation Marketgarden is cancelled and his supplies continue, either his victory requirements are 4 turns sooner, or the German player may choose to bring 1. and 2. SS PzDivs into the fray.

As in PLC, weather is important, with "Morning Fog" a possibility.

Same basic concept and system as Ardennes Petite.

boardgame: Spinergy G-Rings: That's Amoré and Play Ball!
description:
Two themed ring sets for the game Spinergy.

That's Amoré is a 3-ring set with words pertaining to love.

Play Ball! is a 3-ring set with words pertaining to sports.

The rings from the two sets can be mixed and matched with each other or with the rings of the base game or other expansion.

boardgame: Mage Wars: Pharaoh's Cheetah Promo Card
description:
