boardgame: Mage Wars: Vorpal Blade Promo Card
description:
Promo Card for Mage Wars Arena

English-language version was first made available by Arcane Wonders through Gen Con 2014 before being sold through the publisher's website.

German-language version was included in the Organized Play Kit 2. Given as a reward for participating in Organized Play.

In Slow Fox, players try to force their opponent to jump off the board. Basic moves are jumps, and the tokens jumped over change their color and therefore their owner. A player is forced to make a jump; if he cannot make a jump, he makes a simple move changing the color of the moving token.

A simple children´s game that has 2 phases. In the first, you lay cards from your hand, building a road in the kingdom of Ooo using a mechanic that is similiar to dominoes. In the second phase, the game has a simple roll and move mechanic and landing on spaces that tell you to move forward or backward some spaces, makes you or your opponent lose your turn or require a dice roll to get out of. The first player to make it to the end of the road is the winner.

From the publisher:
"Monte sua própria aventura, construindo o caminho do Reino de Ooo e ajude Finn, Jake, Princesa Jujuba e Rei Gelado a percorrerem todo o percurso. Enfrente vários desafios e descubra quem tem mais sorte."

"Build your own adventure, paving the way of Ooo Kingdom and help Finn, Jake, Ice King and Bubblegum Princess to roam all the route. Face several challenges and see who has better luck."

Collect as much money as you can as you follow with your coins a certain route multiple times from start to finnish. The first player that collect 10.000 florijnen is the winner.

De bedoeling van het spel is om met muntstukken en fiches een aantal keren de aangegeven route op het spelbord van start naar finish te volgen. Tijdens het spelen moet zoveel mogelijk geld worden verzameld. Degene die als eerste 10.000 florijnen heeft, is winnaar !

Creepy Towers is a "story box full of games & surprises!"  Within Creepy Towers there is a board game, short story, jigsaw puzzle, and deck of unique cards.  The board game's objective is to race to random locations on the board and be the first player to collect three "ghouls" and then reach the finish space.

Boardless 4-in-a-row type game where the players' moves are dictated by the position of nine rings on the table.

9 Rings 4 a Row is played with 9 rings and 40 pawns in two colors. The pawns fit inside the rings.

Players alternate placing a pawn in a ring and then moving this ring orthogonaly adjacent to another ring. The first player to align 4 pawns in their color in any direction wins the game.

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Foot Clan Street Fight Game
description: The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles' weapons have gone missing and it's up to you to help find them. Roll the die, travel through the path of manhole covers and reveal what's underneath them. Will it be a delicious pizza, a weapon, or the dreaded Foot Clan? Be the first to get two slices of pizza and find your weapon to win!
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
In Nord, each player leads a tribe. He orders workers and fishermen, performs attacks, unites settlements and provides his fighters with raw materials and food.

Nord: Jarl Expansion adds four special Jarls to the game: Ragnar, Eirik, Floki and Thorstein. These Jarls can substitute neutral jarls. They are placed during the setup before the players' jarls onto a central settlement.

For each of them exist a small overview tile and a szenario tile with the rules. There are 4 big wooden jarl meeples with different colors which can be substituted with some meeples of your own or by marking the standard neutral jarls.

For Ragnar is a big settlement included which can be crafted or substituted by 2 adjacent standard settlements.

This expansion for Evolution includes:


     7 "Unstable DNA" trait cards
     6 Alternate format/art Species cards
     Alternate first player marker
     Set of five coasters (not part of game)

description:
Ragnaroll is a game of glory, death and dice. A great winter has descended upon the land, the sun has disappeared, the sea has frozen over, there's no more crops or cattle and all you have left is a few winter stores and, of course, your fluid Viking sense of ownership. In Ragnaroll, you control a band of Viking warriors, each represented by a custom die, struggling to survive. Your band consists of a unique hero, 2 soldiers, 1 shieldmaiden and a loyal, but slightly unreliable, dog. You'll need to fight to protect your precious food stores, as well as raid your opponents for glory and more food. It's a harsh winter wasteland, and the last Viking alive wins, unless of course Ragnaroll happens first.

A round of Ragnaroll starts by revealing an event card, which will provide some incentives and rewards for your Vikings to leave their comfy halls. Next, players secretly separate their warriors into their defenders and their raiders. Players reveal their selection simultaneously and the boldest and bravest player, the one with the smallest raiding party, seizes the Initiative, gaining control of raid order for the round. If there's any ties, you'll need to literally seize the initiative by grabbing the initiative token from the centre of the table.

After all players have raided, they will return to their falls and feed their warriors, but with stores running low, not all mouths are worth feeding. You can send any of your Vikings out into the snow for a long walk, saving precious resources, and if you get really desperate, you can even eat the dog. After you’ve fed, you’ll draw a new event card and start all over again. The game will end in one of 3 ways, each of which show the gods that one band of warriors is fit to fight alongside them in Ragnaroll. When there’s only one player with Vikings left, that player wins, demonstrating they have the strength and cunning to fight with the gods, but if all players run out of stores at the same time, you will all conduct one last glorious battle and the player with the most axes will emerge victorious. However, if any player is able to demonstrate they are truly worthy of the god’s favour by gaining 15 glory, they will immediately win and take their rightful place in Valhalla.

Ragnarol is a game where your choices matter. You’ll need to balance risk and rewards, and manage your precious, dwindling resources, making hard decisions for the survival of your clan. The dice-based combat doesn’t simply mean that your fate into the hands of the gods, as you’ll be able to influence the outcome of the battle using your glory-fueled powers, but you’ll need to choose the battles that are worth the cost.

boardgame: Lords of War: Orcs versus Dwarves 2 – The Magic and Monsters Expansion
description:
The first in a series of sequel decks to the original, UK Games Expo Award Winning Lords of War games, this expansion adds a huge number of new Unit Types, new Ranks and new rules to the base game.

Alongside adding in new gameplay functions and mechanics, this expansion also introduces Moving Units to the Orc and Dwarf Factions.

The first of the major new Unit Types included in this expansion are Magic Units, capable of buffing allies and damaging foes using Elemental Attacks and Spell Tokens.

The game introduces Monstrous Units - extremely tough cards with 'Monstrous Defence' which allows them to ignore low damage attacks.

Finally, the Magic and Monsters Expansion also introduce Flying Units which are only vulnerable to Ranged Units and other Flying Units.

While primarily designed as an addition to Lords of War: Orcs versus Dwarves, this expansion is also compatible with all other Lords of War Factions and adds additional Command Cards, Special Units and Elite Units to the base game.

You'll never win alone.

In Super Duo, a group of wannabe superheroes have come together to deal with the city's most pressing problems. Only two of you can be true heroes. Meet your collective doom, or rise to Fame and Glory.

Super Duo is a short and simple card game. Collect, complete and collaborate in an epic quest. Strategic choices, a little luck, and a watchful eye on your friends are the key to winning.

boardgame: Lords of War: Weather and Terrain Expansion Pack
description:
An expansion released off the back of the Lords of War: Templars versus Undead Kickstarter Campaign, this expansion adds no new Units to Lords of War but instead adds two modifier decks to the base game.

The Terrain Deck enables players to build custom battlefields using cards which have a number of effects on the Units placed on top of them. This includes building 'Walls' and 'Gates' which may be destroyed or landscape features such as 'Hills' which increase damage dealt by Ranged Units or 'Marshes' into which Cavalry cannot be placed.

The Weather Deck meanwhile creates dynamic weather conditions, including 'Night' during which players can play two cards instead of one in their turn or 'Mist' which allows Infantry Units to deal more damage.

These decks can be used together or separately, and the expansion also includes a 'Retrieve The Dragon Egg' minigame which operates much like a 'Capture The Flag' mode.
Bruce Moose is set in motion and he moves around while playing music.  The first kid to get rings off in the antlers. Get all those in your color off and you win...

The goal of the game is to be the first player to reach the star in the center of the game board. The players each have one meeple of a different size. The bigger ones are superior to the ones of the next smaller size. The smallest meeple is superior to the biggest one. If two meeples of two consecutive sizes are on the same grey or black ring, with no other meeple between them, the superior one can exchange places with the minor one, if the minor one ist the preceeding one on the track. If the superior mepple is the preceeding one, the minor meeple has to go back 3 spaces.

THEME
...it's the year 2099.

The polar ice caps melted 10 years ago, resulting in a rapid increase in the world's sea level. 
Cities drowned, the world's plains turned into lakes, mountains became islands.
Whole populations were wiped out.
The food shortage brought the wars...

Now we are all divided into 4 main alliances, where each one fights for the last remaining pieces of land.
Each one struggles to reach the pivotal point and control... The 5th Land

THE BOARD
The board can be setup to show 11.664 different map formations,
assuring a different world every time you play and always the need for a new strategy.

GAMEPLAY
In The5thLand, each player represents one of the 4 main alliances whose aim is to occupy 5 lands, by controling the worlds resources, bankrupting their enemies and ultimately by war.

In the beginning each player starts with 2 Lands, 2 Army units and a Supply Center with 5 of each resources  and 75 Million Credits.

Each Round consists of 4 main Phases where the players have the opportunity to:

    Extract the resources from the territories they occupy.
    Build additional Military units (Land/Air/Navy).
    Trade on the world markets, Resources and Military units.
    Move military units around the globe to achieve a strategic advantage and Attack when the time is right...


The players may act or not in each Phase (clockwise direction) and when all have played, they move on the next Phase.

VICTORY
When at least one player occupies five Lands, the game finishes at the end of that Round. 
If two or more players have at least five lands, then the player with the most lands wins.
If there is still a tie, then the player with the most money, minus any loans, wins the game.

THE NATIONS
The world has come together to form 4 main alliances, each one having a unique advantage in the game:

    Demeter Federation: an Eco-friendly society, whose efforts have resulted in an abundance of food resources.
    Dionysus Coalition: the scavengers of the world, rumor has it that they can smell Oil from a mile away.
    Hephaestus League: the steampunk nation, with a special affinity for Gold.
    Poseidon Empire: the sea power of the game, their motto: 'In a world of water, Ships are gods'.

description:
"The fragile peace between mages has always been kept by the legendary WarMage of High Bastion. From the Stone Seat, the authority of the WarMage over the mages of Elerania has been absolute and unchallenged for decades... until now.
Now the WarMage is dead, the world of mages is in chaos, and the Stone Seat stands empty--waiting for the most ambitious, most powerful mage to claim its glory..."

WarMage: Apprentice Edition is the first installment in the WarMage series, a stand-alone competitive card game in the style of Yu-Gi-Oh! or Magic: The Gathering, for two to nine players of all ages and suitable for challenging both beginners and veterans alike.
Summon your magical might and do battle against other aspiring mages, wielding communal spells from six unique disciplines of magic to launch attacks, hinder your opponents, empower yourself, and control the battlefield in a shifting, dynamic fight to be the last mage standing!

In this game, a myriad of spells from several themed disciplines of magic are drawn from a central, shared deck, and are played in a variety of ways from players' hands in an effort to defeat their opponents. In the basic game mode, the last player standing wins; several variant game modes can also be played, to change the rules of the game itself.

Jiku Kansho is a game about controlling the future by affecting the present and the past. Players choose and keep special properties which potentially affect the future. These properties would bring you more properties, avoid an unexpected future, or block your rivals. When the farthest future is reached, the player with the most valuable properties wins the game. However your aim isn't just to have more and more properties. The value of a property will be chosen by the future. So, choose your future!

What's the meaning of the title Jiku-Kansho?
The direct translation of the title is 'Interfere of Time and Space'. Jiku = Time and Space, Kansho = Interfere.

Die Glasstraße: Oktoberfest is a small expansion that allows its owner to immediately remove any number of landscape tiles (i.e., Pits, Groves and Ponds) from their board that are adjacent to this building. Gain 4 water per removed Pond. Gain 4 Quartz sand per removed Pit. Gain 4 wood per removed Grove. Rotate the Production wheels after every removal if necessary.

This expansion, originally available as a promotional item at SPIEL '14, appears in the Glass Road base game starting with the 2021 editions.

This early Chess variant was proposed by Italian Chess scholar Francesco Piacenza in his book I Campeggiamenti negli Scacchi as Arciscacchiere, and then given its English name Archchess by John Gollon in an unpublished book on Chess variants.

The game is played on a board of 10x10 squares. In addition to the usual Chess pieces and two more pawns, each player also has one centurion, starting in the fourth column, and one decurion, starting in the seventh. The centurion jumps to any square two squares distant, either in a straight line or changing direction like a knight. The decurion moves a single square in any diagonal direction, like the fers of Shatranj.

The options and restrictions regarding promotion, castling and en passant are slightly different from regular Chess and/or slightly ambiguous. For instance, pawns are promoted when reaching the last row and always promote to queens.

As documented by Harold Murray and John Gollon, Shatranj Kamil was an early and largely unsuccessful attempt to expand the game of Shatranj. The board has been enlarged from 8x8 to 10x10, and in addition to two more pawns, each player is given two of a new piece, the camel, which jumps two spaces in a straight orthogonal line (i.e. it is an orthogonal counterpart to the elephant and significantly weaker than the rook). The camels have variously been placed in the corners, in the fourth and seventh columns, and possibly in other configurations. The rules and pieces are otherwise the same as in Shatranj. The word "kamil" does not refer to the camel piece, but instead means "perfect" or "complete".

This game should not be confused with Tamerlane Chess, which is also referred to as Shatranj Kamil; most likely there were several other variants being ascribed the status of higher perfection.

boardgame: Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #17
description:
CWC Berlin 2014 Special is a small expansion of the Settlers of Catan base game, given to contestants at the 2014 Catan World Championship.

After distributing the remaining terrain hexes and number tokens according to the game rules, the Berlin hex "Brandenburg Gate" replaces the desert and the Berlin hex "Museum for Communication" replaces the hex that received the 2 token; the 2 token is removed from the game.  The "Berlin Bear" replaces the robber (malicious gossip has it that Berlin's heraldic animal is collecting resources for the completion of the Berlin airport).

When a 7 is rolled, the resulting actions are carried out according to the normal rules.  Afterwards, each player with a settlement or city at a corner of a Berlin hex receives the resource produced by the terrain hex the "Berlin bear" was moved to.  A player who has various settlements or cities adjacent to a Berlin hex still only gets 1 resource.

スターライトステージ基本セット (Starlight Stage) is a card game in which players produce newcomer idols who have various talents into top celebrity idols and collect popularity points.

There are three types of card decks on the table: 
Idol cards have combinations of three talent types (note, diamond, and heart) and popularity points. 
Produce cards have popularity points. 
Event cards have special resources necessary to get more talented idol cards.

Each player have three initial idol cards of single talent of each type. The start player plays one of idol cards to either 
  (1) take a lesson and acquire a single talent chip, 
  (2) get an event card, only for this action multiple idol cards can be played, 
  (3) get a produce card, 
  (4) replace the played idol card by a new idol card from the table.

Each card has acquire costs which is paid by talents of played idol card, resources from previously acquired event cards, and additional talent chips. Then the next player plays his idol card. When every player take turns, the round ends and each player reclaim played idol cards, and the next round begins with the start player shifted to the next player.

At the end of round when the produce card deck is expired, the game ends and the player with the most popularity points from his/her idol cards and produce cards combined wins the game.

•••

アイドルプロデュースカードゲーム『スターライトステージ』は、
アイドル事務所のブロデューサーとなって、新人アイドル達を個性豊かに成長させ、様々な仕事に挑戦させてたくさん人気を集める事を目指すゲームです。

それぞれのプレイヤーの元には、新人アイドル、新人女優、新人モデルの3人のアイドルがいます。
彼女たちの音楽・演技・魅力の才能を使い、イベントをこなして実績を獲得しましょう。
実績を集めれば、より才能と人気の高いアイドルに成長できます。
アイドルが成長したら、プロデュースに挑戦して賞を獲得したり大舞台を成功させて人気を得ましょう！
1番人気を集めたプレイヤーの勝利となります。
あなたのアイドル達と、目指せ、名プロデューサー！

description:
In the game you play as a Carousel animal who wants to escape the Merry Go Round and become a free animal. the winner is the first to reach the exit on the board and the freedom of the outside world.

The game is made of 3 phases.

phase 1: 
in each turn you get 5 movement tickets and 2 special cards. You now plan haw to move. you can play 3 movement tickets and 1 special ticket each turn. Each tickets allow you to move one coloured tile, in the colour of the movement ticket. When you are in front of a coloured tile corosponding to your carousel animal, you can move down.

phase 2: Here the carousel will spin, each circle of the carousel spin one tile clockwise.

Phase 3: now you carry out your planned movement, you move all of your planned movement at once. Each player does this by turn.

boardgame: Three Kingdoms:  A Strategy Card Game
description:
Three Kingdoms is a turn-based, strategy card game in which players - playing as one of the major kingdoms/factions - compete to control the most spaces on the board.  Using elements of both Hand Management and Area Control, players are rewarded for both their ability to optimize actions and by strategically placing Unit Cards to gain points.  Some luck is involved through both drawing cards and unit placement, but players who can master their decks can dominate the battlefield..

Each kingdom/faction contains a set of 18 cards.  Unit Cards are placed on the Board and determine the end of game (once all of the spaces are occupied, the game is over) and the scoring (capturing other units or occupying spaces.  Strategy Cards allow for creating combinations and rule bending.  The 'Board' is made up of Terrain Cards and can be configured to any shape or design - presenting a new set of challenges, potential strategies, and time limit for each new game.

Players are limited to 1 action per turn: Draw 1 Card or Play 1 Card. But, certain cards allow for the creation of combinations and special rule bending - creating a lot of nuance and strategy as the game progresses.  TK is based on the Three Kingdoms period of China and the historical-fiction book, 'Romance of the Three Kingdoms,' by Luo Guanzhong.

boardgame: Samurai Conquest: A Solitaire Game of Japan's Wars of Unification.
description:
BACKGROUND: Samurai Conquest is a semi-historical simulation of Japan’s Wars of Unification. The Player assumes the role of the head of the Tokugawa Clan during the later decades of Japan’s Wars of Unification (1540-1600). As the Leader of the Tokugawa Clan, you have the powerful ambition of conquering your Clan Rivals and unifying all of Japan’s Provinces under one ruler, you. Will you be able to fulfil your destiny to become the Shogun of Japan?

GAME OBJECTIVE: The Player takes the role of the Daimyo of the Tokugawa Clan and must muster his forces and manage his assets to defeat the rival Clans. The Player must control a number of Settlements whose Fortification Values (FV) total 60 or more by the time the last Campaign Card is played. If the Player controls a number of Settlements whose FV total is 20 or less during the Victory Check Phase, the Player loses.

GAME FEATURES: Samurai Conquest features a 4-Sheet Map, lots of colorful game counters, Campaign Cards, a Battle Mat for resolving Battles and much more. Units include Ashigaru Spearmen, Teppo (Arquebusiers) Infantry, Samurai Foot, Samurai Cavalry and individual Samurai Champions.

If you love movies like Ran, Kagemushi, The Hidden Throne, and Heaven and Earth, you will enjoy this game.

Samurai Conquest can be found at the following URL:
http://www.berserkergames.net

description: The players start at the entrance (sun) to the labyrinth. The first to reach the exit (moon) wins the game. Movement is determined by a number-die (number of spaces to go) and a color-die (direction). The symbol-die is used only on a few special spaces. As usual the rules for the numbered spaces are explained in the game rules.

The players insert numbered tokens into the fox's den. The entrance they use (1-6) is determined by a die. If they push another token out of the den, they get a coin from the owner of the token. If they are the owner of the pushed out token, they pay a coin into the bank. If a player pushes the hound out of the den, he has to pay 2 coins into the bank. The Player, who pusches out the fox, winns the game and the bank.

The game was published in the 1920s or 1930s.

This new black (shadow army) and white (spirit army) expansion contains two complete King Down armies. This expansion also includes the Keepers of the King expansion miniatures and chess booster miniatures for the shadow and spirit army.

The MaVericks is a Kickstarter-exclusive expansion that adds 8 new cards.

The MaVericks will be shuffled into the main Agents deck and will cost both your actions to play from your hand. They will not award IPs to your opponents. Once used, they are placed face-up on the table, for any player to re-activate and use on their turn (again, using up both actions.)

In Amino, players compete to create Amino acids out of the primordial ooze and early atmospheres of their planets. Collecting useful molecules like water, ammonia, and methane, avoid unhelpful molecules like diatomic nitrogen and hydrogen, and add other effects to your atmosphere like additional UV radiation or lightning.

The game is played with all placed cards face down, so you never know how well your atmosphere compares to your neighbors until the round is over. Use your allotted action points each turn to draw cards, place new cards, or even swap cards between players! The player with the most total points after a set number of rounds is the winner!

Numinoes is a strategic board game inspired by Othello (Reversi), Dominoes and modern games.

The starting position is two tiles from each player laid on the board (d4, e5 and d5, e4). The players take turn where they try turn a line by lining up with one of their tiles separated by the opponent's tiles. The players play until there is no more moves possible and the winner is the one who has the highest score calculated from the values of the tiles.

Extra tiles are special moves designed to change the tide in one or another's player favour. They are :stop, :swirl, :gold, :bomb and :star (described below).

A stop tile prevents an opponent to turn all tiles in a line up until the stop tile (included) rather than until the opponent’s bounding tile.

A swirl tile turns all the opponent’s tiles aside in all 8 directions plus all playable line up.

A gold tile behaves like stop tile but cannot be turn ever. It can be laid down at the beginning to add a handicap.

A bomb tile explodes the tiles aside in all 8 directions regardless of the colour. The tile itself can stay on the board for an added handicap.

A star tile gives extra points (e.g. 5 points) and cannot be turned ever but will not prevent a line up to turn.

boardgame: Dungeon Roll Legends: Hero Booster Pack #2
description:
Dungeon Roll Legends: Hero Booster Pack #2 is a set of eight extra heroes, each with its own special and ultimate powers, for use with Dungeon Roll.  The heroes in this pack are already leveled up and more powerful than the previous heroes from the base game and expansions, but are balanced against each other and should only be pitted against one another for a fair game, or put up against previous heroes to give some players a handicap.

The new heroes include:

     Alexandra, Keeper of Knowledge
     Amar-Suen, King of the Ancients
     Cassandra, The Cursed
     Loes Yllavyre, Dragon Lord
     Marpesia, Amazon Queen
     Tou'lak, Minotaur Chieftan
     Tristan, Lone Mercenary
     Szopin, Infamous Warlord

In Tome of Calling, 2 to 4 players summon mighty monsters to fight for supremacy. Unleash the brutal force of a Golden Dragon or destroy your friends' creatures using more cunning ways. Very easy to learn and quick to play, yet complex enough to keep you engaged in the long run.

Rules summary
Every player draws three cards. Active player draws a card and plays it. If the deck or one player runs out of cards, scoring takes place. Winner of a round is the one with the most points on hand cards summed up. Overall victory is achieved by winning 3 rounds.

All additional rules are found on the cards.

Chroma Roll is an abstract dice-rolling game that consists of 29 clear rings and 54 tokens in three colors (red, blue and green).  The tokens and rings are placed on a hexagonal grid before the start of the game, with rings sitting alone on a space or in stacks of two or three, and tokens randomly placed in stacks of three on spots on the game board. Hexes on the board have colored edges (in red, green or blue). Rings are moved by rolling three dice (also in red, green and blue) and moving the rings by selecting a die of a particular color and moving a ring over the hex edges of the same color as many hexes as the die shows (1-6). The object of the game is to collect colored tokens in sets, scoring points at the end of the game for each set collected.

Roll a Ringer is an abstract dice-rolling game that consists of 29 clear rings and 54 tokens in three colors (red, blue and green).  The tokens and rings are placed on a hexagonal grid before the start of the game, with rings sitting alone on a space or in stacks of two or three, and tokens randomly placed in 18 stacks of three on the spots on the board, with six in each of the game colors.  Hexes on the board have colored edges (in red, green or blue).  Rings or tokens are moved by rolling three dice (also in red, green and blue) and moving the ring or token by selecting a die of a particular color and moving a ring over the hex edges of the same color as many hexes as the die shows (1-6).  The object of the game is to collect colored tokens in sets, by either moving a ring over a stack of three tokens, or moving tokens into stacks of at least three rings.

CardBots: Build & Destroy is a customizable mech battle card game.

Use resources granted by tech cards to fill slots and build out a 2D mech, directly on your tabletop. Then utilize game altering special abilities and powerful weapons to destroy your opponents!

Game rules, card gallery, and a free full version of the game can be downloaded as a print & play pack at http://www.cardbotsccg.com

EDIT: Link not working anymore, you can find the game on http://www.geremygeorge.com/cardbots

Pathogen: Z takes place in 2057 after GenTech, a genetic research company, released a pathogen onto a small town as leverage to use its new antigen on human test subjects. Things quickly turned bad as the virus mutated and became immune to the existing antigen. Rather then accept responsibility, GenTech sat back hoping the virus would stop spreading.

Five years later, and nearly 75% of the worlds population has been affected. Some have been reanimated from the dead, others gruesomely mutated into something that no longer resembles a human life form. Pick your side. GenTech? Rogue military? Civilian? Street Gang? Private security? Government? Scientist?

Build your deck and try to survive one more day.

Game Play

Players must construct decks from predefined Base Decks along with optional expander packs to customize their chosen deck. All packs contain no randomization or rarity. Similar CCG themes are present such as playing cards from your hand, drawing from a draw pile and having cards discarded from play through various mechanics.

Various card mechanics allow players to draw additional cards, reduce an opponents hand, introduce survivors (your team), use zombies and mutants to harm your opponent and use defensive barriers and equipment to protect your survivors while trying to defeat your opponent.

Each faction has team members which have skill levels in certain skills. These skill levels give them access to "doing" things through playing cards which match their required skill levels.

For example, a Geneticist has a Skill Level of 3 and can create more powerful mutants then say a Lab Assistant with a Skill Level of only 1.

The end game is to defeat the opposing faction by using your survivors and equipment along with their abilities to either control zombies, engineer mutants or simply use brute force in this post-apocalyptic card game!

The players have to get their 4 meeples from their starting spaces (marked with a "+") to the center of the game board and the spaces of their color, marked 1 to 4. The first to do so, wins the game. Movement is determined a die. The players may chose to use the red symbol-die instead of the black number-die. On the roll of a dot, all meeples on a danger-space ringed with dots or dots and crosses, have to return to the first space on the track. On the roll of a cross, the same goes for meeples on a danger-space ringed with crosses or dots and crosses.

A nagy sikerű, sokak által rongyosra játszott Beugró társasjátékot rengeteg új jelenettel, zsebszöveggel, hangulat-
kártyával töltöttük újra, hogy így megújulva ismét kihagyhatatlan kelléke lehessen baráti összejöveteleknek, osztálytalálkozóknak, családi együttléteknek.

A Beugró - újratöltve az eredeti játék hat feladatához tartalmaz újabb szituációkat és kártyákat.

This game is based on a Hungarian TV-show, where famous actors improvise in strange situations, for example they can only ask questions, or each of them gets a number and is allowed to say sentences containing exactly that many words. In this game the players do the same.

This is the first expansion of the original game.

Upon reaching his 100th birthday Head Wizard Zin has decided to step down and pass the torch, or in this case, staff.  Being such an amazing instructor and father figure he has many great wizards ready to assume his position.  Not wanting to show favoritism among his top pupils, he’s offered all of them a chance to face off against one another for the title.

Not only do they get the title and the coveted seat, they also get the respect that goes with being the possessor of the IRON STAFF!

In Iron Staff you will assume the role of a wizard in the Copper Claw Guild.  You will also be attempting to show your magical prowess by gathering components to complete and cast spells to subdue your opponent.  This is not an easy task however.  The other wizards are just as skilled and cunning as you.  They all have tricks and specialties up their sleeves. Each of the wizards has their own set of special abilities,variable hit points, and mastery (second ability trigger) levels.

description:
Majdan is trying to simulate events which took place in Ukraine in January and February 2014 during what is called an “Euromaidan”. The players create the political situation of this state on their own. Depending on their strategy, the paths of Ukrainians future may develop in many different ways. Nevertheless, it must be remembered that the moral loser will always be a faction which decided for the force solution fighting for control over districts as the first and allowing the blood to be spoiled.

The goal of each faction is to gain as many Victory Points as possible, i.e. retain the power in hands of Government and his supporters or to create a new government by the opposition.

Victory Points are scored by taking Control or getting Support in 25 districts, which differs in value. When two factions meet in one district there is a  Struggle (for Support or Control). To win a Struggle, players use Cards which portrays means used to win: Masses (supporters of Government or Euromaidan), Militia, Berkut, Army, Specnaz, Media or Titushki. Cards have different values (value part of them is defined by a dice roll). For example, Media has value 5 in Struggle for Support, and 0 in Struggle for Control.

Players has several types of action to choose: get a Card, initiate a Struggle, influence a district, make an peace offer. 5 Actions made an Action Round. 6 Action Rounds makes an complete game (unless someone get to automatic victory earlier).

Majdan reimplements Pomarańczowa Rewolucja game - mechanics was slightly changed, as district values on the map. Cards was changed (new images and its quantity).

All changes were fit to the 2013/2014 political situation on Ukraine.

Dungeon Talk (DT) has been described as a hilarious mix of Dixit, Apples to Apples, Munchkin, and with a sprinkle of story telling fantasy RPG. DT can be learned in 5 minutes and played in under 30 minutes with endless replayability.

Dungeon Talk is a 2-6 player fantasy party game for family, adults, and kids and comes with over 280 game cards with 12,000 variations for endless re-playability.

Basic Game Overview: In the basic game, a new fantasy picture is revealed (each picture is on a huge 6x4 card). Players choose a Talk Card from their hand which they feel best represents the picture, is the most silly, or drums up the most laughs. One player acts as the Adventure Master, which rotates every turn, reads each Talk Card aloud then chooses a Talk Card he/she likes most. This continues every turn until a player's Talk Card is chosen three times!

Advanced Game Overview:  After choosing your hero (each having a unique game power), you'll draw 5 Dungeon Talk cards (talk cards have funny sayings, quotes, etc on them).

Talk cards are used by you to describe an adventure card (a fantasy scene with some really cool art). Each turn a new adventure card is drawn from the current Act deck of your quest. The acts are The Fellowship, Journey, and Destination.

Each turn a new player acts as the Adventure Master (AM). Players, except the AM, chooses a talk card from their hand and plays it face down.

The AM then draws a random talk card from the talk card deck and shuffles it in with the players' chosen talk cards. He reads aloud each talk card and chooses one he/she feels best describes the picture, is the funniest, most outrageous, drums up the most laughs, and so on.

If a player's card is chosen he gains a level and loot (loot can be in the form of gold coins, equipment,potions, spells, or weapons which can be used to aid yourself or hinder others).

If the random card was chosen, one player is cursed (a random negative effect is played on him). Throughout the game, players can use their special hero power and unique items they've earned to help themselves or hinder other players getting too close to winning!

The game ends when a player reaches glorious level 5! Whomever has accumulated the most gold wins! There's a few more rules but that's the general gist of Dungeon Talk.

Retrieved from http://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/67991987

The Advocate, Burnie, Tasmania, Australia, 31 Dec. 1932.

				
				
					CONTRACT WHIST.No Hands Down.
WEAKNESS SOLVED.

Why a four-handed game of cards such as the various forms of bridge should be played by three persons is one of those mysteries that have never been explained. Probably this is because there is no explanation. It seems absurd that one of the four players should have to stand out of every game.

And if the game is one of skill, as is confidently asserted, its value is much diminished by the exposure of one of the hands.

In whist each player knew only what was in his own hand. He had to use his reason or his imagination to decide what was in the hands of his partner or of his two opponents.

				
				
					Bridge Without the Dummy.In the various forms of bridge his partner's hand is exposed once the bidding is over. Each player knows two hands, and so much of the real interest of the game is lost.

It is these things that have led to the invention of contract whist, or "bridge without the dummy," as set out by Hubert Phillips in "Contract Whist."

This is distinctly a whist game, since each player plays his own hand. There is no exposure of one hand, as in bridge. It is this, the author claims, which gives the game its special fascination. And, indeed, it is a sensible idea.

On the other hand, the bidding and scoring follow the lines of contract bridge, with changes and simplification.

As there is no recognised organisation to frame the rules of contract whist, the author has done it himself. He does not, however, claim to have invented the game. He says that the idea of bridge without the dummy has existed for years. The rule that only tricks actually contracted for can count towards the game is retained. The things rejected are, "Vulnerability, the bidding of slams, and unnecessarily complicated scoring."

				
				
					The Scoring.The scoring suggested is:

(1) A game is secured by the first side that scores 10 points. Rubber is the best of three games.

(2) Points count toward game only when scored "below the line." These are points for tricks which the declaring side has contracted to make. Tricks at No Trumps count 4 points each. Tricks at a suit declaration count 3 points each. Tricks made by the declarers, but not contracted for are scored above the line, and count only 2 each. Where a contract has been doubled or redoubled, the value of each trick is doubled or redoubled. Over tricks are 5 each doubled, and 10 redoubled.

(3) Where the contract is defeated the defending side scores [above the line] 10 points for each trick which the declaring side has failed to make. If the contract has been doubled the deficiency score is 20 points; if redoubled 40.

(4) Nothing is scored for honors.

(5) The side winning the rubber receives 50 points.

				
				
					Rubber of 100 Points.The average rubber is about 100 points in all.
While the score is the same for all suit declarations, the preference in bidding is the same as in bridge--spades, hearts, diamonds, clubs.

Contract whist differs from bridge in that the play cannot be planned by the declarer from the outset.

The author gives many illustrations which suggest that contract whist could be made a most fascinating game.

This single-tile mini-expansion can be added to any Carcassonne game. The design, however, matches that of the New Edition of Carcassonne (2014).
This promo was given out (for free) when making a purchase at the Hans im Glück booth at Spiel 2014, Essen. Part of the Carcassonne series.

boardgame: Garbage Pail Kids: Super Snot Shots
description:
Garbage Pail Kids: Super Snot Shots supports 2-4 players and is a dexterity game that involves flicking dice (that represent snot) at a target. Each player chooses one of the four snot-covered Leaky Lindsay cards and a six-sided die of the same color, then takes a position around a target card. The cards in the game are all squares with rounded edges; they feature eleven Garbage Pail kids, all featuring classic Garbage Pail Kids art, everyone from Surf's Up Chuck to Dead Ted to Mark Barf.

You make a draw pile out of the cards and select one to be a target, placing it in the middle of everyone and placing your Leaky Lindsay card two card-lengths away. Once set-up is complete, count down "3, 2, 1, SHOOT!", then flick your die from your Lindsay card towards the target card, trying to aim it so that your die stops on top of it. If only one player gets her die on the target card, she claims it; if two or more dice land on the card, then whoever has the highest number showing on her die wins it; if no one lands on the card, then it's a tie and everyone shoots again!

The game continues until no cards remain, and whoever has the most cards wins.

This mini-expansion consists of:

    8 Spell cards
    5 Fate cards

Pocket Tennis is a fast dice game that simulates a match of tennis. It's a very light dice game that contains custom made dice for serving, baseline shots, net shots, attacking shots and a unique Bird Eye Challenge die.

The game rules also include an achievement progress Wall of Fame. Leave your name in history as you win games and proceed to the next spot to write your name!

Easy to play for any tennis fan. Fast, furious grand slamming Pocket Tennis action!

boardgame: Captoons: The Game of Comic Foreplay and Social Intercourse
description: Captoons is a game for seemingly mature, yet frisky adults, that has been called “the most fun you can have with your clothes on!”  And it’s easy.  Each player simply matches a caption card chosen from their hand to the cartoon in play.  The Card Captain, randomly chosen, then announces what he feels is the funniest pairing.  And that’s where the fun really starts.  In a game featuring 450 captions, 80 cartoons and 36,000 possible pairings!!, the Card Captain’s choice may be about as popular as a fart in a hermetically sealed space suit, leading to rather spirited and hilarious interactions between the players.

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 1 – Justice Society of America
description:
The DC Comics Deck-building Game Crossover Pack series is a way to breathe new life into your game with just a few tweaks. Grab your favorite DC set and instead of using the Super Heroes and Super-Villains from that set, use the ones from this pack instead! Playing as a new JSA Super Hero will add new strategies and goals to your gameplay. New Super-Villains will spice up the First Appearance Attacks that players have grown to love and fear. Finally, a few signature Super Powers, Equipment, Heroes, Villains, and even a Location for the main deck will add a little bit of extra JSA flavor to the set.

Crossover Pack #1 features the Justice Society of America, usually known simply as the JSA. These classic Super Heroes have been around for a long time and often claim top talent authors and artists, such as the worldfamous Alex Ross! You will find several pieces of his work in this very set. Their stories continue to this day and feature characters extremely important to the DC lore, such as Doctor Fate and Power Girl.

Naturally, these Super Heroes will be fighting against their own stable of classic Super-Villains, such as Solomon Grundy, Eclipso, and Ultra-Humanite! The JSA flavor continues with some of the Super Heroes’ own abilities added to the main deck: Mystic Bolts and T-Spheres.

Crossover Packs are the perfect way for players to customize their DC Comics experience to suit their own tastes and to play with their favorite characters!

description: Spot it! Fire & Ice pits players against an electric timer. Race to spot matches and beat the buzzer, while looking for your chance to freeze and burn your opponents. Features more than 50 different images of hot and cold objects.

Paper Toss is a fast, fun, new card game where players compete to deliver the most newspapers on their paper route.

Paper Toss can be played by 2 to 6 players in anywhere from 10 to 30 minutes (more players make longer games), and the rules are simple and easy to learn, suitable for ages 5 and up.

The comical art and theme, coupled with the silly, competitive nature of the game make it great for younger children and even the whole family.

Draw cards from the blue "Paper Deck," and take turns trying to toss your newspaper on a new doormat (by playing the "Paper Toss" card), as other players try to throw off your aim with hazards like angry dogs, sidewalk obstacles, and neighborhood kids. If you have the appropriate counter card, you can ignore the distraction.

A simple discard mechanic adds a subtle layer of strategy: choose which cards to hold or discard, keeping your defensive cards, or going on the offense. Wild cards like "Flat Tire!" and "Bully!" help you stick it to your opponent and gain an upper hand.

Once you throw your paper, turn over a card from the green "Toss Deck," to see how good your throw was. With every successful paper toss, you gain a Paper Token. The first player to collect 10 Tokens wins the game! Can you be the first one to toss all your papers?

You and your opponent each command a tiny army of five soldiers. Each round, you and your opponent simultaneously choose one of your soldiers to fight. However, not every soldier is created equal--some are stronger than others, and some have special abilities. Can you anticipate your opponent's move and choose a soldier that will beat his? Will he make the "obvious" choice, or will he pull a bluff...or a double-bluff? Win the war by defeating all of your opponent's soldiers, or having more remaining soldiers when the war is over.

This game requires one card (included in the download) and three hands--one of yours, one belonging to a friend, and another one of yours to hold the card. No tabletop needed.

You no longer have to wait for good weather now, because with WeyKick Mini Golf the beloved sport comes into the house. With the help of a club, the ball must be put in the with as few attempts. For 1-5 players. Includes an attractive wooden game field with 5 distinct minigolf holes, 1 stainless steel club, 2 stainless steel balls, and one scorepad. (Adapted from the publisher's description.)

Black Stories: Ärztlicher Rat is a promotional card celebrating the tenth anniversary of Game: Black Stories. The card was given out for free at SPIEL 2014 in Essen as an advance publication from Black Stories 11. However, Black Stories 11 ended up never being published afterwards, the publisher 
moses. Verlag GmbH decided to call the next generic Black Stories edition after Black Stories 10 Black Stories 13.

This is a Medieval Academy expansion. These variants require the Medieval Academy game. All the basic rules apply except for the modifications described in the rules that come with this expansion. You can play in the same time with as many variants as you want.

This expansion is a single board with 'Gallantry' on one side and 'Magic' on the other.

Gallantry Board
In disc’s order on the Gallantry side of the board, each squire chooses and applies one or two bonus. He places his disc on chosen bonuses,in order to make those bonus unavailable for the others squires. On less than 4 squires games, only first and second squires earn bonus.

Magic Board
As on Gallantry board, on Magic board, squires don’t directly win Chivalry points, but Magic board allows them to move forward on any board. On scoring, (turns 2 and 5, after Gallantry scoring), only the 3 first ranked squires (or the 2 first ranked in 3 players games) earn cases bonus; they could move forward their own discs by a number of cases according to their position on Magic board.  The move can be split on one or several boards.

Move order:
1)First, the 3rd ranked squire on Magic board.
2)After the 2nd ranked squire on Magic board.
3)Finally, the first ranked squire on Magic board.

There is a reminder on the top of the Magic board:
At the end of Turn 2, after the scoring phase, all the discs go back to square 0 on Magic board.

boardgame: Poop: The Game – The Expansion Deck
description: New cards make POOP playable for up to 10 players! All new Wild Cards! POOP is an UNO-like game where the first player to run out of cards wins! Take turns pooping but don't clog the toilet! Some cards make players perform crazy acts.

Rack 'em up and stack 'em up with this multi-level version that's tic tac terrific! The objective is still to get 3 in a row horizontally or diagonally - the new challenge is to get 3 in a row vertically too. Develops problem-solving, logical thinking and spatial awareness.

boardgame: Roll Through the Ages: The Iron Age with Mediterranean Expansion
description:
Roll Through the Ages: The Iron Age, a sequel to the highly-awarded Roll Through the Ages: The Bronze Age, lets you build an Iron Age civilization in under an hour! Do you build provinces, raise armies, and conquer barbarians or build ports and ships to gain trade goods? Explore the strategies of Greece, Phoenicia, and Rome as you erect monuments, fend off disasters, and strive to feed your people.

Roll Through the Ages: The Iron Age gives players different ways to build their empires: the Trade and Naval strategies of the Phoenicians, the conquests of Alexander the Great, and the engineering prowess and gradual absorption of new provinces by the Roman Republic.

Some copies of the game include the Roll Through the Ages: The Iron Age – The Mediterranean Expansion in the base game box. The only indication of this was a sticker on the shrink that read "MED expansion" (and those boxes are heavier).

The Mediterranean expansion adds overseas settlements to the mix via a central pegboard that all players share. Players race to reap the benefits of strategic colonies: Crete for fleets; Spain for mining; Carthage for wheat; Gaul for armies; while Syracuse, the home of Archimedes, and Alexandria provide vital innovations.

The Mediterranean expansion is now available separately from Eagle Games, or as part of a new edition of the game. When sold on its own, the expansion does not contain rules or the updated score pads that include expansion scoring.

boardgame: Merry Christmas!: The Hand-Drafting Card Game
description:
You are a member of HOHOHO – the Helping Out & Happiness Optimization Holiday Organization - an elite group of elves operating out of Santa’s North Pole Headquarters.  Your mission, if you choose to accept it, is to help Santa bring joy to all the good girls and boys.   You’ll coordinate the efforts of busy workshop elves, powerful flying reindeers, and even the big man himself.  Take the right actions and you’ll bring joy to the world and ensure that everyone has a Merry Christmas!

Each player drafts a hand of twelve actions, ten of which will be played throughout the game's three phases.  Elves are used in the Workshop Phase to craft toys from the list.  Reindeer are used during the Sleigh Phase to ensure the toys are delivered to excited children.  Items are placed in the Tree Phase to elevate the atmosphere of joy and wonder. Santa makes frequent appearances throughout the game, offering his help along the way.  As Christmas comes to a close, players are scored based on the toys delivered and the joy those toys inspire.

description:
Two promo character cards for King's Pouch:

    The Painter gives victory points depending on the number of citizen colors in the active area.
    The Physician assumes the role of any other character during the Great Council.

The Hochzeitsturm (Wedding Tower) is a new tile to replace the desert tile. If a 7 is rolled the usual rule applies but also every player with a settlement or city next to the Hochzeitsturm gains one resource of where the robber is placed.

A set of 6 special cards and a rule sheet with a 2-player variant for the game.

There is one special card for 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8 of a kind. A player who plays down any of these combinations, may take that card (or steal it from another player). Each card is worth 5 points at game end.

The rules sheet offers a "Duel" variant for two players.

Promo card Monarchy from the german magazine "Spielerei" issue no. 105 (Oct.-Dec. 2014)
 The achievement Monarchy has a new edge with black color. It is the only achievement of level 5. No black card may be replaced by an achievement. Black cards are not immune from the effects of disasters. In addition to a black card no settlement can be set because the resources are not available. The favor of Nergal does not help on black cards. However, it is possible to set settlements in front of black cards by using the "scale". A black card can replace an achievement that has upside a village or town. Black cards don't count as a color within the meaning of the condition of civilization cards (eg. "disease").
To play a black card, the player needs either more black cards or a corresponding number of color pairs or the favor Nannars. Playing "monarchy" also has to be considered the rule of stages, ie it must already be an achievement of level 4.

Function of the Monarchy promo-card:
With the promo card "monarchy" lying ahead the player receives one additional victory point.

boardgame: A Call to Arms Rules Supplement 1
description:
The A Call to Arms Rules Supplement series brings you an array of new rules, information, articles and ships. It is an invaluable resource for any A Call to Arms player and will change the way you play your games!

Within Supplement 1 you will find wide variety of new articles designed to expand and develop the way you play A Call to Arms. Whether it is new rules for incorporating hyperspace and space stations, advanced fighter combat, or an in-depth study of the Centauri Maximus frigate, you will find something to get your teeth into. Scenarios and campaigns haven't been forgotten either, and you will find and exciting Raider campaign ready to run as well! With the Second Battle of Quadrant 14 scenario plus updates of those in the main rulebook, Supplement 1 is packed with great additions for A Call to Arms!

boardgame: A Call to Arms Rules Supplement 3
description:
The A Call to Arms Rules Supplement series brings you an array of new rules, information, articles and ships. It is an invaluable resource for any A Call to Arms player and will change the way you play your games!

Within Supplement 3 you will find wide variety of new articles designed to expand and develop the way you play A Call to Arms. With complete fleet listings for the Vree Conglomerate ande the aggressive Drazi Freehold as well as a scenario pitting the Army of Light against the Vorlons, plus massively expanded Refit tables, A Call to Arms Rules Supplement 3 is not to be missed!

Drive on Baghdad: 2003 is a solitaire game in which the player takes command of Central Command Forces during Operation Iraqi Freedom. Your goal is to defeat Saddam Hussein’s forces in such a manner as to prevent a post-campaign insurgency from breaking out.

The system plays the Iraqis. You will have to deal with a variety of situations, such as: battling Republican Guard divisions, safeguarding your lines of supply, sandstorms, etc., all the while keeping up the momentum in your drive on Baghdad.

The game has two levels: Standard Rules, which give a quick-play game, and the Optional Rules, which add more realism at the expense of increased complexity.

While this is a solitaire game, more than one can play as a team, making decisions by consensus, or with different players managing operations along each vector of Advance.

Components: One 22" x 34" map & 176 counters

Modern War #20 - Nov Dec 2015

Hyperborea Promo is a promotional mini-expansion that includes four cards:

Ring of Favor is a promotional mini-expansions for Alchemists. It consists of a single artifact card, with the following text:

         You can double the effect of a favor card you play. Limit once per round.

boardgame: Battle of Golems: The Algorithmic Card Game
description:
The first tabletop game that allows you to simply and easily learn programming and robotics without a computer (with conditions, loop, logic, subprogram etc), and to enjoy discovering exciting strategies! For 2 to 4 players, aged 8 years and older (but played from 5-6+ ages). Training of logic, memory, team interaction - all this in a simple, but deep in content, a board game. Play at home with children, in the company, at school and use it in STEM additional education.

Welcome to the Battle of Golems! Algorythmus archmage by Cybertania’s king’s order created Golems - clay robot warriors using coal as fuel. Due to the magic crystal in its head Golem can follow commands and perform short series of them called Programs. King also ordered to build a special field, which would be used for battles between warrior programmers and their Golems. You will take part in the tournament and prove that you’re the best robot engineer of Cybertania.

In 10 rounds each player set up the Program for his Robot-Golem from Action cards and executes it. The Damage tokens and Cards of Fortune will show you how many rounds and lives remain before the victory and whether you can apply Cycles, Conditions or other Bonuses when writing the Program.

You and your opponents in each round usually have four programming slots (depending on the type of Golem), so you need to take care of a short but effective algorithm. You can use both ready-made scenarios (more then 10+ including in Scenario book) and create your own rules, fields, and actions!

Equipment:

• 5 two-sided (summer-winter) board pieces: (4 3x3 sizes and 1 4x4 size) (Print&Play includes only 1 4x4 size)
• 4 Golems figures 
• 6 two-sided golem cards
• 76 action cards
• 12 Damage tokens for 4 players
• 22 two-sided obstacle (barriers) tokens + 8 logic tokens
• 40 fortune cards
• 3 Bots program cards
• Scenery book.
• Game rules.

Try to deliver your packet as soon as possible to mailman Johnny.

Each player chooses a card (indicating means of mail delivery transport) from his hand and passes it face down to his right of left neighbour (direction is given by Johnny the mailman). When all players have a hidden card in front of them, they turn the cards at the same time. The card in front of each player indicates which combination he/she should make. They have to see how much identical cards are revealed by other players, and how much identical cards they have on their hand. This indicates on which number they have to tap (2, 3 of 4) as soon as possible. If they see 5 identical cards, they shout "timbré" (=stamped).

Players who tapped rightfully at 2, 3 of 4 can take pieces of a packet. They get 2 cards (showing parts of a postpacket) from the deck, keep 1 and discard the other one. The player who tapped the 3 can make, after choising from two cards, an additional move: steal a card from an other player. The player who could tap the 4 can choose a piece (out of two cards), steal another and gets the mailman. He/she also decides in which direction the cards are given in the next round. The players who tapped wrongfully, must discard a card from their packet.

If a player can show 5 identical cards, he shouts "timbré". He'll be the only one playing this round. He takes 5 cards from the deck, keep 1 and discard 4, steals a card and takes the mailman. The other players must discard a piece (card) of their packet.

For the next round players take the card in front of them in their hands and play a hidden card to the right or left player, as isntructed by the (new) mailman.

As soon as a player had his 6 pieces (cards) of the packet AND the mailman, he/she wins!

The Zombie is a micro-game of cunning and bluff. Taking the concept of the famous Werewolf game (Mafia), the game features some child trying to escape a zombie invasion.

Players receive a card at the start of the round and they try to eliminate the "right" player depending on their role. Players will vote each round trying to "survive" as long as possible.

The Zombie is a fast game (about 5 minutes) of bluffing and deception, it’s the perfect «filler» for any group. The game contains only 4 cards and it take less than 1 minute to explain. The game is easy to transport, does not take space and can be played almost anywhere; in queues, in a bar, in the car, during a break.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 5: Quest Chambers
description:
Queenie 5 for Escape.  3 new quest chambers (chaos chamber, constellation chamber and snake pit) and 3 new curses (mind control, pitfall and magical bond)

Chaos Chamber - All adventurers must be in this chamber at the same time and draw 1 curse each to complete this task.

Constellation Chamber - All players must be in the corresponding chambers and roll 2 keys to complete the task. A sixth player need not do anything to complete this task.
1: The Constellation Chamber
2: The Starting Chamber
3: A Triple Gem Chamber
4: A Single Gem Chamber
5: The Exit Chamber

Snake Pit - The player who discovers the snake pit takes 1 snake token for every other adventurer and adds them to each of their chambers. First all token snakes must be defeated with 2 torches while in the same chamber as the token. After that has been completed, players must defeat the mother snake in the chamber using 5 torches to complete this task.

Mind Control - You are not allowed to touch your adventurer figure, the draw pile, or gems on the depot. Other players must move them for you.

Pitfall - Immediately place your adventurer figure back in the starting chamber. Then discard this curse.

Magical Bond - You must have 1 hand touching another player at all times.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 7: New Characters
description:
Queenie 7 for Escape.  2 new chambers (dungeon, narrow gauge), 2 new treasures (portal, clone) and 2 new characters (void runner, trickster)

Dungeon - Players cannot get back through the entrance of this chamber.

Narrow Gauge - The Narrow Gauge has 4 entries. Roll 3 torches to get in and 3 torches to activate a gem. It also has a treasure if playing with treasures.

Portal - Place your adventurer in any adjacent chamber - even diagonal.

Clone - Double all key and torch symbol for one action.

Void Runner:
Permanent - You may travel through an open doorway to another open doorway anywhere on the board with 2 rolled adventurers.
Activatable - You may take all adventurers in your chamber with you.

Trickster:
Permanent - You may physically exchange (1 to 1) any amount of dice with another player in your chamber.
Activatable - You may physically exchange (1 to 1) any amount of dice with another player in any chamber.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 6: The Maze
description:
Queenie 6 for Escape.  2 new chambers (mirage chamber, gear chamber), 2 new curses (weakest link, misinformation), 3 new treasures (short cut, time out, genie lamp)

Mirage Chamber - When the Mirage Chamber is revealed, it replaces the Starting Chamber and the Starting Chamber is placed where the Mirage Chamber would have gone.

Gear Chamber - While in the chamber, players must roll 2 torches to slide 1 row or column of chamber tiles 1 space in one direction. All chambers in the row or column must move.

Weakest Link - If you aren't back in the start chamber at the end of the countdown then you all lose the game. This curse can't be lifted.

Misinformation - Every time you discover a new chamber you have to yell "I found the exit" twice.

Short Cut - When discovering a new chamber, look through the whole draw pile and choose 1. Reshuffle when finished.

Time Out - Use to pause the timer/soundtrack and plan. No rolling while paused.

Genie Lamp - Draw the top 3 treasures from the stack and choose 1. Return the others to the bottom.

Expansion to Takamatsu

The green pagoda is placed on the lowest Shogun beside the game board, doubling the value of that card.

The green Samurai - a loner - of the game is placed at the beginning of the game in the room with the value +2. 
The green Samurai effectively blocks a room so that it cannot be entered. 
It automatically moves to the next free room.

A Fantasy Team Brawler Board Game. Play as one of seven unique characters in this exciting, easy to learn game of fantasy combat.

Guided by modern game design principles:

    No Player Elimination
    Simultaneous Turns
    One Hour Play Time
    Easy to Learn and Teach
    Deep Strategies


Game Play Highlights:


    Play on teams and use teamwork and strategy in head to head combat.
    Draft characters or deal them randomly.
    Build your character by choosing your abilities before the game starts.
    Play one ability per turn, simultaneously. You have access to all of your abilities every turn.
    Gain and spend "focus" using your ability cards.
    Reveal rune cards for bonus effects on the ability you play.
    Play a "level up" ability to add a wild card to your rune deck, giving you better odds on bonus effects for the rest of the game.
    Move and attack using your ability cards.
    Make meaningful choices about how to spend focus such as:
        Tactical Movement
        Fast, high damage attacks
        Level up for a late game strategy

Two extra dice, silver plated, limited to 200 sets, with the following rules:
"Yellow dice increase the tension in the auction: You can replace the yellow dice with the metal-plated dice and add both of the yellow dice to the bag at the beginning of the game.
This is only recommended with 3 or 4 players.
When a yellow die is pulled out of the bag, it is immediately removed from the game.
Therfore, for each yellow die pulled from the bag, there will be one less group of dice created for that particular auction (and two groups fewer if the two yellow dice come out at the same time).

There also are sets of 2 "Copper" dice, limited to 300, and sets of "Aluminum", limited to 400.

Big Time Hoops is a fast-playing, card-based simulation of professional basketball for two players. To recreate the flow of the game and remove "housekeeping" altogether, the game eschews individual cards for basketball players and focuses exclusively on the major action elements of the sport.

In general, you'll hold a hand of three cards, most of which will be offensive in nature. You'll play one of them, choosing to pass the ball or drive to the basket; each card will result in a shot attempt with a varying degree of success. Then you'll roll a die. If the shot fails, the card states which side gets the rebound. (Yes, three-point shots are in the game.)

Your opponent can play a defensive card, and when used wisely he'll steal a pass, block a shot or foul your shooter.

Simulating realistic scoring requires a delicate balance in a sport where each team averages 100 points per game. Rest assured that you'll find it here, as the number of two-pointers, three-pointers and foul shots will be very close to their actual real-life averages each and every game.

Playable in an hour, Big Time Hoops will provide all the fun and excitement of real pro basketball -- without the high ticket prices!

Each round is hilariously revealing as the question cards are often taboo, inappropriate, and all around ridiculous.

Walk to Jesus is the first Christian Motivation Family Game in the world. It joins fun, religious education, it can be used for prayer or meditation base and besides all that it is a great game. It is a great way to spend some quality time together with your family.

To win in Walk to Jesus you need Divine Grace cards, which can be obtained by helping other players. Not only does this uphold one of basic Christian teachings, and has general moral value, it also adds to the depth of the game as Walk to Jesus is world’s first game requiring help to other players – your competitors – in order to win. description:
Welcome to Grim City / town of quirky characters
It is very simple: Collect as many points in a same color, then you are well regarded.
Optimize your hand as soon as possible. If a player closes his hand, the fun is over!
Joker and Doubler help you while foreign Hi-Jacks poach in their own ranks and make seemingly safe points to nothing.
Turbulent, exciting and fast it goes on here - can you keep up?

excerpt of the official rules: 
Each player receives 3 cards on hand. 3 more cards are placed side by side for all players on the table.
The remaining cards form a draw pile deck. The players try to collect as many points in a same suit (red, green or black).
During the game the player's hand can gradually optimized with the table cards. In each round, each player has exactly one of several possible actions. 
Note: During the game each player always has 3 cards in hand and on the table are always 3 cards open. 
The game is played over several singles matches. The player with the lowest result at the end of a single match, loses a credit (chip card is rotated accordingly). 
The player is completely out of the game if he can not bring this stake. 
Note: If several players at the same time have the lowest result (tied), each of these players lose a credit.


            
        
    


Willkommen in Grim-City / die Stadt der skurrilen Persönlichkeiten
Es ist ganz einfach: Sammle möglichst viele Punkte in einer gleichen Farbe, dann bist Du gut angesehen.
Optimiere Dein Blatt so schnell es geht. Macht ein Spieler dicht, ist Schluss mit lustig!
Joker und Doppler helfen Dir, während fremde Hi-Jacks in den eigenen Reihen wildern und sicher geglaubte Punkte zunichte machen.
Turbulent, spannend und schnell geht es hier zu - kannst Du mithalten?

Auszug des offiziellen Regelwerks:
Jeder Spieler erhält 3 Karten auf die Hand. 3 weitere Karten werden für alle Spieler offen nebeneinander auf den Tisch ausgelegt. 
Die restlichen Karten bilden einen verdeckten Nachziehstapel. 
Die Spieler versuchen möglichst viele Punkte in einer gleichen Spielfarbe zu sammeln (rot, grün oder schwarz). 
Mit den ausliegenden Karten werden die eigenen Handkarten nach Bedarf im laufenden Spiel nach und nach
optimiert. Pro Runde hat jeder Spieler immer genau eine von mehreren möglichen Aktionen zur Verfügung.
Hinweis: Während des Spiels hat jeder Spieler immer 3 Karten auf der Hand und auf dem
Tisch liegen immer 3 Karten offen aus.
Das Spiel wird über mehrere Einzelspiele gespielt. Der Spieler mit dem schlechtesten Ergebnis eines
Einzelspiels verliert einen Credit (Chipkarte wird entsprechend gedreht). Der Spieler ist ganz aus dem
Spiel, wenn er diesen Einsatz nicht mehr bringen kann. 
Hinweis: Haben mehrere Spieler zeitgleich das
schlechteste Ergebnis (punktgleich), verliert jeder dieser Spieler einen Credit.

English description:

"As almirantes - Portuguese for admirals - in command of both a merchant and a war navy, 2 to 6 players build and navigate their fleets in order to dominate the geostrategic center of the ocean. In this exciting strategy game, however, one must call for divine help as conquering the ocean is nothing compared to holding it…"

Each almirante has the goal of dominating the ocean, that is, conquering and holding the field in the center of both ocean and board. For that, he or she builds and sails a fleet of both fishing boats and war galleons, by cleverly using the resources received in the beginning of each play: winds and coins. In addition, each almirante has three opportunities to use a god card.

Each play starts by receiving 6 winds and a variable amount of coins. Winds allow you to move your vessels: you need 1 wind to sail a fishing boat to another field, and 2 winds to do the same with a heavier galleon. Coins allow you to build new vessels: fishing boats cost you 2 coins, while galleons cost you 8 coins. You can trade these two resources, if you are willing to pay a conversion fee.

You then move your vessels. Sail your fishing boats to the shoal fields to fish and earn coins on your next turns (2 coins per each of the 6 shoals players compete for). Sail your galleons to meet opponent vessels in order to fight battles, or go for the center to conquer it. Battles are always won by the almirante with the most galleons on that field. Some defeated vessels are sunk and some of them may flee.

If the time is right, call upon the gods! Each almirante chose 3 god cards from equal pools of 8 before the game started, kept them secret and can now play each of them once - but only once. Play Aeolus and he will grant you extra winds, or maybe play Ares and he will sink an opponent's vessel...

Your play is now over. Are your galleons dominating the center of the ocean? Good, then slide one point along the scoreboard. Do this 4 times (in a game with 3-5 players) to win. But look out, the other almirantes are now sourrunding you and will want to join efforts to stop your countdown toward victory. Will they get you?


Portuguese Description:

Enquanto almirantes, no comando tanto de uma marinha mercante como de uma de guerra, 2 a 6 jogadores constroem e dirigem as suas frotas para dominar o centro geoestratégico do oceano. Neste entusiasmante jogo de estratégia, porém, têm de apelar a uma ajuda divina já que conquistar o oceano não é nada comparado a mantê-lo...

This lavish 92 page supplement chronicles such legendary battles as Isandlwana, Rorke's Drift and Ulundi. You've seen the silver screen version of events in Zulu and Zulu Dawn - now you can refight these classic engagements on the tabletop...

description:
Publisher Blurb:

In 1775 the simmering resentment of American colonists exploded into a bloody rebellion. Beginning in Boston, the conflict eventually stretched from Canada in the north to Florida in the south and lasted eight grueling years. On one side stood the King's redcoats and settlers loyal to their sovereign, later joined by regiments of 'Hessian' mercenaries. Ranged against them was a citizen army under the inspirational leadership of George Washington, supported by militia and eventually French troops eager to embarrass their old foe. Both sides were forced to learn new tactics for fighting in the wild terrain of North America in a conflict that saw heroism and ineptitude in equal measure. This supplement for Black Powder describes the history, armies, personalities and battles of this conflict. Included are scenarios allowing you to recreate many of the battles of the rebellion. This glorious 144-page full-color book, written by Steve Jones, comes with a free Continental officer figure sculpted by Alan Perry himself! Who else would you ask to design a new figure for an American War of Independence book? A copy of the Black Powder rulebook is required to use this supplement.
