description:
The  Bloody Fields of Mollwitz is the first game in the tactical battle series entitled The Battles of Enlightenment, representing the first battle of Frederick II, King of Prussia.
Game scale:
1 hex = 120 m (131 yards),
1 game turn = 30 minutes
1 counter = infantry battalion/cavalry squadron (hussars: 1 counter = two sqds)/artillery battery.
Game description

The game is divided only on the activation phases, in which the players first can change the order of the commander, and then move troops belonging to the commanders of the wings or infantry line (the same are treated Hussars regiments and artillery). 
In each activation phase players:
1) use their Commanders-in-Chief and dispatch riders to give orders to subordinate commanders;
2) activate infantry / cavalry or artillery moving it first and then carrying out the combat. Inactive player may respond by performing cavalry countercharges; infantry can react with fire. Also in this phase employed to rally broken units and moving commander-in-chiefs.

The principle of the game is based on an order system. The player must carry out the assigned order (of course, he gives them to himself), until the order is changed by the Commander-in-Chief, or the wing/line commander manages to change it by himself.
The possible orders are: STOP, MARCH, ATTACK. 
In the historical scenario it will be possible to give two additional orders: MARCH (IN MARCH COLUMN); DEVELOP.
The game distinguishes the tactics used by the Prussian and Austrian armies, with particular emphasis on platoon fire combat used by the Prussian infantry.
Zone of Control works similarly to other games with the exception that the infantry ZoC does not stop cavalry units, but allows the infantry to fire at the moving cavalry.
Movement is identical as in other games in the hex-and-counter system.
The game discerns fire combat and melee combat.
Infantry and artillery fire combat is based on the shooting unit current morale level - it results in a loss of morale level of the fired upon opponent. 
Melee combat is based on morale checks: first the attacker checks his morale, if he gets a positive result he initiates the melee combat. Then the defender checks his morale and if his result is positive the attacker checks his morale again (by checking the effects of the lack of success).
Cavalry (except hussars) can only attack in melee. Their attacks are resolved in the same way as infantry melee combat (with the exception resulting from the option to counterattack).
The loss of morale level is indicated with special markers.
When the unit loses morale is broken.

There will be four scenarios in this game:
Initial – a training scenario,
Historical - with General Roemer and his cavalry on the map - the rest of the Austrian army enters the map in the course of the game,
Hypothetical 1 and 2 - two armies face themselves already developed on the battlefield.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession

description: Join the SimplyFun Buddies for a hoppin’ good time! Buddy Hop features two games in one! Game one concentrates on building your child’s memory and sequencing skills, while game two concentrates on building quick visual association skills. The durable, soft foam character mats are great for indoor and outdoor play.

This is two games in one! One side of board matches the color tokens to the color of Huey’s shoes and for more advanced play turn the board over and match the color tokens to the correct color word. If you already have this token on your board you get to pass it onto another player! Be the first player to fill up all of the spaces on your Huey board and win!

There is no "taking turns" in Corner Center Match! All players race to match the corner on their cards to the shape and color on the card in the center of the table. Don't forget to check the back sides of your cards if you get stuck, but be quick!

How fast can you match and add your cards to go from 15 Cards To Zero? Win the game by being the first player to get rid of his or her Number Cards by matching them to an individual number on a single die or the sum of two or more dice each turn. The first to match can discard those cards from their hand to get to zero before the other players.

It’s a Grill Party and everyone has their own idea about what foods to grill! Place and swap Grill Food Pieces on the Grill to try to match situations depicted on your Grill Food Cards. Be the first player to match all of your Grill Food Cards to become the Grill Master!

Step right up and roll the dice! This colorful “boardwalk” theme creates a fun carnival like game experience. Roll as many times as you like to try to match the sum on the Number Cards but be careful there is a price to pay!

Roll dice and combine their sums to match target sums displayed on number cards. Collect the number cards you match and secure their points if no other player can match their target sum that round. Secure the most points by the end of the game to win!

Take a fast paced ride down the Letter Slide!

Don't "slide" by the letter tiles that you need. Spell as many words as you can based on the card that has been turned over.  Hurry before the timer runs out!

Grab Letter Tiles to complete as many four-letter words as you can before the time runs out. Place one Letter Tile from each correctly spelled word onto a corresponding letter space. Cover all the Letter Spaces on your Slide Board first to win the game!

This game is no longer in production.
If replacement pieces are needed, please contact SimplyFun. We'll do our best to replace game pieces as long as they are available in our warehouse.

It's all about Perspective!!

Move the brightly colored Game Tiles around on the board to create one or more patterns from your “Perspective”. Solve your Pattern Cards even when it is not your turn but pay close attention and be quick so you do not miss out on a winning opportunity!

Match the Pattern Cards to patterns on the Game Board to earn points. Move Game Tiles to create new patterns and opportunities for more points. Score the most points from matched patterns to win Perspectives!

Who's Like Me? You may be surprised how much we all have in common.

In Who's Like Me?, children learn how to recognize emotions in their friends and see what they all have in common. This game develops skills like observation, memory, distinguishing and grouping multiple elements, and sharing emotions. Make matches and earn points. Score the most points and win the game!

The animals are loose and must be brought back into the enclosures as fast as possible. If only someone could remember where the animals were last!

Dash quickly as you and the other players turn over tiles to collect pairs of animals. Find pairs according to the Goal Tiles. Match a valid pair and receive a positive point, collect a wrong pair and receive a negative point. Score the most points at the end of 3 rounds and win!

This game is no longer in production.
If replacement pieces are needed, please contact SimplyFun. We'll do our best to replace game pieces as long as they are available in our warehouse.

For each game, the set up and game play is the same, but the game objectives are slightly different. In the Bubbles game, the last player to have at least one Bubbles Tile left in their section wins the game! In the Puddles game, the first player to remove all the Puddle Tiles from their section wins the game!

Roll the Die to move the Rubber Duck and discard the Tile where the Rubber Duck lands. Replace a Tile from the discard pile when you roll the Duck Symbol. To Win the Bubbles game be the last player with a Tile left in Play. To win the Puddles game be the first player to remove all your Tiles.

In Cross Lanes, place your card at a 90 degree angle covering a card value that is one higher or one lower than the value of your card. Cover two halves in any orientation and score a Spare or a Strike. Be the first player to place all of your cards and win the game. Be careful if you don't have a play or a Gutterball may be headed your way!

Exercise your math factoring skills and make your horse the Front Runner. The first horse to cross the finish line wins the race.  Solve equations using the dice by plugging in the missing numbers needed to solve the equation.

Feeling fearless? Use a Second Wind card to re-roll the dice and try again. Falling behind? Use a Nose Ahead card for a chance to make up some ground. Solving for multiple operators? Follow the 3 rules for Order of Operations and run a smooth race.

This game is no longer in production.
If replacement pieces are needed, please contact SimplyFun. We'll do our best to replace game pieces as long as they are available in our warehouse.

Fourmation is a game that elegantly combines math with strategy.

The object is to connect four zones in a row: up, down or diagonally. But in order to place your colored marker, you'll have to make the number on the desired spot from the sum of one or more of the number cards in your hand. Outnumber all other players in a contested zone to put it back under your control and complete the winning "fourmation".

The game board can be turned and rearranged to create a different layout every time. This is a fun alternative to flash cards for upper level addition that your kids will love.

Just4Fun is the original version of the game with identical rules but without the modular board.

Tibbar is painting the town red, and orange... and even blue-green!

In Mix to Match, your kids will learn how to combine colors to create secondary and tertiary shades. Send Tibbar around his painter's palette to get to the color shown on one of your cards. Match all of your colors first to win the game.

Embark on a thrilling race to construct a smooth, continuous road from opposite ends of the gameboard in ByPass! Brace yourself for twists and turns, stay adaptable to dodge traffic cones, and strategize your path. Do you have the planning skills needed to be the first to build a completed ByPass? Let's hit the road!

Be the first to build a continuous road across the game board from your start to end zone. While this may seem like an easy feat, your opponents will attempt to block your moves, so be prepared to plan your path and use strategy to build your road tile by tile.

Speaking of tiles, each of the seven in the game has a distinct road pattern, three of which contain a dead end marked with a barricade. All players may use any road tile placed on the board as part of their path, no matter who placed it. Harness planning, strategy, and spatial reasoning skills to capture a bypass and get ahead of your opponents. Play with 2-4 players, ages 8 and up, in 25 minutes.

Objective: Be the first reaching space 100; become really positive about Germany's war occurences
- moving by turns -

All players have to buy in first. Each player moves forward according die roll. Ending on red spaces you must pay or collect play money, skip a turn or move again...

Note:
Likely published ~1915; indoctrination related to spirit of the age & time

In Geheimtraining (secret training) you try to pass the ball which is a disc to your team mates by flickikng it. Around each player pawn lies a rubberband to indicate the area in which you have to land the ball to successfully pass it.

The player who passes 11 perfect shots and shoots a goal wins the game.

Objective: Be the first reaching Paris (space 46); become really positive about Germany's war occurences
- moving by turns -

All players have to buy in first. Board shows German/French/Belgium Border with two separate ways (Aachen or Metz) towards Paris.
Choose one of the two routes. Each player moves forward according die roll. Ending on red marked spaces you must pay or collect play money, skip a turn or move again...
Note:
Likely published 1915; indoctrination related to spirit of the age & time

Objective: Flip carton discs into areas of different values in the box base and colored circles of the erected castle.

Instructions show relations to events of WWI: 
Cannon (on the cover) is described as "Krupp's 42 cm mortar", successfully used during WWI.

From the publisher's website:

No smoke, no mirrors, just a great game!

What: Dungeon Crawl

Scale: 1 figure or counter represents 1 man or creature

Your Role: Players lead a party of Adventurers into a dungeon in search of Treasure and Fame. Save the Princess, kill some Monsters or grab some Loot, the choice is yours!

Playability: Designed for solo, same side and head to head play.

2 Hour Dungeon Crawl can be played in a variety of ways and contains the following:

    Fifteen different Races including Dwarves, Elves, Orcs, Trolls, Wizards, Barbarians, and much more.
    Eight different Professions to breath life into your characters. 
    Rules for your characters to grow in skills and abilities as they succeed.
    30+ Attributes to further define your characters.
    Over 30 Magic Spells and Magical items for you to find and use.
    A system that generates your dungeons, including the Monsters, while you play.


And even better.

Have those cool Dungeon Tiles? You can use them.

Want to go Old School and use Graph Paper? You can do it.

Have metal figures? Plastics? How about paper? Even counters? Yep, you can use them all.

Why?

Because it shouldn't cost an arm and leg for a great Dungeon Crawl...unless you run into a Troll!

2 Hour Dungeon Crawl
No smoke, no mirrors, just a great game.

Shut the Front Door is a simultaneously turned, fast paced matching game about childish insults. The deck contains 50 cards each with 6 childish insults and one compliment on them. There are 30 different insults spread among the cards.

The rules contain several variants but the main mechanic of each is to find matching words between cards, shout it out (Cootiehead!) and discard or add the cards to another pile faster than the other players. Variant rules include relay races, shouting out only the compliments, penalties for making mistakes and even drinking games for adult players.

Other decks in the series, available separately, share the same rules but have words with different themes such as Sci-Fi or Shakespearean insults.

description:
Köbes is a board game for two to four players from 12 years of age. The game is played in the dining room of a typical Cologne brewery/pub. Players assume the role of Köbes, a waiter who serves Kölsch (name used to describe beer in Cologne and Bonn). Each Köbes is his own entrepreneur. He must bring as many Kölsch as possible to the tables of his guests, that he previously purchased at the bar from the host brewer. But it's not as simple as it sounds, as there are always many obstacles in the way.

The game bases its mechanics on the well known and loved game Mensch Ärger Dich Nicht, which uses dice to move around the board. In Köbes, players also use dice rolls to advance their Köbes around the brewery/pub, but are not limited to just the rolled dice numbers.

A dexterity game.

NOTE: Nervíky (Czech/Slovak) = nerves (to be more precise: little nerves).

Set-up: One player (the catcher) has a small cup, and a die with different colours on its faces. The other players each have a differently-coloured wooden piece (yellow, red, green, or blue) with a string attached to it.

Aim of the game: Players try to receive the least amount of penalty points for their poor reaction times.

Game play:

Variant no. 1

The catcher rolls the die, if the roll is yellow, red, green, or blue, he/she tries to catch, with his/her cup, the colour rolled. If he/she rolls white, he/she attempts to catch all of the colours; if he/she rolls black, he/she is not allowed to catch anything. The other players, according to the result of the roll, either try to escape (by pulling on the string) or do nothing.

Variant no. 2

The catcher says three colours at random (e.g., green, blue, black) and rolls the die. 
If he/she rolls one of the colours he/she said, he/she tries to catch as many of the other players as possible and the other players try to escape as quickly as possible (again by pulling on the string). If the catcher rolls a different colour than he/she said, the other players do nothing.

Scoring Points:

There is a penalty point to the catcher if he/she does not catch any of the other players, or if he/she accidentally catches any of the other players.

There is a penalty point to the other players (with coloured pieces with strings) if they are caught by the catcher, or if they accidentally pull their pieces back.

The players place their 15 meeples on the game board, one per turn. When all meeples are on the board the players move their meeples one space at a time. When a player gets 5 of his meeples in a straight line, he may remove any one  meeple of any opposing player. If he does this on the black spaces, forming the name "Erika" he may additionaly reenter one of his own removed meeples. If a player manages to occupy the "Erika" spaces of his own color, he wins the game. Another possible way to win the game is to occupy any black "Erika" spaces, the two spaces left and the two spaces right to the letter "I" in "Erika" (forming a cross).

boardgame: Red Dragon Falling: The Coming Civil War in China
description:
Modern War #19, Sept-Oct 2015

It was the Chinese scholar-historian Wei Yuan, writing in the mid-19th century, who first formally identified his nation’s apparently inescapable cycles of “chaos and humiliation” (quru), followed by “rejuvenation” (fuxiang), followed by “wealth and power” (fuqiang), and then back to quru to start again. More recently, Queens College futurist and sometimes-CIA analyst Dr. Bruce Bueno de Mosquita wrote concerning China that he sees no factors in the present-day environment working to end or deflect that same cycle. It was the shared conclusions of those two men, working over a century-and-a-half apart, which inspired me to design this wargame modeling the military parameters and possibilities inherent in the next phase of Chinese quru – civil war.

The likely starting points for such a conflict would either be the collapse of the Chinese real estate bubble – which is already broadly predicted to be inescapable and will constitute the largest financial collapse in world history – or the growing potable water crisis. (A third of the water consumed by the Chinese is undrinkable by US standards.) Beyond those factors, or in combination with them, there’s also the gender imbalance, the fossil fuel air pollution crisis, the increasingly endemic corruption, the aging population structure, the expanding rural-urban economic gap, as well as the general collapse of ideology and the resultant moral lapse throughout the populace. One, some, or all of these factors may eventually lead to a crisis in China, as so often occurs in history, resulting in a civil war.

Each turn equals two months, probably starting sometime late in this decade or early in the next. The game is designed for two players, but it is adaptable for solitaire. Each hexagon on the 34x22” map represents 80 miles from side to opposite side. Units of maneuver are brigades, divisions and group armies and air corps (in the form of 176 large-size NATO-style counters).

Components: One 22" x 34" map & 176 counters.

Objective: As a hero - collect as much gold as possible;
as dragon - catch all heros; game ends after all gold is collected or all heros are caught/out of the game.
 - acting by turns -
The dragon is placed in the centre of the board, each hero with two pieces of gold on his starting position; remaining gold (4 pieces) are placed at the far end of the cave.
Players move according die roll forward, laterally or backwards.
A '6' can be used only passing (stepping over) the dragon, otherwise not usable. Reaching a piece of gold it should be brought back to starting position. 
After each player's turn the dragon will move segmentally according die roll. A player hit by dragon's head is out of the game - unless he/she can pay a ransom (1 gold).

note:
likely published mid 1950's

boardgame: Cosmic Encounter: White Dwarf Magazine Expansion
description:
User Description

The White Dwarf expansion for Cosmic Encounter has four new Alien Powers cards for the Games Workshop edition of Cosmic Encounter (2nd edition).

Published in the White Dwarf magazine, issue 78 with an article about Cosmic Encounter and its history.

All four of the Alien Powers cards were in Eon expansion sets (Grudge in #1, the other three in #3), and have been republished in a later edition of Cosmic Encounter.

The Alien Powers Cards are:

    Reincarnator - Uses Powers not in the game
    Changeling - Changes Powers with opponents
    Warrior - Adds experience points
    Grudge - Penalizes for refusing to ally

boardgame: Gunslinger: Legends of the Dusty Trail
description:
Around the time of the Civil War and following, the American West was certainly a wild place.   Although it was sparsely populated, many of the inhabitants were desperate and dangerous.  The West had always been home to large groups of American natives, but now had been receiving various undesirables such as cattle rustlers, gamblers, vigilantes, and banditos.  For the most part, those coming West were hungry for gold and were willing to do just about anything to get it.  Many found it too tiresome trying to get rich panning and digging, and instead found it more convenient to steal or kill for it.  As a consequence, it became the common practice not only for nearly everyone to carry a firearm, but also for nearly everyone to use one.  Needless to say gangs, posses, and gunfights were common parts of every day life.  Anybody with a gun had an opportunity to make a name and gain infamy in the great American West.

It is exactly this setting in which you find yourself in.  You have the option of choosing one of seven notorious gunfighters of the old American West:  Kid Rango, Chief War Wind, Pablo ‘The Pistol’ Perez, Walter Winston, Raging Arrow, Luther ‘The Lawman’ Hale, or Little Pancho.  Besides the 7 posse bosses, Gunslinger: Legends of the Dusty Trail also comes with an additional 42 gunfighters.  Each boss and gunfighter comes with their own mugshot and unique battle stats.  With your posse boss, recruit a gang from the various cities to ride out to face your rivals.  Dodge ruthless banditos, determined lawmen, and hostile natives to get to your next gig.  You can knock off the Bank of the Nation, rustle up some cattle, or take on the Union Pacific Railroad.  You can also track down the other gunslingers to collect the bounties on their heads.  The way you stack your posse, the strategy you employ, and knowing when to strike will always give you the edge.  Each boss comes with his own clutch skills that come in handy when the chips are down.  No matter how many times you play, the scenarios are endless and the game never looks the same twice.  The winner is the posse that bags the most gold, even if they end up in pine boxes.

With Gunslinger: Legends of the Dusty Trail, you get three  distinct games in the Gunslinger series.  You get everything you need to play Gunslinger Shootout (arena style combat), Gunslinger Showdown (mano y mano confrontation), and Gunslinger Roundup (recruit your posse, collect the bounties, and get the gold). Anybody can get lucky and be a hero!  It takes one shot in the right spot.

Ideal for small tournament or casual play support.

(8) Fortress Oblivion cards (2) Starbase Omega cards

Star Realms is a space combat deckbuilding game designed by Magic Pro Tour Champions and Hall of Famers Darwin Kastle and Rob Dougherty (developer and cofounder of the Ascension Deckbuilding Game).

Enjoy the thrill fo TCG-Style combat in a deckbuilding game. Play powerful ships, destroy enemy bases, and blast your opponent directly on the way to victory!

description:
Non-CCG card game for two people.

Explore the world in which swords chop to the left and right, while men screaming off their lungs. World of mighty Karmazyns and Brotherhood. World of XVII-century Poland. World of Noble Card Game VETO!

Starting the game in Veto The confrontation, you can choose whether you stand on the side of a knightly family of Michael Volodyovski headed, or insidious Radziwill, where the first saber is Andrew Kmicic. Your actions can support Jeremy Wiśniowiecki or treacherous brothers Radziwill in the struggle for the throne of Poland. By hook or by crook you will agitate and challenge to duels their rivals.

Do not have pity for the vanquished! Grab throne for yourself!

This is a standalone game, based on CCG Szlachecka Gra Karciana VETO! (Noble Card Game VETO!) and contains two complete decks of 55 cards, 28 ducat tokens, 15 stroke tokens and instruction.

----- Polish genuine description -----

Poznaj świat, w którym szablami rąbie się na lewo i prawo, gardłując przy tym wniebogłosy. Świat możnych Karmazynów i odważnych Panów Braci. Świat XVII-wiecznej Polski. Świat Szlacheckiej Gry Karcianej VETO!

Zaczynając grę w Veto Konfrontacja, możesz wybrać, czy staniesz po stronie rycerskiego rodu Wiśniowieckich z Michałem Wołodyjowskim na czele, czy podstępnych Radziwiłłów, gdzie pierwszą szablą jest Andrzej Kmicic. Twoje działania mogą wesprzeć Jeremiego Wiśniowieckiego bądź zdradzieckich braci Radziwiłłów w walce o tron Rzeczypospolitej. Nie przebierając w środkach będziesz agitował i wyzywał na pojedynki swoich rywali.

Nie miej litości dla pokonanych! Zagarnij tron dla siebie!

Gra zawiera: dwie kompletne talie po 55 kart, 28 żetonów dukatów, 15 żetonów kresek oraz instrukcję.

boardgame: Warlord: Saga of the Storm – The Temple of Eternal Flame
description: This Adventure Path Set (#3) contains 110 cards in two different pre-constructed decks; one is Free Kingdoms and the other is Deverenian.

Bohnanza: Ladies & Gangsters is an English language compilation of two stand-alone Bohnanza games previously available only in German: Ladybohn: Manche mögen's heiss! and Al Cabohne.

Ladies is a three- to five-player game that includes female versions of the beans used in Bohnanza. You get more money if a field is harvested with a lady bean as the top card. You get no money if a field is harvested with a baby bean on top. There are some new rules about reordering beans on the fields.

Gangsters is a one- or two-player game where you must pay off the Bean Mafia. Each turn, the Bean Mafia will take beans from your fields if yours match what they are growing. They will also take matching cards when drawn. This game also includes a few new beans with irregular beanometers.

Bohnanza: Princes & Pirates is an English language compilation of two Bohnanza expansions previously available only in German: Bohnröschen and La Isla Bohnitâ.

In Princes, players are a Bean Prince and must get to their sleeping beauty. This is done by fulfilling quests to cross the thorn hedges to get to the castle where she awaits.

In Pirates, players can now buy ships in order to help them trade and sell beans. You can also purchase pirate ships which you can use to raid your opponents bean fields.

Second big expansion for the basic game Clinic, released inside an awesome real Medical Dossier.
It contains :


     2 new big tiles for the common board that allow new abilities for  the nurses which can treat patients with medical pulls and a new kind of Patients, the Elders :
        one Pharmacy
        one Hospice
    
    25 Empty Medical Pills in 4 colors that can be purchased by the players during the Action phase ! 
    16 small grey cubes "The Elders" who are living in the Hospice, they move slowly and require more attention to be treated...
    4 players cubes for the new buildings
    Rules

Particle Accelerator is an educational dexterity game for 1 or more players. Particle by particle, you must create (in order) hydrogen, helium, lithium, beryllium, boron, carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, fluorine, and neon, by flicking protons, neutrons, and electrons into the atomic structure. At every step of the way, what you create must be a stable isotope (although it could be an ion), with electrons in the correct electron shells, or — BOOM! — nuclear meltdown, game over.

Shut the F*** Up! is a simultaneously turned, fast paced matching game about yelling insults. The deck contains 50 cards each with 6 insults and one compliment on them. There are 30 different insults spread among the cards featuring some very strong language.

The rules contain several variants but the main mechanic of each is to find matching words between cards, shout it out and discard or add the cards to another pile faster than the other players. Variant rules include relay races, shouting out only the compliments, penalties for making mistakes and drinking games.

Other decks in the series, available separately, share the same rules but have insults with different themes such as Sci-Fi/Fantasy or Shakespearean.

boardgame: Clinic Expansion: ICU free bonus tile
description:
This expansion only contain a free bonus specialized module to thank the BGG users, it can be found in the upload section with the rules. They allow the author to improve the first version of the English rules.

This new tile allows the player to treat one patient without removing it with his car. A red Patient becomes Yellow and an orange Patient becomes White.

Clones?! Cthulhu?! ZOMBIES!?! These 3 new cities are here to vie for their places in the new world… and to eat some brains. Choose between a malevolent being, power-hungry schemers, and a flesh-addicted horde of monsters in this 5 to 6 player expansion to Civility!

Cthulhu City manipulates the world, cards, and events; Clone City will take advantage of other city’s powers while exploiting weaknesses; and Zombie City starts out intimidating and dominating but will rot away to nothing if resources are not properly managed. Bring new dynamics and strategy to Civility with this expansion that includes everything needed to play 5 and 6 player games when added to the base game.

THE GAME

In the universe of Fallen Frontiers different factions fight for control of both planets and their strategic resources in order to proliferate in a galaxy that holds lots of secrets. The players assume control of an army faction that can be shaped by choosing troops, characters, and cards that best adapt to their game style.

Fallen Frontiers is a battle game based on combat squads. The squads offer protection and firepower to the characters. Characters that are the ones that define the game style because their attributes change the characteristics of their squad. In this way, each squad acts differently depending on the character that leads them. However in Fallen Frontiers characters are not superheroes, they are weak and easy to eliminate when they are not with their squad.

Besides the miniatures that represent their army, players will also have a deck of cards assembled by them. Strategy is not revealed at the beginning of the game, as a player can secretly set up the deck of cards allowing them to change the game style in such a way that opponents will not be able to guess their strategy at first sight.

The factions and the cards use up resources to carry out certain actions. The main resources are moral and Krithium. Players gain moral by achieving targets and causing losses to their opponent's army while their army suffers as little as possible. Using moral points will make an army more powerful but will exhaust it in the process. Krithium is a resource that must be collected during the game. It is the source of energy that enhances a team's weapons and cards. The player will have an armory, a separate deck where the player purchases equipment, that can be used during the game and consumes Krithium. The contents of the armory is also set up by the player just like the deck of cards.

Stimmvieh is a game for party secretaries about collecting the largest donations for your party. However, votes are also valuable, as the two players who have collected the most and second most votes in the end may double their donations. Everyone starts with an equally powerful team of politicians. However, you already have to decide whether you're hunting for votes or donations when you nominate your top candidates.

This is a revised edition of Stimmvieh with 90 cards instead of 115.

Major differences as compared to the 1998 version:

    The new game plays with 3-4, not 3-5. 
    The game was newly balanced
    3 and 4 players use the same deck of cards
    New 3-player-variant
    Cards come in larger size
    Production was crowdfunded


you can win by either only collecting donations or by collecting the second most votes (and enough donations). In few games the player with the most votes actually wins the game.

With three players a dummy called "Angelus" plays with you. You take turns in helping her play. What is more, she can also win the game.

"The game where knowledge and strategy meet"

This is a trivia game with an unusual ladder format that requires each player to climb a twisting pathway in order to win. Players answer trivia questions (taken from a booklet) as they advance. Certain spaces on the ladder affect a player's movement options. The first player to reach the peak of the ladder and answer a final challenge is declared the winner.

Designed for 2, 3, or 4 players age 8 and older. Includes board, pawns, booklet, storage insert, and a die.

Bohnanza Schnupperspiel is a small, promotional copy of Bohnanza containing 54 cards.

Bohnanza is a card game in which you cannot rearrange your hand, thus having to play the cards in the order that you draw them. The aim of the game is to get coins by planting fields (sets) of the beans and then harvesting them. To help players collect sets, the game features extensive trading and deal making.

The Bohnanza Schnupperspiel was included in the Spielbox Magazine special Spiel '14 edition (Spielbox Sonderausgabe zur Spiel '14), which was distributed for free at Spiel '14.

Redistributed as Bohnanza Pocket.

boardgame: Dawn of the Zeds (Second Edition): The Director's Cut
description:
Much anticipated by the beleaguered residents of Farmingdale who were hoping for a glimpse of salvation, Dawn of the Zeds: The Director's Cut instead delivers all sorts of new evil, mayhem, and, yes, a small bit of relief for those Zeds players everywhere exhausted by the never-ending onslaught.

This expansion to Dawn of the Zeds introduces new heroes, Event cards, Fate cards, and new game pieces that will intensify your Zed-killing experiences. The Director's Cut adds a nice balance of nastiness and helpfulness to the game; you will love (and hate) all of the new additions to it.

This game is only available in clamshell packaging.

Note: This game requires that you (or someone in your group of players) own a copy of Dawn of the Zeds 2nd Edition

Lunte: Guy with Match is an additional card for the game Lunte, which can be played (out off sequence) immediately after a defuse card is played, thus reactivating the fuse (the cards are no longer discarded).

Lunte is a fast paced card game, in which players add cards to a burning fuse, until someone detonates a bomb or extinguishes the fire. The aim of the game is to accumulate the highest number of points, by choosing the right moment to detonate or extinguish the burning fuse. Detonated bombs gain the player points, whereas extinguished cards are removed from the game.

The Lunte: Guy with Match card is a promo card that was first released at Spiel '14, in Essen.

Lunte: Fire Distinguisher is an additional card for the game Lunte. It is a 10 point fire extinguishing card; the base game only comes with 6, 5 and 4 point extinguishing/defusing cards.

Lunte is a fast paced card game, in which players add cards to a burning fuse, until someone detonates a bomb or extinguishes the fire. The aim of the game is to accumulate the highest number of points, by choosing the right moment to detonate or extinguish the burning fuse. Detonated bombs gain the player points, whereas extinguished cards are removed from the game.

The Lunte: Fire Extinguisher card is a promo card that was  first released at Spiel '14, in Essen.

It's a presidential election and you need to win the Electoral College. Campaign, fundraise, advertise, and win the swing states! SWING STATE is designed around the electoral college and the strategies involved in winning the 12 swing states during a US presidential election. It is super fun, super suspenseful, and the most realistic "game" about running for president you will ever experience.

The game can be played between 2 players.  It can also be played between 2 teams, ideally with 2 players for each team. One is the presidential candidate for the red team -- she chooses which states to visit each turn. Her teammate is the campaign manager for the red team -- he chooses where to spend the money on advertising.

The game begins with a map that reflects the way all 12 swing states actually lean. Pennsylvania leans Democratic by 4 percentage points (based on a historical average of recent presidential elections). That 4% point lean is reflected by four blue chips placed on PA during setup. Virginia, as you can see in the image, leans Red by 5% points (chips). As players campaign and advertize in these states, the chip stacks rise and fall and often flip from one color to the other.

A big part of the fun is that Swing State is a game that is easy to learn, easy to set up, and easy to play, but has a very rich mix of strategies that change each time you play. There are seven turns before election night.  And what makes this one of the most exciting games you will ever play is that the results of how each state will vote are not revealed until the very end.  At the end of the game -- election night -- all of the campaign funds you have invested by playing cards face down on each state are finally revealed.

A humorous card game where players promote their own kids' growth and success while sabotaging other players' kids with life's issues. Players play as individuals or as couples running a household, and they attempt to score points by "raising children" and "getting them out of the house."  Opponents block children's forward progress by playing impediment cards called "issues" that must be removed by playing suit-specific "remedies," such as T.L.C., Education, Discipline, or Money.  The game is played in hands.
A board with hints going from the ghost to others players. The expansion makes game easier than regular.

boardgame: Dungeon Storming: La Tana di Gwarl
description:
From the back of the box:

"Alone, in the depths of the dungeon, surrounded by monsters. A warrior and a mage against a host of savage orcs. Rich treasures await you, with which you can get new weapons, armor, spells and scrolls. Do you will become so powerful to deal with the Troll Gwarl and become heroes?

A collaborative game for two players or solo. It can be played with more players by combining two or more sets of Dungeon Storming!"

The game is played by moving the miniatures of your heroes on the map, room by room, battling the monsters with a simple roll of a die, dodging the traps, collecting treasures and magic objects, until the heroes reach the last room and try to overcome the boss monster (the heroes can exit the dungeon and buy new objects with the money found).

The map and the layout of the rooms for this dungeon are fixed (all the tables for the encounters, treasures and magical objects are printed on it), but it can be played with four levels of difficulty.

boardgame: Malifaux: The Guild's Judgement – Lady Justice Box Set
description: When the Guild created the Death Marshals Department to directly combat the Resurrectionist threat in Malifaux few were willing to lead the Marshals in their grim task. The weapons wielded to combat the undead corrupt their users just as foully as necromancy corrupts its practitioners, and each qualified candidate was unwilling to give up his humanity for the Guild. That is, until a woman stepped forward and volunteered for the command. Her true name remains as much a mystery to the Guardsmen outside of the Death Marshals as the source of her blindness. Whatever took her sight, the rumor goes, blessed her with supernatural senses and also granted her the ability to resist the necromantic influences that slowly corrupt every other member of the Death Marshals Department. Lady Justice is tireless in her hunt for Resurrectionists in the City. She and her Death Marshals are called on the scene when evidence of necromantic rituals, or some chance encounter with the undead, have exhausted the Guild Guard's resources. Once on the trail of a Resurrectionist, Justice does not tire and does not give up until her suspect is in her custody, or is no longer able to pose a threat to the residents of Malifaux.

Terra Schnupperspiel is a promotional, mini version of 
Terra.

Terra is a 2014 reimplementation of Fauna; both are educational, trivia-type games, designed by Friedeman Friese.

Terra Schnupperspiel was given for free with any purchase, at the HUCH & friends booth at Spiel '14 in Essen. It was only available in German.

A Aventura de Caboclo Bernando (The Caboclo Bernardo Adventure) is based in a true history of an unsung hero who saves many sailor lives at Brazilian coast of Espírito Santo at 1887 setember 7th.

This game mixes strategy, cooperation and the thrill of fighting a perfect storm. The players represent the fishermen at beach who should keep the rope firmly to help Caboclo Bernardo (a Brazilian hero) to save the sailors of a sinking ship and bring them safely to shore. The mission is dangerous, the fishermen may even injure themselves while pulling the rope from side to side to control it over the strong winds.

Also, an element of surprise can put everything away! Secretly, one of the players is an evil traitor who tries to sabotage the mission. He will try to cut the rope and pull it the wrong way, delaying the work of other participants until it is too late.

Portuguese edition:

Este jogo mistura estratégia, cooperação do time e a emoção de lutar contra uma tempestade. Os jogadores representam os pescadores que, da praia, devem manter a corda firme para que o Caboclo Bernardo chegue até o navio e traga os marinheiros em segurança até a margem. A missão é perigosa, os pescadores podem até se ferir enquanto puxam a corda de um lado para o outro para compensar os fortes ventos.

Além disso, um elemento surpresa pode pôr tudo a perder! Secretamente, um dos jogadores representa um malvado traidor que tentará sabotar a missão. Ele vai tentar cortar a corda e puxá-la para o lado errado, atrasando o trabalho dos demais participantes até que seja tarde demais.

Race down the slopes in your hamster ball while passing checkpoints and avoiding obstacles that could give you a nasty headache and end your hopes to be declared Hamster Ball Champion!

Hamster Ball Challenge! is a 2-player racing microgame consisting of 8 counters and a custom deck of 30 cards. On each turn, players choose one of three actions: place a track card, change speed, or move. Track cards contain goals, obstacles, and variable slopes. Increasing speed moves hamsters more quickly to the finish lessens control of the hamster ball. Players may also ram each other.

The game ends when a hamster reaches the finish, when a hamster is disqualified, or when a hamster ball is destroyed. Points are given for each checkpoint passed. Five additional points are given to hamsters that reach the finish. The racer with the most points is declared Hamster Ball Champion!

Eastwood Deadliest Gunslingers is a multi-player card game. Set in the wild west of america. Players Draw five cards to shoot it out for a quick best out of three gunslinger gun fight. 
Each players deck has two card types "Shoot" and "Fate". One the players turn they may play one "Shoot" card to deal damage to the other players character. "Fate" Cards my be played at any time by both players for a variety of out comes.

The winner is the player who wins two out of three gunfights.

boardgame: Africa's Big 5: The Ultimate Adventure Board Game
description:
Choose your favourite animal: Lion, Rhino, Elephant, and Buffalo, Leopard or Giraffe and reap the rewards. Improve your general knowledge about wildlife world wide with 1000 trivia questions and answers.

Pick a Giraffe card (wild card) and perform the animal action or sounds, e.g. crawl like a leopard, trumpet like an elephant, snort like a warthog and many more.

Africa’s Big 5 is a fun, educational game for the whole family. Experience Africa and wildlife like never before. Adventurous trivia game with a twist. Original game play with wildcard factor adds flexibility and excitement. Watch friends and family perform basic animal actions and sounds with hilarious results. Smart device App complements game play.

“The Long Night ends, the Ascension begins!” – CLEON, EMPEROR OF THE IMPERIUM
  
It is the dawn of the Third Imperium. The Solomani War has ended, and the vast Terran empire claimed from the Valani has finally crumbled. Worlds spread throughout distant sub-sectors of space have lost all communications; they either survived or fell into darkness during the Long Night. The Valani empire has been biding its time, licking its wounds from the war. At last, the Emperor has decreed the Ascension; a reclamation of lost, but not forgotten, worlds the Solomani left behind. It is through you that the Emperor will reach out and reclaim what was once his. 
 Traveller Ascension™: Imperial Warrant lets you take the helm of untold adventures, space-faring exploration and the thrill of uncovering long-lost riches left from the First Imperium. Granted an Imperial Warrant from the Emperor, you will Expand, Explore and Exploit any surviving world and bring it back into the Imperium’s fold. Rewards come only to those who are bold enough to seize this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.re, to Expand and to Exploit this new area for the glory of the Imperium… and for your own fame and glory.

description:
Dreams of Dystopia - The Postapocalyptic Deckbuilding Game with a TWIST

In Dreams of Dystopia, set in a distant, postapocalyptic and dystopian future, players assume the roles of mighty "Masters" commanding various "Minions" to compete against each other - choosing between the Divine or Demonic aspect or even trying to tread the perilous path of Twilight - as they do so.

Dreams of Dystopia (DoD for short) is a "Dominion-Style" Deckbuilding Game, in which each player picks their "Master" at the beginning of the game, choosing their (inital) side - Divine or Demonic - and each will begin the game with an (almost) identical starting deck. Over the course of this combat-centered and highly interactive Deckbuilding Game players will build, enhance and expand their decks by recruiting new Minions and buying supporting Weapons, with the ultimate goal of reducing the opponents' Masters Life Points to zero, before your opponents do so to you!

What makes DoD unique and special is that each card has two sides, one representing the Divine and the other the Demonic aspect or incarnation of any Master, Minion or Weapon you can choose, recruit or purchase. The initial choice of Divine or Demonic will heavily influence the various winning strategies you will be able to pursue, but fear not: There are plenty of ways to switch your cards from Divine to Demonic or vice versa, and building decks using cards from both "Factions" is an option as well... DoD can hence safely be called a game "With a TWIST".

The game will come with custom card sleeves which are mandatory to use in order to keep your hand etc concealed from your opponents.

boardgame: Saint Petersburg (Second Edition): The Exclusive Spieleschmiede Expansion
description: A set of five counters, one in each player color. Each counter allows a player once during the game to refill the display up to 10 cards (only during the nobles or exchange rounds), or is worth 3VP at the end of the game.

Players are using a die to move their storks on a hexagon shaped grid in order to catch the frog that's appearing on water lilies and is also moving on the grid based on the die. The first player to catch the frog three times wins.

Ball's Chess, also known as CU-BONO (Romanian for "Who benefits?"), is a chess-like game designed by Albert Edward Ball from Wrexham, Wales. He patented the game in 1933 and once again (with slightly altered rules) in 1969. It was described by the Czech game expert Miloš Zapletal in his book "Velká kniha deskových her" published in 1991. The game is played on a 10x10 square board with 10 flags, two missiles, two 4-jet planes, two field guns, two tanks and two generals per player. |In the 1933 patent, the flags were called "regiments", the missiles "airships", the 4-jet planes "aeroplanes" and the generals "commanders".

The object of the game is to conquer one of the opponent's "capitals", that is, one of the squares the opponent's generals started on. Today the patent specifications (GB19330001481 19330117 and GB19690023908 19690510) are freely available on the internet so that the exact rules can be studied there. The game has at least as much strategic depth and tactical choices as ordinary chess, but draws are much less frequent due to the specific architecture of the game's design.

boardgame: Ogre: Barbarians at the Gate Sponsored Counter Sheets
description:
Barbarians at the Gate

The Vatican Guard captain tucked the loose strand of hair behind her ear yet again.  With the sound of her GEV squadron humming outside the command post, she debated how to distribute her dwindling resources. Reinforce Milan and risk losing Turin?  Venice was in danger too. Unless things improve, the infidels will soon be driving on Rome! Surely these are dark days to test the faithful…

Barbarians at the Gate is an expansion pack for Ogre: Designer Edition.

It consists of four counter sheets with three different factions in the world of Ogre plus a book with the corresponding rules for these counters as well as scenarios.  Included in the set are two sheets of Vatican Guard conventional units; two sheets of conventional units representing the “Sons of Old Nassau” mercenary regiment; and finally, there are four (!) different types of GEV units on the sheets representing the Anarchist Relief Front.

Unique features of this expansion include:


    Counters of the three factions in appropriate colors:
        Purple with gold trim depicting the Vatican Guard
        Black and orange tiger stripes (plus the “White Tigers”) representing the Sons of Old Nassau regiment
        Pink GEVs for the Anarchist Relief Front…of course!
    



    Two new GEV units for all three factions: the Missile GEV & the Heavy GEV!



    Heavy Weapon Teams for both the Vatican Guard & the Sons of Old Nassau.



     “White Tiger” infantry counters and rules as an “elite” cadre.



    Pikemans (Ogre Mk I) for both the Vatican Guard & the Sons of Old Nassau.



    New hex overlays with the associated rules for GEV Ramps and Vehicle Fords as well as overlays for Rapids/Breakers on waterways… (GEVs beware!)



    Variant rules for multiple types of Cruise Missiles; randomized control of a third squad in a two-player game; 4 game scenarios (& 19 sub-scenarios!); a green train to match the Black Rose counters in Ogre: Designer Edition; 224 total counters and more!


(Note: Buying two of this expansion set would put these forces on essentially equal footing with the Paneuropean and Combine forces included in Ogre: Designer Edition.)
