description:
Characteristics
With this game I tried to go a step further from what I tried to do when I created Dition, based on the same design philosophy. My intention here was to make a more consistent game, regarding to the formulation of its rules. I wanted the game to have two basic, simple rules. I was concerned about keeping the concept of simultaneity of actions at all times. Some features of GO, such as "once a piece is placed, it can not move," are not really consistent at all times in the unfolding of the game, regarding the concept with which they were raised. I tried to get rid of all philosophical inconsistency at the time of defining the rules of this game.

Equipment

Pieces
The pieces are circular. These pieces are characterized in that each has a range (a circular area whose diameter is three times the diameter of the piece). Each piece has one side painted white and the other black (like in Othello), and each player may chose one side (color) to play (then, the color of a piece is defined only when that piece is placed on the board, and keeps that color has long has its still on the board). Both sides have in the center a gray circle (its just decorative), whose diameter is equal to a quarter of the pieces' diameter. 
To tell if a piece is within the range of another, just take a piece and place it over the two pieces. If the piece used to measure is supported by the two pieces, one can ensure that a piece is within the range of the other (and vice versa).
A total of 152 pieces are needed.

Board
The board has two concentric squares: the peripheral (whose side is equal to 19x the diameter of the pieces) and the central (whose side is equal to 17x the diameter of the pieces). The peripheral defines the playing area, and acts has a neutral piece ("gray piece"). In this sense, the central delimits its range.

Rules

    At the start of the game the board is empty. Black plays first. Then both players play alternately. In his turn, a player can do one or two "relocation" moves. If he cant, the game ends, and that player loses.


Notes about the terms used:
"Belt": a group of pieces conforms a "belt" if every piece of the group is within the range of at least two of the other pieces of the group.
"Conditioned": a piece is not "conditioned" to a player when it is over the board, with his color facing the board, and since the group of pieces that are within its range (regardless of color) do not conform a belt. 
"Relocate": a player makes a "relocation" move when he takes a "conditioned" piece from the board or from the outside of the board, and then places it on an empty place over the board or at the outside of the board.

Educational/environmental boardgame with several different playing modes modeled on the forums of the World Climate Conference.

Publisher's English box text:
Is there any chance to reduce the man-made CO2 concentration in the atmosphere until 2050 to around 50%?
Te members of this unusual World Climate Conference -with the simulation game "Cooling Down!" must cooperate, otherwise there is no chance. They need education and money, but also courage and luck, in order to bring the important CO2 reduction strategies into being like in real life. To build up a sustainable future, -here is a trainingfield, to learn.
What does it mean to shape and contribute in climate justice and a culture of prevention actively?
Entertaining Learning. Knowledge of the future, cooperation and communication - interesting, exciting and meaningful. Would you like to be a savoir [sic!, one of many] of the world? Here you have a chance to get in touch with this challenge.

From the creator:

A border-less strategy game where players attempt to surround and outflank their opponent(s) without leaving themselves open to attack in kind. No formation can be strong everywhere though, and advancing and retreating can be equally important. Expand around your opponent's placements and leave your options open while closing off your opponent's. Outflank lasts between 30 minutes and an hour on average and plays either two player or four players in teams.

Theme:

Outflank is themed towards medieval pike warfare, advancing in formations linked back to their starting formation and attempting to find the weak spots in enemy formations.

The theme is light since the game is an abstract.

Gameplay:

Starting from fixed positions, players build out formations to surround and entrap their opponent using limited number of actions each turn. With seven actions a turn you can advance or withdraw cards from the field based on the number of soldiers on the individual tiles. You can also use your actions to draw additional tiles or mercenaries that can augment your cards for further costs. The aim of the game is to take four of your opponent's tiles in a four player game and 6 in a four player team game (combined total with your ally).

Quest with us as we embark into an enchanted forest where the mighty Elf King grows tired of the Faeries in charge and requests a dance of leadership for his enjoyment.  Listen in as Katie, Tim, and Matt take on the roles of Faery faction leaders and attempt to win the favor of the Elf King and thus secure a thousand year reign for our faction of elf.

Elf dance is a simple card game that will provide enjoyment for any age. If you are interested in helping this project get funded you can click the little faeries throughout this post!  But hurry you only have four days left before the Elf King grows angry and banishes this game to the planet Thra!

Objective:
Elf Dance is a 2 to 3 player game. Each player has a Native Clan, determined before play begins. The player’s goal is to recruit as many Faeries and Goblins (Faery and Goblin cards) as possible to the player’s Native Clan,while thwarting the other players from recruiting Faery and Goblin cards to their own Native
Clans. The game itself is one of the players: its Native Clan is always the Goblin Clan, and the game itself may possibly come out the winner. Each player’s recruited Faery and Goblin cards are kept in a pile by the player’s side, and whoever has the most
of these recruited cards when the deck runs out wins the game (bonuses and penalties from equipped Enchantment Cards are also counted).

Journey to the West, published in 16th century, is one of the four major classic novels of Chinese literature. In the story, Master Xuanzang and his four disciples traveled west seeking sacred religious scrolls in India. Long after the fellowship passed the Mountain of Inferno, they came to meet another fierce challenge in the Cavern of Gossamer ...

Cavern of Gossamer is a 2-4 player  micro game based on this chapter in Journey to the West. In the game, players are characters in the novel using their abilities and other disciples, spiders, etc. to rescue or slay the Master. The goal of the disciples is to find their Master and rescue him so they can continue their journey. On the other hand, the goal of demons is to slay the Master in the Cavern of Gossamer and consume his flesh for eternal immortality.

Each turn, player can draw a card or take a role action, such as rescue, slay and etc. If player chooses to draw, player draws a card from draw deck then secretly deploys the card into Hot Spring or Cave OR plays the unique draw rule on the card.

If disciple player chooses to take a rescue role action, player will draw two cards in Cave and attempt to bring the Master to player's hand. When any one player draws To the West card in a later turn and the disciple player still have the Master in hand, disciple team wins the game.

As for demon players, if demon player chooses to take a slay action, player will secretly review all cards in Cave then use two spiders to slay any one disciple and three spiders to slay the Master. If the Master is slain, demon team wins the game.

After players have learned the rules and played the game for few times, players can use option rules to keep track of victory points (VP) for ALL 4 players, including players who are controlled by Monkey King and Centipede players. After number of games, the player with most VP wins the game.

boardgame: Wer fliegt mit?: Die große Weltreise
description: The players start from different cities and have to travel by plane around the world and back to their starting point. The players roll a die to earn money. They get as many coins as they roll eyes. When they have got enough coins to pay for their journey to the next city (as indicatet near the black line between the cities) they roll the color-die to determine if the reach the city (blue), wait one turn for better weather (yellow) or have engine trouble (red). In the later case the have to pay for the journey, but have to stay where they are and resume to earn money. The first player to complete his world tour wins the game.

Pocket Boxing is a light boxing simulation game.

3 dice represent a different style of fighter you may choose. There are also 3 different defense styles, a die for injuries and two dice for ‘initiative’.

Bouts will be quick and intense. Mixing and matching fighters and styles will ensure plenty of rematches and title fights!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Pocket Baseball is another game in the Pocket Sports line up of mini simulation dice games.

The game contains custom dice for the Pitcher, Batters, Fielders. It's a light dice rolling game that captures baseball in a pocket sized travel or beer & pretzels game.

There is some light strategy in the form of POWER PLAYS to increase fielders or runners play during an inning.

Each game comes with 1 of 5 unique team counter sets.
A version of Monopoly sponsored by Ducks Unlimited.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Cygnus is a new Prestige planet. This planet is worth zero Influence and takes 6 Colonies to Settle or 8 Ships to Attack. Instead of Influence or resources, the new planet provides one of each role symbol. Place Cygnus into the middle of the planet deck after shuffling.

Promo planet card available as a reward to backers of the Drive-Thru Reviews Season 5 Kickstarter.

boardgame: Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island – Gamer Character
description:
Rules for the Gamer: One new survivor to take with you on your adventures in Robinson Crusoe: The Gamer. Like all characters in Robinson Crusoe, there will be both a male and female side. This survivor is different from those in the base game. They have no invention, using only their wiles and tricks to help the party succeed.

The available actions are as follows:

Dummy Player: "I'm surprised how easy this is!'" Discard 3(D) to gain Brown/Grey Action pawn this turn.

AC Bonus: "Never count your money while you're sitting at the table." Spend 2(D) to ignore one wound when hunting.

Cheat: "I will do the right thing, once I've exhausted all alternatives." After resolving an Adventure card effect, spend 3(D) to NOT shuffle it into the Event deck, and discard it.

Go Fish: "I suggest a new alternative. Let the island win." Spend 3(D) to draw two Mystery cards. Resolve only the first Treasure from the drawn cards. Discard the rest.
family: Game: Robinson Crusoe – Adventures on the Cursed Island

boardgame: Space Cadets: Dice Duel – Drive Thru Promo
description:
The Drive Thru token for Space Cadets: Dice Duel will allow players to heal their Capital Ship once per game.

To start the game, drop the token from 1 foot above the board. If it lands equally in between a grid space, players agree which grid will hold the token. During the game, players may stop on the token with their Capital Ship. Fighters have no interaction with the token. Players must stop exactly on the token, and shout "Pit Stop!". The Capital Ship will heal the last damage received. Players will remove the last energy crystal or retrieve the last energy die. Discard the token from game.

Strongholds is a set of four tokens for use in Eight Minute Empire: Legends. Three tokens are special strongholds each placed randomly on the boards. It is recommended for your first game to place them on boards that are not the center board.

The Mountain Stronghold will allow its controller to gain one coin per turn. The Forest Stronghold generates one free movement point per turn for its controller. The Cursed Stronghold will let you place the fourth "cursed" token at the start of your turn. No cubes may be placed, nor any special abilities used in the region with the "cursed" token. However, the controller MUST move the "cursed" token to a different non-adjacent location at the start of their next turn. Note: You may move cubes THROUGH the "cursed" token.

Objective: Be the first reaching Kapstadt (Capetown) going round Africa on both sides (west & east); game ends after 4 meeples reached Capetown. Winner gets most of payments-in/victory points.
- acting by turns -
Simple 'Roll & Move' game - move one of your 4 meeples forward according die roll. Ending on marked spaces follow instructions glued on lid inside. Push back other meeples, roll again if you have got a '3', skip a turn... 
Theme: 'Free Germany's ex-colonies' related to spirit of the age & time
Note:
Likely published during 1930's

In Ultimate Werewolf: Inquisition – Full Moon, the full moon is rising, and when it does many of the players will get new abilities. As soon as one werewolf is killed, the players have a new option each turn; instead of visiting the home of a resident, they can use the ability that the full moon gives them...but only once per game.

Full Moon includes a full moon card and six new player Inquisitor cards — three new werewolf team roles, two new villager team roles, and a brand new team role, the Purist — each with a unique full moon ability.

Malifaux uses cards, not dice, and the beautifully illustrated card pack is called a Fate Deck. Show your faction loyalties by purchasing the Fate Deck linked to the color of your faction.

Fate Decks are ALL THE SAME, only the box and the outside of the card has changed color, the number and face cards of the playing deck are the same.

Two Fate Decks are needed to play the game, one for each player.

Progress: Industrial Revolution is an expansion for the base game of Progress: Evolution of Technology. It consists of 55 Age IV cards.

The Industrial Revolution expansion depicts the major technologies developments in the area of Culture, Engineering and Science in the period of the first industrial revolution, from the end of the Renaissance to days of the first World War.

Milestones is a mini-expansion for the base game of Progress:
Evolution of Technology. It consists of 16 cards:

    7 Ancient Milestones
    5 Medieval Milestones
    4 Renaissance Milestones


Milestones depict major achievements in human history to which more than one nation contributed significantly.

With the Milestones shuffled into a face-up deck, players work cooperatively to achieve the current Milestone. Each player may use free actions to pay knowledge and thus contribute to the current Milestone and once a Milestone is completed, each contribution player gains specific advantages.

Personalities is a mini-expansion for the base game of
Progress: Evolution of Technology. It consists of 15 cards:

    5 Ancient Philosophers
    5 Medieval Scientists
    5 Renaissance Artists


Each Personality provides a bonus for the controlling player. The Ancient Philosophers also determine the turn order while the Scientist and Artists bring to the game the Heritage mechanism allowing the players to give up their long term bonus for a one-time larger one.

>…incoming transmission…receiving PCX1g82… 
>…engaging PCX1g82 [auto-msg]…
>…engaged…[auto-msg: The leader in intergalactic Space Bean cultivation, trade and shipment, Space Coffee is here. To make your first cup our first priority…[end].

>Space Coffee is a morning-time space race for 1-4 'rising' coffeenauts, and takes about 15-30 minutes to play.

>Requires one cup/pot/french press/espresso machine/device of coffee (or other beverage), Space Coffee Star Map, 2d6, 7x Space Beans/tokens, 12x Ships/tokens.

>Players roll 2d6 and add the values together to generate energy (e). Energy is used to ready or move Ships, avoid black holes, engage in Space Battle, and to harvest or deliver Space Beans to Morning Star - “the premium intergalactic coffee roasting non-stop shop”.

Energy Guage
> 8e - You may not ready a Ship. 
- Use only the highest die as Energy points.

≤ 8e - You may ready one Ship or dock at the Outpost.
- Use only the lowest die as Energy points.
Always remember the age-old adage: “If you ready a Ship, take a sip!”

>Ships are readied at a player's home Outpost and sent out for Space Bean collection and delivery. But space is weird and movement can be difficult in certain areologies. A simple areology index shows how much more energy is required when entering a hex of different types:

Areology Index
Black Space — 1e to enter; sip at will
Planet - 1e to enter
Space Haze — 2e to enter; a sip of coffee upon exit
Asteroids — 3e to enter; a sip of coffee upon exit

>But beware of black holes! At the start of every turn, any Ships within a 2-hex (or 3-hex for increased difficulty) radius are 'pulled' 1-hex towards that black hole! 'Pulled' in? Gone!

>Made it to a planet with a Space Bean on it? Gotta harvest that puppy!

Harvest a Space Bean from a Planet - 1e
-Ships carrying Space Beans may only move up to 2 hexes per turn!

>Deliver Space Beans to Morning Star by moving through its hi-tech non-stop-drop trackway!

Enter an adjacent Black Space hex with an open opposite Black Space hex and successfully move to the opposite hex.
Delivery — 2e; a sip of coffee

>Docking a Ship at an Outpost lets you move any one of your Ships 1-hex in any direction at the start of your turn. And some of the old outposts have copper-coil showers, and a shower is a good thing out in deep space. But bear in mind, it only takes entering a docked Outpost hex with +3e to return a docked Ship to that player's supply!

>Thought you were safe anywhere else in space? Think again! Engage in Space Battle by moving into an occupied hex with +2e remaining! Steal a Space Bean by rolling >3 with the unused die! Force an opponent into an occupied hex, destroy him!

>The game is over when all Ships have returned to a player’s outpost (total win), or when a player’s Black Hole has devoured all of the coffee in the cup/coffee pot/french press/espresso machine/device that started the game.

Blackbeard. Calico Jack. Anne Bonny. Names that live on in infamy, along with their legendary exploits. Can you continue their legacy?

In this high stakes game of ship combat and capture, you are the pirates from this Golden Age, and your hand of cards is your flagship! Seize the most valuable vessels you can, embark on lucrative campaigns, and prey upon the ships of your fellow rogues — but beware the Black Spot, for danger lurks within! You'll find hurricanes, mutiny and vile curses, and there’s not a soul to be trusted. Alliances will rise and fall as you seek to build your fortune. When the last volley is fired and the rum is flowing, whoever pillaged and plundered their way to the most loot earns the title of most "infamous", winning the Pirate Wars — and the game!

Infamous: The Pirate Wars is a game for 2-4 players, played in turns, and lasts anywhere from 30 to 60 minutes. Working from a hand of randomly drawn cards, you capture ships by matching sets of cards of the same type, and by placing those ships “in play” on the table. Ships are made up of valuable Loot cards that provide you with gold, and powerful Attack cards that help you defend it. Balancing the two is a key strategy in the game. For once in play, ships can be used to attack and capture ships belonging to other players.

Action cards aid you in this endeavor, and Campaign cards give you extra objectives to achieve along the way. Event cards round out the deck, providing dangerous obstacles to overcome. The game ends when one player has at least 12 cards on the table (across all their ships), and can discard their last card. The winner is the player with the most loot!

boardgame: Marvel Ultimate Spider-Man Trickshot Duel Action Game
description: Green Goblin hatches an evil plan to break Spider-Man's archenemies out from behind bars on the S.H.I.E.L.D. Helicarrier. Face off in the game arena as Spidey or Green Goblin and fire discs to take out your opponent's hero or villain pucks. Last one standing in the Helicarrier arena is the winner.

This game reproduces the collision of Akita War of the latter half of the Boshin War, Shonai feudal clan and the Kubota feudal clan (Akita feudal clan).
One is in charge of other Ouetsu-reppan allied force (allied force) by a game of two play in new government troop.

Victory condition
If an allied force gains control of Akita, the allied force wins promptly.
Or new government troop wins promptly when new government troop gains control of Tsuruoka.
The draw becomes the victory of the new government troop.

Sequence
1. Reinforcement • Relocation phase     
2. The first Shonai feudal clan action phase   
3. Kubota feudal clan action phase    
4. Allied force action phase
5. The first new government troop action phase
6. The second Shonai feudal clan action phase        
7. The second new government troop action phase
8. Morale recovery phase
family: History: Boshin War & Meiji Era Rebellions

description:
Expansion for Panzer Division Kommandeur 2, 1944 East Front.
Total 80 counters in this module.

Time Scale : 1-2 days(8-12 turns)
Play time : 4-8 hours
Players : 2
Complexity : 3 (Highest 5)

boardgame: One Night Ultimate Werewolf: Bonus Pack 1
description: One Night Ultimate Werewolf: Bonus Pack 1 consists of three new roles, three new artifacts, and three alternative artwork cards for use with One Night Ultimate Werewolf and One Night Ultimate Werewolf Daybreak. the three new roles consist of the Aura Seer, who learns who has woken up in the night and viewed or moved a card; the Cursed, who is a villager unless a werewolf votes for him (in which case he transforms into a werewolf and joins their team); and the Prince, who is a villager with the handy ability of not being able to be killed.

Build words and crush your opponent by flipping to your color (blue or red letter tiles) his or her letter tiles.
Aim: To get the largest number of tiles of your color on the board by forming words

The players have to travel from one of four starting points to a set destinations or reverse. The movement of the meeples is determined by a die. In some cities, the players gain or lose game coins. The first player who arrives at his destinatin wins the game. The second place goes to the player, who has the most game coins at the end of his journey.

This is a children's game based on memory. Two to four players scatter some colored little balls on the play board and then use a tube shaped as a question mark and press it by turns against the balls so they stick inside the tube. When there are four balls inside a tube the oldest ball will fall out from the other side of the tube. If the player guesses the color of the ball that is going to fall he takes it and plays again. When a player gets four balls of his color she wins.

The players start at space 1. The first to reach space 60 wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. At certain spaces, traffic related events hinder or help the players, as described in the game rules. The game is designed to teach children about traffic signs.

A 2-player pure abstract strategy game using a single stash of Looney pyramids. Each player has 3 pyramids in hand, and 9 pyramids are arranged lying in a circle with a gap for a 10th pyramid.

On your turn, place one of your pyramids into the gap and remove a pyramid of a different size, n spaces clockwise or counterclockwise from the gap, where n is the pip-count of your placed pyramid.

If you have no legal move, you lose. If all 3 of your pieces in hand are the same size, you lose.

Description from the publisher:

"My purpose is to destroy the daemonic, and if I must rise to command an entire sector to do so, then so be it.”
   –Torquemada Coteaz

It is your duty to safeguard the Imperium and vanquish corruption, and to accomplish this, you must bring the Traxis sector under the Emperor’s protection. The Threat Beyond is the fifth War Pack in the "Warlord" cycle for Warhammer 40,000: Conquest, and it calls you to fulfill this noble goal with a new Inquisitor warlord for the Astra Militarum. This War Pack also continues the major themes of the Warlord cycle, introducing new warlords and new ways to take advantage of their prowess.

Sixty new cards, including one warlord, his eight-card signature squad, and three copies each of seventeen distinct cards bolster your forces in this expansion. You must vanquish all enemies of the Emperor in the Traxis sector, but in this war you will face Ork warbands, fearsome Noise Marines, and the might of the Eldar Wraithknights. Choose your faction and battle for the fate of the Traxis sector!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
"Les Guerres Indiennes" ("The Indian Wars") is a series of solo games feigning all the conflicts which set the federal government of the United States against the Amerindian tribes throughout the XIXth century. The first volume of this series is centered on one of the characters the most known for this epic, colonel Georges Armstrong Custer.

In this game, three game boards represent the historic space of three major fights which delivers this hero of the western conquest to tribes Lakotas, Cheyenne and Arapaho: Washita (in November 27th, 1868), Honsinger Bluff (in August 4th, 1873) and Little Big Horn (in June 26th, 1876). On these maps, 1 cm represents approximately 200 meters and a game turn represent approximately half an hour of time.
The lenght of this game is 10 turns, it is the time which you have to manage  the 7th of cavalry, the famous regiment of Custer, and allow him to fil his mission in spite of Indians warriors which will oppose you vehemently.

You can repel the American Indians who threaten your survival? You will try to replay history, but you can also explore new tracks by following the thread of the numerous proposed hypothetical scenarios. The task is difficult, but glorious. One thing's for sure, trust, it is a good day to win!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The Werewolves of Miller's Hollow: The Pact
description:
The Werewolves of Miller's Hollow: The Pact consists of The Werewolves of Miller's Hollow base game as well as the three existing expansions for that game as of 2014: New Moon, Characters, and The Village.

The Werewolves of Miller's Hollow is a game that takes place in a small village which is haunted by werewolves. Each player is secretly assigned a role - Werewolf, Ordinary Townsfolk, or special character such as the Sheriff, the Hunter, the Witch, the Little Girl, the Fortune Teller and so on... There is also a Moderator player who controls the flow of the game. The game alternates between night and day phases. At night, the Werewolves secretly choose a Villager to kill. During the day, the Villager who was killed is revealed and is out of the game. The remaining Villagers (normal and special villagers alike) then deliberate and vote on a player they suspect is a Werewolf, helped (or hindered) by the clues the special characters add to the general deliberation. The chosen player is "lynched", reveals her role and is out of the game. Werewolf is a social game that requires no equipment to play, and can accommodate almost any large group of players.

Some versions of The Pact also contain a few promo cards for Dixit that feature art inspired by The Werewolves of Miller's Hollow.

Polak Mały is a new memory based educational board game from Institute of Polish Remembrance. With help of puzzles, that you can also use for memory game, players can learn Polish national symbols such as Poland coat of arms, Polish flag and national anthem. Quick and fun way to teach children from age of four basics of national identity and heritage.

Game features five mini games for example a quick game where three teams are racing to arrange a line of Polish anthem, the one which succeeded to do it first without a mistake wins - you check it by flipping cards if they are correct they will form Polish ensign. You can also play a game for two people by dividing puzzles by colours (red and white) once you have done it you lay them down in six rows (three white and three red). You start by playing memory game by finding cards from red and white rows with matching texts of Polish anthem, if a player succeed to do so he takes cards, player with most cards wins.

BLT - A Dicey Sandwich Game is a dice rolling game where players will press their luck as they try to earn money by building BLT sandwiches, earning tips, and avoiding burnt toast.

A turn consists of a player rolling the 12 custom dice, setting aside the ones they want, and re-rolling the ones they don't. Burnt Toast die can't be re-rolled and will take money away from a player's total. A turn ends when all dice are set aside or a player can chooses to stop rolling. The first player to $50 is declared the winner.

The Golden Fleece Cup is the most coveted championship in the world of sheep soccer. Players take the roles of the two teams who've made it to the championship game. The rules of sheep soccer are vastly different than the game played by humans. So, players will need to strategically place their sheep throughout the board as they jump, nudge, kick, and block the ball as they try score.

Sheep Soccer is a strategy game for two where players use their three sheep pawns to try and score three goals before their opponent.

Kakumei! Jinsei daifūgō (Revolution! Millionaire's life) is a variant of the classic Daihinmin.

This version has a new look and adds event cards.

Hypergames Warriors is an expansion to Krosmaster: Arena. It contains:

    Four figures (Argon Gass, Arthur Bine, Count Frigost, Goultard the Barbarian) and four Sinistro miniatures
    Four character cards
    Four stickers with stats for the Sinistros
    A code to play the characters online


At the moment the box is available only at Auchan Hypergames stores in France.

Saving the world just got a little bit harder in Pandemic: State of Emergency, an expansion for Pandemic that offers three new challenges:


    The Hinterlands Challenge, in which the diseases spread from animals to humans.
    The Emergency Events Challenge, in which unpredictable events have nasty effects on the game.
    The Superbug Challenge, in which a fifth disease that cannot be treated threatens the world! To fight off this threat, you must first find its cure, using quarantines to stop the spread of disease in the meantime, then produce vaccine doses. You must eradicate the superbug disease to win. This is NOT for the faint of heart...


Part of the Pandemic series.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A card game where each card represents a character of brazilian society, or an historical event. The cards have famous quotes of/about these characters that reflects on each card's power.

There are 6 suits: politics, literature, entertainment, sports, society and fiction.

All players begin the game with 2 cards and $5.
Each player can execute an action per round:

    Grab a card from deck at table;
    Get $2 from the bank;
    Put down a card, paying its value;
    Discard a card and get $5;
    Pay $3 and get a card from the discard mount.


Each player can have a maximum of 5 cards in hand.
Each player have his own table.

When the card is placed on the table, its effect is activated.

The game ends when the last card is taken from the deck. Then a last round is played and the game is over.

The points are counted, based on cards' values and number of cards of the same suit.

boardgame: Moral Dilemma: Famous Moral Dilemmas of History
description:
An expansion or booster pack for Moral Dilemma which includes 25 additional moral dilemma cards that can be mixed into the full game or played separately. So you and your friends can debate such historical factoids, like:

"Did you know that the Nazis were the first to realize that smoking led to lung cancer and started the first anti-smoking campaign? True story.
Do you:
a) feel like a bit of a Nazi if you are against smoking?
b) feel cool for copying the Nazis?"

A part of Milton Bradley's "T.H.I.N.G.S." series, players try to roll barrels across the bridge while Go-Rilla raises and lowers it.

From the box:
Can your roll the barrels to safety before time runs out?

Push the explorer's button. A barrel will roll out of his backpack and onto the rocking, rattling bridge.

Keep pushing the button to roll out the barrels. Will they roll safely into the cabin - or get knocked off the bridge?

When the timer stops Go-Rilla pops the bridge open! How many barrels made it into the cabin?

A wind-up dexterity game, part of Milton Bradley's "T.H.I.N.G.S." series of games. Try to flip over all of the discs and capture them with the magnetic hammer.

From the box:
Can you flip the lids before time runs out?

Start the timer by moving the mouse counterclockwise as far as it will go. The platform will start turning.

As lids pass in front of Dr. Wack-O press the button to flip the lips label-side down.

As the flipped over lids pass in front of the hammer, whack them to flip them into the slot!

Whack out all the flipped lids before the timer stops - or Dr. Wack-O will flip right out of his lab!

A dexterity game from Milton Bradley's "T.H.I.N.G.S." line of games. Knock all of the wood slices from the rotating tree before the timer runs out.

From the box:

Can you chop down the stump before time runs out?

Fit stump into base, then stack stump pieces onto buzzard post, facing target pegs in any direction.

Fit post into stump hole and you're ready for action!

Move switch on to start the stump turning.

Pull back on axe trigger and then let go! Aim Jack's axe at the peg to whack a piece off the stump.

Chop down all five pieces before time runs out or the stump will jump and the buzzard will buzz off.

8 Kingdoms, a fantastic collection of card games for 2-8 players, features characters and artwork from the world of Royal Strawberries and Imperial Harvest. Includes:

Kingdom Quest
2-8 players
30 minutes
Ages 12 and up

Summary: Players start with an Emperor (representing themselves and their realm) and are given a hand of cards from which they must draft/recruit new champions to their Kingdom. Points are obtained through a combination of melding sets (identical champions from multiple realms), runs (a string of champions from the same realm), collecting Kingdom Cards (champions from your own realm), and completing specialty Quests.


Control & Chaos
3-8 Players
45-90 minutes
Ages 12 & up

Summary: A 3-suited, trick-taking card game where players earn points for successfully completing their bids. After 5 hands, the game ends and the player with the most points wins! Can you maintain Control or will the four Chaos Cards disrupt your play?


Scourge
2-6 Players
45 minutes (full game); 15 minutes per round
Ages 8 and up

Summary: Players start with an identical hand of cards (their kingdom's champions) and simultaneously bid on cards revealed from the Scourge Deck. High cards win treasure cards, low cards win scourges (which have special abilities), and all ties cancel out. When all cards have been claimed, the round ends and players tally up their scores from accumulated treasures and scourges. The first player with the most points after 5 rounds is the winner.


Redemption
3-8 players
20 minutes
Ages 8 and up

Summary: Players take turns guessing how many cards of each suit are held by all players. When another player believes someone has guessed too high, that player may challenge the acting player. When a challenge is made, the loser must continue playing with one of his cards facing toward his adversaries. New hands are drawn with successively less cards dealt out each round. The last player to still hold cards is the winner.

Goblin Hunters is a fast paced push-your-luck style card game combined with set collection and a betting mechanic for 2 to 4 players and is played within 10 to 15 minutes. Goblin Hunters comes with multiple character cards to choose from, each character comes with their own special ability, which can be used once per turn when they go hunting to give you an added advantage.

The Search deck contains 3 types of cards: Goblin Cards, Wound Cards and Goblin/Wound Cards. Try to collect as many goblins as possible without taking too many wounds. While also trying to collect sets of goblins to gain extra bonuses.

This is not the only way to score bonus points, you can bet on the results of the other adventurers, risking your hard earned goblins points for more bonus goblin points. It's a high risk with a high rewards. So the next time your in the tavern healing from your wounds and mouthing off about who the best Goblin Hunter is, make sure you can put your money were your mouth is and bet on your fellow adventurers.

The goal, to be the best Goblin Hunter in the land by capturing 20 goblins first.

English version of the SárkánySakk board game (hungarian).


     For all ages with simple rules to be learned easily, and still lots of possible strategies for elder gamers to develop.
     3D gameplay experience.
     Uncountable board variations.
     Balanced gameplay, you always have the chance to overcome your opponents playing with any dragon.
     Gameplay length is adjustable to your demand by setting the winning goal to the right value


The heart of the game is moving your dragon on to an orthogonally adjacent cube. When you move from one cube to another, you rotate the cube you are leaving, rotating it in the direction you are moving. If you rotate the cube you just left to the neutral color or your dragon’s color, you get to move your dragon again. In this way, the mottled board slowly becomes green when the green dragon moves, then becomes red when the red dragon moves. The goal of the game is to be the first dragon to score 100 points. Points are scored when you are done moving for your turn. Each cube with your color face up at the end of your turn counts as a point, whether you landed on it this turn or not.

boardgame: Runicards: Dungeons – Os desafios do Deserto
description:
Runicards: Dungeons - Os desafios do Deserto (RDDD) is a board game of action, strategy and adventure that takes place in the same universe cardgame "Runicards: Batalha pela Terra do Fogo" (RBTF). New players do not need the original game to play, but veteran players will feel at ease with this new game.

This is a cooperative game, initially for 1-4 participants. This means that you and your friends are not trying to defeat one another, but will together meet the challenges of the board and try to win in each match group. Each player chooses a hero and every monster defeated and disarmed trap all earn points that can be used to enhance the powers of their characters. Also, you can find treasures to equip your hero with armor, weapons and magic accessories.

boardgame: Lone Wolf: The Battle for Magnamund
description:
In Lone Wolf: The Battle for Magnamund, you  are Lone Wolf, the last of the Kai Lords. Or perhaps you are Prince Pelathar, heir to the throne of Sommerlund. Or maybe you are the infamous Giak warrior Kootak, sent by the Darklords to raze the land. Now you can be all of these... in a hand-painted (by Gary) tabletop wargame.

Battle for Magnamund is a fantasy wargame for two or more players. Each battle pits good against evil, and players may control either an entire army or a single brigade on one side at a time.  The goal is to allow you to re-create any battle or scenario from the classic Lone Wolf books, and the game comes with pre-made scenarios to start you on your way.

The core game set consists of flat cardboard figures (3 mm thick and die-cut) in a plastic base, played on card maps that are grid-based and modular.  The maps are the same material as in Cry Havoc.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Players sit around an electronic sound machine in the shape of a man, controlled by one of the players. One of the players secretly controls the machine, which makes sounds of passing gas. Players bet on who is controlling the machine, and the winner advances along a scoring track.

Esper energy flows throughout the universe, binding it and permeating all things. The mighty champions of the Darkspace Calamity channel esper to fuel their actions and unleash devastating attacks.

The Relic Knights: Battle Deck contains everything you need to power your games of Relic Knights!

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Adventures In Turtleopolis Game
description:
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Adventures In Turtleopolis, is graphically based on the late 80's cartoon that originated from the comic book by Kevin Eastman and Peter Laird.

2 to 4 players play as one of the four Turtles competing around the board while trying to avoid traps and bad luck spaces. The object of the game is to reach the end of each game board, which supposedly represent a different mission:

Game 1: Race for the Blimp 
Game 2: Fight for the Sky 
Game 3: Battle for Cereal Castle
Game 4: Dash for Home

These four boards came on the back of the Ralston Cereal Boxes and as such are in conjunction a very simplistic version of similar games like "Snakes and Ladders". Just throw the dice and let luck decide in which space you´ll end up. Some spaces have rewards while others punishments.

There was another Ralston´s TMNT cereal box board game called "Adventures Under Turtleopolis" that came out during the same time, that featured amazing art with TMNT thematic enemies and elements such as the Technodrome and sewers.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
You are a gemcutter, a master shaper and collector of rare and beautiful gemstones. You are constantly supplied with gems of all kinds from amber to diamonds. You compete with other gemcutters to amass the most valuable collection of gems.

Each player starts the game with a single random gem and the communal Market starts with three random gems. On a player's turn, he or she draws a random gem from the bag and adds it to his or her store. Then, he or she may add one of his or her gems to the Market to gain extra actions equal to the gem's value. The gems are valued 1-5, in order from least to greatest: amber, jet, emerald, ruby, and diamond. Then, that player takes 1 action (in addition any extra actions gained) in which he or she may: trade a gem with the Market, shape a gem, or add another gem to the Market.

When a player trades in a gem, he or she may take other gems from the Market less than or equal to the traded gem's value plus one. For example, if a ruby (value 4) is traded in, it is worth 5 and may be used to trade for a diamond (value 5), an emerald (value 3) and jet (value 2), and many other combinations based on what is available in the Market.

When a player shapes a gem, he or she places it on any of the three appropriate Shaping Tracks on the player aid. A gem may be Polished, then given a Fancy cut, then a Brilliant cut. A gem cannot be given a Fancy cut until it has been Polished and cannot be given a Brilliant cut until it has been given a Fancy cut. Polished gems are worth twice as many points at the end of the game. Fancy cut gems are worth triple and Brilliant cut gems are worth quadruple their values. However, it takes a number of turns equal to its value to be shaped. For example, an emerald (value 3) takes 3 turns to make an uncut gem into a Polished gem. Therefore, the more the points, the longer it takes to shape it.

When the last gem is taken from the bag, the current player finishes his or her turn and then the game proceeds to scoring. The player with the most points wins the game.

boardgame: Office Shark Tank: Don't Get Eaten Alive!
description:
Office work is bad enough but with the addition of office sharks, it's deadly! Start your day at the 9 a.m. starting space and try to avoid being eaten alive by the mechanized hungry office shark. Flick the spinner and depending on what it stops on, you either advance or retreat.  After moving, if you land on one of the "blood spots", it's feeding time! Wind the shark up, set him on the playing board and let him rip! After 3 bites, you're out of the game. If you make it to the end of the day at the 5p.m. finish line, you've made it out of the office alive!

A fun little mini board game to play in the office or at home!

Name That...! Person, Place, Thing... is the quick thinking name game in which 2 or more players, 6 years and older, race to be the first to call out an acceptable answer to the category starting with a given letter. The game consists of a deck of 50 cards, and the object of the game is to finish with the most cards.

To begin play, the deck is shuffled thoroughly and placed face up in a pile on the playing surface. Designate a dealer who will read the categories and starting letters for the first round. The dealer reads aloud the category on the top card and then places it next to the deck to reveal the letter on the next card. Players race to call out an answer to the category starting with the letter revealed on the next card. The first player to call out an acceptable answer wins the first card. In the event of no answer or a tie, the card is set aside and the next winner of a card wins both cards. Play continues until all but the last card has been won. The player with the most cards wins.

Note: This is not the same as Name That...! Person, Place, Thing..., even though it has the same name, and was published in the same year, by the same publisher.

Girl's Time is a 2-5 player bluffing game. The player that gets the most money will win the game.

In this game, there are two customers on the table and they need some goods such as shoes, clothes, etc.  Each player has three cards and on their turn must choose one of three actions:

1. Play cards: You can play up to three cards, face-down, so you can bluff. If somebody think you are bluffing, they may call "check" and reveal all of your cards this turn. If they called your bluff correctly, you must pay them $100 per card, otherwise they pay you $100 per card.

2. Finish a sale: If you think most cards on a customer are correct, you can finish the set and sell them. If most cards are correct, you can earn lots of money. On the other hand, if most cards are incorrect, you have to pay money back to the customer.

3. Change your cards: pay $200, redraw five cards and keep three.

This game is very is to learn and play. In most case, if you want to play correct card, you have to trick the other players because they want to "check" you. If you want to play an incorrect card, you have to trick them to sell the product and lose lots of money. In this game, trickery is the most important thing.

Pithecus is a simple close tactical combat game. The players control groups of early primates: Ramapithecus on one side and Australopithecus on the other.

The primates are rated for strength, movement, and morale. Each may take one of four actions (attack, stay, flee, or fight) each turn, but must make a morale check first. There are some simple terrain rules. Wounds and death are recorded by rotating the "counters," which are actually folded paper cubes.

Two maps are provided, each in two sections.

The game was one of Kaylor and Humrichouser's Games With Depth, which all used folded 3D paper pieces.

Note: Ramapithecus is today not regarded as its own genus, and in any case lived millions of years before any of the Australopithecus species.

Wedge-It is played on a board of forty equilateral triangles arranged in a hexagonal grid. Each player has nine pieces which are equilateral triangles with handles (these are created by cutting and folding provided sheets).

The handle of each piece identifies its front. A piece may move into the space adjacent to its front by rotating around either adjacent vertex. A piece may also jump an opposing piece if its front is adjacent to one of the opposing piece's sides.

The object is to make it impossible for the opponent to move.

This is one of Kaylor and Humrichouser's "Games With Depth," all of which had folded 3D paper pieces.

boardgame: Islamic Jihad: "The Mother of All Surrenders"
description:
Islamic Jihad is a parodic micro-game about the first Gulf War. It can be played by any number of players, each of whom represents an Iraqi Army commander. Each commander has 100 troops and must travel 10 miles (in 1/10 mile increments) in order to surrender to American troops.

Play is entirely driven by die rolls. Each turn players roll on a main table which directs them to roll on one of six other tables, which may require more dice rolls, etc. Eventually each player will find that they move backwards or forwards some number of spaces or lose some number of troops.
publishers: Tri Tac Games LLC (Tri Tac Talents)

description:
These 5 Scenarios add new situations for the crash-landed crew of the Pioneer.

Scenario 7 is Radioactive, which affects Bugs and Shields!

Scenario 8 is Hard Impact, which damages the crew from the onset of the game.

Scenario 9 is Condition Unknown, which forces the players to repair the ship, but also introduces the "Special" tokens provided in the core game.

Scenario 10 is Prime Directive, and offers a solo play scenario!

Scenario 11 is Super Shields which makes use of the Special Shields Tokens and is designed to be an easier Scenario offering some extra Shield help.

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Fear Itself
description:
With the Legendary Villains: Fear Itself expansion for Legendary and Legendary: Villains, you can play through the "Fear Itself" storyline from Marvel Comics. This expansion consists of six new allies from The Worthy: Kuurth, Nul, Skirn, Nerkkod, Greithoth and Skadi; one new adversary group; one new commander; and three new plots.

Players can work both with and against other players to defeat Iron Man in his Uru Enchanted Armor as he leads the forces of The Mighty while laying waste to planet Earth!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Description from the publisher:

As it boots up the SanSan Cycle, The Valley transports players deep into the heart of SanSan, the number one place for all the top tech. This American megacity sprawls along the California coastline from San Diego in the south to San Francisco in the north. There, you'll also find Biotech Valley, most often called just "the Valley", home to the world's biggest biotech firms and its most advanced genomic research. Home, additionally, to Jinteki Corporation's flagship American R&D facility, the J-Plex.

With its sixty cards (3 copies each of 20 different cards), Android: Netrunner – The Valley explores Biotech Valley and its massive investment into bleeding-edge bioengineering. This is a meatspace heavily colored by genengineering, a highly educated workforce, tech startups, capital investments, and the presence of Jinteki's J-Plex. You'll find numerous assets, region upgrades, genetic resources, and a new Jinteki identity. Of course, even in the Valley, you'll need more than good genes to get by; you’ll also find new programs, ice, and currents for both Corps and Runners.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Cabriole is a medium-weight worker placement, card drafting, and resource management game for 2-4 players aged 14 and up. Playable in 60-90 minutes, the worker placement aspect is queue-based, with a simultaneously strategic and tactical resolution phase that might require players to adjust their plans.

The game is set in London during the late 18th century, with players aiming to create the most successful furniture studios by hiring and promoting craftsmen such as carvers, joiners, turners, and upholders; purchasing raw materials such as wood and silk for the best prices; and fulfilling customer orders for different types of furniture. Players will find themselves constantly balancing the need for capital with increasing their studio's prestige throughout the city.

The player who has scored the most prestige points at the end of six rounds is the winner.

Archmagery is a battle of duelling mages, their summoned monsters, dazzling and devastating spells, and powerful magical items, where only the cleverest – or sometimes luckiest – wizard will rise to ultimate power and can claim the title of Archmage!

The aim of the game is to become the most powerful wizard, which can be achieved in a number of ways, from eliminating all your rival wizards, to gaining the Artefacts of Power. Although let’s be honest – killing your opponents to win is much more fun!

Game features include:

    Eight wizards, aligned to the spheres of power: Earth, Air, Fire, Water, Animal, Plant, Acid, and Death.
    A host of monsters from lowly kobolds, skeletons, and gnolls, through to the might of creatures such as the dragon vine, the battle giant, and the iron golem.
    A compendium of spells including those that will transform monsters, steal your opponents’ magic, deflect spells away, bind monsters immobile, strengthen and weaken monsters, and more.
    A hoard of treasures including rings and amulets that grant magical immunity, enchanted horns to summon monsters to battle, magical weapons, powerful arcana to increase spellcasting power, and more.
    Fast and furious game play with battling monsters and spell-slinging as each wizard strives for supremacy.

KaBlooey is a light-hearted party card game that pits player against player in a war for survival. Toss bombs, grenades and even nukes at each other in a bid to be the ultimate victor in this fast-paced game of bluffing and strategy for 3 to 8 players.

The object of KaBlooey is to destroy your opponents’ Bases by blowing the living hell out of them with bombs, grenades, and any weapons you can get your hands on! Each enemy has 3 lives (Bases) and if you’re the last Bomber standing with 1 or more Bases at the end of any turn - you WIN! But, there’s a twist: the cards you play don’t have to be actual bombs! You can bluff your way to military supremacy with a non-Bomb card to try and get your opponents to waste their valuable resources. War is hell, and all’s fair in love and KaBlooey!

boardgame: Egypt: David vs. Goliath – The Italian-African Campaign
description:
This is a WWII simulation about the campaign in North Africa. It is a typical hex and counter game with units representing regiments, brigades, and battalions maneuvering on a paper map. Players attempt to duplicate the historical results to achieve a victory.

Includes a sheet of uncut counters, a paper map, and a rule booklet. It was published in "Ziploc" format so there is no box.

Per a description in Fire & Movement #60 it is a variant for Afrika Korps and requires that game to play.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A roll-and-move game for 2-8 people.
Players agree on how much monney each player gets at the start of the game.

Every player bets on which horse they want to race and then race their horse on the racing-track. If two or more players want to race the same horse, players draw which player gets to race the horse of choice.
Horse 1-4 starts on starting-line 1, and horse 5-8 on starting-line 2. All horses use the same goal-line. As a compensation for starting on starting-line 2, the last four horses get one extra die-roll during the race. The race runs for two rounds around the track.

It's not allowed to pass the horse in front of you in the same track. You have to pass horses by changing the track and pass on the inside or outside. It's only allowed to change track acording to the arrows on the gameboard. So sometimes you'll be blocked from using a good die-roll.

Around the track you'll find spots in diferent colors. Each color have their own special-effect.
Yellow: Double-spot.
Red: Miss the next move, and other horses may pass in the same track (there may be two horses in the red spot).
Green: Double the amount of your next die-roll.
Blue: Use only one die next die-roll.
Black: At the next die-roll, move forward only if getting dice of same value. Unless two-of-a-kind, move backwards in the same track according to the die-roll (you are not limited by other horses behind you).

The first player to pass the goal-line after two rounds wins the race and receives the money in the pot and from each player the same amount of money the winning player put on his horse before the race. Maximum bid on a horse is 500.

While watching a baseball game, all players are dealt 7 cards. The remainder of the deck becomes the draw pile. Before each at bat, all players select one card from their hand, trying to predict the outcome of the at bat. Any number of players may select the same card. If the result of the play is a gomer or triple, player that selected that result receive 2 points. Any other result, players that selected a matching result receive 1 point. All cards selected cards are discarded replaces drawn and then next at bat is played. After the game ends the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Dead Things: The Zombie Boardgame (Third Edition)
description:
The zombies are everywhere, they catch you by surprise and attach when you least expect it. By yourself you might be able to get through the crowd, but it's either all of you or none of you...that was the deal.

In Dead Things, the players need to work together to play against the game. Turn order and movement is controlled by a movement deck and the board is set up so that zombies appear in a programmed order, moving ahead of you if your characters move too slow.

The goal is to find gas, and car keys and leave the board before the zombies overwhelm the party.

Dead Things 3rd Edition is a board game that expands on the original Dead Things game. New maps, new challenges for 1-4 players.

Expansion Set for Warage: Starter Set.

This expansion allows an even more balanced multiplayer match and gives more variety to deck building thanks to the new race and class cards.

Expansion Set for Warage Starter Set.

This expansion expands the cards set and introduces a new game mechanic.

boardgame: Super Smash Bros. The Board Game
description:
Use Nintendo characters to battle opponents using special abilities, items and maneuverability tactics to win the battle. Matches may be timed with a timer or players may use stock number of lives.

Featuring 8 popular Nintendo Smash Bros characters to choose from; Mario, Donkey Kong, Bowser, Link, Pikachu, Fox, Samus, and Kirby!

Each of the characters will have unique special abilities they can perform and that will do damage to opponents just like in the video game. Abilities will do a certain number of damage to an opponent. Once a player’s character has reached damage level of 20 there considered critical. Perform ability 3 to give them a final smash attack to send them flying!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

boardgame: Flash Point: Fire Rescue – Extreme Danger Kickstarter Bonus Pack
description: Flash Point: Fire Rescue - Extreme Danger Kickstarter Bonus Pack is a collection of promos for Flash Point: Fire Rescue given out to backers of the Flash Point: Fire Rescue – Extreme Danger Kickstarter campaign.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Blocks in the West: Card Deck
description: The Blocks in the West Card Deck is an expansion for Blocks in the West and consists of 12 Special Events cards (400g, double laminated, UV matted, 3d).
The Blocks in Afrika: Card Deck is an expansion for Blocks in Afrika and consists of 12 Special Events cards (400g, double laminated, UV matted, 3d)

boardgame: Blocks Series: Early War Card Deck
description: The EARLY WAR Card Deck is an expansion for the Blocks Series and consists of 12 Special Events cards (400g, double laminated, UV matted, 3d)

boardgame: Blocks Series: Middle War Card Deck
description: The Middle War Card Deck is an expansion for the BLOCKS SERIES and consists of 12 Special Events cards (400g, double laminated, UV matted, 3d)

boardgame: Blocks Series: Late War Card Deck
description:
The LATE WAR Card Deck is an expansion for the BLOCKS SERIES and includes:


    1 card deck, with 12 Special Events cards (400g, double laminated, UV matted, 3d)

Taudelon Station is a game that shares elements with traditional card games and modern economy games. Players will use their hands of cards to create resources and currency so they can build various modules. These modules are worth points at the end of the game and they also modify how the game plays as well. A unique aspect to Taudelon Station is that these modifications change the game for all of the players, not just for the players who own the modules.

Players’ hands come from a deck of cards that has five suits, numbered 1 to 9. Players will either play a combination of at least two cards that equal exactly 10 to create a resource (called Elements), or they will play a combination of cards similar to the various types of Poker hands (pair, full house, etc.) to create the currency (called Energy) that works with the resources to let players build the modules.

Meanwhile, unidentified aliens are swarming around the station, and while the game is fully competitive, the players all must defend the station against the aliens or everyone loses.

In Opération Nez rouge: Le jeu, players form teams of two persons: one driver and one passenger. Each team tries to be the first to get home! The passenger shouldn't snooze! Give the right indications to the driver to ensure to come safe! Driver, you'll try to understand what your funny passenger is telling you.

The game is played in successive rounds. The winning team will be the first one to complete three itineraries. One player picks up an itinerary card and will try to give indications by funny words and gesture. The other player (driver) will have to switch cards displayed in front of him to replicate the right itinerary of the objective card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Wrench
description:
Wrench is a kobold artificer who has always been interested in gadgets and engineering. His voracious appetite for learning led him to the writings of Wizgille the Tinkerer, and eventually, to study with Wizgille herself.

*The Good: He can quickly build gizmos that can do amazing things.
*The Bad: Those gizmos are very fragile!

The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Wrench expands the party at the Red Dragon Inn with an ingenious kobold whose gizmo cards stick around to help him out on later turns! Those gizmos will gradually break down, though, and the other players at the table can cause them to break down even faster!

The Red Dragon Inn: Allies is a series of expansions for any standalone The Red Dragon Inn game. Each Allies set includes a single Character Deck, plus components to add a brand new character to your game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A street racing deck-building game.

Each player gets a unique vehicle with varying strengths and weaknesses.

Each round, players will try to have more power than their opponents so as to get a better trophy. However, they must simultaneously strive to have sufficient handling in order to avoid taking damage, which not only saturates your deck with useless cards, but also decreases your overall score.

Once one of the three trophy card stacks (Bronze, Silver, and Gold) is depleted, the game ends. Players count their total score from their trophies and then subtract one for each damage card in their deck. Whoever has the highest score wins!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From the box:

Object of the game: Move all the tees from the tee box to the green. Don't forget this is an exciting, strategy game with lakes, sand traps and obstacles.  So get your golfers together and have fun.

User Description:
Mulligan's Wild is a golf-themed roll-and-move game with a "send your opponent back to the start" mechanic similar to Backgammon or Sorry. Although this game claims to be "Golf game of the year," the golf theme seems entirely pasted on. Players roll dice and try to move their pieces (or "tees") out of their tee box, along the track, to their green and then into their hole.  There is a special "Mulligan's Wild" die included, which allows players to move a number of spaces of their choice. First player to move all tees onto the matching green and into the matching hole wins.

"Get ready for adventure as you cross a deep chasm on rickety swinging bridges!  The trail to the tiki is easy to follow if you know how to use blends and digraphs--but watch out for broken boards!"

Tiki Challenge is an educational game to teach children words using blends and digraphs. Players have a series of tiles displaying blends and digraphs. Each player spins a spinner, moves along a track to a space with a partial word, and must use a tile from his/her hand to complete the word. A player who is unable to complete a word must return to the start. The first player to reach the tiki at the end of the track wins.

Cadence is a 2 player biking microgame in which players are professional cycle racers going head to head on a race circuit. Racers use their stamina, shift gears, make use of abilities like 'drafting' and 'sprinting' and strive to reach to the finish line first. The game makes use of unique mechanics like card interlocking, sliding and rotating cards, 3D like vertically folded racer cards to fit a fairly complex but tactical game in just 18 cards. The race is played on a track made up of 6 'track cards' which are either flat sections or climbs and descends of different gradients. Double sided track cards allow for a second more difficult track to be made from the backs of the cards giving replay ability to the game. There are 2 racers to chose from while they each have their own special abilities the other one does not have. You can play a big game of 3 laps or a short filler of 1 lap, so immerse yourself in the world of bike racing, micro style!

Monsters invade! Heroes are ready to defend in groups of two, but these heroes are out of control and need an intelligent leader to lead them to victory. Can you do it?

Flatten out Monsters is a new type of memory game in which you need to memorize only six cards! Can you effectively lead the heroes and flatten out the monsters?

To set up, monster cards are placed in a 3x7 grid and double-sided hero cards are placed in a 3x2 grid in front of the monsters. Heroes in the same line — that is, a line of two hero cards — will fight the seven monsters lined in front of them. On a player's turn, he must swap two hero cards, then flip them to the reverse sides. These two cards activate the cards in the same line with them and become the attacking groups, then you resolve the attack on the monsters one by one in each activated line. If the two hero cards show the icons needed to defeat the first monster in line, they win the fight and score the monster card. Then they fight against the second monster, and so on until they fail. Play continues in clockwise order until at least two lines of monsters are all defeated, and whoever has the most monster cards wins!

It is your job to help connect coaches to the train. You are not only responsible for adding coaches of the colour assigned to you, but to also complete the train as quickly as possible.

A short train building game for two, consisting of only 19 cards.  The winner of the game is the person with the most valuable coach segment in the color they are responsible for. Train coaches are worth different points based on their class, and coaches can only be added to coaches on the train if they have the same class or colour, where first class passenger coaches cannot be added directly to third class coaches, and vice versa.

Players face a simple choice on each turn: Take a train coach card in their hand, or add a coach coach from their hand to the train. But beware - coaches not placed at the end of the game counts against your score.

One Shot is aptly named. It is said her relic's massive Goliath Cannon can bring down a starship. It is a theory that few enemy commanders are willing to test. One Shot combines her devastating fire power with an unmatched skill commanding her troops, guiding their fire into the enemy ranks with surgical precision.

Gifted with rare and mighty gifts, Relic Knights are the ultimate leaders for your cadres. More than just powerful game pieces, these stunning models are beautiful centerpieces for your miniature collection!

Sophia Drake grew up among the mercenaries of the Galactic Merchants' Guild, so it was no surprise that she would join Black Diamond as soon as she was of age. Now she commands Black Diamond's elite heavy infantry division, the Black Dragons, and seeks to restore Black Diamond and GAMA to its former glory.

Flowering Snail is a refreshing abstract game that challenges players to plan ahead over the two stages of the game! Players compete against each other by increasing their snail population. You need to place your snail cards and flower cards wisely to claim the delicious flowers (for the snails, of course), and maintain your snail population to score the most points!

The game is played in two phases: Multiplication and Die Out phases. In the Multiplication phase, players alternates to place their Snail cards (with values 1 to 3) onto the game board adjacent to other snails already there. As an additional action, they may also place one Flower card onto the game board. After all Snail and Flower cards are placed, players check who controls each Flower cards by adding the values on their Snail cards. The higher sum may take the adjacent Flower card as scores.

In Die Out phase, players alternate to remove Snails cards from the game board if the chosen Snail card has a lower value than all the other adjacent Snail cards, until no more Snail cards can be removed. Each remaining Snail card scores the printed value for its owner, and each Flower cards worths 2 VP. The player with higher VP wins the games.

Castles and Keeps is a 2 player head to head card laying game using a deck of only 9 cards.

On each card there are four castles which contain a different combination of player shields.

Players take turns to place a card matching the castle type and at the same time trying to cover their opponents shields with their own.

Once all cards have been placed, players with three or more castles connected by a road score one point for each.

As soon as a player gets to 30 points they are crowned the champion.

Castles and Keeps is a free print and play game.

An additional Shadowrun: Crossfire character card included with pre-ordered base sets. Oni is basically an ork character with Oni Evolution sticker and 5 Karma printed on the sheet. It also features a race-specific art and description.

boardgame: Shadowrun: Crossfire – Harlequin's Shadow Promo Card
description: Harlequin's Shadow is a promo Crossfire Deck card given out during GenCon 2014. It is used in Close The Portal scenario available for download from Catalyst Game Labs website.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Cube 112 Expansion Pack

This expansion comes with one new ship and contains ship cards, captains, and tokens necessary for use in "Star Trek: Attack Wing".

The oversized borg cube measures an impressive 8 inches on each facing.
