boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Deep Space Nine Expansion Pack (Retail Variant)
description:
The gateway between the Alpha and Gamma Quadrants, Deep Space 9 is a pivotal and strategic Federation space station.

The retail variant of the Deep Space Nine Expansion pack differs from Star Trek: Attack Wing – Deep Space 9 Expansion Pack, featuring an all-new premium paint job and new stats.

Included are the GenCon Star Trek: Attack Wing – DS9 Upgrade Card Pack, two ship cards, a maneuver dial, and all necessary tokens for gameplay.

Sunrise City: Nights!, the first full expansion for this much loved game. Isaias Vallejo, the designer, set out to bring a little bit of the night-life and casino flavor to the base game and to add a couple of fun mechanics to what many have called a "New Classic" board game.

The Sunrise City: Nights! expansion gives you more of what you love most in the base game. 

    The 16 new Role Cards give you more customizable player powers and more options for bending the rules to your advantage.
    Two new meeple Roles have a familiar feel but their ability to roam through the city gives you new options to block your opponents.


The silver Benchmark Tokens are worth 5pts each so you'll never run out of stars.

Nights! also introduces two new game systems to enhance the game and add a casino-feel to your city-building adventure.

    Each player starts the game with a set of five special One-Zone Building (OZB) Tiles that they can play over the course of the game. They play just like any other Building Tile but their size lets them fill in isolated zones or fit neatly onto the edge of a growing skyscraper. OZBs also present new scoring methods based on the current configuration of the city so their potential effect on your score and the city itself, is different each time they're played.
    The Fortune Die is a six-sided die with the red, green, blue and yellow on four sides and purple on two sides. Certain Role Cards and new Building Tiles use the Fortune Die to create a casino-feel with probability-base rewards.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Described by the publisher as a "multiplication and division poker game," It's a Cinch is an educational game designed to teach multiplication tables. Three stacks of poker-style chips with answers to multiplication tables printed on them are placed in the center of the table. Each player is dealt five cards with single-digit numbers. Each player in turn tries to find two cards in their hand that can be multiplied to match the total on one of the three top chips. The player then captures that chip, discards the two played cards, and draws two new cards. They player may then try to capture another chip (up to three per turn). When the player reaches three chips or cannot capture another chip, play passes to the next player.  If a player makes a mistake and plays the wrong cards to capture a chip, the player must put both that chip and one previously-captured chip on the bottom of one stack and pass his turn. After all the chips are claimed, the player with the most chips wins.

There are a couple of variants which add extra rules, as well as a solitaire version.

Educational game of bingo to teach antonyms and synonyms.  Cards are laid out just like a bingo game (5 x 5 grid), but the "free" space is labeled either as "antonyms" or "synonyms".  The rest of the spaces are words.  The game leader pick a word from the 121-calling card deck and call it out.  Those players with "antonym" on their free space will cover any word that is an antonym of the called word.  Those players with "synonym" on their free space will cover any word that is a synonym of the called word.  Any player who covers 5 spaces in one row of their card - either vertically, horizontally or diagonally, will call out "Quizmo!".  They are the winner of that game.

Against the background of the history of the City of Lokeren (a city in Flanders, Belgium) players try to acquire local products (pigs, hares, hats, linseed, corn, turnips) and sell them on the market. With profits made on the market players can construct buildings or improve their modes of transport (cart, horse and cart, truck). In the meantime players can obtain title deeds and chance cards which are worth victory points. The one with the most victory points at the end of the game wins.

Turn the card and try to locate the right animal character on the game board - if there is one! You receive a card for each victory and the player with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner.
Find the same picture on two different cards and mark them with your token. The player who collects the most cards this way wins the game.

Roll the dice to find out your animal colour combination, and try to find it among the tiles which are spread on the table. Once you find your tile, mark it for yourself and move closer to victory. The winner is the one who first is able to place all of his markers.
Contains three different game versions. A fun game to test the speed of the players' reactions. Comes with great, colourful game parts.

This is a smart pack of cards that includes multiple games in one. Each card shows a number, with different symbols and colours to denote how that number can be achieved. For example, the card for 18 shows a red heart (the game's symbol for 2) and a yellow cat (the symbol for 9), a red and yellow pattern, and eighteen dots arranged in a 2x9 grid.

New games playable with the pack appear regularly on the website. The base game is a game that helps to learn multiplication tables while playing the game.

Travel through Sweden to become the first to reach the goal, and thereby win the game.
But be carefull! Landing on an orange spot can be good or bad news for you (move to another place or wait some turns). And if you land on a red place you take one of the 24 Chance cards - this is the same as an orange, but more random.

An exciting drive through Sweden, with a lot of local informations. Family game - also for the little ones.

Travel through Denmark to become the first to reach the goal and thereby be declared the winner.
But be carefull! Landing on an orange spot can be good or bad news for you (move to another place or wait some turns). If you land on a red place you take one of the 24 Chance cards, which is random and unforseen; it could be good - but it could also be bad!.

An exciting drive through Denmark. Family game also for the little ones.

boardgame: As Tears Go By: Operation Starlite
description:
As Tears Go By: Operation Starlite is an introductory level game on the battles of US Marines and the NLF in I Corps of Vietnam in August of 1965.

The Battles of Operation Starlite were the first large-scale fights waged by the Marine Corps in Vietnam. A spy informed the South Vietnamese I Corps commander that an attack on the US Marine Base at Chu Lai by the NLF’s (National Liberation Front) 1st Regiment was imminent. The Marine’s commander, Lt. General Lewis W. Walt convinced his superiors that the best defense was a pre-emptive attack. He also decided against informing or working with his South Vietnamese counterpart, as past intelligence leaks led to the elusive Viet Cong disappearing in the face of the American’s probes and attacks.

Operation Starlite depended upon the coordination of amphibious landed troops of the Marine’s 3/3 Battalion while those of the 1/7 marched from the north to serve as the “anvil”. The “hammer blows” would be delivered by artillery, air and naval support strikes, as well as the 2/4 battalion that would be airlifted into battle. It was hoped that surrounding the VC with multiple threats would trap and force them to do battle.

Once upon a time there were two villages--alike in every way-- nestled in a land surrounded by fog. It was obvious to each that the other did not belong...

The Belongers is a 2-player semi-abstract strategy game, played without a board, wherein each player tries to raze the opposing village to win.

The game is set up by arranging the hexagonal terrain tiles in one of the map configurations available in the rulebook (or a custom map created by the players).

On their turn, each player takes two of the three possible actions: draft a terrain tile to the map, draft a unit, or move and/or attack with a unit.

Additionally, each unit may use its special ability AND terrain bonus once per turn. Each of the units has a unique special ability, and each of the terrains offers a unique bonus...creating huge number of possible combinations for players to explore as they maneuver around the map, attack enemies (either directly, or by pushing them into hostile terrains), and finally, destroying the opposing castle.

A deep strategy war game that plays any small flat surface.

Not playable on their own as you need two starter decks to play the game.

Battleground Fantasy Warfare is a point-based tabletop tactical wargame in which units are represented by cards.

A new faction for Battleground, inspired by the terracotta warriors of Ancient China.
Includes both the base and reinforcement decks in a single package, as well as the basic and advanced rules.

It also comes with all requisite tokens and command dials, as well as more than a dozen ship and upgrade cards. The expansion’s two ship cards allow you to outfit your Star Destroyer as either a Victory I or Victory II, and its fourteen upgrades open a wide range of strategic possibilities as they allow you to upgrade your armament, your crew, and even your Star Destroyers themselves.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – CR90 Corellian Corvette Expansion Pack
description:
The first starship to appear in the classic Star Wars trilogy, the CR90 Corellian corvette is a small capital ship of roughly 150 meters that featured prominently in the Rebellion, largely due to its versatility. Often referred to as “blockade runners,” CR90 corvettes can be outfitted to slip through Imperial blockades, haul cargo, serve as trooper carriers, or engage Imperial ships in battle.

You can add one of these versatile starships to your Rebel fleet with the CR90 Corellian Corvette Expansion Pack. The expansion comes with one pre-painted miniature CR90 corvette, one command dial, all requisite tokens, and ship cards for both the CR90a and CR90b configurations. Additionally, eight upgrades allow you to equip your corvette for battle, including the commander card, Mon Mothma, and the Tantive IV title card.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Nebulon-B Frigate Expansion Pack
description:
The Nebulon-B frigate was designed for the Imperial Navy by Kuat Drive Yards as a warship that could protect convoys from Rebel starfighters, but it was, ironically, a much more impactful ship within the Rebel fleet. At three-hundred meters long, a single Nebulon-B could boast twelve turbolasters and twelve laser cannons.

The showpiece of the Nebulon-B Frigate Expansion Pack is its detailed and pre-painted Nebulon-B frigate miniature, and the expansion then enhances this ship by supplying all the command dials and tokens you need to fly it, along with ship cards that allow you to build it into your fleet as a fighter escort or as a powerful support ship. Additionally, the expansion’s eight upgrade cards, which include two unique title cards, allow you to further customize your frigate’s role within your fleet.

description:
A cooperative game set in the aftermath of the War of Oak and Pine.

In The Autumn War, players each assume the role of a member of the Oak Kingdom or Pine Kingdom or a member of the outcast Walnut tribe. Players work together to reveal map locations, hoping to find the bodies of loved ones they lost in the recent battle.  Along the way, they must overcome hazardous terrain, inclement weather, and insidious creatures.

Players will need to rely on each other, as well as their ability to find useful items and safe locations to protect them from harm and to survive through the night.

Can you find your lost loved ones and put them to rest before they turn into Lonely Ghosts?

Whoya? is the one and only party game that’s all about you. You make the game, you play the game, and you become famous. Whoya? is a fun, hilarious way to get to know all about the people you are with and for them to get to know all about you!

Every round there is a judge that reads the prompt on a Whoya? card out loud. All other players write their response down and turn it in. The reader then selects his or her favorite response, and tries to guess who submitted that response. If the judge guesses correctly, the judge keeps the Whoya? card and earns the point for that round. If the judge guesses incorrectly, the player who submitted the judge's favorite response gets the Whoya? card and earns the point for that round.

Many of the prompts on the Whoya? cards are crowd-sourced from users who have submitted card ideas. On these cards, the first name, last initial, and location that person is from is displayed in the bottom right hand corner of the card.

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Ten Days or Whatever of Kwanzaa
description:
Cards Against Humanity: Ten Days or Whatever of Kwanzaa consists of ten (or so) items that may or may not be expansions for the party game Cards Against Humanity, but we're taking a chance that at least some of them will be cards and therefore justify the existence of this game listing.

The Sloth promo card was given away as a prize for solving puzzles hidden throughout the Ten Days or Whatever of Kwanzaa promotion. 250,000 cards were stored in a safe on the island "Hawaii 2", which was also given out in square foot sections during the same promotion.

Covering the 1814 campaign in a half sized magazine game.
This game uses the same simplified card-driven system as Conquest of Shinshu from the same designer.

The map covers the Eastern France from Sedan to Paris.
The 110 counters includes leaders, strength points and markers.
The game uses only 16 cards.

The victory is basically determined by moral of each force. If one side's moral drops to zero, he instantly loses the game.
Coalition player wins the game by eliminating Napoleon or controling Paris, he also must take a number of important cities each turn.

Issue game of Command Magazine Japan #119.

boardgame: Fox Trott': La Guerre des Oeufs
description: Protect your chicken coop by throwing eggs at the fox. If you hit him, he moves closer to your opponent's coop. When the fox reaches one of the coops(the far right or the far left end), the player loses a point. The player who first loses 5 points loses the game.

Hakkaa Päälle! (Advanced Squad Leader Module 14) provides the ASL player with the complete order of battle for the Finns, including every major vehicle, gun, and squad type that saw combat during World War II. Chapter H distills years of research into pages of detailed notes on the guns and vehicles involved. Included along with three new Finnish squad types are the revised Chapter A rules to handle them.

Finnish ordnance and armor throughout this period are represented from the single Landsverk armored car that was the only combat-worthy Finnish AFV at the start of the Winter War, through the end of the Lapland War, when Finnish armor included captured Russian AFVs and Sturmgeschuetz purchased from Germany. Modern tanks like the T-34/85 or StuG IIIG were in the distinct minority, however, and the Finnish player will often find himself equipped with obsolete tanks such as T-26s and T-28s when facing the enemy.

The Soviet Union was Finland’s chief adversary, and Hakkaa Päälle! also contains new vehicles and Chapter H notes for the Russians. Fan-propelled sleds (aerosans, both armed and not), ad-hoc armed LANO trucks, early war versions of the T-26 (the two-turreted T-26 M31 and the flamethrower-armed OT-26, among others), up-armed T-28s, and the experimental SMK and T-100 tanks are all included. Additionally, a number of Lend-Lease Vehicles (Lee, Matilda II, Valentine VIII, Churchill III, etc.) now see Russian counter form. To simulate the lackluster performance of the Russian forces during the Winter War, SSR-invoked rules for Russian Early War Doctrine are included.

Captured French tanks such as the 38H 735(f) and the 35-S 739(f) used by the Germans in Finland are included in German colors, along with similar German AFV counters whose availability to ASL players was previously limited.

Player aid dividers include the National Capabilities Chart with the new Finnish squad types and other updates, backed by the standard OBA flowchart. Also enclosed are new rules for Prepared Fire Zones and Light Woods, transforming the battlefield for mapboards old and new. The heavily wooded mapboard 52 completes the package.

Contents:

        1 8˝×22˝ Map (52)
        4 Countersheets
        16 ASL Scenarios
        1 Chapter H for Finnish Vehicles/Guns and Russian Lend-Lease Vehicle/Guns
        1 Rules Supplement, with updates pertinent to Hakkaa Päälle!
        4 Chapter Dividers, including a revised National Capabilities Chart with new Finnish squad types, and the OBA Flowchart


Hakkaa Päälle! is not a complete game. Ownership of the Advanced Squad Leader Game System is required for play.

description:
Culica is a collection of 5 games all played with the same equipment. The game components consist of a 3 inch cube which has a 3 by 3 array of holes in each face, a set of pegs (which fit in the cube holes) of 4 different colours and a black bag.

Along with the normal meaning of adjacent, pegs are considered adjacent even if round the corner on an adjacent face.

The following are the rules to one of these games, "CuColours"
14 pegs of each colour are put in the bag. On their turn players draw one cube from the bag and place it in a legal hole on the cube if possible. Pegs may only be placed if adjacent to at least one other peg and no peg may adjacent to a peg of the same colour as that peg. The winner is the player who placed most pegs.
The other games are CuMolecula, CuSnakes, CuMatch and CuCombat.

English description:

Have you ever dreamed of being a child again? To see again the world as if it were a game to your service? To play... we are some famous dragon slayers ... in the playground. To forget everything that is not funny and focus only dream about your friends? What the gray world we live hides a multitude of colors? So Faraway is for you.

Faraway is a narrative card game, in which players have to tell stories mixing reality and fantasy, as if to return to their child age. A narrative experience in a group, in which all players participate and contribute to an adventure in which everyone has fun: a collective experience of creation.

Relying on a evocative and elegant illustrations, let's have fun showing many skills: brainstorm, displayed as good storyteller, propose suggestive outcomes, even master the rules and intricacies if yours is the competition. Faraway is a narrative experience for creative, an opportunity to reunite the family, a stimulating way to learn, Faraway is making dreams come true.

The game takes place over several chapters (depending on number of players), usually 6 to the basic game. In chapters, players will have to play cards in their hand in secret, the narrator will take all the cards, shuffle them and reveal. The other players have to vote the letter they deem best represents the next scene (not being able to vote their own). The owners of the two most voted letters, earn a point and make a proposal to continue the story narration. The other players will vote which of these two proposals is the best. The winner will get another point and will be the new narrator.
The same procedure in all chapters.
At the end of the game, the winner is he who has the most points.


Spanish description:

¿Alguna vez has soñado con volver a ser un niño? ¿A volver a ver el mundo como si fuera un juego a tu servicio? ¿A jugar a que tú y tus compañeros sois unos famosos cazadores de dragones... en el patio del recreo? ¿A olvidar todo lo que no sea divertido y centrarte sólo en soñar con tus amigos? ¿A que el mundo gris en el que vivimos esconde un sinfín de colores? Entonces, Faraway es para ti.

Faraway es un juego narrativo de cartas, en el que los jugadores tienen que contar historias mezclando realidad y fantasía, como si volvieran a tener cinco años. Una experiencia narrativa en grupo, en la que todos los jugadores participan y contribuyen a crear una aventura en la que todos se diviertan: una experiencia colectiva de creación.

Apoyándonos en unas evocadoras y cuidadas ilustraciones, vamos a divertirnos demostrando muchas habilidades: aportar ideas, mostrarse como buen narrador, proponer desenlaces sugerentes, incluso dominar las reglas y entresijos si lo tuyo es la competición. Faraway es una experiencia narrativa para los creativos, una oportunidad de reunir a la familia, una estimulante forma de aprender, Faraway es hacer realidad los sueños.

Designed by contestants of the 2014 series of the hit BBC show "The Apprentice". Manufactured and distributed by Drummond Park

Explore the world with your family and GeoKnow. Learn about language, food, movies, songs, and lots more in a hilarious and and interactive way. Have fun using your best drawing, and performance skills, you have to get your team to guess what country it is!

The object of the game is to end up on your colored circle in POP CITY ESTATES with the most money or win automatically by being the first person to reach the POT-O-GOLD.

Players start with $2,500 each.  Using two dice die the players make their way around the board through the 8 Soda Towns buying properties as the go.

Obsessive Cartographer is a solitaire game for the Tiledeck.

Setup

     Shuffle the tiles face-down.
     Draw one tile and place it face-up on the table.


Overview
You just woke up in a dark room (represented by the tile on the table). You see one to four doorways (spots at the edges of the tile) leading out of the room. This suggests you are in a mappable location. You are obsessed with mapping, and of course have your cartography tools with you. Choose a direction to explore, and try to map all 60 rooms!

Goal
Map the darkness!

Play

     On each turn, choose a doorway to walk through (anywhere on your budding map).
     Draw a room (tile), and place it face-up adjacent to that doorway, such that a doorway on the new tile matches with the one through which you just walked. If there are any other adjacent rooms, their doorways or walls must match: You can never have a doorway up against a wall!


Game End

     If the room you drew can’t legally fit, you have discovered that you have made a cartographic error, and you go into fits thinking about how you can fix your map!  You lose consciousness, and while you lie on the floor, your mind carefully blocks out the whole experience.
     If, however, you place the 60th room legally, you are quite pleased with yourself, pack up your new completed map, and take a nap.  While you sleep, something gives you a mighty whack on the head and eats your map; you won’t remember any of this when you wake.

In a World of Dinosaurs is a territory and resource collection game for 2-4 players where you play simultaneously as Paleontologists in the present, and Dinosaurs in the past, on two separate boards. One board represents the island millions of years ago, and the other represents the island around the early 1900’s 
Players defend their land from opposing teams, expand their territory, and explore the jungle for artifacts, all the while strategically fossilizing dinosaurs in areas of the island they control.

As they kill off these dinosaurs, players assemble their fossilized remains, and send them off to the museum to start attracting visitors. Sure, they may be narrowly avoiding certain death while they do so, but who isn’t?

The better the dinosaurs, the more visitors walk into your museum. The dinosaurs can be assembled in any combination (for example Tyran-dactyl), to immediately attract visitors, or players can hold out to assemble matching parts for more points.

1. An epic military game focused on ranks and branches.

2. Win trick to defeat your opponents.

3. Formulate a plan that works best for your hand

4. Be the first to discard all your cards to win the game.

5. Make strategic moves with variou special cards.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Assault Frigate Mark II Expansion Pack
description:
The largest and most heavily armed of the Rebellion’s first Armada starships, the Assault Frigate Mark II is a light warship seven-hundred meters long that sports a crew just under five-thousand and features a considerable armament of fifteen turbolaser batteries, fifteen laser cannons, and twenty quad laser cannons. Designed in secret by Rebel engineers, the Assault Frigate Mark II frequently surprises Imperial admirals with its speed.

The Assault Frigate Mark II Expansion Pack introduces one fully pre-painted Assault Frigate Mark II starship miniature, along with all the command dials and tokens that you need to bring it to battle. Two ship cards allow you to configure your Assault Frigate Mark II to better fit your fleet, and fourteen upgrade cards allow you to further refine its role within your fleet as you select your crew, upgrade your armament, and assign your commander.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Gladiator-class Star Destroyer Expansion Pack
description:
Originally designed as an escort for larger Star Destroyers, the smaller Gladiator-class Star Destroyer measures roughly five-hundred meters and proved most effective as a long-range patrol ship deployed to the fringes of the galaxy. There, its brutal armament of turbolasers, concussion missiles, laser cannons, and TIE squadrons allowed it to quickly subjugate any local opposition to the Galactic Empire.

The Gladiator-class Star Destroyer Expansion Pack introduces one detailed and pre-painted miniature that the expansion’s two ship cards allow you to add to your fleet as either a Gladiator I or Gladiator II. You’ll also find all the command dials and tokens that you need to bring your Star Destroyer to battle, along with ten upgrade cards that can enhance its attacks, allow it to move between attacks, and improve its accuracy.

Even in the largest of battles, a single pilot can sometimes prove the difference between victory and defeat. Some of the most pivotal battles of the Galactic Civil War have been decided by the Rebellion’s courageous starfighter pilots.

Squadrons play an important role in the tactical fleet battles of Star Wars: Armada. Though they’re dwarfed by the capital ships they accompany, squadrons are not to be ignored; swarms of them can take down even the largest of ships. By adding eight squadrons of A-wings, B-wings, X-wings, and Y-wings to your fleet, including unique squadrons led by such aces as Wedge Antilles and Tycho Celchu, the Rebel Fighter Squadrons Expansion Pack helps to tip the scales in your favor.

The Imperial Fighter Squadrons Expansion Pack for Star Wars: Armada introduces an array of eight starfighter squadrons, evenly split between four different types of fighters. By adding these TIE advanced, TIE interceptor, TIE bomber, and TIE fighter squadrons to your fleet, you'll be able to swarm your foes, protect your capital ships, and send the Rebellion’s hopes down in flames.

boardgame: Thirty Years War: Wallenstein's Bloody War
description:
First published in Double Charge #4.

Covering the Thirty Years War.
This is a third game of 60 series, using 60 counters or less and playable in 60 minutes or shorter. The game uses popular "Taiheiki" system by the designer. And the rule is only 2 pages. Victory is decided by victory points which can be gained by controlling areas or eliminating enemy's leaders.

2nd edition Command magazine #183 (2025)
Original mini-game substantially expanded with scenarios for Schmalkaldic, Knights and Peasants Wars. Plus optional Reformation rules. 111 counters, 12 pages of rules.

boardgame: Warlord: Saga of the Storm – Ports of Treachery
description: This Adventure Path Set (#8) contains 110 cards in two different pre-constructed decks; one is Free Kingdoms and elf is the other. This expansion is for Warlord: Saga of the Storm 4th Edition.

From the box:
CRUX 25 consists of a game board and twenty-five high quality plastic pieces. Alone or with other players this game will fascinate you right from the beginning as you try to put down the twenty-five pieces on the game board. Caution! You might have to use all your abilities to solve the mystery of CRUX 25. CRUX 25, super training for your concentration and cleverness. The ultimate aim of the game is to put all of the 25 pieces on the game board. Colour and size of the game pieces have no influence on the solution of the game.

boardgame: Star Wars: The Card Game – Chain of Command
description:
Description from the publisher:

“I am Grand Admiral Thrawn, warlord of the Empire, servant of the Emperor.”
   –Grand Admiral Thrawn

Stand beside Grand Admiral Thrawn and declare your command of the Imperial Navy with Chain of Command, the fifth Force Pack in the "Rogue Squadron" cycle for Star Wars: The Card Game! This Force Pack grants you the tools you need to scramble your starfighters and control them in tense, tactical dogfights. Like the other Force Packs in the Rogue Squadron cycle, this expansion focuses on your cutting-edge starships and the ace pilots who fly them.

Whether you fly for the light side or the dark side, you’ll find new Vehicles for land and air. You may mire your opponent in endless bureaucracy or challenge him to a break-neck swoop race. You might make a profitable run on a smuggling freighter or return to Luke Skywalker’s beginnings as a farm boy on Tatooine. No matter which affiliation you fight for, the tricks and tactics you need to attain victory can be found in Chain of Command.

Contains objective sets 161-165.

description:
Le Sultan du Feu is a solitaire game simulating the campaign of the Egyptian expedition from July 1798 to September 1801 Directoire required by the Directoire and led by General Bonaparte.

In this game, a map in two parts (2xA3) is the historical area of this campaign: the Upper and Lower Egypt, Palestine and Syria, from the Mediterranean to the Red Sea. 1 cm is about 25km and 1 round of game three months in real time.

A game is played in 10 rounds, the time you have to conquer Egypt and face your enemies, Mamelukes, Turks and English who oppose you vehemently.

boardgame: With All Do Respect: The Party Game With An Adventure
description:
With All Do Respect is the first ever party board game with a realistic adventure to it!

Just imagine asking your friends and family any question you want without getting into trouble! That's right! You can't get into trouble because you said "With All Do Respect!"

Tonight you and all your friends are going downtown for an exciting night out but tomorrow you have to get up early for work or school.

The object of the game is to move around town stopping at different locations to collect party points and then be the first player to make it back home.

You collect party points by answering fun questions about each other or anyone you decide is lucky enough to be on your Vip List. You also collect party points by singing at the Karaoke Club, dancing with all your friends at the Night Club, telling jokes and making up new rules to follow at the Comedy Club, gambling on all your favorite games at the Casino or stopping by the Tattoo Parlor to draw fun pictures of your friends, famous celebrities and much, much more! Along the way try avoiding the police because you could get arrested for driving suspiciously and have to spend time in Crook County Jail!

The first player to collect 12 party points and make it back home - Wins The Game!

From the publisher's website:

"Use Q-bitz Solo as a solitaire challenge or to add another player, a new color and additional pattern cards to the original Q-bitz game. This personal-sized version of our best-selling game includes 20 new pattern cards, a wooden tray, 16 cubes and instructions, all packed in a travel-friendly tin box."

In this first expansion for Epic Death!, it is a time of even more mighty heroes and much-desired ancient treasures, as a host of brave new adventurers step forward to claim lost artefacts of unimaginable power. It's time to go Hardcore!

Hardcore Mode contains two brand new modules for Epic Death! which add all new game-play to the base game. If you're looking to bring in more strategy and depth to the game, this has been made with you in mind!

Hardcore Mode is broken down into two modules: A set of Epic Adventurers (comprising of 40 cards) and a bunch of new Epic Loot (made up of 52 Cards). If you like, they can be played with individually but for the full Hardcore experience it's very much recommended that you bring them together.

You'll also remember the incredible treasures found in the original game. Epic Loot adds an entirely new Loot deck full of Magical Loot which can affect gameplay in surprising and... 'interesting' ways when equipped on your Adventurers. You'll get 40 all new Loot Cards as well as 12 very special Heritage Powers.

There's a reason we're calling it Hardcore Mode...

Housing Crisis is a light two-player strategy game where you assume the role of overwhelmed real estate brokers trying to place lodgers into a new suburban development without wasting any housing space or upsetting them by cramming too many into one plot.

Tokens are drawn at random from each players private store so that two are always to hand and ready to be placed, which keeps the luck/skill balance well proportioned. Additionally, as the token values are fixed for each player, as the game progresses and the board starts to fill, it becomes easier to guess what the other player might be holding, increasing the chance for a strategic play.

Players take turns placing game tokens (representing the daily clients calling into the office) onto the shared game board, housing them as effectively as possible within the parameters of the rules. The winner is the agent with the most points after each player has placed all their tokens down.

boardgame: Hoppers: Chinese Vampire Action Adventure Game
description:
What are Hoppers? Hoppers are Chinese vampires (a.k.a. Jiangshi). Unlike the blood-craving vampires of Western myth that turn into flying bats, Chinese vampires do not transform and their bodies become stiff. The only way for them to travel is to hop and that’s why they are called hoppers (well, that’s why I call them that).

Hoppers is a 4 to 9-player action/dexterity party game for all ages. In the game, players are guardians of the realm and each holds a power stone that gives them the ability to travel into the Mystic Forest. Player must beware, however, because one of the players may be a demon in disguise and hold the evil Hopper Stone.

Each game of Hoppers is played over the course of up to five rounds. In each round, players attempt to pass a dexterity challenge in the Mystic Forest and only successful players can examine the stones of power. When a player’s matching power stone is found, the player has the ability to capture the Hopper Stone and win the game. Otherwise, the game ends in the Hopper’s Way and all guardians disappear into the dark of the Mystic Forest.

boardgame: Spaghetti Junction: Lay it again Sam!
description:
From the back of the box:

Gulf of Mexico, halfway the 20th century (sic): the installation of platforms and pipelines in the Gulf of Mexico has started. Challenging times for visionary pioneers! Who will b the player with the necessary vision, daring and skill to expand his pipeline network in the fastest way? Who lives by the principle "if you can dream it, you can do it" and outsmarts all the other players?

Purpose of this game:

    connect 4 platforms and 1 landfall by pipelines...
    choose the best starting point for your pipeline network...
    install one of two pipe joints per turn...
    foresee the moves of your fellow players...
    and use their installed pipe joints for your network!

boardgame: The world of Stena: let the game begin
description:
From the back of the box:

This is the game about the Stena Sphere. You buy, sell, swap and build Stena's various business areas to win. You will also meet unforeseen events on your way towards honour, success and fame. By using good businessmanship, timing and some luck - hopefully you will end up as a winner! read the rules carefully. No cheating and let the game about Stena begin!

User Summary:

The game plays in a similar manner to Monopoly. Although the board layout is circular, and you're bidding more for assets, employees and properties. The aim of the game is to bankrupt your fellow members.

The hotels and houses of Monopoly are replaced by Head Offices and Offices respectively.

Archer: Fast and Loose is a free print-and-play card game of insults, getting the upper hand, unfortunate phrasing, and "sploosh" based on the hit TV show, Archer.

Each game round comprises two phases: the ISIS Phase and the Missions Phase.  In the ISIS Phase, players secretly choose a room in the ISIS intelligence agency building to move to, with each room conferring a different bonus to the players located there.  During the Missions Phase, players vie to get placed on and then succeed at their preferred mission, with each mission requiring different skill sets, such as Smarts, Guns, Booze, and even Sex!

Each player also has a deck of Agent cards characterizing one of the main personalities from the show, with different wacky and zany take-that effects for each: have players shout "woo-hoo!" when they join a mission due to Cheryl's "Boarding Party", or have the Sterling player play with his fingers in his ears thanks to his cruel mistress, Tinnitus.

Through succeeding at missions and playing cards, players can gain Sploosh, and the player with the most Sploosh at the end of the game wins!

Don't Drop the Meatballs is a novel take on Jenga or Kerplunk. Players pull game pieces from a clump of game bits, trying not to cause an accident.

On your turn, carefully pull out a strand of spaghetti, but don't let any meatballs roll off! It gets harder and harder to do and if one rolls off the table, the player that did it is out of the game. The last person left, wins! For 2 to 4 players.

Contents: Table top, table base, 20 strands of spaghetti, 5 meatballs, rules

Stalingrad: Gorod Smerti (City of Death) is a regiment to brigade/division level boardgame.

.5mi/.67km per hex scale. 11 weekly turns, 4Sep-19Nov42. Odds CRT with Disruption.  Mass or pinpoint Luftwaffe bombing. East bank Russian artillery. Snipers:  Zaitsev, Fritz, Kulikov, Thorwald, and Tanya Chernova. Engineer battalions vs. bloody exchanges.

boardgame: Imperial Settlers: Why Can't We Be Friends
description:
Imperial Settlers: Why Can't We Be Friends, the first Empire Pack for Imperial Settlers, includes new common cards for the central deck, new cards for each of the base game's four factions, and two new cards for use in the solo game with some factions.

One new effect on the cards is "open production", an ability that allows an opponent to visit your building for the resource produced there while giving you the worker who made that trek. Hope you can put him to use once again!

boardgame: Combat Commander: Battle Pack #7 – Leader of Men: Tournament Battle Pack

description:
A game of hilarious medical disasters. Players each take on the role of doctors at a poorly supplied hospital. Doctors compete with each other to treat patients using whatever tools they are able to acquire. Players also have the ability to hinder their fellow doctors in the middle of surgery or just agree to play a round of golf while the patient expires. Patients will begin to stack up fast... both cured and killed. Add em up at the end to determine who among you is the best doctor under the worst conditions imaginable.

Players start with a handful of tools which they quickly put to use when the first patient is revealed. Once the patient is flipped doctors must quickly determine if they can attempt the surgery using the tools they have in their hand.

During surgery other doctors can attempt to hinder the procedure by playing whoops cards which can increase the cost of surgery or steal tools or patients right out from under you.

The length of the game is variable depending on how many patients you want to attempt to treat. This is determined at the beginning of the game. After the last patient has been cured or killed the game is over and doctors count up their living patients subtract the ones in the morgue and arrive at a final score.

After another day in the life of the young child, it's time to go to sleep. But he doesn't even imagines the existence of two mystical beings in his mind, feeding on your emotions while sleeping. One feeds on their dreams while the other feeds precisely the opposite, and therefore seeks to guide the child to the shadowy world of the most ominous and terrifying nightmares. What happens inside the confused and innocent child's mind? Only the battle between these two fantastic endless decide this. Unwary are those who think they are not plagued by such entities, they barely know that life is only a dream and we only puppets...

Limbo is a board game for 2-4 players, based on the universe created by Neil Gaiman. Players face each other for 8 rounds (the 8 hours of the child sleep), trying to influence the movement of the child in main board. For that, they should put their cards on individual boards, forming combinations of runes, skills and summoning creatures to chase the little one. Players actions must be scheduled, and the cards and creatures can be positioned in future rounds, creating a system that must be planned and thinking beyond the present moment.

Animals have always been dependent on the environment in which they lived - had to defend themselves against predators, at the same time being forced to get food and fight to survive. Flying abilities, and exceptional resistance have been really helpful for some of them, while for the others – migrating abilities. The evolution trends are surprising...

The expansion includes three add-ons:
1. "Time to Fly"
2. "Continents" 
3. Mini add-on "Darwin Day"

In the basic version of the game you could create more than 50,000 different animals. Playing only with the "Time to Fly" add-on the number of possible combinations reaches 50 million! That's more than the number of all species living on Earth. But that's not all. Thanks to this expansion there is much more possibilities. Now it’s time to evaluate which species will adapt most to live on land, in the ocean, and in the air!

Minimize is a game that seems to yield some fierce tactics despite being extremely simple. You could even play with Xs and Os.

You almost always want to play your opponent's color, trying to build small "territories" that you can create tiny groups in. The winner is whichever player has the smallest group on the board with no equivalent enemy group. Beginners should definitely start out on the base 5 grid and slowly work their way up to larger boards.

The rules are explained here: http://www.scribd.com/doc/260002289/Minimize

It should be mentioned that it seems to work on square grids as well, and some people even prefer it that way.

Meet the Giges and Gegis in this quick-witted card game.
Be the first to see correct combinations.
Train your concentration, speed, judgment, and awareness.
Win by scoring the most points.
Don't stammer ! Make the right call !

A useful tool to sharpen your mind and keep it sharp.
Easy to learn and easy to play.

Oklahoma Boomers: Silver Mines is a mini-expansion for Oklahoma Boomers, that adds silver mines to the game.

Players will score 10 points for every silver mine located within their homestead at the end of the game.

The mini-expansion contains 5 silver mines represented by translucent crystals, that are placed on the board at the beginning of the game.

Oklahoma Boomers: Sooners is a mini-expansion for Oklahoma Boomers, that adds 3 Sooner meeples to the game.

"Sooners entered the prairie before the official start of the Land Run. Even though that was not allowed. As penalty they must help the boomers by placing cornerstones. But beware! Sooners can suddenly change from a good help to a big threat!"

The mini-expansion contains 3 grey Sooner meeples (and 6 stickers), that are placed on the board at the beginning of the game.

Oklahoma Boomers: Red River is a mini-expansion for Oklahoma Boomers, that adds river tokens to the game.

"The Red River runs along the border between Oklahoma and Texas, flowing towards the Mississippi. At its widest, it is 150 meters wide from shore to shore. Quite an obstacle today, and even more so for the people living there around 1890."

The mini-expansion contains 18 Red River tokens.

Oklahoma Boomers: Santa Fe Railroad is a mini-expansion for Oklahoma Boomers, that adds railroad tracks to the game.

The railroad tracks are placed on the board at the beginning of the game so that they connect two sides of the board. If at the end of the game, a homestead is divided by the railroad tracks into two or more sections, then the player can only score one of these sections. However, of two or more boomers share a homestead divided by railroad tracks, then more sections can e scored.

The mini-expansion contains 15 black railroad tracks.

The Yardmaster Express base game includes 4 Caboose cards. The Yardmaster Express: Caboose Expansion includes 12 more Caboose cards, with values from 3 to 8, as well as 4 blank ones.

At the beginning of the game, players randomly choose one Caboose expansion card. Each such card sets a different extra end game condition. The players that fulfil the condition will be awarded the number of bonus point written on the particular card, at the end of the game.

description:
Lost Legacy Legend is a collection of eight stand-alone games where eight different game designers who are active in the Japanese game market have designed their own games based on the 16 card Lost Legacy system.

The decks are not meant to be mixed with any of the previous Lost Legacy games, nor with each other.

The decks are:
"Dark Congress"
"Pseudepigrapha"
"truth of the universe"
"Red Riding Hood sleeps twice"
"heaven of Hagoromo"
"Muga of frontiers"
"werewolf never sleeps"
"final battle"

A communication and speed game for the whole family where you have to do the appropriate movement linked to the shown card.
Pigeon is flying but what is the mouse doing? 
Do it fast and correcly and win the game!

boardgame: Dice Brewing: Mr. Hop in Essen

description:
This is a checkers variant using an 8x8 board that is viewed as a torus: column A is adjacent to column H and row 1 is adjacent to row 8. Thus, for example, a piece on 1.a3 can move to h4.

GOAL: to be the only player with pieces on the dark squares (called "heaven," light squares are called "hell.")

SET UP: starting position is for a standard game, 12 pieces per side, moving one space forward diagonally, obligatory captures. Kings are made whenever a friendly piece is landed on, and move forward or backward per the norm.

GOING TO HELL (or back to Heaven): when an enemy piece is jumped over, it is moved to the left (of the jumping player) to the nearest empty or friendly occupied  square in the same row. If landing on a friendly piece it becomes a king. If there are no such spaces left in a row the jumped piece is removed from the game. Movement rules on light squares are the same for dark.

The Mage Codex is a cutthroat game of aggressive deck-building and fluid combat set in a fantasy universe.  Your goal is to eliminate all other players by emptying their draw deck.

The objective of the game is to defeat your opponents by eliminating their draw decks.

There are two primary stages to the game: Gather and Battle.

During the gather stage, all players get a chance to draft the cards they want to use into their deck.

The battle stage is all about aggressive deck building, where all the card you want are either stolen from your enemy or you must sacrifice your own life to acquire them.  You will take turns to draw cards (possibly from your opponents sources), then play cards to attack opponents, steal their cards, and protect your own decks.

Will you be the last mage standing?

boardgame: Flapjacks & Sasquatches: Cup of Joe Expansion
description: Expanding the Flapjacks & Sasquatches game with 8 custom dice, 11 new tree types that have in game effects and Universal cards that add or change the rules when played.

English description:
The extension Fief: France 1432 – Tactics introduces four new types of troops. Their special abilities enrich your tactical choices. This extension is particularly interesting with 3 players, and regardless of the number of players, for those who want more control in combat.

French description:
L’extension Tactique de Fief introduit 4 nouveaux types de troupes. Leurs capacités spéciales enrichissent vos choix tactiques. Cette extension est particulièrement intéressante à 3 joueurs et, quel que soit le nombre de joueurs, pour ceux qui souhaitent plus de contrôle lors des combats.

English description:
In this politic expansion, you will find advisers who give a special ability for each family, and the CHARACTER cards that personalize each lord with a trait, good or bad!

French description:
Dans cette extension Politique, vous trouverez les conseillers, qui donnent une capacité spéciale à chaque famille,ainsi que les cartes CARACTÈRE, qui personnalisent chaque seigneur avec un trait de caractère, bon ou mauvais!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Earth is a dead husk. Having mined all of its resources, facing a population many times that which could be possibly sustained and slowly mutating it into a massive, cloying, choking city, we had no choice but to set our greedy eyes on the planets around us and conquer new lands.

After the Information War of the 21st century, the Corporations dissolved national borders and began a new war of their own: the Resource War. Like their European ancestors, who conquered and fought over new land, the corporations started to clash for control of the stars around Earth, as if they had claims to them from the moment of their conception.

Thus began the new arms race, but this time the lessons learned from past wars would be embraced and used to leverage as much advantage as possible. Research into more advanced abilities to control not only the battles, but the hearts and minds of the people who fight them would become key to victory.
Vanquish is a game of intergalactic warfare amongst rival corporations in Earth’s dark future with a near endless number of system configurations and multiple paths to victory – through the council, through research of technologies and advanced abilities, via the creation of popular utopian worlds, or by the might of your powerful fleets.

Each player steps into the powerful and all encompassing shoes of the head of one of the five corporations fighting for the control of the universe. You will need to use cunning, guile, diplomacy, deception, and tactical thinking to make the decisions that will hopefully bring victory, control, renown, and respect to your corporation.

In each round of the game, players select four actions to perform. Players in turn perform one of these. Whenever an action is performed, all other players may also perform that action. The available actions are: Fleet (move ships), Research (develop tech), Council (Create votes on new rules), Planetary (develop your civilization), Energy (generate resources) and Logistics (build new ships). Players score points for colonizing worlds, developing tech, building colonies, passing votes and developing their civilization. The game ends when one player has accumulated 15 points, but that player does not necessarily win, as there are bonus points scored for being the best player in each of several categories.

Set in the Star Wars cartoon time line, Star Wars: Rebel Missions is a cooperative game for 6 to 12 year olds.

Team up with your fellow Rebels in daring missions to uncover the Imperial Codes in pulse-pounding race against the clock! The electronic audio device plays an epic and ominous soundtrack as players move stealthily between the Imperial installations, using dice and card powers to sabotage their factories and thwart their plans. Evade the evil Inquisitor and disrupt the Empire before time runs out to win!

PROMOTES: 
Cooperation and teamwork
Strategic thinking

boardgame: B-17 Bomber Squadron: A Solitaire Game of the WWII Daylight Bombing Campaign over Europe.
description:
B-17 Bomber Squadron'  is a Game where you are a Commander of a Squadron of B-17 Flying Fortresses stationed in England during World War II. Your Goal is to manage your aircraft and Complete a Campaign consisting of a series of Daylight Bombing Missions and score as many Victory Points (VP) as possible to assist the war efforts. Campaigns can be Short (12 Missions), Moderate (18 Missions), or Long (24 Missions).

GAME FEATURES: B-17 Bomber Squadron is a low to moderate complexity level game. Aircraft include the B-17F and B-17G Models, P-47 Thunderbolt and P-51 Mustang Fighter Escort Aircraft. Enemy Aircraft include the ME-109, FW-190, ME-262 and ME-110.

B-17 Bomber Squadron captures the key aspects of the B-17 Daylight Bombing Raids in a fast, fun and easy manner. There are rules for Crew Quality Upgrades, Drop Tanks for Fighter Escort, and dozens of Targets.

NOTICE: B-17 BOMBER SQUADRON is no longer available for purchase as it has been acquired for Publishing! See BIG NEWS Forum below for details.'

description:
The Merchant of Mystery is a single Promo card given out at Game Market 2014 in Japan.

His ability reads:

Play: Look at all cards in the Ruins, take one into your hand.
Trash: if there are 4 or more cards in the ruins, this card's number becomes 15.

SuperSystem 4th edition updates the world’s most comprehensive and versatile super skirmish game with a fresh new look that includes expanded attributes, new and revised powers, improved mechanics that speed play and allow for easier character design, and a new solo-play option!

Built to work with SuperFigs or any other brand of Super miniatures (in 28mm or 15mm scale), SuperSystem provides a flexible setting and campaign rules that will allow players to further develop stories and characters with each scenario played.

All players need to get started are these rules, a few miniatures, a handful of six-sided dice, and their imaginations!
Portal is a victory point based game in which you use cards to drive the movements and actions of your pieces on the game board.

User Summary:

Contact is a 30 minute social game for 4-16 friendly players. It focuses on physical contact between participants; testing imagination, communication, and intuition—An ice breaker meant to challenge social boundaries.

Players gather in a circle and take turns interacting with new partners.  Each pair finds a way to make physical contact that hasn't already been done in the game. If they succeed, they each earn 1 point.

Each player receives a quirk card that tells them one way they must behave during the game (e.g. "Avoid Eye Contact").  If you can guess a person's quirk, you received a point, and they get a new quirk.

The game ends when everyone has partnered with everyone else once.  The person with the most points wins.

NOTE: This was game was a submission to the Golden Cobra Challenge 2014.  However, it is much more of a social/party/ice-breaker game than a role-playing game, and has been added as such to BGG with the permission of the designer.  The rules for the game are available on the Golden Cobra Challenge website.

In route back to your home planet, following a very successful planetary invasion, your spaceship crashes, leaving seventy slugs stranded on a desolate moon.

There is one option for survival - the only functioning escape pod!

In SPACE SLUGS each player takes on the role of a Slug Officer, and are tasked with recruiting a crew of 5 different slugs to activate the escape pod and flee the desolate moon.

Problem is, there is only one escape pod, so the officer that is able to form his crew first, will be able to activate the escape pod and flee, leaving everyone else to an uncertain future.

The game is played clockwise around a table with players either playing slugs cards from their hands, into play, as their needed crew members. Or playing action cards, which allow them to swap slugs around from player to player, make players lose their turns, or even remove slugs completely from the table.

Play continues until a player has, in play, each of the 5 different slug cards. At which time that player promptly wins!

"Quick, silly, and with just enough challenge ... Space Slugs delivers and won’t disappoint." - FATHER GEEK

Out run your formula rivals on the city freeway!

The excitement of the arcade racer comes to the table top. 2-6 players compete in open wheel racing on the city freeway. Reach top speed, dodge traffic, pass junctions and turbo in a battle to tame the Formula Freeway!

Infinite rolling freeway with switching double-sided boards.
Moving freeway traffic vehicles ready for overtaking!
Card strategy racing with no dice!

GAMEPLAY
Race by selecting High or Low gear followed by drawing one of two movement cards from the race deck. Decide to play this card or to move another racer in the hope that the second card will be better strategically. Win by driving through all freeways of the race distance and crossing the finishing line first.

FEATURES
Switchable double-sided boards creating an infinite rolling freeway and randomly closed junctions with traffic cones. Selectable gears, turbos and moving traffic vehicles. Choose to race less junctions or tackle the full distance of all five freeway junctions.

INCLUDES
Two double-sided boards, six race cars with turbos, ten traffic cars and two cones. Race deck of 40 movement, 6 gear, 5 distance & 3 result cards.

Race Track is basically  a roll die and move game with a few extras. There are 12 jigsaw type two lane track sections which can be used to make over a dozen different circuits. 
 There is a "weather" spinner to determine if conditions are wet or dry and players have a token to indicate whether they have wet or dry tyres on. There are two dice, one "slow" die with 1-3 on and one "fast" die with 4-6 on. If their tyres match the weather conditions they use the fast die, if not the slow die. 
 There are a number of squares on the circuit, which, if landed on require player to draw one on 50 Chance cards and follow the instructions thereon.

description:
Grubfist: 3-7 Players. Plays in 15 minutes. Matching / Trading Game. Comes with 7 sets of goblin goodies and 6 gemstones. Standard and optional rules included.

Grubfist is a game of matching sets with things you might find in a goblin’s belt pouch. Players of all ages will find the game tempting to pick up and play. While passing frogs, ants, bits of chain or blunted fish hooks, players may feel the urge to cheat… which is not only allowed, but totally expected from time to time in a goblin’s cave.

The game includes matching sets of goblin goodies which bring a unique feeling to game play, bright plastic gemstones for the final grubbing of each round, and instructions for standard and optional rules.

Keep your favorite goblin treasure, and pass the unwanted to your fellow goblins. Cheat if you feel you can get away with it, and when someone grabs for the gemstones… don’t get kicked out of the cave. There can only be one goblin king!

In Rodeo, 1 player play as bull and the others play as cowboy/cowgirl. During the course of the game, cowboy/cowgirl try to follow the bull's movement and manage to get points by make special move. But, be carefull, if you lose while make your special move, you will lose points. Risk or ride!

English description:

"Caccia a Tito Drvar 1944" is a tactical wargame (TA1) using the same rules of "La Battaglia di Gravellona Toce 1944" which simulates the Operation Roesselsprung (Knight's Move), codename for the avio operation acted by 500th SS Para Battalion to chase Marshal Tito in May 1944. Each point is 40 men and each turn is about 2 hours each.

Italian description:

"Caccia a Tito Drvar 1944" è un wargame tattico (TA1) che intende simulare l’operazione Rösselsprung ("Salto del Cavaliere" in tedesco), nome in codice dell’attacco aviotrasportato del 500° Battaglione Paracadutisti SS su Drvar, città sede del Comando del Maresciallo Tito, comandante dei partigiani comunisti jugoslavi il 25 Maggio 1944.
La scala di gioco è di circa 40 uomini per punto di Valore di Forza e il turno è di 2 ore circa.

description:
Kandahar: Special Forces in Afghanistan (Solitaire) gives you command of a Special Forces team in Afghanistan from 2003 to 2013. Your lead your men on a variety of randomly generated missions; you choose your weapons and equipment, decide on how to posture your team, and plan your approach and method of extraction.

You must choose weapons and equipment that don’t run the risk of quickly exposing your team, and don’t cause unintended escalation. (If the US military must divert regular combat units to your area to quell enemy activity, the value of the Green Berets will be called into question by the traditionalists at the Pentagon.) Your goal is to disrupt the enemy’s freedom of action in your area of operations while creating only a minimal footprint at minimal cost.

Kandahar is a solitaire game in which you assume the role of a Special Forces colonel. The system will generate missions and enemy responses to your unit’s activities.

Each team counter represents about five men, while each commander and officer counter represent one man each. Each enemy combat unit represents various sizes of units, typically platoons.

Each hex on the map represents 200 meters from side to opposite side. Each turn represents anything from hours to days, depending on the tempo of your activities.

Modern War, Issue #21 JAN-FEB 2016

Angriff auf Moskau (Valkyria Chronicles Parallel Worlds)
Blended Wargaming & RPG

This game portrays the battle for Moscow in 41, blended within the Valkyria Chronicles Parallel Worlds.

PRODUCT


    Modular Game Series.
    30 Episodes per Map.
    Simple I-go, You-go system.
    Simplified Terrain Effect Chart (TEC).
    General (Characters) activate combat units.
    Ages 8 and up.




GAME


    Valkyria Wehrmacht Divisions.
    Valkyria Soviet Corps.
    1 Turn = 1 Week.
    Playing time: ½h to +20h. 
    Each commanding General has unique abilities (through Card playing).




ACTIONS


    Deploy powerful Panzer Divisions.
    Deploy fearful SS Hitlerjugend Divisions (These don’t make prisoner).
    Hordes of Soviet Corps.
    Build bridges at rivers.
    Siege Cities.




FEELINGS

    Intense Card Playing.
    Intense Battle Situations.
    Character RPG gaming.

Background:
In Exploit!, you are all undercover spies in the Azzarack pirates. The most recent mission went awry and now you know there are others vying for the Captain’s ear. You must expose these others before they expose you. Use your guile and wit to ‘take care of’ the other spies until you are the only spy left to win.

Rules:
Deal each player an identity card. The identity cards will have a name and 2 roles on it. The roles allow you to do take actions (See role cards for list of actions). You may also bluff to take the other actions, but if another player calls your bluff, you are exposed and you lose (see bluffing on next card).

Bluffing: When you use a role, someone may call you a liar. If they do, reveal your card to them. If the used role is one of the two on your card, they lose and reveal their card. If you bluffed and got caught, you lose and reveal your card. You cannot lie about whether an assassin or accuser correctly guessed your identity.

There are 3 green and 3 blue roles. Each identity has 1 green and 1 blue. There are no repeats of combinations. When cards are discarded, discard them facedown. When the draw pile runs out, do not reshuffle the discard pile. When the draw pile is empty, powers that require drawing a card cannot be used.

Each turn you may take any action on the roles card. Everyone has access to black actions (they are always safe). If players use a green or blue action, their opponents may call their bluff. When an opponent is the target of a spy or assassinate, they may use the sentinel or facade actions to counter them. These 2 blue actions do not require an action to use (though they can still be bluffs).

When you think an opponent has sentinel, be sure to accuse them  because they will block an assassination attempts.

Roles:
Accuse (Black) - Guess a player’s identity. If you are right they die, otherwise you die.
Invent (Black) - Discard your identity and draw a new one.
Assassinate (Green) - Guess a player’s identity, if you are right they die.
Spy (Green) - Look at another player’s identity.
Disguise (Green) - Draw a new Identity then discard one.
Sentinel (Blue) - Prevent Assassination.
Facade (Blue) - Prevent Spy.
Scour (Blue) - Look through discard pile or draw pile (shuffle draw pile after).

In the Mace Exclusive version of Exploit!, all the green keywords are red!

A strange prince, from a distant land. Hassan's combat prowess might only be rated as 'adequate' if not for his astonishing luck.  He credits his forward thinking battle strategies and the mystic Dagger of Rhee-Do. Enemies that would surely have struck him a fatal blow find themselves outmaneuvered and bleeding from dozens of tiny wounds (and with sand rubbed in them no less!) As a prince  of a desert kingdom, Hassan began his journey not long after eternal night fell. For, while relief from the desert heat was relieving at the time, things have gotten a good bit colder, and firewood is notoriously hard to come by in the dunes.

Every year at the McHighland McGames, the McClans of the McNorthlands send their mightiest warriors to compete for the title of McGrand McHigh McHammer (which is really a mouthful). And for the last six years running, the same McClan has held the title. Now, their champion has ventured south to Arcadia and enlisted his mighty hammer in the cause of McRight and McJustice! (Seriously, that’s exactly how his press release reads…) Accents notwithstanding, the McGrand McHammer is a berserking whirlwind on the battlefield. The more enemies, the better!

boardgame: Dogs of War: Tactic Cards & Order of Battle Tiles
description:
12 new Tactic cards with new kinds of rewards
    4 new Order Of Battle Tiles

description:
Spin the spinner to choose a player and pick a card from the next category. Then its time to answer a question, perform a task or match the active player's answer. The one with most victory point chips at the end of the game wins.

The game contains questions and tasks from every possible topic! The game is easy to learn and get going. Scoring is simple and easy.

Explore the labyrinth and try to collect the greatest berry treasure of all. Spin the spinner and move to the next flower of that type on your path; if there is a berry token, you may pick it up. Beware of the berry thief, though! You can head for the finish line whenever you want to, but everyone's berry treasures stay hidden - you won't know how many berries your opponents have until it's time to reveal them at the end.

The modular labyrinth consists of large puzzle pieces, so every game is different. The game is easy to learn and full of exciting twists and turns.

Spin away and move on the game board, performing different physical or intellectual tasks depending on the spaces you land on. Successful tasks will give you candy points, which can be traded for your own puzzle pieces. Whoever finishes their puzzle first is the winner!

This 18 card shrink-wrapped promo pack was given to all Kickstarter backers.  These cards offer more complexity, variety, and interesting mechanics and abilities.

The pack consists of the following cards:

4 Hero A cards: Blade, Hunk, Penguin, and Unicorn

4 Hero B cards: Action, Captain, Ever-Changin', and Promo

3 Villain A cards: Fang, Skull, and Skunk

3 Villain B cards: Franken-, Professor, and Rabid

4 Quirk cards: Inflated Ego, Yoga Enthusiast, and 2 blank quirks for individual customization.

boardgame: Talisman (Revised 4th Edition): The Deep Realms Expansion
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Talisman: The Deep Realms Expansion allows you to travel between the Dungeon and the City regions by venturing into the dark, dank, and haunted passages that connect them. You can cross through the two corpse-filled and rat-infested Deep Realms by means of a perilous bridge or venture even deeper into an ancient system of tunnels inhabited by rats, feral pets, and much worse monsters. In the gloomy and perilous Deep Realms you will certainly encounter tremendous danger, but you may also find tantalizing wealth – or even a Talisman.

To use the Deep Realms expansion, you need the Talisman base game as well as both The Dungeon and The City expansions.

Psychedelic Quilt is a solitaire game that merely requires a single set of A-Type Nestortiles, but could instead be played with a piecepack — although the more suits the better. Build a quilt (of any flat shape) according to the Quilting Rules, so that every swatch is part of the quilt.

Setup
Shuffle all swatches face-down. Draw 1 tile of each color and place them face-up in front of you.

Play
Stitch (attach) one of your face-up swatches to the quilt on the table according to the Quilting Rules below (obviously, the first swatch attaches to nothing, and ignores the Quilting Rules). If there are any remaining face-down swatches of the color you just attached, draw a replacement of that color; otherwise, you get no replacement.

Quilting Rules

     A swatch can only be stitched to a swatch of a different color.
     A swatch can only be stitched to a swatch with one more or one fewer pip.


Game End
If you stitch all swatches into the quilt, you win; otherwise, you lose.

Achievements
If you win the game and complete any of the tasks below, check them off! Check them all off to prove your quilting capabilities!

     Rectangle: Your quilt is perfectly rectangular.
         Magic Rows: Each row adds up to the same total.
         Magic Columns: Each column adds up to the same total.
         Magic Rectangle: Each row adds up to the same total (X) and each column adds up to the same total (Y). X does not need to equal Y.
         Small Square: Play using only 5 colors and the numbers 1 through 5 of each; make your quilt perfectly square.
             Baby Sudoku: Each number appears only once in each row, each number appears only once in each column. Thus, each also adds to 15.
             Magic Sudoku: Each number appears only once in each row, each number appears only once in each column, and each number appears only once in each of the two diagonals. Thus, each also adds to 15.
        
         Large Square: Play using only 6 colors, and make your quilt perfectly square.
             Sudoku: Each number appears only once in each row, each number appears only once in each column. Thus, each also adds to 21.
             Mind-Wrecking Sudoku: Each number appears only once in each row, each number appears only once in each column, and each number appears only once in each of the two diagonals. Thus each also adds to 21. Is it even possible?
        
    
     Candy-striped: Each row of your quilt alternates between only 2 colors.
     Donut: Your quilt has a fully surrounded hole in it.
     Ghost: Your quilt has 2 fully surrounded holes in it.

Rune Crafter is a fast paced dice game where up to players take the role of Rune Crafters, powerful battle mages that use runestones to wield magic!

Players roll the dice to attack, heal, or gain Rune Points which are used to craft more powerful dice so that you can finish off your opponents! The last player standing is the winner!

Rune Crafter offers unique game play rules for defeated players so the fun doesn't end. The game also includes an Event Deck that alters the rules of the game each round, keeping players on their toes.

Vino de vrolijke inktvis (Vino the happy squid) is a game of color, suitable for kids aged 3 years and up.

Every player chooses a color and and puts it in front om him/her.
The youngest player starts and throws the die. If your color has been thrown, you may put 1 of your beads on vinos legs. First player with no beads left, is the winner.

This game is designed to learn different colors and counting.

RootG is a sci-fi strategy boardgame set in a futuristic world where humans are near to be enslaved from the overwhelming techonlogy of the HIksos, an alien race from the outer space.

The player chanllege each other for activating the cube, the weapon create for save the humanity

In R.O.O.T.\G: tile based game two or three players compete to reassemble the electronic board of the ancient Cube. Players place tiles in turns and build up circuits trying to reach their secret objective. Each tile may modify the speed of each player and may turn the tide. Only a perfect balance of strategy, creativity, foresight and promptness will allow you to catch the perfect timing to beat your opponents!

thanks to the three ruleset you can easly play with the "child educational mode" designed to introduce younger people to the dynamic world of eletronics, otherwise you can play with normal, advanced or the "Hard-Code" ruleset for  harder games

Why Triominos? Because it's like dominoes, but with 3 sides... So it's not dominoes... It's triominos. Got it?

Why Turbo? This game is against the clock. You have to be quick before time runs out. If that happens, everyone gets punished... including you!

So the other players aren't my friends? In your dreams! Everyone wants to win! You have to act quickly and get rid of all your stones before the others... Without getting distracted by the evil timer!

To train children's growing understanding of playing games with relaxing fun and social interaction.
The game also stimulates children's recognition, helps them understand abstracts and, not least, trains their language skills.

The game is a memory game, but instead of matching two face down cards as in the classic Memory, the player need only to remember one; the player board contains three objects similar to those in the game, and the player need to find the object from their player board(s).

In turn (starting by the youngest player), each player flips over one face down card and show it openly; if the player see it matches his/her player board (s)he can take it and place it there, if it is wrong the tile is flipped and the next player have the turn.

When matched three items, the player now needs to find (or remember where to find) a "happening". First to match three (or six if played with two boards) items and a happening wins the game.

boardgame: EUROPA - ASIEN - AFRIKA  Eine Reise in heisse Länder mit Sinalco
description:
Objective: be the first reaching target space (135)/ending round trip.
- moving by turns -
active player moves forward according die roll; ending on a marked space follow instructions glued into the lid. 
note:
likely published during 1960's

This game takes place in the universe of Enascentia, a famous Italian setting of the Savage Worlds rpg. The mechanics come from Warage, the card game. The result of this crossover is Warage Enascentia, a great way to expand your collection of Warage cards - thanks to the introduction of new mechanics - and expand your knowledge of the Enascentia setting.

The box comes with 4 complete decks, including 8 new Heroes (2 for each deck)- for a total of 220 cards - and 20 Season cards.

Warage Enascentia is brought to you by the collaboration of District Games and GG studio.

How it works
In Warage Enascentia you are a hero which has particular buffs and abilities. There are eight Heroes, divided in four Tribes: Gromsh, Janah, Kronoss and Senduar.

In Warage Enascentia, as in Warage, your Hero will have to fight against the other(s). You can equip your Hero with weapons, armours, spells, mounts etc, in order to power him up and defeat the opponent(s). 
Each one of the four decks inside the box are ready to play. In each deck - made of 48 cards + 2 Hero cards - you will find already the equipments of your hero, his spells etc. You just have to place it on the table and start playing.

To win the game, you have to: 

    be the last Hero in game (with more than 0 Live Points;
    reach endless LP, or;
    use an alternative strategy - which is usually the ability of your hero (eg. "Headshot").

You are a bounty hunter in the Wild West and you’re planning to retire...
You have found the perfect ranch and all you need is $50,000 to buy it...
But there is a problem; there are others that want the money as well...

Outlaw is a fun dice game where players fight it out to capture the most dangerous fugitives the West has ever known.

On their turn players use 4 dice to try and roll a score of under 10 or over 18. If successful place your marker on the outlaw with that number.
The more notorious the bandit, the more reward money you’ll get. Capture 3 outlaws and the cash is yours!

The first player to $50,000 is the winner.

Outlaw is a print and play game.

The two players each have 3 armies (red, yellow, blue) with 3 divisions. The goal of the game is to bring a provision transports (meeples) from the depot to every division. Movement is determined by a die. On several spaces the transports will be shelled and may be delayed or destroyed. The player who gets the most transports (meeples) to his field kitchens wins the game.

This Game was published during World War II.

Edo Jidai is a game of conquest set in the very end of the 'Age of  War" in Japanese Samurai history.  With few remaining clans battling for complete control of the entire Nation, players must combat one another and conquer enemy castles.

With a time limit rule set, Edo Jidai games can finish within a set time limit or be played out to each round's end game.

Similar to Risk, Edo Jidai goes far beyond what a regular game of conquest can offer with its 150+ game cards that bring players into the context and story of each game.  Card play revolves around attack and defense.  Territory cards can be cashed in for armies, however, have duel purpose and can be used  for combat as well.  This makes hording cash-in cards very difficult ensuring game outcomes are different every time.

Also, a lot of cards can be played at any time in the game (it doesn't have to be your turn), so players are constantly engaged.
