description:
A 37 card expansion for the Chaos Isle: Zombi Deck card game, Apocalypse adds a whole new deck to the game, adding tension and sense of impending chaos. Players draw a card from this deck at the start of their turn and resolve one of its five card types: Ally, Equipment, Location, Zombi or Doomsday card.

Doomsday cards require one or more players to meet some criteria or the card will be added to the "Doomsday Track". When the Doomsday Track has reached its limit based on the number of players in the game, the Apocalypse is triggered, profoundly changing the rules for the remainder of the game, often bringing about about a quick and decisive ending.

Apocalypse requires the original Chaos Isle: Zombi Deck base game to play and can be played with any other Chaos Isle expansion. Apocalypse works particularly well with the Rise of The Creator expansion.

Six Degrees of Separation challenges players to build their own chain of Actors and films that separate two actors by 6 Degrees.

Players must race to be the first to create an alternating chain of 8 actors and 7 films they have in common. With six actors between the first and last, you get a separation of Six Degrees.

There are no turns. The game ends when one player has succeeded in completing a six degree chain.

But it's not that simple...

Films and Actors each have different point values. The winning player is not necessarily the the first player to achieve six degrees, but the one with the highest value chain.

Players will find Action cards that can help them or hinder their opponent(s). Maggie Smith might have a Wardrobe Malfunction on the red carpet or "I, Frankenstein" might be voted Best Film.

Having started his film career in 1978’s “Animal House”, by 2000 actor Kevin Bacon had come to appear in many of the most successful films in the last decade of the twentieth century. Appearing in such films as “JFK”, “A Few Good Men”, “Apollo 13”, and “Hollow Man”, just to name a few, Kevin Bacon seemed to have worked with just about every other popular actor in Hollywood.

1993’s “Six Degrees of Separation”, with Stockard Channing, Will Smith, and Donald Sutherland, speculated that every person on the
planet was only removed from another person by no more than six other individuals between the two.

It didn’t take long for a new parlor game to make its way through the L.A. party scene, and eventually into the general population called “Six Degrees of Kevin Bacon”. Players, starting with Kevin Bacon and another actor from any film that had ever been made, are challenged to create a chain between the two using films in which pairs of actors appear.

Second game in the Grandes Batallas del Mundo (GBM) series.

Batalla de Zama is a "wargame", based on the battle at Zama 202 BC, where Annibal lost against Publio Cornelio Escipion. It was the end of the Second Punic War.

It has simple rules: The units can move one to three spaces (depends of each type) and battles are resolved by dice rolls; thus modified by strength of the units (infantry, cavalry, elephant) and area.
The aim is to kill the opponents leaders or to capture the opponents "flag-spaces".

The miniatures are beautiful hand painted figures.

The rules and the units are similar to the components of the first GBM game (Batalla del Metauro). The board is the main difference. It inlcudes also a new miniature, the Roman Trumpeter. the new rules for this unit are printed on the back of the board.
This game was only published by Rojas y Malaret in Spain.
family: Series: Grandes Batallas del Mundo (Rojas y Malaret)
Series: Romanos contra Cartagineses (Rojas y Malaret)

description:
Fourth game in the Grandes Batallas del Mundo (GBM) series.

German-Italian (Rommmel) vs Ally armies (Montgomery) in the II World War battle, in El Alamein! (North Africa).

Each aplayer controls infantry, artillery, tanks and airplanes. The movement and the combat of the units are different. Combats are solved by dice rolling, with unit and terrain modifications.
On the board there are some special items: railroads along the coast (to move faster), or mines (you can desactivate a mine and move later on this space).

The phases in the round are controled by a cilindrical "time scoreboard".

To win you need to place two units on the flag spaces of the other player's army.
family: Series: Grandes Batallas del Mundo (Rojas y Malaret)

boardgame: Batalla de Zama Complemento de la serie Romanos contra Cartagineses
description:
Big expansion, for the first (Batalle del Metauro) and the second (Batalla de Zama) game in the "Grandes Batallas del Mundo" (GBM)

game series by Rojas y Malaret.
It includes new figures and miniatures: Three trumpeters for the Roman player (unit not included in the first game, only in the second), and two horses and one elephant for the Carthaginian player. The box included all the collectible materials these games always had inside (historical booklet, cards, etc.).

The miniatures are beautiful hand painted figures.
This expansion was only published by Rojas y Malaret in Spain.
family: Series: Grandes Batallas del Mundo (Rojas y Malaret)

boardgame: Gothic Line: The Campaign in Northern Italy – August to October, 1944
description: Gothic Line: The Campaign in Northern Italy – AUGUST TO OCTOBER, 1944 is a simulation of the battles in the Northern Apennines mountains fought to cross the Po River and end the war in Italy by Christmas 1944. This game simulates the campaign at the battalion/division level. Each combat strength point of Divisions/brigades/regiments are represented by standard military unit symbols (with a few exceptions) and each combat strength point is equivalent to one combat maneuver battalion, while units represented by armoured vehicles consist primarily of battalions of tanks (or armoured cars, in the case of the cavalry/armoured car units)(armoured divisions, though having tanks, are still at least half infantry battalion units). The combat system is based on differential, rather than odds, as each combat unit fights one against one as opposed to a hex(es) unit aggregate vs unit aggregate. Corps/army artillery and air are separate components added to combat, as both played an important part in the struggle and really could not be factored into unit value. Gothic Line: The Campaign in Northern Italy – AUGUST TO OCTOBER, 1944 is a desk top publishing type of game, with a self-sticking countersheet mountable on standard 1/2 inch counters, rulebook, charts, and full color map.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Spiritual Warfare: Luther's Small Catechism Edition
description:
A Simulation of the Post-Reformation Smack Down!

The premise behind Spiritual Warfare: Luther's Small Catechism Edition (LSCE) is that there is a very real war that is being waged across the world. Look, you may not think this is your thing. You may be completely unaware of the earth-shattering implications of the Reformation or a guy named Martin Luther. You may not know that there is a legacy of this radical confessing church that exists to this present day. Regardless, this is still a boss game that helps people understand the battle for truth that plays out for every member of planet earth. It is not combat that can be fought and won with worldly weapons. It is a war of words.

The game is played with each side choosing their champions.  The Enemies of the faith can choose between slick Gurus, irreverent Pagans, boastful Humanists, and wicked Fanatics.  The Defenders of the faith square off against them with wise Mentors, sharp Theologians, courageous Missionaries, and generous Stewards.

Game play moves through 4 distinct phases: the Draw Stage, the Activation Stage, the Encounter Stage, and the Resource Stage.  Each player uses their champion to make the best on the spot decisions through each individual phase in order to gain the edge and silence their opponent.

This game comes with:
12 Defenders of the Faith Champion cards
10 Defenders of the Faith Interject cards
12 Defenders of the Faith Possession cards
36 Defenders of the Faith Teaching cards
12 Enemies of the Faith Champion cards
15 Enemies of the Faith Interject cards
14 Enemies of the Faith Possession cards
29 Enemies of the Faith Teaching cards
140 Square Shards
2 Simplified Rules Mats
1 Spiritual Warfare: LSCE Large Rules Booklet
1 Spiritual Warfare: LSCE Gaming Box

This game can also function as a training tool in connection with Luther's Small Catechism to help students of any age learn the six chief parts of the historic, Christian faith.

From the the box:

A great new card game based on Pick Up Sticks.  Pile the cards and take turns picking up as many cards as you can.  Can you move your card without shifting the stack?  It's fun--it's quick.  It's Pick Stick Oh!

Pick Stick Oh! is a new twist on the classic Pick Up Sticks, played with cards rather than sticks.  Players are dealt cards and then take turns tossing them into a pile.  Each card must overlap another card.  After all the cards are placed, each player takes turns trying to take a card off the stack without shifting any other cards sideways (lifting up is okay).  Cards have point values from 1 to 5.  If a player takes a red 2 or 3 successfully, he or she gets another turn.  The player with the most total points at the end of the game wins.

Promo expansion for game Dudab.

The 8 cards give you additional tension and possibilities during the game.

The five star general knowledge quiz war, Quiz Battle is a battle of brains for two players.

Each player has a book of trivia questions and answers.  The box has a sliding drawer that looks like a battlefield and is used as a scorekeeping device.

Players take turns asking each other questions from their own team book.  If the question is answered correctly, the person who answers can push the Score Drawer one "click" forward to advance his troops.  If the answer is wrong then his army must retreat one "click" by pushing the drawer in the opposite direction.

The winner is the first to defeat his opponent by capturing the opponent's base camp and raising his flag (or the player whose forces are advanced the farthest when all skirmishes have been fought).

This 2-4 player game for players aged 10 and up has been created by request of the Nasze Kalety association. It aims at teaching the players about the community of Kalety and its interesting sights and trivia. The players have to find multi-coloured oak leaves and to collect them, they have to correctly answer questions about Kalety.

Be the first player to play a run, 1 through 5, without having two cards of the same color next to each other, in this fast paced card game of swapping, skipping, sabotaging, and strategizing for 2-4 players.

Each player starts with one pawn  at the starting space with the red flag. The goal of the game is to reach the center space (blanket) with all pawns of one's color (4 or 2). Movement is determined by a die. Only on the roll of a 6 another meeple may enter the game. If a pawn gets to a cove it may jump over the blue spaces, if the result of the die allows it. Otherwise it has to take the long way around the cove. If a meeple gets on an occupied space, the other meeple is thrown into the water and has to start again.

—user summary

This 2-4 player game teaches the participants about Georgia, by using trivia cards with questions that the players need to answer to achieve their goals. It has been created as part of a special Polish/Georgian state cooperation program.

Bifrost: Chamado aos Deuses (Bifrost: Call to the Gods) is a Party Game inspired in games such as Uno and the Brazilian game Copo d’Água (AKA Dorminhoco), with a card-buying mechanic, but set in Nordic culture and mythology. The main objective of the game isn't to win, but to not be the loser.

The game 'turn' has 4 parts. First you will receive a call (card) from the previous player, then you can use a rune or viking, and/or pass a card to the next player, ending your turn.

The objective of the game is to get 5 different gods in your hand. Having the call (card) of a god in your hand means that the warrior (player) has been recognised by that god. When you have the 5, you should put your cards face-up on the table. The other players should do the same thing, always in the most discreet way. The person to put their cards on their table last, is the loser.

What happens when you and your friends have to survive a weekend in the world's most diabolical city while trying to be the one with the most money when it's over? Prepare to use money, wits, people, and a dash of good, old-fashioned luck as you swindle, bribe, steal, and kill your way to the top! With an endless number of strategies to employ, pitfalls to avoid, and ways to make money, Devil's Playground ensures that you'll have every opportunity to be the worst person you can be! This game is intended for mature individuals only! The rules and strategy guides are available to read and print on manufacturer's website.

boardgame: Tash-Kalar: Arena of Legends – Legendary Summoner Promo
description:
A promotional legendary being.

"Just shuffle the “Legendary Summoner” in with the other legend cards. This legendary’s effect allows you to use all players’ pieces for the pattern of the next being you summon this turn."

Included in Spielbox 2014/6.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This expansion provides alternate rules for Lords of the Sand, one that players will likely find quicker and more dynamic than the original version. 
It includes new God cards, and introduces stone markers.

A small rule change to Crimson Sun is also included.

The Relay Race For Honey Jars!

Super-Bee! is a fun family board game. It can be played by 2 to 6 players and it takes about 20 minutes to play.

You are a racing bee and you have to find and collect as many Honey Jars as you can. At the end of the game the bee with the most Honey Jars in storage wins the game. If one of the bees manage to get the storage full before everyone else, then that bee wins the game.

Of course, there are plenty of other surprises on the board, other than Honey Jars. You can discover friends that can help you collect even more honey (Super Bee, Caterpillar) or not so friendly characters like the Drone, who'll make you lose the precious Jars. Sometimes you might find a Spider Web, but don't worry, the spider is nowhere near to harm you. But, you can use its spider web to gather even more honey!

Try it once and you'll be hooked forever!

boardgame: Wenn der Hund mit der Wurst...
description:
Objective:
be the first reaching home with your dog & sausage
 - moving by turns -
Each player, starting from home posisition (corner), moves forwards according die roll, trying to catch the sausage in the centre of the board with a matching number. As soon as you got it you should move back home avoiding to lose the sausage to another player/dog...
note: game likely published early/mid 1960s

This Founding Fathers expansion offers more Statesmen, Offices and Actions. Also included are rules for solo games against a virtual opponent or head-to-head in the party vs. party two-player variant. Finally there is the Historical Factions Variant and special High Tension Election rules that reflect the torrid situation in 1860. Plus there are additional cubes to support the above.

boardgame: La Isla Prohibida: La Expansión Prohibida
description:
Free promo item and special award at the IX Festival Internacional de Juegos Córdoba 2014.

Expansion for La Isla Prohibida, with 3 modules:

    El Cervatillo de Medina Azahara (Little Deer from Medina Azahara): A new (Cordobesian) treasure, the player must move it together!
    Poderes de los Tesoros Mágicos (Powers of the Magic Treasures) - Special thanks to: Jeremy Arcus-Goldberg, Eric Herman, and Marty Kane.
    Variantes para la Isla (Variants of the initial board, as included in the German version of the basic game, plus a new "JT@" configuration)


10 años después, as Matt Leacock visited again the Festival a new, enhanced version was re-issued. It included a leaflet with rules, a postcard with variants of the island and a brand-new standee featuring the "Cervatillo Perdido" of Medina Azahara ancient city.

description:
Carcassonne: Halb so Wild II (Halflings II) takes the standard Carcassonne tiles and breaks them in half. More specifically, the sheet of tiles consists of twelve triangular landscape tiles that are half the size of a normal tile.

Additional features derived from The Corn Circles expansion and the Hills & Sheep expansion appear on four of the tiles.

This version was released by Hans im Glück through its Cundco.de webstore. It includes 12 tiles with different designs from Carcassonne: Halb so wild I, released by Spielbox at the same time. These two different versions can be combined.

Part of the Carcassonne series.

The Fierce fight! Barbarossa series tries to portray the Barbarossa campaign with the popular Series: Fierce Fight! system.

This is second game of Fierce fight! Barbarossa series.
Rules to link with Fierce fight! Leningrad Blitzkrieg are included.

This game focuses on Army Group Centre's operations. The Axis player needs to defeat the Soviet armies in Belarus and occupy Smolensk.

Time scale: 10 days per turn
Unit scale: Division
Counter: 252
Map: 1 A1 size
Duration: 1941 Jun-Sep

The players are the owners of competing advertising agencies, producing TV spots, printed ads etc.

The game board and part of the game mechanics are closely related to monopoly (Dice, Movement, Income on start field, chance/community-like cards). The players buy resources like a film production company or a gourmet restaurant, which they and the other players need for their campaigns.

To win, a player has to obtain three different prizes. (1) Young Businessman Award: Obtained by having 250k money. (2) Ad Award: Obtained by reaching one of three fields on the game board. (3) Pitch Award: Obtained by winning three pitches.

For a pitch, two players draw storyboards for an ad campaign about a given topic and then do a presentation of their idea. Then the other players decide who wins the pitch.

The game box shows the ORF (Austrian national public service broadcaster) Logo. Copyright by Berlini/Jung Pollak, Designed by EDGE project, WIEN. Produced by Schmidt Spiele.

This game is a reimplemention of Ludo containing a the normal 4x4 player pawns, a Globe-ludo die (with four numbers, a star and a globe) and a redesigned Ludo board:

The board is like in the normal Globe Ludo, but with added features;
Orange spot = Move 3 ahead
Green spot = Move 2 back
and a spot for taking a chance card.

The basic rules are the same.

A set of different, paper-based parlor games in a cardboard brochure folder containing 3 separate booklets with 24 pages each. Each booklet contains the rules of the game and the corresponding texts for the board games.

Many variations around the general idea of a printed text which is enriched by the players by secretly and subsequently adding names, attributes and other words to the story. This is mostly done by filling in a word, folding the sheet to cover it and passing it on to the next player. In the end the sheet is unfolded and read aloud to the amusement of the participants. As a variation, the words are asked for and filled in by a gamemaster, who doesn't disclose the original text until the very end.

Available topics were as follows:

     Series 1: Sekretär, Lebensart, Literatur I (Assistant, Lifestyle, Literature I) 
     Series 2: Inserate, Heilerfolge, Literatur II (Newspaper Ads, Successful Cures, Literature II) 
     Series 3: Historie, Biographie, Literatur III (History, Biography, Literature III) 
     Series 4: Steckbrief, Gerichtssaal, Literatur IV (Profile, Court of Law, Literature IV)


The Jugendstil (Art Nouveau) design of the games may place them in the early 20th century (around 1900-1910).

In Quiz-a-Majig: The Crazy Quiz Contraption, each player starts with six colored marbles. Choose from one of the five double-sided category sheets and install it in the spinner. Throughout the game, players take turns being the quiz master. On your turn as the quiz master, push down on the lever to get the letter and category dials spinning simultaneously.

Once the spinning stops, the quiz master reads the category and letter aloud - "Countries! L!" All players think fast to come up with a word that fits the category and begins with the letter shown. If you think you've got it, throw one of your marbles into the bowl. Don’t tell out the answer yet because many marbles might go in, but only one will make it to the bottom first. All others will be sent to the tray to be used again later.

The player whose marble made it to the bottom first has five seconds to call out their answer. If they're correct, the quiz master turns the dial counter-clockwise and the marble falls into the quiz tube. But, if they're wrong, the dial gets turned clockwise and the marble can't be used again for the rest of the game!

Keep up the spinning, marble-tossing, and answer-yelling fun for as long as it takes. The first player to get all six of their marbles into the tube wins the game. But if that doesn't happen and the tube gets filled up to the line, the player with the most marbles in the tube wins!

Think fast, pay attention, and try not to lose your marbles as you play the fast-paced game of Quiz-A-Majig.

—description from the publisher

HOF Fire Team is an original dynamic tactical game with a setting from ultra modern to the far future. Fire Team pits squads of soldiers, guerrillas, police, and thieves or just about anyone else with a grudge against one another. Use miniatures from your existing collection, on single bases, or add any new ones of your choice. Players command a number of Teams. These being Fire Teams, Support Teams, Command Teams and trusting to sound small unit tactics and the luck of the dice they attempt to carry out their mission objectives.

HOF Fire Team is played on purpose designed 'Battle Boards' de-marked into a 20mm squared grid. These set squares allow for easy and instant measurement of game distances for movement and weapons fire along with the relation between every miniature in play and possible cover. Play is easy and lethal. Using a unique D6 mechanic based on the Firefight 2.0 engine combined with an innovative 'tech level' for all troops and the 'waypoint' mission driver this game promises fast and intense action in a compact playing area. Play in a place of your choosing even if it is too small for other 'open terrain' wargames.

Leeroy is known for his startling, epic charges. Monsters are often so stunned that they simply drop their defenses. Leeroy never quite understood the need for advanced tactics and battle strategies. The objective is to fight, right? So why wait? Leeroy’s signature battlecry has earned him fame from one end of the realm to the other, although, when it comes to stealth missions, he’s usually best employed as a distraction. Just let him loose, and then exploit the monsters’ weaknesses. The fact that they’re rolling around on the ground laughing just makes them easier to take down.

Hook Line & Stinker Fish is a fun table top card game for kids and adults. Players are dealt a deck of fish that they will play from and try to catch.

Each turn players will continue to play fish from their deck as they successfully catch them by rolling dice and meeting or exceeding the number on the dice icon for that fish card.

When you do not catch a fish your turn is over and you must either choose to put a sea creature into play or take your chance with catching a piece of trash or treasure.

When playing a fish from your fish deck, if the stinker fish card is put into play, your turn is over. If the stinker fish card is in play when a player does not catch a fish, you must pick it up and shuffle it into your deck of fish cards.

Try to score the most points by catching fish and treasures before one player runs out of cards in their fish deck.

Laserblade is a fast-paced skirmish wargame designed with the casual gamer in mind. The rules are easy for new players to understand while options for customising your own units provide the right level of replayability for more experienced players.

The game uses ten-sided dice and players usually have 5-10 models per side. Laserblade was designed with 54mm "army men" style figures in mind but can be played with miniatures from any scale and genre.

The earth has been stripped of resources and now the human race has set out to colonize a New World. On the way players will explore planets and astroid, build outposts and Starbases, excavate alien artifacts, upgrade abilities and often blow each other up. Once players land on the new world they will choose which branch of government to form, and this will determine how they will score points based on the actions they took getting there.

StarDrive is a clever combination of racing and 4X game elements.

A turn consists of rolling dice and placing them on a faction control panel, and then taking an action determined by the dice that were placed. Dice that are not used to perform an action can be saved for later turns or rerolled.

There are eight factions to choose from and many ways to customize them during play

You and your fellow engineers are working cooperatively to launch awesome products and grow the user base. In order to successfully launch products, you must develop the necessary infrastructure while keeping an eye on external events, which might help or hinder your efforts.

Launch & Iterate is a turn based cooperative card game. Tech cards are the primary type of card used in the game. Tech cards enable development of either core infrastructure (e.g. Data Redundancy), or offer one-shot benefits (e.g. Refactor). Tech cards are also used as the primary currency in the game, representing engineering effort required to develop new technology or to contribute to launching new products.

During the game, players focus on launching 1 or (potentially) more products at any given time. Successfully launching products increases the overall user base (in millions of users) and allows players to shift effort onto new products. Players attempt to maximize the total user base by the end of the game by strategically selecting the right products to work on at the right time. Each product has specific infrastructure requirements which must be met, along with a cost (in Tech cards) which must be contributed by players in order to successfully launch the product.

On their turn, players may select a Tech card to play from their hand (making sure that all requirements and costs are met), contribute any number of Tech cards from their hand to help launch an active product, and draw new card(s). At the end of each round, players reveal the next Event card, which can either deliver a benefit or incur a penalty, depending on the card as well as the infrastructure levels players have developed. The game is over after 11 events have been resolved.

A key factor in Launch & Iterate is developing infrastructure across 4 critical technology stacks, (F)eatures, (A)ccessibility, (S)torage, and (T)esting. Each stack is built up over the course of the game, from base level 0 to level 7. Developing infrastructure is critical to successfully launching products, but usually doesn't directly affect the user base. Each stack also offers specific benefits at higher levels, such as reducing or negating negative Event effects, increasing user base, enabling better allocation of resources or increasing development capabilities.

AkTYwuj Warszawę (Activate Warsaw) is a game for 2 to 6 players, aged 8 and up, which focuses on the civic involvement of young people. Its goal is to move around the board and complete tasks found around the landmarks of the Polish capital of Warsaw. In order to do this, players have to answer questions about civic issues and activities.

Magiczna Warka is a game about historical trivia surrounding the town and community of Warka. The goal of the game is to find the fragments of a magical crystal, scattered across different time periods and places in and around the town. Player tasks include reaching a particular space on the board or answering questions to gain the crystal fragments they need to collect to win.

The game's creation has been requested by the Local Activity Group Warka. The project was co-financed by the Polish Ministry of Culture and National Heritage.

boardgame: Plus i Minus: Nietypowo o Zdrowiu
description:
This game aims at educating the players about positive and negative behaviour connected to health, physical activity, nutrition etc. It has been designed by an association specializing in working with troubled youth. The game is informative and encourages players to search for more information on the topics it's covering.

Players move around the board if they are able to complete different tasks, including answering quiz questions, doing light workout (like situps) and showing situations via pantomime. Each completed task allows the player to move forward on the board, and the first player to the finish line wins.
publishers: Centrum Profilaktyki i Edukacji Społecznej PARASOL

description: In this historical strategic/adventure game, the players take on the role of the heroes of the January Uprising in the Kurpie region. Their goal is to complete various tasks around the board, while dealing with random events, such as meeting the Tsar's Courier etc. Each character has a special ability, but there is a limited amount of actions that can be taken each turn, so players best plan ahead. The game aims to capture the struggle of a small guerilla force against the threat of a dangerous, oppresive foe.

The game has been created by request of the Polish Chancellery of the Senate, and has been financed by that institution. Its goal is to teach the players about the process of legislation in Poland. In this simple educational game, players move around the board and interact with the game by drafting and collecting cards.

Magic Jinn knows what you're thinking!

Take an animal in mind and answer Magic Jinn's questions. Will he guess the right animal? Then he wins the game! But can you fool with Magic Jinn and be the smartest around the table? Then you'll be the winner of this game!

Do you dare to battle with your opponents?

Take a card and secretly choose a question to challenge the other players. How many answers will they find? Will they choose 1, 2 or 3 tokens? Is he sure or is he bluffing?

You have 3 seconds to answer. The other players can use their black cat to make it even more challenging and forcing you to find more answers!

The game was produced by request of the Dobra Sieć foundation. It was aimed at teenagers living in the eastern parts of Poland, and was distributed among local NGO, recreation centres, public libraries etc.

The players move around the board and answer quiz-style questions to gain points needed to complete their goals. An alternate type of task may be to reach a particular space on the board, which directs the player's movement.

boardgame: Thunderbirds at War: Bombing the Reich, Jan. to Jun. 1943
description:
"Thunderbirds at War" is a solitaire wargame based on the operational diaries of RCAF 426 Sq., 6th Group, Bomber Command, during its first five months of night operations from January to June 1943.

The game recreates events without excessive technical considerations and favors storytelling.  The player chooses his crew, allocates points representing pilot/crew abilities, and, having assessed his strengths and weaknesses, chooses his flight path to the target. Tables and tracks printed on the map sheet provide quick access to information needed and action outcomes. Random events are revealed during flight as are encounters with Luftwaffe and Flak. The player is called upon to make life and death decisions every step of the way, in short, managing mayhem. Nothing in the game is scripted. Most missions play in five to 15 minutes. It is as close as you can get to flying over the Ruhr in a Wellington without climbing into a cockpit.

Published in Paper Wars #79.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Like the name suggest, Hylaria is a game that invites you and a group of friends (or strangers) to have a wonderfully idiotic time. Set in a world that makes little sense, teams of players try to telegraph the content of their cards to their mates, without their opponents finding out what they mean.

Taught in under a minute, but endlessly replayable because the game taps into the creative nature of human beings.

boardgame: Kingsport Festival: Terrible Old Man Promo
description:
The Terrible Old Man Promo is a set of three promo cards which were given out for free at Conventions.

It includes:

    a card with rules
    The Terrible Old Man (replaces Ancient One Nr.10)
    The Ancient House on Water Street (additional offboard location)

boardgame: Original Rummikub Ergänzungs-Set für 5 und 6 Spieler
description: Expansion for Rummikub to play up to 6 players.

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Heirs of Blood
description:
Description from the publisher:

The lost heir to the kingdom of Saradyn has come forth, but without protection, he will surely fall prey to the deadly temptations of Lady Eliza Farrow, a vampire and the rival claimant to the kingdom. Only the swords of Terrinoth's heroes can protect the heir and lead him safely to his rightful throne!

Heirs of Blood is a campaign book for Descent: Journeys in the Dark Second Edition, offering a completely new campaign playable with the Descent Second Edition Core Set. This campaign book offers thirty-two completely new encounters telling the tale of the heroes' quest to keep Eliza Farrow from taking command of the hidden kingdom of Saradyn. In this campaign, every decision matters and the outcome of every quest has drastic consequences for the resolution of the campaign. Defend the heir to the throne and destroy the forces of the overlord!
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

boardgame: Rivet Wars: Clockwork Soldiers vs. Plague Troopers
description:
A kickstarter exclusive set of three Allied Clockwork Soldiers and three Blight Plague Troopers with their exclusive figures, and cards with special abilities.

Plague Troopers can bolster your defense, infect. Clockwork Soldiers can bolster special abilities and dash. However, neither can take strategic objectives.


     3 Clockwork Soldier figures
     3 Plague Trooper figures
     Game cards

IT IS A TIME OF DECISION!

Summer 1941

Will you relive or will you reshape history?

The stakes are high as you take your role as a leader of a great power in the darkest days of the 20th Century. The scene is set, will the Axis launch Barbarossa, the greatest military campaign in history, which set the course for the rest of the Second World War? You can sit on the sideline and watch as world events occur. You can co-operate with other players to defend and develop your lands, or you can trust no one but yourself, as you build up your power and influence around the globe.

Interact with your friends in 5 dimensions in this realistic civilization game, as you build up your strength using different resources and skills to gain advantage.

Set your heart and mind on one or more of the 5 dimensions.

MILITARY WAR: Great land, sea and air battles.

ECONOMIC CRISIS: Struggle for strategic resources and economic growth.

DIPLOMATIC PRESSURE: Coerce or persuade new nations into trade or war.

TECHNOLOGY RACE: Develop new weapons or industrial production breakthroughs.

MORAL CONFLICT: Join the eternal battle between good and evil.

How will you guide your power?

This inspiring game can be played in either a co-operative or non-co-operative way. In this fast moving non-turn based educational game, opportunities are continuously presented to make decisions and act. The rules you need to play are not difficult but to master the dimensions many skills are needed. Each dimension is learned one at a time and the rules are written to allow players to gradually add more dimensions. As the intricacy increases with added dimensions, you will enjoy the growing challenge to plan more strategically, think more long-term and more effectively leverage the inter dependency of the dimensions.

REALITY CHANGE GAME

As you take your path through the seasons and steps of this challenging lost world, it is like your walk through life, making decisions and developing new people and mind skills and even heart change. The challenge to grow in strength in the game, encourages learning in critical thinking and problem solving, in team work, negotiation, and strategy, which can change the attitudes and thoughts of both your mind and heart.

Write to info@playfordgames.com on the Playford Games web site to receive the improved 2019 rule book.

Complete with an extra strategy guide that provides inspiration and ideas to equip to survive the game and succeed in  your every day conflict when the game is over.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Part of the Catan Geographies series.  This map offers another game using the Catan mechanics.  Rules provide for 3-6 players and require the base game plus the 5-6 player expansion set if playing with 5 or 6 players.

Players win by achieving 10-13 VPs (depending on number of players) through the usual construction of settlements and cities, longest road or largest army, and VP points from cards or map locations.

Variations from the base game include waterways as well as roads, VP intersections, and "Off Map" settlements.  There is also an optional "Rice and Tobacco Variant" which will require the players to provide tokens not available in Catan.

Finally, as with other maps, this map provides history of the Carolinas as colonies, as well as history and statistics about the states and notable locations in each printed on the map.

boardgame: Tales of Danger #1: Days of Discovery
description:
Lost to history, a new land in the middle of the Atlantic Ocean was discovered by Portuguese sailor Raimundo Peres da Costa at the cusp of the 14th century. The process of merely planning and gaining support to make such a risk-filled journey was a difficult path of its own. Yet, in securing the sponsorship of beloved Queen Isabel of Portugal, Raimundo was able to get the supplies and crew needed to accomplish his dream.

Players recreate this accomplishment in a game that plays out as a 3-act story. First, draft cards representing people who have evidence of the hidden land and plans to get there, in order to gain sponsorship from 1 of 6 important figures. Second, use the resources from your sponsor to draft cards representing the supplies and crew needed to make the journey. Finally, sail the treacherous seas to discover the "Land of Danger" by using your hand of cards to pay for the costs of the voyage.

Gameplay is centered around a deck of multi-use cards that will provide different types of help, depending on the stage of the game, random costs incurred while sailing, and the points gained by completing segments of the voyage. While the game is a race to be first to get to the Land of Danger, points are scored along the way for the difficulty of the voyage and the quality of written journals (hidden scoring cards).

The game is playable solo, and also with 2 to 6 players.
family: Series: Land of Danger (Matt Worden Games)

description: The players have to move from "Start" to "Ziel". Movement is determined by two diece. If a player gets to a space with a traffic card, he has to observe the rules on the backside of the card.

In this game, players take on the role of grizzly bears who are trying to collect their beloved honey. Yet, they can never get their precious without taking some risks. Not only are there furious wasps and bees ready for revenge but also their bear buddies may be casting a covetous eye and trying to snatch each others’ honey with their bear hands (no pun intended). There are different types of function cards in the game which allow the players to do different things such as finding out what is hidden in the beehives or taking another player’s tile. Players can use their memory and deception skills to make full use of the different functions and turn it into a mind game, in which the winner will have outdone the others by tricking their opponents and earning the most honey. At the end of game, players score points for each honey tile that they own. The honey bee and wasps cause no instant harm to the players during the game, however if the players still have them in their baskets when the game ends, they will be penalized. The player with the highest score wins the game.
French game called "L'Alaska" could also be called - "The Hunter". It is about hunting in Alaska. Players are going around the map. The first player, who cross finish line - wins the game.

French game called "Game for freedom" made at the end of WWII. The center of game board represents a defeated Germany (Nazi). The 4 board corners show allies: Great Britain, the Soviet Union, the United States of America and France. The track represents various battles and cities of Europe.
The printed paper is glued to a wooden board. It is a simple game, made with the resources available in 1944.
It is marked "Model No. 43667 filed -1944- -EMG. SGDG patented. Composition and Writing Clichés Union."
Components: wooden figures "little horses," which represents soldiers of allies.
It is simple roll and move game. The first player, who cross all 52 cities, wins the game.

boardgame: Crabs Adjust Humidity: Volume Four (fan expansion for Cards Against Humanity)
description:
"Who are you? Where am I?" - Ronald Reagan

Comedy isn't easy. In fact, studies* have proven that it's the most difficult and challenging of all human endeavors. It takes experienced, trained professionals to produce the kind of high-potency, refined humor that generates sustained, clean-burning laughter without unpleasant side-effects.

The result of months of dedicated research and extensive animal testing, Crabs Adjust Humidity: Volume Four is comprised of 112 cards representing the very pinnacle of tasteless, juvenile humor.

* You know, studies.

A courtly game of points and powers. Play cards in turn, scoring points as you build up stacks of each rank. Use the powers of the court cards: Lock a stack with a Queen, unlock a stack with a Bishop, refresh your hand with a Knight, and find refuge with a Castle. When no more cards can be played, the player with the most points wins.

ATTN: Cpt [DATA WITHHELD]  

Captain,

At 0747Z, a foreign object that had entered the Earth`s atmosphere within U.S. airspace airburst in a multi-megaton event over [DATA WITHHELD]. The blast area is Code Black.

Hostile non-terrestrial combatants have emerged from the debris plume and have engaged national defense elements. These elements are being depleted.

Your Lawmaker has been assigned to the task force Mud Eagle 1 (ME1). ME1 is to prepare for  an immediate combat operation outside of the Pentagon’s defense perimeter. You will depart for this operation at current readiness status.

ME1`s mission is to hold it’s position against any attack while evacuations occur. You will not be relieved from this mission.

ME1 is weapons free from the moment you recieve this communication, Cpt. [DATA WITHHELD]

Do not tune in to any civilian communications for any reason.

Katherine L. Weinberg,

Chairman, National Security Council

Mud Eagle One is a cooperative score attack strategy game for 1-2 players. Each player is the captain of a Lawmaker; a warship-esque super-heavy land vehicle. Their objective is to hold at all costs against hostile attackers, inflicting as much damage as possible against enemy contacts until being destroyed.

Players will draw personnel, vehicle, gear and munitions cards that will comprise their forces to be leveraged for their mission. These forces will require a constant stream of logistics support to remain operational, and managing those logistics will be the key to achieving a positive score.

Play time is about 2 hours.

Will the tortoise beat the hares to the finish line?  Players race their rabbit movers along a twisting, colored path.  A wind-up turtle races along its own circular path, trying to beat the rabbits to the finish line.  As the tortoise moves, a colored ball randomly appears on its back, indicating the current player's next move.  The winner of the race is the first one who crosses the finish line, whether it is a player's hare or the wind-up tortoise.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Tortoise and the Hare

boardgame: Land of the Free:  Wargames Rules for North America 1754-1815
description: Land of the Free is a set of wargaming rules from Osprey that allows players to recreate the various conflicts that broke out in North America shortly prior to and just after Independence, including the French and Indian Wars, the American War for Independence, the Northwest Indian War, and the War of 1812. This set of rules lets players begin their campaigns with small warbands of 10-20 miniatures of any scale and develop their forces over time, building them into armies hundreds strong. A unique system of command points and the need to carefully manage resources or risk becoming vulnerable to counter-attack have to be finely balanced against the need to gain objectives throughout the game, creating a challenging, but enjoyable environment for your armies.

boardgame: The Barbie World of Fashion game
description: Traveling around the world, Barbie and her friends are teen fashion models: meeting new dates and earning money to buy kicky fashions. Rolling a die, players move around a 3-D molded playing board, attempting to land on spaces where they can receive date cards or earn money to later buy clothes cards at Shop spaces.  Once a player has at least 5 of these cards, they make their way back to London in order to win the game.

This is an extension to the Fallon game Magellan. This extension contains 50 additional historical cards in 5 themes:

    Temporary history
    Mountains
    Harbours
    Farming and cattle breeding
    Wars and vulcanoes

Hitler Turns South is an effort to link several Europa games into a single Campaign encompassing the entire Mediterranean Theater of Operations of World War II.

In this instance, the Campaign is based on the hypothesis that the Axis powers have turned their attention in 1941 not to invading the Soviet Union, but to securing their southern flank and simultaneously depriving Britain of vital interests: Gibraltar, the Nile delta, the Suez canal, the Near Eastern oilfields, and the head of the Persian Gulf.


     Rules - 20 pages
     National Data Sheets - 54 pages (UK, USSR, Germany, Italy, Vichy France, French North Africa, The Levant, Spain, Portugal, Rumania, Bulgaria, Hungary, Greece, Yugoslavia, Turkey, Egypt, Iraq, Iran)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The Northern Theatre of Operations 1939-40 is an effort to expand the existing EUROPA games both in scale and scope; in scale by making game components and time frames EUROPA standard, in scope by adding an extensive naval module to the rules set, by starting the game at an earlier date, and by expanding the theatre of operations to the entire Scandinavian peninsula.[...]
The campaign begins with the Axis player turn of Dec I 1939.

(from the introduction)



Contents:

    Rules (39 pages)
    Naval Tables (2 pages)
    National Data Sheets (Sweden, Finland, Norway, Denmark, Germany, Soviet Union, France, Britain)
    Order of battle notes (7 pages)

Game developped by the former Danish football player Knud Lundberg.

Each player gets:

    One board, divided into four quarters (A, B, C and D)
    One set of pieces, which are six coloured tokens (Green, Red, Yellow or Blue) and 30 Black. The coloured markers have arrows which is important to the game.


The purpose of the game is to catch the other players snakes, even by hitting it's head or by trapping it:
In the beginning each player chooses his colour, with each their Quarter, (s)he then places the coloured pieces in the shape of a snake (here the arrows is important, as they shows the snake's direction).

The players hide this information and the first player places a black marker on each of the other players Quarters of his/her own board where (s)he believes that the other player's snake(s) are located; the other players place a black marker on the same spots. If a snake is hit it have to be anounced and the black marker is placed under the coloured. When the third piece is placed, the players move their snake one step ahead, and the next player goes. When it is your turn you move your snake one space - but you are not allowed to place the head where there already is a black spot.

The player who get's his snake closed in, or hit in the head is out of the game.

The game is from the 1950s.

Easy roll-and-move which contains a bit of math:
The board resembels a Monopoly board and contains with 24 "value" spots and more.

The goal: Get one million.

The 24 cards are placed under the board on the right space so it can be seen and taken.
When a player lands on a spot (s)he can take the cards under the board - if the card is taken by another player you take it from that player.

When you have one million you are the winner.
You can also play where you play either "x dice roll" or "x mintues" and the one with the most money is the winner.

The game is made  before 1948.

Brilliantly lithographed board, showing an exact reproduction of the Exposition grounds and buildings at Chicago. The object of the game is to move your piece around the board and the person who exits the grounds first wins.  The board measures 13 1/2 x 20 1/2 inches.

A roll-and-move game based on Hans Christian Andersen's fairy tale of the "The Swineherd".

The game board have 50 spots, and have some special, white spots (6, 13, 18, 24, 31, 40 and 47) that have some spcial actions action.
Those spots, as well as spot 1 and 50 have connection to the fairytale.

First to #50 is the winner; You have to land exactly on #50 to win.

Game is possibly from the 1950s

boardgame: Commands & Colors: Ancients Expansions #2 and #3 – Rome vs the Barbarians; The Roman Civil Wars
description: A combined reprint of Commands & Colors: Ancients Expansion Pack #2 – Rome vs the Barbarians and Commands & Colors: Ancients Expansion Pack #3 – The Roman Civil Wars with all of the individual components (separate scenario books, etc.) of those products, but without the boards originally included with them.

From the publisher's website:

Read your opponent! Bluff your way to victory!

The game is about analyzing the state of play and using the knowledge to anticipate your opponent's moves. This cuts both ways: the abilities of the cards you select, your posture, your banter, and the content of your character all give your opponent insight into what your move may be - if you let it.

Gameplay is similar to Rock, Paper, or Scissors however, you declare what attack you intend to use prior to revealing it. If you lied, well, that's fine, but if you told the truth you gain Honor Points which will enable you to deal more damage later on! Do you dare risk dealing more damage at the risk of becoming predictable?
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Bunny Hop is "a child's first alphabet game."

Players draw cards to move along a track marked with the letters of the alphabet.  When a player lands on a space, he or she must name the letter on the space.  An older player or adult determines if the player is correct.  When a player passes the starting space, he or she collects a carrot token.  The first player to circle the board twice and collect two carrot tokens wins.

In the advanced game, each player must name the letter on the space, say a word that starts with the letter and name the letters before and after.

The game board is made of twelve large puzzle pieces which must be assembled before play.

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Flying Attack!
description: Shredder's Foot Clan Ninjas are scaling the high-rise on an evil mission. Send the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles flying into action to stop them! Launch the Turtles from the construction-site catapult to knock enemies off their perilous perch. But beware! Whoever plays Shredder has cunning cards that can thwart the Turtles' high-flying heroics!
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
From the rules:
"Be the first person to give away your cards by matching the same color or shape on the discard pile.  Get a Judas card and skip a turn.  Get a Devil card and you have to draw another card.  Get the Jesus card to help you or someone else win."

Discipleship Junior is a game similar to Uno or Crazy Eights, with a few differences. Each player's cards are kept face-down and are unknown to all players including the owner.  They are turned face-up one per turn, and can be discarded if they match the top of the discard pile. Judas cards cause the drawing player to lose his turn. Devil cards "trap" a disciple card, and must be "cast out" by a card matching the trapped disciple. The Jesus card allows each player to discard all face-up cards.

From the box:
"Collect 5 matching Fruitz from the Seed Pile, the Good Soil Pile, or another person to fill up your row!  When the Fruitz have been pick, the person with the greatest Harvest wins!  Try to avoid the deceitful and wicked Sinful Nature card or you could be in a jam."

Fruitz of the Spirit is a set collection game of set collection.  Cards display pictures of various fruits and are named for virtues mentioned in Galatians 5:13-23.

Players try to collect as many sets of five matching cards as possible from a total of nine different card types.  Each player starts with five face-down cards.  They turn one up at a time, and may take the card from the face-up draw pile if it matches or draw a face-down card.  They may also take any matching cards that any other players have face-up.  A Sinful Nature card causes the player to lose a turn. When a player makes a set of five of the same card, known as an orchard, he may harvest the orchard by turning the cards upside-down and setting them aside.

The game ends when the draw pile is exhausted.  The player with the most harvested orchards wins.

From the box:
"Bug out with the highest score by counting as may insect cards as possible without turning over a bird card."

The Great Bug Hunt: The Unbuglieveable Insect Counting Card Game! is a push-your-luck type card game.  Players take turns turning over cards one at a time from the draw pile of 46 bugs and 6 birds.  They score one point for each bug card turned over.  Tbey may choose to end their turn whenever they like.  However, they must avoid turning over one of the bird cards.  If they turn over a bird, it eats all their bugs and their turn ends with no points scored. If a player ends their turn before turning over a bird, they keep their points.  When all bird cards have been turned over, the entire deck is reshuffled.  First player to a predetermined number of points (40 for 2 to 4 players and 25 for 5+ players is suggested) wins.

Skyraiders over Korea  is a light solitaire war game that and is in no way meant to be an accurate representation of the Korean air war.

You play as a navy pilot flying strike missions against North Korean targets in an Able Dog, AD Skyraider from USN Valley Forge from July 3rd – 19th July 1950.

You will need to balance the mission success against the Skyraider's ability to absorb damage and decide whether to perform another attack run or head for home. It is a fine balancing act and one that can result in mission success or becoming a POW.

Welcome to the “happy valley”

From the publisher's website

A hobby game for 2 players.

A simple, easy to play interpretation of Association Football (Soccer). The design of the playing pieces harks back to the early days of tabletop football games like Subbuteo, where the 2-dimensional card players were known as "flats". For the first time this game is supplied with 12 full teams!

User Summary

This is a game for two players where each player mans a team of eighteen players, eleven of which are on the pitch at any one time. Each team tries to score goals in the oppositions net. As with real football the game is split into two halves of 45 minutes with a break in between, with teams swapping ends at half time.

The game uses player piece positioning as well as D6 dice to create some randomness, in the form of "scattering" the ball a random direction. Players move their pieces up to a specified amount and if they are next to the piece with the ball may tackle.

Rules, tables and diagrams for throw-ins, dribbling, kicking, shooting and other football terms are included.

From the PDF

A Simple Hobby Game for 1 to 8 Players

In the real world, Pro Circuit Racing is a game that models small scale races around a closed circuit. It's a racing game for 1 to 8 players (or more if you feel the need) that is controlled by rolling dice to see how far cars race.

However, it has a mythology all its own...

The PCR Concept
Pro Circuit Racing, or PCR, is a race concept developed to be friendly to the spectators, cheap and environmentally friendly. It is a busy race day with non-stop action.

From the PDF

Welcome to The Belt!

In the 23rd century humanity has been cast to the stars. Hardy spaceship pilots mine the asteroid belt between Earth and Mars for raw materials. It's a dangerous job, but the rewards are generous as the Colonial Administration needs as much of the precious minerals as they can get to ensure the survival of humanity.

- - -

The Belt is a game for two to four players where the objective is to mine as many asteroids as you can and get the rocks back to base before the end of the game. Each player takes it in turns to move their ship towards an asteroid in order to mine it (see Mining). Once the asteroid has been mined the player must move their ship to their base and deposit the asteroid chunks there before moving off to get more asteroids. Beware, though, because pirates roam the Belt and will attack any ship trying to move rocks out of the belt.

The win conditions can be set by the players. You could create a time-limit with the winner being the player who collected the most asteroids; or the first to get # asteroids wins.

Players take turns putting all the asteroid counters within the designated 'field', then the three pirate counters are placed in the centre of the board next to each other.

1D6 die is rolled on each players turn, which provides a random maximum move distance for them that turn. If ships come within 6 hexes of each other or the pirates they may get shot at. Once a player is on the same hex as an asteroid they roll a 1D3 and it takes that many turns to mine it successfully. A maximum of two asteroids can be held by a ship, it must then return to its base to unload.

Rules for targeting and firing on the other ships creates added tension, and repairs can also be affected but leave the ship vulnerable.

A wild card game with a cowboy, a villain and a lucky number. 
Use them wisely or place cards that sum up the deck to exactly 11 so you can get rid of your cards and pass them on to your opponent. The more cards the other players take, the closer you get to victory.

A fast game of observation & memory containing 5 different games. Prove you're fast, perceptive and with a great memory! Be the first to spot identical symbols between the cards. Whoever succeeds-wins! Play in a pair, group or by- yourself. Are you up for the challenge?

This is a very simple track game depicting a bicycle race across country. On a turn, you spin the spinner and advance the number of spaces indicated. The first player who reach the Home space is the winner.
Snake Game is simple spin and move game. Players are moving along snake body with 54 stops. The first player, who reaches the 54th space wins the game.

Pick Card! Act or Draw Card! Guess Card! A combination of Charades and Pictionary onto the words are...Over The Line. A Cards Against Humanity style game. Ultimate party game for friends and family that goes too far, too soon, too often.

Help put together a barnwarming party and make sure everyone has a good time!

Dipsy Dipsy, Ice Cream Delivery Mare Extraordinaire, has just purchased a brand new barn to live in. And you know what that means – barn warming party! There’s just one small problem: she forgot to send out the invitations! Fortunately you (and your opponent) have agreed to help, by gathering all of Dipsy’s friends and customers. All you need to do is make sure that the guests arrive in proper groups and help them to bring the right gifts and and make sure someone brings the gifts and…Oh dear!

The objective of the game is to create complete sets of guests and gifts, and then to claim the set when completed. Players take turns playing a guest and or a gift on a set until it is completed. Each guest requires at least one other guest and up to two gifts in order for the set to be complete. The player who completes the set collects it, and the player who collects the most cards by the end of the game wins!

The object of Fur Pile is, like real life, to get the hook-up.
But like real life, this is easier said than done!

Throughout the game, players match up beautiful young furry femmes with appropriate partners and toys to ensure they have a good evening of fun. Every girl’s needs (partners and toys) must be met, and the winner of each “set” is the player who completes the set - leaving no need unfilled. A winning set (including girls and toys) is placed in that player’s Victory Pile. When sunrise comes, the player with the most cards in their Victory Pile wins the game.

Designed by Roland Guiscard and featuring easy to learn rules, quick game play, and exclusive artwork from furry artists Catwolf and En Den!

Insight Out is "the game that gets your family talking."

From the box:
"The easy-to-learn format is perfect for any child age 3 or older.  Two sets of age-appropriate question cards promote communication and discussion with children of any age."

Insight Out is a roll-and-move game in which players move their cards along a track, drawing cards and answering questions or performing actions based on the spaces they land on.  Questions are designed to stimulate discussion among family members, and there are two levels--for younger and older children.  If a player chooses not to answer the question or do the action drawn, he or she may go back to the original space and re-roll once.  If the re-roll also results in drawing a card, then the player must do the action on the card or go back to the original space and lose his or her turn.  The winner is the first player to reach the Finish space.

An advanced game adds a few extra rules, such as spaces where cards may be given to another player and the need to roll the exact number to land on the finish space.

Each round, one person is nominated by the group to be the Moral Compass. This person gets a second set of voting cards, essentially doubling this person's voting power, helping to break ties in a game with an even number of society members. The Moral Compass can also choose to vote two different ways, which is especially strategic when playing with the "Modes Expansion"

There are now different ways to play each turn! The person asking the question selects which 'mode' they want before pulling a card:


    Strategic mode (also known as classic) - the person asking the question gets a point if they vote the same as society
    Honesty mode - the person asking the question gets a point if they vote the same as society and each member of society that voted the same as the person asking the question gets a point
    Majority mode - every person in the majority gets one point, regardless of who is asking the question.

From the box:
"Domino Discovery is an exciting matching game that reinforces basic learning skills and provides hours of fun.  It is an easy and creative way to involve children in learning activities."

Domino Discovery Fractions is an educational take on Dominoes, with the added element of requiring players to recognize and match equivalent fractions as well as fractions expressed as numbers and as pictures.  From a total of 40 dominoes, each player is dealt five and one starting domino is placed on the table.  In turn, each player must find a domino with a fraction that matches one end of a domino already in play, and play their domino against that one.  If they have not matches, they must draw from the draw pile until they can play a domino.  Play ends when one player successfully plays all their dominoes.

In order to play successfully, players must use their knowledge of equivalent fractions (such as 1/2 = 2/4) as well as understanding visual representations of fractions (such as knowing that a picture of a circle with one of three pieces colored in is equal to 1/3).

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Ancient White Dragon Expansion Pack
description: The Ancient White Dragon Arveiaturace is the competitive prize for Month 1 of the Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, Tyranny of Dragons.  The prize includes one pre-painted miniature and numerous upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Ancient Brass Dragon Expansion Pack
description: The Ancient Brass Dragon Sivart is the competitive prize for Month 2 of the Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, Tyranny of Dragons.  The prize includes one pre-painted miniature and numerous upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

description:
Space Station Disaster is a light strategy game. You and your opponents are building a space station by laying out hex tiles. But most of the tiles contain disasters like aliens, radiation, and fire. Your goal is to use matching equipment cards to fix the disasters. If you fix a disaster you get a point. If you don’t have the right equipment you’ll have to run away or risk being injured and losing points. The game starts off easy but soon you will be cornered by disease and mad robots hoping to draw a teleport tile to escape.

Through clever tile placement, strategic movement and a bit of luck you try and fix more disasters than your opponents before the space station is finished.

The game plays in 30-60 minutes depending on the number of players. With a different order of tiles each game you get a unique experience every time.

Citizen Cane is a dice-drafting game in which 2-4 Christmas elves vie to build their gift-building empires!

Each turn, players roll a die for each elf on their team with up to two re-rolls - then all the rolled dice are pooled and players draft the numbers they want for that turn. Dice are spent to join elf unions that grant players special abilities, buy things with candy-cane currency at the quartermaster's stores or get on with the work of building, wrapping and tagging gifts for Santa.

Citizen Cane is a single-sheet game that relies only on a few items most gamers will already have around the house, and is intended to be printed on the inside face of Christmas cards for gamers.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Symtactics is a simulation of tactical urbanism set in the Hong Kong region in the year of 2047. It involves both tactical as well as strategic decision making. It was printed in an edition of 20,000 for the Hong Kong edition of the New City Reader which was created specifically for the 'Uneven Growth' exhibition on view at the Museum of Modern Art from Nov 22, 2014 to May 10, 2015.

Ideally, Symtactics is played with 5 players. All players must complete tactical interventions at 8 sites around the Hong Kong region while dealing with catastrophic events. Players can either play for a collective win, working together to beat an ever increasing inequality meter or they can play individually.

Interventions are created by matching resource cards to icons printed on the site hexes. A player must successfully match three icons or use wildcard resources as dictated by the current round's market situation. Once an intervention is completed, the resources used go into a pile as marked on the board. There are 8 piles that correspond to the sites on the map and another 8 towards the north of the board, corresponding to the development of global inequality.

Choose your card wisely, then wager victory points to gain the advantage over your competition.

The game of rock, paper, scissors has been played in various forms for hundreds of years. That's Mine is a fast paced time filler game that adapts this enduring mechanic to a card game. It adds support for more than two players and introduces a risk/reward wagering element.

Each round involves all players choosing a numbered card with rock, paper or scissors and resolving battle as expected. Things get interesting when players wager existing victory points to influence when they enter the competition. Every hand is resolved leaving only one winner with a simple yet decisive tiebreaking system.

Each wager builds the kitty and the survivor exclaims "That's Mine" while claiming their winnings.

Experience the thrill of making a single framework, bizarre and imaginative, borrowing the mustachioed smile Gauguin or Van Gogh's restless gaze to achieve a Bacchus certainly more extravagant than that of Caravaggio. But the game does not last long because to win the game then you have to make a genuine portrait, paying tribute to the great masters such as Raphael and Picasso, and composing their own portraits. You have to search in different areas of the game puzzle missing to complete your portrait, and the 10-sided die will tell you if you can take them freely, or you have to challenge your opponent in a feud with the nuts, or you will have to answer a question right on portrait you want to dial or the Artist who painted it ... The questions on Art and its Masters are more than four hundred, and were divided between questions Difficulty standard and ... questions "for the little ones", specially designed to accompany children to the discovery of Art. With "Portrait d'Autore, masterpieces to Male 'gaming experience encompasses a mix of different emotions. The good fortune of being able to identify and take the missing pieces joins skill in answering questions, and everything blends the fun and imagination. The different portraits that will consist in the match, one of the most curious of the other, will not fail to arouse continuous emotions and feelings in the players. The main objective of the Game and what to discover the art and his most famous portraits, while stimulating the observation and discovery, fantasy and imagination, through the magic of a Portrait of the Author that is gradually becoming ... a true portrayal! Portrait of author also proposes to transfer to the players some knowledge of art history, through a lot of questions about the artists, their works, and on Art in general, which sometimes require the observation and research of the details: even children will learn in a spontaneous and always fun, challenging each other with questions to measure ... baby!

boardgame: Novela: il gioco della soap opera
description:
What horrible secret is Reginald, the most popular model in the world, hiding? Who will get the CEO position at Superglobal Inc., the most powerful company in the world? And, above all, will Miriam and Max be able to marry, overcoming the perfidious machinations plotted behind their backs?

Novela is the first board game allowing you to play as the protagonists of a soap opera or telenovela, and live their stories of fame, money, love and power. Every player must attain their three secret purposes... by all means! And every game is a true adventure: constant twists, alliances, betrayals and intrigues will fascinate any TV serials lover, while those who can't stand them will finally be able to take sweet revenge on Ridge, J.R. and the likes. In "Novela" the rules are very simple, and it is up to the players to decide how much to invest in their characters, possibly turning the game in the craziest, intriguing and fun role playing game.

Players roll dice to move around the board.  Each space contains events or allows players to draw cards to achieve their objectives.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You can cross the Cardles in order to discover new games/
 With Cardles you can play cards in a completely new original way. The game contains two bunch of Cardles with which, taking advantage of the innovation of the crossings, they can be used to adapt classic card games or to create new games, especially crafted to use these special cards.

Buying this game you actually buy two bunch of jigsaw puzzle cards.
The book “Twenty and more games with the Cardles” in detail describe the rules of many games, between which the games of combination like 40 Scale and Machiavelli, some varying of Domino, the games for children like Rubamazzetto and One, let alone many solitary ones, all returned still more exciting thanks to the particularitity of the crossings.
 Cardles is a particularly apt game to all the lovers of the cards, children, boys or adults, that they will remain bewitched from the new look and the inusuale format regarding the familiar cards. The visual impact of the Cardles is of sure effect, because every piece of the jigsaw puzzle contain the image of a card, included the Jokers, the figures, and the Ace of Spades “signed” like in the bunch of traditional cards.

English description:

Idiom Link is played with a thirty-six grid square dial. Players have to make words. Each word has to start with the last character of the previous word.

Chinese description:
「連線字龍」遊戲為ＭＪ工作室所設計的中文成語填字遊戲。(ＭＪ工作室成員為Michael Lou樓齊賢、Jack Li李子建)
「連線字龍」遊戲是由三十六個格子所組成的方形字盤，字盤中置入了十個「字龍文字」，可組合成三組頭尾相接的成語，就是後一個成語的頭一個字必須與前一個成語的最後一個字相同（例：「花言巧語」的「語」，接「語重心長」的「語」）。請根據字盤中十個字龍文字的提示，先確定可組合成那三組頭尾相接的成語；再將字盤中的十個字龍文字按照三組成語的頭尾順序，在線條不交錯、格子不重覆的方式下，用一條線將字盤中的三十六個格子全數串連起來（即以第一組成語的頭一個字為起點，第三組成語的最後一個字為終點），即可完成極具高難度的「連線字龍」遊戲。

description: A miniatures skirmish system for Reaper Miniature's wild west line of figures using modified Warlord rules which employ initiative cards and randomly drawn skills to make each encounter different.

Set in 8th century Northern Britain, Before Scotland: 750 AD - Kingdoms is a tactical combat deck building strategy game. It is historical in nature with a rich context and set of mechanics drawn from original sources.

The game begins in 750 AD and lets you play as one of four nations who struggle for supremacy: the Angles, Britons, Gaels, or Picts. After choosing a leader and moral cause that give special abilities to your kingdom, each player draws up to 7 random cards from their Kingdom Reserve deck. They travel through the Environment and scout the Terrain at the battlefield; these are event cards that alter the impending engagement. From the cards-in-hand (i.e. the Warband), each player may select up to 3 Warband Unit cards that face-off with a competing kingdom. After a simultaneous reveal, cards are resolved one-versus-one with additional Supply, Tactics, or Training cards from the Warband that are used to modify the encounter. Winning cards enter the Hoard. Losing cards are flushed into the Barrow. The game ends when the Kingdom Reserves are exhausted and the player with the largest hoard wins.

This game is targeted at the casual gamer and is a gateway to the more in-depth title Before Scotland: 750 AD - Empires which layers in a working economy and geographic constraints.

description:
The 20th investigation for Sherlock Holmes Consulting Detective.
In 1887, you're going to investigate  across London about the mysterious Man without Face.
Will you solve the mystery?
