description:
FreeSpace: Tactics is the official licensed miniatures game for two or more players based on the FreeSpace video games from Interplay. The core set includes four different ships (Apollo, Valkyrie, Anubis and Horus) from the series.

FreeSpace: Tactics uses cards for movement and combat. During set up, players customize their movement decks with a number of Ace cards based on their ship types.

At the start of each turn, players simultaneously select one card from their hand for each ship. Cards are revealed in initiative order and that ship moves and attacks.

The combat system uses d6, d8 and d10 dice - the exact pool comes from the attacker ship type and card used. Damage reduces shields and inflicts hull damage. Missiles roll a special die and may deal critical hits.

Six scenarios come in the core set, and there are guidelines for expanding those scenarios or creating your own.

Space Mines is a game playable with Looney Pyramids -- one stash per player plus a few extras in a new color to be asteroids.

The pieces are placed, lying on their sides, into the play space anywhere they fit -- there is no board or grid.  Then the ships fire and mine in a prescribed fashion, targeting whatever is directly in front of them.  Mining asteroids earns points, and those points can be doubled if you take the fewest losses.

The pirate with the most jewels wins this head-to-head game. Players must move their colored pieces around the board attempting to capture opponents 10 pieces and the five jewels which occupy the board. Players depart either hidden or safe harbour at the start, and

To do so the players move their pieces one or two spaces per turn in any one direction and attempt to land atop an occupied space, capturing either the pirate piece of an opponent or a jewel which resided there.

Captured pieces are removed from the board, and a player wins if he or she removes all of the pieces belonging to an opponent. Otherwise, play ends when all of the jewels have been claimed, and the player with the most jewels wins.

boardgame: Chopper War: A Solitaire Game of Helicopter Combat in Vietnam
description:
CHOPPER WAR is a solitaire game of Helicopter Combat in Vietnam. The player assumes the role of a commander of an Air Cavalry Troop consisting of Gunships, Scout Ships and Lift Ships, plus, a platoon of Aero-Rifles. As commander, you are responsible for securing your Area of Operations (AOR) by successfully locating, fixing and destroying the enemy. Your mission is to destroy as many enemy units with the minimum amount of losses to your Troop.

GAME OBJECTIVE
The objective of CHOPPER WAR is to successfully complete a number of missions and destroy enemy forces in order to gain Victory Points (VP) for the US Forces. The player can choose to play a short game (4 Missions), an average game (6 Missions), a long game (8 Missions) or a full Tour (12 Missions). Once all Missions have been resolved (successfully or not) the Player totals his VP and compares this with the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success.

Units include Huey Slicks, Huey Gunships, AH-1 Cobra Gunships, OH-6 Scout Helicopters, Aero-Rifle Squads (the Blues), and Special Forces Teams. Missions include Hunter-Killer Teams where a Scout and Gunship attempt to locate and destroy the enemy, Hawk Flights in search of VC/NVA Suspects, Rescues of Downed Crews, Fire Support, Firebase Overrun Support and Search and Destroy operations with the Aero-Rifle Platoons.

Sentinel Conflict is a super-powered party game for 3-8 players in which you control the greatest “what if?” fights in the Multiverse!

Story War is all about using creativity and humor in impromptu storytelling to defeat your opponents! In Sentinel Conflict, each player will have a hand of Character cards and Item cards, and will use those heroes, villains, devices, and equipment to assemble the best team to take on the other players!

Dangerous Adventure is a game where players have to complete quests (killing monsters, farming components...) to gain the best amount of Victory Points.

The players must create greats armors, train powerfull powers or buy differents items making their task more easy.

There are multiples actions to do. These actions have a cost in hours, and players must to manage these hours on a big clock that determines the turn order.

Finally, the different kinds of monsters will make players gain experience in combat, may face increasingly difficult challenges

Overview

A deluge of large, mysterious meteors have fallen from the sky and struck the lands of Astralevia. Each meteor contained a monstrous, rock colossus that has come forth from its crater, hellbent on devastating the land.

As dire as a threat these Colossi pose, more than a fight for survival emerged once the rare and powerful Cosmic Ores that compose their bodies were discovered. These special ores are capable of producing weapons and tools of uncanny properties. And so, those guided by greed, power, new order, and even peace (to keep this commodity out of the wrong hands) have quickly assembled bands of warrior-miners to harvest this extremely valuable resource from the Colossi. These warrior-miners—known as Veinreapers for their ability to crack apart the Colossi through their rich veins of ore—have been equipped with special Pickaxes forged from Metal Shards scattered by the meteors’ impacts—the only metal able to penetrate the coarse flesh of the gigantic creatures.

You will play a band of Veinreapers fighting for the greatest share of Cosmic Ores. The outfit that mines the most ores will rise as the strongest power within Astralevia, taking control of the nation and winning the game.

Summary

Players will take turns placing Veinreapers in Kill Formations against Colossi on the game board while collecting Metal Shards and forging the special Pickaxe that their warriors are equipped with. Each Colossus can only be killed and mined by Veinreapers placed in a specific Kill Formation. The number of Veinreapers engaged against a Colossus and the properties of your warriors’ equipped Pickax determine how many Cosmic Ores you mine from each Colossus. The game ends when all Veinreapers have been placed and the player who mines the most Cosmic Ores based on these placements wins the game.

There are two ways to play this fun matching game – Face Up Match Up for the youngest players and Memory Match Up for kids 3+. In Face Up Match Up place all cards face up on a table or smooth surface. Have an adult request a match. For instance, “can you find the “7s?” Children can take turns or compete to see who finds the most matches. Or have the child pick up twelve cards in numeric order. For a more challenging game, turn the cards face down and play a memory matching game. Try to make matches by turning over two cards on each turn. If the cards match, take the cards. If they do not match turn them back over in the same place in the game. A challenging puzzle keeps the fun going for years to come.

Why families love this item:
Make learning numbers fun with a girl’s favorite theme – princesses! Colorful dancing princesses adorn each card. Each card includes the numeral, the number word, and a physical representation of the number in symbol. The compact box makes this a terrific take-along item.

Test your knowledge of science truths with this set of 480 strange science statements! Includes 200 double-sided cards with fun facts and scientific explanations 32 score chips and 18 answer cards in file-cabinet box. For 2–6 players. Grades 5+

How to Play:

1. Take turns reading 3 science statements.

2. Everyone guesses which one is true or which one is false.

3. Turn the card over to find out—along with an explanation about each true or false fact to read aloud.

4. Collect one score chip from each category to win.

Synergy is a four player business strategy board game designed to mirror real life business industries with billions of possible game play scenarios to create a unique experience for every game.

The setup for Synergy involves the players taking on the roles of new venture capitalists, traveling the world to start and develop new businesses in 16 different industries. Every business’s worth and location in Synergy are randomly assigned based on game play, which allows every game to be different. During the game, players will have the chance to acquire businesses, improve their net incomes, and build up the value of their businesses. Each player’s goal during the game is to finish their career with the highest net worth, which factors in the termination value of the player's businesses and the assets they have acquired over their careers.

Unique dice mechanisms are used to determine player movement and the net incomes of the acquired businesses and action cards are used to create significant shifts in the business landscape. Synergy allows a wide range of business strategies to be applied in a entertaining business simulation game. Each game of Synergy requires the player to assess the business landscape laid out during game play and make reactive adjustments to their strategy, just as they might in the real life business world. To add an extra layer of depth, the game is playable with two separate sets of rules - a Standard Version, for most players, and a Pro Version, for more experienced players who want a greater challenge.

description:
Snails try to eat the plants before they can grow, while the gardener tries to trap the snails: a Looney Pyramids game played with a single rainbow stash and two dice on a 3x4 board.

In the solo game the player is the farmer, and in the two-player rules the second player plays the snails. The farmer has all 12 of the transparent colored pyramids (plants), and the snail farmer has only the three black pyramids.

All pieces start off the board, and each turn one player throws both dice, applying the roll to a single piece, which must be moved in a single track, from left to right and top to bottom, circling back to the beginning if it runs off the end. Smaller pyramids can be carried by larger pyramids that they land on top of. Same-sized pyramids can trap pyramids of the opponent. Larger pyramids can capture smaller opposing pyramids (or protect smaller friendly pyramids). Captured snails are available to return to play, while captured plants are removed from the game.

The farmer wins by building a three-part tree of a single color, or by trapping all three snails. The snails win be capturing at least one piece of each color.

The event takes place in 1690, the golden period of the "piracy” and describes the pirates attempt to save New Providence by the English navy sent to conquer it.

The game is created on the analogy of the so called "board game" which must be larger and have more complex rules. It is designed for two to four players but with a little effort it can be played individually. This will need the player to make moves alternately on both sides. This way he can try and clarify the various constructions and tactical maneuvers.

The game is built of two interrelated parts. In one you will be admiral of the navy. On one side are the English ships that need to transport military units in order to to take over New Providence. On the other side is the pirate fleet trying to stop them.
If the British army is successfully transferred to the island it will have to face the pirate band's to repel their attacks and conquer in time three forts that have strategic importance for the conquest of the island

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Durani Empire – Gilded Band
description:
The ostentatious golems of The Gilded Band cause many to underestimate them - a mistake few survive. For beneath the gold plating is a devious and battle-hardened elite unit. Regimental commander Ramaka uses misleading showmanship to his advantage on the battlefield, as well as in the court of his sponsor, Princess Dasra.

All Gilded Band Golems share the unique Passive Ability of ignoring enemy Critical or Lucky dice rolls.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Durani Expansion – The Khan's Pyre
description: An ancient Empire built its wealth upon the stalwart Golems of Arcanum Durani. Hewn from living rock and precious minerals, these glittering brutes overwhelm their enemies with long-range obliteration.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Durani Elite – Winged Vanguard
description: An elite version of the Titan Durani Winged Preserver that can freeze enemies in place with an Immobilizing Stab.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Durani Empire – Jagara Colossus
description: The Jagara Colossus is the largest Golem in the Durani arsenal! The Jagara not only wreaks havoc on enemies with its terrifying Apocalyptic Blast, it's so might that it destroys terrain and obstructions as it strides across the battlefield!

description:
Reapers of the Sands introduces three new Nightmarish golems that will add a deadly edge to your Golem Arcana Army. The Corpse Collector, Necrotic Fencer, and Bone Fiend all are from the Urugal Arcanum, and can be added to your army no matter whom you pledge your allegiance to.

In addition the expansion come with four powerful relics to outfit your army with.

The game re create the counterattack for the German Army by Allied Army executed on 21 May 1940.

It is a free postcard wargame with 11 counters only.(including markers)

The German player win if through 8th turn the units exit by "german exit hexes". The Allied win if avoid the german victory condition.

The die cut version of counters were included in Command Magazine Japan #89.

English and Korean rules available.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Gudanna Dominion – Black Widows
description:
The shrouded webs of the Spider Prince took many years to construct and within them lurk the Black Widows, a shadow regiment formed a decade before The Great Khan’s death. The regiment doesn’t officially exist - yet it features prominently in nightmares of the Prince’s enemies.
 All Black Widow Golems share the unique Passive Ability of gaining a 100% Cover Bonus when in Medium Cover.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Gudanna Expansion – The Khan's Pyre
description: The Great Khan carved out his Dominion with the secrets ar Arcanum Gudanna. Forged from soil and blood, these Golems attack with unrelenting speed and ruthless tactics.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Urugal Expansion – The Khan's Pyre
description: Spun from the bones of the once-living, the Golems of Arcanum Urugal are fearsome fighters designed to close with their enemies and batter them into submission.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Zikia – The Khan's Pyre
description:
Description from the publisher:

Grown from the trees of Eretsu's living forests, the golems of Arcanum Zikia are the masters of ambush warfare. In Golem Arcana: Zikia – The Khan's Pyre, you'll find three new Zikia Golems: the Bramblehorn, the Woodguard, and the Wildwood Dryad with its fearsome thorn eruption attack! Also included are four Relic Cards representing ancient magical artifacts with which you can equip your Golems with to wreak further havoc.

Yomi is a fighting game in card form in which physical dexterity is replaced by hand management skills. Just like in a fighting game, you have to know your character, know the matchup, and know your opponent. Each character has a deck that follows the same conventions as a deck of playing cards. This makes it easy to learn the contents of your deck: numbered cards are normal moves, face cards are special moves, and aces are super moves.

Yomi: Panda vs G.Panda — which can be played as a standalone game or as an expansion for either the first or the second editions of Yomi — features two kinds of panda decks, one inspired by the bluffing in the game Pandante.

Players simultaneously select an undead role.  Each role affects the village population in some way and score points according to the aggregate effect of all roles selected.  When the village is wiped out, the game ends, and the player with the highest score wins.

Players attempt to empty their hands by extending the paths of an erupting volcano.  Every card has to match the prior color, but most cards change the color of that path.  The paths will branch, change colors, and sometimes be blocked entirely.  Furthermore, colored villagers are fleeing from the volcano, and cards can also be played to match their colors.  Players play up to 3 cards each turn, so it is important to plan ahead and build ‘combos’ by saving up sets of cards that can chain off of each other.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From the back of the box (translated from German):

Hello! My name is Bean, Mr. Bean. I have the honor to present to you my unique unique, completely crazy and super oblique party game.

Since it is drawn (if one may call the Doodle above) and circumscribed (what a nice word for chatter) demonstrated and with hands and feet (one wild Gehampel, I tell you) that the laugh lines shake and spill over the bags.

And sometimes I dip itself on and bring even more fun and entertainment (... well, Mr. Bean actually rather creates THE TOTAL CHAOS !! The Red.).

So, take heart! Get into the bean-iest game of all time. Funnier it may not be ...

From the back of the box (translated from german):

The right game for kids with mind!

Guess your friends correctly, test your knowledge, be creative and spontaneous, prove your skills.

Each participant moves his playing piece on the double circuit. Depending on the color of each box a party task  awaits:

On the Green the Alphabet disc arrow is rotated and you have to find a term with the correct letter within seconds.

It applies to YELLOW to find five termns to a specific category  in 30 seconds... the hourglass is running!

On PINK there is a funny question, often with three crazy answers, but only one is correct.

And BLUE brings all the action - lots of original creative tasks: for example: Lightning Draw - tight - feel - represent a concept pantomimic or rewrite funny - not tangled in tongue twisters - etc etc etc!

The multicolored fields bring double pleasure: one can give the task either to one or all other gamers and even be the referee!

From the rules sheet in the game:

How to play
All Letter Tablets are separated, placed face down and shuffled. The Black Tablets are separated and placed face up. Each player takes five Letter Tablets and places them on a Letter Rack, hidden from the opponents. Divide the Black Tablets between all players.

Players in turn each place a Black Tablet on the Playboard leaving at least on square (including diagonally) between all the Black Tablets. Play continues until all Black Tablets have been placed or cannot be placed. The first player must now try to place a complete word anywhere on the board ensuring that it fits between two Black Tablets. If the player is unable to do this a single Letter Tablet is placed anywhere on the board. The player replaces any Tablet used.
Play continues around the Tablet until all the Letter Tablets have been used.

Scoring
Players score either when they place a complete word on the Playboard or when they place Tablet(s) which complete a partly made word. In either case the player scores one point for each Letter Tablet in the completed word.

Quick Step is a smart amusement game full of surprises for 2 or 4 people. Luck is not determining because it depends very much on where to best move your stone. Each game of QUICK STEP is exciting and full of new contingencies to which you must respond tactically smart and clever.

Quick Step is, as the name implies, a fast, quick lively game that invites to play several games in a row to determine the overall winner.

Setup Quick Step:
With 2 players, each player gets four double-sided pieces (green/red) and builds a tower with it. One player puts all the green sides face up and the other player puts all the red sides up. The towers are then placed in the big starting circle marked with red and green. 
With 4 players, the blue/yellow pieces are used also. Setup is the same, but blue and yellow start at the other big circle, playing then in the other direction.

Playing Quick Step:
Roll the Dice. Move one piece that shows your color. If you land on an empty space, turn your piece face down (now the color of your opponent is visible!). If you land on any other piece(s), don't turn your pice face down. All pieces under your piece are now blocked and cannot move until you move your piece away. If you roll a "6", don't turn your piece face dawn, even if you land on an empty space.
It may happen that your color is not visible when it is your turn. Then you just skip your turn. The situation on the board may change and then you can play again.
To reach the end of the track, you have to roll exactly the needed number and you don't turn your piece face down then.

The winner is the person who moves one piece directly into the ending circle.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

The players start at the starting position of their color. Movement is determined by a die. The first player to get one of his playing tokens to the end of the track wins the game. At the roll of a 1,2,3,4 or 5, if a token lands on a free space, it is turned over to it's other color and belongs to the opposite player. If a token lands on an occupied space, it is put onto the first token without being flipped over. Only the upper token in a stack may move. If a token reaches the finish, it is not flipped over.

—user summary

From the back of the box (translated):

Fun games of logic and set theory.

This game will make fun for all because of its many easy and difficult tasks. Here LEGO bricks can not be used as building blocks, but as a sign of thought content. Even children aged 4 to 5 years old want to learn thinking.

Learning to think in the game. For children, gaming is the appropriate learning situation. That's why games are suggested in the book, whose "rules" trigger thought processes:
The player discover Commons and Differents, draw conclusions, identify and apply rules, develop winning strategies, learn to calculate the probability of chance and the like. All this is amusing and fun, just by playing. Also competing games are in there ... and a portion hidden and open mathematics. The game manual is, however, completely without technical terms. IT is -  written for amateurs and still gives parents, kindergarten teachers and teachers' knowledge of logical and mathematical relationships and thus the background knowledge that facilitates the development of suitable game situations, which in turn develop and support basic skills for thinking of the children.

User Summary:
There are different "boards" included (paper with geometric structures printed on them)
Instead of the figures, stacks of 2 to 3 LEGO bricks are used. There are multiple games which can be played on these boards or without a board only with the bricks.
Included is a 96 pages book full with different games for the material included, and hints for which age and school grade they are adequate.
Some examples for the games:
"Nine mans mills" rule
A pachisi game with changing blocking rules
A logical rule guessing game: stacks of 3 colored bricks. Which rule is matching for all. e.g. exact 1 white brick in each stack.

Fun and educational game. Look for the symbol that appears repeated as many times as display in the deck.The first player that finds it, takes the card. The first player that ends up with more cards is the winner.

Description taken from the back of the box:

Each player has to use one petter. The attack begins from the center. Each player has in turn with his opponent one unchecked kick in order to make a goal. The ball has to be beaten from the point where it comes to a stop. It can be kicked into the goal directly or by rebounding from the borders.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Urugal Elite – Carapace Brute
description:
The Carapace Brute is a true berserker, dropping its armor to gain accuracy and damage as it brings the fight to the enemy.

Shatterheart, the Carapace Brute’s passive ability, causes to the brute to lose armor equal to the amount of damage that it takes whenever it is hit. In return this Titan is granted a boost in accuracy equal to the amount of armor lost for one round. Carapace Brute then Trashes his enemies to a pulp. Thrash will reduce its cooldown by 2 every time it hits. This allows the brute to use Thrash repeatedly, as long as it continues to hit.

Space, 2099.  Interstellar, black market commerce is commonplace. In Sector Beta-3, the search is on for minerals among the asteroids (endearingly called 'roids by you and your fellow miners). Your small mining craft will move swiftly through sixteen sub-sectors and collect six types of minerals, as efficiently as possible. The planets in the sector have different demands for minerals, so you must plan your movements and schedule accordingly. Even in this day and age fuel is an issue, and you'll have to call in a supply ship and pay with one of your completed contracts or convert uranium ore into fuel. Other minerals can be used for other purposes as well, although it's usually better to deliver them for profit… and don’t forget to watch out for the Space Police!

'Roid Miner is a light game with minimal rules which can be played seriously or as a beer-and-pretzels game. How is this possible, you ask skeptically? Because the game's design and mechanics lend themselves to "planning/looking-ahead/what-if-he-does-that" play  or "instinctive" play--or anything in-between. In other words, you can play 'Roid Miner in tournament mode or just for fun. Due to the random setup, every game will different. Sometimes you and your opponent will easily spot nice "chains" of pickup and delivery, and your scores will be high. In other games, both of you will struggle to make it into double digits. Sometimes you'll keep to yourselves and sometimes you'll "have at" each other. The best part is that no matter how the game unfolds, 'Roid Miner will always take only about a half-hour to play--and I'm betting you'll set it up for a second game every time.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Zikia Elite – Iron Earthfall
description:
The Iron Earthfall is terror in close range. Using the two giants boulders it carries, this Titan uses its Smash ability to decimate enemy armor. Rock Smash ignores enemy armor, and deals double the enemy's armor value to them in damage.

The Iron Earthfall can launch one its boulders across the battlefield in a devastating bombardment. Bombardment is a powerful area of effect attack when the Golem tosses one of its boulders, however, it will reduce its damage by five for next two turns until it can pick up another boulder. While it searching for a new boulder however the Iron Earthfall will gain an additional movement point.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Gudanna Dominion – Charred Reavers
description:
Led by the ruthless Princess Rataya, the Charred Reavers fight zealously on behalf of Princess Nandanna. The Charred Reavers are a highly mobile attack unit that capitalize on their speed to devastating effect. Constantly on the move, they roam the battlefield for prey.

All Charred Reavers share a unique Passive Ability that grants them an additional 20 accuracy if they have moved during that turn, and all four Blood Knights share the same Passive that allows them to ignore Breakaway Costs when leaving a Region containing an enemy Golem.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Gudanna Dominion – Valkali Colossus Expansion
description: The Great Khan is dead, plunging the world of Eretsu into total war as the Khan's heirs square off against outsiders and each other for control of his shattered Dominion. The combatants' most fearsome weapons are Golems, magically animated behemoths created and piloted by powerful sorcerers. Now it's your turn to take control of a Golem army to fight for the honor of your liege!

This game is about a competition between witches and or warlocks in the quest to find the greatest potion maker amongst them.

Players take turns drawing ingredients from the bag to use with ingredients already in the cauldron to fulfill recipes of all kinds. The board changes a lot during the turn instead of discarding unwanted ingredients back to the bag they are discarded to the cauldron. This burys ingredients that your opponent may need or burys ingredient(s) you are trying to make sure is safe for you to dig up later with a special Metamorph. Maybe you want to try and set up ingredients for a more complex potion hoping that it will still be there when it comes back around to you. Some of the potions allow it's maker to affect actions during the game.

The first witch or warlock to complete the required number of potions ends the game. Each person tallies up the points of the completed potions and subtracts the points of the uncompleted recipes in their hand and whomever has the highest score can claim victory as the world's greatest potion maker.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Zikia – Spirits of the Wildwood Expansion
description: The Great Khan is dead, plunging the world of Eretsu into total war as the Khan's heirs square off against outsiders and each other for control of his shattered Dominion. The combatants' most fearsome weapons are Golems, magically animated behemoths created and piloted by powerful sorcerers. Now it's your turn to take control of a Golem army to fight for the honor of your liege!

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Durani Empire – Scions of Honor Expansion
description:
The Durani are the old power of the world, and their empire has spanned a thousand years unbroken.

Arcanum Durani animates Golems carved from stone and forged from precious metal by tapping the Mana trapped within huge gems found only in the Durani homeland. Atop these glittering behemoths, the Durani war masters of old spread out, conquered, and solidified the borders of their Empire. They built roads, towers, and castles to fortify their captured territories.

New digital game pieces available in the Golem Arcana app can only be used with these Durani Golems, incenting players to purchase them
New Relic Cards that can be carried into battle by any Golem, giving players new tactical options

Up to six players try to find the swimming airport F.P.1 in the middle of the Atlantic, while one player moves the F.P.1 in secret on a second game board out of sight from the other players. The player who gets his plane on the same space as the F.P.1 wins the game. The player who moves the F.P.1 wins the game when he reaches one of two safe spaces (marked with an arrow).

This game is based on the 1932 UFA Motion Picture "F.P.1 antwortet nicht" with Hans Albers, Peter Lorre, Eric Ode, Sybille Schmitz, Paul Hartmann and Rudolf Platte and the song "Flieger, grüß' mir die Sonne"

Five identical sealed packs of holiday-themed Munchkin cards and five Munchkin-themed gift tags. Each pack contains one card providing a very brief explanation of the game of Munchkin, two Munchkin Door cards, and two Munchkin Treasure cards.

Packs are intended to be used as holiday stocking stuffers, hence the title.

Asterians are a mysterious elf-like race of aliens who rely heavily on androids and AIs to complete their mysterious missions within Deadzones.

This starter faction includes the following models, cards and bases:

54-card Faction Deck
1 Asterian Commander
3 Cyphers with Noh Rifles
3 Cyphers with Charge Gloves
1 Cypher with Fission Beamer
1 Black Talon
2 Weapons Drones with multiple weapon options
10 25mm bases
2 flying bases
54-card Faction Deck

The Forge Fathers are short, squat humanoid aliens driven by a lust for profit and glory.  This starter set provides you with a FF strike force to seek their fortunes in the Plague-ridden Deadzones across the galaxy.

This faction starter includes:
54-card Faction Deck
1 Huscarl in Forge Guard Armour
5 Forge Guard with weapon options 
3 Brokkrs 
1 Brokkr with Magma Rifle 
1 Brokkr Engineer
1 Inferno Drill
11 25mm bases

boardgame: Batman: The Animated Series – Gigantik Crime Wave Game
description:
It's batman's greatest challenge yet! Help Batman match wits and do battle against a sinister evil that's plotting to take over Gotham City by storm!

Police Commissioner Gordon has turned on the Bat Signal. Its your mission to save Gotham City from the likes of the ever purrsistant Catwoman, the quacky antics of sewer dwelling The Penguin and their accomplices.

The Batman the Animated Series Gigantik game is an explosive and action-packed game. Race Batmobile play pieces through booby-trapped streets using the Batmobile shield as protection. YOU MUST seek out and capture 4 villains to win! Good luck! It's your Gigantk task to foil the purr-fect crime and to save Gotham City from certain DOOM!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: TV Shows: Batman – The Animated Series

description:
The world is not as it once was.  After years of tension between the mega corporations and corrupt bureaucrats, civilization is made up of vast independent city-states.  You are the leader of one of Empyrion’s ruling classes, a Scion of the state.  The current Grand Scion, and his ruling faction, have grown fat and ignorant in their affluence.  This indulgence is weakness, an opportunity a shrewd Scion would be able to turn into advantage. You are not the only one to have seen it; the other castes have noticed, so make certain you are swift to act. Through cunning and intrigue, and perhaps even diplomacy, navigate the unforgiving political realities of this new world to position your faction as the next ruling class.  Only one may be the Grand Scion of Empyrion; make sure it is you.

Empyrion is a deck-building resource gathering card game where you compete against 2-5 rival Scions by gathering or stealing resources and shoring up your faction by recruiting specialized citizens to work for you or building unique buildings which confer long lasting and expansive powers.  Focus on building, or blocking your opponents building as you vie to gain enough influence points to claim the title of Grand Scion and ruler over all of Empyrion!

Vasa Regalis is a card game version of Vasa Regalis: The Maiden Voyage.

Players are shipyard leaders responsible for the building of the Vasa Ship. At the same time, you want to keep as much material as possible to yourself but beware, if the ship sinks you may get the blame.

The victory conditions are determined by the sum of all players' actions. If players add a lot to the ship, it will succeed but it is the player placing LEAST on the ship that will win. If, on the other hand, players add little to the ship, it will fail and the player placing MOST on the ship will win.

The players start with cards representing goods at different values. Each turn, they may choose to place cards on the ship, inspect cards placed on the ship or replace own cards placed on the ship. When all four parts of the ships have been completed, the game ends and the ship is launched.
  
The challenge of Vasa Regalis is to correctly guess how much the other players add to the ship.

The unique mechanism of Vasa Regalis is the ambiguous victory conditions. The players can analyze the piles and draw conclusions but never be sure how to win. Should they add as much to a part as possible or as little as possible? They will not know the answer until the ship is launched but then it is too late to do anything about it.

You play a Lord of a Season, bringing seasonal changes to the world. As time goes by, one season replaces another in seasonal order; Spring is followed by Summer, Summer by Fall and Fall by Winter. However, you want to turn the time your way to have your season dominating the world.

When all your season tiles have been played to the game board or you have a majority of season tiles in one area, your season dominates the world.

The players take turn to play season tiles to one of six hexagonal areas, each of which has six season slots. The six season slots are arranged in a circle where each slot is adjacent to two other slots. The six areas are also arranged in a circle where each area is adjacent to two other areas.

Within an area, season tiles must be placed in seasonal order. Between areas, season tiles must be similar. If they are not, the later season will replace the earlier (e.g. Spring will replace Winter).

Besides placing season tiles, a player may turn one area clockwise so that new seasons end up adjacent to each other, resulting in one replacing the other. To really twist the time, a player may turn all areas counter-clockwise, resulting in several seasons replacing each other. However, since this turn is twisted, earlier seasons will replace later (e.g. Winter will replace Spring).

Once a season is banished from an area, it may not easily return, so the players will gradually increase their local dominance until a global winner emerges. To win Turn of Time, you must find the turns that brings most of your seasons to the board while blocking your opponents' opportunities to replace your seasons.

The unique mechanism of Turn of Time is the so called circular relation between the players where A is stronger than B, B is stronger than C etc. This mechanism, in combination with the revolving game areas, opens up in-numerous strategical paths. Should you accept being banished from one area to expand in another? Should you place your seasons so that they can attack easily or so that they are difficult to attack? Will the benefits of turning one or several areas outweigh the benefits other players may receive? Will you even be able to foresee all turns in the game if all areas are turned? You must find the answer yourself!

In Iconoclasm, you strive to form and control units by playing your element tokens to it. Once a unit is formed, an icon of the strongest element is placed in it. Once a unit is connected to unit of a different element, the two units clash.

When all elements have been played, the player with the most units wins.

The players take turns to play element tokens to a hexagonal board. Although the players represent one element each, they start with tokens of all elements at hand. Units are formed when 7 tokens are grouped and units are connected when there is a path of tokens between them.
 
Each element has a supporting relation to another element; one that it supports and one that it is supported by. This relation is used to break ties between the elements. The tokens of the weaker element are removed from the board. However, the tokens of the stronger element are weakened and this may result in the supporting element taking over the unit.
  
The challenge of Iconoclasm is to play elements, own and supporting, to obtain a majority at the right time and at the right place.

The unique mechanism of Iconoclasm is the so called circular relation between the players where A supports B, B supports C etc. This mechanism, in combination with the players playing each others' colors, opens up innumerous strategical paths. Should you build a strong unit of your own element? Should you support another element only to take over its unit? Or should you form other units, see them deformed and among the ruins form your own unit? You must find the answer yourself!

Mars and Venus collide in the ultimate showdown! The battle of the sexes has been raging since the dawn of time - now it's finally time to settle the dispute and find out whether the boys or girls will reign supreme.

Players split into two teams: as one team tries to give answers to a category, the other team has to guess which answers they won't get.

Based on the hit TV quiz show. Teams compete head to head to see who can provide the most correct answers and win the points on offer. Take your team to the high-scoring final round and battle it out to be crowned the Eggheads in this exciting trivia shoot-out!
Outwit and outrun the Chaser and your opponents in this race to win the most cash. Gamble or play it safe, just don’t get caught! Packed with over 120 questions

Showdown! is a a 76-page book designed to complement the Agents of SMERSH base game, and the Swagman's Hope and Space Station expansions.

The Showdown! Scenario Book presents a linear history that will take the players through a series of eight Agents of SMERSH games, which get progressively more difficult as the UN Secret Service pursues Dr Lobo.

Showdown! also introduces a new Endgame for Agents of SMERSH, which can be played after any Main Game (i.e. the board game), even if you are not using the linear scenarios.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
scheiße is a card game, played with a standard 32-card French deck.

Starting the first round with one card, the second with to cards and so on up to 7 cards each player. Then it goes down to one card again.
After examining your cards you state the number of tricks you will win. The number of statet tricks is not allowed to be equal to the number of cards dealt to each player. So at least one player has to be wrong.

Each round each player puts down one card.
Colour order is Clubs -> Spades -> Hearts -> Diamonds. If you have the starting colour of a round, you have to play it. Otherwise you're free to play whichever colour you want.

In turns with just one card per player, you have to place your card, such that everybody else can see it, and state if you make the trick, according to the other cards you see.

You will recieve 10 points if you statet your number of tricks correctly and 2 additionaly points for each trick, otherwise you will loose 2 points.

Incursion is a campaign expansion book for Deadzone that introduces two new alien species to the conflict and also outlines rules for multiplayer games to expand beyond the two-player core rules.

From the publisher:

The conflict on Nexus Psi escalates with the arrival of the dour Forge Fathers and the mysterious Asterians! Pursuing unknown agendas of their own, these two factions bring their own respective brands of urban warfare to the Deadzone! Also includes rules for multiplayer games to reflect the increased conflict

Krosmaster: Arena – Dark Heroes, part of season 3 of Krosmaster, gathers the darkest figures of the DOFUS and WAKFU universes: Vampyro, Dark Vlad, Kriss la Krass, Djaul, Dark Crow, and Katar — six true and powerful heroes who are also bad guys, yes, but loved by the community.

Contagion is an expansion campaign for Deadzone.  It includes both a 32 page rulebook; Zombie Rules; AI Rules; AI Card Deck; 30 plastic miniature Zombies (require assembly/painting); and
4 sprues of Ruined Battlezones scenery to expand the terrain of your Deadzone games.

From the publisher:

Nexus Psi has been overrun by the Plague, and it’s everyone for themselves as the living dead wander the streets! Every faction must react to this new threat, fighting to free themselves before they are overrun. This supplement also introduces rules for solo play, enabling you to polish your tactics when no opponents are available!

boardgame: Saint Petersburg (Second Edition): New Society Completition Pack
description:
Was available as an add-on from Spieleschmiede crowd funding Saint Petersburg (second edition)

Includes the rest of the cards of the old "New Society"-Expansion, that are not included in "The Even Newer Society" stretch goal, made with the new artwork

Election is a game of campaign strategy for the whole family. Plan your campaign, win votes and battle for victory in each State. Win enough Electoral College votes to become the next President of the United States. Careful campaign planning and management of campaign funds is required to defeat your opponent. Conduct polls to discover which candidate each state favors. Be sure to read the Daily News card and watch out for the "Dark Horse" candidate that can steal your Volunteers.

To play Election, players first decide on how many “days” there are to the election, with each round of play being one turn.  On each turn players can either Fundraise or Campaign. When campaigning, players can spend money to recruit volunteers, hire consultants, buy ads, travel, or conduct a poll.  Volunteers, consultants and ads can be traded in for Votes, which are then placed on individual states.  Each state also has a “poll”, which determines which party the state favors (e.g. Republicans +3). Players can pay to conduct polls to see the polling information. On Election Day, the outcome in each state is determined by the votes placed on the state plus the polling. The winner of each state is awarded all of the Electoral College Votes for that state. The winner of the election is the player with the most Electoral College Votes.

Election is fun for the whole family and will have your children jumping up and down thrilled to have just become the next President of the United States.

Do you have what it takes to build an airline empire? Find out with AEROSTAR.

This fun filled game will keep you coming back for more. AEROSTAR is an easy to learn yet dynamic game. Do you have what it takes to hire, invest, and build the best airline? If so you will be crowned the next AEROSTAR. Let the bidding begin!!!

Players start a round of play by bidding to hire an executive for their airline. All Executives are not made equal so choose accordingly. Then each player has an opportunity to invest in airline tech stocks, fuel stocks, or several other money making opportunities. This segment is also bidding based so budget wisely or your opponents might outbid you and steal your investment opportunity.

Next Players buy their Airplane Fleets. This segment is Auction/Bidding based also. There are 4 airline tech companies providing 30 different fleet types, all with different money making abilities and fuel efficiencies. After this, Players must buy fleet fuel from the market in order for their fleets to operate. This market price is constantly changing so be careful when building your airline fleet or you might lose money.

Players then have the opportunity to buy Terminals in Major and Minor Cities in a start to creating their Airline Empire. By creating connections players earn money if their fleet can fulfill the connections and have the fuel to do so. Each round ends with connection and investment profits being paid.

A yearly market card is drawn that effects the next rounds game play. In AEROSTAR there are market fuel increases, Union Strikes, and many more global impacts. You never know what might happen in the adventure of becoming the next AEROSTAR.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Jade Guardian Elite Set
description: The Jade Guardian Elite Set introduces the Jade Thornbeast, Jade Phalanx, Jade Harpy, and Jade Dervish. Each figure also includes an exclusive Knight.

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Rangers of the Oath Elite Set
description: The Rangers of the Oath Elite Set introduces the Sworn Creeper, Sworn Woodguard, Sworn Collector, and Sworn Woodguard. Each figures includes an exclusive Knight.

This is a two-player game about building a farming village in rural Vietnam. The board comes in two double-sided halves that can be fit together to form four different configurations. The assembled board displays a 6x6 grid, with each row and column marked by the face of a die. Six of the squares on the board (those with identical row and column numbers) are action spaces.

Players purchase tiles that represent resources, such as food or animals, or buildings. These tiles are then placed on the game grid. On each turn, the active player takes one die chosen from among the dice rolled by the opponent, then rolls three remaining dice and may select one of those three. The player then resolves any actions or income produced from tiles on the board where the rows and columns corresponding to the two dice intersect. Any income may be used to purchase more tiles to be placed on the board.

The players are striving to reach five specific goals. The first person to reach three more goals than the opponent (3 vs. 0 or 4 vs. 1) is the winner. If no one leads by three, the game ends when all 30 non-action squares on the game grid are filled, and the player who has obtained the most goals at that point is the winner.

Air Traffic Madness is a game for 2 to 5 people where players compete to land as many planes as possible. Each plane will score a variable number of victory points plus bonuses depending on how challenging a runway you land it on. Each player secretly chooses three orders cards to issue to their planes, two direct orders and one general order. Then players take turns revealing their orders and moving their planes. The player who lands the planes that are worth the most VPs wins. Beware, there are events that occur every other round plus bonus cards that players can earn which will change the course of the game. Each player also has a few planes worth zero VPs which could be damaging to you if they crash into your high valued planes. Can you navigated the hectic air space and be crowned the king of the air traffic control?  Are you ready for Air Traffic Madness?

オカシノビ is a game modeling the epic struggle between the ninjas of the Mushroom and Bamboo Shoot factions.  It employs hidden team affiliations, asymmetric player powers and inter-player combat using a rock-paper-scissors resolution.  In addition to the Mushrooms and Bamboo Shoots, each game also incorporates a set of neutral factions: so the agendas of each player are never clear.

Players manage a hand of six cards: all different; each with a faction symbol (Mushroom, Bamboo Shoot or Neutral) in addition to the image of a rock, paper or scissors hand gesture. In a player's turn she may:

    Pass a card from hand secretly to another player to inquire as to their faction loyalties.  For the most part, a player responds "yes" if their affiliation matches the card, and "no" otherwise.  Some player affiliations require a false response to any query!
    Attack another player.  Both players select a card from hand, then reveal together.  If tied, another card is played.  Cards played are unavailable for re-use until all six cards have been played: at that point all are reclaimed.
    Reclaim one played card to her hand.


The various player powers affect the actions:  some are strong one-shot powers; others more modest powers that always apply.

The game continues until one faction has achieved their victory.
publishers: Alhara Systems (アルハラシステムズ)

description:
They’re brave, adventurous & ready to explore!
Step out of the dungeon and into the light of the endless wilderness! Face deadly new monsters, defeat insidious new traps and discover incredible new treasures! This expansion set for Wallet Warriors gives you new encounter charts, new scenarios and a new challenge: random Events! Plus, with 24 new map cards to explore, the adventure can go on forever. More markers, figures and dice make the set a must have for die-hard adventurers! So get ready to brave the mountains, swamps, oceans and forests of the Wallet Warriors Wilderness!

With a simple roll of the die your adventurers (such as thief, druid or elf) move through the randomly created wilderness and discover either a monster, trap or treasure! If your level and roll is high enough, or if your abilities are good enough, you can beat the adversaries and continue on to succeed in your quest of finding gold, rescuing the princess or just exploring the entire map!

Odysseus took an epic voyage from Troy to Ithaca. You play as the Fates, who observe Odysseus on his voyage... Not entirely interested in whether he makes it home safe or not, you will bet on the outcome of Odysseus' journey and each of his adventures, and then use your influence with the gods to make sure your bets pay off.

boardgame: ATS Core Module: Bulge – The Battle of the Bulge at Hotton/Melines/Quatre-Bras Crossroads
description:
Small-unit tactical treatment of the battles in and around Hotton, Melines and Quatre Bras during The Battle of the Bulge, using the Advanced Tobruk System rules.
These battles can be re-enacted in twenty all-new scenarios for Advanced Tobruk System, partnered up with four all-new cardstock maps with winter terrain, AFV cards and the usual Battlefield Walkaround with historical notes and all the special rules for this module.  Additionally, 2½ sheets of combat unit counters are included, representing the German and American soldiers and weapons in play during this crucial struggle.

Requires ATS Rules, playaids, and standard ATS markers, sold separately.

Flags of Valour is a toolkit system for war game enhancement.  This particular release is Set 2, and deals with the Japanese and American forces of the Pacific.

FOV is a set of modules that extend the use of any counter based war game so that you can add both the often under-utilized practice of “Fog of War” OR that of higher level Intelligence Gathering and Logistics to virtually any simulation game.  The game tool is also used to represent hidden assets such as Ammunition Supply, Fuel, and other factors common to any higher level logistic advantages gained via logistical planning.  It is in fact this "Level Over" that FOV uses to bring new strategies and tactics to most war games without changing the underlying game rules. The level of  added realism this tool set provides is rarely found in conjunction with any other current game publication.

What the Flags of Valour systems all do is give players of wargames a way to remove most of your counters from the board at the start of any game, and replace them with national hidden placement counters.  Meantime you have a hidden area where normal game counters are kept on LUC (Logistical Unit Cards) until enemy recon or proximity force the regular unit pieces to be placed on the board and revealed. This allows war games to start with a form of hidden movement and place that is traditionally thought of as “double blind” play. Except in this case you can do the double-blind with only one central board (unlike the awkward and messy way that most double blind games take up a lot of room and require two copies of a board or a game).

Depending on the particular FOV release, some provide for additional hidden rules such as Strongpoints, Air Strikes, Guerrilla activity, Supply Lines, and more. Add on rules are completely optional, and are left to the discretionary use of the players using the system.

Each FOV release comes with 2-6 nation state counters.  There are releases for many different eras available, but primarily the counter sets cover WW2 and Modern use.  There are also the “125” series of FOV releases for use with Miniatures games. Those use larger placement counters and much larger LUC (Logistical Unit Cards) on which to place miniatures hidden off the board.

Flags of Valour is a toolkit system for war game enhancement.  This particular release is Set 3, and deals with counters for the units of the Blitzkrieg years - namely France, Belgium, Netherlands, Poland and Norway.

FOV is a set of modules that extend the use of any counter based war game so that you can add both the often under-utilized practice of “Fog of War” OR that of higher level Intelligence Gathering and Logistics to virtually any simulation game.  The game tool is also is used to represent hidden assets such as Ammunition Supply, Fuel, and other factors common to any Operational level command of your troops on a typical battlefield.  It is in fact this Operational over-level that FOV uses to bring new strategies and tactics to most war games. The level of  added realism this tool set provides is rarely found in conjunction with any other current game publication.

What the Flags of Valour systems all do is give players of wargames a way to remove most of your counters from the board at the start of any game, and replace them with national hidden placement counters.  Meantime you have a hidden area where normal game counters are kept on LUC (Logistical Unit Cards) until enemy recon or proximity force the regular unit pieces to be placed on the board and revealed.  This allows war games to start with a form of hidden movement and place that is traditionally thought of as “double blind” play.  Except in this case you can do the double-blind with only one central board (unlike the awkward and messy way that most double blind games take up a lot of room and require two copies of a board or a game).

Depending on the particular FOV release, some provide for additional hidden rules such as Strongpoints, Air Strikes, Guerilla activity, Supply Lines, and more. Add on rules are completely optional, and are left to the discretionary use of the players using the system.

Each FOV release comes with 2-6 nation state counters.  There are releases for many different eras available, but primarily the counter sets cover WW2 and Modern use.  There are also the “125” series of FOV releases for use with Miniatures games. Those use larger placement counters and much larger LUC (Logistical Unit Cards) on which to place miniatures hidden off the board.

A simple roll-and-move game from the British magazine Horrible Histories #23.

The board consists of a track with 33 spaces. On your turn you roll one six-sided die and move your pawn that many spaces. If you land on an event space, you simply follow the instructions.

The first player to reach the finish wins.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
gadZOOks! is a whimsical card game about managing a zoo and breeding adorable baby animals. Players must wisely use their limited resources to bid on new animals and outmaneuver their opponents. Each animal you add to your zoo increases the number of visitors your zoo attracts, and ultimately the most popular zoo wins.

Play is round based. Each round, players draw cards which might include money to bid on auctions or tricks to use against their opponents. New animals and powerful effects are then revealed and placed up for auction. Players get a chance to place blind bids on auctions that interest them and use their tricks to tip odds in their favor. Once everyone has played, the bids are revealed. It is important to bid wisely because losing bids are lost.

Acquiring pairs of animals allows them to mate, produce babies and increase your visitor count for FREE. However, savvy zookeepers always have extra cash on hand to protect their investments from the dangerous cards that come up for auction.

gadZooks! is fast and easy to learn which makes a great game for kids or non-gamers. However, there is enough nuance and strategy to keep more discerning gamers interested.

In COSPLAY: The Game, two to four players compete against each other to try to become a Cosplay Superstar. Using a variety of cards and events, players gather flair for their costumes, then compete in tournaments to advance their rank. The first player to achieve "Superstar" status wins the game.

A game built from the ground up by cosplayers for cosplayers, you'll recognize many in-game items inspired by and making fun of tropes from your favorite cosplay fandoms. Each player chooses a fandom (video games, anime & TV, steampunk or super heroes) and then works to gather flair for their cosplay before the competition. Flair items range from the ordinary (a "dinosaur egg" or a "steampunk gauntlet") to the outlandish (like the "companion sphere" or the "ridiculous oversized hat").

Easy to learn and play and incredibly portable, it's a perfect game to play when you're stuck in a hotel, at the airport or otherwise looking to have some fun. It's a clever take on the cosplay/convention experience, in playing card form.

Played similarly to Monopoly, players use dice to move around a board and buy industries. The goal of the game is to have the best reputation for philanthropy and social justice.

As in Monopoly, any player landing on an industry space owned by another player must pay the owner. However, a player may become "the best in their industry" by giving up their move and paying 2,000 in currency to purchase a ring. When a player lands on an industry space with a ring, the owner of that industry must pay the active player. This represents the social responsibility of being the best in an industry.

To win the game, a player must have four rings on four different industries he owns. He also must land exactly on the Charity Shop space and pay 2,000 in currency.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Monegassen Ramsch contains one Dimension Card and 32 play cards (Ramsche). At the start of the game every player has seven cards on his hand and an empty discard pile in front of him. The goal is to get rid of your cards by placing them into the other player's discard pile. You get points for every card that's in the other players's discard pile.

The main mechanic is similar to Ramschrounds in Skat plus four special effects cards. As you play you can always choose in which discard pile you want to play your card. Sometimes you want to play them in your own, because of these effects like "mirror the discard piles" etc.

boardgame: Disney Fairies: Fly and Go Seek Game
description:
Disney Fairies: Fly and Go Seek Game is a princess themed memory game.

Object: to fly around the house collecting Treasure Triangles.  At the end of the game, the player with the most Treasure Triangles is the winner!

Set Up
1) Decide on four locations around your house to place Treasure Triangles (for example, the kitchen, bedroom, dining room, entry way). These are the places you'll "fly" to!
2) In each location, place six Treasure Triangles as shown. (Wedges meet to form a hexagon).  It does not matter which sides are face-up.
3) Everyone  sits in a central location. 
4) Put all the square Cards inside the bag.
5) Have the sand timer handy.

The player with the next birthday goes first!

On Your Turn:
Reach in the bag and, without looking, pull out a Card.  Follow the instructions below based on what kind of Card you pulled:

     Picture Card


If you pulled a Picture Card, look at the items on both sides of the Card.  Choose one of the sides to place face-up in the center of all the players.  Now it's the next player's turn.

     Moon Card


If you pulled a Moon Card, place it off to the side to begin creating a picture of a full moon.  If the fourth Moon Card is pulled and the picture of the full moon is complete, the game is over (see Ending the Game section below).  Otherwise it's the next player's turn.

     "Fly and Go Seek" Card


If you pulled a "Fly and Go Seek" Card, follow the directions below:
1) Take a look at ALL the face-up Picture Cards.  Try to remember the items on them, but leave them where they are.  (Note: if there are no face-up Picture Cards then your turn is over because there are no items to seek, and now it's the next players' turn.)
2) Flip the sand timer over and go!
3) Hurry to the Treasure Triangles around your house.  If you see items from face-up Picture Cards on any Treasure Triangles take those Treasure Triangles, take those Treasure Triangles with you.
4) When the sand timer runs out, another player should let you know. Immediately come back with any Treasure Triangles you have collected.
5) Compare the Treasure Triangles you have collected to the face-up Picture Cards. Place each treasure Triangle you have collected that matches a Picture Card in your own scoring pile. Set the matching Picture Cards out of the game.
6) If you collected a Treasure Trinagle that does not have a matching face-up Picture Card, put the Treasure Triangle back and your next turn is skipped. Now it's the next player's turn.

Ending the Game
When the fourth Moon Card is pulled from thebag and the picture of the full moon is complete, the game is over.  Players count up the Treasure Triangles they have collected. The player with the most Treasure Triangles wins!

boardgame: The Lion King: Simba's Pride – Pride Rock Pairs
description:
Help pair up each of the animal types and Simba and Nala's daughter, Kiara, with Kovu the male cub she has fallen in love with.

The game is a variant on a memory match game, but the pairs are a male and female of a species and the male and female tiles are different shapes, that form a heart shape when matched.

The game also comes with a display area that is set up to the side of the play area where all the matched pairs are placed.

boardgame: Jinrou: Usotsuki Wa Dare Da? Card Battle
description:
This game is based on a Japanese comedy game series 人狼〜嘘つきは誰だ?〜. 
There are 17 types of roles.
And if you are the werewolf you would have to get rid of the humans until you have the same number of werewolves as the humans do. And humans are to get rid of the werewolves.
However, there is a third force, the fox. The fox wins if he lives on till the end of the game.

The 17 types are namely:
Staple Characters:
Villager 市民 - No Effects
Werewolf 人狼 - Kill someone during the night (werewolf will know who the other werewolf is)
Knight 騎士 - Protect one person from attack by werewolf except himself in the night
Fortune Teller 占い師 - During the night you can see who is a werewolf, a villager or fox (however, you are unable to see their profession, or if the person was cursed)
Betrayer 裏切り者 - Part werewolf team and part villager team, can sway both ways.

Additional Characters:
The Twins 双子 - Can know who your twin is and confirm that he/she is a villager
The Medium 霊媒師 - Able to see the person who was voted off in the morning trial if the person was a werewolf or not
Apprentice Knight なりきり騎士 - similar power as the knight, but you die in the process
The Infected 感染者 - becomes a werewolf when attacked
Pied Piper Pierot 道連れピエロ - able to select someone else to die with him when selected by the werewolf as an attack target or when voted off
The Cursed 呪われし者 - a villager but when seen by the fortune teller or medium, he becomes the bait for the night
The Weak 病弱 - Become the designated person to die from the werewolf attack on the night = number of werewolves+1 from the start of the game 
The calm senate 冷徹な議長 - able to void other peoples votes and count only his in 1 of the trials. Once per game
Thick Haired Laborer 毛深き労働者 - Shows up as a werewolf, but actually is a villager
Sturdy Sailor 屈強な船乗り - when attacked by the werewolf, he dies on the second night
Disciple of the Fortune Teller 占い師の弟子 - Becomes a fortune teller when the fortune teller dies
Assassin 暗殺者- select a person to assassinate in the night, the effects of knight or apprentice knight is voided
Demonic Magic User 妖術師 - Able to fortune tell someone and show it to the werewolves. However, he becomes the attacked person in the night. Villager Team
Targeted Villager 狙われる市民 - Start out as a villager team, but turns to a werewolf when attacked
Werewolf Child 人狼の子 - Startout as a villager but after =number of werewolves at the start of the game + 1 night, turns into a werewolf
Fox Demon 狐憑き - your goal is to just survive and you win
Witch Doctor闇医者

Originally from Hawaii, her knowledge of survival and hunting were taught to her from a young age by her father. Once a demolitions expert in the military, she worked with mercenaries all across the country, until she found herself outside the Golden City...

Cora is the 6th character of Raid & Trade and in this expansion, you will discover a new ability. The ability of detonation!

Challenge the best ski jumpers in the world! See for yourself that you can become one of the sports champions! “ZigZak: Ski Jumps” is a party card game, in which players become ski jumpers trying to fly as far as possible and take the longest and the best jump.

In ZigZak: Skoki narciarskie (ZigZag: Ski Jumps) game all the players perform their jumps in the real time simultaneously. In order to win, the players have to create the line of their jumps using cards from the common pool by putting together their Start and Finish cards. The speed, the style and the length of the jump are all important. The players have to prove their reflex and accuracy while selecting the cards.

THICKET is a two-player abstract strategy game played on a 6x6 grid of dots. Players take turns drawing orthogonal lines between adjacent dots to enclose areas. When a player completes an enclosure, they claim that area and take another turn. No dot can have more than three lines connected to it, and lines cannot be drawn inside claimed areas. The game ends when no more areas can be claimed. The player who has enclosed the most square spaces wins. THICKET combines elements of territory control and tactical line placement, with the three-connection limit adding a unique strategic element to the gameplay.

Components
- A printed 6x6 grid of dots
- Two pencils (different colors recommended)

Setup
No setup required beyond having the grid and pencils ready.

Gameplay

Players take turns. On your turn:

1. Draw a Line:
   - Connect two adjacent dots with an orthogonal line segment.

2. Claim Areas (if applicable):
   - If your line completes a chain that encloses an area of empty squares:
     a. Claim that area by writing your initials inside it.
     b. Take another turn immediately.

3. Observe Restrictions:
   - You cannot draw lines inside claimed areas.
   - No dot may have more than three line segments connected to it.

Game End
The game ends when all possible areas have been claimed.

Scoring and Winning
- Count the number of square spaces within your claimed areas.
- The player with the most square spaces wins.

boardgame: Age of Craft Expansion #1: Blueprints and Visitors
description:
This expansion for Age of Craft introduces two new sets of cards, Blueprints and Visitors, along with new rules for their use. It also includes two new random cards that can be used with the base game rules.

Blueprints begin in a face down stack. Each player is dealt two random Blueprints at the beginning of the game and can choose to purchase more during the building phase. Blueprints can either be events, which are discarded after use, or they can be buildings that are constructed in the same manner as the buildings on offer in the common supply. There is no limit to the number of Bluprints that a player may purchase.

Visitor cards are goals that offer points to the player who meets their requirements. Two Visitor cards are drawn randomly at the beginning of the game. Once a player has obtained a Visitor card, an opponent may take it by surpassing the requirements met by the owner of the card.
publishers: Chicken Dice Games (チキンダイスゲームズ)

description: This expansion for the game Mura Korogashi includes 11 new types of tiles that allow players to build a village in France.
This expansion for the game Mura Korogashi includes 11 new types of tiles that allow players to build a village in Spain.

Go with the Floe is a two-player abstract chase game. The seal player wants to isolate the seals beyond the reach of the pursuing polar bears. The polar bear player wins if both seals are caught, otherwise the seal player wins.

Components
    Modified 8 x 8 board with 52 ice spaces
    2 black seal tokens and 2 white polar bear tokens.
    50 hole-in-the-ice ring markers

Game Play
Starting with the Black player, players take turns moving one of their tokens. A player may pass a turn only if he or she has no legal move.
Either player tokens can:
    1) Move one or two empty spaces in any one direction, like a range-restricted chess queen, or
    2) Move by jumping over one ring marker in any direction into an empty space, as in checkers but without capturing or multiple jumps.

Captures
Only White may capture an opponent’s token and this can only be done from an adjacent space, like a chess king. On capturing both the white and black tokens are removed from play leaving the capture space empty.

After moving or capturing, the moving player places a hole-in-the-ice ring marker on the moving token’s starting space and any empty space passed over.

Game End
The game ends when one of the following occurs:
    * The White player captures both Black tokens. White wins.
    * The Black player has one token isolated and which cannot be captured. Black wins.
    * Both players pass on consecutive turns. Black wins.

Similar to the Anno Domini family of games, Anno Bosparans Fall consists of 336 cards, with a description of a historical event on one side of the card, and the year (and sometimes specific date) in which it happened on the other.

Each player receives nine of these cards, and may only look at the descriptions. Each player in turn puts a card on the table, trying to put them in chronological order. A player may, instead of adding a card, claim that the order in which the cards were put is incorrect. In this case all cards are turned over and the correct years revealed.

If the order was correct then the doubting player receives two cards and skips a turn. If the order was incorrect then the previous player, who accepted the order as correct, receives three cards. The first player that gets rid of all the cards wins. Bluffing is as least as important as knowledge in this game.

However, all events are based upon the fictional roleplaying world of The Dark Eye (Das Schwarze Auge).
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Hunt it.  Find it.  Slay it.

In the small towns and lonely highways of America, monsters lurk in the shadows, stalking and terrorizing innocent victims.  You are a small team of monster hunters — average citizens that know the truth about the creatures of the night.  You are on the hunt for an unknown monster.  Can you identify the monster and track it to its lair before it claims its victims and disappears once more into the shadows?

American Monster Hunters is a cooperative card game set in a mythic rural America haunted by monsters. You and your friends collect clue cards and work together to reveal the identity of the monster, determined by random mystery cards at the start of each game. Each turn, you must decide whether to protect victims, prepare for the coming fight against the monster, or progress the hunt. Variable powers, ever-increasing risk, and fast-paced turns make for a tense, exciting, and unique game each time.

Print-and-play coming soon!

As a newly crowned medieval king with visions of grandeur, do you have the skill and cunning to build the greatest empire in the history of the world? Use espionage, investment, and your loyal armies to expand your kingdom and achieve ultimate glory in this portable, quick, and easy-to-learn war based card game -- the only card game of its kind. Get it now and travel back in time ... to the Age of War!

Age of War card game from Battle Born Games is a medieval era, war-based card game that plays like a board game. Conquering land is the name of the game, and you can do this through battle and espionage. Along the way, enhancing your empire with cities and castles will help you accomplish your goals. You only have three actions to use per turn though, so you must carefully decide how best to use those actions with the cards available to you at that time. Here's briefly how it works:

(1) The player draws cards to start the turn.
(2) The player then takes action!  This is when you play a card to the table, wage battle, and/or upgrade a city or castle.
(3) After the player completes his actions, one army card may be moved from any territory to any territory within his empire.
The cards:
(a) Territory cards are central to the game. You want land above all else, and you'll do anything to get it!
(b) Building cards can be added to any kingdom in your empire, and there are two types of building cards -- castle or city.
(c) Army cards can be added to any territory or group of territories in your empire, and they attack and defend in battle.
(d) Money cards will pay for upgrades to buildings, buying elite army units, and paying off enemy agents.
(e) Mission cards have various purposes, all designed to help you win the game.
Once you've amassed enough land to prove your dominance over every other king in the world, you win the game!

A pencil and paper dice rolling game where each player is collecting treasures and protecting those treasures with forts. Players try to destroy each others forts and steal their opponents' treasures.  You get three dice to roll and assign to your actions. You get to do one action per die, so you can't do every action every turn, you have choose which three to do.

Actions

    Hunt for new treasure
    Build bricks to improve your forts
    Attack to destroy opposing forts
    Raid forts to steal the treasure
    Fortify forts to improve brick strength and raid resistance


The first player to get four treasures, or the last player left with any treasure wins!

The Ultimate Beer Game is a board game in which players travel their pawn across the board, encountering “obstacles” in a battle to get to the finish line first. Obstacles include things like The Bridge of Doom, Potato's House, The Beach, etc. Along the way, players will be instructed to drink, pick cards, and answer slightly humorous questionnaires out loud to the other players. It is designed to be interactive, socially and physically, within the players.

This game boasts 8 pawns in a range of colors, and one blue die, all neatly packed into a drawstring canvas bag to withstand the tests of time longer than the typical heat-sealed plastic bag most games include. This means your pawns and die won’t be rolling around aimlessly inside the box once you open them. (Our pawns are made in the U.S.A. They are eco-friendly, filled with postindustrial wood trim.)

This game includes 52 Beer Cards and Bong Cards collectively, which are packed into a playing card box. Our cards are water-resistant and should clean off pretty easily if wet.

This game can be played while easily substituting alcohol with any beverage, using alcohol is an OPTIONAL part of this game.
This game is meant to be played by those of legal drinking age, and is prohibited to be played by any minor. Some tiles include curse words, obscene ideas, and some offensive language and graphics in general.

Cards for the Masses is a hilarious, thought provoking, and creative game of questions and wacky mixed up answers. Choose and call out a question, select from your hand of 3 cards, an answer you think will be the most popular.

In an age of exploration, rival nations battle for dominance. Discover new lands. Amass great wealth. Gather support. Above all, ensure imperial stability in a time of chaos.

Empire is a board game of strategy, commerce, diplomacy, and power. Two to six players play as 18th century colonial nations competing to inspire the support of their people by providing them with valuable goods. Nations produce goods by working their people on territories gained in a shifting array of ongoing wars. Goods are also sold to an open market where players set the price to finance more armies.

Players can also trade any of their resources with any other player at any time. Empire offers completely free, open and unfettered trading and diplomacy. Empire's turn-less simultaneous action selection system allows negotiation and exchange to integrate smoothly into the game flow.

Empire is fast-paced and filled with tense decisions, negotiation, and trading. The game lasts around 30 minutes, has no dice or similar randomness, and takes about 5 minutes to explain.

The battle for the craddle of civilization, 1947.
