description:
In August 1914, two Russian armies, Rennenkampf’s First and Samsonov’s Second, invaded the German province of East Prussia. The lone German army there, Hindenburg’s Eighth, had to rely on interior lines and railroads to concentrate against each Russian army in turn. This game focuses on the southern half of the campaign, with the northern half factored into the victory conditions. Those victory conditions vary with the strategic options chosen by each player so neither knows the other’s intent.

Tannenberg uses a modification of the Fire & Movement game system. Developed to portray the mechanized battlefields of a later generation, F&M stresses the use of rapidly-concentrated fire support to generate breakthroughs, followed by a rapid penetration by mobile reserves. In 1914, although railroads allowed the rapid shifting of forces on a large scale, no one moved faster than a man on foot on the battlefield, making exploitation ineffective. However, here both players have hidden movement, allowing them to concentrate in unexpected locations to surround and destroy isolated enemy units. The clumsy and inflexible fire support is represented by the artillery brigades available to division and corps commanders. It must be brought to the point of decision, then supplied to give it the firepower to affect the fighting.

The historical outcome at Tannenberg was the destruction of Samsonov’s army, but it need not have been so. Both players have options and can achieve decisive success. The player who makes the better choices, and backs his strategy to the hilt, will emerge the victor.

family: Series: Fire & Movement combat system (Decision Games)

description:
After the Tannenberg campaign of August-September 1914, East Prussia became a strategic backwater as both sides focused their efforts in Poland. But the Russian threat remained, and the following winter the Germans set about cleaning up their northern flank. A massive attack on the open right flank of the Russian Tenth Army sent it reeling; only the sacrifice of an entire corps in the Augustowo Forest enabled it to escape.

Masuria uses a modification of the Fire & Movement game system. Developed to portray the mechanized battlefields of a later generation, F&M stresses the use of rapidly-concentrated fire support to generate breakthroughs, followed by a rapid penetration by mobile reserves. In 1915, although railroads allowed the rapid shifting of forces on a large scale, no one moved faster than a man on foot on the battlefield, making exploitation ineffective. However, here both players have hidden movement, allowing them to concentrate in unexpected locations to surround and destroy isolated enemy units. The clumsy and inflexible fire support is represented by the artillery brigades available to division and corps commanders. It must be brought to the point of decision, then supplied to give it the firepower to affect the fighting.

The historical outcome at Masuria was the clearing of the East Prussian border. It might have gone differently; Russian reserves were available, and the German forces were a hodge-podge of units of widely varying quality. Both sides have a number of options to turn the fortunes of the campaign. The player who makes the better choices, and backs his strategy to the hilt, will emerge the victor.

description:

The plastic tap standing in the centre of the board contains a mechanism that, when the handle is twisted, lifts up small blue marbles (droplets) and releases them at the top, creating a genuine "dripping tap". The droplets can flip one of four "traps", sending any spider that happened to be standing on it back to the starting point.

However, if you prefer to keep your deck-building competitive, there is a new set of SIX all-new superheroes and a slew of new main deck cards to keep your games as hard-fought as ever! Play Impossible Mode to make competitive play even more hard-core.

You'll beg for mercy when you are subjected to their new "first appearance" attacks, and marvel at the rewards you reap for defeating them...which is not easy!

Similar to The Game of Life

Parnjače (Steam Engines) is game about first railroads in Croatia under Austro-Hungarian Empire.
You are one of six magnate whos will try to make money from railroad bussiness, but also try to satisfy wishes of Emperor Franz Joseph I.
To be best of other you must:

     satisfy secret wish of Emperor
     build new railroads
     connect new cities to railroad network
     transport goods from city to city
     upgrade your steam engines
     try to block bussiness of others


Board is modular so you have unlimited versions of board.

Parnjače consists of several phases:
1. phase: new event cards comes to play
2. phase: new cargo cubes randomly comes to cities
3. phase: bidding for order of play/workers (simultanous - players secretly bid for money they want and how workers they want)
4. phase: playes do their actions, in order of play
5. phase: clean-up

On his turn, a player can:

    build/drive asteam engine
    build 1/2/3 new railroads
    buy 5 railroads in his color
    hire additional workers
    connect/build new city
    drive 1/2/3 steam engines
    buy event card


Players build railroads from their supply of railroad hexes when they choose this kind of action.
Players transport cargo cubes from one city to another city that accept this kind of cargo and get paid for this, and also go up in cargo transport record.

Game is over when one of players transport 32 or more cargos.

7-Card mini expansion offered during the Bling Bling Gemstone Kickstarter campaign. The first 500 backers got them for free. The remaining backers could add-on for $2.

From the Kickstarter:

At the start of the game shuffle and deal one of each of these cards to all players. Graphics for these cards are coming soon but they will fit the theme/feel of the existing art. Each card is a one-time use card.


     Cave In! Play on another player, that player loses their turn. 



     Dead Canary: Play this card on another player's turn, this player must hit the tower with their pick, with their eyes closed! 



     Strike it Rich! Play this card on your own turn, you score double points for anything you knock off this turn (even center cores!)



     Dynamite! Take an extra turn (4 hits instead of 2) 



     Broken Handle! (Play on another player) Your pick axe is broken, you have to strike the column while holding one tip of the axe head and striking the column with the other tip of the head (do not use the handle at all!) 



     Claim Jumper: When you play this card, randomly take one collected gem from each player: Warning, if a player has no gems you must take a stone column piece from that player. If the player has no pieces then they steal a piece of their choice from you! 



     Jeweler's Loupe: Play this on any other player, you discover imperfections in their gems. All of that player's red gems are worth one less point during final scoring.

In Súper Maratón Feber there are two controllers for two players. Already prepared from the start, you must go all the track to the finish without cheating. You must overcome many obstacles in the path...

To climb the steps of the journey must be given the right strength. As time goes running... the ball must enter the roulette and must wait for the return to track. De la caja del juego:

En el Súper Maratón Feber hay dos mandos para dos jugadores. Ya preparado desde el punto de salida, debes recorrer toda la pista hasta la meta sin hacer trampas. Deberás salvar muchos obstáculos en el recorrido...

Para subir los escalones del recorrido hay que darle la fuerza justa. Mientras el tiempo va corriendo... la bola debe entrar en la ruleta y debes esperar a que la devuelva a pista. Entonces el llegar a meta es cuestión de pocos segundos y otros pocos obstáculos. Y si llegas el primero, eres el vencedor. ¡Suerte!

description:

From publisher blurb:

Designed for both SURVIVAL HORROR TABLETOP GAME (with 2 weapon set options, bases and weapon and stat cards) and ZOMBIE FIGHTER(alongside 2 zombies, bases and stat card) she will cover all your gaming needs!

30mm Character figures for Modern Horror gaming.

Kinga is a solitaire game of patience meets deck building, set in a medieval or renaissance fantasy world. You need only a standard 54-card deck of playing cards to play.

You play the role of the Usurper, a cunning and skillful silver-tongued devil attempting to wrest control from the four kings of the countryside. You do this by climbing the social ladder in each of the four kingdoms, but beware... too many missteps and your machinations will become all too evident, and then it's off with your head!

Playing a game of Kinga involves taking turns purchasing cards from the center tableaux (sort of a reverse-tableau building mechanic), in order to reveal the kings at the bottom of the stacks, or exiling cards from your hand. The game is won if you reveal all four kings -- however, if you are unable to purchase or exile a card on your turn, you must turn one of the kings sideways, raising suspicion of your plan. If all four kings are turned sideways and you need to raise suspicion again, the kings have caught on to your game, and have ordered your execution!

Kinga contains a novel chaining mechanic for purchasing cards -- You first play a 'base' card that determines your value, then any number of other cards from your hand that match either the value or the suit of the previously played card. Each card beyond the first adds 1 to the total value of your currency, and the suits used determine the cards you may purchase. Playing 3 of a kind in a chain lets you draw 1 additional card immediately, and playing all four suits in a chain allows you to draw 2 additional cards immediately.

boardgame: Letter Rip: The Game of Famous Names
description:
Players roll 10 letter dice two times.  On the score sheet all players write the results of the first and second rolls in the same order making letter pairs.  All players should have the same letter pairs.

A category d6 is also rolled with: sports, TV, books, history, movies & music.

Those letter pairs will be used as the initials of famous people or fictional characters.

"Unique" non-repeated answers will score one point.

Repeated answers will score zero points.

If the "unique" name matches the category then a bonus point is awarded for two total points.

Most points after 5 rounds wins.
The object of The Tootsie Roll Dice Game is to be the first to make specific combinations of dice by rolling your dice as fast as you can.

Castle Men 4X is a 4X micro-game set in a fantasy world.  Players are vying to create the most successful civilization on a small map made from just 9 cards. As each card contains multiple territories of different terrain types, the map varies from game to game.

Players must explore the terrain on the map, expand their settlements by exploiting local resources and of course try and exterminate their opponent.

To help their civilization grow they can gain knowledge which is traded in for developments that impact upon game play.  18 different developments are available to help, on just 9 cards. Each development a player chooses deprives them and their opponent of another alternative one.

Player actions are selected on a roundel, with a range of actions available each turn, although repeat actions are not possible.  Each completed journey round the roundel completes a season of the year and the different seasons confer benefits and responsibilities upon players that affect the progress of the game.

Players win by being the first to accrue a specified number of  victory points.  Victory points are achieved through gaining developments and building developing settlements.

All components for play can fit in a 2oz tobacco tin making it highly portable, and files include labels for the lid of such a container.

Spy Guys is a deceptively simple hand-and-tableau game of deduction and “take that!” where each of the 3 to 6 players is a spy trying to be the first to collect a set of four cards needed to complete their mission and escape! Since not everyone needs the same cards, players will discover fascinating moments of cooperation and conflict. Players with a hint of good memory, a good read of human nature, and a dash of luck can get their spy safely home first!

Holiday Special 2014

Breakthrough in the Wormhole Stasis Travel Stabilization technology has opened up the possibility to travel to the dark and cold distance of the Galactic North -- a system where even time itself is frozen, but which brings power and glory for the worthy. The journey to the Galactic North is a very long one, but the sooner you arrive, the greater the benefit...

Eclipse: The Galactic North is a print and play expansion. It adds Galactic North Bonus Tiles where the players' Ships can move to gain VP.

Tour time, space and beyond.

In this Regeneration Edition of Monopoly, people and places are all up for grabs.

This brand new Regeneration Edition of Doctor Who Monopoly lands on Earth just as the Twelfth Doctor takes to the TARDIS console for the first time. Relive the final days of the Eleventh Doctor, his adventure with the Tenth Doctor and the War Doctor, his quest for the truth about the Impossible Girl and all the excitement of a Regeneration and a new body.

With beautifully crafted special tokens and customised U.N.I.T and TARDIS cards this is the perfect companion piece to the thrills and spills of Season 8.

The aim of the game is to buy and trade properties to collect rent from your fellow players. In this edition the properties are people that the Doctor has met, planets, and locations featured on the show. To win you try to keep your money whilst trying to bankrupt everyone else, as when you are bankrupt you are out of the game.

Xit is a themeless abstract strategy game for two players using some of the principals from the Tixel family of games, including the piece activation mechanics, only reversed.

In Xit, players take turns moving an active (square) piece on a 6x6 (or 8x8) game board. Some of the pieces are missing one or two of their corners. After a piece is moved it is inactivated (by turning it 45 degrees in either direction, or from square to diamond). When a piece is moved past an inactive piece it will push that piece back to its active state it if there is a corner of that piece in its path. Players capture (remove from the board) any pieces of their opponent's activated in this way. Their own pieces remain on the board, ready to be played again.

If a player has no more active pieces, they lose the game.

Siege Warfare is a Customizable Strategy Card Game set in the rich history of the American Civil War. Plan your tactics and engage in epic battles with powerful commanders and military units.

Players take turns playing units, ground, tactics, effects, and resources, and may engage opponents in combat or play cards that interact with other cards, players, or decks.  Combat is a major part of Siege Warfare and is generally how many games are won or lost.  Players must choose their units and attack wisely to ensure victory.

There are many cards in Siege Warfare, and there are no random packs.  All Siege Warfare cards are available in one of the 6 ready-to-play decks.  Players can purchase any of our 6 ready-to-play decks, or customize their own deck from the cards they own.  All decks have 50 cards, and this represents your life total as well as the cards you have at your disposal.

Damage inflicted to an opposing player forces that player to discard that many cards from the top of their deck.  The goal of Siege Warfare is to eliminate all of your opponents.  A player is eliminated when they have no cards left in their deck.

Quantum tic-tac-toe is a "quantum generalization" of tic-tac-toe in which the players' moves are "superpositions" of plays in the classical game. The game was invented by Allan Goff of Novatia Labs.

Quantum tic-tac-toe captures the three quantum phenomena discussed above by modifying one basic rule of classical tic-tac-toe: the number of marks allowed in each square. Additional rules specify when and how a set of marks "collapses" into classical moves.

On each move, the current player marks two squares with their letter (X or O), instead of one, and each letter (X or O) is subscripted with the number of the move (beginning counting with 1). The pair of marks are called spooky marks. (Because X always moves first, the subscripts on X are always odd and the subscripts on O are always even.)

For example, player 1's first move might be to place "X1" in both the upper left and lower right squares. The two squares thus marked are called entangled. During the game, there may be as many as eight spooky marks in a single square (if the square is entangled with all eight other squares).

The phenomenon of collapse is captured by specifying that a "cyclic entanglement" causes a "measurement". A cyclic entanglement is a cycle in the entanglement graph; for example, if

square 1 is entangled via move X1 with square 4, and
square 4 is entangled via move X3 with square 8, and
square 8 is in turn entangled via move O4 with square 1,
then these three squares form a cyclic entanglement. At the end of the turn on which the cyclic entanglement was created, the player whose turn it is not — that is, the player who did not create the cycle — chooses one of two ways to "measure" the cycle and thus cause all the entangled squares to "collapse" into classical tic-tac-toe moves. In the preceding example, since player 2 created the cycle, player 1 decides how to "measure" it. Player 1's two options are:

X1 collapses into square 1. This forces O4 to collapse into square 8 and X3 to collapse into square 4.
X1 collapses into square 4. This forces X3 to collapse into square 8 and O4 to collapse into square 1.
Any other chains of entanglements hanging off the cycle would also collapse at this time; for example, if square 1 were also entangled via O2 with square 5, then either measurement above would force O2 to collapse into square 5. (Note that it is impossible for two or more cyclic entanglements to be created in a single turn.)

When a move collapses into a single square, that square is permanently marked (in larger print) with the letter and subscript of the collapsed move — a classical mark. A square containing a classical mark is fixed for the rest of the game; no more spooky marks may be placed in it.

The first player to achieve a tic-tac-toe (three in a row horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) consisting entirely of classical marks is declared the winner. Since it is possible for a single measurement to collapse the entire board and give classical tic-tac-toes to both players simultaneously, the rules declare that the player whose tic-tac-toe has the lower maximum subscript earns one point, and the player whose tic-tac-toe has the higher maximum subscript earns only one-half point.

Yakkaz is not available to purchase, being the third in a series of specially commissioned welcome gifts for visitors to Le Grand Massif ski resort in Haute-Savoie, France. The previous games in the series were ZOU in 2012 (an adaptation of MOW) and Kizz-Kizz in 2013 (an adaptation of Okiya).

Yakkaz is similar to Yahtzee, but instead of regular dice, five sides have images of the Tootizz, guardians of Le Grand Massif (see the Tootizz story).  The sixth side has an image of Zhinzho the dragon.

On a turn, players roll the dice up to three times and mark one (or optionally two) results on a score sheet. If more than one Zhinzho is rolled, they can be used to improve an already marked score; each line can be improved only once. The highest score at the end of the game wins.

Hal Jordan, Sinestro, Abin Sur and other characters in the the Green Lantern world are hiding.  On your turn, pick any two cards.  If they match, you get to keep them.

Find a pair of the special Green Lantern ring cards and you get to take the ring.  There are several of those in the game, so it moves from player to player.  Be the one who has it at the end of the game and you'll get bonus points.  The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Jewels of Tara follows the tale of Princess Tara & her lost jewelry. The Jewels of Tara are lost in a spiral take on a Snakes and Ladders board. You must find the jewels, which are represented by cards. Take jewels you find to her trusted jewelers in the "labyrinth", who can certify their authenticity. They will also fashion the jewels you find into articles of jewelry. Bring the best jewelry to Princess Tara first to win!

boardgame: Blocky Mountains: Starting Player Route Card
description:
The Starting Player Route Card for Blocky Mountains was first released as a PDF available for download from the publisher's website. This card is intended to be used as a more fun, thematic, and prowess-showing way of determining the start player.

Setup
The illustration on the card depicts blocks with and without numbers. The non-numbered blocks are the basic setup, but by adding the numbered gray blocks, you can increase the difficulty to level 2 or 3 (this is clearly depicted on the back of the card).

Play

     First, all players simultaneously bid by showing fingers from their fist equal to the skill level each thinks she can complete. Then, the player who has shown the highest level (e.g., three fingers = difficulty level 3), has one attempt at the appropriate route.
     If the player masters the route, she takes the white route marker and becomes the starting player on the route map. If she fails, she takes the last position in turn order, followed by the player with the next lower difficulty level, etc.


Very important: If several players have shown the same number of fingers, then whichever of them is first to shout, “Trapper,” is the one who must face the challenge. If there's still a tie, then whoever shouts “Grizzly” first takes the challenge!

A pre-release promo from the Tokyo game market.

It adds a female samurai warrior to the game, who will move before the players do. Whoever supports her the most will receive her help.

boardgame: Disney Princess:  Palace Pets Royal Pet Salon
description: Get regally pampered in the dreamy, 3D Royal Pet Salon. Move your adorable pet from room to room through doorways sized just for castle cuties. Look for posh pet products, from fancy soaps and shampoos to tiaras, collars, and sweet treats—all hidden throughout the salon! If you find something pictured on a face-up card, it’s yours to keep! When the time is up, all of the Palace Pets leave the salon with the grooming goodies. The player with the most wins!

Localized version of TransAmerica, settled in the city of Bielefeld, Germany.

Players work together building a tram railway network, but this time it is across Bielefeld instead of the United States. The first to have their five stations connected wins the round - the others lose points for being too slow! After 3-4 rounds, the player with the most points wins the game!

According to the description on the box, Trans Bielefeld+includes Vexation, but it doesn´t.

Game description from the publisher:

Raid the world of myth and legend! Crush every foe you meet. Backstab your friends and steal their stuff. Grab the treasure and run.

Munchkin Legends Deluxe is the Munchkin card game about legendary adventure...with none of that stupid roleplaying stuff. You'll defeat mythic monsters and grab fabled magic items! Don the Death Mask and Achilles' Heels. Wield Thor's Hammer...or maybe the Magic Bassoon. Start by slaughtering the Heebie-Jeebies and Johnny Zucchiniseed, and work your way up to the Kraken...

Munchkin Legends Deluxe includes a game board and character pawns in addition to the components of the Munchkin Legends base game. The original printing also came with Munchkin Legends 2: Faun and Games, a 56-card expansion that introduces a new race — the Faun — as well as more jokes and puns based on figures from mythology and history. With Faun and Games you can use the "Spear of Destiny" to fight "J.R.R. Trollkin" or fend off the "Wendigo" with your "Hell Toupee".

Foozle is a progressive bid game for three to eight players, in which players attempt to take exactly as many tricks as they bid or be penalized.  Unlike many bidding games, Foozle is not a partners game, but an individual "cut-throat" type of card game.

Try to move Jack or Jill to the top of the hill without getting knocked to bottom by marbles rolling down the hill. Marbles released from a plastic well at the top of the hill.

To play, a player spins the spinner. Then the player moves a marker up the stepping stones on the incline as many spaces as indicated by the spinner. The player then puts a marble in the well. Play then passes on to the next player.

The well releases all the marbles at some indeterminate point when the weight gets too heavy. When that happens if a player's marker is not behind one of the safe spaces, then it may get knocked down. If the player's marker gets knocked down then the player must start over at the bottom of the hill.

Contents:'
1 Game Board
2 Game Board Support Posts
1 Well
1 Well Trap
1 Well Roof
2 Well Roof Posts
1 Spinner (8 spaces, three 1's, three 2's, one 3, and "Pass")
2 Jack Pawns
2 Jill Pawns
30 Pee Wee Marbles

The pawns were made by using stickers which the buyer had to attach to the plastic stand-up playing pieces.

2 Player Variant for Martin Wallace's Automobile. Includes 6 tiles and a rule sheet. Released with Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #6

Starting The Game


    Each player receives 6 RD cubes.
    Place character tiles into an opaque bag or container and mix them.  *Draw 2 character tiles and place these tiles on their character tiles in the character boxes.  These characters are out of play for the first round.
    Next, draw 2 more character tiles, placing them on the empty space in the turn track box.  These characters will be out of play the following round.
    Set aside the salesman pieces of 1 color; these will be neutral.
    Place 1 advertisement marker (white) on each of the first 2 spaces.
    Place 2 discount markers (gray) on the first gray space and 1 on the second space.
    The third spaces will not be used in this variant (you can cover these with a factory from an unused color.)
    Remove 2 “5” value and 1 “4” value demand tiles from the game.


P2: Select Characters


    You may not select characters that have a character tile currently on them.


P3:  Player Actions

II: Place Salesman


    When you place your salesman you must place 1 neutral salesman, in addition to your allowed pieces, in 1 of the same showrooms that you occupied with this action.


P5: Sell Cars Via Salesman


    When it comes to your turn and you have decided to sell a car or pass, you must move one of the neutral pieces to 1 of the sales tracks to the left.  Before being moved to the sales track a neutral salesman can be moved up or down a maximum of 1 segment.


P9: End of Turn


    Remove the character tiles from the character boxes and return them into the opaque bag or container.  Move the 2 character tiles in the turn track box onto the appropriate character boxes; these characters are now out of play for this round.  Draw 2 new character tiles, and place these on the empty space in the turn track box.
    Each player places 1 closed factory marker onto the first open space beyond their farthest factory built.

boardgame: Dead of Winter: Lucky Luke Smith
description:
A promotional card given out during the 2014 Watch It Played Indiegogo campaign.

This promo introduces a new Crossroads Card that you can use in the game.

Part of the Dead of Winter series.

boardgame: Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem – Calaveras Club Expansion
description:
Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem – Calaveras Club Expansion introduces the Calaveras Motorcycle Club to your games of Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem, allowing up to five players to play at once (or six players if you already own the Grim Bastards Club expansion).

The Calaveras Club Expansion also includes the Black Market 3/4 Player Reference Card missing from many first print copies of the base game.

description:
Lets face it, trolls are a bit grumpy and usually don't get along well with others. Fortunately for this group of disgruntled trolls they happen to be quarreling in a field full of boulders. In Me Throw Rock players play the role of trolls who happen to be in the thick of an argument that can only be settled by throwing large rocks at each other. The player who has the best accuracy wins the game!

Me Throw Rock is an eighteen card dexterity game where players throw a set of six double sided rock cards at target troll cards over a series of rounds. Every round a single target troll card is thrown onto the floor. Each player takes a turn throwing their own colored rock cards at the target card until all their cards have been thrown. The player who has the majority of three rock cards that are the closest to the target wins the round. The winner of the round collects the target card and any of the three closest cards that are showing the split side up.

The games ends after all five target troll cards have been collected or if a player has one or less rock cards to throw. After the game has ended the players must tally their points by looking at the cards they have collected. Each target troll card collected scores three points and each rock card of the players color scores one point. The player with the most points wins the game. Ties go to the player who has collected the least number of rock cards of their opponents color.

Me Throw Rock is designed to be played on the floor with a space of about 8 to 10 feet in length. However, Me Throw Rock is not limited to play on a floor and can be played on any surface with a suitable flat target area such as a table or bed.

Take turns placing dominoes which link pictures together to suggest words and phrases.  Earn points when others guess your phrase or when you guess someone else's phrase.  Each domino consists of two simple icons placed at each end and representing common items such as "head", "house", "sun", "knife", etc.

Every person starts with two dominoes in their hand and play on a starting tableau of 4 dominoes in the center of the table.  They also have pen and paper to record their guesses.

On your turn play one of your dominoes onto the tableau to form a word or phrase from the two adjacent pictures.  Any combination is allowed but you're only joining two icons.  Write your phrase secretly on your paper.  For example, "bird" and "hand" could be "bird feeder", "a bird in the hand", "pet bird", etc.

Others now write on their papers what they think your word or phrase is.  Once everyone has made their guesses score points as follows:  everyone scores one point for guessing correctly, the turn player scores one point for everyone that guessed his phrase or just one point if everyone guessed his phrase.  A 2 point bonus is awarded the player who solely guessed the phrase.

First player to 11 points wins the game.

Snelbieden is a fast paced card game focused on trick-taking.
The game originated in East-Flanders, Belgium as a pub game.

The goal is to bid on how many points you will be able to get, and then get those points through trick-taking. Everyone starts at 41 points, the first one to get to 0 points wins.

It can be played with a standard card game deck, using 32 cards (from 7 to aces).

This is a computer Wargame from Bandai.  It is based on an early 80's Japanese anime featuring fighting between piloted mecha.  The game has several miniatures and an electronic device that records attacks and damage.

This is the first game in the series.  Others are Heavy Metal L-Gaim #2 and Godzilla #3

description:
In GoCache, you search for Urban and Wilderness cache cards using your GPSr device and Transportation cards. You can use Lifeline cards to help you on your way, but watch out! Your opponent will be throwing Muggle cards at you to throw you off course!

You have to play GPRs cards and then you can Cache Cards, in order to win the game, you need to "find" 10 caches.

Phases of the turn:

1: Equipping your GPSr and/or Transportation. 
Place the card(s) you wish to equip in front of you. You may only have one of each type equipped.

2: Discard as desired.
You may now discard as many cards as you wish. If you have more than five cards in your hand, you must discard down to five.

3: Draw Phase.
Draw cards untill you have five cards.

4: Play one Cache Card.
Play one cache card of your choice from your hand.

5: Counter Phase.
It is at this time other players have a chance to stop the current player from acquiring any points, by playing cards against this player.

6: Discard all played cards.

7: End of Turn

There's a big chaos among toys in Andy's room. The toys played hide-and-seek and now all of them are lost. Mind the colours symbols and character appearancee and the friends will Surely be found.

The aim of this short game is to get rid of all the cards.

At the beginning of the game cards are dealt evenly to all the players. Three different toys (or other objects) must be placed in the center of the table. Each round players unveil their cards simultaneously and when two or more of them depict the same character (in exactly the same pose) owners of those cards have to touch according toys as fast as possible. Player who touches the toy as the second one (or touches the wrong toy) collects all cards from the table and put them to the bottom of his/her stack. If there's no pair of identical characters, players unveil another cards.

There are 9 cards that change the rules:

    3 cards with Mr. Potato Head - character game (looking for certain character in the ssame pose)
    3 cards with Hamm - colour game (looking for a card with backgground in the same colour)
    3 cards with alien - symbol game (looking for a card with the same symbol in the corner)


If a special card appears the rules of looking for pairs are immediately changed.

A player who is first to unveil all of his/her cards wins.

Available at Lookout Games website

Rules of play:
Whenever any player produces glass (beads) or receives money, this player can use it to adorn the tree. The player places the money or the glass on a space on the tree and immediately receives the depicted number of victory points. You can do this only once per turn. The money and glass placed on the tree stays there until the game is over. Therefore it is no longer available for any player.

Included in Murano: Complete Edition.

The world is moving into a new era, and now more than ever the clans work for the progress and the evolution of their community. Everyone tries to find new ways to improve their lives, new means to grow their lands and new constructions that create an easier life. In Hoyuk: Anatolia, you must prove your quality through three different Achievements: build a Water Supply System for your village so the water can reach your land, throw the best Fest in the valley, and finally, build two Artifacts!

Each player chooses to complete two different Achievements at the beginning of the game. Each player who has managed to keep their Achievements intact until the end of the game will score additional Victory Points but most importantly each Achievement will score a different amount of points. The players use Drop counters in order to build the Water Supply System. Five Drop counters must be placed on five Houses of the same color and of the same Block. Furthermore these Houses must be connected to each other and at least one of them must be placed on the river or the lake of the game board. To place a Drop counter on a House, players must wait until the Construction phase and then choose not to build one of three Elements of their Construction board.

The Artifacts can be built easily but can be destroyed as easy as well because they are affected by all Catastrophes. Players can build an Artifact on the top of their Houses at the same way as the Drop Counters but in this case this House must be two stories high. If players do not complete this Achievement by the end of the game, they can still score Victory points. This is the only Achievement that has an advantage but it also gives less Victory Points.

This is an era where tribes search for a way to upgrade their social status, so they throw several Fests where they meet each other and celebrate. This is the last Achievement where players create the best Fest in the Valley by offering five different materials. These materials can be found on the Fest tokens but also on the Aspect cards. The Aspect cards have different images that must be matched with the Fest tokens. Once players have five Aspect cards that match with five Fest tokens, they discard them to score Victory points at the end of the game.

boardgame: Krimi Total: Die Pracht der Vampire
description:
Translation - "Splendour of the Vampires"

Minni Harkort, the vampire princess of London, has invited the vampires of the city to tell them an important message and to celebrate a lavish party. But only ashes from the hostess is found - the princess has been killed!

8 or 9 players slip into the roles of vampires and one of them is the murderer. His goal is not to be convicted, while all the others try to find out the truth.

From Wikipedia:

Also known as Super-spit in Wisconsin, Spit 2 in Texas, Rush in Missouri, and Spit 3 in Kentucky,[citation needed] California Spit is a fast paced shedding card game that has the added bonus of shuffling the deck.

The two players sit at opposite sides of a vertical playing surface. The dealer deals half of the cards to each player. The cards are held face down. Every round, each player plays four cards face up vertically in between both players and slightly closer to themselves. Once both players have done this, they look for two or more cards having the same number. When a player finds one, he places another card on top of the cards with that number until all of the cards with the common number are covered. In other words if there are three fours out then all three cards can get a new card on top of it. If a four is played on top of a four it is called a double, and the player may place a third card on top of it. This requires that players pay attention to the cards they are laying down on the pile. If a player runs out of cards then that player wins. When there are no more groups of cards remaining, each player scoops up the four piles directly in front of him or her and places them face down on the bottom of his or her deck. That round ends and the next begins.

In some versions of the game, there is a move called "spitting" in which the player is allowed to spit on the other player to distract him/her. It is common that they call "doubles" to inform the other player what they did. ment for {21+ age} a longer period of time usually 3 hours is the min, and 4 is the max.

Marketing Basics is a mini-expansion for Brew Crafters, comprising merely four cards:

     Workers:
         Marketing Specialist
    
     Recipes:
         Everyone's Pale Ale
         Starling's Irish Dry
         Point Placid Oak Aged Porter
    


It was originally offered as a last minute "thank you" to backers of the Kickstarter campaign, with the note that it "will not be immediately available for retail sale with the base game."

The three recipes in the Marketing Basics expansion can be substituted for the three basic recipes in the main game. The normal beer batch tokens from the basic recipes in the main game should be used, remembering to count any batches of Irish Dry Stout and Everyone's Pale Ale as 3 Rep at scoring.
A simple trivia game about the history of the Nordic countries.

This game is about collecting flags of countries in European Union. It lets children learn useful informations - capital city, population and where to find each country on the map of Europe.
Each player gets a card with flags printed on it. Then everyone chooses his starting point - one of the capital cities. Every city is connected to 3-5 others by lines with number on it. To move from one country to another, player must roll such number. By visiting every country he gets a small flag, which he puts on his card mentioned earlier. The first one to collect all of them - wins.

boardgame: Tales & Games: The Three Little Pigs – Bonus Card
description:
This card is a promo card that was included in Tales & Games: The Hare & the Tortoise, and later in Tales & Games: The Pied Piper, as well.

The card reads as follows:

Bonus Card for the game

The Three Little Pigs™

Use this card in the Veteran Pigs variant. Award this card at the end of the game to the player who has blown away the most points.

Each time you blow on an opponent's house, you receive the tiles that were blown away, instead of removing them from the game. Form a separate stack of these tiles, so you don't confuse them with your own building tiles.
At the end of the game, each player counts the pig heads on the tiles that he blew away. Whichever player has the most "bluster" points receives this award.

If it is a tie, nobody gets this award.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
This is a logic game created by Martin Nedergaard Andersen. Each player becomes a choreographer of one of the Princesses. He gets a dance board which he has to fill with tiles of five different colors. The tiles have different length and shape so each player has to think fast and grab the tiles that he needs before his opponents take them. The first player to fill his board gets 5 points but beware of mistakes! If you used a tile that you were not allowed to or a tile was wrongly placed the player receives penalty points. Additional points are scored for unfilled hexes on opponents boards.

The first player to score 25 points is declared the best choreographer and wins!

The usual Uno rules - Players race to empty their hands and catch opposing players with cards left in theirs, which score points. In turns, players attempt to play a card by matching its color or number to the topmost card on the discard pile. If unable to play, players draw cards from the draw pile until they are able to place a card. Wild and special cards spice things up a bit. Additionally, when playing their penultimate card from their hand, players must shout 'UNO' or face a forfeit of drawing two further cards.

WHAT MAKES THIS DIFFERENT FROM TRADITIONAL UNO: Altimeter Alert Card. When played, the player of the altimeter alert card chooses one color and each player puts 1 card of that color, face down, in front of them. Then each player reveals their card and whoever has the highest number wins. If 2 players have the same card, the number is invalid and the next one who has the highest number wins.
Whoever wins then plays his card and the game continues.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
A regular Anno Domini set consists of 336 cards, with a description of a historical event on one side of the card, and the year (and sometimes specific date) in which it happened on the other.

This small expansion set Anno Domini - Mixed Edition was a free giveaway by the publisher. It only contains 12 event cards out of 72 mixed cards without a common theme. Since this is not enough for a real game, it only comes with a brief summary of the gameplay. Those six different bags of cards can be recognized by the number of each of the first card in a bag: U001, U013, U025, U037, U049, U061.

Each player receives nine of these cards (or less, if you want the game to be shorter), and may only look at the descriptions. Players then put in turn a card on the table, *trying* to put theirs in chronological order. A player may, instead of adding a card, claim that the order in which the cards were put is incorrect. In this case all cards are turned over and the correct years revealed.
If the order was correct then the doubting player receives two cards and skips a turn. If the order was incorrect then the previous player, who accepted the order as correct, receives three cards. The first player that gets rid of all the cards wins.

Bluffing is as least as important as knowledge in this game. Most events are rather obscure or are hard to date exactly. Anno Domini is a blast to play, it is a very enjoyable social event and you even learn something from it.

Part of the Anno Domini series

description:
Nine-hole course to play with dice instead of clubs.
and 90 questions on how the good golfer should behave on course.

The game is a normal game of golf played by up to four players throwing dice in turns.

The golf course is made up of nine borads, each representing a different hole. If the ball goes off the fairway during the game, the player must answer some questions in order to continue.

Extension of Vem aí a Troika. Now you can evade taxes , manipulate political life , receive money from the Owner of It All (DDT) - and even take you into bankruptcy.

A satire to the case of bankruptcy of the BES.

description:
Turro is an abstract strategy game for 2 players. Your goal is to reach the opponent's end of the board with your Turro stone or capture the opponent's one.

Turro is a game of careful planning, area control and and flexibility on the board. With the two winning conditions you always have to keep your mind open for either way while keeping an eye on the plans of your opponent.

Capturing opponent's stones is key to make way to victory. But captured stones stay on the board remaining a threat if freed in the wrong moment. And capturing in the wrong moment can offer your opponent a free move to turn around the whole match.

Will you be able to break through? Or will you get captured on your way there?

Explore North America!

Explore the continent’s cities and landmarks — from the cold expanses of Canada to the sun-drenched beaches of the Caribbean.

Each player receives one Destination card to determine his or her starting position, or "hometown". Another 12 Destination cards are then dealt to each player. The goal is to plan a round trip from your "hometown" via the 12 destinations. The first person to complete a round trip wins!

To aid the traveler, train stations, airports and harbors are available as a quick travel option if a player has a corresponding Compass card. The Compass cards can help or stall your trip in a number of ways, including changing the outcome of the game. A player is never safe until he or she has returned home!

Designed for 2-4 players and takes you on a different path around the continent each round!

boardgame: Golf: A game of Mathing through Hazards
description:
Golf is a simple fast paced card game for 2-7 people in which players take turns solving problems using the four basic arithmetic operators; addition, subtraction, multiplication and division.

Golf is designed to be versatile to fit your play group, with simultaneous or sequential play which will help to keep even the most easily distracted player from becoming distracted.

This game has some great educational potential, and is a fantastic game for the in-between times during your board game night.  Much like many other filler games in this time range (~30min), the joy is in the exercise and this exercise keeps the downtime low and your mind always active.

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Visions of Dawn
description:
Description from the publisher:

Trolls and ogres are two of the largest and most belligerent monsters in Terrinoth. Only their age-old hatred for each other has safeguarded the innocent from their depredations. Now, this ancient war has been supplanted by a budding alliance. In Visions of Dawn, you can lead an army of ogres and trolls to rampage across the land, or you can be one of the noble few to stand in their way.

Visions of Dawn offers ten plastic figures previously only available through the first edition of Descent. You may take up arms with four heroes of the realm: Master Thorn, Nara the Fang, Ispher, and Sir Valadir. You may play as the overlord, commanding a monstrous legion composed of ogres, trolls, and manticores. To expand your experience as hero or overlord, this Hero and Monster Collection offers two brand new quests, Trucebreaker and Visions of Dawn, each challenging you to quest and battle for the future of the realm.
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

description: The mini game is a faster version of the Logo board game. It has customised rules and enough cards for up to 6 games. The cards are taken from the original Logo board game.

boardgame: Bounty Hunter: A Game of Adventure in the Old West.
description:
Bounty Hunter is a game where players assume the roles of Bounty Hunters in the Wild West. Your Bounty Hunter must track down and defeat Wanted and desperate Outlaws and turn them in for reward money. The more Outlaws your Hunter turns in, the greater his or her Fame or Stature will be, which, may make it more difficult to apprehend Outlaws. There will be plenty of obstacles and hazards to hinder your Hunters progress including hostile Indians, bandits, highwaymen, harsh climates, hazardous terrain and much more.

Game Objective: The objective in Bounty Hunter is to be the Player with the highest Stature Rating at the end of the Game. The Game ends when all Outlaws have been captured and Victory conditions have been compared. Players can choose to play a Short Game with 11 Outlaws, a Medium Game with 15 Outlaws or a Long Game with 21 Outlaws.

Game Features: Bounty Hunter contains many colorful counters featuring Outlaws, Cowboys, Townfolk, Gunslingers, Soldiers and Native American Warriors. The game includes a large, 6 sheet map with large hexes, representing various terrain of the Western Territories of the United States. There is a nice variety of weapons and items to equip your Bounty Hunter with in order for him or her to bring justice to Wild West.

Combat can be played out on the Game Map or on the Combat Mat provided.

BOUNTY HUNTER can be found at the following URL:
http://www.berserkergames.net

This two player trick-taking game combines elements of German Whist and Watten and offers scope for strategic play.  At the start of play, one player chooses a trump suit and another chooses "supertrump," i.e. chooses a single rank of cards (two through Ace) that becomes part of the trump suit and beats all other trumps.

The game is played in two stages.  After each of the first 13 tricks the players replenish their hands from a draw deck; each trick won at this stage counts for 1 point.  The second set of 13 tricks is then played out, with each trick won during this stage counting for 2 points.

Detailed rules can be found in a forum posting below.

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Gotham City Docks Promo
description:
Given to retailers as part of the DC Comics Deck-building Game Crisis League Kit.

This promo is a Location with Cost 4, with 1 VP.
Card text:

When you play this card, leave it in front of you for the rest of the game.

Ongoing: Once during each of your turns, you may put a card with cost 6 or less from the Line-Up on the bottom of the main deck. If you do, replace it.

Card game. Objective is to be first to collect three sets of cards. A set consists of a Job card + a Skill associated with the job + 2 x Personal cards (being adjectives associated with the job).

There are also Guess, Swap and Challenge cards which are used to allow cards to be questioned, in case the owner of the set has played cards that don't match to the Job card.

The Job card is always face-down but other cards are exposed, allowing opponents to consider these cards and make a guess as to the Job card.

There is a lot of bluffing because if you can complete a set with the wrong cards, it still counts to your target of three sets.

A 30 second timer is included so that the challenges have a limited time to prove or disprove their opinions.

A card game with 32 cards plus a rules card. The cards are illustrated with photographs from the famous annual rally from Paris to Dakar. There is also text on the cards which, if you read the cards from the first to the last, tells the story of participants. Finally, each card is numbered 1 to 32.

The object is to collect sets of cards in numerical order (3,4,5 for example. All the cards are dealt out. In your turn you can ask any player for a card. If he has it, he passes it to the question player who can then ask for another card from the same or different players. When someone is asked for a card that he doesn't hold, the questioner's turn ends. Once sets of at least three cards are collected, they are laid face-down in front of the player, who may add to them during the rest of the game. The game ends when the last card has been played. The player with the most cards on the table, wins.

Simple trick-taking game for youngsters. There are 32 cards, broken down into 4 sets of 8 cards. The cards are numbered (and have nice illustrations and text on them, none of which is relevant to the game-play). Every player gets 3 cards. Play is traditional with such games. A trick is won by the highest card played to a trick, unless it was trumped. Play continues until the last card is drawn from the deck. The player with the most tricks, wins.

In Comrade, you alternate between the roles of a dissident and an informer in a totalitarian regime, the former trying to influence the people with the word of freedom and the latter trying to "protect" the same.

As an informer, you win by identifying the dissident player and play him or her your card. As a dissident, you win by playing your card to as many comrades as possible so that they may learn the secret word of freedom and vote for it.

The game starts with one of the two words of freedom in play, "glasnost" or "perestroika". Your hand consists of one card only that tells you your current role. The card is played through three distinct phases:

1. Vote: Selected players vote secretly for or against freedom.

2. Committee: If the vote says freedom, the selected players vote again but this time openly. If they succeed in identifying the word of freedom all except the informer win. If they fail, the dissident player is revealed and an easier target for the informer in the third phase.

3. Interrogation: The players take turn to give or receive 1 card until all have 1 card again. The dissident tries to play his or her card to players who have not yet seen the card while the informer tries to play his or her card to the dissident.

The players may never reveal their cards.

The continuous change of roles makes the game unpredictable and keeps the players in uncertainty. A strong advocate of freedom one turn may suddenly be an informer the next.

You must trust each other to successfully vote for freedom but you cannot trust anyone!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Gulag, you take on the role of a loyal or disloyal worker in a totalitarian regime, the former trying to complete a Canal and the latter trying to sabotage the same.

As a loyal worker, you win by finding other loyal workers and play work cards to add work to the Canal. As a disloyal worker, you win by finding other disloyal workers and play strike cards to prevent work from being added. The Canal is completed if enough work cards are played.

The game starts with each player drawing one worker card, looking at it and giving it to the player to the right. Each player knows the loyalty of one other.

The workers secretly select one work card or one strike card each. The supervisor then decides whether to supervise one of them. If supervised, the worker must replace the selected card and select the other (opposite) card instead.

The commissar shuffles and discloses the cards and add the played work cards (if any) to the Canal. When all players have played the role of the commissar, the game ends.

The semi-hidden information where each player knows one player and is known by another player creates a paranoid gameplay. "He knows my loyalty but which is his loyalty?" "Should I play according to my goal and hope to escape supervision or should I play against my goal and hope to get corrected?"

There are many questions but whom can you trust to answer?

In Politburo, you play a politburo member in a totalitarian regime, responsible for a five-year plan. You have a secret personal objective as well but anyone not following the party line may be purged.

At the end of the game, you score 1 point for each plan that was executed according to your objective but you also lose 1 point for each time you have been purged.

The players take turns to play the general secretary and lead the turn through three distinct phases:

1. Plan: The general secretary proposes one of the three ministries of defence, finance and security and calls for an open vote. The ministry approved by a majority of the members enters the execution phase.

2. Execution: The general secretary appoints three members for a secret execution where they choose between repression and reforming.
   a) If they are unanimous, the ministry adds 1 point for repression and subtracts 1 point for reforming.
   b) If no unanimity is attained in three attempts, the ministry is unchanged and the three members enter the purge phase.

3. Purge: The members secretly points out another member to purge.If a member is pointed out by the other two, he or she loses 1 influence.

The players’ objectives may be related to the repression or reforming of one or more ministries or to the other members’ influence. They may coincide or collide.

There is a delicate balance between pushing the common agenda towards your own agenda and avoiding the "blame game" that may follow. You need political alliances but your allies will take any opportunity to purge you!

Travel the States is a card game for two players. Each player must build the best arrangement of cards in their playing area to gain the most victory points at the end of the game.

The cards show American tourist attractions, e.g., Manhattan, Graceland (Memphis), Niagara Falls, and Grand Canyon, and they are associated with five multicolored zones. In addition, each card shows a transportation icon. To place a card in their playing area, the player must care for the limitations and pay for the traveling costs by discarding cards with the appropriate transportation icon.

Whoever scores the most victory points according to the number of cards in their playing area and attractions in the zones and states wins.

A set of war games rule designed  for 1/200th 1/300th and 20mm miniatures.

They are a brigade level set of rules, with each stand representing a company.

The Units are groups of stands representing battalion sized formations, although there are no limitations for forming units.

An option is given for 20mm variation that says it is Brigade level but is probably Battalion level as each stand represents a platoon.

The game uses a chit system to achieve simultaneous movement.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: This Salish (Northwest Coast of North America) themed bingo game features art from Doug LaFortune. There are 36 different hexagonal bingo tiles and 6 hexagonal boards with 6 unique tile spots. When the dealer calls out an animal, each of the 2-6 players searches his or her board. The one who has that animal claims it. This continues until one player fills all six spaces on his or her board.

Promotional game for Estonian Business software company Directo.

There are piece of map with Finland and Baltic States on the board. Each player has 6 bidding cards in hand: 4 cards with destination cities and two special cards: 
"Any city" allows You to move to any city what is not destination for any other player this round.
and "Trouble" moves the specialists/deals on the board.

In each round new specialists/deals appears on the board. Players choose one of their cards and try to hire a specialists for his company or make a good business deals.
They reveal the destinations simultanously.
If only one player chooses the destination he gets all specialists/deals from that city.
If more than one specialist choose the destination they share the profit but make blind offer for the specialist(s). Who offer more money puts it on the bank and hires the specialist to his company.

The more different specialists You have the more money You earn at the end of each round. But beware to hire same kind of specialist already working in Your company - both leave!

After six rounds the player with the most money wins.

It's Time to Conquer Earth is a card and dice game, where you play the part of a wacky alien, scrambling to get a foothold on earth for your impending invasion before those silly humans realize what you are up to and retaliate.

Players take turns rolling their ships, (represented as dice of varying sizes,) to either abduct numerous different characters on the board, ranging from cows to kings, or to acquire tech, which is used to buy bigger and better ships, or to play one of the cards special to your given alien race. These cards will let you modifiy your rolls, make different characters easier to abduct, let you mess with other people's ships, and numerous other effects.

Characters have varying difficulties, representing how well you have to roll to abduct them. You start out with a 6 sided die, but you may buy larger dice with tech as the game progresses. Upon abducting a character, you may choose to brainwash them, netting you influence points, or you may choose to keep them contained to take advantage of their various effects.

Each time a character is abducted, the threat level goes up, representing how aware the humans are of your shenanigans. As the threat gets higher, so to does the difficulty of the game, as the humans start to retaliate or protect themselves in different ways. The game ends once the threat reaches 100, where the humans are finally acutely aware of your presence and ready to retaliate. At that time, each player counts up their influence points, and the player with the most influence points wins!

Spy Tag is a high-speed race to spot the spies and tag them before time's up!

To play, deal an even number of agent files to all players (the masterminds), who lay these agent files in front of them, face up, then set the timer. The first mastermind draws a spy card from the pile, scans the other player's agent files for the matching card, then slaps the spy card on top of the appropriate one. The mastermind who just got slapped is next to pull a spy card, and play continues until the timer goes off. The player with a spy card covering one of their agent files when the timer goes off must pull a card from the secret attaché. If the card is empty, the player is safe; if the card shows the stolen gizmo, that player is out and play continues until only one spy remains standing – the winner!

The kids in Unser Baumhaus want to build a treehouse in the garden, and plenty of nails and wood boards are available in the tool shed. To build the floor of a treehouse, you must first install four wood dice, then combine three building tiles in the proper order. Time is short, though, as dark clouds are covering the sky, signaling an oncoming storm.

If players manage to finish at least one level of the treehouse and put a roof in place, they win the game together.

CineCats (Micettini) is a very easy game to test your skills in describing,listening,singing,interpreting,observing...

You must figure out the CineCat as suggested by the dealer, who can describe it with the help of the illustration on the card, a verb, a sound, miming it, singing it, or reciting a "quote" from a movie!

The player who knows the game best starts as dealer: he shuffles the deck and places a dozen cards face up on the table.

The first player who uses his hand to cover the correct card, wins that cards and takes it for a victory point!

As soon as a card is guessed and taken, the dealer re-fills the table with a new card, until all the cards are taken. In the end, the player with the most cards... is the winner!

When the players have learned how to call, describe, mime, sing or quote the CineCats, every time a gamer takes a card, he becomes the dealer and describes the next CineCat;
of course the dealer on his turn can’t be the one to cover the card he just called!

And if someone slaps his hand on a wrong card... He must replace one of his cards on the table!

From the box:

"MVP Date before it's too late!  MVP Dating prepares you to be the best in the game by giving you chances to gain and use Morals, Values and Principles (MVPs) in dating scenarios.

The Game of MVP Dating is a roll-and-move game. The object is to master the outer "date-for-fun" path by accumulating three "dating experiences" chips and gaining 5 morals, values and principles (MVPs) cards.  Players may use MVP cards to avoid negative "1-on-1" dating experience card penalties or to dump a date.  When a player has accumulated the correct number of dating experiences and MVP cards he or she can move to the inner "Get Married" track.  when they land on a "Get Married" space and have only one date left, they may stay on the space until they roll a 6 to get married and win the game.

This game is designed to teach dating morals and values to unmarried teens and young adults with the ultimate goal being marriage.

Publisher's description: "Find your way to the center of the web!  Spider-Man's spider-sense is going crazy!  Mysterio, Master of Illusion, is up to his dirty tricks again.  He has hypnotized everyone in New York City in order to trap Spider-Man.  Hobgoblin, Venom, Carnage, and Green Goblin are also on the loose.  Only you can help Spider-Man and Black Cat stop Mysterio and restore peace to the city."

The Amazing Spider-Man was published as part of the Marvel Comics Super Heroes Game Book, and the board is a double-page spread of the book which is laid flat to play. This is a fairly standard roll-and-move game where players use an attached "electronic die" to move their pieces around a spiral spiderweb toward to the center.  Various spaces on the board move players back or forward or cause players to gain or lose a turn.  First player to the center captures Mysterio and wins.

As in Spoons, players in Cake Off! want to collect all the cards of one type in their hand.

To start the game, take all the cards for one type of pastry for each player in the game, shuffle them, then deal a hand of five cards to each player. On a turn, all players simultaneously pass a card to their left-hand neighbor, and they continue this process until one player has all five cards of the same type in her hand. She then slaps the pie plate in the center of the table, after which all of the other players do so. Whoever slaps last gets a cake stamped on her hand, then players shuffle the cards for another round.

As soon as one player has five stamps on her hand, the game ends and whoever has the fewest stamps wins.

SOLOmino is a Mau Mau variant that replaces the standard numbered and colored cards with domino-style cards that feature a colored number on each half of them — although some cards also feature action symbols that put twists on gameplay.

To start, each person takes a hand of eight cards. On a turn, a player either draws a card from the deck and adds it to her hand, or she plays one card from her hand onto one or two cards already in play. When you cover a card already in play, the half of your card doing the covering must match the covered card in either number or color. If the card you play features an action symbol, that action takes effect immediately, skipping the next player's turn, changing the order of play, forcing the next player to draw cards, swapping hands with other players, and so on. As soon as a player's hand is empty, she wins.

SOLOmino includes a variant that allows a player to play out of turn if she can play a card that matches a card in both color and number. When this happens, the player lays down the card as if it were her turn, then the next player in clockwise order takes the next turn.

From the back cover:

A game for two or more enterprising players, Corporate Ladder combines general knowledge, business sense, gut instinct and the wisdom of experience to take you from the company mail room to the loftiest position of all -- Chairman of the Board.

Should you consider inviting your boss and his or her spouse to a cocktail party in your own home?

What's the difference between a golden parachute and the golden handcuffs?

How many semester hours do most MBA degrees require?

Do you know the original name of the Amex?

What's the most common flaw in an executive's attire?

Answers to 1800 questions like these may surprise you as you scratch and claw your way to the top playing Corporate Ladder.  You'll have to make decisions on office politics, corporate etiquette, personnel, finance and banking, manufacturing, advertising, accounting and many more.

Corporate Ladder is ideal for families, cocktail parties, business groups and students.  Scheme, perform, wriggle, wend your way to the top, and once you get there, don't look back.  Somebody might be gaining on you!

From the front cover:
Experience Court and Jail Procedures!
Object: life and crime, court action, detention.  Player with most money at game end is winner.  Game can be played in short amount of time or can be lengthy - you decide.
Equipment: Game board, player pieces, sentence log, playing money, sentencing cards, appeal/parole cards and pair of dice.

User Description:
Roll and move game where players go along the outer track receiving money for various life events, but also are at risk of being convicted of various crimes. Cards from the "Sentencing" pile determine how long a player must go to jail (i.e. the inner track) or whether they have received probation. Various spaces on the inner track (each circuit around being one year) also earn you money and random card draws may win you appeals, probation, or an overturn of your conviction. Score sheets ("Sentence log") allow players to keep track of all the crimes they've been convicted of and how much time they've served for each. According to the game rules, there is no set goal; players must agree on a length of time to play or an amount of money to reach first.

The game is apparently intended as a criticism of the lenient "revolving door" of the U.S. criminal justice system, similar to "Capital Punishment" or "Public Assistance." Although the game was made in 1995, it fails to take into account the then new but growing "three strikes" system in many states, as well as criminal court's tendency to impose harsher sentences on repeat offenders.

1994 anti-nationalized healthcare game in response to the early, failed attempts by the Clinton administration to introduce a system like Obamacare.

The object of the game is to outlive the other players and benefit the most, financially, from a nationalized healthcare system.  The instruction book is filled with right-wing sarcasm -- the playing pieces represent various tropes like "pork barrel" etc. -- and the cards punish or benefit players (ala Community Chest and Chase) using hypothetical examples of pro-liberal or anti-conservative bias in the Federal government's bureaucracy.

Around the board, players get appointed to various positions in the healthcare bureaucracy and suffer various illnesses.  Each "position" has two spaces to win appointments to (rather than purchasing like in Monopoly), and unlucky players who land on your department have to pay their healthcare fees to you.  Beware of getting caught in the inner circle of healthcare waiting periods!

This game is similar in tone to Public Assistance or Capital Punishment, in both its conservative/Republican anger and its cringe-worthy cards and spaces.

Object: to obtain as much money as possible.  Each player is a ripped off customer at the start of the game.  You can advance to a higher pay rate by drawing a Ripoff or Profit card giving you a promotion, or by landing on a space indicating that you are hired or promoted to that particular pay status.  Land on the Stock Market space and you get to play the stock market.

Korea: Race to Pusan is a low complexity wargame covering the Battle of Pusan Perimeter. The game includes an 11x17 map, countersheet, and 4 pages of rules. The game uses the standard WWII rules found in the TwoBuckGames WWII Series.

Each player has an objective that he or she must meet in order to win - either number of units destroyed or occupying certain hexes. Each counter can move one hex per turn. The combat is a simple system in which the players must roll greater than or equal to the number printed on the counter to achieve a hit.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Brandywine is a large scale game of the early Revolutionary war using the rules from the American War of Independence Campaign Sets. The game is approximately three times as large as those found in the Campaign Sets.

The game includes an 11x17 inch map, counter sheet, and three pages of rules.

Players take turns moving their forces and attacking enemy units in order to achieve the specific objectives for a battle. One important difference to typical wargames is that before the moving player can attack, his units are subject to defensive fire from the opponent.

boardgame: American War of Independence: Campaign Set 4 – More Battles
description:
Nine battles of the early Revolutionary war using a common set of rules. Each battle includes its own specific rules page and map.

Battles included in this set include Monmouth (initial), Monmouth (final), Harlem Heights, Fort Washington, Ticonderoga Defiant, Paulus Hook, Red Bank, Bound Brook, Fort Clinton.

Players take turns moving their forces and attacking enemy units in order to achieve the specific objectives for a battle. One important difference to typical wargames is that before the moving player can attack, his units are subject to defensive fire from the opponent.

boardgame: American War of Independence: Campaign Set 3 – Other Battles
description:
Nine battles of the early Revolutionary war using a common set of rules. Each battle includes its own specific rules page and map.

Battles included in this set include White Marsh, Bennington, Princeton, Kettle Creek, Burgoynes Stand, Barren Hill, Brooklyn, the Clouds, and Springfield.

Players take turns moving their forces and attacking enemy units in order to achieve the specific objectives for a battle. One important difference to typical wargames is that before the moving player can attack, his units are subject to defensive fire from the opponent.

boardgame: American War of Independence: Campaign Set 2 – The Southern Theatre
description:
Ten battles of the early Revolutionary war using a common set of rules. Each battle includes its own specific rules page and map.

Battles included in this set include Charleston, Cowpens, Eutaw Springs, Camden, Guildford Courthouse, Kings Mountain, Hobkirk Hill, Yorktown, Gloucester Point, and Cornwallis breaks out (hypothetical scenario).

Players take turns moving their forces and attacking enemy units in order to achieve the specific objectives for a battle. One important difference to typical wargames is that before the moving player can attack, his units are subject to defensive fire from the opponent.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Vinegar Hill is a low complexity wargame covering the Australian Castle Hill Convict Rebellion of 1804. The game includes an 11x17 map, countersheet, and 3 pages of rules. The game is based on Peter Schutze's Eureka Stockade.

Each player has an objective that he or she must meet in order to win - either number of units destroyed or occupying certain hexes. Each counter can move one hex per turn. The combat is a simple system in which the players must roll greater than or equal to the number printed on the counter to achieve a hit.

Bedlam is a card-shedding party game with ever-changing rules.

Players are dealt a hand of cards at the beginning of the game and play same suit or number onto the discard pile if they can. However each player has their own rules card (e.g. When playing a red card, players must say 'better red than dead!') which they enforce by giving penalty cards to players who do not abide by them. There is also a shared rule card visible to all players.

The first player to get rid of all their cards is the winner.

3 gewinnt! ("3 wins") is a children's game similar to Connect Four.

Players take turns dropping checkers into a seven-by-six grid (the "cheese"), with the intention to make a three-in-a-row. Each checker shows a mouse: either boy or girl, and either red, green or blue. A three-in-a-row consists of three boys, three girls, or three mice in the same color. Each player has a hand of two checkers, and chooses which one to use each turn.

When a player creates a three-in-a-row, he gains a piece of cheese. Initially, these have one hole. When the pieces of cheese with a single hole run out, pieces with two holes are awarded, and when those run out, pieces with three holes are awarded.

The game ends when all the cheese has been claimed, or when the grid has been filled completely. The player with the most holes in his pieces of cheese wins.

In Alle Vögel sind schon da! players want to help all of the birds get to the party taking place at the birdhouse, but they need to ensure that the birds who show up have at least one present with them.

To set up, players lay out the bird house tile, then surround it with the double-sided bird cards, with the bird side of each card being face up. Shuffle the peephole cards, then set them as a deck to the side.

On a turn, a player sees the color of a bird or a gift peeking through the peephole on the topmost card, then chooses one of the bird cards and turns it over, announcing what's on the other side. If this matches the peephole card, then the player reverses the bird card and the next player takes a turn; if not, that bird card is removed from the game, then the player reveals one bird card after another until finding a match.

If all the peephole cards have been removed and at least one bird card is still on the table, then the players win and the birds get to go enjoy themselves at the party. If not, then the birds get to peck the players on the head in frustration.

A game for 25 to 40 year olds on their way up the ladder of success. Are you a Yuppie (young urban professional)? Or do you just aspire to be one? How do Yuppies get ahead and stay ahead? Why are they so successful?

The Year of the Yuppie Game is the game of success for 25 to 40 year olds on the way up. You'll experience money--and lots of it. You'll move up the ladder of success, screwing over fellow employees, friends and relatives. You'll suffer breakups, divorces and mergers. You'll play the stock market for big stakes.

In the process, you'll laugh your head off!
