Up to 5 players being the game with a car and $50,000. The first player to escape the "rat race" receives $100,000 and the game is ended. The player with the most money is declared the winner.

The year is 1932. The Japanese have attacked Shanghai, America has a bobsledding team, the five day work week has begun, and Prohibition is in full swing. All in all, it is a good year and tonight you have to collect the White Lightning. These Tennessee Mountains are full of moonshiners waiting for you to transport their shine. Now all you have to do is brave the dangers and make it back. You are not alone and, although others might not be as brave as you are, some might be smarter. You have to get more shine then they do and buy back the farm.

Moonshine Run is a game about both luck and guts, where each player is racing down the mountain to deliver their Moonshine to their customers. Along the way, they can pick up more Moonshine Jugs and different Items that may help them out. However, there is also hazards up on the mountain that could cause the player to lose their turn and any Moonshine Jugs they have collected.

There are six rounds during a game of Moonshine Run. Each round, a player pays Moonshine Dollars to access Road Card. Each road card if flipped as it is drawn. A play may come across some Moonshine Jugs, Item Cards or Hazard Cards. It is up to the player to decide when enough is enough and to call it quits.

This is a fast, press your luck game where you must know when to stop.

To purchase a copy of Moonshine Run, visit our homepage at madman.mx
designers: Games by a Madman or Two

description:
GeoCards Europa includes 47 cards with which players can play three games that challenge their geographical knowledge about Europe. Each card is double-sided, with the outline of a country, its name, its population and its size on one side of the card and its capital and the surrounding countries on the other. In all three games, whoever collects the most cards wins.

In the first game, shuffle the cards with the capitals face-up, then take turns naming the country depicted on the card. Guess right and you claim the card; otherwise, remove the card from the game.

In the second game, deal the cards out evenly among the players with the capitals face-up. On a turn, you hold one of your cards capital side-out toward your left-hand opponent. If he can name one of the countries adjacent to the depicted country, he claims the card; if not, the next opponent in clockwise order can try to do so. Remove the card from the game if no one guesses correctly.

In the third game, deal ten cards capital side-up to each player. On a turn, choose two cards and hold them out to your left-hand opponent. If he can guess which country is larger in size or population (depending on what choose at the start of play), he claims both cards; otherwise, remove the card from the game.

Bizoo consists 112 cards with different animals.
The aim is to form the couples with the same type of animals to achieve the highest score at the end of the game.

Each player gets 8 cards at the beginning of the game and in his turn player may perform one of the 2 possible actions:
a) form a couple and draw 2 new cards from the deck
or
b) activate an action of any of his cards.
Each animal species have its own ability.

The game ends when a player:

    reaches the score of 45 points with couples;
    or lays his last card on the table.


The winner is the one that has the most points.

ChromaWerks was designed for the 18 Card Microgame Contest on BGG.

In ChromaWerks each player uses 9 double sided cards (shuffled) to make a 3x3 grid.  Each quadrant of each card is a different color and type of machinery: Red Gears, Yellow Drive Belts, and Blue Pipes.  There are also sections of 'broken' machinery with images of damaged components on a black background.  Players must take turns completing one of four actions (Flip, Rotate, Slide, or Swap cards) to try to get all four center squares to be completed parts of the machine (solid colors).

A print and play expansion for Jane Austen's Matchmaker offered originally on Jane Austen's birthday December 16, 2014.

Expansion consists of a single card featuring Jane Austen.  If a player manages to marry the "correct" gentleman and lady, they can take the Jane Austin Card and add their old maid score to their final score. (A cheat sheet is provided for those who don't know who ended with who.)

Printed on a postcard-sized, er, postcard this promo station card was given away free with Lookout Games's magazine Neues von Ausguck (Winter, 2014); it may also be available with purchases from their online store (while stocks last!).

Place a track card and this station between Llanberis and the Pub.
When you excavate, you can choose this track card or the track going up the mountain. The game does NOT excavate, or Build, towards/on the Hotel.

Whenever you build in Pen-Y-Gwrd, move your Surveyor along one Station.

From Wikipedia:
The Pen-y-Gwryd Hotel was originally a simple farmhouse dating from 1811 and would, later, become an Inn. The hotel's most notable mountaineering connections are largely due the first successful Everest expedition in 1953 and the Kangchenjunga expedition in 1955, where training and testing of oxygen equipment for those expeditions took place, at Helyg near Capel Curig. On the right at the hotel entrance there is a Tyrolean style Stuberl with the signatures, written on the ceiling, of the team that did the first ascent of Everest in 1953 and of the successful first ascent of Kangchenjunga in 1955, these include Sir Edmund Hillary, Tenzing Norgay, Sir John Hunt, Charles Evans, George Band, Joe Brown, John Angelo Jackson, Wilfred Noyce, Tony Streather, Tom Mackinnon, Norman Hardie, Neil Mather, John Clegg and others including Noel Odell from Mallory's 1924 expedition and Chris Bonington of later successes. There are many photographs and exhibits provided by the original team members in the main bar and guest lounge. In the past each year, now every five years, Pen-y-Gwryd hosts the Everest and Kangchenjunga reunions.

SCRAM!'' is an exciting strategy game for the entire family! 

In SCRAM! up to 4 players are represented by a color on the game board. Players share from a common pool of tokens - each with their own special ability.

Players take turns placing tokens to create chain-reactions that move their tokens into scoring position and send opponent's tokens scrambling away... or make their token SCRAM! by driving it off the game board!

The player with the highest value tokens on their own color at the end of the game wins!

Alas, the corruption that dwells within us all.  Oh, what scandal, what deception!  Oh, what cruel manipulation of these two houses!  Shall we pity them and grant them leave while these matters are sorted?  Nay, the iron is indeed hot and we must strike with all intention.  Fellow citizens of Verona, I plead for your joint purpose as we bring the dynasty to level.  With power in excess, take what you will, and may the best of us rise to our place.

Council of Verona: Corruption is an expansion for Council of Verona that adds 21 Corruption Cards into the game. Corruption Cards may add new influence spots, be played face-up for an ongoing or instant ability or played face-down for an end-game ability or as a bluff.

Corruption Cards are dealt after the Character Card Draft and played either face-down or face-up on any Character Card on The Council or in Exile, adding an exciting additional layer of bluffing into the game. Each player will be dealt four Corruption Cards of which they will choose two to keep.

Pirates Enjoy Math (Don't Say Arrrrrrithmetic) is a micro card game for 2-4 players in which players use math and the Order of Operations to try to become the pirate with the most Loot.

The game consists of two rounds per hand and as many hands as players would like (recommended play is first to 29 points).  The first round is a card drafting phase where players try to build a Crew that will help them get the Loot.  The second round is where players use their Crew to create a mathematical formula that will get them closest to the Loot Value.  The winner gains the Loot, plus each player has a chance to earn Bonus Loot based on the cards they play.

To Reign Supreme is a 2 player area control war game that uses 18 cards.  Each player battles to conquer 6 contested Territories, using the abilities of the territories to build their military and increase their wealth.  Battles are conducted via a card based mechanic that limits chance and increases decision based strategy.

Each player has 5 Military Strategy cards and can choose 3 to use throughout the game.  Each player starts with the same 5 Military Strategy cards, differing only in card color, and must choose the three that best match their intended strategy.  The other two cards and the 2 Rules cards are used to track Wealth, Military, and Battle specific stats for their country.

My Big Break is a board game about auditioning in musical theatre. Components of the game are pawns, dice, money, credits, bitter cards, closing cards, and the board pieces. The general idea is that players go on gigs and accumulate a resume in the form of credits so that they eventually are qualified and ready to audition for their 'Big Break'. Booking this gig wins the game.
 Player take turns. Each turn has four phases. The first is an income phase, where players earn credits and/or money (except when in NYC, where they have to pay to live there). The second phase is an audition phase where they roll dice to see if they are cast in a gig. The third phase is a movement phase. The last phase the players either can play a 'bitter card' or they 'take class'. Playing a bitter card generally has a detrimental effect on another player. Taking class involves exchanging money and other resources to gain special advanced credits.

Bitter cards are generally earned by failing to book auditions. Closing cards are earned by completing professional gigs. Specialty Credits are required to audition for more advanced gigs, such as Cruise Line and Bus 'N Truck. A player must possess all three specialty credits (acting, vocal, and dance) and must have 15 total credits or more to audition for their 'Big Break'. If a player fails this audition, he/she loses an advanced credit, a generic credit, and gains two bitter cards. Play continues until somebody books their 'Big Break'.

A future expansion of My Big Break is planned which will contain the option to work for a Theme Park and a Dinner Theatre. Players will also be able to wait tables in NYC and take an agent there.

The goal of the Game is to add different coloured cards to rows on the table. You get points for every card placed during your turn. The game ends when one player places all his cards or no cards are left in the pool.

boardgame: Like the Tide into a Breach: The Battle of Neville's Cross
description:
Like the tide into a breach: the Battle of Neville’s Cross is a simulation of the battle that took place between the armies of England and Scotland in 1346, during the Medieval period. The battle forms part of both the Hundred Years’ War and the Second War of Scottish Independence.

The game is played over ten turns. Chronologically, a turn corresponds to about half an hour. At the end of the 10th turn the battle ends and the situation will be assessed using a scoring system. The resulting score will determine the outcome.

Combat results are resolved by the rolling of two six-sided dice, with various modifiers applied as detailed in the rules. Different types of unit have different characteristics, with terrain affecting how far a unit can move, how easy it is to break and its ability to either engage in melee or attack using archery.

Discover how quick your wits are in this fast-paced matching game for 2 to 5 players. The rules are simple. Take 10 cards and lay them out on the table. Each card illustrates two hands, each with anywhere between 0 to 5 fingers raised. On the count of three, the players raise one hand, showing any number of raised fingers (between 0 and 5). With their other hand, players quickly try to pick up as many cards as possible that match any two raised hands among the players.

boardgame: Bibi & Tina: Voll Verhext – Das Spiel zum Film
description: The game is set during the costume party from the film Bibi & Tina: Voll Verhext. Characters from the movie are placed on areas of the board, and players are to deduce where the villian from the film (Hans Kakmann) is hiding. When the player correctly guesses where the villian is, they get a token. The player with the most tokens at the end of the game wins.

Official description:

"It's a mad dash to get your car to exit the street as fast as possible. One  player is the Road Builder. He extends the road on every turn by placing a new tile down. The other players are Drivers  They roll the game die and decide which way to move, hoping to speed off the board before another tile is played. It's a frantic race to the finish!"

In each round, a different player takes the role of the Road Builder, and all of the other players become Drivers. The Road Builder's goal is to lay down all of their tiles before a Driver can Exit the roadway. The Drivers are trying to exit from the roadway before any other Drivers, as well as before the Road Builder lays the last tile.

The road tiles have special features, including tunnels (which allow the player to "warp" to any other tunnel space when landing on one by exact count), or the dreaded "No Exit," which blocks the road.

Recommended to play enough rounds so every player has a chance to be the Road Builder. Rules are included for both "Road Builder versus everyone else" and team play.

Game description from the publisher:

It's harvest time, and all the farmers, young and old, are on their feet and in high spirits, ready to celebrate a good harvest.

Players in Funny Farmer want to help bring in the harvest, and Johnny Tractor is ready to go out into the field. But how successful you are with the harvest and how many vegetables you bring back depends on how well you control Johnny. You can't take too long, though, as a thunderstorm is coming, and those torrential rains will end the harvest.

boardgame: Mia and me: Einhörner in Gefahr
description:
Game description from the publisher:

After defeating Panthea, there is at last peace in Centopia — at least until a circus island called Funtopia suddenly appears, with this place being run by a merry circus director named Rixel. The elves fear that Rixel is holding animals captive on his island, and they soon discover that the ringmaster has been sent by a powerful dark elf from Dystopia with orders to return a winged unicorn to Dystopia.

In Mia and me: Einhörner in Gefahr, the young players must help Mia and her friends keep the unicorns from being captured by Rixel and banish the danger from Centopia.

boardgame: Guerra de Mitos VI: Portals activated
description:
Guerra de Mitos VI: Portals Activated is a card game set in a fantastic world where different mythologies coexist. It is a different, dynamic game designed to be able to build different decks with very distinct and varied strategies, and each installment increases both the number of available mythologies and the game possibilities.

The game contains 150 cards split between the Hindu and Babylonic. Portals Activated includes new cards for GDM.

Each player in Guerra de Mitos (War of the Myths) needs to build his own deck of at least 51 cards (1 pantheon / 20 gods / 30 designs) of his choice, and each installment increases the diversity of characters, actions and strategies you can use in order to achieve victory.

Each player has to bring into play the cards his oracles offer him and play actions from the designs deck both in the influence phase (where the gods plan from their pantheon) and in the confrontation phase (where the characters who decide to go to battle fight to decrease the opponent’s power) to obtain supremacy or consign the opponent to oblivion.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: You’ll have to roll with the punches when you go to the mattresses with this villain! Featuring a random die roll element to his attacks, Dice is a promo Villain A card offered through the Dice Tower Season 11 2015 Kickstarter campaign. It will also be available at conventions later in 2015.

Armada is a multiple-deck deckbuilding game of fast-paced space combat. Each player controls two to five decks, each of which represents one ship in the fleet.

Each card in a deck represents one system coming online. As the AI alerts the control crew to which systems are ready, power can be diverted, weapons reloaded, hardware destroyed, AI disrupted — all using a rules light system that is easy to understand but difficult to master.

Each player attempts to use his ships to destroy his opponent's ships and be the last player remaining.

In the distant future, mankind has spread across the stars of our galaxy. Matter configuration technology requires vast quantities of Osmium. Interstellar corporations are locked in a battle for control of Osmium reserves and, thus, control of the galaxy. Cryogenically preserved crew sleep for centuries, awaiting their awakening to the sound of klaxons as an enemy armada enters sensor range.

Battle will be joined. Ships will be destroyed. Men may die. When the cryopod opens only one thing is certain: only one armada can survive.

Can you get your dragon around the island before anyone else? You'll get to try in Drachen-Rallye which pits players not only against one another but against gusty winds that sometimes blow you ahead and sometimes blow you back.

To set up the game board, place the dragons on their start spaces, then assemble the windmill-like second board and place it on the first game board with one of the arms of the windmill directly behind the dragons.

On a turn, you first roll a die. If you roll a number, you move your dragon clockwise exactly this many spaces; if you land on a green space, you then move forward two spaces, and if on a red space, you move back two spaces. If you roll a tailwind, you turn the windmill clockwise 1-3 spaces, pushing any dragons sitting at the front of that wind or riding on the clouds; if you roll a headwind, you turn it 1-3 spaces counterclockwise. (Once you've played a few times, you can use a second die that allows you to choose headwind or tailwind as you like, in addition to the distance that you move.)

The first player to circle the island and land on or pass its starting space wins!

Game description from the publisher:

The mouse has packed her backpack for an adventurous journey through Germany! There are many exciting things to discover because each province has its own stories.

In Die Maus: Reise durch Deutschland, players help the mouse by traveling with her, working on a new route across Germany each turn and answering tricky questions about the exciting things that take place in each region of the country.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Traditional Japanese festivities and sixteen new travelers are now part of the wonderful Tokaido journey, with these matsuri (the Japanese word for "festival") being triggered by the arrival of the travelers at the intermediate inns, punctuating the path with new and unique events.

Matsuri is a Tokaido expansion that brings new cards and tokens to the game. With this expansion, the players live the Japanese feasts and festivals. These cards modify the way each section of the road is played. This doesn't transform the game, but adds even more depth and strategy in the same way that Crossroads did.

From the publisher's website:

Since it's release in 2010, Colonial Battlefleet has taken starship gaming by storm. It combines fast and easy gameplay with a tactical depth few other systems can match. Colonial Battlefleet: Star's Reach is a new expansion for the Colonial Battlefleet core rules.

Colonial Battlefleet: Star's Reach takes the top-selling Colonial Battlefleet rules to the next level by adding a slew of new features fans have been clamoring for!

This supplement includes:

3 new fully developed races. Each race has its own culture, armory, tech levels, and special abilities to supplement the "build it yourself" options found in the core rules.

New fighter types, and new fighter options including blister turrets, and shields.

New weapon systems, including 3 varieties of Spinal Mount allowing you to bring unprecedented (yet balanced) power to be battlespace!

New options for movement, shields, and damage control.

New tech enhancements for your ships including a true cloaking device.

15 new starship designs, using the new races and technology.

You demanded, we delivered!

Princess Sissi has made a big mess in her dressing room. Now she is called to the great ball and the players have to help her find her accessories quickly.

The playing field in Schnell, Prinzessin Sissi! consists of an open 6x6 grid of tiles, with the tiles depicting six different accessories and one flowerpot. The princess is placed on any tile to start.

On a turn, any player rolls four dice: One of them shows which accessory the princess is looking for, the other three show numbers from 1 to 3 or an arrow. All players now look for a path from the princess to one of the matching accessory tiles, using the following rules: Each of the movement dice must be used to move the princess in a straight orthogonal line; an arrow lets you move the princess to the edge of the grid. At the end of the three moves, you must reach the accessory tile. The first player to find a solution calls out and demonstrates it, receiving one diamond if correct. If the path leads past one or more tiles with a treasure icon, there is a bonus diamond. If the path doesn't work out or leads past the flower pot, the player loses a diamond.

The first player to collect five diamonds wins!

Negative Feedback is a game about negative feedback loops where the players compete to obtain a hand with 0 cards in it. The players go about satisfying this desire by taking cards from other players hands, swapping hands, and other actions. In effect, although it sounds paradoxical, players run out of cards by taking cards from other players. As an added bonus every card has a whimsical statement on it!

From the publisher:

So you think you know Eau Claire?
Inside there are hundreds of trivia questions about your town and your area businesses.

This is a low complexity game of the Battle of Brandywine using the Two Buck games AWI rules but with a little more complexity added. Leaders are featured in this game and the map is twice as large as a normal Two Buck Game.

Set up is quick and plays in under an hour and as a wonderful teaching tool for young gamers but has enough what if options to have a lot of replay value.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: In Sheila Shark, the little fish are splashing in the coral reef when suddenly Sheila, the little girl shark, comes swimming along! Luckily, though, Tinto the octopus is nearby. Can the players manage to spoil Sheila's meal with Tinto's help?

Shop till you're top! Shopping Queen is a game based on the successful television show on the German TV channel VOX that features a group of girls, €500, and four hours of time for the perfect outfit!

In the game, the women go up and down the shopping mall from one store to the next in a pink shopping mobile. The time is running out, and only if the "candidates" answer the questions about the fashion world correctly and excel at various actions will the shop doors open.

Whoever thinks she has found the right style may enter the catwalk where each promising shopping queen has to face the judgment of the other players. Star designer Guido Maria Kretschmer also plays an important role in the game.

Ausgerechnet Fernweh uses the same game play as the other Ausgerechnet titles: Players deal three piles of 15 location cards from a deck of roughly 200 cards (naming locations related to Hamburg for this edition), then place an interlude card between each set of piles to make a deck of 45 cards. One location card is placed on the direction board, which has arrows pointing north, south, east and west.

During a turn, a player draws a location card – keeping the latitude and longitude on the back of the card secret from all players – then places the card next to the direction board, indicating how he/she thinks this location relates to the starting one. Is Lüneburg west or east of Blankenese? Why sure, so place it by the west arrow. Location cards can be placed between cards already on the board.

After each location card is placed, any player can challenge the positioning of that card. That player reveals the back of the most recently placed card as well as one adjacent card. If the challenge was correct – and the card placed wrongly – the challenger wins a point chip from the placer and the card is removed; otherwise the challenger hands over a chip to that person.

After fifteen cards have been placed, the interlude card signals an interim scoring in which all players secretly guess how many cards have been placed correctly. Guess correctly, and you score two chips; if no one is spot on, the closest guesser wins a chip.

After three rounds and the final interlude scoring, the player with the most chips wins.

Ani-gram-it is a beautifully designed board game that teaches children the ins and outs of the animal kingdom through play. Children are challenged to “build” animals from the inside out, using intuitive building blocks of anatomical information. Each play leads to new discoveries about how animals are made—and reinforces how they are classified in the natural world.

Perfect for travel and impromptu sessions of biology, Ani-gram-it To Go comes complete with 100 laminated, color-coded cards and can be played on a table, the floor, the grass, a picnic blanket, or any other number of spaces! It’s a great fit for the whole family, with little ones learning to name body parts and recognize different animals before they can even play.

Ser Snapjaw is the greatest Wyrm Claw Exemplar in an age. Legendary among lesser kobolds, they rally to Ser Snapjaw with zeal, his cunning leadership allowing them to fight with discipline that matches the mightiest armies.

This model may played in your game of Super Dungeon Explore as either a Wyrm Claw Exemplar Hero or as the dreaded mini-boss, Ser Snapjaw.

The monkey is loose at the zoo! When the zoo closes in the evening, the cheeky monkey jumps down from his tree and steals the zookeeper's key. In no time, he has freed all the animals from their cages, and together, they experience the craziest adventures. What could have happened? No doubt, you can think of many funny stories. Remember them well, because only then you can help the monkey to end the chaos!

Der verdrehte Sprach-Zoo (The Twisted Language Zoo) is played in two phases. During the game, players take turns drawing two tokens: one animal, and one object. The player has to come up with a story of what has happened with that animal and that object. For example, when a player draws "snake" and "balloon", he can say "the snake has found a balloon in a tree, and uses it to fly away".

When there is a story about each animal, the second phase of the game starts. Now, the players take turns remembering the stories. On his turn, a player draws an animal card. He has to remember what object was connected to that animal. The other players may give hints. If the player gives the right answer, he may move the monkey one space on the score track.

The players win together if the monkey reaches the last space of the score track (the tree). If they run out of cards before the monkey reaches that last space, they lose together.

The game comes with a mechanized gameboard for hiding and revealing the answers, and several rules variants.

The objective of Dominant Clan is to be part of the largest clan with the highest rank.

The game uses Action cards in manipulating the gameplay. Some action cards allow players to swap identities, stumble players, nullify effects and even changing the scenario of the game.

At the beginning of each game which is the revealing phase, each player is dealt with 3 Item cards which work as identities. Similar matched identities creates clan which introduce the leader system. Dominant leaders have higher abilities than any player in the game where they have the ability to use their member's cards which is the key to securing the outcasts. Ranks of each player plays the role of power and defence. Ranks are given to those who are worthy of becoming a leader.

In this game there is the Item Deck and the Action Deck. When the game starts, each player is given a choice to choose either drawing an Item card or an Action card. Then the player can either keep those cards or use the Action cards. For every player can only hold up to 3 cards in-hand, it forces players to a good challenge of planning and execution among members. This also leads to multiple strategic ways of winning the game. Backstabbing betrays can happen and so does denouncing friendships for a game! As much as sabotaging is a prominent factor of the game, it is also a heavy cooperative game that fosters communication among players.

After a number of clan members is reached, the game ends with a rank comparison with the outcasts and the dominant leader. The highest rank holder wins the game. With a draw as a result, the dominant team takes the victory.

Dominant Clan is currently in the process of publishing and is estimated to be released in late 2015.
One of the first modern European card games designed by a Malaysian.

The Commanders is a expansion that adds 8 new cards.

The Commander Agents are cards that are given to players before the game begins. Each Commander bestows a special benefit to the player who owns it, and each benefit lasts throughout the game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A simple, dice based horse racing game set in Vienna / Austria.
Six tin horses and dice trays come with this game, which probably was produced in Austria in the late 1930's (swastika symbol used as a watermark on the rules).
The Publisher might be 'Mohehauser' or 'Hohehauser' according to the stamp on the game's backside.
The course has 100 fields from start to finish. All multiples of 10 are colored in black. Fields 35, 56 and 68 show hurdles, field 77 a water jump. In clockwise order, players roll one die and move their horses forward accordingly. If a horse stops on a black field, the player loses one turn. On a hurdle the player looses two turns, on the water jump he's out of the game. The player who first reaches field 100 is the winner. If he however exceeds 100, he has to move back the exceeding number of steps.

A betting mechanism is built into the game: Each player has to pay 10 or 20 units to enter the game. Losing a turn costs 2 units, loosing two turns 3 units. Dropping out of the game costs 10. The winner takes it all.

German rules:
1. An diesem sehr interessanten Spiele können 2 oder mehrere Personen teilnehmen; jeder Teilnehmer setzt nach Übereinkommen der Gesellschaft 10 oder 20 Marken in die Kassa. Jeder Spieler bekommt einen Reiter und setzt denselben der Reihe nach mit den Vorderfüßen zwischen die 2 Linien Nr. 1, dann wird gewürfelt und wer das meiste wirft, beginnt das Spiel und setzt immer den Reiter mit den Vorderfüßen in den Raum der gewürfelten Nummer. Wirft jemand einen Zehner, z.B. 10, 20, 30, so zahlt er 2 Marken in die Kassa und darf einmal nicht würfeln.
2. Kommt jemand auf ein Hindernis, so bleibt er auf seinem Platze, darf 2 mal nicht würfeln und zahlt 3 Marken in die Kassa, hat aber jemand das Unglück und fällt ins Wasser, so hat er 10 Marken zu zahlen und muß vom Spiele austreten. Wer Zuerst die höchste Nummer erreicht hat, gewinnt das Spiel; wenn er aber darüber hinaus kommt, muß er um soviel Augen wieder zurück als er geworfen hat.

Theme
Global trade needs to have the monopoly of goods. To obtain it, you must prevail over your competitors!

Tradeworld is a dice rolling pocketgame, where players have to collect the five colours of the cubes for declare themselves the Lord of the Global Trade!

Gameplay
Each player, in his turn, roll a die and compare the value came out with a table of permitted actions and performs one of his choice. The choices are between moving their marker on the map of the world, take / exchange a cube from the map or take / exchange a cube with the opponent, if present on the same continent.
When a player has collected at least one cube for each colour (12 cubes in 5 colours are contained in varying numbers in the game) is declared the winner!

How high can you stack the scoops?  Listen carefully to the ice cream dish as it tells you either how many scoops or what color scoops to stack.  Take turns placing the right scoops on the dish.  Make any scoops fall and you take them into your pile.  Be the first player to stack all of your scoops and win the game!  A sweet way to learn colors, numbers, and following directions!

Whereas the hat, car, iron, and other token pieces represent players in the normal version of Monopoly, Monopoly: Assassin's Creed lets you choose between the series' different protagonists. You can play as Altair, Ezio, Connor Kenway, his grandfather Edward Kenway, Aveline, or the protagonist of Assassin's Creed Unity, Arno Dorian.

Other game pieces have also be changed to match the Assassin's Creed theme. Money has been replaced with Abstergo credits, and the usual real estate is swapped out for historical locations featured in the Assassin's Creed series, with Abstergo Industries and Abstergo Entertainment replacing the game's original prime properties: Boardwalk and Park Place.
Race game using a d6 die to control movement on board. Starting player chosen by draw of a city card.
Simple race game featuring Charles Lindbergh's flight across the Atlantic Ocean. Players markers are planes.  The spinner is on the board and represented as the propeller Lindbergh's plane.

With Tour du Jour you can organize one-day classics or your own multi-stage tour! Use a single racer or a team consisting of a maximum of four members. Whether you enjoy hills or mountains, cobblestones or coastal roads, Tour du Jour has it all. Find out how your team holds up under the onslaught of the gruesome secteurs pavés of Mons-en-Pévèle or Trouee d'Arenberg and brutal côtes such as the Kapelmuur and Oude-Kwaremont. Prefer long or short or false flat stages? It's all up to you. Mountain top finishes? Check out the tiles for 3 infamous climbs: L'Alpe d'Huez (France), Alto de l'Angliru (Spain), and Plan di Montecampione (Italy). Rain? Snow? Got it covered. Labyrinthine, narrow city streets, complete with 90 degree turns, nasty road furniture that pops up unexpectedly? Tour du Jour has that as well. There's no end to the possible configurations: this versatile game contains enough tiles to lay out any type of race and blast off.

The gamebox itself—containing 6 teams of 4 racers, game token holders, a set of 40 cards, 45 terrain tiles, and a 20-sided die—has been reduced in size and weight in order to save on shipping charges.  Less box, more game!

The course is laid out from the terrain tiles each of which has a number indicating its degree of difficulty.  On a player's turn, they roll a die and if the die roll is higher than the difficulty, the player may advance.  If there are still unused pips to cover the degree of difficulty of the next time, the player may advance another tile until all the pips are used up (or insufficient pips remain to advance a tile.)  Unused pips are tracked and carried over to the next turn.

The cards are event cards which are drawn at the end of a turn.  Most are blank (no effect), the rest contain an event (usually bad, but not always) that impacts the race.

For a single race, the winner is the first to cross the finish line while for multi-stage races, the lowest aggregate score (aka time) is the winner.

boardgame: Terra: 221B Baker Street / Carcassonne Promo Card
description:
"Where is Carcassonne located and how many Sherlock Holmes short stories exist?
This SPECIAL CARD for the ... quiz game TERRA ... brings new guessing material into play!"

A promo card from Spielbox #07/2014 [B12247] featuring the city of Carcassonne on one side and Sherlock Holmes' residence 221B Baker Street in London on the other side.

This is a bonus question card just like the regular ones.

The beautiful Filly Butterflies are excited, because Princess Scarlet is visiting. She has brought sparkling crystals, which she generously hands out in the magic Papillia garden.

To reach all Fillies, the princess travels from place to place. With a little luck of the dice and courage to some take risks you can get hold of a lot of colorful glitter stones. Here we go!

In Filly Butterfly: Sammle deine Funkelsteine (Filly Butterfly: Collect your Glitter Stones), each player attempts to collect as many crystals as possible.

At the beginning of the game, all players receive the same amount of crystals.

The board shows six locations in Papillia; each location has some spaces for crystals. A Princess Scarlet pawn is placed on one of the location.

Players take turns rolling the die and moving Princess Scarlet a number of spaces clockwise, as indicated on the die. If Princess Scarlet ends her move in a location that still has an empty spot for a crystal, the active player has to place one of their crystals there, and may then roll the die again, if desired. On the other hand, if Princess Scarlet ends her move on a location that has no more empty spots, the active player may take all crystals from that location, and his turn ends.

The game ends when one player has no more crystals left. Then, the player who has collected the most crystals wins.

boardgame: Spurs: A Tale in the Old West – Pioneer Promo
description: The Spurs Pioneer Promo adds a new character to the Spurs Universe known as the Pioneer. He is a balanced character whose special ability is he reduces terrain movement costs.

Full Throttle! is an expansion kit for any of the three "Legend" games covering the Mille Miglia road races across Italy – Legend: The History of 1000 Miglia, Legend: Winds of War, and Legend: The 1000 Miglia Action Game. Full Throttle! adds more player interaction, competition and fun to all three games.

Full Throttle! includes two decks of cards: the 72 full-sized card Main deck (used in game play) and the 40 mini card Locales deck (used as reminder markers).

There are 12 different card types in the Main deck, consisting of 7 obstacles which can negatively affect a player, and 5 situations that can negatively or positively affect the chance of the obstacle's effect. The obstacles are: Pothole, Landslide, Spectators, Animal, Intersection, Fog, and Railroad Crossing Gate Down. The situations are Fatigue, Hangover, Sober Up, Fog Lifts, and Railroad Crossing Gate Up.

The Main deck cards are organized into 6 suits: Mountains, Forest, Farmlands, Towns, Bad Roads, and Night. The Locales deck consists of 6 card types corresponding to the suits in the Main deck.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
1-4 players work together to prevent the fabric of all reality from being torn asunder while competing against one another to further their own personal goals.

The ultimate Creator of all universes is also a mindless force of chaos and destruction. That which it has created it will eventually destroy. It is mindless, but all-powerful. The first of its creations to survive and assume intelligence consider themselves Its sons and daughters: the Excubiae. At the beginning of time, they worked together to seal away the Great Nameless, in order to protect the Realms they had created for their own progeny.

In true Shakespearean fashion, these siblings developed alliances and rivalries, and began working with and against one another, all while their Creator attempted to work Its way through their ancient seals...

In The Great Nameless, each player assumes the identity of their very own god. They lord over their own Realm, create miracles, and force their worshippers to do their bidding.  They may even manifest in physical form, greatly affecting the battlefield before them.

The game consists of different decks, tailored to each god, and a central, autonomous deck that represents the unstoppable force that is The Great Nameless.

Players race up and down a doom track to seal away their Realms from the center of the board, and work to help their allies and sabotage their enemies.

Players accumulate victory points for placing seals, destroying the armies of The Great Nameless, destroying the forces of their foes, and playing valuable cards of their own.

boardgame: Bad Lands: Curse of the Skinwalker
description:
Bad Lands: Curse of the Skinwalker is a western horror co-op survival game where you and your friends take control of a posse of heroes fighting off a pack of vicious were-beasts and other folklore creatures.

Build your own adventures - The Bad Lands box comes with 6 heavy duty, double-sided, linen coated Board Tiles. You only need 4 Board Tiles play. We will include several different pre-made adventures for you and your friends to play, or you can make your own! The board tiles are double sided, and you can lay them out in a variety of ways to build your town your way.

Form your Posse - Bad Lands is a co-op survival game for 1-4 players. While most players will just control 1 Hero per game, you can still quite easily have a larger posse. The more Heroes in the adventure, the more Villains you will have to face, and the tougher the game will be.

Kill Some Bad Guys - Bad Lands features a whole host of vicious Skinwalkers and minion beasts for your Heroes to vanquish. The core boxed game comes with a deck of 30 Encounter Cards. You draw a card, and it tells you what Villains are going to jump out and attack the Heroes.

Get some Loot! - Of course, no adventure is complete without cool rewards. When you defeat tough Villains, they will leave a Loot Marker behind. If your Hero picks one up, you can draw a Loot Card from the deck. Each Hero may carry an unimited amount of Loot Cards, but may only equip and use two.

This expansion includes new variants of play and a sticker sheet with 24 stickers, 6 stickers for each color, which are attached to the back of the playing pieces.

Variant 1 - Adds a General that may order adjacent pieces to move/rotate as a free action per turn.
Variant 2 - Adds a Hero with the strength & weight of 2 men.
Variant 3 - Adds the Fury that will rout the enemy that routed the Fury.
Variant 4 - Adds a Knight that has a free action of move/rotation per turn.

From the author:

The rules and components needed for various expansions and variants for Nika.

Nika is the ancient Greek word for “Conquer!” The concept of Nika came to me in a dream while I was studying abroad in Athens. The rules were honed across nearly two years of testing. The board shape and core mechanics were inspired by ancient Greek geography and warfare. Greece has many narrow valleys and mountain passes, and sometimes the only way to defeat an enemy force was to collide with them head-on and literally push them off the field. This othismos (“shoving”) is thought to have been the critical phase of most battles between hoplite armies, who were the heavy infantry that dominated ancient Greek war for four centuries. In this game, you command a Greek city’s hoplite soldiers who must force their way through enemy lines. Your troops can push or rout those who would block their way across the narrow battlefield. Coordinate attacks with your ally to secure victory, but make sure to watch your flanks!

boardgame: Camp Grizzly: Expansion #3 – Behind the Bear
description:
Camp Grizzly is a semi-cooperative survival horror board game.

The Behind the Bear Expansion includes: 
- Introduces a new deck of OTIS cards with hidden traitor rules.
- 18 NEW CABIN/SURVIVAL/OTIS CARDS INCLUDE; Low Hanging Fruit, Mostly Armless, Daddy Dearest, It was me all along, Heavy Breathing, One Last Scare (and more!).
- NEW COUNSELOR JUMBO CARD AND PAWN: Saffron - The 'Flower Child'

boardgame: Camp Grizzly: Expansion #4 – Jump the Bear
description:
Camp Grizzly dips its toes into the '80s with some of the "best worst" horror tropes.

18 NEW CABIN/SURVIVAL CARDS INCLUDE; Your Doppelganger, Telekinesis, Indian Burial Ground, The Trees Have Eyes, Jump the Bear, It Was All A Dream, Meemo (and more!).
4 NEW JUMBO FINALES INCLUDE: Bthulhu, Ghost Pirates, The Time Tower, O.T.I.S.
NEW COUNSELOR JUMBO CARD AND PAWN: Chet - The 'A-Hole'

Camp Grizzly's "Rated R" expansion introduces a new "SNUFFED" card mechanic to the game. Snuffed cards make the game more difficult every time a Counselor is killed.

18 NEW SNUFFED DECK/CABIN/SURVIVAL CARDS INCLUDING: Hundred Yard Honey, Bear Skinned Rug, Stuffed and Mounted, Slippery When Wet, Chipper Shredder, Big Wayne (Jumbo card and pawn), Chubby Bunny (and more!).
4 NEW JUMBO FINALES
NEW COUNSELOR JUMBO CARD AND PAWN: Jason - The 'Horror Fan'

boardgame: Fritz und Franz: Die beiden Ausreißer
description:
Up to two players play the two runaways Fritz and Franz. The other players play the brother, sister, wench or servant, who try to catch the runaways. The runaways get a headstart and start at space 6. Every time a pursuer gets on the same space as a runaway, he gets 2 gamecoins. At space 7 the drawing of a card decides, which way to follow. Only Fritz and Franz may choose their way. On several spaces, the players get benefits or obstructions. If Fritz or Franz are the first to arrive at space 60, both runaways have won the game. If a pursuer is the first to arrive, he wins the game.

The game was published in the 1920s. It seems to be inspired by "Max und Moritz"

The players start at the Driving School at space A and have to traverse the game board till they arrive at space E back at the Driving School. On several spaces the players get points, penality points, obstructions or benefits. The first player to arrive at space E gets 12 poits, the second 6 points, the third 3 points and the rest get 1 point. The player who gets the most points, wins the game.

Simple "Pairs" card game featuring Superman characters from the 1966 era of the comics.

Rules (From the Rules Card):
Shuffle the cards and deal one at a time until all the cards have been distributed.  (Players will not all have the same number of cards.)  Each player arranges his hand and lays down any pairs he has been dealt.

Player to the dealer's left begins by drawing a card from the dealer's hand.  If he can make a pair, he lays them down.  Play then moves clockwise, the player on the left taking a card from the player on his right.  The game continues until all the cards have been paired.  If a player has used all his cards he waits until his opponents have played all of theirs.  The first player to use all his cards receives a bonus of five points, but his pairs can still be captured.

Using the points on each card the scores are then added up and recorded.  The first player to reach a score of 50 or the player with the highest score over 50 is the winner.

A Superman card has no value alone.  However, it can be used to (a) cancel out a pair of minus cards, (b) capture an opponents pair, (c) protect one of the player's own pairs.  It is placed on top of the pair it has captured, cancelled out, or protected from being captured.  Players must wait their turn before using a Superman card.  After playing the Superman card they also draw from the player on their right.  Once a Superman card is used it cannot be used again.

In Terrenus, the players become gods playing a game with the mortal world. The game is player in four phases, each a simple mini-game with different rules, but each building where the other left off. Each phase is roughly ten turns per player. The four phases relate to various godly activities:

Creation - The gods build the game world, using a tile placement mechanism. Each god will place ten, and score points based on number of edges connected and terrain features completed.

Population - Each god has 40 tribes to competitively place on the board, usually 3-5 at a time. The finger of god touches an origin square, and life spreads from it. Terrain features limit how many tribes can fit in an area. Points are scored based on how terrain is filled. This phase feels a little like Go.

Construction - With the world populated, the gods migrate tribes together. When enough are in a square, they build a temple to whichever god has a majority in their stack, and leave the board. Rival tribes are captured and converted by this process.  Points are scored by using tribes to build temples, and by converting rivals' tribes. This feels vaguely like the city building mechanic from Civilization (original).

War! - The world is now full of temples. The gods each have a monster and a hero to summon from their temples on their turn. Monsters seek to defile rival temples, making them more difficult to summon from. Heroes go to fight rival heroes and monsters, or just vandalize a rival temple. Points are scored for slaying, defiling and robbing. This phase can feel like the endgame of Chess.

Each phase has a method of earning bonus points. At the end of the last phase, the gods total their scores to determine a winner.

Long ago in Seven Cities of Gray, two adepts began quests to create libraries of spell books. They sent their allies out across the lands to gather rare elements and the mana needed to aid their growing hunger for power.

Mage Quest is a microgame for 2 players lasting about 30 to 45 minutes. The game is a prequel to Mage Clash.

The object of the game is to create four Spell Books by collecting and trading Elements while moving across lands and seas with ships and caravans.

During the game, each player has two actions per turn with players alternate turns. Players may take any action in any order during their turn and may take the same action twice.

The Actions are:
A. Build a new Ship
B. Convert a Ship into a Caravan or Convert a Caravan into a Ship
C. Move any or all Caravans or Ships
D. Collect Elements
E. Trade (within a City):
1. Trade Elements for Elements 
2. Trade Elements for Mana
3. Trade Mana for Allies
4. Invoke Speed
F. Create a Spell Book

Simple children's race game. Players roll a die to determine how many spaces to move and race around board to get back to the nest which contains the "Golden egg". It would take 68 spaces to go around the track from, and back to, the nest.

Markers - Each player gets one marker of a unique color.

Die - The player can advance as many spaces as thrown on the die. However there are three exceptions:


     A player cannot pass another Goose. So if a player rolls a 3, and the space 3 spaces ahead is occupied, then the player can only move forward two spaces. 
     If a player lands on an egg spaces then he must move behind the the last player on the board. If there is still a player in the nest, then the move is back to the nest. 
     You don't have to return to the nest by an exact roll. 


This game should not be confused with McLoughlin Bros.' "Goosy Goosy Gander" (1896), which is referred to as "Game of Goosey Gander" on the inside of the box. (Last-minute realization, perhaps?)

boardgame: Game of Fun at the Circus
description: Essentially a Snakes and Ladders type game, but with a circus theme. The game uses a spinner for movement. It has four colored wooden pawns for the players to mark their positions.

boardgame: Tiny Epic Galaxies: Satellites & Super Weapons Mini Expansion
description: This mini-expansion introduces a press-your-luck element to the game, as well as super weapons with uniquely powerful abilities. These weapons can be destroyed for Victory Points and Energy.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Pudding is very popular with people in Taiwan, especially children. Almost all of us have eaten pudding, and it is an unforgettable childhood memory for us — but pudding can be used for more than what you think...even for the fun party game Pudding Wars!

Three robbers took a treasure and hid it in their castle. This castle is on the island in the middle of the deep lake in robbers forest. Each player tries to conquer the treasure, the fastest player wins the game.

First, each player has the get hold of the necessary attributes to make the crossing to the castle: a hat as a disguise, a boat as transport and a key to get in. These attributes are available on special fields in the robbers forest. A simple dice game helps the players with that.

When someone has collected the necessary cards, he may try his luck in the castle. In his turn, this player may choose one of the seven cards face down (three robbers, three neutral cards and the treasure). Either he won immediately (treasure map), he may try again in his next turn (neutral card), or he must begin again (robber card).

With beautifully illustrated game board.

In the Wallenstein: Military Leaders expansion, each player receives one military leader figure to place on one of his controlled counties. While performing actions in this county, he receives extra benefits, such as reduced costs by 1 when constructing buildings or additional grain when producing it.

At the end of the turn, he moves the leader to an adjacent county under his control, if possible, while leaving a marker at the old location. The more markers you have on the board at the end of the game, the more points you're awarded.

Wallenstein: Military Leaders was presented as a Kickstarter exclusive while also possibly being available at conventions.

User summary:

A free game distributed on Game Market fall 2014, making a satire of the current meme that cool boys would impress girls by slapping their hand into the wall which the girl was leaning against, stopping her from simply walking away.

This action is called a "kabe-DON" or a "wall slap". Puru puru kabe don translates to "Trembling wall-slap".

The game consists of a standee card with the picture of a girl, and four double-folded cards with the picture of a boy, holding his hand on the edge of the cards.

Each player gets 1 folded boy card and one try each. The goal of the game is to tip the folded boy card so that it leans on the girl standee card, without the latter tipping. If the player succeeds, he gains 1 point. If the girl card tips, or the boy card doesn't lean on the girl card, the player scores –1 points. If he refuses to try, he scores nothing. Play as many rounds as there are players, switching start player, and the player with the most points at the end wins.

boardgame: Head Honcho: Jet Combat Over the Yalu
description:
Head Honcho is an introductory level mini-game on air combat set during the early years of the Korean War. The game is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh, with graphics by Bruce Yearian, and is published by High Flying Dice Games.

Head Honcho: Jet Combat over the Yalu is also the Consimworld Donation Drive Gold Level game for 2015. Head Honcho features such aircraft as the F-86, the F-84, F9F Panther and other aircraft of the war, all battling the Communist MiG-15s for air supremacy.

Head Honcho is not meant to be the most accurate of air warfare simulations. Rather, players should find this an entertaining diversion that still captures the “essence” of the violent and tense nature of Korean War jet combat. Similar to other minigames using this design (Dueling Eagles, Fighting Eagles), I also hope Head Honcho serves as a vehicle by which newcomers to the hobby of simulation gaming can be introduced.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Extra Resources is a new Action card for Jerusalem. (set #2)

Just add the card to the basic Action card deck to play with it.

The player who plays this card receives one silver Bezant from the bank and add one Prestige point to his total by moving his cylinder one space up the Prestige Track.

Foreign Baron is a new Action card for Jerusalem. (set #2)

Just add the card to the basic Action card deck to play with it.

The player who plays this card gets an extra Baron (use a simple white pawn to represent him). The player may use it any time during his turn in the phase nr. 2 (even in a following round).
To use the Foreign Baron and block an Area (small or large), the player must place at least three new Squire in that area.
At the end of the round the Foreign Baron is removed from the gameboard and he is discarded.

The Artist is a new Action card for Jerusalem. (set #2)

Just add the card to the basic Action card deck to play with it.

The player who plays this card removes one Squire behind his screen (return him to the supply) and add one Prestige point to his total by moving his cylinder one space up the Prestige Track.

The Pass card is a new Action card for Jerusalem. (set #2)

Just add the card to the basic Action card deck to play with it.

The player who plays this card may move one of his Squire inside an area blocked by any Baron. He may enter the blocked Area or he may leave the blocked Area.

The card called "The Merchant" is a new Action card for Jerusalem. (set #2)

Just add the card to the basic Action card deck to play with it.

The player who plays this card receives from the bank one silver Bezant for each floor built of the highest Tower on the gameboard.

boardgame: Super Dungeon Explore: Mistmourn Coast Warband
description:
Perpetually locked in an unnatural fog, the Mistmourn Coast is home to fickle and cruel faeries who make sport from tormenting trespassers. Trolls are some of the few mortal creatures who call the Mistmourn Coast home, and even they have been deeply affected by its magic.

Like all Super Dungeon Explore warbands, Mistmourn Coast comes with two spawn points, a hero, and a mini-boss.

Contents

     1x Mistmourn Shaman Hero
     1x Salt Mini-Boss
     1x Tribal Stone Spawning Point
     1x Mistmourn Chieftain
     2x Ghostfire Warriors
     2x Spirit Walkers
     1x Salt Pillar Spawning Point
     2x Mist Hounds
     6x Sorrows
     16x Model Game Cards
     1x Pinch of Salt Treasure Card

A simple race game for children. The movement was controlled by a d6 die, numbered 1-6. There were six different colored cars for the players.

The track had four different kinds of problem spaces - tire trouble, equipment trouble, a smash-up, and running wide on a curve. There were also spaces to fill-up with gas that had to be hit with an exact throw.

From back of box:

The Seattle Seahawks and the most popular board game have teamed up to create the ultimate gridiron fantasy for fans of all ages. Buy, sell and trade the most valuable players in the history of the Seahawks, from Steve Largent and Dave Brown to Shaun Alexander and Dave Krieg.

What Seattle fan hasn't dreamed of owning and running the Seahawks? At the end of the game, only one Seahawks fan will remain standing and control the definitive Seahawks team of all time... Will it be you?

20 pages of easy to play WWII skirmishing for two or more squads of two to ten figures. Comes with mission and objective cards and two play summary sheets.

For use with 28mm miniatures.

Both players selects an objective, and two blanks at the start of the game. Thus nether side knows what (or where) the other sides objective is.

Fall, Caesar is a social game of assassination set in ancient Rome. Players take the role of one of twenty city leaders, some more powerful than others.

Each round of the game, one player is eliminated by a majority vote. Players will have a chance to force weaker players to vote with them, or force quiet players to vote by playing "The Wine". Only players who were in the majority on the previous vote are eligible for elimination in the next.

Once two players are left, the eliminated players vote for a winner.

A merchant ship carrying thousands of small plastic duckies has shipwreck in the middle of the ocean. Duckies have fallen into the water and have swept away by the current to Lofoten islands (Norway). Duckies are cute and funny, but they endanger the ecosystem of the islands.

Fishermen, seeing them go to sea to fish with their nets. But it will not be easy at all: a strong storm, heavy seas, ice sheets, and several orcas  will complicate the task.

Game inspired by a true story that occurred in 1992. A merchant ship lost in the middle of the ocean 29,000 plastic duckies. The little duckies are still adrift on the seas of the world. The oceanographer Curtis Ebbesmeyer follows their trail since. Thanks to him, nowadays we know more about ocean currents.

OBJECTIVE
in Duckies in Lofoten players are sailors and have to cooperate tile by tile, chosing the correct action dices, in order to build the best strategy. They are not alone, an Icebreaker will help them to fish all the duckies before to be catched by ice.

The players have to guess words drawn by their teammates

Each turn one of the teammember as a designer draws a card, chooses one of the three words and shows it to the designers of the other teams.

He starts drawing the chosen thing by using the special wooden pieces (triangle, square etc.) and his teammate may say what
word the drawing reminds him.
If it is wrong word the next designer starts to draw it.
Team who is the first to say that word scores a point.

The first team with total of 5 points wins.

boardgame: Mahou Shoujo: Fight Like a Girl!
description:
Mahou Shōjo, meaning Magical Girl, is a turn-based paper card game based off the anime genre with the same name. Two players face off against each other in sparkling combat in order to defeat the other and capture their gems so that their rival cannot transform. Decks span from a number of themes including Nightmares, Daydreams, Sweets, Flowers, the Constellations, and even in the Internet. Playtime is under 20 minutes, which is more than enough time to show your rivals what true (sparkling) pain is.

The object of the game is score three of your rival's gems. Gems are gained when a RIval's Magical Girl's HP reaches zero. To do this, players using a series of spells and abilities (and sometimes a little roleplaying) must engage in battle. As battle is happening, Magical Girls have a limited supply of magic they are able to use. If their magic is drained to zero before their hit points are, they are safe from being defeated. Some Magical Girls will be able to become Grand Magical Girls, which are very powerful for only a short amount of time. The game ends to be short but action packed.

The objective of the game is to be the first to reach the score of 25 points by forming words on set with 6 letters drawn at random.

One player has pink letters other player orange letters.
Player has 6 letter tiles before his turns and he places them on the board to form a words and score points. Each letter gives him 1 point. If he is using opponents´ letters he also takes the points from him and opponent decrease his points accordingly.

Player who first reaches to 25 points is the winner.

Guess what's behind the clues! A range of puzzles in pictures that allows children to develop their observation and deduction skills.

Three are 42 cards with three fun and colorful illustrations on one side of the card and the answer on another side of the card. 
Which child will be the fastest to guess the answer?

Player with the most guessed cards at the end of the game is the winner

Guess what's behind the clues! A range of puzzles in pictures that allows children to develop their observation and deduction skills.

Three are 42 cards with three fun and colorful illustrations on one side of the card and the answer on another side of the card. 
Which child will be the fastest to guess the answer?

Player with the most guessed cards at the end of the game is the winner

Guess what's behind the clues! A range of puzzles in pictures that allows children to develop their observation and deduction skills.

Three are 42 cards with three fun and colorful illustrations on one side of the card and the answer on another side of the card. 
Which child will be the fastest to guess the answer?

Player with the most guessed cards at the end of the game is the winner

Zhukov's Brutal Battle: Operation Mars, 1942 is a brigade to corps simulation of the battle, in 11 2-day turns, 25Nov-16Dec42, 7 miles/11 kilometers per hex, and 35 ground units on each side.  (The weather reduced flight operations considerably.)

The surrounding of the German 6th Army in Stalingrad had happened, and in the game von Kluge must try to (irretrievably) withdraw as many panzer divisions as possible to send to Stalingrad, while stopping Zhukov's attempt to retake Rzhev and erase the salient it was in.

The Russians get points for any victory point cities they take, for clearing out German defense zones along the front line, and for eliminating German units, outright or in exchanges.

This heretofore little-known battle was the subject of Col. (rtd.) David Glantz's revelatory book, Zhukov's Greatest Defeat.  Historically, the Russians took horrendous casualties and achieved little, other than preventing panzer divisions from being transferred south, to try to help save 6th Army surrounded in Stalingrad.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Disney Planes Sky Race Action Game
description:
Fly your plane through the Wings Around the Globe Rally to win. But, watch out—flight conditions change and you’ll face surprising challenges. Toss with your eyes closed, between your legs, lying down, and more!

Players assembles their gliders, set Stage Markers (checkpoints) around the house at various locations, and randomly distribute Flight Control tiles at each Stage Marker. These tiles add to the game's variety as each new checkpoint presents new challenges to reach the next Stage Marker. "Toss your plane upside down", "Toss covering one eye" and "Toss over your head" are just a few of the possible twists.

Each player gets one toss to reach the next Stage Marker. The plane closest to that marker wins that stage and collects the Flight Control tile (each worth a random amount of points). After completing all stages, the player with the most points wins!
Adds 4 new Character Cards to your game of Till Dawn to expand your game to 12 players. The base game will only play up to 8 players. More players does not lengthen the play time.

The 'Till Dawn' backer kickstarter exclusive promos pack includes the following:

- the Nosferatu character card + voting cards [4], 
- one card for the Hunt deck [Chuck Norse - Vampire Slayer],
- two cards for the Power deck [Hunter Hunted + Vast Wealth],
- one event card [Betrayal Alliance],
- a Sun/Moon standee token for the Night Track [replaces the glass bead].

You will receive THE RING and the two cards used in conjunction with the ring. The Ring card and the Alpha Werewolf.

Text from The Ring card:

"Anytime on your turn, you may demand tribute from any single Vampire. That Vampire must show their allegience by kissing your ring, or bowing before you.

If the Vampire will not bow or kiss your ring, they must give to you as follows:
 4- 8 Players: 1 Blood Token
 9-12 Players: 2 Blood Tokens"

Part of the "Smell-o-rama!" collection.

Quench your thirst for fun with Dr. Fizzy’s Fabulous Floats.

A cola-scented game filled to the brim with fast moves and keen sight. It’s a soda-licious, refreshingly fun card game for everyone. Open the game and mmm-mmm, the cola scent brings everyone to the table.

Quickly gather the ingredients to build your ice cream float - a frosty mug, ice cream, soda and a spoon or straw. Now sit back and…

Yuck! Goat soap and mashed potato - instead of ice cream and soda?

You were a little too quick with the ingredients you picked, and now you’re the one in a fizz! Start again and think to sweeten your drink. Make three fabulous floats and win.

Kids and adults alike savor the fun game features - The Float, The Flip, Slap a Card, and Slap a Cap. (Even better, reasoning is a required ingredient.) Dr. Fizzy’s Fabulous Floats is deliciously good fun for the whole family.

WYSIWYG [wiz-ee-wig]: What You Steal Is What You Get. In WYSIWYG you play a light fingered thief. Your goal is to loot, pickpocket, and burgle your way to fame and riches. But watch out for the city guard! They'll put a stop to your life of crime at every turn of the die.

Each player is attempting to collect Items, everyone gets one go at stealing from the target. As you collect the items, you will trade them in at a Fence each fence is represented by a color wink. when you have 3 items with the same color wink. i.e. the locked Chest, The Ancient Tablet, and the Treasure Map. You trade them into the Green Fence. You get a Green wink to represent being "Solid" with this Fence.

Winner is the first thief to become "Solid" with 5 of the 7 available Fences. But keep an eye out. The other thieves maybe aiming to take your ill-gotten goods from you! And with the Fence Rewards come item cards that can assist in their nefarious plans to take your loot, freeze it in place, or use a fire bomb to wipe it from existence. Because, in WYSIWYG there truly is "No Honor Among Thieves"

Simple race game using 2 dice two move horses around track. Track has obstacles. Players must agree on how many laps for race before race begins.

Whole game is contained in a 3.5x4 inch folding "matchbook." Dice are formed by putting two cardboard cutouts together to form 3-D form.

A simple bowling game that was in a matchbook format about 3.5x4 Inches. The bowling balls were formed by interlocking two cardboard cut-outs. To score the players rolled the ball towards the inclined scoreboard made from opening matchbook cover. Cover has different score on various holes.

Simple children's game where two player use a mechanical flipper to toss "bugs" through a vertical barrier. The barrier is a disc with a yellow square net, but it has a circular hole in the center. The bugs have hooks so that they can get caught on the net to slow down game even more.

To start players split bugs and start flipping them through the barrier on a signal. First player to get rid of his bugs wins.

Hungry puppies want to eat.

There are food bowl for 4 puppies what starting to rise up if You pull from the bone and the dog food (balls in 4 colors) come over the edge. Players are trying to catch the most food with their dog hammers to be the winner.

Catch as many sandwiches as possible jumped out from magic toaster.

One player presses the timer-button and some toasts jumps out. All players play simultaneously and try to be the fastest and sharpest to catch the right toast with his/her stove. If you have a bread that does not match what You need on your plate, you will place in the slot in the right corner.
The game continues until the toaster is empty. The winner is the one who has collected the most correct slices.

Stockyards is a trick-taking, set collection, economic card game for two to four players. In the game, players are selling sheep, swine, and steer to the auction house at wholesale price. After the wholesale pay out, an auction commences. The players leverage trick-taking mechanism to win a meat-packing company a lot of livestock while earning a commission. After earning a commission minus any penalties, the non-winning players may attempt to fulfill contracts using a set collection mechanism. Players will place contracts secretly on a company. At the end of the game, the contracts are revealed for each company and livestock are processed earning the contracting player money. If a contract is not fulfilled, the player loses money. The game lasts for ten rounds and the player with the most money at the end is the winner.

A game filled with colour that children win by being patient and not rushing to put all their tokens on the merry-go-round.

Each player has 6 tokens representing children who want to go on the ride . The first player throws the color dice. There are two possible outcomes:

    purple, the player places a "child" token in the purple box and can reroll.
    other colors, e.g. blue: If the blue attraction is free, then the player can place a "child" token and may choose to reroll or pass. But if the blue attraction is busy, the player removes the token "child" of the ride and his turn is completed. The first player to place all his chips "child" on the ride wins.

Match colours, quick reaction: A blue father cat, a red mother cat... Quick! You must be the first to find their 3 kittens!

All players have two big cats, each face down. Kittens are also face down, but at the center of the table.

The players must find three kittens in the same color as their big cats , like a memo. The first player who has collected all her kittens must shout "MIAOU", the turn is over and he wins a "mouse" pawn.

Is repeated until a player gets five mice pawns. He will be the winner of the game!

A highly amusing and entertaining game for little folks

Kitty Wampus is a very simple game. Each players gets 4 wooden disks of the same color.  In turns a player spins the Kitty Spinner. If it lands on his color he covers one of his white spaces with his disk. If it lands on another color his turn is over.  The person to cover his 4 white spaces wins.

Unemployed Clowns is a game where each player portrays a Clown at the union waiting for a job.  Utilizing variable player victory conditions, and a Mafia style deduction element, players will utilize card play to attempt to achieve or avoid work. Any number of players may win since they will have different victory conditions.
Monopoly clone series done in multiple cities, with local businesses donating to charity in return for being included as properties to be purchased.  Big Appleopoly charity is Phoenix House.

Rolling in the Money is an educational game designed to teach children to count coins and make change. There are two levels. In level one, players roll five "money dice," (which have the names and values of US coins on each side, as well as an X, indicating no money). They then take the indicated plastic coins from the tray.  Next, they may choose to roll as many dice as they wish that did not come up with Xs a second time, and take the indicated coins.  They may then do so a third time. The goal is to get a total as close to one dollar as possible without going over. After each player has rolled, the player closest to $1 wins. Five rounds are played, and the player who wins the most rounds is the winner. This level teaches counting and adding coins.

In level two, each player starts with five dollars.  On each round they roll two money dice and pay the amount indicated to the bank by making change from one dollar. At the end of the round, the player with the most change left wins, and keeps his change.  All other players put their change back in the tray. At the end of five rounds, the player with the most money in his or her pile wins.  This level teaches making change from a dollar.

Level one offers a small amount of strategy (the press your luck mechanic of deciding whether to roll more dice and how many to roll) while level two is entirely luck.

Spinning a top knocks balls into holes numbered 1-8. The rules sheet indicates that each player should take 5 turns and the player with the largest number of points wins.

The rules do no indicate how many players but more than 4 seems unwieldy.

One game seen has 7 red balls and one yellow ball. The yellow ball was noted to be of a different material. In some games a special colored ball scores in some special manner such as double point value. However since the rules don't stipulate any such special scoring presumably the game originally had 8 red balls.

Time Bingo is an educational game designed to teach children to read analog clock faces. This game plays the same as traditional bingo, but with a twist. Each player is given a card with a series of different-looking analog clock faces depicted--with Roman numerals, Arabic numerals or no numbers. The caller then draws cards with times depicted in digital format. The called may either read the time aloud, show the card to the player or do both. Players must then read the clock faces on their card and mark any matching times. First player to complete a row or column of clock faces calls bingo. The caller then checks to make sure they have made the correct matches, and if so declares that player the winner. If not, play continues until a row or column is correctly completed.

boardgame: King Down: Keepers of the King
description:
Expansion to King Down which contains 20 new miniatures and 20 new character cards.

New minis are Bow (Archer), Bash (Paladin), Block (Guard), Beast (Beast) and Cog (Maester). A motley crew of powerful but capricious characters.

Bow (a.k.a The Archer) can "take" pieces from a distance, without moving, while Beast (a.k.a The Beast) cannot move, unless it is taking another piece. Bash (a.k.a The Paladin), on the other hand, can move quite freely, yet he himself is taken whenever it takes. Block (a.k.a The Guard) cannot take, yet no other piece can take him down. As for Cog (a.k.a The Maester), well let's just say he's a very resourceful guy...

Simple game with a spinning top to knock over four "bowling pins" and score points. Two pins are on 5 points and two pins are on 10 points.

The scoring board is enclosed in a wooden frame. A twist is that between where the top is dropped and the scoring "bowling pins" are four pegs which are fixed to the board. These pegs cause the top to bounce around.

The top is a wind-up top in two parts. The bottom metal part of top has the round top with a metal spindle. The spindle fits into the wooden upper part which has a spring inside the center hole and a pin on the outside edge to interlock with the bottom part. Thus one can wind up the top and holding the top between two fingers you can push the top loose by pushing down with a third finger.

A simple race game for children. The players use marbles as markers. A die, d6 numbered 1-6, is used to control the number of spaces to move. A twist is that some holes are large enough for the marble to fall though the board and the marble must be started again.

Scoots are ball bearings inside a plastic cone. Cone is big enough that ball bearing rolls inside the cone.

There are two blue scoots, two red scoots, and one yellow scoot used as a target for the game Scoot.

Scoots game package has three games:

Scoots:
Players shoot their "scoot" towards target and try to knock opponent out of the way. Closet to target wins.

Five Little Indians
Players try to knock over cardboard Indians for points. Each Indian has a different point value.

Marble Arch:
Where Indians are punched out leaves holes in bottom edge of cardboard that become a foldout target. Players try to shoot their scoot through the scoring holes for points.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Wrath of the Righteous Adventure Deck 2 – Sword of Valor
