description: Take back the fallen city of Drezen and reclaim lost treasures of the faith with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Sword of Valor Adventure Deck. This 110-card expansion adds new locations, monsters, villains, and more to the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game; it also includes rare loot cards and five new scenarios that make up the complete "Sword of Valor" adventure.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

boardgame: Run, Fight, or Die!: Big Box
description:
The Run, Fight, or Die!: Big Box includes the Running Late But We're Worth It expansion inside.

It is designed to hold the following five parts of this series plus promos:


    Base game
    Running Late expansion
    Horde expansion
    5/6 player expansion
    Co-op expansion (expansion pack 3)
    Kickstarter promos


The Running Late expansion includes 4 new female characters, a new custom die, 4 new location cards, 12 new loot cards and rules.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Knock-off uses the frenzied flipping of two electrical panic buttons by each of two contestants as competition. Object of button play is to direct Big Bopper, a grinning 6 armed ball-shaped robot, as he moves to knock off your opponent's fall guy before he can knock off yours.

Big Bopper runs across what looks like a raised bridge and the two fall guys are at either end. Wires from switches for control run along outside bottom edge of the bridge walls.

The toy is battery operated.
Players strap a pad with suction cups to their feet and try to pick up rubber dics on the floor which have point values. High score when all discs have been picked up is the winner.

The cards are divided between the forces of good and evil i.e. He-Man and Allies against the cohorts of Skeletor.

Each player gets 4 cards and another card is revealed. If you card is a 'Good' character, the first player must play a 'Bad' character to win the trick and vice-versa. Players draw a new card and the top card of the deck is revealed again, and play continues. The player who wins the most tricks after the last card is revealed from the deck, wins the game.

Cat On Yer Head is a crowd game that starts with a few simple rules that can then be modified while it is played. An imaginary cat, passed between players, chases an imaginary mouse who must escape it and eat cheese; or hide down a mouse hole; or turn into a cat-eating monster – or whatever the players invent. Because that’s the aim of the game: to build upon its basic rules, redesign it on the fly and make it sillier while you play.

The game is now available in digital and print editions as a 48-page illustrated storybook. The book includes full instructions so that anyone can play, fun extra features and details of numerous popular variants in five sections:


     Getting Started
     Extending the Game: Cheese
     Extending the Game: Mouse Holes
     More Features
     Adding Your Own Rules


A free sampler PDF including the Getting Started section is also available.

All versions are available from www.catonyerhead.com

Players listen to a story being read from the included booklet. At specific points the players can turn 1 memory card trying to find the one with the illustration that belongs to the specific point in the story. If a player turns the wrong memory card this card is placed back (face down). If the correct memory card is correct he/she can keep it and the story continues until a next memory card needs to be found. Now the player left of the player that found the correct memory-card can turn a card first. This continues until all memory cards have been collected by the players. The player with the most cards wins the game.

Players build a diorama from the box by putting the 2 side panels between the box lid and bottom. Players build the starting scene by placing some 5 or 6 (of the in total 16) playing pieces into the slots inside the box. Now all players try to memorize the scene before 1 player turns away the scene and adds a playing piece to the scene. Now the scene is turned back. The rest of the 
players now try to find out which playing piece has been added.

The first player to state which playing piece has been added scores 1 point. Now the next player can add a playing piece to the scene etc. etc. until a player scored 3 points and wins the game. Players can also start with a lot of playing pieces in the slots and remove a piece in each turn. A combination of these 2 versions is obviously the hardest way to play the game.

Simple children's golf themed race game. Players use dice for movement along track.

Boxtop has stylized chimp in white shirt with white/red check pants and cap in a backswing with a driver to hit a golf ball. Stylized fairway scene in background.

Aggressive worker placement. Control a tribe of stone creatures fighting, mining & building in the heart of an asteroid. 1 to 4 players. Caverns & Crystals is a worker placement game that relies on direct conflict between players as each tries to gather the most resources and build their tribe of Stone Imps to its maximum potential.

The board is made up of six hexagonal cards in a circle representing an asteroid drifting through space. As the game progresses (depending upon the turn cards that are placed in the center)three resources are placed upon the cards: Stone, used for spawning more members of your tribe, Metal, used to beat your opponent into submission and precious power crystals which will win you the game! By tactically placing your Stone Imps onto different Cavern Cards on the board you can dominate an area and ensure it is your imps who bring the crystals home. Be careful however, as somewhere in that asteroid there is a very hungry space squid placed secretly by another player that will devour your tribe and a vacuum that will eject them into space!
Most importantly you have an Imp king leading your tribe who will give your imps a dramatic boost when used with forethought. At the beginning of the turn a set of six cards is passed around to each player who takes one secretly. Each card gives your king a power to use for that turn whether it is sneaking into an opponents home to take their resources or surprising your opponents by teleporting your tribe at the last minute. You will have to think very carefully as you select your card and ponder what your opponents have chosen.       
Caverns & Crystals can even be played Solo where it ceases to be a game of deducing your opponents' actions and becomes an intense puzzle where a player must be as efficient as possible to succeed. The Game Itself comes in a small tin ensuring it remains safe and undamaged between plays.

So do you have what it takes to build your tribe and Rule the Rock!

You and the other players are patrons at a strange and bizarre open market. You are all are here to indulge in your obsessions, whether they be artifacts, guns, or art. Vendors display their wares of various shapes, sizes, and legality. You wander up and down the aisles, searching for that which strikes your fancy. Suddenly, you hear a scream, and see the body. Somebody has been murdered! There is a killer on the loose, and they will stop at nothing to get their hands on what they want.

In Obsession for Murder, players have secret identities that determine what types of Items that player wants to collect. One of these identities is The Murderer, who tries to eliminate the other players and be the only player left. The other players, while attempting to collect their chosen items, can also accuse each other of being The Murderer. If they are correct, they win. If they are wrong, the accused is lynched, and play continues until the Murderer wins or is correctly accused.

There are 4 different moving playing pieces in two colors: 1 favorite, 1 herold, 5 servants and 4 mounted knights. The players have to get their favorite into the opposite castle or capture the enemy favorite to win the game. On the game board, which looks like a chess board,  only the red and green spaces are used. Movement is diagonal. The brown spaces in the middle of the game board between the red und the green area are safe spaces (Asyl).

The favorite moves 1 space in any direction. 
The herold moves 1 or 2 spaces in any direction.
The mounted Knights move 2 spaces in any direction and can jump over other piecess, they may not occupy Asyl. 
The servants move 1 space in any direction. Knights and servants may not move backwards till their own favorite has passed Asyl and is on the enemy half of the game board.
Any piece can capture any enemy piece if it moves onto the space it is occupying.

An electric version of the Jack Straws game. The twist in this game is that metal tweezers are used to pick up the straws through a metal lid. Touching the lid with the tweezers sets off the alarm as in the electric game Operation.

The straws have various colors and a point value is assigned to each color.

There were multiple version of this game.

Originally offered as a bonus for preordering the Alien Frontiers: Outer Belt expansion, it was also made available for purchase upon the Outer Belt's release.

Components


     3 Alien Tech Cards (for use with Alien Frontiers)
         Neutrino Missile Salvo
         Pocket Universe
         Ring Repeater
    
     2 Salvage Cards (for use with Alien Frontiers: Outer Belt)
         Micro-Singularity
         Tactical Meteoroids
Ars Victor: First Wave Promo is a set of four promo tiles for use with Ars Victor.  The front side contains tokens for Warlords and Fanatics, while the back of the tokens are Bannermen and Champions.

boardgame: The Red Dragon's Lair: Secret Passage
description:
The Red Dragon's Lair: Secret Passage is a fantasy adventure for two players in which you become a Warrior or Witch Doctor on a quest to slay an evil dragon. You control the actions of a daring adventurer finding the way through a deadly jungle filled with monsters, and treacherous traps. In the mysterious dark an deadly jungle your quest continues against the evil forces that oppose you. Your daring efforts to reach the Red Dragon's Lair is where your adventure begins and your quest awaits.

Your goal is to survive the jungle and find The Secret Passage, enter the Red Dragon's Lair and slay the Red Dragon. You and the other players join together looting monsters and plundering the dungeon in search of magical items and weapons!

This game is a character-driven game, which uses both cards and dice to deal with the many obstacles within the dungeon and the Red Dragon's Lair. Players play cards from their hands and roll dice to complete their goal.

Players choose a character deck at the beginning of the game then equip their special items and weapons. Each character is unique and uses different styles of play.

Working together players battle monsters and avoid traps on their way to the Red Dragon's Lair. If you slay the Red Dragon you win the game!

In addition to being a standalone two-player game, The Red Dragon's Lair: Secret Passage functions as an expansion for The Red Dragon's Lair.

boardgame: Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem – Grim Bastards Club Expansion
description:
Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem – Grim Bastards Club Expansion introduces 'The Grim Bastards' Motorcycle Club to your games of Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem, allowing up to 5 players to play at once (or 6 players if you already own the Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem – Calaveras Club Expansion).

It includes everything you need to add one additional player to your game, including 'The Grim Bastards' Clubhouse Card, 1 Player Blind, 10 Plastic Dudes (Orange), 87 Additional Tokens (Money, Actions, Heat), 1 Custom Dice, Rules and Black Market Reference Cards for 3/4 & 5/6 Players.

NOTES

+  The 'Grim Bastards' Clubhouse Card was also included on its own as a promotional bonus in first print copies of Sons of Anarchy: Men of Mayhem. It only included the Grim Bastard Clubhouse Card (nothing else) and could be used as a substitute for one of the other Clubs.

Some GenCon attendees also got the opportunity to gain the Orange Dudes & Dice featured in this expansion, but again they were a promotional bonus only and lacked the other contents featured in this full expansion.

+  The Grim Bastards Club Expansion also includes the Black Market 3/4 Player Reference Card missing from many first print copies of the base game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Game: Sons of Anarchy – Men of Mayhem

description:
Renaissance Wars were a series of conflicts that involved most of the city states of Italy in a struggle for power and were marked with numerous alliances, betrayals and battles.

"Cattura" means "to capture" and that's the goal of the game, capture cards from the battlefield and score the most victory points at the end of the battle.

During a turn a player can either place a card on the battlefield, resolving any actions in the red zone of the card or capture a card from the battlefield, rezolving any actions in the blue zone of the card.

A game ends when either a player captures four cards in front of him or a player has no more cards in his hand.

Scoring is based on the value of captured cards cards and the combinations of crests

1.000 questions, that will bring to life your most vivid memories!

Everything that you and your friends loved from the greek 80’s, 90’s and 00’s! TV shows, Movies, Actors, Singers, Hits, TV spots, Cartoons, pick-up lines and everything you can imagine!
 You heard about it. You lived through it. You have it in your hearts! If you forgot it, now is the time to bring it all back with this game of memories! Alone or with your teammates, choose wisely your questions, according to your opponents, and prepare yourself for answering your opponents questions.

Let’s BoardGame!

boardgame: Formula D: Circuits 6 – Austin & Nevada Ride
description:
Formula D: Circuits 6 – Austin & Nevada Ride is a double-sided game board that adds two new tracks to Formula D. The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas was specially designed to accommodate Formula One cars and has been proudly hosting the United States Grand Prix since 2012. Racers, rev your engines on this highly challenging professional circuit!

A formidable second track based in Las Vegas and through Nevada provides racers with even more thrills! From the famous Bellagio to the Valley of Fire, racers have to survive the death-defying jump over the Colorado River in their attempt to win!

Mark of the Werewolf is a game of deduction, accusations, bluffing, survival, and mob mentality involving two teams: Villagers and Werewolves. The Villagers don’t know who the Werewolves are, and the Werewolves are trying to devour the Villagers one by one before the Villagers discover who they are.

Mark of the Werewolf takes place over a series of “Day” and “Night” Phases.

NIGHT: During the night the Werewolves pick an innocent Villager as their victim, eliminating them from the game. The Seer will pick a player and learn if they are a Werewolf or not. The Moderator will lead the players through the Night Phase of the game following a script provided.

DAY: During the day, the players try to discover who the Werewolves are. They do this by lynching one player during each Day Phase in an attempt to eliminate the Werewolves from the Village before the Werewolves kill all of the Villagers. Once a player has been lynched he must remain silent for the rest of the game. It is often best to have lynched players get up and watch from an area close by.

Winning the Game
Repeat the “Day” and “Night” phases until all Werewolves have been killed, or the number of Werewolves is equal to the number of Villagers (at which time the Werewolves overtake the village and win the game).

Zug um Zug: Deutschland – Deutschland 1902 is an expansion for Zug um Zug: Deutschland — itself a reworking of Ticket to Ride: Märklin — that adds new tickets to the game providing for more variety during play. There are also passengers, positioned in cities before the start of the game. During the game, players collect passengers to score even more bonus points for the most passengers in given color.

Part of Ticket to Ride series.

Triskelion is a connection game for three players that is played on a hexagon-shaped board made of hexagonal cells.  A distinctive feature of the game is that playing pieces are shared by all players, that is, players do not each have their own color of pieces.  Instead, each player is associated with two opposites sides of the board.

Only white and black stones are used. The board starts empty. Taking turns clockwise, on his turn each player drops a single stone onto any empty space. The color of the stone must be different from the previous stone placed (thus, the stones alternate). The first stone to be played is black.

A player wins in either of two ways: (1) By connecting his/her two sides with a chain of stones of either color (i.e. a black chain or a white chain); OR (2) by making a Y shaped chain that connects one of his/her sides with each of his/her opponents' sides on the opposite half of the board.

The name Triskelion (pronounced try-SKEL-ee-un) is the name of a Celtic motif design that consists of three curved branches radiating from a center.

boardgame: C'est pas Sorcier: Mystères de la science 2
description:
Discover the mysteries of science.

The player in his turn draws a card and announces the theme. He reads the first clue, then the next until any other player finds the right answer and wins the card. The first player who has a card of each theme wins.

There are 40 2-sided picture cards with 5 themes such a: 
ecology, geology, mathematics, medicine etc.

boardgame: C'est pas Sorcier: Mystères de la science
description:
Discover the mysteries of science.

The player in his turn draws a card and announces the theme. He reads the first clue, then the next until any other player finds the right answer and wins the card. The first player who has a card of each theme wins.

There are 40 2-sided picture cards with 5 themes such a: chemistry, physics, biology, astronomy, archeology.

boardgame: Les énigmes des Chevaliers et Châteaux-Forts
description:
Tournaments and fortresses, to you to win the battle!

The player in his turn draws a card and announces the theme. He reads the first clue, then the next until any other player finds the right answer and wins the card. The first player who has a card of each theme wins.

There are 40 2-sided picture cards with 5 themes such a: 
castle and city, nobility, knights etc.

During the medieval goings-on around Orléans, you must assemble a following of farmers, merchants, knights, monks, etc. to gain supremacy through trade, construction, and science in medieval France.

In the city of Orléans and the area of the Loire, you can take trade trips to other cities to acquire coveted goods and build trading posts. You need followers and their abilities to expand your dominance by putting them to work as traders, builders, and scientists. Knights expand your scope of action and secure your mercantile expeditions. Craftsmen build trading stations and tools to facilitate work. Scholars make progress in science, and last but not least it cannot hurt to get active in monasteries, since with monks on your side, you are much less likely to fall prey to fate.

In Orléans, you will always want to take more actions than possible, and there are many paths to victory. The challenge is to combine all elements as best as possible with regard to your strategy.

The Orléans: Deluxe Edition contains the following upgrades to the base game:

     47 metal coins for use in place of money tokens
     104 wooden discs and stickers for use in place of worker tokens
     16 wooden wheels for use in place of technology tokens
     14 wooden meeples for use in place of citizen tokens
     4 custom meeples for use as individual player markers
     Orléans: Tavern & Depot tiles
     90 wooden tokens for use in place of goods tokens
     Black pieces for fifth player


The deluxe edition is a single print run limited edition that will not be manufactured again.
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Hupsis is a dexterity game, where a card board waiter tries to keep his balance and not to drop any plates from his tray.

At the start of the game you have to assemble the card board waiter, which is swinging on a stand. You also have a common stock of card board plates, with different foods printed on them. At the start of the game every player gets few banknotes and when you run out of money, you drop out of the game. Last player with money wins the game.

On your turn you add a plate in to waiters tray. If the waiter swings and plates drop, then you must pay him a tip. If plates don't drop, then you succeeded. In both cases it's the next players turn to add plate. This goes on until only one player has money.

In El Grande, players take on the roles of Grandes in medieval Spain. The king's power is flagging, and these powerful lords are vying for control of the various regions. To that end, you draft caballeros (knights in the form of meeples) into your court and subsequently move them onto the board to help seize control of regions. After every third round, the regions are scored, and after the ninth round, the player with the most points is the winner.

In each of the nine rounds, you select one of your 13 power cards to determine turn order as well as the number of caballeros you get to move from the provinces (general supply) into your court (personal supply).

A turn then consists of selecting one of five action cards which allow variations to the rules and additional scoring opportunities in addition to determining how many caballeros to move from your court to one or more of the regions on the board (or into the castillo—a secretive tower). Normally, you may only place your caballeros into regions adjacent to the one containing the king's pawn. The one hard and fast rule in El Grande is that nothing may move into or out of the king's region. One of the five action cards that is always available each round allows you to move the king to a new region. The other four action cards vary from round to round.

The goal is to have a majority of caballeros in as many regions (and the castillo) as possible during a scoring round. Following the scoring of the castillo, you place any meeples you had stashed there into the region you had secretly indicated on your region dial. Each region is then scored individually according to a table printed in that region. Two-point bonuses are awarded for having sole majority in the region containing your Grande (large meeple) and in the region containing the king.

El Grande Big Box, the 20th anniversary edition of El Grande, includes all previously published expansions: Grand Inquisitor & Colonies, Grandissimo, King & Intrigant, King & Intrigant: Players' Edition and King & Intrigant: Special Cards as well as something currently known only as the "Anniversary Extension".

description:
User summary:

Marionette Kingdom is a role selection game with a draft mechanism and limited random card drawing.

From a deck of 9 possible roles, each player gets 3 cards, chooses one and sends the other two to the player on their left, selects one of the ones he got, and discards the last. In clockwise turn order, the players then choose one of their taken roles to activate, gathering resources, buying buildings with special effects and/or worth victory points. The first player to 10 points wins.

Marionette Kingdom is a follow-up to the game Greedy Kingdoms, and contains some aspects similar to Bruno Faidutti's Citadels.

The game also contains rules for playing An honourable act, which is the Greedy Kingdom mechanism applied to the cards in this game.

User summary:

A quirky card game with a trick-taking-ish mechanism. Players are dealt ingredient cards numbered –1 to 4, with varying colours. These are played, in turn, in a kettle that can hold up to a certain total. when the kettle is filled, the player who played the last card on the kettle distributes the ingredients to all players in a certain order. Then, the players in order get to use their ingredients to create magic items from a lineup.

The first player to create all five types of magic items, or who has most types when the game end conditions are met, wins the game.

User summary:

A roll-and move dice-driven battle board game based on character from Project Shrine Maiden.

The players walk around on the board, defeating characters and gaining them for their team, finally trying to take on the final boss.
family: Video Game Theme: Project Shrine Maiden

description:
Old dogs can be taught new tricks! Let’s find out how incredible those doggy acrobats can be!

All rules remain the same as the base game with the following modifications:
1.Deal one Red and one Green Ball card to each player.
2.Put the Acrobat cards from the base game back to the game box. They won’t be used in the game.
3.Some Acrobat cards show fewer dogs on them. When completing these cards, simply put unused Doggy tiles aside.
4.Scoring: 
(1)Each Acrobat card is worth 1 VP.
(2)Each set of three Acrobat cards of different colors grants 3 VP.
(3)The player with the most Acrobat cards of the same color gains 5 VP. If tied, no players gain the bonus.

Simple children's game which combines Bingo with a top.

The bingo aspect is that the winner is trying to mark a line of three on a 5x3 card of animals. Instead of "Bingo!" the player shouts "Pink Elephant!" Winner of the particular game gets point chips from other player depending on circumstances. PLayers then shuffle cards and start another round.

Overall winner is player who has the most points at the conclusion of play. (The rules are nebulous on how many rounds should be played.)

Another twist in this game is how the squares to be covered are to be selected. Instead of a draw bag as in Bingo a top is used. On the tops spindle are placed two plastic discs. One red and one white. The top with the two discs is spun in the game box bottom which has a gridded board with various colored animals in it.

The red disc indicates what color of animal should be covered and the white disc indicates what animal. There are also provisions in the rules for covering multiple spaces. As an added twist it is acceptable to nudge the top to try and get it to throw a disc off in a particular direction!

Game description from the publisher:

Life is either a daring adventure or nothing. You're not sure which, but at least you've narrowed it down. You are rich with life experiences, but have had trouble trading them for goods and services.

It's time to seek your fortune, or anyone's really — whoever's is closest. To the west there's a land of milk and honey, full of giant bees and monstrous cows; to the east, a land of eggs and licorice; to the north, treacherous swamps; to the south, loyal jungles. But all of them have been thoroughly pillaged. You've heard legends, though, of a fifth direction as yet unspoiled, with its treasures conveniently gathered into troves. You have your sword and your trail mix, handed down from your father, and his father before him. You've recruited some recruits and hired some hirelings; you've shined your armor and distressed a damsel. You put up a sign saying "Gone Adventuring". Then you put up another sign, saying "Beware of Dog", in case people get any ideas. You're ready. You saddle up your trusty steed, and head florst.

Dominion: Adventures, the ninth addition to the game of Dominion, contains 400 cards, 60 tokens and six mats. This expansion has 30 new Kingdom cards, including the return of Duration cards that do things on future turns, plus Reserve cards that can be saved for the right moment. There are also 20 Event cards that give you something to buy besides cards, including tokens that modify cards.

Part of the Dominion series.

description:
Made From Scratch is a culinary card game in which players compete to complete recipes.  The cards that comprise a player's hand consist primarily of ingredients; such as poultry, butter, tomatoes, and spices. In playing those ingredient cards and providing more of the ingredients on a recipe than their opponents, a player gets to claim the recipe and bring it into their prep area. Limited kitchen space forces chefs to try and prep, cook, and score their recipes before they burn.

Made From Scratch captures the essence and complexity of preparing gourmet dishes and packages it in an easy to learn game that is guaranteed to leave you hungry for more.

boardgame: La Vie est une Jungle: Trahir est un plaisir
description:
A trivia game where you can team up for a while with another player to answer the questions together and advance twice faster.
The twist is that the first one to break the alliance will continue alone, while the unfortunate partner will be send with the last player on the board...

User summary:

This is a Lost Legacy set that was handed out as a promo sheet (2 A4 sheets) on Game Market spring 2013. It runs on the same system as the other Lost Legacy sets, and contains no special rules.

Please refer to the family entry for details on the various Lost Legacy sets.

User summary:

This Lost Legacy set was first handed out as a promo sheet (2 A4 sheets) on Game Market fall 2014 in Tokyo. The customers had to cut out and sleeve the cards themselves.

Please refer to the Family entry for details on the publications of the different sets.

Kite Fight includes three games in one box, with players using the kite, wind and lightning cards in different ways depending on what they're playing. The games included are:

• Catch the Wind — Imagine playing in a park with the land divided into quadrants by wind cards. Over the course of the game, players place kite cards in different parts of the park (that is, the space between wind cards) to try to have the highest valued kite cards on top of the stack, but the first card played in a quadrant determines which cards can be played to that area subsequently. Designer Jason Kotarski describes this game as "sort of a twist on trick-taking with players playing to multiple tricks at once over multiple rounds".

• Ride the Skies — Wind cards fill the air (or rather the table as they're laid out in a grid), and players place their kite cards on top of them to ride the wind. The trick, though is that the card being placed must be equal to the sum of the card being played on and one of the adjacent cards in the grid. Once all of the wind cards are covered, players check each pile to see who played the most cards on each pile, awarding the wind card to that player.

• Full of Air — Catch the most wind cards by participating in kite fights and outnumbering everyone else. To set up, a few wind cards are placed face up on the table, then players take turns placing a kite card at either end of the row, creating a sequence of 1-10 in each row. When a row is complete, whoever contributes the most claims the wind card from that row. Lightning cards let you zap another player's card and replace it with one of your own.

boardgame: Lost Legacy: The Qualities of an Adventurer
description:
User summary:

This expansion, of unknown compatibility with all Lost Legacy sets, was first handed out as a promo sheet on Tokyo Game Market spring 2014. Unlike the other promo sheets, this is not a separate set, but a set of 8 special ability cards that are possible to use with any Lost Legacy set and that gives the player a special ability throughout the game.

The promo status of this expansion was primarily because it wasn't fully tested and the designer was not sure it wouldn't completely break certain sets or combinations of sets.

User summary:

A half unofficial Lost Legacy set, based on the Japanese indie board game scene, where every card represents an indie board game publisher.

Please refer to the family entry for details on the publication of various Lost Legacy sets.

In Big Easy Business, 2-4 players are aspiring business moguls in New Orleans buying up businesses and attracting tourists in order to prove their savvy and best the competition. They must compete to buy and utilize nightclubs, popular restaurants, tourist attractions, trinket shops, street bands, and more.

Players begin the game as one of several possible businesses, all with unique abilities. During a round, custom dice are rolled to create the action pool for all players. As players take their turns, they select a die for its action and execute it. Depending on what you choose, you'll be attracting tourists or cashing them out, buying new businesses (with new abilities), hiring new employees, etc. Combine that with blocking opponents out of actions, taking advantages of citywide events, and playing in a high-stakes poker game using the tableau you build...and you have Big Easy Business.

User summary:

A half unofficial Lost Legacy set, based on the Japanese indie board game scene, where every card represents an indie board game publisher.

The Lost Legacy in this set is "Lost Legacy Legend".

Please refer to the family entry for details on the publication of various Lost Legacy sets.

For thousands of years the Dragons have ruled the world of Erien, but their domain has become infested by other races - Elves, Dwarves, Fae, and worst of all, humankind. The dragons have done what they can to slow the process but the humans bred like rabbits and the other races weren’t much better. It didn’t take long for the inevitable conflict over resources to begin and the dragons soon realized that they didn’t stand a chance against the combined strength of the new races. Those dragons that survived are now forced to fight amongst themselves for food, a home, breeding grounds and anything else they want. They have taken to the skies to battle it out with others of their kind, knowing only the strongest amongst them will survive to become the lords of the skies.

Players take on the role of Dragons as they battle against one another in the skies over Erien. Their old sense of pride and self-importance mean that they must have the best feeding grounds available even if it means fighting other dragons to get it.

The game uses a system where at the start of each turn the players plot out their dragon’s movement for the turn. Movement is truly 3D allowing the dragons to fly up, down, left, right or any combination of directions. Elevation is tracked with stacking tiles and turns are shown with angled tilt blocks. Then with a bit of luck as their plans unfold on the game map their plans will have put them in to a better position to strike either with breath weapon or claw.

The Enchanter is a special, promotional package that is being offered exclusively to BoardGameGeek. This package includes the Enchanter along with 10 power cards, plus a new event and an artifact. A must-have for all Darkest Night fans!

boardgame: Sentinels of the Multiverse: Villains of the Multiverse
description:
Description from the publisher:

The many villains from the Multiverse have been bested by the Freedom Five or Dark Watch or the Prime Wardens or even heroes not from a particular team. Many of those villains are prideful and would not deign to work with others, as their goals would not align. However, some of them have seen the value of cooperating towards a common goal, and those villains have joined forces, even at cross purposes, to defeat the heroes who have harried them for so long.

Some of these villains, such as Plague Rat or Biomancer, are more hesitant to work with other villains, whereas others, such as Citizens Hammer & Anvil or Sergeant Steel, are more than happy to join forces with other like-minded combatants.

Villains of the Multiverse is a collection of ten super-powered villains for Sentinels of the Multiverse, and rather than being standalone villains, they work as part of a team of villains in the play style introduced in Sentinels of the Multiverse: Vengeance to group villains together as a team fighting against the hero team. Additionally, Villains of the Multiverse includes four new environments that can be used in any Sentinels of the Multiverse game.

description: Inspired by Homer’s Iliad, PERGAME is a turn based strategy game that simulates the battle between Achaean and Trojan forces during the Trojan War. Keeping true to the spirit of time and the epic, the game focuses on the actions of heroes and constant involvement of the gods who are always ready to lend a hand to one side or the other. Common soldiers also have a role to play, and initially, their role comes down to guarding their champion. But beware, without a phalanx to support him, even Achilles may be felled by the hand of well-trained rank and file troops. During the battle, each player will try to defeat the opposing army in the field or to push it back to its base – Troy, or the Achaean camp. And then, when the going gets rough, and the battlements have to be breached, every single warrior is precious. Only good coordination of all elements – the power of heroes, the much needed support of the soldiers and timely interventions of the gods, can secure victory. Tyche be with you!

Spin the spinner and move your playing piece clockwise around the board. Object of the game is to collect he most Gargoyle character playing cards as possible and to get rid of as many Xanatos and/or Demona cards collected during the game as you can before the game ends.

After all 32 cards in the deck have been played, players may disregard all spaces landed on except the Elisa Maza space. As soon as a player lands on any Castle Space the game ends. The winner is the player with the most points at the end of the game. Points are scored by number of Gargoyle character cards you have. Each card is worth one point. If any players have Xanatos and/or Demona cards, subtract one point each from your score.
An edition of Monopoly specifically geared towards China. This version plays the same as the regular Monopoly, though the sets are named after well-known places throughout China.

A Bandersnatch is a 3D figure with feet, a body, a tail and a nose. To win the players are trying to build Bandersnatch in rounds as in Cootie. But there are a couple of twists to the simpler game of Cootie.

To win a chance on getting a body part the player must win a spinner. The catch is that there is one less spinner than there are players. So in each round one player won't get a chance to obtain a Bandersnatch part.

The players all grab for a spinner when any player builds a hand of 4 cards of the same color. So there is an action/dexterity element to the game too.

Play begins with dealing 4 cards to each player. On a signal of go from the dealer all players look at their cards. If no one has four of a kind then each player passes card card face down to player on left. Again on go player pick up card and dtermine if they have four of a kind.

4:20 is a dice rolling game for 1 to 4 players. The aim of the game is to roll as many joints from your boss’s stash as possible.
But what fun is that unless you get to dabble in the goods?
By the time the clock hits 4:20am the player with the most J’s is declared King of Toke!
Just make sure you stay awake! Take too many puffs and risk ‘greening out’.
Move around the apartment, gather Joints, feed the munchies by raiding the refrigerator,
session up in the living room, or strip down and jump in the hot tub. The night is yours!

Setup:
Place the Board in the middle of the table. Place the time marker on the 4:20pm square of the time track.
Shuffle the Kitchen, living room, Hot Tub and Bedroom card decks and place each deck beside the corresponding room on the board.
Place the Joint tokens beside the board in reach of everyone.
Each player chooses a character and takes the corresponding player board and avatar.
The person who’s the biggest stoner starts. If you can’t decide who that is – play rock paper scissors to decide.
The Starting player places there character on any room in the house (except the bathroom). In clockwise order each player places their character in any empty room they choose (once again, not the bathroom). Only one player can be in any room at the same time.

How to Play
The starting player takes the 5 dice and rolls them all. They can then put aside any die they wish to keep (this is called “locking”). The player has 2 rerolls (unless stated otherwise like in “The Bedroom”) so can roll the dice 3 times in total per turn. After your rerolls you apply your results.
Each Turn Consists of
Resolve room power on board if it states “Start of Turn”
Roll all Dice. Put aside any dice you wish to keep (All clock dice are automatically “locked” – see below)
Reroll (this is optional) any dice up to 2 times except “locked” dice. They must be put aside.
Resolve dice (see below for results and order)
 The Dice Results are as follows

    J = Take one J token (1 per J)
    M = Move. You must move to another location in the apartment. If it is occupied by another player they must move to your location as only one player per room is allowed. If 2 M’s are rolled you do not move and must take a card from the room deck you are in. If 3 M’s are rolled and you have no rerolls left you must move to the bathroom square. Your turn is over and disregard any other dice results except clock rolls. (do not collect J’s or dish out puffs)
    C= Clock. Time passes and for every C rolled the time marker moves one square up. Every C rolled is instantly “locked”. You cannot reroll any C results (unless a card states otherwise).
    P= Puff. All other players take a puff and must move their awake marker up one square on their player boards.


Order in a turn
When applying results, do so in the following order.
1               IF you have 3 “M’s” Proceed directly to bathroom , Ignore all other dice except clocks this turn. Advance clock if applicable and your turn ends.
2               Take all J’s
3               Everyone else takes Puffs
4               Move or take room card and resolve (if applicable)
5               Advance clock
6               End your turn. Pass dice to player on your left
 
Bathroom:
Nature called and you had to go to the bathroom. Damn, the locks broken.. How the hell do you get out you ask?! Getting stuck in the bathroom is no fun at all.
As soon as you enter the bathroom lose 5 J’s (they fell in!)
Roll dice as usual but.....Bathroom rolls are a bit different
No” J” results count when rolling dice, you can still make the others take puffs and the clock still ticks on as normal.

Escaping the Bathroom:
The only way out of the bathroom space (unless you have a card)  is by rolling and “locking” 2 “M’s”. You must then go to any empty room in the house and play on as normal. If you have any rerolls left this turn you may continue to roll unlocked dice. Resolve dice normally at the end of your turn (ignoring the 2 “M’s” used to escape the bathroom).
 Time / Awake Tracks / Game End
If any players awake track hits “white out” that player has fallen asleep and is out of the game. If all other players fall asleep the last player awake is the winner.
As soon as the time track hits 4:20am the game is over. All awake players add up their J’s and the stoner with the most wins! If that is also tied the most awake stoner wins....
If both J’s and Awake are tied..... geeeze?!?  Play again!!

In Reino de Ambar (Kingdom of Amber), players assume the roles of heads of influential families, politically and militarily, in the kingdom. In the shadows, players compete for prosperity and reputation. As head of the family, each player tries, through the skillful use of his seven cards Action, increase the power, influence and prestige of his family, avoiding in turn be destroyed by barbarian hordes who claim ravage the kingdom.

When you play an action card, you can add influence in places where influential people in the kingdom. So, for example, when you choose to increase its influence on the Market, Gold increases its access; or when providing care to the Castle, receives more influence; or when visiting the wicked witch who lives in the Cave removes a marker of influence of one of his opponents.

Each player along just seven rounds, following his own path, make its own choices with action cards from his hand to try to gain prestige.

In each round the players have also should try to solve two of the most serious problems plaguing the Kingdom. Whenever a problem is resolved their savior can enhance their prestige or position, use the special Confident or hire the services of places or personalities. In contrast, if the problems that appear in each round to win the barbarians are resolved. Rarely, the best choice is obvious, but often not be. A good player will find the right moments to make the best use of their cards and solve problems.

At the end of the game, the player with the most accumulated prestige is the winner.

Three Inch Glory is a wargame set in the 18th Century and designed for play with Playmobil® figures.

Requirements:

     two armies of 10 to 100 figures each
     a ruler for measuring formation maneuvers
     a 20-sided die or a computer for the spreadsheet calculator


End conditions differ by scenario, but the basic game is to play until an army surrenders or is eliminated.

Three Inch Glory is a wargame set in the 18th Century and designed for play with Playmobil® figures.

Requirements:

     two armies of 25 to 250 figures each
     a deck of cards for officer/orders activation
     a ruler for measuring formation maneuvers
     lots of 6 sided dice for resolving attacks


End conditions differ by scenario, but the basic game is to play until an army surrenders or is eliminated.

The third iteration of the game has been simplified from the previous edition. Notably, the rulebook is half the previous length.

From the designer:
A game of dinosaur hunting for ages 7 and up! These straightforward, fun rules take up an entire 2 pages, including character record sheets! Plenty of fun with miniature figures, toy dinosaurs and anything else you have lying about.

User note:
The game calls for a deck of cards for activation (though the specific rules are ambiguous). Uses 1-2 d6 with modifiers for most actions. Solo play is possible, because dinosaurs move relative to the nearest hunter.
Bugs! is a simple miniatures combat game played with plastic insects, tokens for goals (like candy), a few polyhedral dice, and a ruler. A player must get at least one bug on each goal to win.

boardgame: Come With Me If You Want To Live!
description:
Come With Me If You Want To Live! is a roll-and-move game with "take-that" cards.

Players take the role of leaders who seek to save a chosen few. Each is convinced that only they can save their followers from a coming apocalypse. Your goal is to have the most followers at the end of the game.

1936 - Spain divided, is based on the classic system of "A House Divided", modified to play modern warfare wargame.
After the failed coup, led by the army, against a weak republic, the Spanish territory and society, it is split almost exactly in half, into two opposing camps.
This is a game for two players, Nationalist and Republican.
The Nationalist army with better troops form North Africa, best foreign support of Hitler and Mussolini, get advance control of the country.
The Republican, despite having major cities, will have to organize a new army, on the basis of militia enlisted from the start of the war.
The objective of the game is that the Nationalis player controls  whole country before the end of the war. Otherwise the Republican wins the game.
It is also possible that the Republican player wins before the end of the game.
The game features a map of 25,5x22 , one 5/8?? counter sheet.
The army of players is divided into grades. These are, columns, regular, veterans and elite. The two armies will evolve during the game to better qualities or worse depending on conditions of each side.
The full game lasts 29 turns, each player starts with 14 combat units.
There aviation support units, artillery and armored.
Also special units such as moros, requetés, Italian, international militia etc ...

Do you follow all sports on TV or are just turned into a sportsnerd at the olympics? Are you an athlete or a couch potato? This is the quiz game that reveals if you are a champion or belong at the substitutes' bench. Contains 265 fun and tricky questions about sports divided into these categories:

"Snow & Ice", "The ball is round", "Olympic", "The Pro question" and "Misc".

The game gives some ideas on how to play the game but there are no strict rules.

All aboard for Fast Fun for Little Ones with UNO Choo-Choo Game. Preschoolers depart on an UNO adventure featuring a train filled with adorable animals. Play by matching color or animal cards, or play a conductor card he's wild and matches anything! When you have only one card left, cheer 'UNO Choo-Choo!' to let everyone know the winning train is leaving the station! Includes 28 cards, 4 caboose partitions and train. For 2 to 4 players. Ages 3 and older.

The Gods try to make the stars. The stars consist of the Angel, the Human, and the Devil. Sometimes you can see the Angel Human and the Devil Human.

The big star is the high score, but the biggest will be gone with the SUN. So the points are nothing.

KeYstoNe is a trick-taking game with a "must follow" rule. The winner of trick takes the cards and makes a line of the cards - either ascending or descending.

Each card has a number and points. The player pays points for each his lines and gets the points from the cards. The longest line is not scored.

After two games, the highest player wins the game.

The illustration is based on the contributors of the famous podcast "Board game laboratory". They are Kawasaki, Yoshida, and Naoe.

This is the released as one of the 500 YEN games event. The event is that the user's small event on the Game Market 2010 by Takuya.

This game is the trick taking game from OKAZU brand by Hisashi Hayashi(Trains, String railways and more).
In the Game Market 2015 Osaka, the Takamagahara will publish this game with the original rule and the original illustrations.

The rule is the "Must follow", "Fixed trump" and "Trick taking rule". But the scoring is related in the law of the savanna. The lion will take the other animal card. The grass-eating animal will take the grass. And the grass is the scored points.

This edition is re-producted at any points. The illustrations are re-illustrated for this with many respected to the original illustration, added the package illustrations.
At last, this is included in the new cards for the variant. The included special cards can be replaced their cards. Then you will see the new experience!
Thank you very much!

Adds solo play to Bullfrogs.

Includes:


     Full color rules sheet
     2 custom speckled dice

A game for 1 or more players who take it in turns to use the chopsticks to pick up pieces.

Take it in turns to throw the dice, whichever colour it lands on, use the chopsticks to pick up that coloured smiley face wooden block and place it on top of your opponents block to create a tower. Whoever knocks the tower over loses.

A party game where the players must complete activities from cards to reach the final space of the board.

The activities are all cumulative thru the end of the game and can be something like "put a ball in the middle of your arm", "maintain this card on your right elbow", "put a finger in your left ear", "sing a song", etc.

description:
Freak Show Shuffle is a quick card game.

In a series of timed rounds, trade cards from your hand face-down to other players.  At the end of the game, you'll score points by collecting as many copies of a specific circus "attraction" card.

There are special cards too: the "Ringmaster" is a wildcard used as any type of circus attraction card you want to increase your score.  There are also "Freaks!" in the deck, which detract from your score unless you shoot the moon with the "Talker" card. The Talker makes all your Freaks! count as attractions and add to your score.

boardgame: Montevideo y Maracaibo. Escenarios para Cien Cañones por Banda (combate naval 1750-1800)
description: Published in Alea magazine 31, add two new scenarios (Montevideo 1814 abd Maracaibo 1823) and new counters for Argentina, Colombia and Spain, and two correction counters for Cien Cañones por Banda.
Published in Alea magazine 32, add two scenarios and new counters for Cien Cañones por Banda (Algeciras July, 7 and July, 13 1801). also expands the rules for Gunboats and Shore Batteries.

DRK-Das Reich is the capstone game for the DRK-IISS game set.  That is the final game in the 3 game set.  This game builds on the first 2 in the set, DRK-Totenkopf and DRK-Leibstandarte.  This game focuses on the Das Reich sector of the southern part of the salient.  After a good start and rapid movement this sector of the front became quiet.  Das Reich, from 8 July, did little in the way of attacking.  DRK-Totenkopf, at a minimum (and DRK-Leibstandarte is recommended), is required to play this game.  The game system features the representation of units at Platoon and Battery level for the Germans and Company and Battery level for the Red Army.  The Command and Control and Fire Support Enhancements are built into this game, just as they are for DRK-Totenkopf and DRK-Leibstandarte.  The Command and Control Enhancement provides Company sized replacement units for the Germans and the representation of communications between HQ (the concept of Radio Line of Sight is used) and the HQ ability to respond on its own initiative when commo is bad.  The Fire Support Enhancement provides for artillery unit missions of Direct Support, General Support, and Reinforcing fires.  This helps to restrict the fire of artillery in the game to something more like what happens in real life.  Artillery cannot be gathered from around the map to support attacks or defense engagements.  It must be planned.
Both of the previous 2 games start the battle from 9 July.  This game does that and also provides a battle start, along the entire IISS front, from 4 July.  The same game design features, from DRK-Totenkopf and DRK-Leibstandarte, are included in this game.
There are 22 scenarios included.  These include the Das Reich scenarios for 9-13 July plus a couple of smaller regimental scenarios, and daily scenarios for each day from 4-8 July for all 3 divisions.  A real action packed monster.

DRK-IISS is the Enhancement package for the DRK-IISS game set. This package provides the charts and counters necessary to play the detailed, or enhanced, rules for Air, Maintenance, and Logistics, for the IISS Panzer Korps area of the battle.

The Air Enhancement provides the Air units and supporting charts and tables to recreate the air war in a more detailed manner. Air units can conduct Close Air Support (CAS), Combat Air Patrol (CAP), Escort, Interception, Airfield Ground Attack, Ground Attack (GA), and Interdiction missions.
The Maintenance Enhancement provides units that represent the Recovery units for recovering wrecks on the battlefield. Battlefield recovery can be tricky when the enemy is nearby. This is just one of the many issues you will need to consider when planning to recover wrecked units. And if you lose these units then your ability to get replacements is seriously hampered.
The Logistics Enhancement provides supply convoy units that are required to move supplies to units and around the battlefield and has a more natural use of supplies in the structure of unit organizations.

IISS Enhancement comes with (in Full Color):

       Air Operations Charts
       Logistics Charts
       Maintenance Charts
       600 Unit Counters and Markers

This is a great game, with plenty of detail, of the critical Battle of Quatre Bras fought between Marshall Ney and the Duke of Wellington on 16 June 1815.  The battle is an excellent example of a Meeting Engagement, which will provide you the ability to easily alter the flow of the battle, and its tempo.  The French II Corps, reinforced with the Light Cavalry of the Imperial Guard, is ordered to take the crossroads and send some troops to the east to help Napoleon, who is fighting the Prussians.  This game will smoothly connect to the Ligny battlefield maps when the Ligny game is released.  This game contains the Order of Battle charts, and supporting charts, for the entire 4 game set.

Scenarios include:

    The Battle of Quatre Bras - for those of you who want to play the entire event.
    15 June 1815 - for those who want a really small scenario that plays quickly.  The French Lancers of the Imperial Guard attempt to outmaneuver the small Dutch/Nassau force holding just north of Frasnes and take the crossroads in a quick battle.
    Counter-Attack at Thyle - for those who want a really small scenario that plays quickly.  The French must take the town of Thyle and the British must hold it.  Counter density is very low.
    Hooves of Destruction - where the French Cuirassiers and Lancers of Pire's 2nd Cavalry Division manage to catch a British Battalion unprepared for a cavalry charge and the unthinkable happens.
    The Guard Attacks - the British Guard has popped out of the Bois de Bossu and is hitting the French to push them south.  Their over-zealous pursuit puts them in a bad position when the French cavalry again begins some fine charges.


Key Features                                                                     

       (8) 11" x 17" game maps of the Quatre Bras area
      (8) Full Color Order of Battle Charts
     Numerous charts and tables
      Variable combat strengths
       Reduction by Levels of Order
       Restructured rules and Quatre Bras Rules Supplement with National Doctrinal Rules
       Approximately 1400 Die Cut Unit Counters and Markers
       Infantry and Cavalry units at the company and squadron level and artillery at the section level
       Battalion level infantry and Regiment level cavalry units
       (5) Scenarios that allow play on smaller parts of the battle or the entire affair
       Highly detailed without overbearing rules
      Excellent Solitaire Play


(from Grognard sim's website)

description:
Demi: Labyrinth of the Gods is a cooperative game where each player takes on the role of a modern day demigod (there are eight to choose from). These 2-4 players must then work together to navigate a labyrinth that shifts and changes as you play, while trying to accomplish the task of finding three ancient artifacts the gods have demanded in order to prove your worth and gain access to the exit - but there's a catch!  This labyrinth is filled with mythic monsters and traps...

This is a tile-based game.  A grid of thirty face-down tiles represent rooms within the labyrinth.  Players move together as one, flipping over a room to move into it, one room at a time.  To represent the fact that the Labyrinth is always in motion, when the players enter a new room they choose the orientation of that room (provided one entrance/exit points back the way they came).  They then replace the room they came from with a room from the next difficulty tier.

Within each room they draw from an encounter deck and face off against monsters out of Greek mythology, traps, or if lucky - a pile of resources they can use to upgrade their gear or utilize their special abilities.

Each of the eight demigods has three special abilities:  A Free Ability they can utilize anytime the text applies, a Cost Ability they can pay resources they've gathered to activate, and a Coin-Flip ability they receive later in the game, letting them tempt the fates for a positive or potentially negative outcome.

Winning means reaching opposite corners three times to gather the three artifacts, then back once more to find the exit - the shifting rooms that are constantly replaced as you progress means player tactics in room orientation to determine the route become a deciding factor in the group's ability to make it out alive.

Translated from the Box:

Once and for all decide which sex is the strongest!
De Jongens Tegen De Meisjes (The Boys Against the Girls) is a battle between men and women. In seven different rounds plus a final round two teams play against each other. Bet on the opponent's answers wisely and collect the most coins to win the game.

Seems to be a simple race game with dice. The set includes d6 dice, 6 plastic horses with jockey (yellow, white, black, green, red and blue) and a board with 6 lanes which has a number of spaces marked off. Guessing that the main game is a simple race to end of row and back which would be about 30 spaces.

Boxtop has a race trail rail from left right and curving around back to right. Above rail mostly red and below yellow.  "Thorobred" is at the top of the box, in yellow print on the red backtop. There are four horses showing on left side of box.

A dice-rolling game where you (and up to 5 more friends) explore an ancient dungeon with monsters and treasure! Defeat the boss monster, and win the game!

On each of your turn, navigate the dungeon to encounter monsters and treasure. If you encounter a monster, only one of you lives!

SQUASH the competition in the farming game that grows on you!

Share a plot of land between your friends and compete to harvest the most zucchini. During your turn, you have the choice to lengthen a vine or grow a zucchini in size. Once you grow a zucchini to 3 cards in length, draw an Action Card. You might get to fertilize your plants, kill your opponents' chances with blight, or harvest a vine for points. The first player to 30 points is the winner!

It's time to get growing.

Who can build the most AWESOMEST TreeFort?
August 1978 – no computers; no cable TV; no money; no girlfriend; and no grown-ups around to nag about chores! School’s gonna start next week and you’re sick and tired of hanging at the mall and the local swimming hole. “What do you wanna do today?”…”I don’t know, what do YOU wanna do?” “Hey, let’s build some tree forts!”

Tree Forts is a fast-paced and surprisingly strategic card/board game that will take you back to the days of lava lamps, Atari video game consoles, and lazy afternoons spent scavenging for stuff to add to your fort. Players compete to determine whose fort is best - but be careful - your neighbor might have some water balloons or a mud flinger to pelt your fort (and damage your chances of winning)!

Players start with a Pallet card and use candy and resources to purchase and build additions to their forts. Each addition has a point value and when you reach a certain point value you win. Player interaction occurs through mud flingers and water balloons that may be used to remove points from player's additions.

Werewolf: Full Moon is an expansion to Corey Fields' version of Werewolf.


     Instructions Book
     28 Cards
         2 Wolf Pup cards
         2 Spirit cards
         1 Elder card
         2 Skinwalker card
         2 Thief cards
         2 Zombie cards

Werewolf: All Hallows' Eve Expansion added 11 new cards to Corey Fields' version of Werewolf.


     22 cards, 2 each of the following
         White Wolf

Werewolf: Silent Night Expansion adds 23 Christmas themed cards to Corey Fields Kickstarter Werewolf deck.


     23 Cards, 2 each of the following cards

Let’s go see the Wizard of Oz

Based on the public domain book The Wonderful Wizard of Oz is a children’s novel written by L. Frank Baum and illustrated by W. W. Denslow.

Follow the Yellow Brick Road is a tabletop game. It is a bit like Candyland but with some strategy.

Help Dorothy, the Lion, the Scarecrow and the Tin Man in their quest to find the Wonderful Wizard of Oz. Follow a path with many challenges and stunning surprises in this fun game for 2 to 4 players aged between 9 and 99 year old (more or less).

Play a game in 15 to 30 minutes with a balance mix of chance and strategy. Will you be the first to reach the Emerald Castle? Will the Good Witch of the North help you with Silver Shoes? Beware of the Wicked Witch of the West and her Winged Monkeys. The Wonderful Wizard of Oz will bring more surprises.

Tentacles are cropping up all over the place and they're reaching for YOU! But you can trick the tentacles into going after someone else. Are you quick enough to rid yourself of all those grasping tentacles? Or are your friends quicker? Only one way to find out!

This game will accommodate 3 to 8 players (ages 8 and up).

OBJECT OF THE GAME: Be the person with the least number of Tentacles in your hand at the end of the 9th turn.

TO PLAY: Determine who the dealer is by whoever owns the most tentacles... or failing that, any manner that suits you. Each player will start with three cards in hand. During each turn, you and your fellow players will simultaneously pass cards to the left and right. After all cards have been passed, the players will then pick up the cards passed to them and draw a new card from the deck. Once everyone has drawn a new card, the turn is over, and it's time to move on to the next turn!

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Catapult Pizza
description:
Information from the back of the box:

Use your ninja skills to knock down villains (Shredder, Kraang Droid, and Foot Clan Soldier) by hitting them with pizzas launched from your pizza catapult! With four catapults launching pizzas at the sae time, it's pies to the sky, dude.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
Tower Seven puts you in the shoes of a 1980s action hero. During a public unveiling of Tower Corporation's new product, terrorists have taken over the main facility and are searching for a dangerous technology prototype. Trapped in the building, it's up to you to find what they are after, thwart their escape, and save the hostages.

Tower Seven is a cooperative strategy card game set in a 1980s office tower that has been taken over by terrorists. Via exploration, card play and die rolls, you will battle villains to find items and clues of their ultimate plan.

To win, you must find all the clues, recover the item card the clues pointed you towards, stop the escape plan, and ultimately defeat the final boss. The game includes a Threat Level mechanic that rises when certain events occur in-game. If the Threat Level reaches too high, hostages may be executed and you may lose the game.

Upon winning, a scoring system helps you determine how well you did versus the game. Multiple variants and difficulty levels are available to aid replayability.

boardgame: Lek og lær: farver + former + mengder
description: An educational game aimed at preschoolers, where the point is to match shapes, colours and numbers. It is played as a lotto game – tiles are drawn from a pool, and are placed on matching squares on the players' boards. The twist is that the active player does not show the tile to the others, but instead calls out the type, colour and number of shapes depicted (e. g. "six red triangles"). The first to cover all squares on their board is the winner.

boardgame: A Very English Murder: Murder at Woodperry Hall
description:
The year is 1955 and everything seems quite normal in the village of Thornbury and its most famous residence, the stately—yet slightly run-down—Woodperry Hall. England has been at peace for a decade, the family and guests are at the Hall for a evening meal, the cook has prepared a delightful supper, and the staff have arranged the summer’s mauve and white flowers in crystal vases in impecible fashion. Everything is simply tip-top ... except for the still-warm body found on the grounds of Woodperry Hall. Was it murder or suicide? Was it simply an accident, or something far more sinister? Oh, without a doubt it’s murder alright, and now Oxford City Police detectives will arrive and dramatically solve the case. Who will be discovered to have commited the crime?

A Very English Murder: Murder at Woodperry Hall is a three- to eight-player murder mystery game set in the fictional village of Thornbury, near Oxford, England in 1955. It is a narrative game where players cooperatively construct a story about a murder that takes place by answering questions and trying to appear innocent. Each player assumes the role of a character who is frequently found in classic English murder mystery novels. Every turn, one player assumes the role of an Oxford City Police Detective to question the other players who, in turn, build a case against each other to make sure another character is responsible for the murder that took place. At the end of the game, the Detective will solve the crime and—in dramatic fashion—reveal who the murderer is. Will you turn out to be the one who committed the murder?

description:
“Moew~! Those boxes are mine. All mine!”
It’s known that cats are obsessed with cardboard boxes and in Cat Box they fight for them!

Goal of the game
Players will have a secret CAT color (identity) and the winner is the player with the most cats visible of his type in the center of the table when the game ends.

Components
Cat cards x 20 (double-sided will either cats or empty boxes in 2 x 2 grid)
Cat Identity card x 4 (1 of each color)

Player turn

1) Player chooses one out of two possible actions:

a) Play a card from hand

b) Use a card from another player's hand

2) Draw back up to 2 cards.

Game End
The game ends when all players have no cards in hands. Players reveal their Cat identities and count the number of their cats that are visible in the table. Each visible cat earns the corresponding player 1 victory point (VP).

Publisher's Description:

Find the Fantastic Four!  You want the Fantastic Four team--Mr. Fantastic, Human Torch, Invisible Woman, and The Thing--to go on an exploration mission with you.  You must reach them all before you can fly away with them.  But Dr. Doom, Mole Man, and Annihilus are out there to prevent you from carrying out your plan.  However, you can count on Silver Surfer to give you a helping hand.

Fantastic Four was published as part of the Marvel Comics Super Heroes Game Book, and the board is a double-page spread of the book which is laid flat to play.

Each player starts with four markers and rolls two dice (or in this case presses the "electronic die" twice), multiplying the numbers.  They then place a marker on a space that displays the resulting number.  If another marker is already on the space, the marker must be placed on another matching space. If the square has instructions, the player follows those instructions. If the player lands on a space with a villain, their marker is stuck there until all other markers are placed. A Silver Surfer space allows the player to move the marker to any space. The first player to place one marker on each member of the Fantastic Four wins.

User Summary:
Among a group of detectives there is the perpetrator of a murder.

The culprit is one of the players, and the other players become detectives. The players investigate the "time of the crime", "weapon", "motivation" and "perpetrator" of this incident. The detective who manages to correctly identify these four elements wins. Otherwise, it will be the culprit's victory.

Will any detective manage to identify the culprit's trick and find the evidence to drive a confession from the culprit!?

Designer Summary:

探偵達の中に殺人事件の犯人が紛れ込んでいます。
プレイヤーの一人が犯人、他のプレイヤーが探偵となり、この事件の「犯行時間」「凶器」「動機」そして「犯人」を調査していきます。
全てを言い当てることができればその探偵の勝利。誰も犯人を当てられなければ、犯人の勝利となります。
探偵達は、犯人押しかけるトリックを見破り、すべての証拠を突きつけ、犯人を自白に追いやることができるでしょうか！？

Players race as kangaroos to the finish line, dodging obstacles, and sabotaging the plans of others in the Outback.  Each kangaroo has a special ability and can use that ability along with purchasing Item Cards to give them an edge in the race.  When two kangaroos land in the same space they box each other and betting takes place.  It's a fight to the finish line.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Ancient and mysterious entities, known only as Artifacts, drift from planet to planet engulfing entire civilizations. They travel together, appear on a planet, and cause nations to frenzy. Most recently, they appeared on Gallia. Players represent factions fighting for control of these Artifacts. Some want them destroyed entirely. Some simply don’t want them in enemy hands. And still, others want to use their power to enslave the world. Players will have to build their teams and enter the battlefield. Lose, and you have failed your people, perhaps your entire civilization.

Skirmish Dice is a customizable skirmish game. Using powerful leaders, sizable brutes, clever totems, and more, players customize their teams and send them into battle. Units are represented by dice on the field, and each unit’s corresponding card holds its actions, abilities, statistics, and more. The game is highly tactical and meant to move, meaning you can get the experience of a skirmish/squad level miniatures or card game in a smaller timeframe.

While the units are dice, the game doesn’t suffer from luck-induced chaos. The dice faces are significant to character abilities and are rolled to receive offensive and defensive bonuses. It’s streamlined and quick to learn, but the strategy runs deep as team building can offer hundreds of combinations within factions.

Seafari Under the Sea is a spin-and-move game where players move around a track of bubbles "under the sea."  Each player has a puzzle picture card, and all the puzzle pieces are face-down on the table.  As they move, players land on spaces that allow them to pick one, two or three puzzle pieces.  They must take face-down pieces and try to match them to their puzzle.  If they match, the may be added to the puzzle.  If not, they are returned to the face-down pile.  Some spaces also force players to put a puzzle piece back or stay on the space until a certain number is spun.  The first player to complete his or her puzzle wins.

Alpha-Bus is an alphabet learning game for young children. The board is shaped like a bus and consists of an outer track of spaces marked with the letters of the alphabet in order. Inside the track are spaces where tiles with each of the letters printed on them are placed face-down. Each player rolls the die and moves their piece along the track. When they land on a letter, they choose a face-down tile of the same color. If the letter matches, they must say the name of the letter and then may roll again. If the letter does not match or they misidentify it, their turn is over. The first player to reach the end of the track wins.

The board is two-sided. On one side, players must match upper-case with upper-case letters. On the other side, a more difficult match between lower-case and upper case is required.

boardgame: The Ten Million Year FilmCow Wars Card Game
description:
The Ten Million Year FilmCow Wars is a card game starring many of your favorite and least favorite FilmCow characters. It can be played by 2-4 people, and each game lasts around 15-30 minutes.

Can you score more points than your friends and/or bus stop acquaintances? I have no idea, maybe. I am not a mystical seer. The veil of time covers even my eyes."

The game is played by drawing s hand of cards and choosing one to play and one to discard. Each card falls into one of 5 color coded "suites". The first player to collect one of each suit wins the game. Each card features conditions such as requirements to play, or effects that happen once in play.
Original Spin is whatever the equivalent of "vaporware" is. It doesn't exist. No one has seen an actual final copy.

Publisher's Description:

Be the first to get your X-Team to the X-Mansion!  There's danger in the air!  Juggernaut, that unstoppable engine of destruction, is roaming about the X-Mansion.  Professor X's life is at risk.  He telepathically calls his X-Men to the rescue.  However, they are already on a mission to prevent Magneto from enrolling the Morlocks and their leader, Callisto, into his deadly plans.  Help the X-Men stop the villains and restore safety to the X-Mansion.

X-Men was published as part of the Marvel Comics Super Heroes Game Book, and the board is a double-page spread of the book which is laid flat to play. It is in the Pachisi/Sorry family.  Each player has four markers. A roll of 4 is needed to get a marker on the start space. After that, they must enter a track and make their way around to their color of ladder  They must then make their way up the ladder and roll the exact number to land on the X-Mansion.  Along the way, if they land on another player's marker, they may either send it back to the start or take another turn. Spaces marked with villains move the marker backward or cause loss of a turn. The first player to land all four markers on the X-Mansion wins.

A promo card for Seasons.

Type: Familiar
Prestige Points: 8
Invocation cost: 0 crystals

Continuous effect: Every opponent must from now on address you formally and call you «My Lord» until the end of the game. Each time an opponent neglects this rule, he loses 2 crystals.

The 5 expansions included in the Expansion Pack:

    Tactical Expansion
    Political Expansion
    Crusaders Expansion
    Templars Expansion
    Teutonic Knights Expansion

Each player is a farmer living in the remote village of Farmaria. The aim of the game is to grow their farm and col-
lect as many Victory Points, (V.P.) as possible. On the Farm are a number of fields and commodities owned by each player. The V.P. represent the reputation of every player in Farmaria along with the quality of their farm. The game requires strategy along with commercial and business development aptitude.

This is a Strategy game for 2 to 5 players The players invest on their farms using an Action Choice Token System while at the same time the try to upgrade their property in an efficient way, stop other players from winning or interrupt their plans and avoid catastrophic events. The player with the most Victory Points wins the game.

A simplification of Eleusis, an inductive reasoning card game, designed for classroom use.

The dealer chooses a secret rule. Players attempt to identify the rule by playing a card in turn, and the dealer identifies whether the card matches the rule or not.

According to Robert Abbott, designer of Eleusis, "[t]he chief feature of Eleusis Express is that if you figure out the rule, you simply say what it is. The dealer then says whether you are right or not, and if you’re right, you score extra points and the hand ends."

Publisher's Description:
Defeat Sabretooth! One evening, while Jubilee thinks she is alone in the X-Mansion, she hears strange noises coming from the surrounding woods. She sets out alone to investigate, even though she knows it might be dangerous. Fortunately for here, Wolverine is secretly keeping an eye on her. Sabretooth's scent is in the air. . .

Wolverine was published as part of the Marvel Comics Super Heroes Game Book, and the board is a double-page spread of the book which is laid flat to play. It is a roll-and-move game where players use two tokens, representing Wolverine and Jubilee, along a track.  Each player in turn "rolls" the electronic die and moves one or the other token along the track, following any instructions on the space where they land.  Spaces may move players forward or back or cause them to lose or gain a turn.
