Since players have two tokens, a small element of strategy is introduced in deciding which token is more advantageous to move with each roll. First player to reach the finish with both tokens wins.

Publisher's Description:

Race to Florida!  The United States is on RED ALERT!  Red Skull is threatening to spread his infamous dust of death all over the country.  All the states, except Alaska and Hawaii, are at risk.  It's up to you, the Avengers and Force Works, to get to Florida as quickly as you can and put an end to Red Skull's deadly plan.  Remember, Kang and his temporal duplicates are out there to prevent you from accomplishing your mission!

The Avengers was published as part of the Marvel Comics Super Heroes Game Book, and the board is a double-page spread of the book which is laid flat to play.  It is a roll-and-move game where players must move four markers (one at a time) in a numbered sequence from state to state on a map of the United States, starting in Washington and ending in Florida.  Some states contain instruction to move forward or back or lose or gain a turn.  If a player lands on a state occupied by another player's token, they must move back five spaces.  First player to reach Florida with all five tokens wins.

Take Conan and his companions East, to the mysterious land that is home to great sorcerers whose spells are feared by those from the West, and become swept up in a sinister assassination plot. Take part in a daring assassination attempt before escaping from a flaming tower where the only way out is up!

The first expansion for the Conan board game: The Tower of Khitai, by Antoine Bauza and Ludovic Maublanc. 
 The box includes 22 miniatures, two maps, as well as various cards/tokens and tiles. All this will allow you to play out a total of six scenarios (4 in the box, 2 on the website).

Publisher's Description:

Lure Absorbing Man to the Water!  Absorbing Man has decided to eliminate the Incredible Hulk. He has set up a trap for the Hulk in a hydro power plant.  You must join forces with She-Hulk and the Hulk to get rid of Absorbing Man, once and for all.  Here's the plan.  Since Absorbing Man can absorb the properties of anything he touches, She-Hulk and the Hulk will attempt to make Absorbing Man turn himself into fire.  Then they will throw him into the sea, where he will disappear forever!

The Incredible Hulk was published as part of the Marvel Comics Super Heroes Game Book, and the board is a double-page spread of the book which is laid flat to play.  It is a fairly standard roll-and-move game where players must move two tokens representing Hulk and She-Hulk one at at time along a track by rolling an electronic die.  Along the way, some spaces will move players forward or back or cause a loss or gain of a turn.  If a player lands on another player's token, he or she moves back four spaces.  First player to reach the end with both markers wins.

Object of the game is to be the first to get 1 figure to the opposite end of the board.

One player controls 4 miniatures, the other player controls 1.

The single figure can move forwards, backwards, sideways but not diagonally. And only 1 space at a time. The opposing player can only move forwards, one piece at a time. No jumping and no capturing.

The winner is the first to reach the opposite site end.

OBJECT:
To win the majority of 9 games played simultaneously.

INCLUDES:
54 pegs
9 up gameboard
Instructions with box

(source: Text on box)
An educational card game based around asking each other questions on a variety of rocks and minerals. There are a total of 400 questions on 40 different cards. Simple game that is easy to pick up.

boardgame: Spyfall: Ice Hockey Stadium promo cards
description: This promo pack adds seven Ice Hockey Stadium location cards and one Spy card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
User summary:

The players are lord of house during Victorian England, and must, during six rounds, build up noble fame by having a staff of maids, important connections and land strips.

Each round, a number of cards (maids, noble connections and lands) are laid out, and each player has 2 pawns that he may use to claim cards. Knocking away another player from a card costs money that goes into the treasury; and the treasury can also be claimed. Lands cost money to claim, but brings an income each round.

The 3rd and the 6th round there is a scoring round, in which players get points for maids, combinations thereof, noble connections and land. The player with the most points at the end wins.

boardgame: ATS Core Module: Sujewo Heights – Tigers in the Mud 1944
description:
Tigers in the Mud - Eastern Front Stand at the Panther Line: An all new take on the classic Ostfront battle with remastered, ENLARGED MAP that makes this a one-of a kind experience with armor, armor and did we mention armor? By early 1944, Army Group North held a front that bore the marks of previous battles; a long line built around Leningrad. Of course, STAVKA’s Baltic Front planners were determined to break the hold and finally free the city. 1943 ended with Army Group North facing the Russian Third Shock Army. Fighting had continued unabated through a muddy fall, and on into the month of December. Although the Germans had successfully held their front, the fighting power of Armee Gruppe Nord had been greatly reduced. The dead and wounded landsers were replaced by Latvian, Estonian, and Spanish soldiers. When OKW pulled the 5th Mountain Division out of the line, without a replacement, the time had come for a shortening of the front. The result became known as the ‘Panther Line’.
Welcome to the ATS CORE MODULES, all new for 2014. New map, larger, on cardstock, folding with no trimming needed -- did we mention new terrain art throughout AND larger hexes? Perfect for those classic -- YET ALL NEW ATS COUNTERS -- the you'll have on the map deciding history ... all ... over ... again! This module is for owners of the ATS 2014 Color Rules plus ATS Standard Small Markers + Large Markers but may also be used easily by owners of any past edition of the ATS rules. All Special Rules and AFV Cards needed for play are included, along with ALL NEW FOLIO STYLE packaging.

You also need to own the following to play:

    Russian Personnel 2014 Edition
    German Personnel 2014 Edition
    ATS Russian AFVs and H Weapons - 2014D
    ATS German AFVs and H Weapons 2014D

In the year 4010, humankind has ships in space but no colonies. Earth explodes and the last of our kind must set aside their differences and team up to tame a wild world and save humanity before time runs out! In this action packed cooperative strategy game, players will captain their starships as humankind’s Last Starfleet! Everyone wins in Last Starfleet… or all is lost.

Each ship has its own deck of cards which players will use to upgrade their ships, guard against planet attacks and land their crew in order to win the game! Each planet will pose unique challenges to the team and landing your people is never an easy task.

Things We'll Add to Deluxe Copies:
Ship Expansion - Galactic Command, Pangaea government
Ship Expansion - Pay Dirt, orbital extraction and mining station
Ship Expansion - Eagle Eye, the lost cosmonauts
Planet Expansion - Cryton, the siege of Cryton
Planet Expansion - Kurmaraja, the sleeping giant
Planet Expansion - Terra Nine, the jungle of madness
Planet Expansion - Derelict One, tragic site of the first use of a dark matter core
Orbit Deck Expansion - Asteroid Belt, great and treacherous orbital rings

boardgame: ATS Core Module: On the Road to Rome – Gothic Line 1944
description:
On the Road to Rome 1944: SANTA MARIA INFANTE: A brand new take on the struggle along the ridge ... along the road to Rome. The Germans are dug in, the Americans well-armed ... the outcome is in YOUR hands. The battle for Santa Maria Infante was important in the opening of the Allied offensive toward Rome on 11 May 1944. Strong Allied forces were concentrated on the west side of the Italian peninsula to renew an effort stopped by the winter and heavy German resistance. Eighth Army was poised for attack through the Liri Valley along the axis of Highway No. 6; main Fifth Army faced a chain of mountains, in a 13-mile zone from the Liri River to the sea. The forces in the Anzio Beachhead were ready to strike when their opportunity came as a result of progress on the other fronts.

Welcome to the ATS CORE MODULES, all new for 2014. New map, larger, on cardstock, folding with no trimming needed -- did we mention new terrain art throughout AND larger hexes? Perfect for those classic -- YET ALL NEW ATS COUNTERS -- the you'll have on the map deciding history ... all ... over ... again! This module is for owners of the ATS 2014 Color Rules plus ATS Standard Small Markers + Large Markers but may also be used easily by owners of any past edition of the ATS rules. All Special Rules and AFV Cards needed for play are included, along with ALL NEW FOLIO STYLE packaging.

You also need to own the following to play:

    American Personnel 2014 Edition
    German Personnel 2014 Edition
    ATS U.S. AFVs and H Weapons - 2014D
    ATS German AFVs and H Weapons 2014D

Imagine an intricately mechanized clash of spy against spy, introducing corporate espionage to a Steampunk world of industrial revolution; filled with all of the plot twists and thrills of both genres. That's the world of Steam Spy, a Steampunk themed role playing card game for 2 players. Steam Spy unites the character development and narrative of a traditional role playing game with the intimacy and spontaneity of a card game.

Players create a unique secret agent character who works for one of the organizations designated to protect and serve one of the world’s largest corporations.  Then they face off against an operative from an opposing secret industrial organization in a one on one battle of wits, luck and exotic steampunk gadgets.  One player instigates some kind of espionage mission while the other does their best to stop them.

Players collect, play and draw cards that help create a series of exciting encounters that ultimately determine if the instigating antagonists succeeds at their mission, or the determined protagonist chases them down and defeats them; players then advance their spy character, or create a group of secret agent specialists in preparation for the next face off against operatives from opposing agencies and corporation.

Steam Spy: Shadows and Circuits allows you to investigate the mysterious as an agent of the Shadow Bureau, the Eastern European agency that helps protect the lines of transportation developed by the Transylvanian Transportation Company. Or you can join the Dragon Sect and take advantage of the marvelous technology developed by its parent company Orisaku Artificials.

Dark Suns, expansion of the sci-fi card game Nova Cry, presents 30 new cards including 2 ships, 2 new captains, 1 new crew member type, new alien crew members, and action cards

Nova Cry is an explosive sci-fi adventure for 2-6 players. Loaded with customizable options, each player chooses 1 ship, 1 captain, 3 crew members and 1 personal weapon as they set out into the danger-filled spaces of Gallator Seven. Will you explore creature-infested planets? Race an opponent to an undiscovered planet? Challenge another captain to a duel? Blast your opponent’s ship to smithereens? It’s all up to as you carve out a strategic path to victory in this remarkable world of heroes, villains, and awe-inspiring spaceships.

The first player to score 15 victory points or be the last undestroyed ship wins.

Players score points by reaching planets, winning duels, and performing actions using their available crew members (skilled in Exploration, Alien Technology, Medicine, Psychology, Repair, and Ship Upgrades). Players also collect Intel points to initiate ship battles and character duels, and will have to choose from 1 of 4 strategies during their turn to plan their victory!

boardgame: Zombie Apocalypse Geocaching: Exclusive Kickstarter Pack
description:
Kickstarter backers received the following promo cards, which could also be purchased separately during the Kickstarter:

    Group of three survivors
    Serum Z9Y
    Serum Z4Y
    Timed high explosive
    The Zombie Hunter
    Zombie Overlord


They also received 26 card stands so that the Zombies will be 3-D when they walk around the board.

Do you think that you can explain in a minute what an old chestnut or a permafrost is? Will you be able to do it while standing on one leg or by using verbs only?

Воображарий: Вечеринка (Imagination: Party) is a fun game about guessing words based on the hit Russian game Воображарий. Each turn, a player is selected to describe a word or a short phrase. A special card dictates whether the player have to give an explanation, to act or to do a unique show! If you are active, creative and positive you'll get a chance to become the brightest star of your party!

Imagination: Party is a standalone game, but it can also be combined with Imagination.

boardgame: Winter Offensive Bonus Pack #6: ASL Scenario Pack for Winter Offensive 2015
description:
The ASL Winter Offensive 2015 Bonus Pack features three Pete Shelling scenario designs and two redesigned boards. The new maps "freshen" certain older boards using overlays specifically designed for each board.

ASL Winter Offensive 2015 Bonus Pack contains:

2 ASL/ASLSK geomorphic standard mapboards, labeled 10z and 17z (to honor their heritage)
 Scenarios:

WO15 - Liberation Day - 6 turns, board 17z and 10z, 27 January 1944 Oredezh, Russia

WO16 - Wildcat Strike - 6-1/2 turns, board 10z and 67, 17 September 1944 Saian Town, Angaur Island

WO17 - Poteau Party - 7 turns, boards 2b and 17z,  21 December 1944 Poteau, Belgium

Proceeds from the sales of each WO Bonus Pack 2015 are donated to the WWII Foundation.

Published in  Command Magazine 22 (Antietam: Burnished Rows of Steel), Smithereens Variant is an expansion for Smithereens, with new rules for pro Axis Yugoslavians and Nazi secret weapons. Also add 12 new counters for play this expansion
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Lords of Xidit: Bonus Titans Promo
description: Lords of Xidit: Bonus Titans Promo is a set of three new titans to mix into the titans from the Lords of Xidit base game, adding three more titans for you to fight!

Earth is long gone as the perfect place to live. As one of the architects sent to the red planet, you will be tasked with creating an infrastructure for those, who wish to start a new life.

Mars: Kolonizacja (Mars: Colonization) is a quick card game that gives you three different ways to activate each card: by playing from hand, activating one of the cards on the table or activating enemy's card. Keep in mind – Mars is not a place for gentlemen and your decisions often affect not just you, but other players as well!

To win the game, gather the largest number of colonists by developing newer and better buildings.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: The Big Bang Theory: Das geniale Spiel
description:
Leonard, Howard and Raj want a killer robot; Penny, Bernadette and Amy a preserved snowflake; and Sheldon? How about a "device for virtual presence"?

The Big Bang Theory: Das geniale Spiel challenges you to fulfill the wishes of the seven main characters from the television show, and you do this through two-colored cards that allow you to play them on one side or the other. Paradox tiles can change up the rules.

Iqishiqi (pronounced EE-chee-shee-chee) is an abstract strategy game designed by Joao Neto that is played on a hexagonal board composed of hexagonal cells.  Players own designated edges of the board, and by clever placement of stones they push a neutral stone around the board.  A player wins if he/she moves the neutral stone to one of his/her edges, or leaves his/her opponent unable to move.  The game can be played with 2 or 3 players.

Kiqsuyapi - A game for three to eight players who are puzzle fans and fast thinkers. Here the players themselves decide about the level of difficulty, because they take turns in being the puzzle master who can ask the questions, and it is totally up to him or her what is asked: How many feathers were there altogether? How many headbands were worn? Or how many weapons were pointing to the left? Was the second Indian from the right painted with war paint? Or is there an Indian wearing a shirt, and carrying a tomahawk? The player who is able to give the first correct answer, gains a token.

The 10 playing cards in Kiqsuyapi are double-sided. They determine the level of difficulty of the game. With only 4 cards, the challenge is still moderate. With 10 cards even true champions are going to work up a sweat. And if you still consider this too easy, the expert version will be an even greater challenge for you. Here, all players have to answer and the right answer will gain you a point, as before. Wrong answers, however, will cost you points! This applies in particular to those questions that ask for two or even three aspects at once. For example, how many Indians are wearing a shirt, a headdress and are carrying a bow?

In Unser Sandmännchen und seine Freunde, the little Sandman wants to distribute his sleep sand, but the beds of all of his friends are empty. To help the Sandman wish them a good night, players need to find those friends. If they can get the friends to their beds in time, they win the game together.

Fishing, trumpet playing, washing hands: all of these things can be a lot easier to explain with a banana in hand.

The gist of Alles Banane! is simple: Cards that show various activities are laid out on the table, then one player pantomimes one of these activities with the help of a yellow wooden banana. Everyone tries to guess what the active player is doing by placing their player chip on the corresponding card.

boardgame: Die Welt: Wetten, da liegt Manhattan!
description:
Die Welt: Wetten, da liegt Manhatten! features gameplay similar to Die Welt: Singapur, wo liegt das nur? (which is derived from Deutschland: Finden Sie Minden), with players trying to locate places or landmarks on a world map.

To set up, players lay out game boards that feature land masses with no names on them. On a turn, players are confronted with a location, such as Timbuktu or Medina, then they all try to guess where in the world that location is. They can play it safe and choose a large block of land – scoring only a few points in the process if correct – or they can go small and pinpoint the location more precisely on one of the game boards, scoring far more points if correct, but upping their chances of scoring nothing if they miss their mark. In the end, whoever scores the most points wins.

Die Welt: Wetten, da liegt Manhatten! is playable as a standalone game or the ninety locations included in the box can be used with the Die Welt: Singapur, wo liegt das nur? base game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Posse: Wild West Justice is a wild west themed card and dice game for 2 to 4 players.

Players are lawmen tasked with capturing the outlaws terrorizing the local population. Players will recruit posse members to help bring the lowdown lawbreakers in for some wild west justice. The player that collects the most reward money wins the game.

Since 1896, masochists around the world have raced each other in an event that killed its first participant: the Marathon!

In 26.2: The Marathon Dice Game, players simultaneously choose how many dice to roll and roll them for points (with each point representing 1/10th of a mile). The more dice they roll, the more points they may score, but it also increases the chances of busting out and scoring no points that turn. However, rehydrating via water tokens helps mitigate this risk. The first player to reach 262 points wins!
Simple roll-and-move-game. The first player to reach space 120 wins the game. The angular spaces are event-spaces, which are explained in the game rules..

Simple roll-and-move-game with health graphics. The first player to reach space 100 wins the game. The yellow spaces are event spaces, which are explained in the game rules.

The game was published in the early 1960s.
Dream Team is a communication game in which members of team create lists of items, then try to get their teammates to surf the same thought waves in order to guess these items.

Draftamania: Rise of the promotions is the fast-paced card game of wrestling promotion management. A game of Draftamania takes place over a number of turns. Each turn comprises of three phases:

The Pre-Show phase - Where players draft wrestlers to their roster and buy special Booster cards (to both improve their Roster and to hinder their opponents). Each player draws a House Show card and books their wrestlers to appear in their show. Finally decisions are made about the outcomes of the various segments such as which wrestlers are receiving pushes.

The Showtime phase - Where players take turns describing (commentating) their Show and roll the dice to determine the results of the various Segments. Even the best laid plans and flawless booking can be undone on the night by bad luck, injuries or a crucially timed disruptive Boosters played by an opponent.

The Post-Show phase - In the aftermath of the show players vote to give the best General Manager award to one player. Additionally, gate receipts are calculated, Rosters reorganised and Wrestler’s appeased.

The phases are played simultaneously by the players with the exception of part of the Showtime phase where players take turns to commentate on their own show and roll the dice to decide the results of Segments. During the each of the phases the players will have the opportunity to build a roster of wrestlers for their Promotion.  These wrestlers are further developed with Booster cards, such as pleasing moves, story lines, equipment and arenas, dirty tricks to play on rival player's promotions.

Finally each player will then book their Wrestlers into Shows and Pay-Per-View special events in order to gain Popularity Points (PP). At the end of each Post-Show phase, the players count their Popularity Points; the player who reaches the goal number (different for the different variants) has managed to impress Network TV bosses, signed a TV Network deal and wins the game.

Countess Rubina von Schnackelstein has buried her gemstones.  Instead of the lush diamond plants though, which she had hoped for, mole hills have started to pop up all over her lawn.
You decide where the cuddly bgurrowers get busy digging in search for the precious gems.  If you manage to unearth the most jewels, you will be crowned king of the moles.  Be wary of the beetles though:  they work for the countess and try to steal the gemstones back from you!
The sequel to admin test item.

User Summary: 
亡国のタイドライン (The Tideline of Exile) is a game by 中村 泰之 (Yasuyuki Nakamura) for the doujin circle merry-andrew-works.

This is a card game with a WW2 theme of Submarine vs Submarine combat. The game uses an identity concealment system. In the confusion of war, players collect gold, confidential documents and defectors. The submariners try to evade mines and torpedoes, navigate dangerous waters and escape depth charges launched by the enemy.

Designer Summary:
潜水艦ｖｓ潜水艦 Ｕボートがテーマの正体隠匿系カードゲーム。戦争の混乱で四散した「金塊」「機密文書」「亡命者」を集め、機雷や魚雷、爆雷の待ち構える危険な海域を仲間と共に脱出しましょう！
publishers: メリーアンドリューワークス(merry-andrew-works)

description:
Take Care of Polar Bears is an environment friendly serious game. It offers an opportunity to experience the ecological crisis of Polar Bears indirectly. It gives you the educational values as well, which means that can help to not only understand the causes of climate change problems better, but also know how to deal with them by role-playing in this game.

This board game is played by using cards, not dices basically and the board can be made up with 17 glaciers tiles arbitrarily. There are 54 cards including moving cards, recharging cards, and hunter cards, etc. Players can have three cards in their hands and they use one of their cards in each turn. By using these cards efficiently, the player who reaches the finish tile at first will be a winner. This board game can be used in anywhere, for example, schools, cultural organizations, business training, when someone needs an education about environments or ecological values.

Flavor backstory:

The Enclave is a city of opportunity and adventure; a place to earn a name for yourself. It's treacherous surroundings offer endless chances to take on daring quests and slay fearsome monsters.Of course slaying monsters requires quality gear, and you won't just find it laying around. But no worries, there are always tasks to be done to earn some coin; such as helping the blacksmith forge some tools, or helping the Inn Keeper fix up the place. While the Enclave has always been known as a "safe haven" from the terrors that surround it, recently it's fortification has been tested due to a Dragon that has made home near by. Although adventurers do come through often, not many are brave enough to go toe to toe with a dragon. That's where you come in! It's about time someone stood up to that gigantic, fire-breathing, armor-like-scale covered, razor fanged, flying pest. No true Hero wants to share that glorious triumph, so your on your own. It's time to forge your legacy!

Gameplay Description:

Each player stars the game by choosing a Hero. There are 4 races to choose from, Human, Elf, Dwarf, and Orc. Each has a unique starting deck of skill cards and 1 unique trait card. The skills are Strength, Dexterity, and Focus. These will be the power behind your actions. Each side of the main deck has 4 revealed cards. One side represents outside the Enclave, the other represents inside it. You will use the cards in your hand drawn from your deck to interact with the revealed cards. This includes slaying a monster, completing a task, or buying gear. Your actions reward experience in the form of skill cards. These are collected and then added to your deck once you level up. You can either buy bigger and badder gear, or upgrade what you have. You can also spend all of the cards in your hand to gain a new talent card which is added to your deck. Once you feel your ready to go, you can face off with the Dragon. Defeat it, and you've not only won the game, but also forged your name in glory for all of eternity! Or at least until an expansion comes out with a bigger dragon....

What makes it Unique:

Experience: Unlike other deck-building games, you don't "buy" cards from the main pool and add them into your deck. Instead you complete tasks and defeat monsters to earn "experience" in the form of skill cards. Those are what get's added to your deck. How does more of the same card help you? Most importantly, adding MORE of these cards allows you to level up your character. This also gives you a chance to really fine-tune your character. If you want to wield a sword, you'll need strength behind it, and so completing Strength tasks earns you more Strength cards. The more Strength cards in your deck, the more often you will draw them. Also, the more cards in your deck, the more turns you can take before you need to rest (recycling your discard pile for your deck). There are also trait cards such as Relentless, Fearsome, Agile, and Clever. Each of these provides a different effect when played. At the end of the game, you've built a truly unique Hero with a unique deck of cards to power it!

Leveling: Each player only starts with a hand of 3 cards, which is then increased by leveling up your Hero. The larger hand size gives the player more potential power behind their actions, which is not only required to defeat the Dragon, but feels very rewarding.

Theme implementation: The tasks in the game are simple labor jobs but the skills required to complete them are intuitive and logical. For example, the Blacksmiths' task requires Strength to forge ingots while the Alchemist' task of preparing potions requires Focus. This helps eliminate that disconnect many deck-building games have.

These are just some of the many unique mechanics that make The Enclave a deck-building game that we feel truly stands out among the rest!

The game is set to release at the end of April, 2015.

On the farm in Wettpuzzeln auf dem Bauernhof things have been moved around: The cow hides behind the goat shed, the dog is running around the scarecrow, and where is the tractor? Everything is messed up and not even Farmer Paul can be found. Now it's time to quickly find the right parts and clean up the farm once again!

A lot is going on the vegetable garden, and in Ratzeputz the small gardeners need to work with Till the dwarf to plant and harvest the colorful carrots. But watch out as underneath the garden bed Ratzeputz lives with his insatiable family of rabbits — and carrots, as is well known, are their favorite food.

As soon as the young gardeners plant a new turnip, hungry Ratzeputz immediately comes out to see what's present, and as the players rotate the garden turntable the cheeky rabbit will move with it. If they have bad luck, then "plop!" — the carrot falls down a rabbit hole. Gardeners can be unlucky at harvest, too, with the rabbits also being happy to take home a turnip.

In the end, whoever has the most carrots wins! Ratzeputz includes two levels of difficulty and bonus game rules for children ages five and up.

boardgame: Space Cadets: Away Missions – Promo Cards
description:
The 20 "Promo Cards" will be:
 Promotional Rocketeer Item cards
1) Tricorder
2) Sniper Scope
3) Test-tube of the Common Cold
4) Scanner
5) DDT
6) Lucky Rabbit’s Foot
7) Power of Love/But That’s What Makes Us Human
8) Thermite
9) Fiberweave Spacesuit
10) Sonic Shotgun
11) Space Monkey
12) Thermos of Freeze-dried Coffee
13) Wire Cutters
14) Firecracker
15) Space Rations

Promotional Rescued Thrall cards
1) Nurse
2) Chemist
3) Traffic Cop
4) Smuggler
5) Photographer

These were first given to Kickstarter Backers of this project.  They will be available in limited quantities after the game is released.  [There were NO Exclusives in this campaign.]
It was later available in a pack of promos for backing Game Boy Geek 2017 Kickstarter campaign

Numerabis combines memory and math.
There are 49 face down tiles with the numbers from 1 to 49.
Players roll tow dies to determin the task for a round. This coulod for example be to reveal 3 odd numbers for example, or 2 cards that add up to more than 60.
If a player reveals cards fitting the task, he can keep them.
The game ends as soon as there is onle one or no more tile left.
The player with the most tiles wins.

boardgame: Bolt Action: Ostfront – Barbarossa to Berlin
description:
Take charge of Operation Barbarossa and drive towards Moscow or command the steadfast defenders of the Soviet Union. From the early battles for Leningrad and Sevastopol to the tank clash of Kursk and the bitter urban warfare of Stalingrad, this new Theatre Book for Bolt Action provides wargamers with new scenarios and special rules that give them everything they need to focus their gaming on the Eastern Front.

Contents:

Introduction
    Special Rules
    Scenarios

boardgame: Trip from New York to San Francisco
description:
An unusual board game based on the "new Union Pacific Railroad".

Objective: be the first reaching space 45. Roll the die and move forward. If you land on one of the special spaces follow instructions.

The game board shows forty five beautifully executed views of places along the Union Pacific route, starting with "Rail road depot in New York" and ending in "San Francisco, the metropolis of California". Along the way, we have, among others, views of Pittsburgh, Omaha, "surprise by savage Indians", "wild heard of buffalloes", "railroad bridge over the Platte river," Cheyenne, Green River, Humboldt Valley, "Trukee" Valley, Sierra Nevada and Sacramento. The text is titled "Trip from New-York to San Francisco by the Union Pacific Rail Road. A new illustrated Travel-game with 45 stations, 1 dy, 12 cars and 12 numbers charts". The text is written in German and English and explains the game and the views. The cover of the game box (19x24) has a lovely colored litho view of two women joining hands across the American Continent, one representing America and the other Asia.

(Trivia: It is very likely that this game was also sold by the on-train concessionaire ("news butchers") on the trans-continental trains of the Union Pacific, just as promotional picture booklets were sold showing the scenic views along the route -- in other words, a tourist's memento).

A simple TCG game with an interesting mechanic about the disposition of card in a 3x3 grid (resembling Tic-Tac-Toe)

There are 4 types of cards: attack (red sword icon), defend (blue shield icon), revitalization (green cross icon) and neutral (yellow circle icon). Also, the cards may have arrows in one or more 8-way directions (up, diagonal up-right, right, diagonal down-right...) Each player starts with a 3x3 grid, 10 points of life and a deck of 25-40 cards. With the flip of a coin is decided the order of playing, at the first turn you must draw 5 cards and play 1 card, for the rest of the match you must have 5 cards in your hand (except when you have 0 cards on your deck)

The purpouse of the game is to combine at least 2 cards to produce a combination of at least 3 symbols. To combine the cards you must put them in the 3x3 grids and achieve to match the arrows (for example, a card in the second row and second column with an up arrow matches with a card with a down arrow that is in the first row and second column). The effect is determined by the greatest symbol value (except neutral, that haves no effect), in case of draw (for example, 2 sword and 2 crosses) the player chooses what effect it should be. Attack effect (sword) deducts 1 point of defence from the life of the opponent for each sword in the combination OR you can disable a card in the rival's 3x3 grid. Defence adds to the player 1 point of life for each shield in the combination. Revitalization can undisable 1 card in your grid.

There are 2 ways to win the game: by reducing the rival's life to 0 OR when the rival have cards in the hand and cannot play a card (the grid is filled with 9 cards that cannot combine in this turn) It should be noted that when a player haves 0 cards in its deck, losses 1 point of life at the start of his turn.

boardgame: Star Wars Rebels: Abenteuer auf Lothal
description:
Together with the Rebel heroes Ezra, Kanan, Zeb, Sabine and Hera in Star Wars Rebels: Abenteuer auf Lothal, players explore the planet Lothal. With the help of dice throws, skillful bluffing, and Chopper the grumpy Astromech droid, they are making rapid progress — but who is bold enough to risk a battle with the dreaded Inquisitor and his clone troopers?

Who will be the first to get back to the Rebels' spaceship "The Ghost"?

The international (France/UK/US) release is titled "Star Wars Rebels Adventure Game".

boardgame: Viaje de España al Japon en Globo, Bicicleta, Vapor y Diligencia
description:
Viaje de España al Japon en Globo, Bicicleta, Vapor y Diligencia (eng. Travel from Spain to Japan with Balloon, Bicycle, Steamboat and Stagecoach) is a vintage spin and move game published in Spain. Players spin the spinning die with 4 paintings on the sides: Balloon, Bicycle, Steamboat and Stagecoach. Player could travel around the board only with that vehicle, which picture he see on the top of the spinning die.
The first player, who cross all route, wins the game.

Audatia: Armour Expansion Pack expands an Audatia Duel Deck with a range of armoured combat cards.

The expansion enables you to use armoured fighting techniques when duelling against an armoured or unarmoured opponent.

Assassins of Vance is a game for 2-4 players in which you maneuver to become the leader of the Assassins Guild in Vance, city of splendor. It is the first board game designed by Vic Davis, owner and operator of Cryptic Comet Games. Vic's previous games have been computer turn based strategy games and have won praise from critics and fans alike for their stimulating board game like mechanics.

Players first select a player card that represents their Assassin each possessing a unique one time use special ability and then assign dice from a pool to 4 attributes: Combat, Stealth, Traps and Lore. The assignments indicate how many dice are rolled for each corresponding challenge type and what face results constitute success when the dice are rolled.

Play then proceeds in rounds where each player takes his turn. 2 and 4 player games last 8 rounds while 3 player games last 9 rounds.

During their turn players can choose 2 of 8 possible actions (some actions can only be chosen once)

1. Visit the Guild Healer (pay gold to remove wound tokens)
2. Draw Guild Stipend (gold) (only once per turn)
3. Buy an Item Card
4. Buy a Spell Card (if you have a skill card that allows this)
5. Visit the Guild Sorcerer (pay gold to charge a spell)
6. Draw 2 Secret Objective Cards (keep 1 or none)(only once per turn)
7. Play a Guild Card for its Guild Event text
8. Attempt a Mission Contract Card (only once per turn)

The objective of the game is to accumulate Influence Points (IP). Influence Points can be gained by either successfully completing Mission Contract cards or buying influence cards at the end of the round during the Influence Bidding Phase where cards are auctioned off to the highest bidder.  Each influence card has an IP value and a unique passive special ability that can help the player out during the game.

Mission Contract cards are at the heart of the game and represent assassination targets that the guild has available for its guild members. Players can play Guild Cards face down before the round begins to the revealed Mission Contract cards on the table in order to help their own or hurt their opponents' chances of accomplishing the mission. Missions vary from level 1 to 5 and offer an array of rewards from Influence Points to gold and additional ability die. However, the greater the rewards, the higher the risk that your assassin will be wounded or fail the mission. Take too many wound tokens and you are out of the game.

Each mission is composed of a chain of challenges corresponding to the 4 ability types on the player cards i.e. combat, stealth, etc.. Players determine the level of the challenge (number of successes needed) from the contract card and then select a challenge card from the top 3 revealed cards of the Challenge Deck.  The challenge card indicates the type of challenge and the pass/fail rules.  Players roll the appropriate number of dice, use Guild Cards, Item Cards, Skill Cards, Spell Cards and fate tokens (rerolls) to modify the results and hopefully win the challenge. Players can press their luck along the challenge track to earn better rewards but must be careful as a red skull challenge that is failed means that the mission is failed and all rewards are lost. Sometimes things just don't go well from the start and the player may elect to abort the mission.

Players also have the opportunity to use Guild Card Interrupts to take actions during their opponents turns to disrupt their carefully laid assassination plans.

When the last round finishes players add up their IP from their completed Mission Contract cards, Influence Cards and completed Secret Objective cards(uncompleted cards penalize the player). The player with the most IP wins and becomes the Master of The Assassins Guild in Vance.

The Manic Martians Game is a fun game for little ones. Bop a pop-up martian with a space rocket bopper to pick it up.

The Manic Martians Game is fun for two players. Your little one can watch as the martians go in and out of the craters, and then bop one on the head with the space rocket bopper to pick it up! This enjoyable game for 2 players is great fun for children and adults, and easy for little ones to understand.

The game has a round plastic board with small plastic martians set into holes in the board. With batteries, the martians will move up and down in their spaces. Each player has a small rubber mallet and must try to strike the martians with the mallet. A perfect strike will "stick" the martian to the mallet and the player may remove it from the board. The player with the most martians at the end of the game wins.

Pretty girl is a simple game for the younger audiences.
The game contains: board (58x47cm), dice, 8 figurines, 60+24 counters.

On the board is shown part of a city where is a shopping mall. Fields on the board are following streets and sidewalks. In front of stores are marked fields next to which are one or more counters. On the beginning of the game players place their figurines on any field on the board. Figurines are moved in any direction for the number rolled on dice. In order to collect a counter, figurine must stop on marked field and player choose on of counters beside. Players have task to collect as much counters as possible. Every counter represent one clothing item. Assembled set worth extra points. Players also have to visit cafe, hairdresser and bank.

Get ready for Doompocalypse!!!

The first major expansion to Assault on Doomrock, featuring added simple terrain rules and cards to every possible deck.

What do you get in the expansion?

Expansion rulebook
    7 new oversized encounter cards
    9 oversized revised encounter cards from the base game
    54 AI intelligence cards for the new encounters
    6 AI cards for old encounters
    9 double sided male/female hero cards (3 are new)
    11 revised trait cards
    3 setting cards
    14 area cards
    20 terrain cards with 26 terrain tokens
    2 large tokens for dividing the battlefield
    18 starting ability cards
    18 level up ability cards
    10 common item cards
    10 epic item cards
    6 wilderness event cards
    6 mountain event cards
    5 additional silver/gold tokens
    2 boss markers
    2 minion markers
    3 hero markers
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In the Ultimate Brain Challenge you'll need to muster all your mental dexterity to solve riddles, brainteasers, and conundrums. The game includes challenging categories such as Brain Bending Conundrums, IQ Squares, Picture Problems, and Word Mixers.

The goal is to be the first player or team to pass 10 challenges. There is a time limit of 4 minutes per card challenge, but a shorter time limit can be set for faster game play.

boardgame: The Big Box of Brain Stumping Fun
description:
In case you don't have enough problems in your life..here are 330 more! The Big Box of Brain Stumping Fun features riddles, including logic problems, visual conundrums, lateral thinking problems, and world riddles.
 Noggin Workshop's The Big Box of Brain Stumping Fun can be played for any length of time.  Players determine how many cards they want to play and then count out that many cards.  Other cards are put back in the box for another time. One player reads out the question and then places it face up for everyone to solve.  Once a player solves the problem, he/she checks the answer on the back of the card. If it's correct, that card is kept as a means for collecting points. If it's incorrect, the player is eliminated from the round, while other players continue to solve the problem. The winner with the most cards is the winner.

Increase your knowledge of Shakespeare and his literary works by playing this fun trivia game. Vote for the correct answers and win some Shillings along the way!

Place your color book pawns next to answers you believe are true. Receive a Shilling for each positive point you earn by selecting a true answer. The player with the most shillings after five rounds wins!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Battle Roll is a 2 player dueling dice game! It's quick, addictive, portable, and loads of fun! It comes with eight beautiful precision engraved and painted dice with incredible detail. It only takes about 5-10 minutes to play, making this a great game to pull out in an airport, waiting room, or anywhere else! Because of the short game length it's also great for using in a tournament scenario with groups of people. Don't let the game length and portability fool you into thinking that there isn't a lot of depth or variety in playing Battle Roll. The outcomes are nearly endless! A game could range from a complete blow-out, to a neck-and-neck down to the last roll, sudden death victory! It all depends on how the dice fall! Battle Roll is really fun to play, but it also looks cool! The combination of  custom polyhedral dice and acrylic life-stones are sure to attract attention and questions like "What are you playing?" It all fits nicely into a custom zippered pouch making it easy for Battle Rollers on the go to sneak in a quick game wherever they are.

Windy woody is a reaction game with a kite flying theme.

The modular board consists of 5 tiles printed on both sides.
There is one tile showing 6 kites of different colours, one tile showing boys who let the kites fly and three tiles with lines connecting the boys and the kites.

Further there are two dice (colour, task), 6 clothing cards and 21 ribbon cards to count the score.

There are 4 different levels of difficulty. For every level, the players try to be the fastest to grab the right clothing card. The way to determin the right card depends on the level.

Kites colour
Roll the colour die, follow the line form the corresponding kite and check which kind of clothing of the boy flying the kite has this colour.

Missing colour
Roll the colour die, follow the line form the corresponding kite and check which kind of clothing of the boy does not fit the colours (besides the main colour, each kite has 4 additionals clours for the ribbons and spars) of the kite.

Ribbons
Roll the colour die, follow the line form the two kites with corresponding ribbons and check which kind of clothing is identical for the two boys flying these kites.

Combination
Roll the colour die and the task die. The task die tells you according the which of the three rules to play.

The fastest player to grab the correct card scores a ribbon card. 
After every round the board is modified.

The game ends as soon as all ribbon cards are distributed, the player with the most cards wins.

Camel Up: Supercup includes four modules for the Camel Up base game. (does not work with the second edition of Camel Up)

Overview

Module 1, which includes the longer Race track and the new Supporting dice, makes for a longer and more exciting game, as Camels may move twice per Leg now. Also, you may spice this module up with an additional action.

Module 2 includes the Photographer and gives rewards for correctly predicting the movement of Camel stacks.

Module 3 introduces bets on positions, which makes betting more flexible.

Module 4 introduces Betting Partnerships, which allow players to cooperate more openly in games with 6 or more players.

Mighty Mice can be accommodate 2-5 players as a competitive game and 2-8 players as a co-operative game

To start, place an elephant in the center of the table and divide the mice evenly among the players. The first elephant is the base on which all mice or other elephants will be piled. Throw the dice, then add one or several mice or an elephant on top of the previous element. A steady hand, strategy and luck are definitely your best assets! Any mice that fall while you are adding to the structure become yours. Be the first player to dispose of all your mice to win!

In the co-operative game, players pick 15-24 mice dice (depends on the age of the players) and try to place all of the mice on top of the structure while the elephants try to interfere. All players win together when all of the mice are placed.

10 alternate zombies and 2 new street tiles that can be used with Escape: Zombie City.

The new zombies are: 1 x Queen, 1 x Robust, 2 x Runner, 3 x Gully, 2 x Leech and 1 x Burner, all of which have advanced properties.  The new street tiles are "the scrapyard" which can have equipment from any of the three equipment piles and "the radio tower" which allows you to move some of the zombies away from the church.

Rainbow colours and objects is a fun game where everyone can have fun together. The underlying theme is about recognising the pictures on the tokens, or matching the colours or creating a memory game with them. It indirectly teaches the youngest children to recognise and name colours of the rainbow while having a lot of fun. The dice with a colour on each side indicates which picture needs to be selected (or found from an overturned token). Granna have created many variations to this game.

boardgame: Star Realms: Mercenary Garrison Promo Card
description:
Promotional card that came with the Legion Supplies Flip Box. The Legion Supplies Card Box (STR982) has 3 copies of this card. A version of this card with alternate art was released in the Star Realms: Promo Pack I.

Mercenary Garrison
Unaligned Base
Outpost 5
(Gold deck construction icon)
Cost 4
No Primary Ability

Ally Abilities

    Star Empire: 2 Combat
    Machine Cult: Scrap a card from your hand or discard pile
    Trade Federation: 3 Authority
    Blob: Scrap up to two cards currently in the trade row
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

Breaker Bay, the second Data Pack in the "SanSan Cycle" for Android: Netrunner, continues the cycle's guided tour of the SanSan region with sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) focusing on the University of the Californias at Breaker Bay and its students. New resources, programs, and operations reflect everything from their living conditions to their parties, as well as the knowledge they hope to obtain and the efforts the world's largest megacorps make to recruit the best and brightest of them.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
This queenie has 2 alternate zombies, a street tile and eight new item tokens.

The two new zombies, Hammer-Zombie and Combi-Zombi, are tough and will each take multiple players to defeat (respectively 7 fists and 3 fists + 3 bats).

The new street tile is "the shack" which can be used as a safe house for one player who can not make it back to the church.

The new items give the player actions they can perform, but they can not be stored in the van:

     flashlight: explore an empty location and take a new item. Discard the flashlight.
     defibrilator: as long as the player is carrying the defbrillator, one calm face heals every panic face (this helps all players sharing a tile with its owner)
     crossbow: counts as a fist or a bat whenever you fight a zombie
     map: allows a player to move to any tile with 2 runner icons (even traveling through buildings to get there)
     backpack (3x): allows the player to carry 2 more items
     kevlar vest: players can use their calm faces when zombies are on the same tile as the player

This queenie contains 10 new task cards.  Select a number of them depending on your desired difficulty and combine them with the zombie deck.  When/if they come out, the task on the card must be completed before you can win.

Some of these task cards are:

     Comic Collector: go to the supermarket and roll 7 fists. This mus be accomplished before players can leave the town (need to have the latest issues to read on the road)
     Gang Fight: go to the gas station and bring the pain! (roll 7 bats)
     Voluntary work: go to the hospital and roll 5 calm faces

boardgame: Cinelinx: A Card Game For People Who Love Movies – Red Band Expansion
description:
54 NEW cards that add a mature, comedic skew to the original movie themed card game.
     Find connections between R-rated Comedies and Thrillers from the 90′s to today.
     First expansion to Cinelinx: A Card Game For People Who Love Movies.
     Professionally printed on premium playing card stock.
     A new take on movie trivia games. Dominoes for movie buffs.

Visual creativity game where players interpret inkblots to tell stories.  Has many models of play a few of which we review here.  More on our website.

Story Play: The Simplest RoarStack game, for casual group play or children,  is Story Play wherein you relate your card to the previously played card, possibly within the optional theme of the round.  In Story Play, the added sayings and card value/suits don't matter, just the inkblots and the story. This is a good mode for young children and people more interested in collaboration and storytelling than the competition.

Point Play: If you want to keep score and compete with others, then you can add the dimension of point play where victory points are scored based on phrase+value on the story cards when the conversation of the group matches the phrase. You play your victory cards at any time. Victory points can come from mundane things in the conversation from the weather, to social relations, to pop culture. Your inkblots and storytelling can help lead the conversation, but you don't score victory points from what you do/say, only from what your friends do/say.  This mode encourages talking with and listening to your friends and is great for party play. Even when it is not your turn to play a card, you need to pay attention and engage in dialog, maybe even trying to guide the conversation so you can score points. However, you also have to be active in the round to score so you have to keep that story going when it is your turn.  Victory scoring is generally simple -- you play the victory card in front of you so just count them at the end of the round.

Gestour/Best play: A mode good for larger groups or big/noisy parties is Gestour/Best mode where everyone competes to try to extend the same story. A gestour is a teller of legendary tales. In this mode, the gestour starts a story and everyone picks a card from their had and tries to be the best extension of that story based on the inkblot on their card. After the cards to play are exposed, the gestour defines how they will define "best". There is an optional 30 second free-for-all period where everyone tries to convince others that theirs is the best extension. The gestour then begins calling on players who have 10 seconds to tell their card's story. The gestour picks the "best" answer and that person becomes the new gestour. Everyone else draws a replacement card. The first person out of cards wins the hand.

Twisted Play: Another mode of play is Twisted play, which is good for people who play traditional card games. In Twisted play, you use just the playing card deck, and play your favorite classic card game, with a twist -- you can use the inkblots to connect cards or as wild cards. For example, in Roaring Rummy you might connect a random card into a part of a straight by telling the inkblots' secret stories and connecting misfit cards to those before and after it in sequence. In Roaring Go Fish, when you pull a card from the pool you can also match it if you can tell the story to connect it to your matching card.  In Solitary Stories you can play any card anywhere, but only if you can tell its story. Here, the role of the stories is to directly help you score points in a classic game. These add RoarStack Story play to existing games, making almost any card wild by using your creativity. It adds a new twist and often a lot more laughter, to well-known games.

Ideation Play: Another mode of play is ideation -- the generation of ideas related to solving a problem.  Here, the player(s) takes a problem to be solved and uses the cards to help explore the problem, the constraints, and potential solutions. RoarStack is not only a fun game; it can also be an innovation tool for improving creativity and problem-solving. Creativity in innovation and problem-solving is often about linking disparate ideas together. Given a topic, people see different things in the cards, and seeing things in the cards makes connections to other ideas. In the words of the very innovative Steve Jobs: "Creativity is just connecting things."  Dr. Boult, one of the creators, has use it in multiple corporate innovation tranining sessions and in the classroom with significant results.

Kickstarter edition sold out. Second edition printed in 2017 with new custom case and printed instructions.   THe second edition also modified some of the "point" questions based on user feedback from the first edition.

In Director's Cut: The Card Game, players are Hollywood movie producers in the early 20th century. Each player will try to put together the best movie-making teams in order to maximise the profit from each movie they make.

As movie producers, players will hire cast and crew from a central shared market, while trying to deny their opponent access to key cast and crew members. Once a player has assembled a set of cast and crew cards, they will play these cards to produce a movie from a shared set of screenplays. Each screenplay has its own set of cast requirements and awards cash and other bonuses, based on the quality of the production. The richest player at the end of the game is the winner.

Director's Cut: The Card Game is a game about set collection and resource management. It's suitable for 2 players and takes around 15 minutes to play.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Thirteen official plexes grace the continental jewel that is India. The largest is the Bengali Mahaplex with over 274 million citizens living within its walls. And every last one of them is a fan of Fury! Developed decades ago as the very first exotic Slaughterball team, Fury is a perennial favorite not just in India, but in all 72 metroplexes. Their distinctive feline beauty and grace, coupled with the savagery of their gameplay, has earned them a place in seven of the last ten Slaughterbrawls.

Fury’s greatest strength, their savage claws that can rip other neohumans to shreds, are also their greatest weakness. These felinoids are very inept at handling the ball, and their razors are the worst shooters in the league. Fortunately, Fury razors are also the fastest, so they can usually get pretty close to the goals to take easy shots.

So, you’re not going to win on goals. You better pounce on your opponents and do some damage. Thanks to the Savagery skill, you’ll be racking up the slaughter points. Don’t save those reactive cards (Penalty, Lost Momentum, etc.). You need to be sacrificing your cards to send opponents to the Slaughter Box early. Soon enough your opponents will team up against you to eliminate your butchers and slashers. So, attack with them early and often.

Perhaps the toughest team in the league, the Gruesomes have yet to prove themselves as contenders to the Slaughterbrawl crown. Like their other teams inspired by classic literature, Chemozoic Incorporated contracted Honeydew Labs to create this team genetic template. And while the athletes certainly look the part, their traits and skills have made it difficult to rack up the victories necessary to snag the championship.

The Gruesomes as a team rely on their resilience and sheer strength to dominate opponents and survive attacks. Unfortunately, they lack the hand-eye coordination and speed to have full access to all possible Slaughterball strategies. Indeed, their shooting game is perennially inadequate. Even so, previous coaches have managed to build them into a respectable foe over the course of a season by training them with new skills and concentrating on the strategies explained below.

Coaching the Gruesomes is a challenging task, but your victories will be that much sweeter knowing it was your coaching ability, not the athleticism of your athletes, that won the day. They are slower and less accurate than other teams, and as such you cannot rely on a shooting game to win. Instead, you should doggedly attack your opponents, even taking risky chops to knock them down. You should be chopping every turn. And get everyone moved toward the center of the pit as soon as possible. Field coverage is imperative for the Gruesomes, so they are always in range to attack someone.

Your athletes’ resilience will help them stay out of the Slaughter Box, and the great Brawling values will usually result in chop success. Once you have your opponent on the ground, send your slashers and butchers over to foul them on the ground and then cancel any Penalty cards with the Scare Ref skill.

Interrupt cards are very important to your fouling strategy, so be sure to cycle through your hand quickly. If you don’t have any Interrupt cards, discard your whole hand to find some. Being able to attack prone opponents and get four extra dice is paramount. Especially when you can avoid Penalties.

During the Third World War, robotic forces first assaulted islands across the world. Usually, they were able to conquer and eliminate their human foes in short order. Not so in Japan. The Japanese military and civilians fought back with intractable resolve, and saved their greatest city: Tokyo. And when the war ended with a human victory, the Japanese people rebuilt more quickly than anyone else.

With the formation of the United Globe government, and banning of advanced robots, Japan quickly retooled their industrial efforts to genetics. Today, there are hundreds of gene-tech labs in the Tokyo Metrofecture (TMF). But it was the megacorporation Honshu Tek that first engineered the Katanas team genetic template.

Tapping into their rich history, Honshu Tek created a team of samurai-themed athletes that quickly became the most popular team in the TMF. Now, with many years of strong performances, the Katanas frequently break into the playoffs and have more than one championship trophy hanging in their dojo.

Attack your opponents, repeatedly. With three skilled butchers, you should concentrate on weakening opposing teams from round one. Get your butchers and slashers into the center so they can use their mobility and power to dominate your opponents. Be sure to set up field coverage with your butchers. Their automatic carve skill can really block off entire slices of the pit. No one wants to run around those guys. After you’ve thinned out your opponents, you can have your razor snag the lonely ball and start scoring goals.

Legion is one of the few teams in the Global Slaughterball League that is completely owned by a regional government. In this case the Metroplex Roma subsidiary SPQR. Current SPQR CEO Tiberius Antonius Caesar-Caesar has dedicated vast sums of tax revenues to develop and market Legion into a juggernaut of popularity and success. Thanks to Caesar-Caesar’s guidance over the past two decades, Legion’s legions of fans have propelled the team to be one of the most financially successful in the league.

Legion is a thoroughly disciplined team of well-armored athletes, each with their own skills to contribute to the team. They excel at cooperating with each other to overcome opponents through teamwork. The cleavers are the foundation of this team unity. They are tactical geniuses who grant all teammates a bonus to their tests, plus they can hand out additional Onslaught tokens!
It will require an experienced coach to fully understand and lead Legion to victory. You’ll have to familiarize yourself with their impressive list of skills, and stay aware of every team member at all times to make sure you utilize them to their fullest potential. Legion is one of the most complicated teams to lead to victory, so be sure you are up to the task.

Even though all Legion athletes have strong skill sets, it is the cleavers’ tactical leadership that propel them to excellence. The value of more onslaught tokens cannot be overemphasized, and you should exploit that ability every turn. Also, do not forget the Leadership bonus that each of your athletes earns from the cleavers, and that you can add up the +1 bonus to tests from each of your cleavers in the pit.
To retain the extra tactical choices that they provide, the cleavers should be protected at all times. Do not expose them to attack or you’ll lose them to the Slaughter Box, along with their bonus dice and onslaught tokens.

Also, don’t forget the other athletes on the team, who also have great skills and traits. The razor is well-armored, with just a slight reduction in Speed. The slashers can double-team opponents for vicious effect. Even the butcher is better than average with his Awesome Brute skill. And all Legion athletes can stand up adjacent teammates with Help Up. Once you learn how to synergize all these skills, you’re well on your way to invincibility!

boardgame: Slaughterball: Team Carnage Arena – The Dungeon
description:
Located deep beneath the teeming Great Lakes Metroplex, the Dungeon is where the ogres and orcs of team Carnage inflict maximum mayhem on their opponents. Both the Octagon and Hexagon of the Dungeon enjoy (suffer?) an excess of spiky bits!

The Dungeon is an arena board for Slaughterball.

Special rules
Anyone knocked down or injured in a spike space suffers four extra knives of damage. The spaces do not block movement or the ball.

boardgame: Slaughterball: Team Fury Arena – The Jungle
description:
It’s no coincidence that the Fury slaughterdome named the Jungle is lost deep within the vast metropolis that is the Bengali Mahaplex. With over 270 million citizens, the BMP is a huge durocrete jungle, teeming with life and motion. Finding the Fury arena is a task in itself. But, once you find it, you’ll be amazed! The dome boasts ancient Indian architecture, holographic and genetically engineered flora and fauna, and flaming doom!

Fury’s owners not only genetically engineered their team, they also genetically engineered plants to impede everyone in the pit. These neovines are attracted to movement, crawling and writhing to tangle those who run by.

The Jungle is an arena board for Slaughterball.

Special rules:
While in the Fury pit, the following rules apply:

Entangling vines: The first time an athlete tries to leave an entangling space during a Move action, the athlete must make an Agility test. If the athlete does not get 2 or more knives, he trips and falls down in that space. This ends his movement and no points are scored. Entangling spaces are indicated by the green leaf in the center of a space.

Fireblast: Every coach gains this ability while in the Fury pit.

At irregular intervals, the meat grinder and the launchers belch forth great gouts of flame! At any time except during  an action, any coach can sacrifice an Interrupt card call forth such a conflagration from the meat grinder or any one specified launcher.

boardgame: Slaughterball: Team Gruesomes Arena – The Lab
description:
The Lab Arena is in a gothic slaughterdome that broods within the Wallachian Metroplex. Massive generators shoot bolts of electricity above the city as well as through the pits! These random bolts can stun even mighty  butchers.

The Lab is an arena board for Slaughterball.

Special rules:
All coaches gain the following ability while playing a game in the Lab:

Shock attack: At any time except during an action, any coach can sacrifice an Interrupt card to call down a bolt of electricity on an athlete in an electricity space (indicated by a faint electricity symbol).

The wildly arcing electricity in the pit will likely result in many more knockdowns. This will give the slower Gruesomes more chances to chop and foul. Teams in general will have fewer options because more of their players will be knocked down. With few “safe” spaces on the board, teams will clump up, giving the Gruesomes an easier time wading into groups of opponents.

boardgame: Slaughterball: Team Katanas Arena – The Dojo
description:
The Dojo graces the top of the Honshu Tek Arcology Prime in the Tokyo Metrofecture. The arena sports pits that are smaller than league standard, ensuring that the swift Katanas butchers have an even easier time getting to and brutalizing opponents.

The Dojo is an arena board for Slaughterball.

Special rules:
There are no special rules in the Dojo. The reduced size means coaches will have to adjust both their slaughtering and shooting strategies.

Notes: Both the Dojo Octagon and Hexagon have one fewer ring of spaces, making the pits smaller. This reduction in size means that athletes are closer together and don’t have as far to run away from rampaging butchers and slashers. This should result in more brutal games.

boardgame: Slaughterball: Team Legion Arena – The Colosseum
description:
Metroplex Roma boasts an exact replica of the ancient Roman Colosseum, but at twice the size! Plus, it sits atop the Caesarium Arcology, making it one of the most recognizable points of the Roman skyline.

In ancient times, gladiators fought each other and wild animals in the Colosseum. SPQR has opted to emulate this in the Legion’s home arena by unleashing random mavericks into the games. These athletes owe allegiance to no team, and earn money for each athlete that they slaughter! This results in a very chaotic game as the wild mavericks engage in all sorts of mayhem. Indeed, sometimes they even help a team that they might like. Often, Legion benefits from this maverick benevolence, making opposing teams wonder if there is some corruption going on. No proof of such has ever been presented, however.

Of course, some mavericks are more popular than others. Simba Khan elicits the most fan excitement, of course. Other butchers are also favorites, such as The Barbarian, Warren Johnston, Sandy Petersen, the Unspeakable One, and Four-Fisted Farouk. Razors are the least favorite, of course, because the fans want wild mavericks who can inflict maximum damage.

The Colosseum is an arena board for Slaughterball.

Special rules:
The Legion pits include spaces called Maverick Pillars, indicated by the M. These spaces block movement and no athlete can enter them. However, random mavericks appear on them to wreak havoc with all teams.

When the goals are opened, the first maverick is revealed. Draw a random maverick card and place the matching maverick figure on a random Maverick Pillar. This is a Wild Maverick, who owes allegiance to no single team. It is his job to cause as much mayhem as possible, attacking and thwarting all teams.

To simulate this, each coach can control any wild maverick during their Onslaught phase.

boardgame: Slaughterball: Team Nemesis Arena – The Core
description:
Polished to a shiny black sheen and glowing with neon highlights and sponsors, the Core Arena in the San Angeles Metroplex is where Nemesis calls home.

The Core is an arena board for Slaughterball.

Special rules:
All athletes in a Nemesis pit gain the following skills:

Strategic Enhancement: Whens standing upright on a Power Up space, take a special action to draw one card from the deck.

Power Cache (exhibition and league games only): When standing upright on a Power Up space, take a special action to knock yourself down. No points are awarded for this. You can take an Edge token of your choice from the supply. This simulates the athlete opening a small cache in the floor to grab a holotrophy that grants the coach a bonus.

boardgame: Slaughterball: Team Swords of Damocles Arena – Olympus
description:
The Octagon and Hexagon of the Olympus Arena in the Elliniki Historiplex are decorated with motifs and architecture of ancient Greece. The Swords of Damocles are rarely defeated in their home pits, thanks to the extra goals.

Olympus is an arena board for Slaughterball.

Special Rules:
Each Olympus pit has double the number of goals, making it easier for all teams to score points. But the razors of the Swords of Damocles are best-positioned to exploit this benefit, and games here are often very high-scoring.

All the goals start closed as normal, but since you only have four closed goal tokens, you’ll need to use some other markers (Onslaught tokens perhaps) to indicate that the extra goals are closed.

Valhalla Arena is a huge slaughterdome that includes home pits for both of Extraterrestrial Cruise Lines’ Viking-themed teams: the Valkyries and Ragnarok. The ladies’ arena, while brighter and cleaner, is both vicious and virtuous. Each Valkyries pit contains several Choosing spaces, which can be used to heal athletes in the Slaughter Box, or kill them!

Valhalla is an arena board for Slaughterball.

Special rules:
All athletes gain the following skills while in the Valkyries pits:

Chooser of the Living: While upright on a Choosing Space, take two special actions to remove all the injury tokens (if any) from one athlete in the Slaughter Box. Then, immediately relocate that athlete to any unoccupied Re-Entry space or starting space.

Chooser of the Slain: While upright on a Choosing Space, take two special actions to kill any one athlete in the Slaughter Box. That athlete is removed from the game. Unless using the optional Lasting Injury rule in League play, the athlete is resuscitated to full health for the next game.

King Elvish has proclaimed his daughter, the Princess, has decided to marry. The Princess has narrowed her suitors down to the finalists present. You are all Knights in service of King Elvish. And like all Knights of the Kingdom you are madly in love with King Elvish’s daughter. After much discussion, King Elvish and his daughter have agreed that the most valiant of her chosen suitors will become heir to the throne. The first Knight to present the Princess with the 3 Gifts of Gallantry she desires will win her favour. To acquire these gifts you must complete quests and obtain Laurels of Valor. Many Quests are impossible to complete alone. So, to demonstrate you are a born leader, you must hire and lead an adventuring party chosen from the most Motley Crew of the Kingdom. But, as a Knight of the Kingdom, you have worked your whole life in service and in turn been supported by the King. You have no gold to spare. In order to secure the necessary gold to pay your crew you must moonlight on OPQ (Other Patron’s Quests). Use your gold and crew wisely to quickly complete as many quests as possible. Thereby securing your place as the true Rock Star Knight of the Kingdom and rightful heir to the throne.

In Hard Day’s Knight each player holds 3 action dice, 1 patron die, some gold coins, and a hand of Motley Crew cards. Each round begins with the available Quests of the day posted at the center of the table. A round is a single hard day in a Knight’s life attempting to complete as many Quests as possible. All players simultaneously roll their 4 dice at once. The patron die randomly determines the turn order for each round. To complete a Quest and receive the Laurels and daily income from that Quest card, players must match their action dice with the dice icons on the Quest cards. But don’t be so quick to grab those Quest cards. Each player has a chance to hire Motley crew members and disrupt the dice on the table, or possibly the turn order, as you fight it out to be the first to complete the available Quests of the day.

Educational game for kids to learn colors, different shapes and the numbers 1-5.
The number of shapes on the Players Card have to match to the written number on the board, to place the card in front of him.
First games should start with 4 open Cards for each kid.
There is a round course with fields showing a shape and a number.
There is 1 pig figure which is moved by the Players.
On Players turn he puches an arrow, rotating to a field showing 1 to 4 feet. The Player can choose if he moves the pig this number of feet forward or back on track.
If the Player has a card showing exact the number of this kind of shapes he can turn the Card face down.
Next Players turn.
Winner is the first player having all Cards turned face down.
In further games the number of cards can be increased to 9 and it's possible to add a Memory part. All Cards are face down first and you have to know without looking first which of your cards match the pig field. (found Cards are turned to face up)

The adventure game based on the film
Through luck with the die and skillful challenges with Buzz and Woody you want to be the first to reach the removal van on one of the target squares.
On players turn, he rolls a die and moves along a path to reach the target the van.
There are a number of action fields of the kind:

     Van moves to a further away target field
     Additional turn
     Take the long way.
     Challenge: Duel with one other player using special Cards. Winner moves 3 fields.

On the steps of the Prado, four enemy tribes try to dispossess each other. The tribe chiefs have to be rescued. Otherwise the tribe is lost.
Position of the pawn gives the number of fields it can be moved. Captured pawns are out of the game.
The owner of the last tribe chief wins the game.

Bau den Turm is a dexterity game. You roll a die to determine which of six wood element you have to put on a hemisphere. 
When you roll the colour of an element already placed, game also passes to the next player.
The game ends when the structure collapses. The perpetrator loses the game.

Like its predecessor, [d0x3d!] v2 has players working together as a skilled team of hackers, pulling off a digital heist on a secured network to find and reassemble the pieces of their digital assets and escape before being detected by the network's administrators.

Players work against time, changing network topology, and increasingly aggressive sysadmins to score their objectives and get out before they're caught, or enough of the network is taken out of service to make reassembly or escape impossible.

The v2 version of [d0x3d!] changes the modular board - instead of square, adjacent tiles with unrestricted movement, the network is now made up of (24) hex tiles with asymmetric pathing to challenge the players by forcing them to forge a path through the system, and adds two new hacker roles (the forensics ninja and the traffic spoofer) to complement the powers of the other players.

[d0x3d!] v2 is released with a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 license (http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/3.0), allowing for the free sharing and remixing of the game.

When your two choices are make laws, or go to prison, you’ll scrape the bottom of the barrel to stay free.

ACHTUNG! is an abstract strategy game about creating a multitude of pointless laws and annoying rules for everyone in the game to follow.

As players draw and play cards, the number of laws continues to build until it becomes impossible for the average player to follow them all. When a player breaks a law, and someone else notices, they get called out by having ACHTUNG! shouted at them, and they must discard from their hand of cards. Once a player loses all of their cards, they’re put in the gulag and must catch someone else breaking a law to re-enter the game. The game is over when a lone lawmaker remains.
