The gameplay is separated into 5 distinct card types: Global Laws that everyone at the table must follow, Directed Laws that only effect a single player, Amendments that modify the text of cards already on the table, Filibusters that interrupt the main-game for a chance to win valuable cards, and Abolish cards that remove other cards from play.

ACHTUNG! is currently in its second edition. The second edition contains updates to the first edition rules and cards, such as allowing players to place markers on law cards to point out individual laws to play. Adjustments have also made to the Filibuster cards in the second edition to speed up gameplay.

Regatta is a combinatorial game for two players. It's the follow-up to Tixel, which itself is a follow-up to Tix.

On a turn, a player can either place a new piece or move one of her own pieces already on the board. Pieces can be placed either side up, effectively mirroring the shape of the piece. The only way to change which side is facing up is by first picking up a piece in one turn (as a bonus move action, see below), and then placing it anew in another turn with the other side facing up.

A piece can be "active" or "inactive"; only active pieces, which are at a 45º angle, may be moved. Making pieces inactive is done by moving next to or passing an active piece.

Chaining moves is important as this temporarily unlocks three additional move types (aside from placing or moving a piece). If a piece that is moved is made inactive at its end position — i.e., it is self-inactivated — a player can take an extra move if she so chooses; aside from placing or moving a piece, she can now remove a piece from the board, activate an inactive piece, or pivot an active piece to reorient it.

Another difference between Regatta and Tix/Tixel is that in Regatta pieces aren't at any time allowed to protrude outside of the board.

There's also a variant that allows the game to be played by three players.

Io is an Othello variant with a more flexible move protocol and a more 'organic' character. It knows neither Othello style compulsory capture nor compulsory passes. It ends when the board is full, the winner being the player with the most territory, and cannot end in a draw.

The legendary centaur Chiron from Greek mythology.

"You can play this card at any moment.  Until the end of the turn, each Art of War card that you convert into Power Points is worth 3 Power Points instead of 2."

A promo pack of 3 cards release by Iello.  One cat, one mirror, one ladder.

All cards have a zero value

Once a land of peace, Neotopia is now a world filled with danger where only those with the courage to be victorious will prosper. The SD collectible card game gives you the chance to command an army of Superior Defenders to conquer these dangers in an epic struggle for planetary supremacy! Armed with a deck of cards, each player's objective is to win battles among SD Gundams and advance their fleet of battleships into enemy territory, ultimately conquering their opponents base.

Selling precious fabrics, the finest of spices, and the freshest of fruits in Istanbul was very good for business. But now the customers at the bazaar have discovered a new delicacy: coffee! As the cunning merchant you are, you sense the fortune you can make by selling the tasty bean.

To overcome these new challenges, you will need more help than just that of your assistants. Luckily, for a baksheesh, the guests at the tavern are willing to take a little side job. Also, at the guild hall, you can find the valuable help you need.

The first expansion for Istanbul, the 2014 Kennerspiel des Jahres, Istanbul: Mocha & Baksheesh brings four new places, more interaction between the players, and new ways to gain the coveted rubies. The Bazaar favorite of all gaming enthusiasts just got a little more attractive.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: MindMaze: Verzwickte Rätsel – Geld, Ruhm und Macht
description:
In MindMaze: Verzwickte Rätsel - Geld, Ruhm und Macht, as in other MindMaze sets, players take turns asking questions to try to solve mysteries, in this case dark, morbid tales.

In general, a referee tells a strange story, then the players must determine why and how the story happened by asking "Yes" or "No" questions, with "Not Important" also being a plausible answer. Players take turns asking questions, with a point being subtracted for each question. Players are awarded points for asking certain key questions that eliminate faux versions of the story. The list of useful key questions is attached to every riddle. This sort of gaming procedure effectively encourages players to develop careful analytical questions and eliminates random questions.

Wrummy 3-4-5 is a fun new twist on the classic card game rummy that you play by spelling out words! This new 108-card deck allows you to play rummy, but also can be used to play games like poker, solitaire, and bull$#@! (aka Cheat).

What sets this apart from past combinations of rummy and word games is the scoring system.  Very much similar to classic rummy, letter cards are colored according to their rarity, and thus their points.  The most rare are 15 points, like aces.  The less common are 10 points, like face cards, and the common cards are 5 points.  There are also 4 wilds, which score 0 points.

boardgame: Marco de los Apeninos a los Andes
description:
Game based on a character from a Spanish children's film about a young boy's journey from the Appenines to the Andes.

32 cards in four suits of 8 cards. The first player lays a card. The next player must play either the same colour card or the same value of the played card. The four cards numbered 5 may be played as a Joker and can represent any umber or colour.

The object is to get rid of all the cards in your hand. First to do so scores nil points. The other players score points equal to the value of each card left in their hand. Wild cards still in a hand score 25 points against the holder. The player with the lowest score when a pre-arranged number of games have been played, wins.

Land of legends is a board game that will intrigue any and all fans of strategic and PvP type battles. The number of player may vary from 2 to 4 with an age limit of 13 and up. The rules of the game are very simple.

The main objective of the game is to rule over the entire island.

In the Land of Legends game there are 2 races: human and undead.

At first you have to choose between those races, as well as the place where player want to place his castle.

Locations of castles can be established through discussion, if positions cannot be agreed upon players must roll the dice to establish castle placement.

In the game there are two playing fields: The World Map and The City. The World Map is where you will send your armies to battle your opponents and capture enemy castles; throwing dice will move your army in the direction of your choosing. During your journeys you will encounter bonuses and obstacles. Also during your journey throughout the world map you can develop your castle. While on the playing field of the World Map players will land on certain places that will move them to the City Map where they will have the opportunity to hire mercenaries such as: Wizards, Archers, Swordsmen and Horsemen. Players will also be able to buy or find various artifacts and potions for their hero.

Game based on a character in a Spanish children's series.

All the cards are dealt out and play continues in the same way as quartet games. You ask another player for a certain numbered card. If that person has it, the card is given to the player who asked for it. Then he may ask another player (or the same player again) for another numbered card. If he gets a positive response, his turn continues. But if the player asked does not have that cards, the questioner's turn ends and the player asked starts the next round of questions.

The object is to lay face-down in front of you any set of 3 consecutively numbered cards. These are safe and cannot be taken by anyone else. On future turns you may add further numbers as long as they are of a consecutive number. When all the cards are placed in such 'runs', the game ends and the player with the most cards, wins.

This is a Disney related game and the name means 'Duck Tales'.

The shuffled deck is placed face down. The first player draws a card. If it is a 1, he plays it front of himself. If it is not a 1, it starts a personal discard pile for that player. Play continues in this vein but once owned discard stacks start to appear then a player may add his drawn card to any stack of the other players or himself as long as the number on the played card is consecutive with the top card on any player's stack.

When the last card has been taken from the central pile, the game continues and the first player to get rid of his discard pile, wins that game. He scores nil and the other players score 1 for each card in their hand. After a pre-arranged number of games, the player with the lowest score wins.

User summary:

ちからあわせる二百万！ (Chikara awaseru nihyakuman!) is a Japanese card game for younger children. The game uses the popular character Gunma-chan, who is the mascot for the Gunma prefecture in Japan. The game involves drawing cards up to "200" without overdrawing them, so it can be played by children so long as they can count to 20. Objects found locally in Gunma appear in this quick party game.

From the Publisher:
"『ちからあわせる二百万！』は、ゆるキャラで大人気！群馬県のマスコット「ぐんまちゃん」を使ったお手軽パーティカードゲーム。
数字を「２００」ピッタリにするだけの簡単バーストゲームなので、
０から２０までの足し算が出来るお子さんなら楽しく遊ぶことが出来る。
群馬の郷土アイテム「上毛かるた」がモチーフの「つちけ、すもの、おかめきけ」といった役札要素で、最後に大逆転も起こるかも？"

Go Fishing is a magnetic fishing game for kids where you use your pole to try to find the color of fish that comes up on the spinner and put it in your boat. They game combines the dexterity of catching fish with a magnetic pole with the memory of where the colored fish are.

Flipped Progress is a game pre-developed as a Global Game Jam challenge of 2015 which the theme was "What do we do now?".

In this game players shall cooperate to balance the environment between nature and pollution, also, managing energy points which are required to perform actions.

Every turn, the board acts through a dice and the possible outcome is: nature grows, industries produce both energy and pollution, cities grow or finally new location tiles are placed. Players can shift the odds on their favor against the board by using a dice system in which faces can be changed.

Each tile generates its own type of tokens and as they get filled up with these tokens they can or spread it to neighbor tiles or it's own tile is flipped to reveal an opposite aspect of itself, for example, woods can become a desert and a city can become ruins.

At the end of a turn, players choose how to spend energy: changing the environment or generating progress.
Finally, At the count of 5 progress points, the players wins.

The All-Star Baseball Card Game is a system for using your own collection of baseball cards to simulate a game, a series, or an entire season. Teams are only limited by your collection and your imagination!

The game uses a 10-sided pitcher's die, and a 12-sided batter's die to determine outcomes based upon the players' statistics and the included tables. Each roll of the dice represents a single at-bat; the game information is then recorded with your own pencil and paper.

Advanced rules are included to permit for stolen bases, extra bases, and double plays.

144: Champions of Hope is a blend of Euro-Victory Point Race and Theme-Heavy Ameritrash strategy game for 2-4 players. Set in the world of Traesparin, the setting for the novels 144: Wrath and 144: Redemption, players take on the role of Champions to expand, build, and battle their way into legend. Recruit fearless Heroes and discover powerful Artifacts as you amass your forces. Will you master arcane magic or win through the strength of steel? Champions can even join together to take on greater threats as they vie for the most Glory.

Players start the game with two Lands under their control. Each turn, the controlling Champion (the player whose turn it is) rolls a ten-sided die for Production. The result of the role is compared to their Lands and Resources are acquired by all players accordingly. The controlling Champion can then use acquired Resources to expand to new Lands (thereby increasing their Resource production), recruit Heroes (cards) to increase their Magic and/or Combat power, improve their land with Tower Upgrades that add to the Production roll's result, or purchase Artifacts (cards) that give Glory and additional passive or active benefits. Additionally, players may undertake secret Quests that provide Glory, primarily, at the end of the game.

The main source of Glory, however, is Battle. The controlling Champion will end his or her turn with the option of sending forces into Battle, challenging their choice of Rank 1-5. In Battle, Champions must choose whether they will fight with the strength of Combat or the power of Magic. The chosen Battle Rank card is turned over and the controlling Champion faces the challenge by rolling a ten-sided die and adding their chosen power. If the result exceeds the target, they win and receive both Glory and Reward (in the form of Resources). If they lose, Glory is lost.

Additionally, the controlling Champion may solicit aid from another player before the Battle begins. Players can combine their powers to take on greater threats (and earn greater Glory) than they could on their own.

End game is triggered when a player reaches 144 Glory (tracked around the edge of the game board). Points from Quest cards and remaining Resources are added in to determine the player with the most Glory, the Champion of Hope!

Fool's Dice is a simple race and gambling game where players roll dice and attempt to move their marker up a metaphorical mountain. Players initiate "scrambles" where they roll specially-designed "target dice" to create a target number, then roll "number dice" to see if they can score under that target number. If they do so, they can move their marker a specified number of steps up the mountain. They can choose to gamble and continue their "scramble" until they fail to meet their target.

If the target roll is not met, they will fall back to where they started at the beginning of their turn. Additionally, they must pay a penalty. These penalties are devised at the start of the game by the players and provide the "party" atmosphere, since penalties can be things like "Wash one dish in the sink," or "Run around the room 10 times."

Players also have three chances to knock a player's marker back down the mountain. This action, combined with the supposed humorous nature of the penalties, provides as the box says, "An extremely unique and wildly intense party game for two to six players."

According to the interior of the rule book, the first printing of this 1991 edition was of 3000 copies.

A promo holiday-themed Gear card and recipe with a corresponding set of 3 hard plastic armor kit compatible weapon bits.  The 3 weapon bits are a left-handed Xmaxe, right-handed Xmaxe, and no-handed Xmaxe.

This was included as part of the X-mas Holiday Pinup Twilight Knight, a scenic pinup figure that is unrelated to the game.

The original release was limited, only available for approximately 10 days at the end of December '14 into the beginning of January '15. It has been available during the Holiday period of most years since.

Gear Recipe
Cost: 2x Leather, 2x Bone, 1x Fresh Acanthus
Innovation Requirements: Sculpture, Storytelling

As per the store's advise: Promo cards are intended as light-hearted content that is created for fun and should not be taken seriously in the context of Kingdom Death: Monster. Promos are not considered official additions to the rules and players should add them at the discretion of each player group.

Quartermaster General: Air Marshal adds two entirely new mechanics to Quartermaster General.

Bolster cards are played directly from your hand at a time specified on the card, adding a whole new element of surprise and challenge to the game.

Your Air Forces provide an additional dimension to battle. Air Force pieces are deployed to the board through play of a Deploy Air Force card. Once in play, Air Forces may support Army or Navy units in the same space and apply additional pressure to adjacent enemy pieces.

Pack of 4 figures Krosmaster Kerubim. This pack contains the promos released during the second wave issued in French Flunch restaurants from December 2013 till January 2014. 
Each pack includes 4 figurines, 4 game accessories.

The 4 figurines are:
- Lou 
- Joris 
- Young Kerubim
- Old Kerubim

boardgame: Quartermaster General: Air Marshal – Peace Treaty promo tile
description:
Sent to backers of the 2015 Kickstarter campaign for Quartermaster General: Air Marshal.

Details on this card:
Can be used immediately after the players draw their cards at the beginning of the game, before discarding any cards to form the opening hand. If a country from each team agrees, both of those countries may immediately shuffle the cards drawn back into their decks and draw again.

Quote about this card by the games designer Ian Brody:
Maybe this should "simulate" something like the German/Soviet treaty, or the Soviet/Japanese treaty, but I felt like I loved the iconic imagery of the three leaders, Churchill, Stalin, and Roosevelt sitting at Yalta.

boardgame: Quartermaster General: Air Marshal – Air Supply promo tile
description:
Sent to backers of the 2015 Kickstarter campaign for Quartermaster General: Air Marshal.

Details on this card:
You may remove an Air Force from the board at the beginning of your Supply Step to prevent an unsupplied Army or Navy from being removed this turn.

Game designer notes:

(First FAQ - No, the Air Force does not have to be supplied.) I'd expect to see this rule used about once per game, Can allow for some strategies that have hitherto been a bit to dependent on supply. Can vastly decrease the impact of supply in certain situations.

(Second FAQ - an Air Force can only save one piece; it doesn't put a piece back in supply, to supply other pieces.) Of course the rule is optional, as are all the "tiled" rules.

ChitChat is a card game for two players that simulates the exchange of small talk between two acquaintances at a social function. Each player has a pet topic and the first player to make a couple of points on their pet topic wins the game. Alternatively if one player lets the conversation lag, then their opponent has won.

The game is played with a deck of cards formed from two regular decks.
The suits are considered to match the following topics:
    Hearts    - sport
    Diamonds  - economy
    Spades    - politics

The cards are laid out in a track as played, showing the flow of conversation. The game tracks who is currently winning the argument by having the layout stepped towards the currently dominant player. Strategy revolves around the tension between holding cards back to block versus capitalizing on an advantage. You can win by scoring a few points, or by running your opponent's hand down so that they are unable to play.

Pocket Imperium: Mech Worlds is a promo tile for Pocket Imperium, it adds more variety and a different way to take advantage of Expand and Explore actions.

Just before placing the 6 standard sector tiles around Tri-Prime, randomly select one to remove, and replace it with the Mech World sector tile. Complete the rest of game setup normally.

Yellow Mech Worlds
These systems have been completely taken over by self-replicating machines that will help whoever is controlling them to rapidly construct fleets. If, during the execution of an Expand action, a player adds one or more ships in a hex containing a yellow Mech World, 1 additional ship (if available) may be added. Yellow Mech Worlds have a system value of 0. This means that 0 points are scored for controlling them during the Exploit phase and only 1 ship may remain there at the end of a round.

Blue Mech Worlds
Endowed with life forms that thrive in the low-density atmosphere of these worlds, these systems and their launch facilities allow fleets that launch from them to rapidly reach deep space. If, during the execution of an Explore action, a player begins movement of a fleet from a blue Mech World, the fleet being moved may move 1 additional hex this turn.

Three Sticks is a board game that takes geometry to the next level. Players are challenged to think creatively and use only three types of sticks to create geometric shapes. In each turn, players add two sticks to the board and earn points for the shapes created. With compelling game mechanics and exciting surprises, Three Sticks is a promising entertainer for both kids and adults.

Players begin with six sticks, chosen randomly using reload cards. The three types of sticks, purple, orange and red, are 3, 4 and 5 units long respectively and can be placed along the blue dots marked on the board. While all three sticks can be placed horizontally or vertically, red stick can also be placed diagonally.

Each player places two sticks in their turn, with each stick's tip touching the tip of at least one other stick on the board. If a geometric shape is created in the process, the player earns points. Points are awarded as per three parameters :
1. Perimeter of the shape 
2. Bonus as per the type of the shape 
3. Points in blue circles, if the shape surrounds any such circles on the board.

Once all six sticks are used, a player may collect six more by picking a reload card. Additionally, five power cards are distributed to each player at the beginning of the game. These cards provide special powers to the players that can improve their position in the game or slow down the progress of other players. The cards may be used in any turn up to a maximum of two per turn.

The first player to reach a score of 500 wins the game.

HACKER RAT
This is a strategic hacker-powered version of russian roulette.

Players take turns drawing cards until someone draws an hacker RAT and loses the game. The deck is made up of cards that let you avoid blowing up by peeking at cards before you draw, forcing your opponent to draw multiple cards, or shuffling the deck.

The game gets more and more intense with each card you draw because fewer cards left in the deck means a greater chance of drawing the hacking RAT and blowing up computer devices.

Make a 5 in a row in any color for the win!

Materials
8x8 checkerboard
28 Reversi (Othello) disks

Setup
The 28 disks are placed on the border spaces of the board in an alternating pattern of black and white.

Play
Decide who goes first in any convenient manner, turns then alternate.

On a turn, you must either,

1) Move a piece (black or white) any distance in a straight line, orthogonally or diagonally, without jumping over other pieces, then flipping the moved piece over and ending the move in an empty space that is adjacent (orthogonally or diagonally) to at least one other piece on the board or

2) Move a piece (following the movement rule above) from a row consisting of three or four like-colored adjacent (orthogonal or diagonal) pieces, but in doing so, you lose your next turn or

3) After moving a piece, on the same turn, you may perform a custodian Capture by having the moved piece, and another like colored piece on either side of at least one straight (orthogonal or diagonal) occupied line of opposed colored pieces (1-3 pieces if you are player one or 1 or more if player two). The trapped piece(s) are then flipped over.

Restrictions
A player may not repeat the board position of the previous turn.

Object
The game ends, and a win is made, when player one makes a five in a row (orthogonally or diagonally) in either color or player two a row of five or more in either color.

boardgame: BattleLore: Second Edition – Great Dragon Reinforcement Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Unleash a dragon's fire with BattleLore (Second Edition): Great Dragon Reinforcement Pack! This Reinforcement Pack features a Great Dragon that can be included in any army, alongside three new lore cards to customize your lore deck and a unique terrain tile. With the Great Dragon in your army, you can defend your hoard of treasure, or unleash a devastating firestorm on any unit that dares to approach!

What's more, this Reinforcement Pack includes a pre-built scenario: "Into the Fire". In this scenario, both armies start on one side of the battlefield and they must race to be the first to overpower the Great Dragon and claim its hoard. Whether you use the Great Dragon in this scenario or muster him with your army, it brings an undeniable threat to every game of BattleLore (Second Edition).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: BattleLore: Second Edition – Mountain Giant Reinforcement Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Bring the might of a Mountain Giant to your army with the BattleLore (Second Edition): Mountain Giant Reinforcement Pack! This Reinforcement Pack features a Mountain Giant that can be included in any army, alongside three new lore cards to customize your lore deck and a unique terrain tile. With the Mountain Giant at your side, you can hurl massive boulders at the enemy, or even use the Mountain Giant's crushing blows to reduce occupied buildings to rubble!

What's more, this Reinforcement Pack includes a pre-built scenario: "The Sound of Thunder". In this scenario, the Uthuk Y'llan call upon the power of the Mountain Giant in order to liberate prisoners captured by the Daqan Lords. Whether you use the Mountain Giant in this scenario or muster him with your army, he brings an undeniable threat to every game of BattleLore (Second Edition).

boardgame: The Mountain: A Solo Board Game Experience
description:
The Mountain is a board game experience for one player. It explores a pensive man's descent from a mountain from the moment he reaches the peak. You navigate the mountain while exploring the man's thoughts as he contemplates about the unknown abyss that lies exactly after his life's biggest accomplishment.

There are five exit points on the board, one for every element that will affect your journey - frost, sun, wind, sky and horizon. As you try and find the path down, you learn more about yourself through the story cards, divided into five different story lines that affect you as a protagonist.

However, the path down is not immediately obvious. Your movement subdues and stimulates the elements. If three or four elements are acting, you start suffering from ennui; a feeling that perhaps getting to the bottom of the mountain is not that important after all. If all five elements are raging, you will succumb to nature and die.

Traverse through the safest path and take care of yourself. This could be either the most important journey of your life, or your last.

Story Writing by - Johnathan Harrington, Mathew Agius Muscat, Fran Borg
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Reprint of the german versions of Cornucopia and Alchemy, published in one box.

boardgame: Quartermaster General: Prisoner Exchange promo tile
description:
This promo tile could be acquired by purchasing Quartermaster General at certain conventions.

Text: "Can be used at any time, but only once per game. If a country from each team agrees, both of those countries immediately draw a card."

This game is a free web-published board game on Command & Conquer 1: Tiberian Dawn.

It is a wargame for 2 players. Build your base, harvest precious crystals of Tiberium, and crush your enemy.

You have tokens for your units and your buildings. Maintain power in your base, harvest Tiberium and eliminate the construction yard of your opponent.

Additional features allows you to play with event cards which are spicing up the game !

From the publisher's catalogue:

The players' task is to develop industry and infrastructure in Silesia - region know for it's abundant natural resources. They erect mines, ironworks and factories as well as extend their reach by laying rails. At the same time they have to take care about Silesian environment by reforesting wastelands.

User Summary:
Silesia is new board games by Krzysztof Matusik in the game line started by Cargo Train. The basic components are 110 cards. Silesia is a card game where players will focus on building, mining and producing in combined engines.

From the publisher's catalogue:

You are a city founder. Like each and every city, yours also needs inhabitants. You've got a whole year to create and ideal community. During each season you'll be able to choose from among many professionals. Pick them and take care of relations between them. Use their abilities to help develop your growing community.

User Summary
People Are People is a new board games by Krzysztof Matusik from game line started by Cargo Train. The basic components are 110 cards. People Are People use card drafting mechanic like in 7 Wonders. In this game players try to build their own ideal, perfect, utopian society. But as we all well know, people are only humans and it isn't as easy as it sounds.

boardgame: The Magnates: A Game of Power – Princehood

description:
DOV is the base set of rules for WW2 and Modern Tactical wargaming at the Squad and Platoon level.  It is the base product for the "Valour" series of games, and handles all the rules for the small-scale tactical combat play for that system.  Unit's and counters represent squads, individual tanks, support weapons, crews, etc.

This game can be played either stand alone with miniatures for 15mm, GHQ, or HO miniatures. The company also has several hex map and counter add on modules that can be used for more traditional non-miniatures based play.

DOV is a fast paced set of game rules that strikes a good balance between realism in war game simulation play and playability. Unit cards contain detailed weapons, vehicle and nationality based attributes that give players a crisp experience of the small scaled fighting realities of that historical era.

When combined with the add on modules for Flags of Valour, this system also integrates with a new kind of Fog of War set of mechanics never before used in war gaming.

The base rules are an 80 page booklet, 12 page data book, Command and STRAT cards, along with a sheet of control and marking counters for Unit Status.

Comes with data cards on the vast majority of German, American, Russian, British, and Commonwealth troops used during World War 2.

'From the game description:'

Strike off the list to pile on the points. A fun and challenging list game!
Packed with 80 entertaining Strike Off list cards, plus rules.

To play the game, you draw a card, read a question on it and the next player tries to list as many items that would answer the question as he/she can. I.e., if a card asks "There have been 6 James Bond actors - name them", the following player would earn a point for each correctly named actor, and additional STRIKE OFF bonus of one point if they name all 6. The first player to reach 15 points wins.

Alternatively, players can divide into teams or if they prefer longer or shorter game, they may agree on a different points total at the start of the game.

description:
In Argent: Hall Monitor, a small abstract strategy game that uses the pieces from Argent: The Consortium, students battle over a hallway in Argent University, which is set in the World of Indines universe created by Level 99 Games.

Each student has a different power depending on the magic school that student follows, with the school being signified by its color, and your goal is to reach the other side of the hallway with one of your students.

Players take turns placing mages from a common pool of available mages. When the mage is placed it enacts whatever power is associated with its school of magic. For example, placing a Sorcery mage will remove an adjacent mage from the board. Other powers are passive, such as the Nature mage which cannot be directly targeted by any enemy mage. Players must use these powers in creative and strategic ways in order to defeat their opponent and make it to the end of the hall.

Players roll the die to move around the game board, attempting to land on Destination Spaces in order to collect charms to put on their charm bracelets.  Once a player has collected 4 charms (one of each possible color), they only have to return to their Home Space to win the game.

This 110-card expansion adds new locations, monsters, villains, and more to the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game; it also includes rare loot cards and 5 new scenarios that make up the complete "Demon’s Heresy" adventure.

boardgame: Warmachine: High Command – Faith & Fortune: Engines of Destruction
description: EXPAND YOUR COMMAND! Bolster your armies with Engines of Destruction, an 84-card expansion for WARMACHINE High Command Faith & Fortune! Engines of Destruction contains 5 copies each of 16 different cards, plus 4 additional warcaster cards, to bring new strategies to your Retribution of Scyrah, Convergence of Cyriss, Highborn Covenant, and Four Star Syndicate decks.

Upstairs is an exciting, tactical race for the entire family. Two to four players take control of one first-class secret agent each (m/w ^^). Your group entered the base camp of the evil dude, but instead of the villain all you find in the basement is a giant bomb. To make matters worse, there is no easy way out  the janitor has locked all the doors shut. The only escape route is via the roof. But there is no need to be afraid, because just like in every good action movie, the bomb will only explode when the (first) agent is taking off with the helicopter. Good for him, bad for the rest that is left behind.

Upstairs - A game ...

for a gang of up to four friends, where the one that comes second is the first to lose the game. So you all better hurry up, and race as fast as possible through the labyrinthine high-rise building, just to be the lucky, or in fact the only survivor. Upstairs is an excellent racing game with a great charm.

In Upstairs, the 9 playing cards make up the high-rise building, from which the players have to escape. Not only do they have to run through the labyrinthine base, but also to dodge the extremely aggressive guards, who in the harder gaming variant can end the life of an agent faster than he would have liked. Winner is the first player to reach the roof. Only then will the jump  naturally, and befittingly, in slow-motion - succeed in the nick of time, with the agent grabbing the skids of the descending helicopter, carried off to the next hotel, where wine, women and song are waiting for our hero. The short playing time of around 15 to 20 minutes makes Upstairs the perfect warm-up game for any games meeting.

12 wolfs are around the moon.
On his turn, a player rolls two dice and choose the sum of one or both dice to place his pawn onto the wolf with the same value. 
Game ends if all wolfs have at least on pawn on it.
The players count their points depending the positions of their pawns and the player with the most points is the winner.

In Trickerion the players take on the roles of rival stage illusionists, each with their own strengths and characteristics. They are striving for fortune and fame in a competition hosted by a legendary magician, looking for a successor worthy of the mighty Trickerion Stone, which is fabled to grant supernatural power to its owner.

Dahlgaard's Gifts, an addon pack for the game, contains the following:

Magician Powers
Your Trickerion Shards gain a whole new gameplay significance with the Magician Powers variant: they will unlock Power Card slots that allow you to customize your Magician by learning diverse abilities to enhance your game. As there are 90 different Power cards available, it will open up a multitude of new strategies and adds even more replayability to the game.

Duel of the Magicians
A rule variant designed specifically for 2-player games. The Duel of the Magicians adds various turn setup cards that change the available spots turn by turn, making the 2-player game more tense and diverse. It also comes with special Performance cards with neutral Trick Markers on them, making Linking easier in two-player games.

Additional game content
On top of the two major game modules, Dahlgaard's Gifts also comes with:

- 25 additional Trickerion Shards and a large acrylic Trickerion Stone turn marker
- 8 additional Special Assignment cards (2 for each Location)
- 4 Magician Meeples to replace the wooden cylinders on the Initiative Order track

Game Description:
Two players take up the role of opposing sides as infantry squad leaders. Your squad has been called upon to re-gain control of a sector that is getting overrun by your opponent. Kill enemy targets, complete objectives, and regain control of sectors to score points to win. As a leader you will need to monitor your squad's tenacity,  manage risks, apply countermeasures. You will also decide when to take tactical halts allowing your members to catch their breath, apply first aid, and reload weapon systems.

Welcome to Gauntlet of Heroes. Are you ready to take up the gauntlet against other Heroes and face the many challenges that await your party?

Your heroes delve into a long forgotten crypt, rumored to be filled with treasure and danger! Equip your team of brave Heroes as they defeat monsters and maneuver past obstacles to find the treasure and gold that is hidden there. Your Heroes will also face opposition from other parties searching the dungeon. Your Heroes will journey until they find and defeat the elite monster in the final room. The party of Heroes with the most gold wins. So gear up and get out there and show your opponents who the REAL Hero is!

The goal of the Gauntlet of Heroes is to be the player with the most Gold Pieces when the game ends. The player gains Gold Pieces by completing Challenges (defeating monsters, overcoming obstacles, and collecting treasures).

In Fidelitas, players take on the role of faithful citizens in a medieval city exerting influence in order to gain the credibility needed to lead the charge against the corrupt crown. Players play character cards to various locations that make up the city in order to meet conditions of hidden objectives. Each type of character card is associated with a unique action that manipulates cards in the game; use the Butcher to bully other characters to new locations, use the Student to gain more cards, use the Soldier to keep the peasants from loitering too long.

These nine promo cards include the Candlestick Maker Promo Cards - a new Virtus Card and a new Missio Card; the Barkeep and the Tavern, two new Missio cards; and the Party Pack Promos that include 5 cards - The Bloody Butcher, The Chiselerwith two new versions of The Chiseler and The Herald with art by Jaqcui Davis, as well as The Bloody Butcher, The Chiseler, and The Heralds.

description:
With the rise of a powerful yet mysterious Shadow Organization, several countries send out their own Zero Agents to investigate. Upon hearing that this organization has already gathered dangerous intel, the Zero Agents must infiltrate the enemy HQ and quickly hack into their systems and recover the master plans.

As a Zero Agent, your other objective is to ensure you have more intel than the others in order to give your Country the advantage in the overall power struggle for leadership.

Will you accept this mission?

Zero Agents is a fast paced, tile-based miniatures game that utilizes dice and card strategy. The game requires your agent to hack computers that randomly appear, while fending off guards that continue to spawn in response to player dice rolls. Your goal is to get as much intel through hacking and stealing from other players as possible. Once the guards sound the alarm, there is a mad dash to the extraction point before the enemy base is overrun by guards!

Design a Brotherhood completely customized to your mercenaries: select the source of your heroes, their profession, choose merits and defects, and choose from a broad range of equipment, weapons, special abilities, and more!
 
Circle of Blood offers you the ultimate experience Heroic Action; play against other players or cooperate with them to overcome challenges of intense scenarios. The city is alive, and has a system of completely independent Artificial Intelligence makes a Gamemaster not necessary.

Description from the publisher:

"What roads would any dare to tread, what safety would there be in quiet lands, or in the homes of simple men at night, if the Dúnedain were asleep, or were all gone into the grave?" –Aragorn, The Fellowship of the Ring

For nearly seven-hundred years, the Witch-king of Angmar terrorized the North from his home in Carn Dûm. Though his forces were eventually defeated, the shadows of the Witch-king's evil have lain across the lands through all the centuries that followed his departure from the region.

Now, in The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Wastes of Eriador, several of Middle-earth's heroes must join forces with the noble Dúnedain who have long guarded the region’s simpler folk from the untold evils that still reside within Carn Dûm and its environs. Following immediately upon the heels of the epic events that unfold in The Lost Realm deluxe expansion, the scenario from The Wastes of Eriador tasks you to rescue a captive Ranger. However, as you pursue the enemies who have captured him, your trail leads you ever northward, ever deeper into the hills of Angmar. There, as day fades into night, the howling of wolves suggest that your party of hunters may soon become the hunted...

description:
Imagine an intricately mechanized clash of spy against spy, introducing corporate espionage to a Steampunk world of industrial revolution; filled with all of the plot twists and thrills of both genres. That's the world of Steam Spy, a Steampunk themed role playing card game for 2 players. Steam Spy unites the character development and narrative of a traditional role playing game with the intimacy and spontaneity of a card game.

Players create a unique secret agent character who works for one of the organizations designated to protect and serve one of the world’s largest corporations.  Then they face off against an operative from an opposing secret industrial organization in a one on one battle of wits, luck and exotic steampunk gadgets.  One player instigates some kind of espionage mission while the other does their best to stop them.

Players collect, play and draw cards that help create a series of exciting encounters that ultimately determine if the instigating antagonists succeeds at their mission, or the determined protagonist chases them down and defeats them; players then advance their spy character, or create a group of secret agent specialists in preparation for the next face off against operatives from opposing agencies and corporation.

Steam Spy: Action and Adventure gives you the opportunity to leap into action as a Federal Agency agent, a straight shooting provocateur using the advanced weaponry of North America’s 
Renningson and Sons Firearms. Or you can join the Unified Alliance and use the resources of Navidad Pharmaceuticals to keep you and your team safe in the most thrilling adventures in the wildest environments in the world.

The 10th War is a head to head battle game about World War 10. It is based in the future around 2300 AD. There are currently two factions, the rebels and the oppressors. Customize your deck, command vehicles, events, characters, and equipment. Use those cards to attack their forces, roll dice, and once their characters have all been cleared out of the way, you can attack and destroy their base, winning the game.

Armor & Ash is a medieval fantasy themed battle card game for two players. The base set contains four different factions, each with its own unique warriors, heroes, spells, and arcane items.

Control a band of Vikings, Forest Elves, Ghob-lings, or Undead and unleash havoc and devastation on your foes. Defeat the opposing Hero or eliminate their army and declare yourself the victor.

Faction decks are pre-constructed, so there is nothing to collect or anything additional to buy.  Each faction deck contains:
19 Common Units
1 Elite Unit
3 Heroes (only 1 is used during game play)
5 Spells
2 Items

In Armor & Ash, players roll dice to score wounds on opposing units.  Units remain on the Battlefield until they receive enough wounds to be defeated.  
Spells may be played at any time.
Heroes can move freely in and out of battle.
There is no resource pool threshold needed to recruit units.

Hex6d is a scale-able strategy/family game for 2-6 players that takes, on average, 10-20 minutes to play. Manage your cards and playing pieces to win!

The playing field consists of blank (black) hexes and numbered/lettered (purple) hexes that represent cards from a standard playing card deck.  Be the first player (at the end of your turn) to properly align your pawns on the playing field hexes so they individually match every card in your hand.  
Ex: If a player has a 4, 5, and 9 in their hand, their pawns must be on a 4, 5, and 9.  If a players has a 6, 6, and J in their hand, their pawns must be on a 6, 6, and J.

Set-Up:
Standard game: Place the starting hex in the middle and then around that, the same number of Playing Field hexes as there are players.  The Playing Field hexes are oriented so the middle playing line is connected to the Starting hex and additional playing lines are able to connect to Playing Field hexes to the right and left (if they are being used).  
Modified game (Number and/or Placement of Playing Field hexes): To make the game harder or easier, you can use less or more Playing Field hexes than the number of players.  Additionally, Playing Field hexes need not be connected to each other.  Example (on back page): You could have three Playing Field Hexes and neither of them connect.  Note:  It is recommended the number of Playing Field hexes should not be more or less than one, from the numbers of players.  Example: If there are 6 players and only 3 Playing Field hexes, the game could get congested.

Modified game (Number of Pawns/Cards per Person):  To make the game harder or easier, players can start off with more or less than 3 pawns.  Each player must be dealt the same number of cards as they have pawns.

Pawn Movement:
Ace through 10, move one pawn a respective number of hexes: A = 1 hex, 2 = 2 hexes, 3 = 3 hexes, 4 = 4 hexes, …. 9 = 9 hexes, 10 = 10 hexes.
Jack = Move an opponent’s pawn 5 hexes.
Queen = Move one or more of your pawns a total of 5 hexes.
King = Move one of your pawns anywhere from 1 to 5 hexes.
Joker = Everyone’s cards/hand are passed to the right.  Note: No pawns are moved.

Dark Age 2013 Core Rules contains all the rules needed to play the game, documents and rules for each of the game's eight factions, as well as the rules for our extreme tournament format, including diverse scenarios to increase your game play experience all spread across 88 full color and beautifully illustrated pages bound in a hard cover.

description:
In Moly Diggins, players try to explore winding tunnels to collect earthworms. The game consists solely of ten tunnel cards that each bear earthworms in their paths, and players take turns placing these in order to get their mole to the worms.

In the beginning of the game, two cards are placed and one worm is attached to every worm-sign. The active player places a new card — every card is printed on front and back with different tunnels — and attaches a worm again. Then he moves his mole as far as possible, taking every worm on the way, but sometimes it's better to move not so far in order to have an advantage for his next turn.

When all ten cards have been placed, the second phase of the game starts. Now the active player takes one already-placed card without a mole, removes all remaining worms to the storage, then places it again, attaching worms again, too. In the second phase, players have the opportunity to enter the connecting tunnels of the other players.

The player who has found the most worms at the end of the day is hailed as the greatest earthworm hunter.

boardgame: Malifaux: Masters of the Path – Yan Lo Box Set
description: Yan Lo revolves around powerful models called Ancestors, bringing them back and making them fight for him. Due to the cost of ancestors his crew tends to be rather elite, but he buffs himself through the power of Chi as the game continues and can become a true nightmare to deal with by game's end.

boardgame: Malifaux: The Torch and The Blade – Sonnia Creed Box Set
description: Burn the Witch. Sonnia creates a lot of fire and punishes Magic users. She is one of the few Guild Masters who can summon minions as well as pillars of flame. Can do obscene amounts of damage by catching enemy models under her blast templates. Arcanists: Tremble.

boardgame: Malifaux: The Latigo Posse – Perdita Ortega Box Set
description: Perdita is great at range and can use her ranged abilities in melee as well. She's quick on the draw and can shoot her opponent for missing her. She can cycle her hand when she's down to the dregs. She’s fast and she’s impressively hard to hit with 7's in Df AND Wp. And that's before the rest of her Family starts supporting her. The Guild's answer to Neverborn.

Players place dice on the hex board to form towers. Dice rolls are used to determine the coordinates where dice can be placed.  Dice can be placed either on a vacant hexes (gaining power tokens), or to increase the height of the player’s towers, or used to reduce an opponent’s tower. Power tokens can be used to adjust dice throws or directly alter towers. Towers have influence over neighbouring hexes, the closer the hex and the higher the tower the more influence. At the end of the game each unoccupied hex is a Victory Point awarded to the player who owns the tower that has most influence over that hex. Victory Points are also awarded for remaining power tokens.

姫騎士逃ゲテ~ (Himekishi Nigete) is a partnership game by the game designer entaku_P. It translates as "Princess Knight, run away?".

In the game, one of the players plays the Princess Knight to escape, and the other players play the Orcs, teaming up to catch the princess. Players draft action cards and the Orc team members work cooperatively but in silence. So it becomes difficult to select the right cards to win. Players need to draft cards and read each other's thoughts well to win.

Publisher summary:
"プレイヤーは1人と姫騎士と3人のオークに分かれて、それぞれの陣営の勝利を目指します。
最終的に姫騎士が捕まるとオーク陣営の勝利、姫騎士が逃げ切ると姫騎士の勝利になります。
プレイヤーはアクションカードをドラフトして、それぞれ1枚のカードを選びます。それぞれ勝つためにはオーク陣営はカードを介した無言の連携が、姫騎士は不利を脱する的確な読みが求められます。"
