description:
The world of cryptozoological creatures, strange phenomenon and mysterious locations seem hopelessly entangled with the world of the tabloid press.  This expansion introduces the tabloid headlines that can work for or against cryptozoologists' expeditions for various unknown creatures.

This expansion adds a third draw option to Legends and Lies which is to draw from the Tabloid Headlines which is made up of four face up cards. When cards are drawn from the headlines they are immediately replaced. At the end of each round any cards left in the headlines are added to the Tabloid pile. Headlines and Hoaxes expands Legends and Lies to up to five players.

boardgame: The Red Dragon's Lair: Secret Passage – Exclusive Kickstarter Pack
description:
Exclusive pack purchasable during the KS campaign for the game The Secret Passage to the Red Dragons Lair.

Contains:


    2 Jungle Card Allies
    2 Jungle Card Lost Treasures
    2 Character Friends
    2 Buttons

At the start of the game a moderator hands out a role card to each player, assigning them to either a small team of informed werewolves, who know each others identities, or uniformed villagers, who do not know any player identities. The game then alternates in phases of “night”, in which werewolves agree to covertly kill a villager, and “day", in which villagers debate the identities of the werewolves and vote to kill a suspect. Play continues until all of the werewolves have been killed, or until the werewolves outnumber the villagers.

There are NO DUPLICATES in the Werewolf House deck of over 36 different character cards. Each card features a unique, full color character illustration, so even after dozens of game rounds players will still be discovering new art.

CORE CARDS - The base version of the game can be played by 7-12 people, using 6-10 basic villager cards and 1-2 werewolf cards - all included in the Werewolf House deck.

ADVANCED CARDS - It’s fun to mix it up. Dozens of characters with unique abilities have been carefully curated to add complexity to the gameplay, without burdening the moderator with messy role management tasks.

The object of All About Town is for players, using dice and playing pieces, to acquire 8 Travel cards by moving around the game board. You can adjust the length of the game by changing the number of collected cards required to win.

Players can acquire money from Banks and Money Spaces. Surprise Spaces allow players to draw Surprise Cards that provide money, movement, both or cause them to lose a turn. Whenever a player acquires a Travel Card, another one is turned up.

All About Town: Indianapolis appears to have been sponsored by local businesses. Ads for various business appear on the rulebook, Surprise Cards, Travel Cards and money, some with addresses and phone numbers to those businesses.  A full Participant Listing appears in the rulesheet.

Part of the All About Town family of games.

Imagine an intricately mechanized clash of spy against spy, introducing corporate espionage to a Steampunk world of industrial revolution; filled with all of the plot twists and thrills of both genres. That's the world of Steam Spy, a Steampunk themed role playing card game for 2 players. Steam Spy unites the character development and narrative of a traditional role playing game with the intimacy and spontaneity of a card game.

Players create a unique secret agent character who works for one of the organizations designated to protect and serve one of the world’s largest corporations. Then they face off against an operative from an opposing secret industrial organization in a one on one battle of wits, luck and exotic steampunk gadgets. One player instigates some kind of espionage mission while the other does their best to stop them.

Players collect, play and draw cards that help create a series of exciting encounters that ultimately determine if the instigating antagonists succeeds at their mission, or the determined protagonist chases them down and defeats them; players then advance their spy character, or create a group of secret agent specialists in preparation for the next face off against operatives from opposing agencies and corporation.

Steam Spy: Intelligence and Propaganda includes allows you to become one of the top spies for M5, the Western European agency that works to collect and protect information for the massive Encyclopedia Englandia Corporation. Or you can join Das Wort, one of the original “reality defining” news organizations. In order to present the world as they want it to be seen (in addition to selling papers), their mysterious organization, the Diamond Club, is often accused of creating events of world significance.

boardgame: How I got to the E.R
description:
How I got to the E.R is a party game that has no room for a solid plan and keeps the creative juices flowing, but thats what ultimately makes the game great! With the lack of time to create your story, what comes out is normally weird, sometimes creepy, and usually hilarious.

GAMEPLAY: Each player is dealt three unique cards and attempts to tell the best story they can within one minute. Once their time is up, it's up to the other players to judge how entertaining the story really was.

Unlike most conventional board and dice games where you aim (or hope desperately) to roll high so to be crowned ultimate champion, in Go!Short you’re going to be the winner if you continuously roll low.

So easy to pick up, great fun and perfect for killing some spare time, Go!Short is a new and fast paced game that challenges the regular table-top format by being played out over a series of six ever moving tiles — or what some would technically call, beer mats.

Geeks and Tonic is a hilarious, fast-paced and slightly nostalgic drinking game. With characters like "Mrs. Pongman" and "Trashd Brandycoot", it takes everything you love about your favourite games and turns it into an evening (or morning if you're into that) of inebriated bliss. The cards are easy to understand and follow even after you are well into the game, which is really helpful. The expansion possibilities are limitless!

With fast paced, turn based, PVP drinking combat! Keep up or be taken out!

GOAL: To get 5 Points in Chieveable cards before your opponents do; and to drink and have fun :)

CONTENTS: 108 card deck: 12 heroes, 60 action cards, 35 cheivables, 1 Instruction card(extra hero card if you choose a reward that includes it!)

The Dark Lord is dead. The war between Good and Evil is over. Evil has prevailed and left The Land in ruins - it is your destiny to be victorious over the powerful beings who strive to take the Dark Lord's mantle as their own.

Twelve aspiring Lords seek the Dark Throne, which rises in the Eye - the menacing ring of fire in the center of The Land.

Dark Lord is an independently developed board game for savvy gamers about domination and control, melding card and board game mechanics.

Set out from one of four Keeps that surround The Land. Build an army by exploiting the many resources The Land has to offer and expand your Lord's power.

Dark Lord features 470 playable cards with a wide variety of uses. Summon powerful Creatures with Summon cards, hinder your enemies with Spell cards and grow your power with Utility cards. Each class makes use of cards specialized and specific to that class.

Success in Dark Lord requires the ability to effectively manage resources, out-think your opponents and create and seize opportunities through the use of cards and combat.
The game is played on a board showing a baseball field, and each player has four tokens representing his batsmen. It is a simple roll a dice and move game.

This game has a very interesting "back story" in the convoluted early history of the Monopoly game.  It is based on the expired 1904 Elizabeth Magie patent for The Landlord's Game.

In 1934, Charles Darrow tried to sell his version of Monopoly to Milton Bradley, but they passed on it, knowing the real history of the game.  He took Monopoly to Parker Brothers and the rest is history.

As Monopoly began to become very popular in 1935, Milton Bradley came out with their own "property trading" game, Easy Money.  This was based in part on Monopoly and also Finance, which was another very similar game that had been on the market since 1932.

A race ensued to see who could patent this type of game first.  The patent office decided that these games were derived from The Landlord's Game.  Parker Brothers purchased the second Landlord's patent from Elizabeth Magie Phillips in November 1935, and Charles Darrow was granted a patent on Monopoly (as an "improved version") the following month.

Parker Brothers then pressured Milton Bradley to change Easy Money, and remove what they considered aspects of the game that infringed on Monopoly.  This included the use of property cards.

Milton Bradley went along with this, but in 1937, perhaps as a bit of "payback," they briefly marketed this game Carnival, based on the earlier (expired) Landlord's Game patent.  This game has what amounts to property cards (the "Concessions").

So, it may very well be that this game was put on the market by Milton Bradley largely to thumb their noses at Parker Brothers.  There was nothing Parker could do to prevent it.

The iconography on the game board also ties it in directly to Easy Money, since the exact same Bank illustration is used.  There are also references to "concessions" and it's very possible this is all in the nature of "subtext" as part of a dialogue between the two major game makers.

It was only available for sale for a brief period of time, perhaps just long enough for Milton Bradley to make their point about patents.  Parker Brothers had been attempting to get a third trading game patent (and actually did so in Canada), but they gave up on the effort in the United States, and this game may have played a part in it.

If Milton Bradley had challenged the Monopoly patent in court, it likely would have been invalidated, and Parker Brothers knew this.
Some of the other early Monopoly-related variants are quite valuable (the Knapp/Electronic Laboratories "Finance," "Boondoggling," Rudy Copeland's "Inflation," Cadaco's "Movie Mart," the 1935 version of "Easy Money," etc.) and this game is just about as rare as these are.
Typical spin and move game. The player who finished first, wins the game.

boardgame: 皇帝なき帝国 (Empire Without Emperor)
description:
User summary:
皇帝なき帝国 (Koutei Naki Teikoku) is themed on the Interregnum in the Holy Roman Empire. But the game centred around the placement of numbered tiles and their effect. The main focus is on control using the Knight placement from Chess.

An area-control game themed on middle-ages Germany, where your goal is to gain control of Cities and Nobles. Players control "Knights" which have "Military points". Majority of Military points are required to control Cities.

On the other hand, you need the majority of number of Knights (regardless of Military points) to control Nobles. You must spend one turn to "prepare" the Knights, or they would be treated as "unprepared", or woth 0 Military points. But since they are initially face-down tiles, you can't be sure which of your Knights are the stronger ones.

The game was originally published in 2012 and a new edition with updated artwork was released in 2017, but gameplay remains the same.

Publisher summary:
神聖ローマ帝国、大空位時代を舞台に都市の支配権を争う。騎士タイルをボード上で配置し、連なることで勢力も合わさるのでより適した配置ができれば勝利につながるでしょう。

中世ドイツを舞台に、都市や貴族を支配することを目指す陣取りゲームです。
プレイヤーの手下の騎士は「武力ポイント」を持ちます。それは都市を支配するのに必要です。
貴族を手下にするには騎士タイルの枚数で相手を上回っている必要があります。
騎士タイルはめくり、いったん控えの間カード上に移されます。この手順から運の要素があるのです。
publishers: ガーデンゲームズ (The Garden Games)

description:
暁の鉄道　(Akatsuki no Tetsudo) has the players building the famous Yamanote railway line in Tokyo during the Taisho era. The winner is the player who makes the most money, by dominating the best routes and carrying luggage.

Publisher's summary:
大正時代の東京を舞台に山手線を構築する鉄道敷設、荷物運搬ゲーム。よく利用される路線を支配していればより多くの収益がもらえ、さらに運んだ荷物自体も価値があり、ゲーム終了時により裕福ならばゲームに勝利となる。
publishers: ガーデンゲームズ (The Garden Games)

description:
In Canals of Amsterdam, players take turns placing influence in the markets of Amsterdam and dividing the city with canals in order to take advantage of that influence and gain wealth.

Each player starts with a number of influence tokens valued 1-4, with the exact numbers dependent on the number of players. Twelve market tokens worth 4-8 are placed at random on the empty rectangular area representing Amsterdam. A single canal rings the city.

On a turn, the active player draws one of their influence tokens at random, then either:


    Places it on an empty space on the board, or
    Discards it, then places as many canals (wooden sticks) on the board as the number on that token.


When an area is enclosed that contains one or two market tokens, whoever has the most influence in this area gains all of the tokens as well as wealth (VP tokens) equal to the number of spaces in this area. If players are tied for influence, then they sum the market tokens and area wealth and split this, rounding down.

When all markets are controlled, or all players are out of tiles, or all canals have been placed, the game ends and the wealthiest player wins.

Canals of Amsterdam includes two variants, one in which each player starts with a single canal in reserve that can be used at any time on their turn and another in which players each have a hand of three influence tokens.

Publisher's summary:

世界遺産アムステルダムの運河を建築し、街を分割して支配するゲーム。分割されたエリア内で市場マスが１～２マスになればエリアが確定し、その段階でより大きな影響力をエリア内に及ぼしていればエリア内市場と土地を支配できる。
publishers: ガーデンゲームズ (The Garden Games)

description:
User summary:
ドラコの山分け　(Dorako no Yamawake) is a pirate themed card game. Players turn up treasure cards in the centre, then compete for them using their cutlass cards. You can gain points by collecting treasure cards of up to 2 different colours, but you start losing points from the third and later colours.

Publisher's summary: 
手番プレイヤーは任意の枚数の財宝カードをめくり、それを取る気ならば刀剣カードで取る気を示す。より高い数値を示した人から順番に１枚ずつ財宝カードを取る。財宝カードは２色までがプラス得点になるが３色目からはマイナスになる。
publishers: ガーデンゲームズ (The Garden Games)

description:
Brick Work is the brick building, dice rolling, worker placement strategy game for two players ages 9+ that takes between 15 and 30 minutes to play.

At the beginning of the game, each player builds an object -- called a brickprint -- out of neutral (yellow) bricks. The first player to rebuild both brickprints using bricks of their color (red or blue) is the winner!

Each round, all four dice are rolled, then players alternate turns by choosing one die from the shared pool and placing it onto the game board to collect bricks of their color, connect bricks in their collection, or manipulate future die rolls. When all of the dice are placed on the board, the round ends, turn order changes, and a new round begins.

However, a player can "bump" a die off an occupied action space. A bumped die is rolled and returned to the shared pool, extending the round by one turn. By strategically choosing when to bump -- and when not to -- you might be able to eke out a few extra actions over the course of the game!

Brick Work is a fast, fun, deceptively strategic game that combines the joy of brick building with creativity, puzzle solving, and modern game mechanisms.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: NAVTAC: World War II Naval Miniatures Rules
description: This game is designed for 1:4800, 1:2400, or 1:1200 scale naval miniatures. 1:250 scale aircraft can conveniently be used with the smaller scale naval miniatures. Two twenty sided dice will be needed for play, in addition to the appropriate miniatures. These rules follow the SeaTac game system developed for modern naval combat. These rules, like all our games, attempt to be easy playing and fast, while retaining a great diversity of information.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Far to the South of Conan's home there lies a dry, arid land where a rugged, dark-skinned people eke as much of a life as they can out of the sands. The environment is not the only danger in these lands however, there is a darker, more sinister power at work, one that man should have known to leave well enough alone but the promise of power is too tempting for some. Now the dead rest uneasily beneath the sands and monstrous beasts devour men like cattle as the great and powerful watch on, eternally.

Presenting Conan: Stygia, written by Bruno Cathala, the author of Five Tribes and co-author of Cyclades and Abyss. Delve deep into the sands of Stygia, through undead crypts and hordes of undead abominations to discover the real evil that lurks beneath this sunburnt land.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Malifaux: Relic Hunters – Lucas McCabe Box Set
description: He's not Indiana Jones for legal reasons. He's on a horse. He’s faster than almost everyone because of it, and he definitely plays a control game utilizing Slow and Paralyze. His totem helps him synergize well with Hounds and his ability to toss his Upgrades onto friendly models can result in being able to attack from some very unexpected vectors. Again, call him a Treasure Hunter or he'll rip your lungs out. Dual Faction with 10 Thunders.

boardgame: Malifaux: Shadows of Redchapel – Seamus Box Set
description: Seamus loves Terrifying models and heals as opponents fail Wp duels. He is one of the most resilient Resser Masters and can step out from the shadows of Back-Alleys around the board to shooting one of the most devastating guns in the game at unsuspecting foes. Seamus is a serial killer mixing Jack the Ripper with Mr Hyde, summoning more Zombie Ladies of the Night as he hacks them down.

boardgame: Malifaux: Open Graves – Nicodem Box Set
description: The summoner in Malifaux. Nobody does it better than Nicodem. If you like winning through attrition, this is your guy. Nicodem can simply summon more and more as more things die. He can summon like crazy and then he buffs the crew to keep them going.

boardgame: Malifaux: Body of Evidence – McMourning Box Set
description: Works in The Guild's morgue. He can dice enemies with his scalpel and infect survivors with Poison. Douggie is a surgical strike, hard-hitting and deadly either from the initial attack or the plethora of follow ups he can make once you're poisoned. Not as straight up powerful as Lady J, but can edge an advantage out over her with layered damage sources and just being mad as hell. Dual Faction with Guild. Unique to his Resser incarnation is the ability to summon Canine Remains from enemies killed by Poison and Flesh Constructs from those killed by Expunge.

Pre-war uranium mines with a hidden underground equipment and crews managed to survive the apocalypse and are still extracting valuable uranium. Over the following decades, many of the heavy mining machinery were transformed into deadly weapons and sent to defend the underground fortresses. This way a new town was founded. Uranopolis - a rich mining town, effectively guarding their treasures from being plundered by grabby neighbors.

Neuroshima Hex! Uranopolis brings this army to life in the Neuroshima Hex! base game.

—description from the publisher

EVERYTHING DIES…EVEN MACHINES

We have watched the Saints of the Forsaken march across thewastes to do battle for the ruling Prevailers. The Outcasts have scavenged a living in the corners of the frontier. We have reeled in terror at the cannibal rituals of the horrific Skarrd tribes.The savage honor of the Dragyri warrior castes have dragged gasps of awe from each of us. We have even locked our doors at night against the inscrutable monsters of the Brood. The world is soaked in blood and littered with the remains of those who could not weather an existence here. It is a hard life filled withpain, suffering and barbarism……and it just got a whole lot worse. Within this book you will find a new evolution to Dark Age. This includes the Cult of Metamorphosis, a new tribal faction within the Skarrd created from the fully transformed cabal of the Heretic Saint Johann, and the C.O.R.E. – a never before seen army of tireless robots on a murderous rampage of expansion. Samaria…and Dark Age…will never be the same again.

All will be reduced to cinder!

We have winessed the birth of Metamorphosis and the revival of the C.O.R.E., but now, from of the flaring sands of the east, the blazing subterranean dwellings of the Dragyri, and even the very sky above, new threats rise to challange the factions of Samaria.

Dark Age Conflagration is the newest expansion  book for the Dark Age Miniature game line. Conflagration contains 140 full color and beautifully illustrated pages nestled in a hardcover binding. Inside readers will find generic forcelist additions to all of the factions  as well as the forcelist of the new Kukulkani, Ancient warriors imbued with alien technology hell bent on sacrificing every living thing they come across for their alien masters. The Dragyri Sub-Faction of the Fire Caste, unlike their Air and Ice Cousins the Fire Caste use alien technology and what can be deemed dishonorable methods. The new Outcast Sub-Faction of the Slavers of Chains Barrow, these ruthless Outcasts capture and enslave those they come across without mercy or morals to fight for their amusement or greater gain.

All contents have been writen to work in conjunction with the new 2013 Core Rules.

"His will be done". Deadly Archangels will be descending from the heavens to unleash the wrath of the Lord on to the unholy of Samaria with Fanaticism, the next trilling expansion to the Dark Age table top game.

Fanaticism brings forth the Council of Prevailers and their Warknights, the Archangels. Prime human specimens, augmented by physical grafts, crammed into a highly advanced suit of armour, with a mind wiped clean by psycho-genic conditioning, making them the perfect soldiers and all the latest miniatures belonging to all the various factions that belong to this awesome game.

This is an essential source book for any Dark Age collector who wishes to know the rules and backgrounds in exquisite detail for to all the heroes and warmachines belonging to the Fanaticism expansion, all encased in a beautiful hardback book.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Laugh Riot!: The Comedy-Improv Card Game
description:
Laugh Riot! is based on a comedy-improv exercise that professional comedians and actors have enjoyed since... like, forever.

108 cards supply endless fodder for the funniest stories that you’ve ever heard, and that you’ve ever told.

The game is based on a comedy improv exercise called “Yes and…”

This exercise is the foundation for good improv: Agree with what your partner has said, and then add something to it.

Laugh Riot! takes the “Yes, and” exercise and combines it with a simple and competitive color and suit matching game. There are 5 different types of cards, that are played in different ways, allowing for chaining of multiple cards, forcing other players to draw cards, and skipping other players, making for a fun and friendly competition to win-while also cooperating to tell a funny story.

On each player’s turn, s/he plays one or more cards and uses whatever word is on the card in a sentence that continues the story, and starts with “Yes, and.”

The humor tends to come naturally by just following the rules of the game. There is no pressure to be funny or creative, and really anyone can tell a funny story.

User summary:

Grecia is a Party Game designed for 4 to 8 players above 18, which involves some drinking and moderate physical contact - like hugs and kisses (but this is not a rule, is just something that often happens). Grecia it is not necessary an adult game, although by experience I have noted that when is the case of having only adults playing it, mostly end becoming one.

Every player must choose one among the eight characters available for playing; they impersonate a Hero or Heroine who wants to become a God. In order to do that, the player must advance his pawn through the game board to the finish place, Mount Olympus. The player  simply throw the dice and moves across the game board, adding or subtracting modifiers as he can, using his Drachma coins to gather advantage over the other players.

There are several things that a player can do in order to win some extra points for boosting his pawn and get first to the finish line. He can win extra Drachma coins by winning some challenge, for example. Besides that, that are some events that can punish the player who lost a challenge, or simply got unlucky on the dice toss. The punishment often involves some drinking or some nudity but, as always, that all can be avoided spending some Drachma coins. In the end, the "temperature" of the game tends to increase, but it will depends on the group of players because the limits are decided before by the players.

Description from the back of the box:
Welcome to ancient Greece! A fascinating place where gods vie followers as heroes struggle to get a seat on Mount Olympus. In this game, you will play a hero with a great ambition: Becoming a god. For this you should go through a tortuous path, full of intrigue, riddles and many wine-fueled orgies.

The objective of the game is to be the first to reach Mount Olympus. Players will have to go a long way to go facing obstacles, challenging mythological creatures and doing rituals and offerings to the gods to get to your destination.

Description from the back of the box:

Be the first one to pull your team over the line.  The proper placement of four different coloured player cards on either side of the rope will show your ability as a quick thinker.  This is a fast-paced tug of war game for two or four players that teaches you to match up colours.  You still have to watch out that you don't lose your balance and let the other team pull you into the water because you didn't match up your colours correctly.

boardgame: Shootout! The High Noon Card Game
description:
Shootout! The High Noon Card Game casts you and your friends as cowboys in the wild west. The most dangerous duelist is the one who makes the best hand possible. Once you've got the best weapons, the most impressive title, and familiarized yourself with your foe, you'll want to pick a fight with someone else at the table- If they don't come looking for trouble before you're ready for 'em. Experience tense, high-stakes western duels as players square off one by one until the last gunslinger standing wins. It is best suited for 2-6 players ages 9 and up and a round takes about 5 minutes per player.

To begin every player is dealt five cards. Each turn a player reveals a card, draws a card, and discards a card before the turn passes to the next. The heart of the game is the information a player gives about what they are already holding by what they choose to take and what they discard. Using this information (or misinformation, at the highest levels of play) allows you to improve your chances by reading your opponent and evaluating your options-- It's basically the super-exciting high-stakes game of poker that only exists in the movies.

The lean, 88-card deck has been balanced to a very deliberate point to make it an ideal family game. Special cards are very clear and provide their entire ruels explanation on them in order to keep the fun moving along. The elements of chance allow children, grandparents, and those totally unfamiliar with games to still have a fair chance of turning things around and winning in the end without leaving the outcome purely to chance like many other family games. This game was built from the start to provide fast, clear, fun gameplay that rewards investment without demanding any from the player. The game is light on text and appropriate for all ages.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #15
description:
The Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #15 is composed of 2 expansions:

Asgard's Chosen: Little Ice Age - 3 glacier tiles
Steam: Demanding Suppliers - 5 demanding supplier tiles

This is available in Game Trade Magazine (Issue 180 - Feb 2015).

There is something magical about the ice.

Components:

3 glacier tiles

Set-up:

Use 1 more Land Tile than is required for the number of players. Place the 3 Glacier tiles in any 3 different terrains on the board with either side face-up.

Gameplay:

If a battleground contains a Glacier, gain plus 1 power to attack or defend for each creature whose heraldry symbol matches that Glacier tile. If, after you were successful in a battle (attack or defense), either player used a matching heraldry symbol for the Glacier space, you may move the Glacier tile to an uncontrolled terrain with either side of the tile facing up. If there are no spaces available remove the Glacier Tile

No control cubes may be placed on a landscape with a Glacier tile. The land is considered controlled as long as the hero remains on that space. Control Cubes may be placed on the landscape immediately after the Glacier has been moved. The controlling hero cannot be substituted for instances where control cubes need to be removed.

This expansion was later included in Steam: Map Expansion #5.

Components:

5 Demanding Supplier tiles

Setup:

Either:
Version 1: Place each Demanding Supplier tile on 4 different cities matching the supplier colors and 1 on a gray new city (this must be the first gray city built); reduce the number of goods to be placed in those cities by one.

Version 2: Place each Demanding supplier tile in 5 different towns. Remove one of each color cube from cities on the board  (take from a Goods Supply Space if 1 color isn't in play in cities).

Gameplay:

Move Goods: The  Demanding  Supplier Tile is effectively a goods cube that may be delivered to a city in the color shown.

Each time any goods cube or another Demanding Supplier tile is delivered from, to, or through a city or town containing a Demanding Supplier tile    your track points from the move are reduced by 1.

If you move a Demanding Supplier tile for victory points, ignore other Demanding Suppliers and add 2 VPs to your total score along with your track points gained through movement.

A holiday-themed roll-and-move game. The game is played on a board resembling a Christmas tree. The goal of the game is to reach the top of this Christmas tree first. 
On your turn, you spin the included top, resulting in a number from one to three. You then either move that many steps in your current row and cover the field you leave with a Christmas decoration token. However, if you "rolled" a three, or no fields are available, you move up one row. If you reach the top, you win!

A) The idea behind Blitzball is to recycle the game BloodBowl, an all other game similar to BloodBowl (Kaosball, Slaughterball, Elfball, etc…).

With BlitzBall, you can use any figures you want, provided you can classify them into 4 classes :
a. Leader.   b. Receiver.   c. Marauder.  d. Blocker.

For example, a human thrower in BloodBowl may be recycled in Leader for the game BlitzBall.
A human catcher may be recycled in...Receiver.
An Orc lineman may be recycled in Marauder.
An Orc Blitzer may be recycled in Blocker. Etc..

You don't have any figure ? Tokens, symbolizing players, are provided with the game.

B) At the beginning of a match of BlitzBall, you choose a team of 12 players, and you field 9 of them in the pitch (so 3 are on the bench).
Instead of using the specific pitch of Blitzball, you can use the pitch of Bloodbowl : in this case, you choose a team of 14 players, and you field 11 of them in the pitch (so 3 are on the bench).

Each position has its own profile :
Movement, Brutality, Resilience, Agility, Passing, Control, Initiative.

In order to have a fluid game, there are NO special skills in BlitzBall.
All aspects of this game is managed by the player's profiles.
Movement : used for ...movements.
Brutality: used for tackles.
Resilience: used for knocked down players, in order to avoid serious injuries.
Agility: used when a player throws, catches, or picks up the ball.
Passing : used when a player throws the ball. A good Passing allows a player to make longer passes.
Control : used when a player kicks the ball, or attempts a Field Goal.
Also the Control is used to give inspiration to teammates.
Initiative : used when a player wants to perform complex movements (feint, jump, sprint, blitz). Also used to pick up the ball in certain conditions.

C) What about the turn sequence ?
At the beginning of the game a team makes a kick-off.
Then it's the turn (called activation) of the receiving team, followed by the turn of the kicking team, etc...until a team scores (TouchDown or FieldGoal). At this stage, the scoring team makes a kick-off, and so on...

D) What happens during a turn (activation) ?
Normally, a coach (you or your opponant) activates up to 3 players.
It is a standard activation : only 3 players are activated, and they can performs any actions allowed in the game.
Sometimes a coach may opt for another mode of activation : Pass mode, Kick mode, Defense Mode, Movement Mode, depending on the tactical situation on the pitch. For example, the Movement mode improves the movements of the players, allows to activate the entire team in some conditions, but the only action allowed is "Moving". Same thing for the Pass mode. The Kick Mode activates only the ball carrier and allows to escape from adverse pressure.
The Defense mode is the best mode if you want to tackle the opposing ball carrier, but this mode is available only if you roll the appropriate number on the dice...

E) End of game and scoring.
You and your opponent agree on a time limit (45, 60 or 90 minutes), or on a maximum scoring (30 points for example), etc...
A team scores with TouchDown (5 or 7 points), Field Goal (3 points), or eliminated opponents (2 points).

F) Style of Play.
Special cards are provided with the game : they allow to customize the style of play for your team : Pass play, Run play, Aggressive Play, Tactical Play, Polyvalent play, Mixed play.

G) Edited february 18, 2016. You have 2 types of optional players : Goliath and Flanker.

User summary:
だいなしカードゲーム (Dainashi card game) means Spoil and in this game, players try to reach a winning combination of cards whilst also spoiling their opponents' efforts. The cards are in numbers and colours and each player sets the number cards out in front of the other players. The deck also has Special cards with a variety of effects. If you can get four or more cards of the same colour in front of you, you win.

Publisher summary:
"３～５名で遊ぶカードゲームです。
各プレイヤーは手札の中から数字や特殊カードをセットして勝利条件を目指します。
他プレイヤーの勝利条件を邪魔しつつ、勝利を目指します。"
publishers: デバッグフリーク (DEBUG FREAK)

description:
BCE 58: Julius Caesar is broke. His time as proconsul has cost him most of his personal fortune, and much of his clout within the Republic. Luckily, he is still in command of Rome’s most prestigious army. He has heard rumors of the riches coming back with the traders from Gaul. When reports of unrest among the Helvetii and a threat of invasion from the Germanic Suebi, the opportunity could be ripe for him to “help” the Gauls.

In Valour, you take on the role of a Gallic chieftain as you rally your tribe in an ultimately futile attempt to turn back the tides of history. You will navigate your chieftain pawn around the map while generating and spending site-located resources (food, wood, wine, and gold) to amass an army and build the roads for them to travel. As you choose where to deploy your your defenses, Caesar and his generals begin a slow buy inexorable march from Rome, taking city by city until nothing is left. Do you race to the front? Or do you hunker down and wait for the final battle? Or do you use one of the small selection of political action cards to collaborate with the invading forces and hope for the best?

Make sure Caesar tells the world about you and your tribe as he executes his campaign of glory, and guarantee your place in history.

A two player science fiction wargame played with cards on a tabletop, where each card represents a single space ship or piece of terrain. Objectives are either destroy all of opponents ships (cards) or varied by scenario.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary:
すべってコロリン (Subette Kororin) is a racing game with penguins in the Antarctic. Although it is a simple roll and move game, the way you move depends on whether you roll a 1 or 2, or a 3-6. If you only roll numbers from 3 to 6, then you add these together and move forwards. But any roll of a 1 or 2 counts as a negative multiplier, and you might go forwards or backwards.

For example: A roll of 3,4,6 gives you 3+4+6=16 forwards. A roll of 2,2,6 gives you 6*-2*-2=24 forwards. But a roll of 1,2,2 gives you 0*-1*-2*-2=0.

Publisher's summary:
ペンギンたちの南極でのレースゲーム。双六ですが、１と２が、マイナスの掛け算の目。３～６が足し算の目。掛け算の目を活用すれば一発逆転も狙えます。
publishers: ガーデンゲームズ (The Garden Games)

description:
From the game's website:

Unleash the fun in your festivities with this award-winning party game!  Discover cool, fun things about yourself, those you’ve known for years, or those you just met with lots of hoots along the way!

Each player uses their personal answer dials to privately give their personal "Yes" or "No" response to the card read. Players then use the number wheel on the dial to privately answer how many total players in their party they think would answer "Yes" to the played card.  See how everyone answered and let the fun begin!

The game is Penny for Your Thoughts!  Players score custom party pennies with accurate predictions about what the other players thought about the card read and how many they thought would answer "Yes".  First player to 10 pennies wins the round.  Play again--because one round is never enough!

Makes for great inter-generational play with questions meant to bridge the generation gaps.

It’s time to bust out the fireworks, but be careful. You don’t want an M80 to explode in your hand, you will lose it. They are powerful little things. Players take turns playing a card on a players hand, Follow the rules of the cards to see what happens. The goal of the game is to acquire the most points by the end of the game. There is a chance to secure points with the dud card, but other than that whatever you have in your hand at the end will be your score. You must watch out for the M80, they empty your hand. Enjoy and remember, fireworks are not toys!. Set things on fire responsibly!

Set Up:

    The last person to light a firecracker goes first. That person chooses a dealer.
    Deal 4 cards to each player and everyone discards 1 


Form a discard pile next the remainder of cards which is now the draw pile.

    Each player will organize the cards in their hand and hold then face up in front of them. Only the top card will be visible. The game consists of always showing one card from your hand while the rest of your hand is underneath.


Play:
The person to go first will decide on who will get the first card. They may choose themselves or any other player. 

    They will draw one card from the draw pile and give it to that person face up so everyone can see.


That card is to be played on the card showing in that players hand. 

    If it’s a match, it lights a firework showing as the action on the card goes into effect. 
    If it’s a lighter, the player gets to decide on the card in the other persons hand they wish to act on, but they don’t get to look, just announce. Ex. 2nd card, 3rd card down.
    if it’s a firework card it goes on the top of the pile in that player’s hand.
    a match or lighter played on a match or lighter just gets added to that player’s hand with no action.
    The player who was chosen will go next. (You could keep choosing yourself, but there are M80’s in the deck that will wipe you out).


Conclusion:
Play continues until there are no more cards to draw. The cards left in your hand at the end of the game are totalled and scored. if you have any cards in front of you as result of the dud, you add them to your hand total and that’s your score. The highest score wins. If a tie, the player with the most of the highest point value cards wins.

boardgame: Dragon Valley: Kickstarter Exclusive – Kings Aid Cards
description:
If Kickstarter backers pledged at Noble level or higher each copy of Dragon Valley came with three exclusive King's Aid Cards (Lure, Teleport and Monument) that will not be included in the production run of the game.

Lure - Why let your opponents get all the glory for defeating formidable foes?  Use rumors of treasures to lure dragons and orcs to your heroes.

Teleport - Your lands are vast.  To quicken your advance to the front lines or into a siege in the valley, the King can send a mage that specializes in teleportation.

Monument - In an effort to demonstrate the greatness of your Barony, this ability allows you to use building materials towards a great monument, scoring end game points.

Welcome to Dazzling Zoo! In our zoo, we only have 5 different animals. Just don't turn round and leave! Our animals are not as usual. Come in and try to figure out their face, color and living place. Only the visitor who has clearest mind will successfully recognize the right animal. So, feel free to explore this dazzling zoo and look how colorful our zoo is, and make sure that you won't feel dizzy after visiting our zoo. Have fun!

Dazzling Zoo is a game that need players to recognize different conditions the next card shows on its back. There are 3 conditions on each card's back, which is 'animal', 'animal's color', and 'living place'. Once the zoo face shows up, player need to figure out the right answer and touch the right tile as soon as possible. However, players should also beware of trapping situation, that if the zoo face is exactly the same as the answering tile, the right tile immediately becomes the wrong tile. Therefore, it is not a game for the quickest player, but for the player with cautiousness as well.

The D&D Attack Wing version of the Silver Dragon figure also comes with additional play materials such as Cards, Tokens, Maneuver Dials, and a 6” x 6” D&D Attack Wing base.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Eve's Mystery Mead
description:
A limited release promo for The Red Dragon Inn first released at Gencon 2014 and available at the SlugFest Games booth. Shuffle or replace a card in any Red Dragon Inn Drink Deck with Eve's Mystery Mead to add a bit more excitement to your game!

A second edition of this card is part of the The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox (Collector's Edition).

boardgame: The Russian Empire Strikes Back: Lodz 1914
description: This is a operational scale war game based on the World War One battle between Germany and Russia.

User summary:

Poor Potter is the English name given to the Japanese game 貧乏陶芸家 (Binbou-Tougeika) by the game designer Taiki Shinzawa.

The players are a group of young potters, who, due to poverty, can't afford the clay to produce pottery works. Under the circumstances, you show the plan of your upcoming work to solicit clay from other potters, and the player who has supplied the highest amount of clay can receive the work. The produced work may be worthless now, but its value is sure to rise in 10 years time.

Players bid on pottery work cards by the weight of the clay they supply, and the player who earns the highest score by adding the points for the clay and cards wins. An electronic scale is provided as a component of the game and used to weigh the clay, as the precise weights matter to the result.

Designer's summary:
"あなたたちは若き陶芸家です。ですが貧乏なので陶芸を作る粘土もありません。なのでこんな陶芸を作りますという企画を出して材料の粘土を仲間に出してもらいます。そうして出来た陶芸は粘土を出してくれた仲間にあげます。いまは価値の無い陶芸ですが、10年後には立派な値になっていることでしょう。
粘土をお金に重量で、オークショナーが出した陶芸カードを競って、集めていきます。ゲーム終了時にもらった粘土とカードの得点を合計して最も高い人がゲームに勝利します。重量の判定には電子ばかりを使用します。"

A game of chess played on a game of chess with two sets of chess pieces: a big set that carries a small set. The goal of the game is to take the small king. On a move the small pieces can either move across the tops of the big pieces or pilot the big piece they're on. Small pieces cannot leave their big piece board unless there is an adjacent big piece to legally move to, and can move across several adjacent big piece boards in a move as long as the move is legal and uninterrupted by gaps.

"RUS: Age of the Vikings" is a strongly themed military and economic strategy wargame set in Viking Age Russia, in which up to 5 players compete to become Czar of the Rus.  It deliberately sets out to marry the elegant inclusivity of Eurogames with the thematic gameplay of a classic Amerigame.

Each turn, players choose 6 of 9 Actions that drive the game.  Will you send out Envoys to engage in Diplomacy, Harvest your crops, Trade with foreign powers, Construct settlements, invest in Culture & Technology or Recruit troops to engage in warfare?  The player who chooses the Action gains a bonus, but all other players simultaneously take part in each Action Phase that is chosen.

It is possible to win the game without ever engaging in warfare, but for those who prefer blood, combat uses a Tactics Card system which means you have to out-think your opponent to beat them in battle.

As in history, Politics Cards provide a random element, introducing new Kingdoms (like the Muscovites or the Golden Horde) or triggering good harvests, famine and ultimately the Black Death.

The first player to score 8 Victory Points wins the game, but if the Black Death is triggered before this happens, all Kingdoms are hit by the plague.  The survivors then take stock to see who is the most powerful in the new world that emerges.

With virtually no down-time and multiple routes to victory, RUS: Age of the Vikings provides a dynamic strategic experience within a playing time of about 30 mns per player.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Saratoga is a board game that represents air-naval military maneuvers. Each player has 4 ships (one aircraft carrier, two cruisers and one destroyer), 12 jets and 6 surface-to-surface missiles. On land area are airport, port, launchers of missiles and anti-aircraft guns.

The main characteristic is that the game board is covered with a net of equilateral triangles. Vertices of these triangles are points at which the players hold position and move figurines.

Players have task to shoot at the opponent's ships and planes, as well as targets on its territory. The destroyer is sunk when received one hit. The carrier and cruiser are sunk when they receive 3 hits. Each hit in any target counts as one point. The game lasts as long as players have airplanes, ships and missiles to shoot at opponent. The winner is the player who has the best ratio of given and received hits.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
English description:
The 'Fabeltjeskrant' animal trump game is a colourful cordage for children aged 3 and above. Each round players play a card. The highest card takes all played cards. Player with the most cards wins the game.
The 'Fabeltjeskrant' theme is known by any Dutch person which makes it very recognisable for everyone.

Dutch description:
Met 'De Fabeltjeskrant Dieren Troef' proberen de spelers elkaar af
te troeven met hun Fabeltjeskrantdieren! Het programma is in 2005
uitgeroepen tot 'Beste Nederlandse Kinderprogramma Aller Tijden'.

De Fabeltjeskrant Dieren Troef is een kleurig kaartspel voor kinderen vanaf 3 jaar. De spelers leggen om de beurt een kaartje op tafel: wie het hoogste kaartje legt, krijgt alle gelegde
kaartjes. De speler die uiteindelijk alle kaartjes bezit, wint DierenTroef!

In Villagers & Villains: City Builder, each mayor gets two City Upgrade Tokens. Mayors use these tokens to develop their village by placing them on the new City Cards. Since 18 City Cards come with City Builder, and each time you play the game a few City Cards are used, players get a wider variety of game experiences. During play, mayors gain added play options by placing their City Tokens on any available City Card. Each City Card in the expansion grants unique abilities—and provides added points to the final score—for up to 2 mayors per card.

The City Builder expansion comes with components and rules to increase the maximum number of players from 5 to 6. In addition, this expansion comes with 6 town mats to give your village an identity, provide a quick reference guide for game play, and to organize game components, tokens, and gold.

The City Builder expansion also comes with new play options including: team play, cooperative play, and house rules for added variety.

Quest for Excellence is a take on the game Scrabble, but it adds a different way of playing. There are these cards, kind of quest cards with questions on. If you get the questions right you score extra points in the game, with a bonus if you collect sets of 3 of the card types, by getting all three questions on a card correct. If you do you keep the card and add it to you set of those cards. There are also no double or triple words score blocks on the board, but point amounts for placing a vowel or consonant on a specific place on the board. The board looks like a Scrabble board, but the scoring just works different. The tiles also have a score amount as stated on the board. I would so think of this game as Scrabble, but with more bells and whistles.

boardgame: Every Round Counts: The Dice Game
description:
A push your luck dice game

You are a bartender serving drinks in the bar, the more you serve the bigger your chance of you getting the tips

The one with the most tips wins the game

A Tribal deck-building card game

You are an Orc Lord trying to build the biggest following to become the Orc Overlord

Each round you draw 5 cards from your deck, each player in turn places their cards in front of them (following the text if any), each player is trying to get the highest combat rating from their cards. The player with the highest combat rating takes the round card (which is worth 5 points at the end of the game),then starting from the second player, the players takes a card from the draft card selection area, the player who finished first in the round chooses the last. These cards are add to the players discard pile, the game continues for 12 rounds at the end of the game the players add up the card value on the bottom of each card and adds it to any round cards they have won, the player with the most points wins the game.

Mobile Armour Jousting pits two mobile armours - the agile mechanoid suits you see on the news putting down Jovian revolts and guarding the President - against each other in one-on-one combat. The aim isn't so much to kill your opponent as to force them out of the ring, sumo-style; once you damage their engines a bit they'll probably do it themselves just by accident, MAs are hard enough to control at the best of times.

A lot of colonists think that the MAJ circuit is their ticket Earthside, and the prizes are certainly magnificent. But the tricks you learn docking with fuel ships or dodging bandits under the rings of Saturn don't prepare you for a Mobile Armour. There's nothing like it; these beasts are as twitchy as they are powerful. And that's not to mention that the Extra-Solar Tournament is the only open tourney that unranked knights from the colonies can even enter, and that's held right the way out in the Reaches where you're just as likely to be hit by a lump of floating rock as a missile from your opponent...

MAJESTY - Mobile Armour Jousting: Extra-Solar Tournament - is a dexterity game in which you move little cardboard tokens around on a hex map. Players take turns to flick discs on their control boards - one for Throttle and one for Manoeuvre thrusters - which determine whether their Mobile Armour executes a perfect slide behind the enemy mecha to deliver a solid shot to the rear or spins wildly in place before careening off in the wrong direction. Players' mecha retain momentum from previous turns' acceleration, and half the challenge is to stay in the arena while being buffeted by cannon fire and homing missiles.

The use of small control boards means that unlike many dexterity games, MAJESTY doesn't require a large, flat and smooth surface upon which to play.

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Triple Alliance
description:
From the Avalanche Press website:

In December 1912, Germany, Austria-Hungary and Italy renewed their 30-year-old military pact known as the Triple Alliance. In the months that followed, staff officers from all three fleets prepared detailed plans for operations against the French and British fleets in the Mediterranean in case of war. Yet when war came in August 1914, Italy refused to join her Alliance partners and those plans would never be used.

What if Italy had honored her Alliance obligations, and joined the Germans and Austrians in a naval war against Britain and France? What if the Triple Alliance plans had been put into operation, with the Austrian and Italian fleets combining to challenge the French and British? The Great War at Sea in the Mediterranean is no longer confined to the Adriatic Sea, but now rages across the entire Mediterranean basin.

Triple Alliance is a book supplement for the Great War at Sea series based on those plans. It is not a complete game; ownership of Mediterranean and Dreadnoughts (and only Mediterranean and Dreadnoughts) is required to enjoy all of the scenarios.

The book includes 90 new scorchless, sootless laser-cut playing pieces. There are several versions of the Austrian battleship Radetzky, both the small dreadnought and full dreadnought variations presented by her design team in 1907. The all-big-gun battleship originally proposed for the Italian Navy by designer Vittorio Cuniberti is included, along with other ships cancelled for financial reasons. And the French receive a full class of unusual battleships proposed but never built, that carry the big guns of a dreadnought but also the not-quite-as-big guns of a semi-dreadnought.

And there are some pieces to replace armored cruisers and semi-dreadnoughts from our Mediterranean game, with gunnery ratings more accurately reflecting their capabilities. Plus new seaplane pieces rated for use with the aircraft rules that began to appear in Great War at Sea games after publication of the most recent edition of Mediterranean.

The thirty scenarios study the Triple Alliance war plan in a story all their own, following the outbreak of war and the conduct of operations as the joint planning staff foresaw possible events. The scenarios follow a single story arc, but also present a study of the Triple Alliance’s strengths and weaknesses. It’s a similar approach to that in High Seas Fleet, but with its own story line.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
In the future, crime is out of control. Aliens, gangs, and worse run amok in the unified worlds of the United Galactique. The best solution was the MegaJail, provided by MegaJail Inc., a maximum security franchise.

In MegaJail, the players are Wardens proving who is the best at running a wing of a MegaJail in order to secure a lucrative MegaJail contract. Whoever scores the highest Reputation (your score) after a pre-set turn limit wins. Lots of things influence your Rep - you lose it when Cons (prisoners) escape, or get injured, paroled, or other things. The MegaJail Inc. management also dislikes you spending too much cash, so keep an eye on your budget!

You get Cons in a draft at the beginning of each turn. Then you have a phase with players taking to actions to build new things (like Cells or Facilities). During Lockdown, Cons are put in Cells, which is the major puzzle of the game. Finally News Events happen, Cons may act out, and a new turn is started.

Just scoring the best isn't enough though - Cons cause Hazards, such as Unrest, Contraband, and ongoing Damage - and Cons can get Angry from overcrowding, high Unrest, or other events. If all the Cons are Angry at the end of the turn, the entire MegaJail melts down, and everybody loses.

Part puzzle, part jail sim, part campy. Can you be the best Warden and run your own MegaJail?

ATS OBJECTIVE SCHMIDT is a detailed historical module that tells the story of the Battle of the Huertgen Forest with a broad sweep, and detailed historical map. The presentation is a large as the battle was ... in terms of importance to Allied hopes to be ‘home for Christmas’ ... 1944. Those hopes were dashed by bitter German resistance in the dark, dank wooded hills and villages around the Kall River. Names like Kommerscheidt, Vossenack, and Germeter, not to mention the infamous Kall Trail ... ring out in the history of the battle. Every one of those places is depicted, and more, allowing the tactical-level gamer to contest every inch, or pull back and await reinforcements ... or lay in a mortar barrage ... or stand pat and await new orders. Whatever was possible to local commanders is in YOUR hands as you re-make history in minute detail.

The map presentation is extensive, providing almost a dozen square feet of gaming area with evocative terrain artwork by a professional military illustrator What’s more, when combined with ATS HUERTGEN HELL, OBJECTIVE SCHMIDT creates another CH monster battlefield measuring some six feet in length ... there just has never been anything like it. You’ll be able to gaze across the expanse of the battlefield on both sides of the Kall River, including multiple villages, hundreds of terrain features. And yet, many scenarios play on portions of the overall map area, making the module easy to dive into and get your dice rolling without delay.

The module includes two sheets of 176 5/8” die-cut German and American Personnel and Weapon counters, plus a sheet of 108 AFV/VEHICLES in 3/4” size, a total of 460 counters in all—everything you need to play (just bring your standard ATS markers). You also receive 10 scenarios (all playable on the map provided) in color format, and color Battlefield WalkaroundTM special rules in 3-hole format. THIS IS NOT A COMPLETE GAME! Ownership of the ATS 2014 rulebook and play aids plus standard system small and large marker counters is required (plus one or more 10-sided dice).

Test your steed's speed as the classic card game meets the world's most majestic breeds in the Horses edition of UNO.


     112 Custom Cards featuring beautiful horses of all colors and breeds.
     Same great UNO play PLUS the special Horseplay card, which forces other players to swap hands.
     Be sure to shout "UNO" as you go for the win.

Two promo cards given for free if you pre-ordered this game from Plaid Hat Games.

EMP Grenade:
Reveal this card. Choose a vehicle within 4 spaces of your agent, hunters cannot move the chosen vehicle or use its equipment (example: motion sensor) until the beginning of your next turn.

Proximity Mine:
Do NOT reveal this card. Mark 'M' on a space up to 2 spaces away from your location. At the start of your turn, if there are 1 or more hunters within 4 spaces of the marked space, reveal this card and stun those hunters.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Play deadly games of Imperial politics in Relic: Halls of Terra! The quest to save the Antian Sector from Chaos takes you to mankind’s homeworld in the Sol System, where you must persuade the ruling powers of the Imperium to support to your cause. You'll have to prove your worth in tough negotiations with powerful organizations such as the self-interested Imperial Nobility, the ruthless Inquisition, and the mysterious Adeptus Astra Telepathica on your way to stand before the High Lords of Terra and the immortal Emperor himself. But be warned: Imperial politics are just as dangerous as any battle inside the Warp Rift, and blackest corruption festers even in the heart of the Imperium.

Relic: Halls of Terra contains:

     A side board depicting the Sol System: Holy Terra, Mars, Titan and Luna
     Complete deck of orange Threat cards specific to the Sol System
     Affiliation tokens that tie characters to powerful organizations
     Three new characters and a new nemesis
     Additional Scenarios, Missions, Corruption, Threat, and Relic cards
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Players try to buy lots on the game map. Lots will in urn bring the revenues. The lots that pay out are determined by dice.
In the first phase, every player gets one lot for free
In the second phase players can buy additional lots, using the revenues as money.
In the last phase of the game, the lots pay dividends, but every turn there is a high chance that one random lot becomes worthless (i.e. contains a "Donnerloch").
Players win when they buy certain combinations of lots or -if no one is able to - have the most money at the end.

User summary:

戦列ペンギン (Senretsu Penguin) is a follow-up game to Siege Penguins, and continues the story of the epic penguin civil wars.

It is a 2 player game about the war between the North and the South (like the American Civil War) in the world of penguins.
Each player uses 5 action cards effectively to change the war situation and aims to march towards the stronghold of the opponent, or defeat his armies thoroughly so that their morale is broken.

Publisher's summary:
"ペンギン世界の南北戦争を題材とした２人用対戦ゲームです。
５枚のアクションカードを駆使して戦況を動かし、
最終的に相手の本拠地に攻め上るか、あるいは、
士気がドン底に落ちるほどコテンパンに叩き潰すことを目指します。

User summary:
蜃気楼の12時間 (Sinkirou No Junijikan) is a game which takes place in a mirage house that exists for just 12 hours. In this game of mystery and suspense, four gentlemen, namely an Englishman, a Detective, a Thief and a Murderer, enter the house (Sinkirou-tei). Whilst they play out 12 rounds of the game, each person tries to keep their identity secret whilst also achieving their hidden goals.

Publisher summary:
"蜃気楼邸に集まった4人の紳士が織り成す
サスペンスパーティー。
プレーヤーは英国紳士・探偵紳士・怪盗紳士・殺戮紳士
のいずれかに扮して、自らの正体を隠しつつ12ラウンドを過ごし、
それぞれに定められた勝利条件を目指します。"

User summary:
The Ruinous Beauty is the English name given by the game designer to his game 傾国-KEIKOKU- although the game is not released in English yet. The Ruinous Beauty implies a femme fatale.

A two-player game staged in the spring-fall era of China (770 – 220 BC). Players play cards face down, determine their powers, and may move their troops forward as much as they have bet on the outcome. The first player to gain 21 points wins. Wisely using the Spies that you can send into your enemy's country, and the Imperial Ordnance that give you an advantage in battle, are the keys to victory.

Publisher summary:
中国の春秋戦国時代をモチーフにした2人専用ゲーム。伏せられたカードの強弱で勝負し、賭けたチップの分だけ兵を進めます。21ポイント先取すれば勝利。敵国に送り込んだ【潜伏者】と、戦いを有利に進める【勅令】をうまく使い、対戦者を出し抜きましょう!

boardgame: Greyhound vs. Bear: The Raid on Astrakhan, 1942
description:
The German raid on Astrakhan marks the eastern-most limit of the Axis invasion of the Soviet Union during WWII. What is remarkable is the small number of German units assigned to this sector, just the 16th Motorized Infantry (Greyhound) division, given the frontage and lack of substantial information regarding the Soviet’s forces. It was due to this lack of intelligence about the Soviet’s intentions and movements that the division’s reconnaissance battalion, along with some sympathetic Cossacks, was sent out from Elista on September 13th. The unit’s four day raid took them 90 miles beyond the Axis occupied village to the outskirts of Astrakhan during which they destroyed a fuel train, gathered intelligence about the nature of the Soviet’s forces, and set off alarms in the region that sent STAVKA scrambling to deal with the threat.

Greyhound vs. Bear portrays the German raid and Soviet response in a quick playing and easy to learn mini-game. Players use a standard deck of playing cards to activate their units and resolve combats. The game is also easy to play solitaire.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Standard Deck Activation System (High Flying Dice Games)

description:
The people in Errandtown need your help!

In this game your goal is to complete errands that the people in Errandtown need help with. The players take turns to move through town and collecting what's on their current errand. To really master this game you'll have to remember where everything is in town and know where to find it when you need it. Errands is a quick and easy game to learn but quite hard to master.

For each errand you complete you get a certain score. The first player over a set value (depending on the amount of players playing) is the winner.

On your way to victory you'll find clipboard cards that will help you or will make it harder for other players to complete their errands.

The game is perfect for both young and old players. For the youngest players it may be a good way to learn English.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary:

漢獣 (Kanju) is a language-based game whereby, from the kanji radicals, you try to guess the four-character idiom cards in your opponents' hands. The game starts by selecting two of the five idiom cards you're dealt. Therefore, the game is very language-dependent and you need a reasonable knowledge of Japanese to play it.

Publisher's summary:
漢字の部首や画数をヒントにして、対戦相手の四字熟語を推理する推理ゲームです。 四字熟語は最初の手札の５枚の中からプレイで使う２枚を選び、ゲームをスタートします。

User summary:
パーフェクトアライアンス (Perfect Alliance) is a game from Japan produced by Loto&Beyser games.

It is a draft game where you use resources to complete quests. During the draft, if the conditions fit, the players become allies, and the goal is to complete the quest together with your ally.

Publisher summary:
リソースを使ってクエストの条件をクリアするドラフトゲームです。ドラフトでは条件が揃うとプレイヤー同士が仲間になり、 最終的に決定した仲間と一緒にクエストのクリアを目指すことになります。

The S. Jorge Castle in the city of Lisbon knew several lords throughout the centuries. It passed from Roman hands to Arabs and finally to Christians. Chief among them were the Vizier Al-Fihrî, D. Afonso Henriques, and King D. Manuel II.

In O Castelo de S. Jorge each player takes the role of a character from the S. Jorge Castle's history. To become the winner and true Lord of the Castle, they vie to obtain Trophies and accrue the highest possible number of victory points.

Each player's turn consists of two phases. In the first you may move your character to another space on the board. In the second, you may take one of two possible actions: collect a Trophy from your character's current location or place a new Trophy somewhere on the board.

The game ends when the time comes to place a new Trophy on the board and none yet remain in the pool. Whoever has the most points at this time is the winner!

Poupar para Ganhar! is a simple game in which 2 participants attempt to play Action cards that allow them to save more for their family than the other. Which Action cards gain you the most points is dependent on your Objective cards.

Turns go through 3 phases:


    Claiming Objective cards
    Playing Action cards
    Drawing Action cards to bring your hand total up to 3


Play ends when both piles of Objective cards have been depleted OR when a player reaches the third turn phase and is unable to draw back up to 3 Action cards. Whoever has managed their households the best and accrued the most savings points is the winner.

War can be profitable.  Five medieval factions are at war.  It's your job to keep them supplied.   Visit the Medieval underground and acquire weapon contracts.  Use your wagons, actions and gold wisely.  Be quick to finish the contracts before your enemies do!

The medieval underground contain contracts of items to purchase.  These contracts will contain 3, 4 or 5 items.  There are 10 different weapons to shop for.  Your playing hand starts with five items that you need to match to the contracts in the dungeon market.  Don't worry if you are missing an item, you can always trade in the market if the item is available.  Purchase new wagons to hold more items to complete more contracts.  Watch out for the events that will destroy the market and steal your weapons!  Plan your 2 actions per turn well and you will profit from war!  End of game bonuses await you if you are clever enough to sell to the same faction or to all of them.

The game works very well with 1, 2, 3 or 4 players.  A 2 player game is about 20 minutes.

Promo Pack #1 consists of three cards all labeled Promo Pack #1 in white text at the bottom

Toltan the Bard is a neutral Supporter card with a Fast Action that allows you to return one of your treasures to the bottom of the Vault deck to gain 4 IP.

Crystal Skull is a consumable Vault card that costs 2 coins. It has a Fast Action that lets you learn a spell from the tableau without spending INT, placing this card on it. It can never be researched further.

Ancient Tablet is a Treasure that costs 4 coins. It has an Action that allows you to move one of your mages on the board to any empty slot.

These were later reprinted and the text at the bottom reads "Lost Promo" vs "Promo Pack #1" at the bottom of the card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Huida de Silver City: La Feria de los Monstruos
description:
En esta ampliación, los jugadores se encontrarán con nuevas opciones para el mapa de la feria. Recuerda que este mapa viene en el reverso del nuevo mapa básico de la reedición de HSC.
En la expansión se incluyen las nuevas Cartas de Saqueo para utilizar en los edificios de la zona de la feria, así como las nuevas armas que encontraréis en las cajas de dichos edificios y las nuevas fichas de exploración que habrá por el tablero.

Además se incluyen nuevos modos de juego para Huida de Silver City, con misiones espontáneas durante la partida, que harán que huir sea más complicado.

---

This expansion offers the players new options for the Fair map. Please remember that this map is featured on the back side of the new basic map of ESC's reprint.
The expansion includes new Loot Cards that you can use for the Fair's locations, new weapons that you will find in those locations' boxes, and new Exploration Tokens that will appear all over the board.
 It also includes new game modes for Escape from Silver City, with spontaneous missions that will hinder your escape.

boardgame: Huida de Silver City: Carne Fresca
description:
En esta ampliación para Huida de Silver City, los jugadores se encontrarán con el lado más oscuro de la terrible infección que asola la ciudad.

Ahora los personajes tendrán su versión zombie, con sus propias habilidades y objetivos para ganar la partida.

Además, la cepa de la infección de Silver City ha mutado y han aparecido nuevos efectos en los zombies recientes.

¡Prepárate para correr! Esta vez la cosa se ha puesto fea de verdad.

---

In this expansion for Escape from Silver City, the players will face the darkest side of the infection that is ravaging the town.
 Now the characters will have a zombie version, with different skills and objectives on their shambling way to victory.
 Moreover, Silver City's infectious strain has mutated, and recent zombies show new features.
 Get ready to run! This time, things are getting really nasty.

INFO: 2 or 4 players, 10-15 minutes per game.

HOOK: High quality miniatures, fast paced gameplay, each card is its own piece of art.

BRIEF OVERVIEW: Summoners is a spell casting, card dueling board game. Players choose a summoner to command and duel head to head. Summoners all have their own unique abilities to use along with a spell deck of 25 cards. Each Summoner has 4 unique pets that they control. Gain mana to cast summoning spells and bring your pets to the game board or cast other spells to deal damage, enhance pets, or heal your summoner.

There are four summoner classes and each starts with 20 health. Attack with your pets or cast your spells cards to take your opponent's summoner to zero health. If you've played any CCG's such as Magic, Game of Thrones, or Star Wars, the dueling wizard mechanics will be familiar to you.

WIN: The first player to take their opponent's summoner to zero health wins.

Battle Crusaders is a card game consisting of 110 fighter character cards with stats on and 50 magic cards which can change the state of play at any time.

Before the game starts the players can choose how many hands they play in multiples of the number of players. This means that the game can be played for short periods (e.g. 1/2 hour or a few hours). The winner is the player who has won the most hands at the end.

The dealer rotates clockwise round the table and after shuffling the cards, starting with their self, deals out seven cards to each player but also finishing with their self, so they will have eight cards. The reason being that they will go first as will every dealer in turn which makes the game fair. The rest of the pack is then put into the centre of the table and the top card is turned over and laid face up on the table next to the pack. If this is a fighter card the player can either then go against that fighter or, choose to go against the next player in turn with the stats and if the card wins that card is laid down on top of the other one for the next player to fight. If the first card down is a magic card it is ignored and they must go against the next player with a fighter card or lay down a magic card and so on.The winner of the hand is the first person (or persons,which it can be with one of the magic cards)to get rid of all their cards.

The more the game is played the more skilled the players can become, especially with the magic cards,and reading the play ahead.

Welcome to CRANIUM Playground, the game where you race around the monkey bars matching,drawing, dancing, and making letter shapes as you do!

On your turn players will roll a die and move their playing piece around the box and complete a task based on the colour they land on. These consist of Red Data Head (Counting), Yellow Star Performer (Dancing), Blue Creative Cat (Drawing), and Word Worm (Letters) The first player to make it all the around the playground (board) wins!

ATS HUERTGEN HELL is a detailed historical module that tells the story of the Battle of the Huertgen Forest with a broad sweep, and detailed historical map. The presentation is a large as the battle was ... in terms of importance to Allied hopes to be ‘home for Christmas’ ... 1944. Those hopes were dashed by bitter German resistance in the dark, dank wooded hills and villages around the Kall River. Names like Kommerscheidt, Vossenack, and Germeter, not to mention the infamous Kall Trail ... ring out in the history of the battle. Every one of those places is depicted, and more, allowing the tactical-level gamer to contest every inch, or pull back and await reinforcements ... or lay in a mortar barrage ... or stand pat and await new orders. Whatever was possible to local commanders is in YOUR hands as you re-make history in minute detail.

The map presentation is extensive, providing almost a dozen square feet of gaming area with evocative terrain artwork by a professional military illustrator What’s more, when combined with OBJECTIVE SCHMIDT, ATS HUERTGEN HELL creates another CH monster battlefield measuring some six feet in length ... there just has never been anything like it. You’ll be able to gaze across the expanse of the battlefield on both sides of the Kall River, including multiple villages, hundreds of terrain features. And yet, many scenarios play on portions of the overall map area, making the module easy to dive into and get your dice rolling without delay.

The module includes two sheets of 176 5/8” die-cut German and American Personnel and Weapon counters, plus a sheet of 108 AFV/VEHICLES in 3/4” size, a total of 460 counters in all—everything you need to play (just bring your standard ATS markers). You also receive 10 scenarios (all playable on the map provided) in color format, and color Battlefield WalkaroundTM special rules in 3-hole format. THIS IS NOT A COMPLETE GAME! Ownership of the ATS 2014 rulebook and play aids plus standard system small and large marker counters is required (plus one or more 10-sided dice).

It's 1878, and All Fathomed Out pits 3-6 players against each other in a race to discover and catalogue the most noteworthy new species brought up from the uncharted waters of the South Pacific.  These moustachioed men of science face not only stiff competition from their fellow explorers as they dive (only one man can take credit for discovering each species, after all, and science waits for no one) but also the multitudinous natural dangers and perilous creatures that lurk in the briny deeps.

As you explore the beautiful hex-based ocean you'll uncover aquatic species ripe for the cataloguing, environmental events both fair and foul, and underhanded shenanigans the unscrupulous can use to tip the balance in their favour (the scallywags!).

How to Play:

In All Fathomed Out, the goal is to amass the most impressive collection of previously unknown aquatic species as you possibly can before your competitors beat you to it.  You'd best hurry though, for only the first specimen of a given species is worth academic accolades and can be catalogued; the rest are old news best used for bait!

Each turn you move across the ocean floor, flipping over new tiles as you venture forth into the unknown or hustling over previously mapped areas on your way back to the ship to drop off your catch or in search of better hunting grounds.  Be careful about wandering too far, though, as the air hose connecting you to the ship and all that sweet, sweet oxygen is only so long and it'd be a real shame if the ship got hit by a sudden typhoon and was sent careening away...

Exploring a new tile nets you a number of cards (listed on the tile itself) from the game's four decks that could be the specimens you're looking to collect, environmental events that shake the game up, or shenanigans that you can use against other players (or keep for later).  You've only got so much space, though, so at the end of your turn you can only keep two cards.   Choose carefully!

Once one player catalogues five fish or the ocean is completely explored, the game is over and the player with the most impressive haul (determined by adding together the publication points from each specimen they have catalogued) wins!

"The game of great lines from great movies! Great lines from some so-so movies! And okay, some really memorable lines from a few bad movies!"

Movie Time! is a trivia game about movie quotes.

Players are given quotes from movies and they must identify which movie they come from. Each quote gives a score between 1 and 4, depending on the difficulty of the question. The first player to 50 points wins.

In the playground, on the building site, on the farm, at the fire station, important things suddenly go missing.  Help Pete put things back where they belong!

Pit, such mit! is a children's game teaching the matching of items with their correct environment.

At the beginning of the game, each player receives tokens representing various items that match one of the four board sections.
