On a player's turn, he rolls a die and moves Pete around the board that many spaces.  He then checks to see if any of his items match the section he lands on, such as a fire hose at the fire station, or a shovel at the building site.

The first player to place all of his items wins!

1985 supplemental trivia card set for use with Trivial Pursuit board.

500 cards with 3,000 questions in 6 categories: GEOGRAPHY, ENTERTAINMENT, HISTORY, FAMOUS PEOPLE, SCIENCE/NATURE, POTPOURRI.

A chess variant played on a 11x11 board, with the pieces updated to 18th century warfare.

Each player has:
1 King (moves like a King in chess)
2 Life Guards (moves like a Queen)
2 Cuirassier (moves like a Bishop)
2 Dragoons (moves like a Bishop)
1 Life Hussar (moves like a Knight)
2 Winged Hussar (moves like a Knight)
2 Light Cannons (moves like a Rook)
11 Fusiliers (moves like a Rook, but only 1 square, captures like a Bishop, but only adjacent pieces)
2 Artillery Batteries (moves like a Rook, but only up to 2 squares and captures like a Rook, but not by replacement)

A game similar to chess, but unlike chess the game is played on a point-to-point movement board.
The aim is to capture the enemy king.

It was designed not merely as pastime, but also to teach military and political principles.

Each player has 30 pieces:
1 King
1 Marshal
1 Colonel
1 Captain
2 Chancellors
2 Heralds
2 Chaplains
2 Knights
2 Couriers
2 Adjutants
3 Body Guards
3 Halbardiers
8 Private Soldiers

Asterion, the Minotaur, awaits in the centre of the labyrinth. His labyrinth is strewn with treasure chests… and wings plucked from his more mythical victims. You can work together or against one another to ensure that the minotaur's horns impale your opponents' people, while your own flee or fly away with all the treasure they can carry.

Dare to enter the labyrinth, find its treasures, discover the true nature of your opponents, and escape before they can lead Asterion to you.

MATERIAL

- 68 labyrinth cards (17 for each player)
- 1 minotaur card
- At least 20 black counters
- Box

GAME PLAY

Players take turns placing a tile on the table, trying to connect their prisoners to the treasures and the outside of the labyrinth (or at least two wings) but not to Asterion.

The player that escapes with more prisoners and treasures wins the game.

You take control of six cars with the aim of being the first to drive them across the city and park them in their designated bays.

Each player has six cars and a set amount of money. Each car has a traffic sign associated with it and they must be parked in the correct bay on the opposite side of the board from where they start.
 The board is in a grid with some squares being blank, others with a 'hazard' (where a fine must be paid), others redirect the player in a different direction and another square awards the player with money for good 'road craft' The first player to park their cars is the winner. Any player who becomes bankrupt automatically loses

The aim of this game is for children to learn and realize certain rules which should never be forgotten.

The role of children in this game is twofold.
They move in the roads, either as a pedestrian or as a driver.

As drivers, they must previously obtain a driving licence and they must drive by the right side of the road paying attention to the lights, etc ...

As pedestrians, they must not ever forget before leaving the pavement to look to the left, look to the right and walk fast so that they can go safely and quickly home.

Who will answer at least five questions correctly?

Who will arrive more quickly at an end?

A educative traffic game.

Top Gun is a military aviation exercise. Each player has 6 aircraft. All take off from the same runway. During the flight planes can be shot down if it stops on a field where anti-aircraft guns operate. Every plane that succeed to land on second runway gets one point. During the flight, the planes can drop a bomb on a target. Each target hit is worth 1 point. Planes can also shoot down planes from other teams. For each downed plane also receives one point. When all the airplanes complete their flight - the players count points. The winner is the player who has the most points.

Set contains two boards. Board A is for 2-6 players (each can play against everyone). Board B is for 2 or 4 players.

On the board A, six squadrons (teams) of Harriers compete at a military training area. Each team has a base with a runway and 5 platforms from which aircraft can takeoff and land vertically. In the bases are ammunition depots and anti-aircraft guns. They use training bombs and missiles.

Each player has a task to achieve as many hits as possible in the enemy bases. Every hit and shot down aircraft counts as one point. The game lasts as long as players have planes and bombs. The winner is the player who has the best ratio of given and received hits.

The other board (B) is predicted for two players (or two teams - Hot team and Cold team). Cold team has ships with ski jump runways and 5 smaller ships with platforms (concept of small ships where Harriers can takeoff / land). Hot team has platforms and ski jump runways on land. Each team has 12 planes and two transport helicopters. Helicopters carry commandos and if one of them lands in an enemy base - that is the end and victory. In case that nobody manages to do it, the winner is determined the same way as described for the board A - counting caused and received hits. On the board B are used markers of AA guns (each team has 3 of them) which can be placed anywhere in own territory / waters.

Content:
Box 34 x 24 x 5 cm (13,5 x 9,5 x 2 in); Board A : 94 x 62 cm (37 x 24,5 in); Board B : 64 x 48 cm (25 x 19 in); 2 control panels; 40 pieces (harriers and helicopters in flight); 40 markers (harriers and helicopters on ground); 144 markers (armament); 6 markers (AA guns); 12 markers (damage); 7 dice; and detailed rules.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

boardgame: Got It! A Scavenger Hunt Game
description:
All you have to do is get five objects and bring them home. But then you discover you'll have to navigate waterways and toll roads, and you can't go there without a pass. And you have to figure out the best route to take based on the roll of the die. And while Action cards can help you, they can also help your opponents, and even snatch defeat from the jaws of victory. And you don't think the other players are going to sit idly by and watch you move about unimpeded, do you?

Got It! is a game that's easy to learn, but tricky to win -- and since the objects you must get change each game, it's never the same twice. But with skillful use of your moves and a little luck, you'll be able to look at the other players with a grin and say "got it"!

Playing Got It! starts with the deck of Object cards. Each player is dealt five objects, and must roll the die and move around the board to land on the matching spaces. Landing on an object space "gets" the object, but it's not safe until the player gets it back "home" to their starting space. There are specially marked spaces that provide toll passes and boat passes -- a toll pass can get a player from one side of the board to the other quickly, while a boat pass lets players go after objects on the islands. There are also spaces that let players draw "action cards", some of which are used immediately and others which are held on to and used on later turns. The action cards can provide help (extra moves, extra turns, etc.) or hindrance (lose a pass, lose a turn, etc.). The first player to get all five of their objects home wins.

User summary:
廻る呪いと勇者たち (Mawaru Noroi To Yuusha Tachi) is a game from the doujin circle 'emag Image' in Japan. The English name would be Circulating Curses And Heroes because 廻る呪い refers to the Curse cards circulating between the players. In this co-op deck-building card game, the players share their cards to improve their status and decks in order to fight a dragon.
 Publisher summary:
「廻る呪いと勇者たち」は、プレイヤー間でカードを分け合い、自身のステータスやデッキを強化し、竜を討伐する協力型のカードゲームです。

Golf Anywhere
Golf in good weather or bad
Get that low score you've always wanted
It's fun and easy, anyone can play
A great way to learn the concepts of golf
Fun for the whole family

Each player takes a score sheet (Included) and a pencil.  Each die is a club with it's name and available yardage's and or penalities on each side.

Pick a die and roll that "club" take it's yardage that you rolled and subtract it from the distance to the hole.  Keep rolling till you sink the ball.

Complete 9 or 18 holes and the winner is the player with the lowest score.

John wants to place his cowboy to the gold mine and so does Carl & Abby. There is only one vacancy for the job. How to solve? Let's settle it the "Wild West Way".

Wild West Way (WWW) is a free print & play card game for up to five players. Each player is a new comer to the town & wants to build up his/her fame through different achievements. Before building up the fame, a player also needs to build up his/her various skills like shooting, brawling, gambling or horse riding. The more skillful a player is the higher chance a player can win over certain characters or buildings.

In each turn, players place their meeples to different buildings. A player may borrow money from the bank, take a wanted poster from the sheriff's office, donate to the church, buy items from the general store, or meet various town characters at the saloon. A player may also challenge other players with a gun duel, bar fight, horse racing or even a poker game.

When the game ends, the player with most fame points wins & becomes the new mayor of the town.

Microgame submission for 2015 Geekway to the West Design Competition.  The game is a two player card game that takes about 15 - 20 minutes to play.  It utilizes a deck of 22 cards--20 tactics cards and 2 Cool Down cards, as well as a range location card.

During the first part of the game, each player drafts eight tactics cards which are added to a Cool Down card to form his or her hand.

During the second part of the game, players simultaneously choose tactics to send their micromechs out to attack.  Attack, ranged attack, defense and speed values are determined not only by the card played, but also by the cards that have been played previously.  Cards can only be replenished into the hand by playing a Cool Down card.  First player to incapacitate his or her opponent is the winner.

In Historia, the players guide their civilization through 12,000 years of human history, from the first sparks up to the development of Singularity. Civilizations discover new technology and expand over the planet; they trade with more developed neighbors to import new technologies or exploit the land to have more power for future actions. Civilizations develop their military and wage wars with each other. They build wonders to make their civilization stand above others and to eventually attract tourists and newcomers to their land to follow their leaders and use their national advisors.

The Capitals Expansion introduces 7 Capital tiles and 14 Treasure tokens. During the game a player can build a Capital to gain Treasure tokens to spend in abilities and special powers.

In Historia, the players guide their civilization through 12,000 years of human history, from the first sparks up to the development of Singularity. Civilizations discover new technology and expand over the planet; they trade with more developed neighbors to import new technologies or exploit the land to have more power for future actions. Civilizations develop their military and wage wars with each other. They build wonders to make their civilization stand above others and to eventually attract tourists and newcomers to their land to follow their leaders and use their national advisors.

The greatest nations have been led by Leaders and wise Advisors able to choose and influence the Destiny of their nation.

In the Destiny Expansion, there are 16 Destiny tokens, with walls or arrows symbols, to place on the development matrix. A wall will prevent an improvement way on the matrix, while an arrow will facilitate the movement allowing to the player token to jump a space in a predeterminated direction.

In a distant future, psychic powers make warfare obsolete across Europa...until the ruler of Europa disappears, plunging the entire moon into a succession crisis.

Psychic scheming meets dynastic intrigue in this card game of prediction and influence. Players vie to become the power behind the throne, predicting who is next in line to become ruler of Europa and manipulating the court to make those predictions come true.

At the start of Heir to Europa, players predict how many coteries they'll win to their side. Each turn, you play one card from your hand, representing your soliciting help from a political ally. If that ally has a special ability and their faction is still loyal, you may activate that special ability as well. Each time a faction comes into play, its loyalty lowers by one. You must decide when it's best to play a lower value card to use an ability while you still can.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Fairies playing dodgeball with wild magic.

A dice game for 2-6 players. Each player takes on the role of a fairy. They gather magic and throw it at members on the opposite team. Fairy's have individual stats and powers.

Each turn players take mana tokens from the mana pool, pass their tokens to a teammate, or throw magic at an opponent. Throwing at an opponent entails rolling a number of dice equal to how many mana tokens they have. The game has custom dice with hit icons on 2 of the 6 sides. The attacker compares the number of hits they rolled to the defender's catch value. The defender loses health on a hit, or gathers some of the mana that the attacker spent.

Players need to be careful, as the magic is a bit unstable. At the end of each turn the overload die is rolled. Any player whose number comes up on the overload die has to check how much mana they have. If they have too much, their mana explodes causing them to lose health.

The Valiant is a card-based combat system.  In the Core Game are the 6 Heroes archetypes and all of their respective cards.  Thematically, the Valiant uses "fantasy" and "role-playing" conventions.

Rather than building a deck with dozens of cards, Heroes in the Valiant are comprised of only 8 cards, all of which begin the game in play  In this respect, customizing a hero is more like filling out "action bars" in an MMO than building a deck in a CCG.

Players can use the core game for dueling single heroes or for team combat. With future expansions, the Heroes from the core game can be used to play cooperative scenarios.

Synergo is a tile-laying abstract strategy game.  Each hex tile has 1-3 colors on it, and to play it onto the board it must simply agree in color on each edge with every other tile it touches.

Players get points for playing tiles. Although three different "Game Types" are outlined in the rules, the basic concept is that the more tiles you are connected to by color, the more points you score for that play.

In all game types, the winner is the player with the most points after the last tile has been played.

Invent fake endings to real Shakespeare quotes! Great fun for fans of the Bard and ignorant buffoons alike.

We invented Fakespeare one evening when we meant to play Dictionary but picked up a Shakespeare anthology instead.  The game evolved as the night went until we were crying with laughter.   We knew we were onto something great and decided to make it into a game that we could share with others - thus Fakespeare was born.

Bribes, scandals, strife, and strategy — just another day in the game of Politics.

Politics is a fast and furious card game for 2-9 players who compete in an election across 22 U.S. regions, trying to win the presidency. Each round by region, players spend cash and play action cards to give themselves bonuses or their opponents penalties (by implicating them in scandals). The player with the higher influence in a region wins the region (worth 1-6 votes). The player with the highest number of votes after all regions are played wins.

Being the third expansion for Day of Days, Brécourt Manor comes with a scenario booklet including 17 new maps: Missions range from overrunning enemy artillery guns to weaving your forces in and around trench-lines and even storming Brécourt Manor itself.
 The two new Landmark tiles and 16 variable trench map squares give more flexibility for building new maps and stories. New Order cards feature multiple objectives for more team-oriented style gameplay. Also included are new cards for the two characters Pvt. Fields and Gef. Maier from Day of Days, who return as specialists in infiltration tactics and assault tactics, respectively.

Linknotize is the first ever board game to integrate the use of online information and tools into more traditional off-line game play. Linknotize asks players to roll two color-coded dice and then pick two, corresponding color-coded topic cards.  The topic cards are chosen from six subject areas: History, The Arts, Sports, Geography, Entertainment, and Science. By using a device like a smartphone, tablet, SmartTV or computer, the player or team must “link” the two topics by traveling through the embedded links within an online encyclopedia like Wikipedia. The player or team has three minutes and/or a maximum of ten clicks to “link” the starting card topic to the destination card topic with the object to accomplish it both the fastest and/or with the fewest number of clicks. The first to combine online search and discovery with offline traditional board game features, Linknotize allows players to capitalize on their general knowledge of a wide variety of cultural and historical topics along with their web browsing skills to connect two topics online while racing the clock.
 Linknotize forges a path for a completely innovative category of gaming that juxtaposes technology with more conventional play. The result is the perfect party game that is designed to entertain all audiences. Testing knowledge of categories ranging from pop culture to geography to history to sports, the game is as much about quick thinking as it is about strategically analyzing relationships to link seemingly unrelated topics.

Playing on the “six degrees” phenomenon, Linknotize, proves the idea that everyone and everything can be connected. While it may not seem obvious on the surface, our lives are truly a “World Wide Web” of related concepts, interactions and ideas that lead to common destinations.

boardgame: A Hill Near Hastings: The Beginning of the Norman Conquests
description:
A companion game to Stamford Bridge: End of the Viking Age in the same "Shields & Swords" series, A Hill Near Hastings: The Beginning of the Norman Conquests recreates *the* battle that will forever be tied to the year 1066.

This game sees the addition of cavalry and bow units to the "Shields & Swords" system, and features special rules that recreate, in a simple way, the telling differences in organizational structure and discipline between the Norman and Anglo-Saxon forces.

The Anglo-Saxon Player will have a tough time of it, plugging holes in his line and crossing his fingers that his Shield Wall holds and the hill he occupies will be enough to soften the Norman blows. But the Norman Player can't sleep-walk his way to victory, and must identify and exploit weak spots in the enemy host.
family: Series: Shields and Swords (Tiny Battle Publishing)

description:
What is more fun than building a Robot? Destroying other Robots! Fast to play and an easily expandable design to add more destruction!

So what is BEEP. BEEP. ATTACK!?

Step one:

Build a robot.

Step two:

Wait for another robot to be built.

Step three:

ATTACK!

BEEP. BEEP. ATTACK!

A Casual/Filler style game that involves building Robots and destroying each other.  Unfortunately, you are a dumb Robot and are forced to replace your parts as you acquire new parts of the same type, but luckily for you, you can always salvage that good part you just dropped off.

Make no mistake you must be able to destroy the enemy robot, but an all glass cannon build will surely let your opponent destroy you unless you get the Weapon, "Thrusters" or are playing a Variant mode!  Sometimes the best offense is a little defense!

Expansion coming at end of year adding in a few new cards that add to the dynamics such as: Augments, Group Salvage, and more Parts including some with Options to not replace!

boardgame: BEEP. BEEP. ATTACK!: Robotic Masters Coop expansion
description: The first expansion to BEEP. BEEP. ATTACK! is a Cooperative Expansion where players fight Master Bots in an Epic 3 stage battle!

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Skull & Shackles – "Goblin Lockpick" Promo Card
description:
Subscriber promo card.

Goblin Lock Pick
Item (Tool, Alchemical)

Check to Acquire (Dexterity, Disable, Craft 6)

Powers

Reveal this card to add 1 die to your Disable Check. Bury this card to defeat a barrier; you are dealt 2d4 Fire damage.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Skull & Shackles – "Vomit Twin" Promo Card
description:
Subscriber Promo Card.

Vomit Twin
Spell (Magic, Arcane)

Check to Acquire (Constitution, Fortitude 6)

Powers

Display this card next to a location. When you encounter a card, you may evade it and move to this location. Move this card next to your previous location. Each character at that location must succeed at a Constitution or Fortitude 9 check or recharge a card from his hand. At the end of the scenario, if you do not have the Arcane skill, banish this card.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
From the publisher's website:

After the Gloaming and the corruption of their King, the Huranii became frenzied tools of the Darkness. With the ritual sacrifice of their tail, they claim complete servitude to the Rat King. Tailless are quick but cowardly, preferring to strike the least threatening and weakest targets until they can swarm over their stronger enemies.

From the publisher's website:

In dark caverns deep beneath the old buried cities of the world, lie the spawning pits of the Rath. These ponderous, lumbering rats are a perversion of the once graceful Huranii, their great eyes now filled with malice and hate. Masters of ranged combat and shadow magic, the Rath are feared by even their own kind. The shadows are not safe when Rath are nearby.

Players are collectors during the Era of Taisho(1912-1926), collecting the seven kinds of the animal cards.

The cards are a cat, a dog, a snake, a fish, a tiger and a elephant. They are three types, rarity, normal and variety.
There are two rules variants.

One is the trick taking game.
Each player is dealt three cards as hand. At first of the round, the turn player deals one card to each player. The turn player starts to choose to change the revealed card to the handed card or to keep it.
The trick is started as a "must follow". If the player has the same type of the lead card included in the opened card on the front of the player, the player must play the card or keep the opened card. When the player play the card, the opened card is added to the player's hand.
The winner is the player(s) played the different card against the lead card. The player get one card from the opened cards.
The rare and variety card have the special effect when following.

Two is the bluff game.
The preparation and the object are same. And at first of each round, one card is dealt to all players. The turn player plays one card with face up. Other players play one card with face down. After that, the turn player can change the played card by himself/herself.
The winner is the player(s) don't have the card with face up by the turn player on his/her hand excluded in the played card.
The card handling is more technical.

This is a wonderful trick taking game.

The cards are 4 colors and the numbers are 5, 6, 8, 13. But the same numbered and same colored cards are included.

The trick is the same with like Wizard, Skat and so on. But a player can play any cards with the same number. And the cards are added the number each other.
So if you have 13(Max), you may lose three 5 cards. Then this game is a wonderful trick taking game.

The points are calculated by the cards with the money.
Theme is the era of war from 1400-1600. But the war is very beautiful.

From the publisher's website:

As the Darkness grows in the land its influence spreads into more than just the weaker willed creatures. Agents were once people, perhaps even Heroes, until the Darkness invaded their minds and slowly corrupted them. With this corruption came great power at the cost of freedom. Agents are a deadly boss-type monster that the Heroes can encounter in their adventures at their own peril. If an Agent is defeated, one of the Heroes is granted that Agent’s ability card, bestowing them with a unique power.

The Earth needs to be fed: move your counters around the board and fill up your boxes with fruit before the hungry caterpillar can eat it. By achieving this, you'll feed the planet! Feeding the planet is a collaborative game for all the family, to teach everybody how nice it is to play and win together as a team.

Feeding the planet is a collaborative game developed for Expo 2015 in Milan.

How to play:
On the board there are some tokens face down: each token can show a fruit, a caterpillar or the Expo2015 mascot.
Each player throws the dice and moves their counter: according to the token, the players pick fruit, move their counter wherever they want or draw a piece of the caterpillar jigsaw.
When all players have completed their individual boards and the harvest is safe, the players have won!
However, if the jigsaw with the caterpillar is completed, they have lost.

From Publisher:

This is a game packed full with ideas and suggestions, stories, riddles, and poems all about the pieces included. It is a fun way to aid development of language skills, imagination, concentration, and reactions. For 1-6 players and a reader. Please note: This game can be played without the other games in the Ratz Fatz series. For ages 3-12.

As the poems and stories are read by the reader (and players are encouraged to make up their own) the players attempt to be the first to grasp the appropriate wooden object when it is referred to in the story.  The game can also be played with one player. making the game more of an activity for the reader and player.

boardgame: Ultimate Battle Soul: Konjiki no Gash Bell!! (Final war in ancient times BOX)
description:
Theme:
究極バトル魂：金色のガッシュベル!! （古の最終決戦BOX） or Ultimate Battle Soul: Konjiki no Gash Bell!! (Final war in ancient times BOX) uses characters from manga and anime Konjiki no Gash Bell!! (known in the West as Zatch Bell!)Pitting one team of Mamono demons against another leads to a tournament of battles in order to become the king of Mamono world.

Game-play:
A chess like game with a dice-driven combat, modified by using magic powers of the demons. Demons' skill are described on the "spell book" cards. One card represents one demon and contains 6 unique skills. Players construct up to 4 demons team with unique names. Figures of the demons move between 3 zones: offense, defense and moon light zone. Demons' skills are triggered on specific zones and require AP (action points). Each demon figure has a counter with a max. 12AP, which are simultaneously its life. During a turn player can do 2 actions.

Goal:
The game is won by a player who resets all opponent's Mamono demons counters (life) to zero.

Components:
There are preconstructed starter (this BOX), and two other sets:
Vol.1 降臨編 - booster singles, 7 different figures, 2 figures (coloured and uncoloured) in 1 booster
Vol.2 回帰ゼオン編 - booster singles, 7 different figures, 2 figures (coloured and uncoloured) in 1 booster
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Contains 17 cards:
2 x All You Can Eat Flapjacks
2 x Smart Axe
2 x Sasquatch Repellent
3 x Cord Wood Pete
2 X J.L. Beaver Whisperer
2 x Standing Chop
2 x Sasquatch's Coffee
2 x Hatchet

boardgame: Flapjacks & Sasquatches: Mound of Bacon expansion
description: This expansion features 2 Mound of Bacon cards to add to the Jack deck and 1 custom Bacon die. When you play the Mound of Bacon card you roll the Bacon Die.  The Bacon die has "zzz" for the 1 face meaning you ate too much bacon (like that's possible!) and fell asleep, lose your chopping roll this turn.  On the 2-5 sides there is 1 slice of bacon and you add 1 chop to your tree.  On the 6 side there are 2 slices of bacon giving you 2 chops to your tree!

These 15 wacky characters will add a whole new level to your game.  Deal 1 Lumberjack to each player at the start of the game and they can play it whenever they want!

Example Lumberjacks; Nelson "Free" Love, Chainsaw Sue, Chet "Awesome Axe" Studman, Hairy Harry.

Make the biggest rabbit clan ever by expanding the warren, and finding food enough for the whole family!

Each turn, players place cards to dig tunnels, build and upgrade rooms, and find food. Players score points when there is both food and room enough for more rabbits in the warren!

This game features 7 players with unique abilities! 20 special upgrade rooms! Poker Rooms! Powder Rooms! Cigar Rooms! All original hand-painted watercolors by Aaron Youngren! A different game every time you play!

This is GETTYSBURG: INTO THE WHEATFIELD for ATS. No one that has studied this battlefield is unaware of where the infamous Wheatfield is. We've all walked that hallowed ground. Now, students of the battle that own CWATS can re-fight the battle in detail on the historical map. You'll need to own CWATS of course -- but if you buy direct from CH, you'll also receive a copy of the DEVIL'S DEN counter sheet—allowing you to choose one module or the other with FREEDOM! Isn't that what all the shooting was about in 1863! Of course, we suggest you nail BOTH CWATS modules: Devil's Den and Into the Wheatfield, but the choice is yours! Order your men Prone, call for a Bayonet Charge! Choose your cannon ammunition. Send a Courier out to the end of your line. It's all up to you as YOU are In Command (at least we hope you are, as the rules say, units that are not In Command ... are Out of Command ... or Isolated .. or OOC and Isolated ... oh the detail!
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Crazy for you  is a russian roulette card game for 1-4 players, playing time is 30 minutes. Set in a unique fight between friends and kisses, You put the stack of cards on the table face down. Players take turns drawing them. Like Russian Roulette, if you draw an YOU NOT GETTING ANY (blocked) card, you die and you’re out of the game with no kisses.

How to win: Be the person who has some clothing he/she is announced the winner!

How to Play: You put the stack of cards on the table face down. Players take turns drawing them. Like Russian Roulette, if you draw an YOU NOT GETTING ANY (blocked) card, you die lose you’re out of the game. But you can save yourself from losing by playing a Deactivate card to keep the game from being Blocked, or make someone steal a kiss.

There are also special cards that make the game more interesting. For example, attack cards that feature girl taking out their clothes, or allow you to spank in the future or shuffle a kiss. Use the cards in good fun or sexy cruel intentions against your opponents.

King of Tortuga is a pirate-themed player elimination card game for up to eight players. The setting is the pirate enclave of Tortuga during the heyday of piracy in the early 18th century where a number of crews are in the city and decide to fight to see who is the best. As the Captain of the crew if you are successful you will be declared the King of Tortuga and the the most powerful pirate this side of Blackbeard.

The game is easy - each player is given five cards that have a variety of attack, defense and special cards. Each card has either a point value and/or an explanation of what they do to let you know what to do.  Each player also is given 12 markers. The markers represent your crew.  As your crew inflicts damage your opponents will lose more crew members until eliminated until opponents are gone... but you.

You are an apprentice sorcerer in this new CCG. But can you master the elements when put to the test? So begins your Quest for Arete!

Quest for Arete (Are-a-Tay) is an Elemental card game of sorcery and duels.  This beautifully illustrated game is suitable for players age 12 and up. The game is designed to be simple to learn, but with multiple layers of complexity that may be added based on player experience and ability. In the game, each player is a sorcerer’s apprentice charged by their master to make their way in the world seeking to increase their Arete – or excellence.

The back story of the game sets it in the pre-history of modern chemistry when magicians and sorcerers were actually trained in the undiscovered properties of the natural elements.

As an apprentice, you must duel any other sorcerer you meet using magical elements (represented by the cards) that combine make potions and spells. Each duel, won or lost, increases your experience and understanding of the elements and therefore, increases your Arete.

With additional rules applied, the challenge increases as players contemplate element stacking, spell interactions, time constraints, character cards and special character abilities.  Decks are custom built by the player to suite their style of play and the strengths of their chosen character.

The game is available in a Starter Set that contains everything needed for two players. The kit includes 2 play mats, a full-color 24-page rule book, a timer, 4 character cards, two starter decks (identical) and two expansion decks (unique).

Once they learn the basics using their starter decks, the players can duel for first draw from Expansion Deck A. Each player chooses 5 new elements and customizes their deck. They may then duel again for first pick until Expansion A is depleted. As they repeat this process with Expansion B, their decks become more and more different - focusing on their unique preferences and character strengths.

Did they choose wisely? Have they mastered the elements and gained Arete?

Can you do better?

Quest for Arete can range from a simple game to high levels of complexity that will satisfy even the most avid gamer!

To locate a Quest for Arete  distributor or retailer near you, visit www.QuestForArete.com

Nova Insula lets the players define the shape of a new island rising from the sea and compete on who gets most vegetation and different animal species to live on it. The winner will make the biggest impact by growing thick forests for their animals to thrive and hunt for food in.

In the game the players take turns in placing either hexagonal terrain cards to form the island and grow vegetation on existing terrain or animal cards representing groups of reptiles, arachnids, mammals and insects to live on the island.

The game ends when any player runs out of either cards, after which victory points are calculated based on how well and much each player has contributed to the now fully formed island. The player with most points is naturally the winner.

Board game Football is a simulation of the real sport football (soccer). Each player has 11 figurines (10 players and one goalkeeper). In one move player rolls two dices, black determines how many fields one figurine moves and white for how many fields moves the ball. The ball can be moved only if a figurine of a player is next to it. When figurine player is moving  - it can push the ball with it. At the end of its movement ball can be 'shot' to another own figurine or into the net. When the ball figurine enters the 'goal' that is score. The opponent puts the ball on the center of the board and begins new action. Players agree on how long the game lasts.

Werewolf for 2 players? That's impossible right? 
Not if you play YIKES! The deduction card game for 2 players.

YIKES! Captures the feeling of Saturday morning cartoons and replacing running around in circles, with putting  your deduction skills to the test. Awaken your inner "meddling kid" and unmask your opponent.

Draw a Secret Identity Card. The card shows what type of ghoul you are and the Clue that can unmask you.
Players select Clue Cards from a row in the middle of the table.
You are allowed to pick up any Clue except the Clue shown on your Secret Identity Card. Every Clue helps you with unmasking your opponent, but also reveals a part of your secret identity. You gain 1 Candy Bar if you escape (collect 8 different Clue Cards). 1 Candy Bar if your opponent made a mistake. And 2 Candy Bars if you unmasked your opponent successfully (by giving him the right Clue Card).
Collect 5 Candy Bars and win the game.

Each player has vessels: Battleship, cruisers, battlecruisers, destroyers, torpedo boats and landing boats and submarines. On the board is a strait. On different sides of the strait players have their own port where are ammunition depots and workshop for repairing damaged ships.

The main characteristic is that the game board is covered with a net of equilateral triangles. The vertices of these triangles are points at which players hold the position and move figurines.

Each player has a display, which shows the characteristics, loaded ammunition, and received damage of vessels. The main objective of the game is to sink as many opponent ships as possible and to hit as many targets in opponents harbor. The game lasts as long as players have ships and ammunition, but may be finished earlier. The winner is the player who gave more hits, but the game can end differently - if a player manages to make a landing. That is a case when at least one landing boat succeed to land on the opposite shore.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
Košarka is a simulation of the real sport basketball. Each player has 5 figurines. Board is covered with fields (hex). On the board are drawn lines like on real basketball court. In each turn player rolls two dice. Player move two figurines by numbers on dices, and marker (ball) by sum of the dices. One attack lasts maximum 6 moves.

In the game there are fouls, outs, free throws, dead ball, 3 second rule, ...

Cyvasse is originally a fictional two-players game in George R.R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire series; he described it as "a bit of chess, a bit of blitzkrieg, a bit of stratego. Mix well and add imagination."

This fan-made implementation by Alex McRitchie is played on a hexagonal board and features 10 different playing pieces, including cavalry units, ranged units (a catapult, a trebuchet and crossbowmen), and special units such as dragon, war elephants and mountains.

Like in Stratego, before game starts players secretly place their units on the board, using a screen. Each unit can only capture units that are as strong as it or weaker, except that some units can "trump" stronger units: a trebuchet is the only unit that can kill a dragon. Many units have unique powers, like the ability to move twice or to attack from a distance.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire

description:
This game adds a test match flavor to Pocket Cricket, stepping away from the T20 style format and building a new game, Test Cricket features a new bowling mechanic for separate Pace or Spin bowling and 8 stylized dice. Designed for longer play and more realistic appeals.

The game comes in two black or white pouched versions and includes the dice and a rule sheet. A piece of paper and writing implement will be needed to keep score. There is a scoresheet available for download on the PocketSports website

This expansion for the Dark Horse base game adds the ability to expand the game to a 5th player. The rules card explains how to integrate this additional player and offers a variant to help offset the increase in game length.

Go Fish Yourself is more than just one deck of cards.  The Naughty Edition will allow players to keep the nonsense going.  It's sure to push some comfort zones.  It comes with 52 new cards and challenges.  This expansions (or edition) can be played as standalone game or combined with the original.  Players will be able to mix and match their favorite pairs from all or any of the expansions to create customized decks.

Go Fish Yourself is more than just one deck of cards.  The Fishy Edition will allow players to keep the nonsense going.  This expansion will come with 52 new cards and challenges.  This expansion (or edition) can be played as a standalone game or combined with the original.  Players will be able to mix and match their favorite pairs from all or any of the expansions to create customized decks.

boardgame: Backcountry: The Game of Wilderness Survival – AT Edition
description:
Backcountry is a strategy board game about backpacking created to be played on the trail or at home.  The AT edition is built around the Appalachian Trail.  The game offers 3 different starting positions and five characters to choose from with which to begin your hike.

Gameplay includes managing food, navigating  bear and snow danger zones, trading gear and planning your route. It offers the full experience of hiking in the wild. You will get the experience of all the joys and struggles of the Backcountry. Find your way through the trail using a Map and Compass. Speed up your hiking with Poles. Prepare for the Snow Danger Zone with Snow Gear. Hike to the top of Mount Washington or be the first hiker to reach Katahdin. You can barter Gear with your friends or buy Gear with Food Units.

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Calling All Turtles Card Game
description:
Based on the Nickelodeon Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle Series, up to 4 players are dealt an equal amount of cards and play cards similar to the classic card game "War", with a few caveats.

The highest number takes the cards. If you play a turtle that matches your headband the value is doubled. If a villain is played, you must find that villain's tile before the included sand timer expires. The villain tiles are placed randomly on one of three different boards that have been placed in different rooms. Lastly if, after the using the red filter decoder in the headbands, one of the cards has a pizza icon on it and someone shouts, "Pizza," they take the cards.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
Set up:
Pick a color.
Set up the bowling pin cards in a triangle like shown. Put your strike/spare cards off to the side. Hold your star cards in your hand.

Play:
Each player throws a card at their pins to see who goes first. 
Whoever is touching the most cards gets to go first. 
If there is a tie, keep throwing to someone gets more.

You take turns throwing your cards. Your hand can not go past the front pin of the pins in front of you. 
The winner of the previous round goes first.
If you get a spare (win a round), then win another round. That spare becomes a strike, just flip it over.

Scoring:
Each round each player throws 3 star cards. Whoever touches the most combined cards wins a spare. If the player touches all the cards over the course of the 3 throws, they will get a strike card. First person to get 3 strike cards wins. All ties void each other out for each round.

Alternative.
If you want to add a new challenge use the numbers on the back of the cards to score points. You get points for the cards you land on. You can play to a predetermined score. Or you can win unless you have more points than your opponent.

Sky Scrapers is an abstract and strategy board game for 2 players.

Each turn, a player moves one of his own stacks a number of spaces equal to the height of the stack, stacking it onto of another stack. A stack is owned by the player whose piece is on top.

The game ends when a player cannot make a legal move. Then, each player counts how many skyscrapers (stacks of four or more pieces) he has. The player with the most skyscrapers wins the game.

Categories with dice.
5 letter dice have all the A-Z letters.
2 Dice has 12 categories (6 categories each).
20 coins to win.

The game is played simultaneously.
The first player rolls 6 dice: one category die to his choice and all 5 letter dice.
Now there is one category and 5 letters to choose from.
The first player to call out loud a valid word that matches the category die and one of the letters, wins one coin.
The game continues on this manner until there are no more coins to win. The player that collected the highest number of coins wins the game.

Ever played the classic Trivial Pursuit with help of your smartphone or tablet? Now you can! Shuffle Trivial Pursuit combines the classic gameplay with some new features. Play in 2 teams against the time and when you think about the answer to a question your time is decreasing. When your time is out, the game is over and the other team win! Or play without the app just with the card. Your handcards chose whats your next question and you can even steal won cards from your opponents. [User summary]

Dogfight is a board wargame for two players. There are two levels. In the first level there are WWII fighter planes. The second level is more complicated: modern jets use air-to-air missiles beside gun.

The main characteristic is that the game board is covered with a net of equilateral triangles. The vertices of these triangles are points towards which players hold the position and move figurines.

The objective is to shoot down all enemy planes. One figurine can shoot on enemy figurine by moving behind it. The game continues until one player runs out of the plane.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
Supersonična trka  is a very simple game for 2 to 4 players. Board has 3D elements.

Each player has one plane. All planes circle around the airport. There are several paths that are intertwined. Shorter paths have more obstacles (clouds) that slow down the planes. The winner is the player whose plane first gets to the finish line.

Typical roll and move game. Players are going around the game board. The first player who cross the route, wins the game.
Great graphics feature the intrepid Ahrens-Fox fire pumper truck, a folding board is traversed by 12 cast lead miniature Ahrens-Fox pumpers in 4 different colors.

Board game Dinosaurusi is a simple game for 2 to 4 players. Each player has one figurine. The aim is to get to the finish as soon as possible. Paths which are moving figurines on are mutually intersect. In addition to the path are arranged markers (food) that players collect. Longer paths have more markers. The player whose figurine first reaches the finish receives 6 points, second 4 and third place 2 points. It is also important to collect as many markers. Each marker delivers one point. The winner is the player who at the end of the game has the most points.

Ko to leti is a game for 2 to 4 players. On the board is shown sky where there are many aircraft. Each player has one figurine. The figurine represents a fighter reconnaissance plane. The task is to collect markers. That means that the reconnaissance aircraft photographed and recognized the plane. The game lasts until all of the markers are collected. The winner is the player who has the most.

This is a board game about Swedish politics. It is for you who like to play boardgames with friends and family and is interested in politics and want to learn more and challenge your friends in who know the most.

How to play:
All players choose what politician to play with, and then you move your player with the help of two dices. For advanced players you can use stepping cards to adjust your steps back or forward, depending on on what strategy you have. On each step you can earn - or steal, a percentage (points) depending on the type of card you get. The player with the most percentage in the end of the game will win.

Card categories:

- Quotes
You need to answer what politician who said the quote.

- Media
The media cards will give you points och tell you to give point sto your fellow players depending on what you have done and what the media is reporting.

- Politician parties
Questions about the different political parties.

- Debate
Here you choose your debate strategy against one of the other players, and dependent on the result you will either steal percentage from the other player, or give away. This part is based on rock, paper and scissors.

Who knows the most
On this type of cards you can choose, dependent on the category of the card, if you want to answer it or let one of the other players do so. Dependent on your strategy, and if the answer is correct or not, you and the other player will either get percentage, or you will have to give percentage away.


Swedish
Jakten på Makten är ett brädspel om politik och samhället för dig som gillar sällskapsspel, politik eller bara vill ha ett väldigt unikt svenskt spel där du och dina motspelare garanterat lär er något och får något att diskutera. Alla kan spela spelet, oberoende av politisk inriktning.

Så spelar du:

Ni väljer vilken makthavare ni ska ha som spelpjäs, den turas ni om att förflytta längs spelplanen med hjälp av två tärningar (och med hjälp stegkort för er som är strategiskt lagda och inte gillar tärningar). För varje varv ni går kommer ni närmare mandatperiodens slut. När det sker är det den som har flest procent (poäng) som vinner. Procenten kan du få när du landar på spelplanens olika rutor, nedan står det vilka rutor som finns.

Citat:

Du får ett citat uppläst och ska gissa vilken politiker som har sagt det. Gissar du rätt får du en procent.

Media:

Mediakorten innehåller påståenden som du drabbas av, du kan t.ex. behöva ge bort en procent för att du sagt att Stockholmare är smartare än lantisar eller få en procent för att du höll ett fantastiskt sommartal.

Partikunskap:

Du svarar på en fråga som har med partierna att göra, svarar du rätt får du en procent.


Debatt:

Du möter valfri spelare och den som vinner debatten får procent. Debattera gör ni genom att visa varsitt debattkort samtidigt. Om den ena har ett kort där det står "humor (tar fakta)" och den andra har ett kort där det står "fakta (tar arg)" vinner den förstnämnda. Det är lite som sten, sax, påse.

Vem vet mest:

Precis som i TV-programmet får du välja om du vill svara på frågan i kategorin du hamnar på eller om någon annan spelare ska göra det. Svarar du själv får du procent vid rätt svar och blir av med procent vid fel svar. Väljer du att någon annan ska svara får ni båda procent ifall svaret är korrekt.

Border Reiver Miniature Wargaming Rules.

Rules for fighting out raids and retribution between rival border clans in sixteenth century Britain. Each player raises an army of between 10 and 30 models: each represents one Reiver and ideally players will have mounted and dismounted versions of each.

Models are classified according to armour, weapons, and their ability to fight as border raiders. Dice are rolled to resolve combat and morale tests.

4 scenarios are included as are campaign rules.

Although these rules are intended for wargames set in the sixteenth century, they should provide a workable foundation for raiding games set earlier and later in history; they have worked quite well for me with Highwaymen, for a variety of ‘sword and shield’ skirmishes, and for the medieval border wars between England and Scotland.

Sixteen Stone is an abstract strategy game played on a 5x5 grid. Each player has 8 stones in either red or blue. The objective of the game is to remove all of your opponents stones from the board by either pushing them off of the board or by capturing them. Sixteen Stone was placed into the Public Domain on 12/3/2015 by the Designer.

It is featured in the 2018 Green Box of Games Design Contest expansion rules booklet.

TGABA (Trivia Game About Black Americans) can be played by two or more players, or by a group divided into teams. A moderator is needed to ask questions and decide which participant answers correctly and fastest.

One questions is asked for each card.  The team answering each question correctly "owns" that card; the team "owning" the greatest number of cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Knight Fight is a dice game for two players lasting around 5 minutes, that sees two brave Knights face off in mortal combat. The objective of the game is to score three hits on your opponent.

The game is played with two sets of polyhedral dice: d4, d6, d8, d10x2, d12 and d20. The d4, d6, and d8 represent weapons, the d10 gauntlets, the d12 a helmet and d20 a shield.

The game is set up by both players rolling their d12. The result is each players' Defence number. The number the opposing player needs to beat to score a hit on them. This Defence can be added to when the player chooses to Defend.

Players secretly choose dice to roll, and roll them simultaneously onto the table. Players can choose to Attack (with a weapon and a gauntlet), Defend (with a weapon or a shield), Jostle/Disarm (with a Gauntlet) or Regather (where a player only feigns to roll dice, allowing them to return dice rolled on previous turns to their hand).

The dice results are compared and players may retrieve only one of their rolled dice, the rest are "placed with their squire".

The game can be played using optional Knight and Items cards that introduce variable player powers and greater tactical depth.
